english_verse: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
translation_verse: “Kibite mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nyi ɔnyɩn ii biti ɔ nyɛ ɔtɔ kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa, kɛ ba tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Emanuweli,” kɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ bʋ anɛ asɛ.”

english_verse: From David to the time of the exile in Babylonia, the ancestors of Jesus were: David, Solomon , Rehoboam, Abijah, Asa, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram; Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, Manasseh, Amon, Josiah, and Jehoiachin and his brothers.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyosiya mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyekoniya maa mu‐supu ana. Kanan saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Babilon abi ba kɔ kɩtaa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi waa bamʋ anyɛ mʋ.

english_verse: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyekobu mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Gyosefu, Mɛɛrɩ mu‐kuli mʋ. Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ maŋa ɩ kʋʋgɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Krisito mʋ.

english_verse: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: Aso mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Sadoki, nɩɩ Sadoki mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Akim, nɩɩ Akim mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Eliyudi.

english_verse: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Yesu Krisito kakʋʋgɛ asɩn ndee. Ba taa mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ sa Gyosefu kekili, amaa pɔyɩ kɛ ba bɩɩ abaa mʋ, ɩ ba fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ sʋ ɔtɔ, nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩ ba kanɩn.

english_verse: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
translation_verse: Gyosefu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ mu‐kuli mʋ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, nɩɩ maa mee biti fɛɛ ɔ ŋaabaa Mɛɛrɩ asa nsana tɔ sʋ mʋ, ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ baala kina mʋ.

english_verse: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
translation_verse: Amaa Gyosefu, maa fɛ falɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ idee tɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Gyosefu, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, man waa kufuu fɛɛ fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ taa Mɛɛrɩ kili, lɩɩ fɛɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ɔtɔ.

english_verse: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
translation_verse: Ee biti ɔ kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa, kɛ fʋ yɩla mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ Yesu, kpalɩ fɛɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ mʋ asa lɩɩ bamʋ alibi tɔ.”

english_verse: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ ba waa, kɛ ɩ ba tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Gyosefu maa kyiŋi mʋ, o kili Mɛɛrɩ taa mʋ kpee mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ɔ waa mʋ.

english_verse: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
translation_verse: Amaa Gyosefu man dɛ yɛ Mɛɛrɩ halɩɩ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ. Nɩɩ Gyosefu yɩla mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ Yesu.

english_verse: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ Yesu Krisito Betilehem kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, kanɩn saŋa maa mʋ Herodi ɩ gyɛ owura. Nɩɩ ɩ ba fɛɛ, anyaasɩnpʋ akʋ ba lɩɩ kyɔwɛ ɩlɩɩ ba Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ,

english_verse: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
translation_verse: nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, “Nkɩɩ kebii mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ yɛ o be gyii Gyiwu abi kuwura mʋ? An wu mu kikyikpebi maa paatɛ lɩɩ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ, nɩɩ an ba yɛ an be muŋa bʋnyaa mʋ.”

english_verse: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Owura Herodi maa nu falɛ asɩn mʋ ɩɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa yɛ Gyerusalem abi kpini.

english_verse: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba gyaŋɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fɩnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba kʋʋgɛ Krisito abɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ?”

english_verse: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Bee biti ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Betilehem kadɛ tɔ Gyudeya Ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.” Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋ wʋla kyʋrɔɔ yɩla yɛ,

english_verse: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
translation_verse: “Fʋ Betilehem mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, halɩɩ fʋ maa gyɛ pɩɩ gbaa mʋ, fʋ gyɛ ɔkparɛ lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ tɔ nɩɩ owura lala mʋ ii biti ɔ lɩɩ, nɩɩ mʋ ee biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ ɔkpapʋ sa mɛ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi.”

english_verse: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Owura Herodi tɩɩ anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ kɛ ɔ bɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu kikyikpebi maa lɩɩ mʋ.

english_verse: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o sun bamʋ Betilehem tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan kpee naa kɩɩ danbɩrasa biti kebii mʋ. Nɩɩ fan kan wu mʋ mʋ fan ba tɔwɛ mɛ, kɛ ngbaa mɛ n kpee naa muŋa bʋnyaa mʋ.”

english_verse: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
translation_verse: Ba maa nu Owura mʋ asɛ lʋwɛ nɩɩ ba maa sʋ bee kpee mʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ ba bwii wu kikyikpebi mʋ nɩɩ ba daa wu kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ kɩ gyankpaa bamʋ kpee naa yɩlɛ opula mʋ nɩɩ kebii mʋ bʋ mʋ sʋsʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

english_verse: They were thrilled and excited to see the star.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu kikyikpebi mʋ, bamʋ ansi i gyii naa kyʋn sʋ.

english_verse: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
translation_verse: Ba maa loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba wu kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ, nɩɩ ba kyʋn muŋa sun mʋ. Nɩɩ ba saŋɛ bamʋ ɩsʋla nɩɩ ba lɛɛ suwa yɛ tulale mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ firanki‐ɩnsɛnsɩ yɛ tulale mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ mɩɩrɛ kɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
translation_verse: Kamaa tɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ da bamʋ amu tɔ idee tɔ yɛ ba man bɩla bwii kpee Owura Herodi asɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba bɔla ɔkpa pʋpwɛ sʋ bwii kpee bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Ba taa Yesu Sɩlɛ Kpe Egyipiti

english_verse: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
translation_verse: Ba maa kyʋn kamaa mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ba lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ Gyosefu idee tɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ taa kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi sɩlɛ kpee Egyipiti naa kyɩna tʋtɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan lɩɩ tʋtɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Herodi ɩɩ kɩɩ ee biti kebii mʋ ɔ mɔɔ.”

english_verse: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ kʋsʋ taa kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi kagyanbwɛ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ kpee Egyipiti.

english_verse: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba kyɩna naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Herodi wu. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ba tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Lɩɩ Egyipiti tɔ nɩɩ n tɩɩ Mi‐bii.”

english_verse: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
translation_verse: Owura Herodi maa bɩɩ fɛɛ anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ ba pɩna mʋ mʋ, ɔ nyɛ agbʋ gaa. Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba mɔɔ nbii‐nyɩnsa‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta kyʋn nsu nnyɔ sʋ, nɩɩ ba bʋ Betilehem yɛ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ mʋ kpini. Ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba waa kanɩn nɩn lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ ba wu kikyikpebi mʋ, ii biti ɩ waa fɛɛ nsu nnyɔ.

english_verse: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyeremiya sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ba tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ,

english_verse: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
translation_verse: “Ba nu kusu ɩlʋn kʋ lɩɩ Rama kadɛ tɔ, ɩɩ saawʋ yɛgɛ ɩ sʋ kʋwɛɛ, Raheli ɛɛ sawʋ ee su mu‐bii ana sʋ. Asa ba tɔwɛ yuula mʋ kɔkɔlɔ kpɩnɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ mu‐bii ana ba wu.” Ba Taa Yesu Bwii Kpe Nasareti

english_verse: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
translation_verse: Owura Herodi kelewu kamaa mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ Gyosefu idee tɔ Egyipiti ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ,

english_verse: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
translation_verse: nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ taa kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi bwii kpee Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba daa bee biti ba mɔɔ kebii mʋ ba wu.”

english_verse: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ kʋsʋ taa kebii mʋ maa mu‐nyi kpee Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
translation_verse: Amaa maa nu yɛ Akelasi i yii mʋ‐sɛ Herodi ayaa tɔ ee gyii kuwura Gyudeya mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa mʋ yɛ tʋtɔ kikpe. Ɩbwaarɛ maa da mu kumu tɔ idee tɔ sʋ mʋ, o kpeli kpee Galile kɩbaafʋn.

english_verse: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna Nasareti kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ tɔwɛ lɩɩ kebii mʋ sʋ mʋ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Bee biti ba tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Kanasareti‐nyɩn.”

english_verse: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
translation_verse: Fan waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa kɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa.

english_verse: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
translation_verse: yɛ, “Fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki tiri tɔ.”

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Nyɔɔma ifuu nɩɩ ba taa waa Gyɔn atɔ bunsa, nɩɩ kʋtɔbwaaya ɔwʋlɛ kɩrɩsʋ i ŋmina mʋ kasɩyɛ tɔ. Mʋ agyitɔ‐gyisa mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ ntuntunbi yɛ ŋmasan.

english_verse: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
translation_verse: Asa ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ yɛ Gyudeya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ yɛ Gyɔdan kabaafʋn kpini ba mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
translation_verse: Ba lɛɛ bamʋ alibi tɔwɛ, nɩɩ o gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
translation_verse: Gyɔn maa wu Farasii yɛ Sadusi abi gaalaagaa ba maa ba mʋ asɛ, kɛ o gyee bamʋ kekyugyee mʋ, ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ anansɛ banɩmʋ, anɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ waa fanɛ yɛ fan sɩlɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ ba asa sʋ mʋ tɔ?

english_verse: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
translation_verse: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla taa kpanfa yɩla iyu mʋ ilin tɔ o gyoo. Nɩɩ oyu kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi mʋ, bee biti ba ŋɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ kɛ ba taa waa fʋlɔn tɔ.

english_verse: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Ɔ sʋ mʋ kʋtɔ kyaalɛsa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, kɛ ɔ kyaalɛ ayubi mʋ lɛɛ afʋnfʋntɛɛ mʋ tɔ waa kikyunkun tɔ. Ee biti ɔ taa afʋnfʋntɛɛ mʋ waa fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ i mee dun mʋ tɔ.” Yesu Kekyugyee (Maki

english_verse: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ ba Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ Gyɔn asɛ, kɛ o gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
translation_verse: Amaa Gyɔn kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ Yesu nfɛɛrɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fɛɛ fʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fu gyee mɛ kekyugyee, nɩɩ fʋ ɩ ba mɛ asɛ yɛ n gyee fʋ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Yɛgɛ ɩ waa kanɩn nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkpa nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa a maa waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ biti mʋ kpini.” Nɩɩ Gyɔn kyula gyee Yesu Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
translation_verse: Gyɔn maa dan gyee Yesu Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee lʋwɛ mʋ, Yesu lɩɩ ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ sʋsʋ bugi tɔ, nɩɩ o wu Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ maa kpɩlɩgɛ fɛɛ kuwura‐lɔpɔ ba gyɔŋɔ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ obii‐bitisa, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ bɔla mi ansi.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Yesu maa nu yɛ ba kɩtaa Gyɔn tii obu mʋ, o bwii kpee Galile.

english_verse: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Nasareti kpee Kapanum kadɛ tɔ naa kyɩna. Kadɛ maŋa bʋ Galile ɔbʋn lala ɔkaa, Sebulun maa Nafitali kabaafʋn mʋ.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone without eating for
translation_verse: Maa waa noŋmina nkɛ adɩna kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, akʋn ɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ kan fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ, tɔwɛ abuu nɩmʋ kɛ i biliŋi agyitɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
translation_verse: Yesu bwii lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ba bɩla kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘Man tʋʋ fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔ kɩɩ.’ ”

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
translation_verse: Sɩtaanɛ bɩla taa mʋ kpee kɩbɩɩ lala kʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ mu kuwura‐gyii kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ yɛ ɩmʋ bʋnyaa mʋ.

english_verse: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ kan muŋa sun mɛ mʋ, n biti n taa ɩnɩmʋ kpini sa fʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Sɩtaanɛ, natɛ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘Muŋa sun fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ mʋ nkʋn nɩɩ fu sun.’ ”

english_verse: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
translation_verse: “Sebulun ɔsʋwʋlɛ yɛ Nafitali ɔsʋwʋlɛ, ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ kii kpee ɔpʋ asɛ, ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Gyɔdan benbe mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ ba bʋ Galile,

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ asawu mʋ, nɩɩ ba buu mʋ.

english_verse: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
translation_verse: Yesu maa kyɩna ɔ naa Galile ɔpʋ sʋ mʋ, o wu ɩkɩn akɩtapʋ anyɔ, Simon mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Pita yɛ mu‐supu Andiruwu yɛgɛ bɛɛ tʋʋ kɩsawu ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ bɛɛ kɩtaa ɩkɩn.

english_verse: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
translation_verse: Maa kyaga sʋ ee kpee mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa wu ɔkʋ maa mu‐supu, bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyemisi maa Gyɔn, Sebedi mu‐bii ana. Ba maa bamʋ‐sɛ Sebedi ba bʋ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ bɛɛ lɔŋɔ bamʋ asawu. Nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ bamʋ.

english_verse: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ ba yɛgɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ yɛ bamʋ‐sɛ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ. Yesu Kyɛ Alɔpʋ (Luka

english_verse: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu natɛ kyaabɔɔ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ asa asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ sʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ yɛgɛ ɛɛ kyɛ alɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

english_verse: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
translation_verse: Mʋ kɩtɩɩ ɩ yaasɛ loo Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini, nɩɩ asa ba taa alɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ alɔ yiri‐yiri bɩya mʋ. Akʋ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ ɩɩ kɔ bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ nbwɩɩ libi bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ, akʋ bɛɛ lɔ kigbingbin, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba gyɛ amʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ, nɩɩ Yesu kyɛ bamʋ kpini.

english_verse: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Asa damantɛ ba lɩɩ Galile yɛ Dekapoli yɛ Gyerusalem yɛ Gyudeya yɛ Gyɔdan benbe kpini ba buu mʋ.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu sakyɔ mʋ, o dii kpee kɩbɩɩ‐bi kʋ sʋ naa kyɩna kɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, nɩɩ fanɛ ɩkpa ɩ man kyiigi ɩ kyɔ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi mʋ, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan nyɛ loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ kakaakʋ.” Yesu Asɩn Kaapʋsa Lɩɩ Agbʋ Sʋ

english_verse: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ kan bɩya fʋ saraga kɛ fʋ ba lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kaswɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ fu nyiŋi fɛɛ fʋ waa fʋ bɩrɩsa alibi kʋ mʋ,

english_verse: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: “Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyɛ bamʋ lɛɛ.

english_verse: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
translation_verse: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɔɔta tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o yuula bamʋ nkɔlɔ.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
translation_verse: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩya bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ii biti ba gyii kasɛ mʋ fɛɛ bamʋ kapatɩyɛ.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
translation_verse: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee wu bamʋ bɩrɩsa ana kʋwɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o wu kʋwɛɛ.

english_verse: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
translation_verse: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ineesi kʋ man bʋ bamʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ii biti ba wu Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
translation_verse: “Fan du nɩn fɛɛ ɩŋmaŋɛsa sa asa dulinyaa tɔ. Kadɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan kɩbɩɩ sʋ mʋ mɛɛ taalɛ baala.

english_verse: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
translation_verse: Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, fan man bɔ ntan kpini‐kpini. Fan man taa sʋsʋ bɔ ntan, lɩɩ fɛɛ tʋtɔ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya.

english_verse: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
translation_verse: Fan man taa kasɛ bɔ ntan, lɩɩ fɛɛ tʋtɔ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ayaa ɔgyagakpa. Fan man taa Gyerusalem kadɛ bɔ ntan, lɩɩ fɛɛ kɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Owura kparɛ mʋ kadɛ lala.

english_verse: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
translation_verse: “Fan du nɩn fɛɛ nfɔlɛ sa asa dulinyaa tɔ. Amaa nɩɩ nfɔlɛ kan pan ɩmʋ ɔkɔn mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ bɩla taalɛ taa waa ɩmʋ kɛ ɩ waa ɔkɔn? Ɩ man bɩla ɩ sʋ kyaarɛ, amɔɔ ba taa kyɛɛgɛ fɔyɩ kɛ asa ba natɛ sʋ.

english_verse: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee tuwa fɩtɩla, kɛ ɔ taa waa kolobi tɔ. Amɔɔ ɔ taa gyaga fɩtɩla ɔgyagakpa kɛ kɩ baa kɩ kɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
translation_verse: “Fan man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n ba nɩn kɛ n ba lɛɛ Mosesi nbara mʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ lɩɩ tɔ. N man ba nɩn kɛ n ba lɛɛ ɩmʋ lɩɩ tɔ, amaa n ba nɩn kɛ bamʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

english_verse: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
translation_verse: Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan nu Mosesi nbara mʋ maa tɔwɛ adɩdaapʋ mʋ yɛ, ‘Man kan mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa mʋ, bee biti ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ.’

english_verse: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
translation_verse: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ agbʋ waa mʋ tɔmaa mʋ, bee biti ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ saalɛ mʋ tɔɔmaa yɛ ɔ man bʋ sɛhn tɔ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ asɩn Gyiwu kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Sanhediri mu ansi. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ saalɛ mʋ tɔɔmaa yɛ, ‘Fʋ omelensipu nɩmʋ’ mʋ, ii biti o loo awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ.

english_verse: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
translation_verse: yɛgɛ fʋ saraga yɩla tʋtɔ kɛ fu bwii kpee, kɛ faa fʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ fan lɔŋɔ abaa nsana, pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ bwii ba lɛɛ fʋ saraga mʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan samaa fʋ asɩn ogyikpa mʋ, sun mata mʋ pɔyɩ asɩn kigyii kakɛ mʋ kɩ fʋʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ɩ man kan ba kanɩn mʋ, ee biti ɔ taa fʋ sa asɩn ogyipu mʋ, kɛ mɔɔ mʋ ɔ taa fʋ waa abu tiipu ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ abaa tɔ kɛ o tii fʋ obu.

english_verse: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin fɛɛ, fii biti fʋ baa fʋ bʋ tʋtɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ‐ka fʋ kʋkɔ mʋ lʋwɛ. Kanyɩn Yɛ Kakyɩɩ Biti Kɩgyagaa Asɩn (Matiwu

english_verse: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ba bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mʋ‐ka mʋ, dagaa maa kyʋrɔɔ kekina ɔwʋlɛ sa mʋ.’

english_verse: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
translation_verse: “Fan bɩla nu Mosesi nbara mʋ maa tɔwɛ adɩdaapʋ mʋ yɛ, ‘Man bɔ ntan kɛ fʋ laaŋɛ, amaa ɩ dagaa fʋ maa waa kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bɔ ntan sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.’

english_verse: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
translation_verse: Amaa fan yɛgɛ fanɛ katɔwɛ kɩ baa kɩ gyɛ Ɛɛhn abɛɛ ayee. Nɩɩ fʋ kan bɔ ntan kʋ tii sʋ mʋ, ɩ lɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ asɛ nɩn. Fan Man Taa Alibi Ka Alibi Kʋkɔ (Luka

english_verse: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
translation_verse: Amaa mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan waa fʋ alibi mʋ, man taa alibi ka mʋ kʋkɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan kpa fʋ ɔtan gyisa sʋ mʋ, biliŋaa bɩna sʋ sa mʋ kɛ ɔ kpa tii sʋ.

english_verse: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taa fʋ kpee asɩn ogyikpa kɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ fʋ kaalɛ mʋ, taa fʋ balɩmaasʋ tii mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan nyan fʋ, fɛɛ fʋ sʋla mʋ ɩsʋla kpiya mʋ mayɩlɩ kʋlʋn mʋ, sʋla ɩmʋ kpiya mʋ ɩmayɩlɩ anyɔ.

english_verse: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba kʋlɛ fʋ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, sa mʋ. Abɛɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba yɛ fʋ paala mʋ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, sa mʋ. Nyɛ Kebiti Sa Fʋ‐dʋn Ana (Luka

english_verse: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
translation_verse: “Fan nu Mosesi nbara mʋ maa bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Fan nyɛ kebiti sa fanɛ tɔɔmaa ana, kɛ fan kisi fanɛ‐dʋn ana.’

english_verse: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
translation_verse: Amaa mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fan yɛ, fan nyɛ kebiti sa fanɛ‐dʋn ana, kɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ sa fanɛ anyanlapʋ,

english_verse: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
translation_verse: kɛ fan baa fan du fɛɛ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ. Mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ kyɔwɛ ɩɩ lɩɩ ɛɛ sa alibi awaapʋ yɛ adanbɩrasa awaapʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ yɛgɛ bʋlayɩ ɩɩ da ɛɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa ii kyiigi, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa ɩ man kyiigi.

english_verse: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ kebiti sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti fanɛ nkʋn mʋ, mɩnɛ kʋkɔka nɩɩ fen biti fan nyɛ? Man gyɛ kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ alibi awaapʋ gbaa bɛɛ waa nɩn abɛɛ?

english_verse: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
translation_verse: “Fan baa fan da sʋ kɛ fan man waa fanɛ adanbɩrasa asa ansi sʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ wu fanɛ. Nɩɩ fan kan waa kanɩn mʋ, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan nyɛ kʋkɔka lɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ fʋ kan kɛɛ atiripu afulee mʋ, man yɛgɛ fʋ‐bɩna kɩbaa ɔ bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fu gyisa kɩbaa mʋ waa mʋ,

english_verse: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ fɛn kan fɛn kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, fan man waa fɛɛ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ wuya ana mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bee biti kɩyɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa yɛ agbenbi nkpankyɛɛrɛ bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, kɛ asa ba nyɛ wu bamʋ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ba wʋla nyɛ bamʋ kʋkɔka lʋwɛ.

english_verse: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ fʋ kan fɩɩ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, loo fu obu to kɛ fu tii, kɛ fʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa fʋ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee wu mʋ mʋ. Saamʋ fʋ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa asiiri tɔ mʋ, ii biti ɔ ka fʋ kʋkɔ.

english_verse: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan kan fen kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, fan man tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ kasɛ fɛɛ ɩdakpa asunpu lɛɛ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ nɩn fɛɛ bamʋ kʋkʋlɛ bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ, ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ ɩ ba tɔ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man waa fɛɛ bamʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ‐Sɛ nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii tiri fanɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ fɛn kʋlɛ mʋ mʋ.

english_verse: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ fen biti fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, falɛ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fan maa tɔwɛ. ‘Anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ sʋsʋ, yɛgɛ asa ba bʋnyaa fʋ kɩtɩɩ.

english_verse: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
translation_verse: Yɛgɛ fu kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ ba, kɛ fʋ yɛgɛ fu kebiti kɩ waa kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ki du sʋsʋ mʋ.

english_verse: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ asa ba waa fanɛ alibi nɩɩ fan taa kɛɛ bamʋ mʋ, fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ gbaa ii biti ɔ taa kɛɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ fan kan fan man taa asa alibi kɛɛ bamʋ bɩrɛ, fanɛ‐Sɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa fanɛ alibi kɛɛ fanɛ. Noŋmina

english_verse: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
translation_verse: Saamʋ, asa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu fɛɛ fu ŋmina kɔnɔ, amɔɔ fʋ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee wu mʋ mʋ nkʋn. Fʋ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa asiri tɔ, ii biti ɔ ka fʋ kʋkɔ. Fan Taa Fanɛ Ansi Gyaga Sʋsʋ Atɔ Sʋ (Luka

english_verse: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
translation_verse: “Amaa fan gyankpaa kɩɩ biti Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ɩkpa yɛ mʋ ɩkpa kikyiigisa mʋ, kɛ ɔ taa atɔ nɩmʋ kpini tii sʋ sa fanɛ.

english_verse: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ fan man tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba ɔkɛ. Fan yɛgɛ ɔkɛ atɔ ɩ kɩɩ ɩmʋ gbaa‐gbaa ɩmʋ asɩn tirisa sʋ.

english_verse: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
translation_verse: “Fan man kʋʋla kapatɩyɛ yɩla kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ sa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, nfɩɩ nɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩtabɩrɩta yɛ nkandɩra ii nyita ɩmʋ, nɩɩ ayu bee loo bee yuuri ɩmʋ.

english_verse: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
translation_verse: Amaa fan kʋʋla kapatɩyɛ yɩla sʋsʋ sa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩtabɩrɩta yɛ nkadʋrɔ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ nyita ɩmʋ, nɩɩ ayu ba mɛɛ taalɛ loo naa yuuri ɩmʋ.

english_verse: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kapatɩyɛ bʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ gbaa ɩ bʋ.

english_verse: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ fu ansi ɩ kan ɩ man bʋrɔn bɩrɛ, fʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ kpini bʋ kitentenbiri tɔ nɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fɩtɩla mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ tɔ mʋ gyɛ kitentenbiri mʋ, ii biti ɩ waa kitentenbiri tumm.

english_verse: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
translation_verse: “Ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ sun anyɩrɩpɛ anyɔ. Ee biti o kisi ɔkʋ kɛ ɔ nyɛ kebiti sa ɔkʋ, abɛɛ ee biti ɔ nyɛ saŋa sa ɔkʋ kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ. Fan mɛɛ taalɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ maa afulee. Fan Man Yɛgɛ Asɩn Ɩ Tɔɔrɔɔ Fanɛ (Luka

english_verse: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛn taasɛ yɛ, ‘Mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a gyii?’ abɛɛ ‘Mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a nuu?’ abɛɛ ‘Mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a bun?’

english_verse: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ nkpa yɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan gyii, abɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fen nuu, abɛɛ fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ yɛ atɔ bunsa asɩn ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ. Nkpa ɩ man kyɔ agyitɔ abɛɛ? Nɩɩ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mɔɔ man kyɔ atɔ bunsa?

english_verse: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
translation_verse: Fan kɩɩ nbwiibi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ afuu tɔ mʋ, be mee duu nɩɩ ba mɛɛ tɩŋɛ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ kʋʋla agyitɔ bɛɛ gyaga agyan sʋ, amaa fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ɩɩ sa bamʋ agyitɔ. Fanɛ asɩn ɩ man tiri ɩ kyɔ bamʋ nɩn abɛɛ?

english_verse: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ atɔ bunsa asɩn ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ? Fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ iyu ntʋtʋlɛ ɩ dan mʋ. I mee sun kusun nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ lʋʋ atɔ bunsa ɩ sa ɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ.

english_verse: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
translation_verse: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, halɩɩ Owura Solomon maa mʋ atɔ bunsa danbɩrasa kpini mʋ, ɩ man bʋrɔn fʋʋ ntʋtʋlɛ mʋ.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ falɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ lɔŋɔ afitiri mʋ nɩɩ ndaga ɩ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔkɛ bɛɛ taa ɩmʋ funti waa fʋlɔn tɔ mʋ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ sa fanɛ atɔ bunsa kʋkyɔ? O, fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ man kyɔ?

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ be mee nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ falɛ atɔ kpini sʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi ɩ payɩ. Nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ nyi fɛɛ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ maa tiri fanɛ.

english_verse: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
translation_verse: “Fan man bun kʋpwɛ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ man bun fanɛ gbaa kʋpwɛ.

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fii wu keyuubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ bɩrɩsa kinsi tɔ, nɩɩ fu mee wu kagyakpoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ fu kinsi tɔ?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
translation_verse: Nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ fʋ bɩrɩsa yɛ yɛgɛ n lɛɛ keyuubi fu kinsi tɔ, yɛgɛ kagyakpoti dɛ ngbaa‐gbaa fu kinsi tɔ?

english_verse: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
translation_verse: Fʋ nnɔ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ wuya, gyankpaa lɛɛ kagyakpoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fu kinsi tɔ mʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ lan wu danbɩrasa, kɛ fʋ lɛɛ keyuubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ bɩrɩsa kinsi tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan gyɛ alibi awaapʋ mʋ gbaa fen nyi atɔ danbɩrasa kɩtaa sa fanɛ‐bii ana mʋ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ mɛɛ yɛ o kyula sa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ?

english_verse: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
translation_verse: “Fanɛ anɩmʋ nɩɩ mu‐bii ɩɩ kʋlɛ mʋ bodobodo kɛ ɔ taa kubuu sa mʋ?

english_verse: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: “Fan kʋlɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, ee biti ɔ sa fanɛ. Fan kɩɩ biti Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, fen biti fan wu. Fan da mʋ kebunɔ, ee biti o tigi sa fanɛ.

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ ɛɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, ɛɛ nyɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ ee biti mʋ, ee wu, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ da kebunɔ mʋ, ee tigi ɛɛ sa mʋ.

english_verse: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: Abɛɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ ɔkɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ taa kɩnansɛ ɛɛ sa mʋ?

english_verse: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti asa ba waa sa fanɛ mʋ, fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan baa fɛn waa kanɩn fɛn sa bamʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ. Kebunɔ Bɔntɛsa Yɛ Kyɛlɛgɛsa (Luka

english_verse: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee nu mɛ asɩn nɩmʋ, nɩɩ ee buu ɩmʋ mʋ, du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyaasɩnpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ mʋ kɩkpaara gyaga kɩfʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ kii kpee nkpa tɔ mʋ bɩrɛ kɩ bɔntɛ, nɩɩ kɩmʋ kebunɔ gbaa man yɛlɩgɛ, nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee wu kɩmʋ bɛɛ bɔla mʋ ba man kyɔ. Oyu Kʋmaa Kɩmaa Kɩmʋ Abi (Luka

english_verse: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
translation_verse: “Fan baa fan da atɩŋɛsa atɔwɛpʋ sʋ. Ansi sʋ mʋ be du bɔhn fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ, amaa bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, be du nɩn fɛɛ agboogboo mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ agbʋ.

english_verse: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Bamʋ atɔ waasa nɩɩ fen biti fan taa bɩɩ bamʋ. Kididobi oyu sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩyɛ kimango abi abɛɛ? Abɛɛ kɩkpalɛ oyu sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩyɛ akutu?

english_verse: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
translation_verse: Kanɩn nɩɩ oyu danbɩrasa ɩɩ sɔyɩ abi danbɩrasa, nɩɩ oyu libi mɔɔ ɩɩ sɔyɩ abi libi.

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: Oyu danbɩrasa mɛɛ taalɛ sɔyɩ abi libi, nɩɩ oyu libi mɛɛ taalɛ sɔyɩ abi danbɩrasa.

english_verse: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
translation_verse: Oyu kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi danbɩrasa mʋ, bɛɛ ŋɛ kɩmʋ nɩn tʋʋ, kɛ ba taa waa fʋlɔn tɔ.

english_verse: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ fen biti fan bɩɩ antɩŋɛsa atɔwɛpʋ mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ awaasa sʋ. N Man Nyi Fanɛ (Luka

english_verse: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee nu mɛ asɩn nɩmʋ, nɩɩ o mee buu ɩmʋ mʋ, du nɩn fɛɛ omelensipu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ mu obu gyaga kusipu sʋ.

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
translation_verse: Bʋlayɩ da nɩɩ ɩbʋn ɩ bɔla, nɩɩ afuu lala ɩ da nɩɩ obu mʋ lɩɩ tɩyɛ ɔlʋn tɔ birimm.”

english_verse: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
translation_verse: “Man gyɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ’ mʋ, ii biti o loo sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ, amɔɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ kebiti mʋ nkʋn.

english_verse: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛ talɛsa maŋa kan fʋʋ mʋ, asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, an bɔla fʋ kɩtɩɩ sʋ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn sa asa, nɩɩ lɩɩ fʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ an gya nbwɩɩ libi, nɩɩ an waa asɩn kyinkyinsa gaalaagaa.’

english_verse: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
translation_verse: Amaa n biti tɔwɛ tigi bamʋ yɛ, ‘Man nyi fanɛ. Fanɛ alibi awaapʋ banɩmʋ, fan lɩɩ mɛ sʋ natɛ.’ Ɔnyaasɩnpʋ Yɛ Omelensipu Ibu Kɩpɔyɩ (Luka

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
translation_verse: Bʋlayɩ da nɩɩ ɩbʋn ɩ bɔla, nɩɩ afuu lala ɩ da obu mʋ, amaa kɩ man tɩyɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba pɔyɩ kɩmʋ gyaga kɩfʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
translation_verse: Yesu maa tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ i kyinkyin sakyɔ mʋ gaa.

english_verse: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ tɔ sʋ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ɩ man du fɛɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
translation_verse: Amaa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ fan gyɛ kuwura‐gyii abi mʋ, bee biti ba taa fanɛ funti kewu tɔ kitentenbiri tɔ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ kusu yɛ anobi keduŋi bʋ.”

english_verse: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ tɩyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ asa ba taa alɔpʋ kʋkyɔ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ bamʋ sʋ mʋ bɩya Yesu. Nɩɩ ɔ sa nbwɩɩ libi mʋ kɔnɔ gya bamʋ kʋsaa alɔpʋ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ alɔpʋ mʋ kpini.

english_verse: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɔ kibootoo ba muŋa mu amulii sʋ mu ansi tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ nɩɩ ɩ pɩrɛ fʋ mʋ, fɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa mata ɔnyɩn mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ pɩrɛ mɛ, fʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.” Opula tɔ nɩɩ mʋ kibootoo mʋ kɩ lʋwɛ.

english_verse: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “N biti n naa kyɛ mʋ kʋlɔ.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
translation_verse: Yesu maa nu kanɩn mʋ, i kyinkyin mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ mʋ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini tɔ mʋ, n man pɩɩta wu ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ falɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii damantɛ nɩmʋ kedubi.

english_verse: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
translation_verse: Ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ dɛ mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ ɛɛ lɔ mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ, ɩɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa.”

english_verse: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba lɩɩ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ yɛ kyɔwɛ kɩtɩyɛ, kɛ ba maa Abraham maa Isaki yɛ Gyekobu ba ba bʋga kyɩna bamʋ ɩkyɩnakpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kuwura‐gyii kensi‐gyii ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, “Kpewu! Ii biti ɩ waa sa fʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ.” Nɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩɩ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya. Yesu Kyɛ Alɔpʋ Kʋkyɔ (Maki

english_verse: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
translation_verse: Ba kuusi yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti anɛ asɛ? Fʋ ba nɩn yɛ fʋ ba waa anɛ awʋrʋfɔ pɔyɩ kɛ ɩmʋ saŋa mʋ ɩ fʋʋ, abɛɛ?”

english_verse: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ asa kpini ba lɩɩ ba naa gyaŋa Yesu. Ba maa wu mʋ mʋ, ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Yesu maa ba Pita kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, o wu fɛɛ Pita mʋ‐saa kyɩɩsa dɛ ɛɛ lɔ kibirima.

english_verse: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
translation_verse: Ɔ kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ nɩɩ kibirima mʋ kɩ yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Ɔ kɔɔlɛ anɛ alɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa anɛ alobi sʋla.” Bamʋ Nɩɩ Bee Biti Ba Buu Yesu (Luka

english_verse: When Jesus saw the crowd, he went across Lake Galilee.
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu asa damantɛ ba maa kyaabɔɔ mʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mu asunpu mʋ yɛ ba faa kpee benbe.

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ kʋ ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ n biti n buu fʋ kpee katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii kpee mʋ.”

english_verse: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
translation_verse: Mʋ ɔbɩɩlapʋ kʋ gbaa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, yɛgɛ n naa pula mɛ‐sɛ pɔyɩ.”

english_verse: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
translation_verse: Abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba naa kyiŋi mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, mɔlɩgɛ anɛ! An biti a muuri!”

english_verse: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba buu mʋ loo.

english_verse: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
translation_verse: Ba maa gyan ɔbʋn lala mʋ sʋ bee kpee mʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ afuu lala i tigi yɛgɛ ɩɩ da apaa ii loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ. Amaa yɛgɛ Yesu dɛ nsɩɩrɛ.

english_verse: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin asa mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nyɩmɩsa ndee? Halɩɩ afuu yɛ apaa gbaa ii nu mʋ asɛ.” Yesu Kyɛ Anyɩn Mʋ Nɩɩ Nbwɩɩ Libi Ba Tɛ Bamʋ Sʋ Mʋ (Maki

english_verse: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
translation_verse: Yesu maa fʋʋ ɔbʋn mʋ benbe mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Gadara ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ, anyɩn anyɔ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ bamʋ sʋ mʋ ba lɩɩ nkyan tɔ ba gyaŋa mʋ. Ba kyɩna ba libi gaa fɛɛ ndʋnbwɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ bɔla kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ.

english_verse: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Akuuri damantɛ kʋ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ kɩbaafʋn bee gyii.

english_verse: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ kan fii biti fʋ gya anɛ bɩrɛ, yɛgɛ a naa loo akuuri damantɛ nɩmʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
translation_verse: Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan natɛ!” Nɩɩ ba lɩɩ anyɩn mʋ tɔ naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ akuuri damantɛ mʋ kpini ba sɩlɛ kpɩlɩgɛ kidenbi mʋ naa tɩyɛ ɔbʋn lala mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba wu.

english_verse: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ akuuri mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba sɩlɛ kpee kadɛ tɔ, naa tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa kpini yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa anyɩn anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ bamʋ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
translation_verse: Yesu dii loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ, faa ɔbʋn mʋ ba mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
translation_verse: Yesu maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ Farasii abi mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Man gyɛ alanfɩya wuya nɩɩ ɔkyɛpʋ tiri sa, amɔɔ ɔlɔpʋ nkʋn.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
translation_verse: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taa nta pʋpwɛ waa kʋtɔbwaaya ɔwʋlɛ taakpe dɩdaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa kanɩn, nɩɩ nta mʋ ɩɩ bɩlɛ mʋ, ii biti ɩ kyatɛ taakpe dɩdaa mʋ nyita, kɛ nta mʋ ɩ kyɛɛgɛ fɔyɩ. Amaa bɛɛ taa nta pʋpwɛ waa taakpe pʋpwɛ tɔ, kɛ ɩmʋ anyɔ mʋ ɩ man nyita.” Yesu Kyiŋi Kebii Kʋ Nɩɩ Ɔ Kyɛ Ɔkyɩɩ Mʋ Nɩɩ Nkalan ɩɩ Da Mʋ Mʋ (Maki

english_verse: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
translation_verse: Nɩɩ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ yaasɛ kpee kanɩn ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini. Yesu Kyɛ Agyaatanapʋ Anyɔ

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ akʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa asɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn ee yii Ɩbwaarɛ!”

english_verse: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
translation_verse: Yesu maa bɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nfɛɛrɛ libi nɩɩ fɛn fɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ kanɩn?

english_verse: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: Amaa n biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ, mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n sʋ ɔlʋn kasɛ sʋ n taa alibi kɛɛ.” Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ wuya mʋ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ, taa fʋ kalan kɛ fu kpewu.”

english_verse: The man got up and went home.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ kʋsʋ nɩɩ o kpewu.

english_verse: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
translation_verse: Fan naa kɩɩ biti Asɩnkyan yɩlasa kasɛ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ‘Abaa kʋwɛɛ kuwu nɩɩ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ n biti, man gyɛ atɔbwaaya kɩtaa lɛɛ saraga sa mɛ.’ ” Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ yɛ, “N man ba nɩn kɛ n ba tɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, amɔɔ alibi awaapʋ.” Ba Taasɛ Yesu Noŋmina Asɩn (Maki

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ ee kpee mʋ, nɩɩ agyaatanapʋ anyɔ akʋ ba buu mʋ, bee kuusi bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ!”

english_verse: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
translation_verse: Agyaatanapʋ anyɔ mʋ ba maa kpee mʋ, asa akʋ ba bɩya ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ.

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
translation_verse: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ ee kpee mʋ, o wu lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Matiwu maa tɛ lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛkpa. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Buu mɛ,” nɩɩ Matiwu kʋsʋ buu mʋ.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba kpee Matiwu kɩkpaara sʋ bee gyii mʋ, lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ gaalaagaa yɛ alibi awaapʋ ba ba, nɩɩ ba maa bamʋ kpini ba bʋga gyii.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus' disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and other sinners?”
translation_verse: Farasii abi ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, ba taasɛ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkaapʋpʋ maa lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ bɛɛ bʋga bee gyii?”

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
translation_verse: Yesu maa san ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa kʋ ba Yesu asɛ, ba muŋa mu ansi tɔ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mi‐bii‐kyɩɩsa dan wu nɩmʋ. Amaa dan waa aniya ba taa fʋ kɩbaa mata mʋ, ee biti o kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi nɩɩ o wu ɔkyɩɩ mʋ, tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mi‐bii‐kyɩɩsa, yɛgɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ dɛ fʋ. Fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.” Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya opula maŋa tɔ.

english_verse: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
translation_verse: Yesu maa dan gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ waa asa damantɛ mʋ kɩyan, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “A man pɩɩ wu ɩnɩmʋ kudubi Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ ba ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ anɛ yɛ Farasii abi mʋ a ŋmina kɔnɔ, nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɩrɛ be mee ŋmina kɔnɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ afɔ bee biti ba bɔɔta tɔ, yɛgɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ bʋ bamʋ asɛ? Ɩ mɛɛ ba kanɩn. Amaa saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba nɩɩ bee biti ba taa ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ. Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ bee biti ba ŋmina kɔnɔ.”

english_verse: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔkʋ mɛɛ tɩn kikyibi pʋpwɛ taa baata tii kaalɛ dɩdaa ɔkyatɛkpa ɔbɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa kanɩn mʋ kikyibi pʋpwɛ mʋ kii biti kɩ kyatɛ lɩɩ, kɛ ɔkyatɛkpa mʋ kɩɩ yɛlɩgɛ tii sʋ.

english_verse: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ fɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ yɛ, “Nɩɩ n dan nyɛ mata mʋ kaalɛ gbaa, n biti n nyɛ alanfɩya.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu kʋsʋ buu mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ gbaa ba buu mʋ kpee.

english_verse: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
translation_verse: Ba maa kpee mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nkalan ɩɩ da mʋ nsu kudu anyɔ mʋ, ba bɔla Yesu kamaa nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa mata Yesu kaalɛ anobi.

english_verse: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu naa loo ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ o wu nleebi afʋlɛpʋ keli mʋ tɔ yɛ asa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ bee su mʋ,

english_verse: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
translation_verse: ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ lɩɩ nfɩɩ, kebii mʋ man wu nɩn, ɔ dɛ nsɩɩrɛ nɩn.” Amaa ba ŋmasɛ Yesu.

english_verse: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
translation_verse: Ba maa gya asa damantɛ mʋ lɩɩ kamaa tɔ mʋ, Yesu loo kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ kyɩna.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
translation_verse: Yesu maa naa loo katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ mʋ, nɩɩ agyaatanapʋ anyɔ mʋ ba kpee mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ n taalɛ kyɛ fanɛ?” Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɛɛhn, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, an kɔɔlɛ gyii.”

english_verse: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ mata bamʋ ansi tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩ waa sa fanɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ki du mʋ.”

english_verse: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i bugi. Nɩɩ Yesu da bamʋ amu tɔ kenken yɛ, “Fan man tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ sa ɔkʋ.”

english_verse: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
translation_verse: Amaa ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba yaasɛ asɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ Yesu sʋ kanɩn ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kabaafʋn kpini.

english_verse: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
translation_verse: Amaa Farasii abi mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nbwɩɩ libi ɔbɩlɩsa ɔlʋn nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi falɛ.” Kusun Awaapʋ Mʋ Ba Gyɛ Gbɛrɛɛ

english_verse: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu natɛ ndɛ lala yɛ ndɛ‐bii tɔ kpini kyaabɔɔ, yɛgɛ ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ kyɛ alɔ yiri‐yiri kʋmaa nɩmʋ.

english_verse: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu sakyɔ mʋ, bamʋ asɩn ɩ waa mʋ kʋwɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bɛɛ yɩyɛɛ nɩɩ ba man nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba waa. Be du nɩn fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba man sʋ ɔkpapʋ.

english_verse: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Kesintin tɔ mʋ, ayu kɩtɩŋɛ mʋ kyɔ, amaa kusun awaapʋ mʋ ba du gbɛrɛɛ falɛ.

english_verse: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kʋlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, kadɔɔ wuya mʋ kɛ o sun kusun awaapʋ kpee mʋ ayu kɩtɩŋɛ mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
translation_verse: yɛ Filipu maa Batolome, yɛ Tomasi maa Matiwu, lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ, yɛ Gyemisi Alifeyo mu‐bii, yɛ Tadiyosi,

english_verse: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kan kpiya fanɛ asɩn ogyikpa mʋ, fan man yɩyɛɛ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan tɔwɛ, abɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ mʋ sʋ. Saŋa mʋ kan fʋʋ mʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ bamʋ.

english_verse: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
translation_verse: Amaa fan kpee Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ fɔyɩsa mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
translation_verse: Fan kɩɩ! N sun fanɛ nɩn fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ n kpee akuntun tɔ. Fan baa fan sʋ kanyaasɩn fɛɛ anansɛ, yɛgɛ fan du bwahn fɛɛ awura‐lɔpɔ. Asa Bee Biti Ba Nyanla Abɩɩlapʋ Mʋ (Maki

english_verse: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
translation_verse: yɛ Simon mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Seloti mʋ, yɛ Gyudasi Isikariyoti, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba lɛɛ Yesu sa mʋ. Yesu Sun Abɩɩlapʋ Kudu Anyɔ Mʋ (Maki

english_verse: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu sun abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan man kpee bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ kɩbaafʋn abɛɛ Samariya abi kadɛ kʋ tɔ.

english_verse: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
translation_verse: nɩɩ fan man taa taakpe abɛɛ kaalɛ nyɔsapʋ, abɛɛ asɩbɩta, abɛɛ kikpebi, kpee ɔkpa maŋa. Osunpu dagaa fɛɛ ba sa mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki tiri mʋ.

english_verse: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
translation_verse: “Kadɛ lala abɛɛ kadɛ‐bi kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan loo kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, fan biti ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, kɛ fan kyɩna mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o be gyii asa asɩn mʋ, Sodom maa Gomora abi kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ gbaa ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bɔ bamʋ lɛɛ.

english_verse: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
translation_verse: “Kanɩn nkɛ maa tɔ mʋ, nyɩmɩsa ii biti o gyii mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mu‐supu kadɛ‐boyi, kɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ, kɛ ɔsɛ gbaa ɔ waa obii kanɩn. Nbii bee biti ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ akʋʋgɛpʋ ana sʋ, kɛ ba yɛgɛ kɛ ba mɔɔ bamʋ.

english_verse: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
translation_verse: “Fan baa fan da sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa kʋ bee biti ba kɩtaa fanɛ kpee asɩn ogyikpa, kɛ ba tɩŋɛ fanɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa.

english_verse: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, bee biti ba kpiya fanɛ awura yɛ alʋlʋnpʋ ansi tɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔkpa tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ sa bamʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ.

english_verse: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa ii biti fan tɔwɛ, amaa fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ii biti ɔ bɔla fanɛ sʋ tɔwɛ.

english_verse: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
translation_verse: Asa kpini bee biti ba kisi fanɛ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ kenken naa fʋʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

english_verse: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kan nyanla fanɛ kadɛ kʋ tɔ mʋ, fan sɩlɛ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee kadɛ kʋ tɔ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan sɩlɛ kanɩn kyaabɔɔ Ɩsɩrayɩ ndɛ mʋ kpini sʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n ba.

english_verse: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
translation_verse: “Ɔbɩɩlapʋ man kyɔ mʋ ɔkaapʋpʋ, nɩɩ kɩnyɛ man kyɔ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ.

english_verse: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
translation_verse: Ɩ dagaa ɩ maa kʋkwɛ ɔbɩɩlapʋ fɛɛ maa ba waa fɛɛ mʋ ɔkaapʋpʋ. Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mɔɔ ɔ ba waa fɛɛ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ. Nɩɩ ba kan bɛɛ tɩn asɩn bɛɛ gyaga mɛ, kɩkpaara sʋ ɔbɩlɩsa sʋ yɛ n gyɛ Beelisebu mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ bee biti ba taa atɩɩ libi gyaga fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan gyɛ mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ abi mʋ ɩ kyɔ mɛ lɛɛ mʋ. Fan Man Sɩlɛ Ɔkʋ Amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ (Luka

english_verse: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
translation_verse: Fan man sɩlɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ mɔɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ nkʋn, yɛgɛ ba mɛɛ taalɛ mɔɔ ɔkala. Amaa fan sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ nyita ɔkala yɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ, Sɩtaanɛ fʋlɔn tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
translation_verse: Man gyɛ kobo kʋlʋn nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ sɔɔ alangba anyɔ, amaa bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn mɛɛ yɛ ɔ tɩyɛ kasɛ sʋ wu, yɛgɛ fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man sa ɔkpa.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
translation_verse: Halɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kumu tɔ inyini gbaa, Ɩbwaarɛ nyi ɩmʋ kɔnɔ.

english_verse: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man waa kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan sʋ kyaarɛ fan kyɔ alangba damantɛ.

english_verse: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
translation_verse: “Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asa tɔ yɛ n gyɛ mʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n biti n lɛɛ mʋ kaapʋ mɛ‐Sɛ ansi tɔ sʋsʋ.

english_verse: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mɛ asa tɔ yɛ n man gyɛ mʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ, ngbaa mɛ n biti, n kina mʋ mɛ‐sɛ ansi tɔ sʋsʋ. Nbarɩgɛ Tɔ Asɩn (Luka

english_verse: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
translation_verse: “Fan man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n ba nɩn kɛ ɩ bɩya keyuula dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ. Man gyɛ keyuula nɩɩ n bɩya, amaa keluu nɩɩ n bɩya.

english_verse: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n ba nɩn, ‘kɛ nbii‐nyɩnsa ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ‐sɛ ana sʋ, kɛ nbii‐kyɩɩsa gbaa ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ‐nyi ana sʋ, kɛ asaa‐kyɩɩsa ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ‐saa ana sʋ,

english_verse: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
translation_verse: Kɛ nyɩmɩsa mʋ dʋn ana ba baa ba gyɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ asa.’

english_verse: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
translation_verse: “Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ‐sɛ abɛɛ mu‐nyi ɔ kyɔ mɛ mʋ, ɔ man dagaa maa waa mɛ obuupu. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa abɛɛ ɔkyɩɩsa ɔ kyɔ mɛ mʋ, man dagaa maa waa mɛ obuupu.

english_verse: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man taa mu oyu laasa sʋla buu mɛ mʋ, man dagaa maa waa mɛ obuupu.

english_verse: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ nkpa mʋ kɩbaa sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ pan ɩmʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ ɩmʋ. Kʋkɔka Mʋ Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Ii Biti Ɔ Sa (Maki

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ lɩɩ maa gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kʋkɔka mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi lɩɩ maa gyɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kʋkɔka mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ.

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
translation_verse: “Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, kɔɔlɛ mɛ nɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ mʋ, kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ nɩmʋ.

english_verse: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa mɛ abɩɩlapʋ nɩmʋ tɔ ɔkʋ nkyu yuulisa kawɛ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ maa gyɛ mɛ ɔbɩɩlapʋ sʋ mʋ, n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ɔ man yɛ ɔ pan mʋ kʋkɔka kpaa.”

english_verse: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Gyɔn Okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa bwii bee kpee mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ Gyɔn n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn ɛɛ sa sakyɔ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fan kpee Gyɔn asɛ kiperi mʋ sʋ fanaa kɩɩ? Fan kpee naa kɩɩ kifitiri mʋ nɩɩ afuu ɩɩ da kɩmʋ ii biliŋi tɔ nɩn?

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyɔn maa ba mʋ, o mee gyii nɩɩ o mee nuu, nɩɩ ba yɛ, ‘Nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ Gyɔn sʋ.’

english_verse: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
translation_verse: Yesu maa kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ asɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee ɔ naa kaapʋ kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ san Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
translation_verse: Saŋa maa tɔ mʋ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu mʋ bʋ obu kitii tɔ. Maa nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Krisito ɩɩ waa mʋ, o sun mʋ abɩɩlapʋ,

english_verse: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: ba naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ ba mʋ, abɛɛ a kɩɩ ɔpʋpwɛ ɔkpa?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fen bwii naa tɔwɛ Gyɔn asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan nu yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan wu,

english_verse: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: fɛɛ agyaatanapʋ ansi i bugi, nɩɩ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana ba kʋsʋ natɛ, nɩɩ abootipu n‐yɩɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ, nɩɩ atikpiri asʋ a tigi, nɩɩ alewupu ba kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Asɩn Danbɩrasa mʋ sa atiripu.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man yɛgɛ mɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii.”

english_verse: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen kpee naa kɩɩ? Nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ o bun ataa danbɩrasa mʋ, nɩɩ fan naa kɩɩ? Ayee, asa mʋ nɩɩ bee bun ataa danbɩrasa mʋ, ba tɛ iwurakpa nɩn.

english_verse: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mɩnɛ gbaa nɩɩ fan kpee naa kɩɩ? Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ abɛɛ? Ɛɛhn, n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ ɔ kyɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

english_verse: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, nyɩmɩsa kʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɔ Gyɔn okyugyeepu mʋ. Ɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ pii sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ kyɔ mʋ.

english_verse: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Gyɔn okyugyeepu saŋa ba lɩɩ ndaga mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi ɩ payɩ yɛ bee biti ba loo sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba sʋ ɔlʋn yɛ kensi payɩ bee loo kɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan kan fen biti fan kɔɔlɛ kyula mʋ, Gyɔn ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ ee biti ɔ ba mʋ.

english_verse: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
translation_verse: “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ n taalɛ taa kaala ndaga asa? Ba du nɩn fɛɛ nbii ba maa tɛ agya tɔ bɛɛ tɩɩ abaa bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
translation_verse: ‘An fʋlɛ nleebi sa fanɛ, amaa fan man kyaa, nɩɩ an bɩɩ alengyi ɩlʋn, amaa fan man su.’

english_verse: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
translation_verse: Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n maa ba mʋ, n gyii nɩɩ n nuu, nɩɩ ba yɛ, ‘Ee gyii gaa nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔtabʋʋpʋ yɛ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ bamʋ kyɛmɩnɛ.’ Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan o buu Ɩbwaarɛ kanyaasɩn ɩkpa sʋ mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ kanyaasɩn mʋ gyɛ kesintin.” Ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ, bamʋ laakʋ (Luka

english_verse: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa mʋ asɩn kyinkyinsa gaalaagaa mʋ, ba man yɛgɛ bamʋ alibi kɩwaa, kanɩn sʋ Yesu tɔwɛ yii bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fanɛ Kapanum abi, fen biti fɛɛ ba kʋsaa fanɛ kpee sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee, bee biti ba tʋʋ fanɛ waa awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ n waa fanɛ kadɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ daa ɩ gyɛ Sodom nɩɩ n daa waa ɩmʋ nɩn, naafɔɔ kadɛ mʋ san kɩ bʋ tɔ ba lɩɩ ndaga.

english_verse: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
translation_verse: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Sodom abi kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bɔ fanɛ lɛɛ kakɛ talɛsa mʋ tɔ.” Fan ba nyɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ Yesu asɛ (Luka

english_verse: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ‐Sɛ, fʋ gyɛ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n yɩn fʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ taa falɛ asɩn baala anyaasɩnpʋ yɛ anyipu sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kaapʋ nbii wuribi.

english_verse: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Ɩ gyɛ kanɩn nɩn, nɩɩ kanɩn ɩ gyɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti.

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: “Mɛ‐Sɛ taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ waa mɛ abaa tɔ. Ɔkʋ man nyi obii mʋ amɔɔ Ɔsɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man nyi Ɔsɛ mʋ amɔɔ Obii mʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Obii mʋ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ kaapʋ bamʋ mʋ.

english_verse: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ nsʋla budusa nɩɩ fan kpɩnɛ mʋ, fan ba mɛ asɛ, kɛ n sa fanɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ.

english_verse: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
translation_verse: Fan kyula kɛ fan buu mɛ, kɛ fan kasɛ mɛ tɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ sa fanɛ kala ana, lɩɩ fɛɛ n du bwahn nɩɩ n bɩya mɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ lɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ.

english_verse: This yoke is easy to bear, and this burden is light.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan kan fan buu mɛ mʋ, ɩ man bʋlʋn nɩɩ mɛ kasʋla gbaa man budu.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
translation_verse: Farasii abi mʋ ba maa wu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Kɩɩ! Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɛɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ.”

english_verse: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ii kyinkyin asa mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Dawidi kanaana‐bii mʋ do?”

english_verse: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu nɩɩ n sʋ n gya nbwɩɩ libi n kʋsaa asa sʋ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fan bɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ wʋla kɩ bʋ fanɛ asɛ.”

english_verse: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Abɛɛ fan man pɩɩta kalɛ Mosesi nbara mʋ tɔ wu fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu ba maa sun kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, bee nyita nbara, amaa ɔkʋ man bun bamʋ kʋpwɛ?

english_verse: He went into the house of God, and then they ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are supposed to eat.
translation_verse: O loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ naa taa bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, nɩɩ maa mʋ asa mʋ ba wɛ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara tɔ mʋ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ Dawidi maa bamʋ ba waa kanɩn, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu nkʋn.

english_verse: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘Man gyɛ atɔbwaaya kɩtaa lɛɛ saraga sa mɛ nɩɩ n biti, amaa kuwu abaa kʋwɛɛ nɩɩ n biti.’ Nɩɩ fan kan nu asɩn nɩmʋ kasɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ fen mee bun asa mʋ nɩɩ ba man waa alibi mʋ kʋpwɛ.

english_verse: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
translation_verse: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ ee kpee mʋ, ɔ naa loo bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa.

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, “Tɛɛŋɛ fʋ kɩbaa mʋ.” Nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛŋɛ kɩmʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ kɩ nyɛ ɔlʋn fɛɛ kɩbanban mʋ.

english_verse: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
translation_verse: Yesu maa bɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ mʋ ɔ lɩɩ kanɩn opula maŋa, nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba buu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ mʋ alɔ kpini.

english_verse: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔ da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ ba man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa nɩn kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ,

english_verse: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
translation_verse: “Kɩɩ! Mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n lɛɛ mʋ ndee, mʋ, nɩɩ n sʋ kebiti sa nɩɩ ɔ bɔla mi ansi. N biti n taa mɛ Kayaayu waa mʋ tɔ, kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ asɩn kyiigisa sa ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini.

english_verse: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
translation_verse: “Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man tii mɛ sʋ mʋ, ii kisi mɛ nɩn, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ kʋʋla ɛɛ kyɛ mɛ tɔ mʋ, ɛɛ yaasɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ n kʋʋla mʋ nɩn.
