english_verse: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ asa akʋ ba taa ɔnyɩn kʋ bɩya Yesu. Ɔ gyɛ gyaatanapʋ yɛ naamuu lɩɩ fɛɛ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mʋ sʋ. Yesu kyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ mu ansi i bugi nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
translation_verse: Yesu bɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Nɩɩ Kuwura‐gyii kʋmaa tɔ asa ba kan barɩgɛ tɔ bɛɛ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kɩ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩɩ kɩ tɩyɛ. Nɩɩ kadɛ abɛɛ kɩkpaara sʋ abi ba kan barɩgɛ tɔ bɛɛ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kadɛ abɛɛ kɩkpaara maŋa kii biti ki boyi.

english_verse: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Fan yɛ Beelisebu ɩɩ sa mɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ n sʋ n gya nbwɩɩ libi n kʋsaa asa sʋ. To, kanɩn bɩrɛ anɩmʋ ɩɩ sa fanɛ abɩɩlapʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ bɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi bɛɛ kʋsaa asa sʋ? Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ abɩɩlapʋ ii biti ba bun fanɛ kʋpwɛ fɛɛ fan man sʋ kesintin.

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when the demon doesn't find a place,

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and the person ends up in worse shape than before. That's how it will be with you evil people of today. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Mark
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɩɩ kpee kɛ ɔ naa taa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana n‐yaayu libi asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba libi ba kyɔ mʋ tii mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, kɛ ba kpee naa kyɩna tʋtɔ. Saamʋ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa o du mʋ, ii biti ɩ baa ɩ kyɔ kanɩn. Kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ baa i du sa ndaga kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ asa libi.” Yesu mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana (Maki

english_verse: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ fʋ kan kyula yɛ oyu kʋ abi ɩ gyɛ abi danbɩrasa mʋ, oyu maa gbaa gyɛ oyu danbɩrasa nɩn, amaa nɩɩ fʋ kan kina yɛ oyu kʋ abi man gyɛ abi danbɩrasa bɩrɛ, oyu maa gbaa man gyɛ oyu danbɩrasa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ oyu abi sɔyɩsa nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ bɩɩ kɩmʋ.

english_verse: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
translation_verse: Fanɛ asa libi fɛɛ anansɛ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fɛn taalɛ tɔwɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn, yɛgɛ fanɛ atɔ waasa gyɛ alibi? Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, kɩmʋ ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ.

english_verse: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
translation_verse: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, katɔwɛ gyaga kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔsa kʋmaa nɩmʋ tɔwɛ mʋ, ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kɩmʋ kɔnɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ, kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ talɛ asa mʋ.

english_verse: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ ntɔwɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɩlɛ sʋ talɛ fʋ fɛɛ fu gyii asʋ abɛɛ fu gyii kʋpwɛ.” Gyiwu abi bee biti asɩnkaala lɩɩ Yesu asɛ (Maki

english_verse: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ akʋ yɛ Farasii abi akʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ an biti fɛɛ fʋ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa kaapʋ anɛ.”

english_verse: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ alibi awaapʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be mee sun Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ kɩɩ bee biti asɩn kyinkyinsa. Amaa baa mɛɛ nyɛ ɩmʋ, amɔɔ sɩnkaala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ ba Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyona sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ n biti n waa kaapʋ bamʋ.

english_verse: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Gyona gyii nkɛ nsa ibiri ɔkɩn lala ɔtɔ tɔ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ gbaa n biti n gyii nkɛ nsa ibiri kasɛ tɔ.

english_verse: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
translation_verse: Kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ talɛ asa kpini mʋ, Ninewe abi bee biti ba kʋsʋ, kɛ ba bun ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa kʋpwɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyona naa tɔwɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Amaa mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n kyɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyona mʋ n bʋ nfɩɩ.

english_verse: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ talɛ asa kpini mʋ, Seeba Owura‐kyɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ kata‐kata mʋ, ii biti ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɛ o bun ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa kʋpwɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɩɩ koo kata‐kata ba nu Owura Solomon kanyaasɩn kɩkaapʋ mʋ. Amaa mɛ mʋ nɩɩ n kyɔ Solomon mʋ n bʋ nfɩɩ.”

english_verse: it says, “I will go back to the home I left.” When it gets there and finds the place empty, clean, and neat,
translation_verse: kɩ tɔwɛ sa kɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, ‘N biti n bwii kpee mɛ kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ mʋ sʋ.’ Nɩɩ ɔ kan ba fʋʋ nɩɩ o wu fɛɛ sɛhn man bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ, yɛgɛ kɩ lɔŋɔ kɩ dɛ yɛ ɔdan mʋ,

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
translation_verse: Yesu maa san ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa sakyɔ mʋ, mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana ba ba yɩlɛ nbwama bee biti fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Fu‐nyi yɛ fu‐supu ana ba yɩlɛ nbwama bee biti fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ fʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐nyi, nɩɩ anɩmʋ ana ɩ gyɛ mu‐supu ana?”

english_verse: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “These are my mother and my brothers!
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ, tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ! Mi‐nyi yɛ mi‐supu ana ndee.

english_verse: Anyone who obeys my Father in heaven is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti mʋ, ɩ gyɛ mi‐supu yɛ mɛ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ yɛ mi‐nyi.”

english_verse: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Asa damantɛ ba maa kyaabɔɔ mʋ mʋ, o loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ naa kyɩna yɛgɛ asa mʋ ba yɩlɛ ɔbʋn‐kaa.

english_verse: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa aŋasan tɔ kaapʋ bamʋ atɔ damantɛ. Ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, “Ɔdɔɔpʋ kʋ ɩ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ o kpee ɔ naa ŋmatɛ ayu.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ afʋlɛ sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩsɛ ɩ man kyɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ kɔyɩ bilen kpalɩ fɛɛ ɩsɛ mʋ maa man bʋnyɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ, nɩɩ iwii mʋ ɩ dan nyan kɩtaa ɩmʋ.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ kaapʋ asa?”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya sʋ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ, ɩ ba tɔ asa nɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ‘Fen biti fan nu kɛ fan nu, amaa fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan nu kasɛ. Fen biti fan kɩɩ kɛ fan kɩɩ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan wu sɛhn.

english_verse: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ gaalaagaa yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, bee biti gaa fɛɛ ba wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen wu yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fen nu falɛ, amaa ba man nyɛ ɩmʋ. Yesu kaapʋ ayu kɩŋmatɛ kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ (Maki

english_verse: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
translation_verse: Ayubi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, amaa dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ atɔ tɔɔrɔɔsa yɛ kafulee biti bansa mʋ, ɩɩ nyan kɩtaa asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi.

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, “Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa naa ŋmatɛ ayubi danbɩrasa mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

english_verse: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
translation_verse: Asa ba maa dɛ lʋwɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, ɔdɔɔpʋ maŋa mʋ‐dʋn naa taa afitiri libi ŋmatɛ waa ayu mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ natɛ kyʋn.

english_verse: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
translation_verse: Ayu mʋ maa nyɛ ɩtɔ ɩɩ kʋʋgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ afitiri libi mʋ gbaa ɩɩ dan.

english_verse: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ kadɔɔ wuya mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, man gyɛ ayubi danbɩrasa nɩɩ fʋ ŋmatɛ fʋ ndɔɔ mʋ tɔ? Fɩnɛ nɩɩ afitiri libi nɩmʋ ɩ lɩɩ?’

english_verse: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Mɛ‐dʋn kʋ ɩ waa kanɩn.’ “Nɩɩ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Fii biti fɛɛ a naa kyiigi ɩmʋ abɛɛ?’

english_verse: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
translation_verse: Halɩɩ oyu maŋa kibi kɩ maa du pii iyu abi kpini tɔ gbaa mʋ, kɩɩ dan kɩ kyɔ apʋnfa kpini, nɩɩ kɩɩ ba waa oyu nɩɩ nbwiibi bɛɛ ba waa bamʋ nsʋswaa kɩmʋ n‐yaabi sʋ.” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ kɩfɛ tinsa sʋ (Maki

english_verse: Jesus told them another story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a farmer plants a mustard seed in a field.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, “Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa taa masitadi oyu kibi pii naa duu mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

english_verse: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
translation_verse: Aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ sa sakyɔ mʋ. Ɔ man tɔwɛ sɛhn sa bamʋ yɛgɛ ɩ man gyɛ aŋasan tɔ.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I will use stories to speak my message and to explain things hidden since the creation of the world.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Weeds
translation_verse: Yesu waa kanɩn nɩn kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ ɩ ba tɔ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ, N biti n tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn baalasa mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ man nyi ɩmʋ lɩɩ koo dulinyaa npiili asɛ.” Yesu lɛɛ afitiri libi kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ

english_verse: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kaapʋ anɛ kɩŋasan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bɔ lɩɩ ayu yɛ afitiri libi mʋ kasɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The one who scattered the good seed is the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔdɔɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ o duu ayubi danbɩrasa mʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ.

english_verse: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
translation_verse: Ndɔɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ dulinyaa, nɩɩ ayubi danbɩrasa mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ asa. Afitiri libi mʋ mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ asa mʋ.

english_verse: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔdʋn mʋ nɩɩ o duu afitiri libi mʋ ɩ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ. Ayu mʋ kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa mʋ ɩ gyɛ dulinyaa laalaalʋwɛ. Nɩɩ atɩŋɛpʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kpantɛ afitiri libi mʋ tuwa mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa dulinyaa laalaalʋwɛ nɩn.

english_verse: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
translation_verse: Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ, n biti n sun mɛ sʋsʋ nbɔɔ, kɛ ba ba tasɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ pɩna asa nɩɩ bɛɛ waa alibi mʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa alibi mʋ, lɩɩ mi kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Kɛ ba taa bamʋ tʋʋ waa awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ tɔ, opula mʋ nɩɩ kusu yɛ anobi kiduŋi bʋ.

english_verse: But everyone who has done right will shine like the sun in their Father's kingdom. If you have ears, pay attention! A Hidden Treasure
translation_verse: Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ bee biti ba ŋalɩgɛ sʋ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ bamʋ‐Sɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ. Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ nɩɩ ee biti o nu mʋ o nu.” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ atɔ pulasa kparɛ yɛ kigyibi kparɛ mʋ sʋ

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when someone finds a treasure hidden in a field and buries it again. Such a person is happy and goes and sells everything in order to buy that field. A Valuable Pearl
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, “Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki du nɩn fɛɛ atɔ kparɛ fɛɛ suwa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa baala nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ naa benti kasɛ kʋ sʋ. Maa wu ɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o bwii bun ɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩ maa waa mʋ ɔkɔn gaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa taa mʋ atɔ kpini fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ naa sɔɔ kanɩn kasɛ maŋa.

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
translation_verse: “Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ bɩla ki du nɩn fɛɛ, ɔyaawʋ‐gyipu maa naa ee biti kigyibi kparɛ mʋ.

english_verse: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net
translation_verse: Nɩɩ maa naa nyɛ kʋkʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kɩkparɛ mʋ, ɔ naa fɛ mʋ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ɔ naa sɔɔ kɩmʋ.” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ kisewu mʋ sʋ

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a net is thrown into a lake and catches all kinds of fish.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, “Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ bɩla ki du nɩn fɛɛ, ɩkɩn ɔkɩtapʋ maa tʋʋ mʋ kisewu kɩtaa ɩkɩn kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ.

english_verse: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
translation_verse: Ɩkɩn mʋ ba maa bɔla kisewu mʋ nɩɩ akɩtapʋ mʋ ba bɩɩtɛ kɩmʋ ba ɔlɩɩkaa nɩɩ ba kyɩna tasɛ adanbɩrasa mʋ waa alantan tɔ, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man bʋrɔn mʋ fɔyɩ.

english_verse: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
translation_verse: Kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba waa dulinyaa laalaalʋwɛ mʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ mʋ bee biti ba ba tasɛ asa libi lɛɛ asa danbɩrasa tɔ,

english_verse: Then those evil people will be thrown into a flaming furnace, where they will cry and grit their teeth in pain. New and Old Treasures
translation_verse: kɛ ba taa bamʋ tʋʋ waa awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ tɔ, opula mʋ nɩɩ kusu yɛ anobi kiduŋi bʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples if they understood all these things. They said, “Yes, we do.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan nu ɩmʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ kpini kasɛ?” Nɩɩ ba yɛ, “Ɛɛhn, an nu ɩmʋ kasɛ.”

english_verse: So he told them, “Every student of the Scriptures who becomes a disciple in the kingdom of heaven is like someone who brings out new and old treasures from the storeroom.” The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Kanɩn sʋ, nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba waa ɔbɩɩlapʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii sʋ mʋ, du nɩn fɛɛ kɩkpaara wuya mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ atɔ kparɛ pʋpwɛ yɛ adɩdaa lɩɩ mu obu to mʋ.” Ba kina Yesu mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kadɛ tɔ

english_verse: When Jesus had finished telling these stories, he left
translation_verse: Yesu maa bɔ bamʋ aŋasan anɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: and went to his hometown. He taught in their synagogue, and the people were so amazed that they asked, “Where does he get all this wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o kpee mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kadɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ naa piili ɛɛ kaapʋ asa bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, “Fɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ nyɛ falɛ kanyaasɩn yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩmʋ lɩɩ?

english_verse: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
translation_verse: Man gyɛ kapinta mu‐bii mʋ ndee? Man gyɛ mu‐nyi ɩ gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ mʋ? Nɩɩ man gyɛ mu‐supu ana ɩ gyɛ Gyemisi yɛ Gyosefu yɛ Simon yɛ Gyudasi mʋ?

english_verse: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
translation_verse: Man gyɛ mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ ana kpini ɩ bʋ ana asɛ nfɩɩ? Nɩɩ fɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kpini lɩɩ?”

english_verse: So the people were upset because of what he was doing. But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their own family.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kina kɩkɔɔlɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ katɩn kʋmaa, amɔɔ mʋ kadɛ tɔ yɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi nkʋn asɛ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ.”

english_verse: And because the people did not have any faith, Jesus did not work many miracles there.
translation_verse: Yesu man waa asɩn kyinkyinsa gaalaagaa tʋtɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ ba maa man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ.

english_verse: When Jesus got out of the boat, he saw the large crowd. He felt sorry for them and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Yesu maa kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ o wu asa damantɛ mʋ, bamʋ asɩn ɩ waa mʋ kʋwɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ mʋ.

english_verse: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ba!” Nɩɩ Pita lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ ɔ naa nkyu mʋ sʋ ee kpee Yesu asɛ.

english_verse: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
translation_verse: nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ kamaa abi mʋ yɛ, “N kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu mʋ i kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ falɛ ɔlʋn ɛɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩmʋ.”

english_verse: Herod had earlier arrested John and had him chained and put in prison. He did this because John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take Herodias, the wife of your brother Philip.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyɔn daa kyɩna ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ fʋ kɔɔlɛ fu‐supu mʋ‐ka Herodiya kili.”

english_verse: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mu‐nyi tɔwɛ waa mʋ yɛ ɔ naa tɔwɛ Herodi yɛ, “Sa mɛ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu kumu tangɩran tɔ nfɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.”

english_verse: It was taken on a serving plate to the girl, and she gave it to her mother.
translation_verse: Ba bɩya Gyɔn kumu mʋ tangɩran tɔ ba sa kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kɩmʋ naa sa mu‐nyi.

english_verse: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba ba taa mʋ kubuni naa pula. Nɩɩ ba naa tɔwɛ Yesu. Yesu yɛgɛ asa ngbɩn nnuu ba gyii ŋmɛ (Maki

english_verse: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
translation_verse: Amaa maa wu apaa mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa mʋ, nɩɩ o piili ee munla, nɩɩ o kuusi yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, mɔlɩgɛ mɛ!”

english_verse: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Bodobodo anuu yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ nkʋn nɩɩ an sʋ nfɩɩ.”

english_verse: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
translation_verse: Yesu maa nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔkʋlɩ lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ nkʋn kpee opula mʋ nɩɩ i du surum. Sakyɔ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ ba taa ayaa tɔ lɩɩ ndɛ sʋ ba mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɩ man dagaa fɛɛ ba kpee. Fanɛ nɩn fan sa bamʋ agyitɔ kɛ ba gyii.”

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan taa ɩmʋ bɩya mɛ nfɩɩ.”

english_verse: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
translation_verse: Ɔ tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ ba kyɩna afitiri sʋ. Ɔ taa bodobodo anuu mʋ yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ mʋ, kɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ bodobodo mʋ tɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ mɔɔ ba taa sa asa mʋ.

english_verse: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
translation_verse: Bamʋ kpini ba gyii ŋmɛ, nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba tasɛ awuribi mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii san mʋ bɔla alantan kudu anyɔ.

english_verse: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ opula tɔ nɩɩ Yesu yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ gyankpaa mʋ faa kpee benbe, nɩɩ ɔ sa sakyɔ mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ba natɛ.

english_verse: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
translation_verse: Maa yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba kyʋn mʋ, o dii kpee kɩbɩɩ sʋ mʋ nkʋn ɔ naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ. Kalaatɩpwɛ asɛ mʋ, mʋ nkʋn ɩ bʋ tʋtɔ,

english_verse: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
translation_verse: amaa saŋa maa tɔ mʋ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɛɛ wʋla fʋʋ nkyu nsana, nɩɩ afuu a maa waa mʋ, nɩɩ apaa a daa a kyenkpen ɔkʋlɩ mʋ.

english_verse: A little while before morning, Jesus came walking on the water toward his disciples.
translation_verse: Ɩ ba fʋʋ kɩbwayɩkɛ sʋ kerefi asa naa fʋʋ kerefi asiye nsana mʋ, Yesu natɛ nkyu sʋ ee kpee mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: When they saw him, they thought he was a ghost. They were terrified and started screaming.
translation_verse: Ba maa wu maa naa nkyu sʋ ɛɛ ba mʋ, bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Obuni nɩn.” Nɩɩ ba kuusi lɩɩ fɛɛ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

english_verse: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ opula tɔ yɛ, “Fan nyɛ nkɔlɔ, mɛ nɩn. Fan man sɩlɛ kufuu.”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Lord, if it really is you, tell me to come to you on the water.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ fʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ n natɛ nkyu mʋ sʋ ba fʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: At once, Jesus reached out his hand. He helped Peter up and said, “You surely don't have much faith. Why do you doubt?”
translation_verse: Opula tɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa kɩtaa mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fu kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii man kyɔ. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ man kɔɔlɛ gyii?”

english_verse: When Jesus and Peter got into the boat, the wind died down.
translation_verse: Ba maa dii loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ afuu mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ.

english_verse: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba muŋa sun mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kesintin, fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii nɩn.” Yesu kyɛ Kanesareti alɔpʋ alobi (Maki

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and came to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
translation_verse: Ba maa faa ɔbʋn mʋ, nɩɩ ba naa dan Kanesareti ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ba maa bɩɩ Yesu mʋ, ba sun tɔwɛ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyaabɔɔ bamʋ mʋ kpini. Nɩɩ asa ba taa bamʋ alɔpʋ kpini ba mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, ɔ dan yɛgɛ kɛ alɔpʋ mʋ ba dan taa bamʋ abaa mata mʋ kaalɛ kɔnɔ‐bi, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mata maŋa kpini ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Oyu kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ i du kɩmʋ mʋ, ee biti o kyiigi kɩmʋ tʋʋ.

english_verse: “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? They don't even wash their hands before they eat.”
translation_verse: “Mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ be mee buu anɛ anaana tɔ kɩkaapʋ mʋ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba mɛɛ fwɩɩ bamʋ abaa pɔyɩ nɩɩ bee gyii.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Why do you disobey God and follow your own teaching?
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ lɩɩ fanɛ anaana tɔ kɩkaapʋ sʋ, fen mee buu Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ.

english_verse: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
translation_verse: Amaa fɛn kaapʋ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ abɛɛ mu‐nyi yɛ, ‘Kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ nyɛ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ mʋ, n taa waa saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ,’

english_verse: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
translation_verse: ɩ man bɩla i tiri fɛɛ o buu mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi kɛ ɔ kɩɩ bamʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩ fanɛ anaana tɔ kɩkaapʋ sʋ mʋ, fen mee buu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ.

english_verse: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
translation_verse: Bee sun mɛ kɩyan nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa nbara nɩɩ bɛ sʋ bɛɛ kaapʋ.’ ” Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ waa nyɩmɩsa ineesi (Maki

english_verse: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
translation_verse: Fan man tʋwɛ bamʋ, ba du nɩn fɛɛ agyaatanapʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ gyankpaa agyaatanapʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ gyaatanapʋ kan ɛɛ gyankpaa gyaatanapʋ mʋ, bamʋ kpini bee biti ba tɩyɛ ɔbɔ tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
translation_verse: Yesu man bugi kɔnɔ tɔwɛ mʋ sɛhn. Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, “Tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ɔ natɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɛɛ saawʋ ee tii anɛ asʋ.”

english_verse: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
translation_verse: Man gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii loo nyɩmɩsa kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi, amɔɔ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi.”

english_verse: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fu nyi yɛ Farasii abi mʋ ba maa nu falɛ asɩn mʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ?”

english_verse: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, “Lɛɛ kɩŋasan maŋa kasɛ kaapʋ anɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
translation_verse: Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man pɩɩta bugi?

english_verse: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
translation_verse: Fan man nyi yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kii loo nyɩmɩsa kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ, ii kpee mʋ ɔtɔ tɔ nɩn nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ ɔpɩɩ tɔ?

english_verse: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
translation_verse: Amaa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ lɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩn, ɩnɩmʋ ɩɩ waa nyɩmɩsa ineesi.

english_verse: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ libi fɛɛ, nyɩmɩsa kɩmɔɔ, yɛ kɩlɩɩ okuli abɛɛ ɔkaa kamaa, yɛ kɩkyɩɩ yɛ kɩnyɩn biti kɩgyagaa, yɛ keyu, yɛ antɩŋɛsa kɩtɩn, yɛ kɩtɔwɛ asɩn libi gyaga asa sʋ.

english_verse: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ waa nyɩmɩsa ineesi, amaa nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa mɛɛ fwɩɩ mʋ abaa nɩɩ ee gyii mʋ mɛɛ waa mʋ ineesi.” Kakanaan‐kyɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii (Maki

english_verse: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ fɔyɩsa mʋ, nkʋn asɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mɛ.”

english_verse: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
translation_verse: Asa damantɛ ba ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba bɩya alɔpʋ fɛɛ, ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana, yɛ agyaatanapʋ, yɛ aboobi, yɛ atikpiri, yɛ alɔpʋ gaalaagaa, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ naa yɩla mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ.

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
translation_verse: Yesu lɩɩ tʋtɔ, nɩɩ o kpee Tayi maa Sidon ɔsʋwʋlɛ kɩbaafʋn.

english_verse: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kakanaan‐kyɩɩ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ ba mʋ asɛ ɛɛ saawʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ! Nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mi kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi sʋ bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa.”

english_verse: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba muŋa mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, kyɛ mɛ tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɩ man dagaa ba maa taa nbii agyitɔ tʋʋ sa igyoonɔ.”

english_verse: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɩ bʋ tɔ kanɩn, amaa igyoonɔ mʋ gbaa bee gyii agyitɔ wuribi mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ana iteeburu sʋ ɩɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔkyɩɩ, fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ kyɔ gaa! Kanan mʋ nɩɩ fii biti mʋ ɩ waa kanɩn sa fʋ.” Nɩɩ opula maa tɔ kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya. Yesu yɛgɛ asa ngbɩn nna ba gyii ŋmɛ (Maki

english_verse: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
translation_verse: Yesu lɩɩ tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa ɔ naa Galile ɔpʋ mʋ ɩkaa sʋ. Nɩɩ o dii kpee kɩbɩɩ sʋ naa kyɩna.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
translation_verse: Asa damantɛ mʋ ba maa wu anaamuu ba maa tɔwɛ, yɛ boobi yɛ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana ba maa natɛ, yɛ agyaatanapʋ ansi ɩ maa bugi mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ, nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩsɩrayɩ abi Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Yesu tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Asa nɩmʋ asɩn ɩ sʋ mɛ kʋwɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa bʋ mɛ asɛ ndaga nkɛ nsa ndee ba man sʋ agyitɔ ba gyii. N mee biti fɛɛ ba taa akʋn kpewu, man gyɛ kanɩn bee biti ba lɩɩ tɩyɛ ɔkpa tɔ.”

english_verse: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
translation_verse: Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a nyɛ agyitɔ kɩkyɔ afitiri nɩmʋ tɔ sa asa damantɛ banɩmʋ?”

english_verse: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Bodobodo afɩnɛ nɩɩ fan sʋ?” Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “An sʋ asunɔ yɛ ɩkɩn‐bi gbɛrɛɛ kʋ.”

english_verse: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ asa damantɛ mʋ yɛ ba kyɩna kasɛ.

english_verse: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa bodobodo asunɔ mʋ yɛ ɩkɩn‐bi gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ gyayɩ‐gyayɩ ɩmʋ tɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ ba taa sa asa mʋ

english_verse: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
translation_verse: Bamʋ kpini ba gyii nɩɩ ba ŋmɛ. Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ ɩmʋ agyisan wuribi mʋ bɔla alantan asunɔ.

english_verse: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
translation_verse: Yesu maa kala sakyɔ mʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ faa kpee Magadan kɩbaafʋn.

english_verse: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ ɩɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ bodobodo asɩn nɩɩ n tɔwɛ? Amaa fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ lɩɩ Farasii yɛ Sadusi abi kɩfɛ tinsa mʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kpee kabaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri Kasariya Filipi kadɛ tɔ mʋ. Maa bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ nɩɩ asa ba yɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n gyɛ?”

english_verse: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lan nu kasɛ fɛɛ man gyɛ kɩfɛ tin agyitɔ mʋ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba baa ba da sʋ, amaa Farasii yɛ Sadusi abi asɩn kaapʋsa nɩɩ ɔ dɩyɛ. Pita kɩlɛɛ tɔwɛ ɔsa kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu gyɛ (Maki

english_verse: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kyɔwɛ maa kpee ɔ naa tɩyɛ mʋ, fɛn taalɛ fɛn bɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔkɛ kyɔwɛ ii biti ɔ da, lɩɩ fɛɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ ɩ payɩ kɔɔ’.

english_verse: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nyenyenfuu mɔɔ fɛn taalɛ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ndaga bʋlayɩ ii biti ɔ da, lɩɩ fɛɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ ɩ payɩ nɩɩ i bii tɔ.’ Fɛn taalɛ fen wu abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ i du, nɩɩ fɛɛ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ fɛn bɩɩ nkɛ nɩmʋ tɔ asɩn, kɛ fɛn lɛɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Akʋ ba yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu mʋ, nɩɩ akʋ ba yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Eligya, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyeremiya abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.”

english_verse: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa ɩɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, Yesu piili bugi tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “I tiri fɛɛ n maa kpee Gyerusalem naa gyii awʋrʋfɔ gaa lɩɩ abɩlɩsa, yɛ aseepu‐bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ kpini abaa tɔ. Bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ, kɛ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ, n kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ danbɩrasa, kɛ fan baa fɛn da Farasii abi yɛ Sadusi abi kɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii tin bodobodo mʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa asɛ yɛ, “A maa man bɩya bodobodo mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ.”

english_verse: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n baa yɛ Ɔsɛ mʋ bʋnyaa, yɛ mɛ nbɔɔ, kɛ n ba ka ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kʋkɔ lɩɩ mu kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, fʋ gyɛ Pita, nɩɩ kanɩn kɩfʋlɛ maŋa sʋ nɩɩ n biti n pɔyɩ mɛ, Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ, nɩɩ lewu gbaa mɛɛ taalɛ gyii kɩmʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fanɔɔ fan fa? Anɩmʋ nɩɩ fan yɛ n gyɛ?”

english_verse: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Simon Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kakpaa mʋ Mu‐Bii.”

english_verse: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ Simon, Gyona mu‐bii! Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa fʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩ lɛɛ falɛ kesintin asɩn nɩmʋ kaapʋ fʋ, amaa mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ɩ lɛɛ falɛ asɩn mʋ kaapʋ fʋ.

english_verse: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: N biti n taa sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii ɩsafwɩɩ kpini sa fʋ. Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fu kyula kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa kyula kɩmʋ sʋsʋ. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fu kina kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa kina kɩmʋ sʋsʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu da mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ amu tɔ yɛ, ba man kan waa tɔwɛ ɔkʋ yɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito. Yesu tɔwɛ mu kelewu asɩn (Maki

english_verse: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita taa Yesu kpee ɩkaa sʋ tɔwɛ yii mʋ yɛ, “Afuloo! Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ! Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa ɩmʋ kpee kata‐kata. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ ba fʋ sʋ kakaakʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, “Sɩtaanɛ, taa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ! Fii kun mɛ ɔkpa nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man gyan Ɩbwaarɛ atɔ sʋ, amɔɔ ɩ gyan nyɩmɩsa lɛɛ sʋ nɩn.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ee biti o buu mɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa kina mʋ n‐yɩɩ, kɛ ɔ sʋla mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ oyu‐laasa kɛ o buu mɛ.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa mʋ, ii biti ɔ pan ɩmʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ii biti o bwii nyɛ mʋ nkpa mʋ.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kan nyɛ dulinyaa tɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa, nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa mʋ, mɩnɛ tɔnɔ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ? Abɛɛ mɩnɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ taalɛ taa kyɛrɛ mʋ nkpa?

english_verse: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin nɩn fɛɛ asa kʋ ba yɩla nfɩɩ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa maa ba yɛ mu kuwura‐gyii mʋ.”

english_verse: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ ansi sʋ. Mu ansi sʋ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ, nɩɩ mʋ atɔ bunsa ɩ kyɛɛgɛ waa parɛparɛ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba nu kasɛ nɩn fɛɛ, Gyɔn okyugyeepu asɩn nɩɩ Yesu ɩɩ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa bamʋ kanɩn. Yesu kyɛ obii‐nyɩnsa kʋ kigbingbin (Maki

english_verse: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Nkɛ nsiye kamaa tɔ mʋ, Yesu taa Pita yɛ Gyemisi maa mu‐supu Gyɔn, nɩɩ ba dii kpee kɩbɩɩ lala kʋ sʋ bamʋ nkʋn.

english_verse: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba wu Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ anyɔ, Mosesi maa Eligya ba lɩɩ opula tɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Yesu asɛ.

english_verse: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɩ bʋrɔn fɛɛ a maa bʋ nfɩɩ. Nɩɩ fʋ kan fii biti mʋ, n biti n yii abu asa, fʋ lɛɛ kʋkʋ, Mosesi lɛɛ kʋkʋ nɩɩ Eligya lɛɛ kʋkʋ.”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Pita maa san ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ ŋalɩgɛsa ba bun bamʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ tɔwɛ kabwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ tɔ yɛ, “Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Mi‐Bii‐nyɩnsa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ kebiti sa, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ bɔla mi ansi. Fan nu mʋ asɛ!”

english_verse: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
translation_verse: Abɩɩlapʋ asa mʋ ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba kyʋn bun kasɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu ba bamʋ asɛ taa mʋ kɩbaa mata bamʋ, tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan kʋsʋ, fan man waa kufuu.”

english_verse: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
translation_verse: Ba maa kʋsʋ kɩɩ mʋ, ba man wu ɔkʋ, amɔɔ Yesu nkʋn.

english_verse: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ɩ dagaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya maa gyankpaa ba pɔyɩ?”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kesintin nɩn, ɩ dagaa Eligya maa gyankpaa ba, kɛ ɔ lɔŋɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa yɩla o gyoo.

english_verse: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
translation_verse: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩmʋ, Eligya tɛɛ wʋla ba nɩɩ asa ba man bɩɩ mʋ, amaa ba waa mʋ kanan kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti. Nɩɩ kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ gbaa ii biti o gyii awʋrʋfɔ bamʋ abaa tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
translation_verse: Ba maa bwii ba sakyɔ mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔnyɩn kʋ ba Yesu asɛ ba muŋa mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, wu mi‐bii‐nyɩnsa kʋwɛɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ kigbingbin wuya nɩɩ ee gyii awʋrʋfɔ gaa. Ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɩyɛ fʋlɔn tɔ abɛɛ nkyu tɔ.

english_verse: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
translation_verse: N taa mʋ ba fʋʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ, amaa ba man taalɛ kyɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “O, fanɛ ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa mʋ nɩɩ fan man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, nɩɩ fanɛ ɩkpa ɩ man kyiigi! Nkɛ nfɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n gyii fanɛ asɛ? Nɛnɛ nɩɩ n biti n bɩla nyɛ kenyiita sa fanɛ? Fan taa kebii mʋ bɩya mɛ nfɩɩ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu pʋntɛ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ kɩ kʋsʋ kebii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ bamʋ nkʋn ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ an man taalɛ gyan kabwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ kebii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sʋ?”

english_verse: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ kpini ba ba Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa asa abaa tɔ.

english_verse: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
translation_verse: Bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ, kɛ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ, n kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.” Abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, bamʋ nkɔlɔ i nyita bamʋ gaa. Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ tʋʋtʋʋ (Maki

english_verse: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
translation_verse: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba maa ba Kapanum kadɛ tɔ mʋ, kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ tʋʋtʋʋ akɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ ba ba Pita asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ ɔkaapʋpʋ ɩɩ ka kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ tʋʋtʋʋ mʋ?”

english_verse: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɛɛhn, ɛɛ ka.” Pita maa ba kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu daa kɩtaasɛ yɛ, “Simon fʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ gyɛ nɛnɛ? Bamɔɔ asɛ nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ awura bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ kagya kasɛ tʋʋtʋʋ? Bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bɛɛ lɩɩ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ‐bii ana asɛ nɩn abɛɛ afɔ asɛ?”

english_verse: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
translation_verse: Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bɛɛ lɩɩ afɔ asɛ nɩn.” Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, “Kanɩn bɩrɛ ndɛ‐bii ba man tii sʋ.

english_verse: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
translation_verse: Amaa a maa mee biti a maa waa bamʋ agbʋ sʋ mʋ, kpee ɔpʋ tɔ naa tʋʋ fʋ darʋwa. Ɔkɩn mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ daa kɩkɩta mʋ, bugi mʋ kɔnɔ, fii biti fu wu kederebi mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ fʋʋ maa fʋ tʋʋtʋʋ. Kɛ fʋ taa naa ka bamʋ.”

english_verse: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fu kinsi ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ waa alibi mʋ, kyɔlɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ. Ɩ bʋrɔn sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa kinsi kʋlʋn loo nkpa kakpaa tɔ, ɩ kyɔ fʋ maa sʋ ansi anyɔ kɛ ba taa fʋ tʋʋ awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ.” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ kabʋlʋpʋ fɔyɩsa (Luka

english_verse: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
translation_verse: Ɩ man dagaa fʋ gbaa fʋ maa wu fʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ kʋwɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n wu fʋ kʋwɛɛ mʋ?’

english_verse: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ fɛɛ kebii pii nɩmʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ɔkparɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ kebii pii fɛɛ ɔnɩmʋ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ nɩmʋ.

english_verse: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ man kan bɩla nu sa bamʋ mʋ, taa asɩn mʋ naa tʋʋ Krisito kʋbʋ mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla kina kunu sa bamʋ bɩrɛ, taa mʋ fɛɛ ɔdakpa osunpu abɛɛ apoo lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ.

english_verse: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kan bɔla mʋ sʋ, kɛ nbii mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii falɛ tɔ ɔkʋ waa alibi mʋ, ɩ bɔ sa mʋ fɛɛ ba taa kubuu lala ŋmina mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ naa tʋʋ ɔpʋ bunkyinsa tɔ.

english_verse: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fʋ kɩbaa abɛɛ fʋ kɩyaa ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa alibi mʋ, tɩn kɩmʋ tʋʋ. Ɩ bʋrɔn sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa kɩbaa abɛɛ kɩyaa dɩyɛ loo nkpa kakpaa tɔ, ɩ kyɔ fʋ maa sʋ abaa anyɔ yɛ ayaa anyɔ kɛ ba taa fʋ tʋʋ fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ki mee dun kakaakʋ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ fu‐supu abɛɛ fʋ‐daa kan waa fʋ alibi mʋ, kpee mʋ asɛ naa lɛɛ mu alibi mʋ kaapʋ mʋ fanɛ asa nyɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan nu kasɛ sa fʋ mʋ, fʋ bɩla bɩya mʋ ba ɔkpa danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ nɩmʋ.

english_verse: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ ba nɩn kɛ n ba mɔlɩgɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba fɔyɩ mʋ.”

english_verse: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
translation_verse: Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ gbaa mee biti fɛɛ apii‐pii nɩmʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn maa fɔyɩ. Nɩɩ Fʋ bɩrɩsa nɩɩ ɔ waa fʋ alibi mʋ, kʋtɔ nɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ waa

english_verse: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ ɔ man kan nu sa mʋ, biti nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn abɛɛ asa anyɔ tii fʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kɛ fen kpee mʋ asɛ. Saamʋ, asɩn mʋ ɩ nyɛ yɩlɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɩ dagaa asɩn kʋmaa ɩ nyɛ asa anyɔ abɛɛ asa asá be gyii ɩmʋ adansɛ.’

english_verse: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan ŋmina kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o ŋmina kɩmʋ sʋsʋ, nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan kina kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o kina kɩmʋ sʋsʋ.

english_verse: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
translation_verse: “N bɩla n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ anyɔ ba kan waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ bee biti kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ii biti ɔ waa sa bamʋ.

english_verse: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ asa anyɔ abɛɛ asa asá ba gyaŋɛ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, n bʋ bamʋ asɛ.” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ o mee su kʋwɛɛ mʋ sʋ

english_verse: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita ba Yesu asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɩlɩɩ ɩfɩnɛ nɩɩ mɛ‐bɩrɩsa waa mɛ alibi nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n taa kɛɛ mʋ? Ilii isunɔ abɛɛ?”

english_verse: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
translation_verse: “Lɩɩ fɛɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ owura kʋ maa biti mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba ba kaapʋ mʋ akɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
translation_verse: Maa piili ɛɛ taasɛ mʋ akɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa ɔnyɩn kʋ bɩya mʋ. Ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ owura mʋ kʋkɔ afulee nderebi ngbɩn‐ngbɩn.

english_verse: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
translation_verse: Maa man taalɛ ka mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ owura mʋ yɛ, ba taa maa mʋ‐ka yɛ mu‐bii ana yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ naa fɛ kɛ ba ba ka kʋkɔ mʋ.

english_verse: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kyʋn muŋa mu amulii sʋ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ɔbɩlɩsa, nyɛ kenyiita sa mɛ, n biti n ka fʋ kʋkɔ maŋa kpini.’

english_verse: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ asɩn ɩ waa mʋ owura mʋ kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kʋkɔ mʋ kpini kɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ natɛ.

english_verse: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ mʋ bɩrɩsa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ muŋa mu amulii sʋ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Nyɛ kenyiita sa mɛ, n biti n ka kʋkɔ mʋ kpini.’

english_verse: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
translation_verse: “Amaa o kina mʋ bɩrɩsa maŋa kɩkʋlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ naa tii obu naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ nyɛ afulee ba ka mʋ kʋkɔ mʋ.

english_verse: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
translation_verse: Mʋ bɩrɩsa ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ ba maa wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ, ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ gaa nɩɩ ba naa tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ kpini sa bamʋ owura mʋ.

english_verse: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ owura mʋ tɩɩ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa ba nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn wuya. N taa fʋ kʋkɔ kpini nɩmʋ kɛɛ fʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ fʋ maa kʋlɛ mɛ sʋ.

english_verse: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nyɛ agbʋ gaa, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ naa tii obu yɛ ba nyanla mʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ ka kʋkɔ mʋ kpini.”

english_verse: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Falɛ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ii biti ɔ waa fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ, amɔɔ fʋ kan taa fʋ bɩrɩsa alibi kɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ ɔ lɩɩ Galile kpee Gyudeya kɩbaafʋn, Gyɔdan ɔpʋ mʋ benbe.

english_verse: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
translation_verse: Anyɩn akʋ ba bʋ tɔ nɩɩ be mee taalɛ kili, lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ asa ɩ waa bamʋ kanɩn, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba kina kekili lɩɩ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kusun sʋ. Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ kɔɔlɛ kɩkaapʋ nɩmʋ kyula mʋ, ɔ kɔɔlɛ.” Yesu sʋ kebiti sa nbii wuribi (Maki

english_verse: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
translation_verse: Asa damantɛ ba buu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ alɔ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Farasii abi akʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba ba tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ. Ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ dɛ ɔkpa sa ɔnyɩn fɛɛ ɔ yɩlɛ asɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ kina mʋ‐ka?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan man karagɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ wu fɛɛ, ‘Lɩɩ npiili asɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ waa bamʋ ɔnyɩn yɛ ɔkyɩɩ’ nɩn abɛɛ?

english_verse: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ, ɔnyɩn ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ mu‐nyi yɛ mʋ‐sɛ, kɛ maa mʋ‐ka ba bʋga kɛ bamʋ anyɔ ba waa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn?’

english_verse: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba man bɩla ba gyɛ anyɔ, amaa ba gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn. Kanɩn sʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa bʋga waa kʋkʋlʋn mʋ, ɔkʋ ɔ man barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Mosesi sa kɔnɔ yɛ ɔnyɩn ɩ taalɛ sa mʋ‐ka kekili kikina ɔwʋlɛ kɛ o kina mʋ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mosesi sa fanɛ ɔkpa yɛ fan kina fanɛ‐ka ana, lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ bʋlʋn nɩn. Amaa man gyɛ falɛ nɩɩ ɩ kyɩna i du lɩɩ npiili asɛ mʋ.

english_verse: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ yɛ ɔkyɩɩ lɩɩ mu‐kuli kamaa, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ kina mʋ naa kili ɔkyɩɩ pʋpwɛ mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nyita kekili.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Man gyɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ kɔɔlɛ falɛ asɩn kyula, amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba taa asɩn mʋ sa bamʋ nkʋn.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa kʋ ba taa nbii wuribi ba Yesu asɛ yɛ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ, kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa bamʋ. Amaa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba gya asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩya bamʋ mʋ.

english_verse: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ ba Yesu asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, mɩnɛ kʋtɔ danbɩrasa nɩɩ ɩ dagaa n maa waa kɛ n nyɛ nkpa kakpaa?”

english_verse: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ taasɛ yɛ, “Ɩmɔɔ?” Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “ ‘Man mɔɔ, man lɩɩ fʋ‐ka abɛɛ fu‐kuli kamaa, man yuuri, man yɩlɛ antɩŋɛsa kamaa,

english_verse: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
translation_verse: Maa taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyʋn. Ɔnyɩn atɔ wuya mʋ (Maki

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ taasɛ mɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn sʋ? Ɔkʋ man gyɛ ɔdanbɩrasa, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn. Nɩɩ fii biti fʋ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ, buu Mosesi nbara mʋ.”

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan yɛgɛ nbii wuribi mʋ ba ba mɛ asɛ, fan man tii bamʋ ɔkpa, lɩɩ fɛɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyɛ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ be du fɛɛ nbii‐wuribi mʋ lɛɛ nɩn.”

english_verse: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
translation_verse: Amaa asa gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa mʋ, bee biti be sii kamaa, nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kamaa mʋ, bee biti ba ba gyankpaa.”

english_verse: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: buu fʋ‐sɛ yɛ fu‐nyi,’ kɛ ‘fu biti fʋ bɩrɩsa fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.’ ”

english_verse: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “N buu ɩnɩmʋ kpini. Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ ɩ san mɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ kan fii biti yɛ fʋ baa fu du kyɛɛkyɛɛ bɩrɛ, kpee naa fɛ fʋ atɔ kpini kɛ fʋ taa afulee mʋ sa atiripu, fii biti fʋ nyɛ kapatɩyɛ sʋsʋ, kɛ fʋ lan ba buu mɛ.”

english_verse: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn mʋ maa nu falɛ mʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyʋn lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sʋ atɔ gaa.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, ɩ bʋlʋn sa atɔ wuya fɛɛ o loo sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: N bɩla n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɩ bʋpɔɔ sa nyɔɔma fɛɛ o loo baasa ɔbɔ tɔ ɩ kyɔ atɔ wuya o loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ gaa nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Kanɩn bɩrɛ anɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ nɩmʋ?”

english_verse: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Yesu kɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nyɩmɩsa asɛ bɩrɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ waa, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ bɩrɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ waa.”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
translation_verse: Pita lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kɩɩ! A maa yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ an buu fʋ falɛ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a nyɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan bun dulinyaa pʋpwɛ, nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kyɩna mɛ bʋnyaa kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ, fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan buu mɛ mʋ gbaa, fen biti fan kyɩna nwuragya kudu anyɔ sʋ, kɛ fan gyii Ɩsɩrayɩ nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ sʋ kuwura.

english_verse: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ akpaara, abɛɛ pɩɩkyɩɩ, abɛɛ mʋ‐sɛ, abɛɛ mu‐nyi, abɛɛ mu‐bii ana, abɛɛ adɔɔ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ ɩmʋ ɩlɩɩ kɩlɩfa, kɛ ɔ bɩla nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

english_verse: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa kʋsʋ kakaakʋ nyenyenfuu, ɔ naa biti asa kɛ ba sun kusun mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

english_verse: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
translation_verse: Maa naa nyɛ bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba tɔwɛ kyula abaa yɛ ee biti ɔ ka bamʋ kederebi kʋlʋn kakɛ kʋlʋn ibiri mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa bamʋ kpee mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

english_verse: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
translation_verse: “Kyɔwɛ maa waa fɛɛ kerefi akpanɔ mʋ, ɔ bɩla kpee kadɛ mʋ kɩgya asɛ naa wu akʋ ba maa yɩlɛ ba mɛɛ waa sɛhn.

english_verse: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ‘Fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan kpee naa waa kusun mɛ ndɔɔ tɔ, kɛ n ka fanɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa.’

english_verse: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kpee. “Kyɔwɛ amu nsana yɛ kerefi asa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩla taa akʋ kpee kanɩn.

english_verse: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
translation_verse: Kalaatɩpwɛ sʋ kyɔwɛ maa tɩyɛ ɛɛ mata afatɛɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩla kpee naa wu akʋ ba maa yɩlɛ ba mɛɛ waa sɛhn. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, ‘Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fan yɩlɛ nfɩɩ kakɛ ibiri nɩɩ fan man sʋ kusun.’

english_verse: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Ɔkʋ man taa anɛ yɛ a naa sun sa mʋ.’ “Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ‘Kanɩn bɩrɛ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan ba kyʋn kpee naa waa kusun mɛ ndɔɔ tɔ.’

english_verse: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
translation_verse: “Kalaatɩpwɛ maa fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ndɔɔ wuya mʋ tɔwɛ asunpu mʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ, ‘Tɩɩ asunpu mʋ, kɛ fʋ ka bamʋ kʋkɔ. Piili lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sii kamaa ba mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa ba mʋ.’

english_verse: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asunpu mʋ nɩɩ ba kpee ndɔɔ mʋ tɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ mata afatɛɛ tɔ mʋ, ba ba kɔɔlɛ kederebi kʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kakɛ ibiri kʋkɔka.

english_verse: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Ba maa kɔɔlɛ kederebi kʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn mʋ, nɩɩ be piili bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ bɛɛ sa ndɔɔ wuya mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
translation_verse: ‘Bamʋ nɩɩ ba sii kamaa ba falɛ bɩrɛ, saŋa gbɛrɛɛ nɩɩ ba sun kusun, yɛgɛ anɛɛnɛ bɩrɛ an yɩlɛ kyɔwɛ kenken nɩmʋ sʋ waa kusun ibiri. Amaa ana bamʋ kpini nɩɩ fʋ ka kyɛɛkyɛɛ?’

english_verse: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ kadɔɔ wuya mʋ lɛɛ bamʋ ɔkʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Kyɛmɩnɛ, n man sisii fʋ. Man gyɛ kederebi kʋlʋn kʋkɔka nɩɩ maa fʋ an tɔwɛ kyula kakɛ ibiri kusun?

english_verse: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ taa fu kederebi natɛ. Kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ pɩrɛ mɛ yɛ n ka asii‐amaapʋ mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ maŋa nɩɩ n biti n ka fʋ gbaa.

english_verse: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
translation_verse: N man sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n waa mɛ kɔkɔlɔ biti lɩɩ mɛ afulee sʋ abɛɛ? Abɛɛ fʋ sʋ kayɩɩ basa lɩɩ mɛ kɩdanbɩrasa kɩwaa sʋ nɩn?’ ”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Asii‐amaapʋ bee biti ba biliŋi agyankpaapʋ kɛ agyankpaapʋ ba biliŋi asii‐amaapʋ.” Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ mu kelewu yɛ mu kikyiŋi asɩn (Maki

english_verse: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ n waa sa fanɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu wu bamʋ kʋwɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ mata bamʋ ansi. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i bugi nɩɩ ba buu Yesu.

english_verse: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba pʋntɛ bamʋ sʋ yɛ ba bun bamʋ nnɔ sʋ, amaa ba kuusi kenken yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ.”

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
translation_verse: Yesu maa kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
translation_verse: kɛ ba taa mɛ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ abaa tɔ, kɛ ba waa mɛ ɩbasasɩn kɛ ba tɩŋɛ mɛ, kɛ ba da mɛ mata oyu laasa sʋ. Amaa nkɛ nsa akɛ mʋ, n biti n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ.” Sebedi mʋ‐ka kʋkʋlɛ sa mu‐bii ana (Maki

english_verse: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sebedi mʋ‐ka maa mu‐bii ana anyɩnsa anyɔ mʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ‐nyi ba muŋa Yesu ayaa tɔ kʋlɛ mʋ kʋtɔ kʋ.

english_verse: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti?” Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kyula sa mɛ fɛɛ, mi nbii nnyɔ nɩmʋ, ɔkʋ ɔ kyɩna fu‐gyisa sʋ kɛ ɔkʋ mɔɔ ɔ kyɩna fʋ‐bɩna sʋ fu kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn kʋlɛ falɛ. Fɛn taalɛ gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n gyii mʋ?” Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɛɛhn, an taalɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fɛn taalɛ gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n gyii mʋ, amaa n man sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ n lɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kyɩna mi‐gyisa sʋ abɛɛ mɛ‐bɩna sʋ. Ɩkyɩnakpa ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ wʋla lɔŋɔ yɩla sa bamʋ mʋ.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
translation_verse: Abɩɩlapʋ kudu mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, ba maa nu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, ba nyɛ agbʋ waa Gyemisi maa mu‐supu Gyɔn.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ bamʋ ba bʋga nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fen nyi fɛɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ agyankpaapʋ mʋ bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ ɔlʋn asa sʋ, nɩɩ alʋlʋnpʋ gbaa bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ ɔlʋn asa sʋ.

english_verse: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
translation_verse: Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ tɔ bɩrɛ, ɩ man dagaa ɩ maa waa kanɩn. Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan ii biti kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa waa fanɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ,

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
translation_verse: nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ waa ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa waa fanɛ kɩnyɛ,

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
translation_verse: fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa gbaa n man ba nɩn kɛ ba sun mɛ, amaa n ba nɩn kɛ n ba sun, kɛ n taa mɛ nkpa kɛɛ, kɛ ɩ ka asa gaalaagaa alibi kʋkɔ.” Yesu kɩkyɛ agyaatanapʋ anyɔ (Maki

english_verse: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
translation_verse: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba maa lɩɩ Gyeriko kadɛ tɔ mʋ, asa gaalaagaa ba buu bamʋ.

english_verse: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ, agyaatanapʋ anyɔ kʋ ba tɛ ɔkpakaa, nɩɩ ba nu fɛɛ Yesu ɩɩ kyʋn mʋ, ba kuusi tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ?”

english_verse: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, an biti fɛɛ anɛ ansi i bugi nɩn.”

english_verse: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩmʋ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ falɛ, nɛnɛ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ? Ɔnyɩn kʋ ɩ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ nbii‐nyɩnsa anyɔ. O kpee ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Mi‐bii, ndaga kpee mɛ kadɔɔ mʋ tɔ naa sun kusun.’

english_verse: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: “Amaa paa agyipu mʋ ba maa wu mu‐bii maa ba mʋ, ba tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, ‘Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kapatɩyɛ ogyipu mʋ’. Fan ba kɛ a mɔɔ mʋ kɛ a taa mʋ kapatɩyɛ mʋ.

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taasɛ fanɛ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, fan tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu ii biti bamʋ.’ Nɩɩ ee biti ɔ yɛgɛ fan taa bamʋ bɩya mɛ.”

english_verse: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
translation_verse: “Tɔwɛ Siyon abii mʋ yɛ, ‘Fan kɩɩ, fanɛ owura ɩɩ ba fanɛ asɛ, O du bɔhn nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuruma sʋ, kesintin tɔ ɔ tɛ kuruma kadanba sʋ.’ ”

english_verse: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
translation_verse: Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba naa waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu sa bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ ba waa mʋ.

english_verse: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Ba taa kuruma yɛ mu‐bii mʋ ba, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ atɔ bunsa gyaga bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Yesu dii kyɩna kadanba mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
translation_verse: Yesu maa loo Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, kadɛ mʋ kpini kɔɔlɛ ɔlanba, nɩɩ asa bɛɛ taasɛ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ nɩn?”

english_verse: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba maa wu atɔ kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa, yɛ nbii mʋ kikuusi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Hosana ɩ gyɛ sa Dawidi mʋ kanaana‐bii mʋ”, nɩɩ ba nyɛ agbʋ.

english_verse: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ Farasii abi ba maa nu Yesu aŋasan mʋ, ba bɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ.

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, ‘Bee biti ba tɩɩ mɛ kɩkpaara yɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛkpa’, amaa fan taa tʋtɔ biliŋi bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ ɔbaalakpa.”

english_verse: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan kan kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ tɔ mʋ.” Ba taasɛ Yesu katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ mʋ ɔlʋn lɩɩ (Maki

english_verse: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fu nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbii mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ?” Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ “Ɛɛhn, fan man pɩɩta kalɛ lɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘Fʋ kaapʋ nbii yɛ nbii wuribi yɛ ba yɩn fʋ.’ ”

english_verse: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Betani kadɛ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ dɛ tʋtɔ. Yesu tɔwɛ nnɔ waa figi oyu mʋ (Maki

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ngbaa mɛ n biti n taasɛ fanɛ asɩn kʋ, nɩɩ fan kan lɛɛ mɛ kɔnɔ mʋ, n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n waa ɩnɩmʋ.

english_verse: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kaakɛ nyenyenfuu Yesu maa bwii ee kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, akʋn ɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, nɩɩ fan kan kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii, nɩɩ fan man gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ mʋ, man gyɛ yɛ fen biti fan taalɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa figi oyu nɩmʋ nkʋn. Amaa fɛn taalɛ tɔwɛ kɩbɩɩ nɩmʋ yɛ, ‘Kʋsʋ lɩɩ nfɩɩ naa tɩyɛ ɔpʋ tɔ,’ kɛ ɩ waa kanɩn.

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ Yesu kɔnɔ yɛ, “A man nyi.” Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Kanɩn bɩrɛ ngbaa mɛ, n mɛɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n waa ɩnɩmʋ.” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ ɔsɛ kʋ yɛ mu‐bii ana anyɔ sʋ

english_verse: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan bɩla nu kɩŋasan kʋ. Ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna dɔɔ oyu sɔyɩ abisa. Nɩɩ o ŋmina iyu kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ, nɩɩ o kuu wayɩn ɔnyɩyɛkpa ɔbɔ kʋdɔɔ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ opula mʋ nɩɩ akɩɩsʋpʋ bee dii bɛɛ kɩɩ kʋdɔɔ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa kʋdɔɔ mʋ waa paa agyipu kʋ abaa tɔ, nɩɩ o kpee ɔsʋwʋlɛ kʋ sʋ.
