english_verse: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Yesu loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ. Maa bʋ tʋtɔ ɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ kadɛ mʋ abɩlɩsa ba ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ waa atɔ nɩmʋ? Nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ sa fʋ falɛ ɔlʋn.”

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Gyɔn kekyugyee mʋ, fɩnɛ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ? Ɩ kyɩna lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn abɛɛ ɩ lɩɩ asa asɛ nɩn?” Ba tɔwɛ bamʋ wʋlɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ yɛ, “Nɩɩ an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn’, ee biti ɔ taasɛ anɛ yɛ, ‘Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii?’

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Amaa an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Kɩ lɩɩ asa asɛ mʋ’, an sɩlɛ asa mʋ kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ kpini ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Gyɔn kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.”

english_verse: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘N mɛɛ yɛ n kpee.’ Amaa kamaa tɔ mʋ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ o kpee.

english_verse: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔsɛ mʋ kpee mu‐bii banban mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ kanɩn asɩn kʋlʋn maŋa. Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Nsɛ, n biti n kpee.’ Amaa ɔ man kpee.

english_verse: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
translation_verse: “Bamʋ anyɔ maŋa, anɩmʋ ɩ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ ii biti?” Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ.” Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, apoo lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ agyamilo‐kyɩɩ bɛɛ gyankpaa fanɛ bee loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyɔn ba fanɛ asɛ nɩn yɛ ɔ ba kaapʋ fanɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa ɔkpa, nɩɩ fan man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii, amaa lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ agyamilo‐kyɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii. Nɩɩ halɩɩ fan maa wu ɩnɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, fan man yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa kɛ fan kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ paa agyipu mʋ sʋ (Maki

english_verse: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa maa fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o sun mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ ba kpee paa agyipu mʋ asɛ naa kɔɔlɛ mu ogyikpa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ nyɛ mʋ bɩya mʋ.

english_verse: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ paa agyipu mʋ ba kɩtaa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba dayɩ ɔkʋ, nɩɩ ba mɔɔ ɔkʋ, nɩɩ ba twɩɩ ɔsasapʋ mʋ abuu.

english_verse: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o bwii sun mʋ ayaafɔlɛ akʋ nɩɩ ba kyɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa sun mʋ, nɩɩ paa agyipu mʋ ba waa bamʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba waa abanban mʋ.

english_verse: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: Laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, o sun mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa bamʋ asɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ‘Bee biti ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa mi‐bii.’

english_verse: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kɩtaa kebii mʋ, bɩɩtɛ mʋ lɩɩ kadɔɔ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ kadɔɔ wuya mʋ kan ba mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa paa agyipu mʋ?”

english_verse: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɩ waa kanan kʋmaa ee biti ɔ mɔɔ kanɩn asa libi maŋa, kɛ ɔ taa kadɔɔ mʋ sa paa agyipu pʋpwɛ kʋ, mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba baa bɛɛ sa mʋ mʋ ogyikpa lɩɩ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ mʋ tɔ kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan man pɩɩta kalɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘Kubuu mʋ nɩɩ apɔyɩpʋ ba kina mʋ, kɩmʋ ɩ ba biliŋi kɩbɔntɔnbʋlɔntɔ kubuu kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ obu mʋ tɔ. Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ɩ waa ɩnɩmʋ, nɩɩ i kyinkyin anɛ.’

english_verse: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa mu kuwura‐gyii mʋ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ, kɛ ɔ taa sa asa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa mu kebiti mʋ.

english_verse: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩyɛ kubuu nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, ii biti o boyi tɔ buriburi, amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ii biti ɔ patɩyɛ tɔ.”

english_verse: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
translation_verse: Ba biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kɩtaa mʋ, amaa ba sɩlɛ sakyɔ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ be nyi maa gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.

english_verse: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lʋwɛ yɛ, “Ba tɩɩ asa gaalaagaa, amaa gbɛrɛɛ falɛ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ.” Lenpoo kɩka asɩn (Maki

english_verse: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Farasii abi ba naa gyaŋɛ ŋmina kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋʋ mʋ ɔdʋʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa tɔ.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: Ba ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, anɛ naana Mosesi kɩkaapʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kan kili nɩɩ ɔ man kʋʋgɛ nɩɩ o wu mʋ, ɩ dagaa mu‐supu maa bwii kili okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ, kɛ ba kʋʋgɛ nbii sa olewupu mʋ.

english_verse: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla taa aŋasan tɔ tɔwɛ sa asa mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
translation_verse: “Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ, owura kʋ maa waa kɩkyɩɩfɔ kensi‐gyii lala sa mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa.

english_verse: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o sun mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ ba kpee naa tɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ da kɩbaa tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ ba be kensi‐gyii mʋ tɔ, amaa ba kina kɩ ba.

english_verse: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla sun ayaafɔlɛ akʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ‘Fan naa tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ n da kɩbaa tɩɩ mʋ fɛɛ n kʋʋla lʋwɛ. N mɔɔ ɩnaatɛ gbelu yɛ akpensen‐kpesen mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ nfɔ, nɩɩ n kʋʋla kʋtɔ kʋmaa lʋwɛ. Fan ba kɩkyɩɩfɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ tɔ.’

english_verse: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
translation_verse: “Amaa asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da kɩbaa tɩɩ mʋ, ba man tʋwɛ mʋ. Akʋ ba kyʋn bamʋ ndɔɔ ana tɔ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba kyʋn asun tɔ.

english_verse: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ mɔɔ ba kɩtaa owura mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ dayɩ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ.

english_verse: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
translation_verse: Owura mʋ nyɛ agbʋ gaa. Nɩɩ o sun mʋ anaakɔpʋ, nɩɩ ba naa mɔɔ kanɩn amɔɔpʋ maŋa nɩɩ ba tuwa bamʋ kadɛ.

english_verse: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ, ‘N lʋwɛ kɩkyɩɩfɔ kensi‐gyii agyitɔ mʋ, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ n da kɩbaa tɩɩ mʋ, ba man tʋwɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba man dagaa agyitɔ mʋ kigyii.

english_verse: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ fan loo kadɛ tɔ kpee agbenbi sʋ, naa tɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen wu mʋ ba kɩkyɩɩfɔ kensi‐gyii mʋ tɔ.’

english_verse: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
translation_verse: “Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ owura mʋ ba kɩɩ lenbu mʋ tɔ mʋ, o wu ɔnyɩn kʋ maa man bun kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ atɔ bunsa.

english_verse: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ an biti a nu lɩɩ fʋ asɛ yɛ, ɩ dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ a ka lenpoo sa Roma abi Owura Kasaro, abɛɛ a man ka?”

english_verse: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
translation_verse: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔnyɔsapʋ mʋ yɛ ɔsasapʋ gbaa ba kili mʋ, halɩɩ naa fʋʋ osunɔsapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ owura Herodi kamaa tɔ abi kpee Yesu asɛ. Ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ an nyi fʋ maa gyɛ kesintin gyipu, nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ asa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn bɔla kesintin ɔkpa sʋ, yɛgɛ fʋ mɛɛ tʋwɛ lɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ fɛ, nɩɩ fʋ mɛɛ kɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kɩkparɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
translation_verse: Fan taa kederebi mʋ nɩɩ fɛn sʋ fɛn ka lenpoo mʋ kaapʋ mɛ.” Nɩɩ ba taa kederebi mʋ ba sa mʋ.

english_verse: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Owura lala Kasaro.” Ɩmʋ sʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Kasaro lɛɛ mʋ, fan ka sa Kasaro, nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ, fan taa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.”

english_verse: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
translation_verse: Ba maa nu falɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba natɛ kyʋn. Kekili yɛ kekyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ asɩn (Maki

english_verse: At last the woman died.
translation_verse: Laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ mʋ gbaa ba wu.

english_verse: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
translation_verse: Mosesi nbara mʋ kpini yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ asɩn kaapʋsa mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ nbara nnyɔ nɩmʋ sʋ nɩn.” Anɩmʋ mu‐bii ɩ gyɛ Krisito nɩmʋ? (Maki

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Kanɩn kakɛ kʋlʋn maŋa mʋ Sadusi abi akʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ. Sadusi abi ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kaapʋ yɛ kikyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ kʋ man bʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan fɔyɩ ɔkpa! Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan maa man nu Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ fan man nyi Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
translation_verse: Amaa lɩɩ alewupu kikyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ asɩn mʋ, fan man kalɛ wu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ sa fanɛ mʋ nɩn?

english_verse: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
translation_verse: Sakyɔ mʋ maa nu falɛ mʋ, mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan. Nbara mʋ tɔ kɩkparɛ (Maki

english_verse: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
translation_verse: Amaa Farasii abi mʋ ba maa nu fɛɛ, Yesu man yɛgɛ nɩɩ Sadusi abi mʋ ba nyɛ bugi kɔnɔ mʋ, ba tɩɩ abaa gyaŋɛ.

english_verse: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
translation_verse: Ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ tɔ mʋ, kɩmɔmɔ ɩ gyɛ kɩkparɛ?”

english_verse: This is the first and most important commandment.
translation_verse: Kɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ nbara mʋ tɔ kɩgyankpaasa yɛ kɩkparɛ.

english_verse: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ du nɩn fɛɛ kɩmʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ‘Nyɛ kebiti sa fʋ tɔmaa fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.’

english_verse: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
translation_verse: Farasii abi ba maa ba gyaŋɛ mʋ, Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, “kyɩna mɛ kɩbaa gyisa sʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa fʋ dʋn ana waa fʋ ayaa kasɛ.” ’

english_verse: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
translation_verse: “Mɩnɛ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ fan sʋ lɩɩ Krisito mʋ sʋ? Anɩmʋ kanaana‐bii ɩ gyɛ mʋ?” Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩ waa nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu tɔ Dawidi tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ?’ Lɩɩ fɛɛ Dawidi tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Owura Dawidi maa tɩɩ Krisito yɛ, ‘Mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ’ mʋ, nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ bɩla taalɛ waa mʋ kanaana‐bii?”

english_verse: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
translation_verse: Bamʋ ɔkʋ man taalɛ bugi kɔnɔ tɔwɛ asɩn akʋ, nɩɩ lɩɩ kanɩn kakɛ maŋa taa kyʋn, ɔkʋ man bɩla taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ taasɛ mʋ asɩn‐taasɛsa kʋ.

english_verse: And now your temple will be deserted.
translation_verse: Fanɛ kɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɩɩ fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ki biliŋi kɩtakpan.

english_verse: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
translation_verse: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔbɩlɩsa fanɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa gyɛ fanɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Oo! Gyerusalem asa! Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ, nɩɩ fɛn tʋʋ abuu fɛn mɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun fanɛ asɛ mʋ. N kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ saŋa gaalaagaa fɛɛ n bʋga fanɛ‐bii ana, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kakyaasɛ‐nyi ɩɩ bʋga mu‐bii ana ɛɛ waa mʋ atɛɛ tɔ mʋ, amaa fen mee kyula.

english_verse: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ kʋbʋ damantɛ yɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: “Nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi mʋ ii yii anɛ naana Mosesi ayaa tɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ nbara mʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan waa mʋ, fan nu sa bamʋ kɛ fan waa. Amaa fan man waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ be mee buu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ, bɛɛ waa kɛ asa ba nyɛ wu bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ abwaarɛsɩn gyaga ɔwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba taa ɩmʋ mata bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ yɛ abaa sʋ, nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ ɩkaalɛ nnɔ ayolobi bʋnswɩɩsa.

english_verse: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kpee kigyii nsarɛ tɔ abɛɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa tɔ mʋ, ɩkyɩnakpa kparɛ‐kparɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba kyɩna.

english_verse: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bee biti fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyaŋa bamʋ kadɛ tɔ mʋ, ɔ gyɔŋɔ fwaala bamʋ, nɩɩ ba bɩla bee biti asa ba tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ ‘Akaapʋpʋ.’

english_verse: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: “Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ, fan man yɛgɛ ba tɩɩ fanɛ yɛ, ‘Akaapʋpʋ,’ kpalɩ fɛɛ fan sʋ Ɔkaapʋpʋ kʋlʋn nɩn, fanɛ kpini fan gyɛ asupu yɛ adaa nɩn.

english_verse: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Fan man tɩɩ ɔkʋ yɛ ‘Ɔsɛ’ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔsɛ kʋlʋn nɩɩ fan sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ.

english_verse: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
translation_verse: Abɛɛ fan man yɛgɛ ba tɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋ yɛ ‘Ɔkaapʋpʋ’, kpalɩ fɛɛ Ɔkaapʋpʋ kʋlʋn nɩɩ fan sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito mʋ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fen puni akulapu‐kyɩɩ fɛn gyii bamʋ akpaara, yɛgɛ fɛn kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ bʋnswɩɩsa, kɛ asa ba nyɛ wu yɛ fen sun Ɩbwaarɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ mʋ fanɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ kyɔ gaa.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn natɛ nkyu sʋ yɛ ndɛ‐ndɛ sʋ, kɛ fɛn nyɛ kyɛɛgɛ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn ɔ ba sun Ɩbwaarɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen sun mʋ. Amaa nɩɩ fan kan nyɛ mʋ mʋ, fɛn waa ɔsa mʋ ɔ dagaa awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ ɩlɩɩ ɩnyɔ fɛɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ fen du fɛɛ agyaatanapʋ nɩɩ fɛn gyankpaa asa mʋ, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn kaapʋ yɛ, ‘Nɩɩ ɔkʋ taa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ bɔ ntan mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ sɛhn, amaa nɩɩ ɔ kan taa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ suwa bɔ ntan mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa buu ntan mʋ sʋ.’

english_verse: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
translation_verse: Fanɛ agyaatanapʋ melensipu! Suwa mʋ ɩ kyɔ abɛɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ? Nɩɩ man gyɛ suwa mʋ maa bʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ sʋ nɩɩ suwa mʋ biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ abɛɛ?

english_verse: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Nɩɩ ɔkʋ taa saraga kaswɩɩ bɔ ntan mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ sɛhn, amaa nɩɩ ɔ kan taa kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ sʋ bɔ ntan mʋ bɩrɛ, ɩ dagaa maa buu ntan mʋ sʋ.’

english_verse: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
translation_verse: Fanɛ agyaatanapʋ banɩmʋ! Kakɛɛ mʋ ɩ kyɔ abɛɛ saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ? Nɩɩ man gyɛ kakɛɛ mʋ maa gyan saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ abɛɛ?

english_verse: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ bɔ ntan mʋ, ɔ taa kɩmʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ bɔ ntan nɩmʋ.

english_verse: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa sʋsʋ bɔ ntan mʋ, ɔ taa sʋsʋ kuwura‐gya yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ bɔ ntan nɩmʋ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn barɩgɛ fanɛ ɩpʋ atɔ, fɛɛ akatɛ, yɛ gaabu, yɛ akolonku ntun kudu‐kudu, nɩɩ fɛn taa ɩmʋ ketun kʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn fɛn sa Ɩbwaarɛ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ tʋwɛ asɩn kaapʋsa kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ nbara mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kigyii asɩn ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, kʋwɛɛ kuwu, yɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii danbɩrasa. Falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ ɩ dagaa fan maa waa, yɛgɛ fɛn taa ntun kudu sʋ kutun kʋlʋn mʋ fɛn sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
translation_verse: Fanɛ agyaatanapʋ agyankpaapʋ! Fɛn taalɛ fen wu kisusonbi kɛ fen fura mʋ fɔyɩ, amaa fɛn mɩyɛ nyɔɔma.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn fwɩɩ fanɛ ɩlapɛ yɛ nwɛ‐bi nmaa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, yɛgɛ ɩmʋ ɩtɔ tɔ ɩ gyɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan taa kensi payɩ libi yɛ kekisi bɔla ɩmʋ.

english_verse: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
translation_verse: Fanɛ Farasii agyaatanapʋ! Fan gyankpaa fwɩɩ nwɛ‐bi yɛ ɩlapɛ mʋ ɩtɔ tɔ, kɛ ɩmʋ nmaa gbaa ɩ lɩɩ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan du nɩn fɛɛ nkyan mʋ nɩɩ ba pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba fuuli ɩmʋ sʋ parɛparɛ, nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn yɛgɛ ɩmʋ ɩtɔ tɔ bɩrɛ, abuni awuya yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɔɔ ɩ bɔla ɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
translation_verse: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fan du. Asa ansi sʋ mʋ, fan gyɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ana, amaa fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi yɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn wuya ana nɩn.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ Farasii abi, fan gyɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana, fanɛ laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn pɔyɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nkyan, nɩɩ fan lɔŋɔ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ana nkyan sʋ yɛ ɔdan.

english_verse: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Nɩɩ an daa an bʋ tɔ anɛ naana ana saŋa mʋ nɩn, naafɔɔ a mɛɛ yɛ a tii bamʋ sʋ kɛ a mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ.’

english_verse: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
translation_verse: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ ii gyii kesintin lɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ, bamʋ‐bii ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: So keep on doing everything they did.
translation_verse: Kanɩn bɩrɛ fan tiisi lʋwɛ fanɛ naana ana alibi kusun mʋ.

english_verse: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
translation_verse: “Fanɛ anansɛ yɛ anansɛ bamʋ‐bii ana! Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fɛn taalɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ kɩpwɛ kibun yɛ awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen nɩmʋ tɔ?

english_verse: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ɩnɩmʋ kpini kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ii biti ɩ ba ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa sʋ.

english_verse: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩn yɛ fan man bɩla yɛ fan wu mɛ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ.’ ”

english_verse: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
translation_verse: Yesu maa lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ ee kpee mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ bɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ ibu mʋ i du.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Yɛ fan wu atɔ nɩmʋ kpini? N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ halɩɩ kubuu kʋlʋn gbaa mɛɛ yɛ o sii kɩmʋ bɩrɩsa sʋ, kɛ ba man nin kɩmʋ tʋʋ.”

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba ba mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Mɛ, ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ’, kɛ ba pɩna asa gaalaagaa.

english_verse: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Fen biti fan nu ɩkɔ sʋ kusakusa, kɛ fan nu ɩkɔ asɩn kata‐kata, amaa fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa fanɛ. Kanɩn atɔ maŋa ɩ dagaa ɩ maa waa, amaa ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ tɛɛ fʋʋ.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
translation_verse: Yesu maa tɛ olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nkʋn ɩ ba mʋ asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Tɔwɛ anɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ ba? Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ gyɛ fʋ kɩba mʋ yɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ sɩnkaala?”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
translation_verse: Ndɛndɛmandɛ bee biti ba kɔ yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ, kɛ kuwura‐gyii kɩ kɔ yɛ kuwura‐gyii. Akʋn lala yɛ kasɛ kigyingyen ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋ katɩn kʋmaa.

english_verse: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Kanɩn nkɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ, ɩtɔ wuya ana yɛ atalapʋ, bamʋ laakʋ.

english_verse: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini i du nɩn fɛɛ keduŋidi gyankpaasa.

english_verse: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ bee biti ba taa fanɛ sa kɛ ba nyanla fanɛ kɛ ba mɔɔ fanɛ. Nɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ asa kpini bee biti ba kisi fanɛ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
translation_verse: nɩɩ antɩŋɛsa atɔwɛpʋ gaalaagaa bee biti ba ba pɩna asa gaalaagaa.

english_verse: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, ‘Kɩɩ, Krisito mʋ bʋ nfɩɩ’ abɛɛ ‘Mʋ ɩ bʋ falɛ’ mʋ, man kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ gyii.

english_verse: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Fan kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ fanɛ kɩkʋsʋ sɩlɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ man ba waa awɔɔ saŋa, abɛɛ Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ.

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan kan wu kʋtɔ kisisa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ bɩya alibi kɩ maa yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Daneli wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla lɩɩ koo dɩdaa mʋ,

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: saamʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya mʋ ba sɩlɛ kpee abɩɩ tɔ.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɔ bʋ ndɔɔ tɔ mʋ, ɔ man bwii kpee naa taa mʋ kaalɛ.

english_verse: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man kan tɩn nkɛ mʋ sʋ bɩrɛ, naafɔɔ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ ɔ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ, amaa lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ tɩn nkɛ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fan wu ɩnɩmʋ kpini mʋ, fan bɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kɩba mʋ ki tiri tɔ, fɛɛ n ba.

english_verse: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɩna i du Nowa saŋa mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ii biti ɩ baa i du mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ kɩba saŋa.

english_verse: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: “Amaa lɩɩ kakɛ mʋ abɛɛ kerefi mʋ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ waa mʋ ɔkʋ man nyi. Sʋsʋ nbɔɔ ba man nyi, halɩɩ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii mʋ gbaa man nyi, amɔɔ Ɔsɛ mʋ nkʋn.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ pɔyɩ kɛ nkyu i muuri dulinyaa mʋ, asa ba kyɩna ba maa sʋ bee gyii yɛgɛ bee nuu, anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ bee kili, naa fʋʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Nowa loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ,

english_verse: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: yɛgɛ ba man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba, halɩɩ nkyu ɩ ba taa bamʋ kpini kyʋn. Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kɩba mʋ saŋa.

english_verse: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Anyɩn anyɔ bee biti ba baa ba bʋ ndɔɔ tɔ, n biti n taa ɔkʋ kɛ n yɛgɛ ɔkʋ.

english_verse: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
translation_verse: “Kanɩn sʋ fan baa fan da sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan man nyi kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, fanɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ ba.

english_verse: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
translation_verse: Fan baa fen nyi ɩnɩmʋ fɛɛ, nɩɩ kɩkpaara wuya dɩn o nyi kagyanbwɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ oyu ii biti ɔ ba nɩn, naafɔɔ ɔ dɩn kyɩna kun, kɛ oyu mʋ ɔ man ba nyɛ boyi loo mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ yuuri.

english_verse: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan kʋʋla fan gyoo, lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n biti n ba saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fan man kɩɩ mɛ ɔkpa. Kɩyaafɔlɛ danbɩrasa yɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ libi (Luka

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
translation_verse: Amaa a taa fɛɛ kanɩn kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa libi nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti ɔ kyɩna tʋtɔ kyɛɛrɛɛ,’

english_verse: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: “Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ kesintin gyipu yɛ kanyaasɩn wuya? Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ taa mʋ waa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ fɛɛ ɔ sa bamʋ agyitɔ saŋa kyɛɛkyɛɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋrɔn sa kanɩn kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa fɛɛ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ maa ba tu mʋ yɛgɛ ɛɛ waa kanɩn.

english_verse: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ taa mʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ waa mʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
translation_verse: kɛ o piili ɛɛ dayɩ mʋ bɩrɩsa ana ayaafɔlɛ mʋ, yɛgɛ ee gyii ee nuu maa atabʋʋpʋ.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
translation_verse: Kanɩn kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti ɔ ba kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɩɩ mʋ ɔkpa, yɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nyi sʋ.

english_verse: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Ee biti ɔ bɩɩtɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kʋsʋ gaa, kɛ ɔ gya mʋ naa waa kebunbun ansi wuya opula, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba baa bɛɛ saawʋ yɛgɛ bee duŋi bamʋ anobi mʋ.”

english_verse: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ amelensipu anu mʋ ba maa dan taa bamʋ ɩfɩtɩla mʋ, ba man taa fɩtɩla nfɔ kɩtaa tii sʋ.

english_verse: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
translation_verse: Amaa anyaasɩnpʋ anu mʋ bɩrɛ ba kɩtaa fɩtɩla nfɔ ɩpʋrʋntʋwa tɔ tii sʋ.

english_verse: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
translation_verse: “Ɩ maa ba fʋʋ kiyiridi nsana mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kuusi tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ ɩɩ ba! Fan lɩɩ ba gyaŋa mʋ!’

english_verse: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ abite kudu mʋ kpini ba kʋsʋ diyaa bamʋ ɩfɩtɩla.

english_verse: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ amelensipu mʋ ba tɔwɛ anyaasɩnpʋ mʋ yɛ, ‘Fan sa anɛ fanɛ nfɔ mʋ ɩkʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ anɛ ɩfɩtɩla tɔ nfɔ ɩ lʋwɛ.’

english_verse: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ anyaasɩnpʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Ayee! Anɛ gbaa anɛ lɛɛ ɩ man kyɔ kɛ ɩ fʋʋ ana fanɛ. Amɔɔ, fen kpee bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛɛ mʋ asɛ kɛ fan naa sɔɔ.’

english_verse: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
translation_verse: “Amaa ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, n man nyi fanɛ!’ ”

english_verse: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Yesu lʋwɛ yɛ, “Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛn baa fan da sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan man nyi kakɛ abɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n ba.” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ sa bamʋ afulee mʋ (Luka

english_verse: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
translation_verse: “Ɩ maa kyɛɛrɛɛ kamaa tɔ mʋ, bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ naa bwii ba, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ ba ba kaapʋ tɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ agyaga tii afulee mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
translation_verse: Kanɩn bɩrɛ, naafɔɔ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ taa mɛ afulee mʋ naa yɩla afulee ɔyɩlɛkpa, kɛ n maa bwii ba falɛ mʋ, n nyɛ mɛ afulee kibi yɛ agyaga tii sʋ.’

english_verse: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
translation_verse: Yesu bwii bɔ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, “Sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ii biti ɩ baa i du fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa kpee ɔkpa, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ taa mʋ kapatɩyɛ waa bamʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: “Mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ yɩn mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Fʋ waa kusun, fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ danbɩrasa yɛ kesintin gyipu. Fʋ gyii kesintin lɩɩ atɔ pii anɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n taa atɔ damantɛ waa fʋ abaa tɔ kɛ fʋ kɩɩ sʋ. Ba kɛ a bʋga gyii kensi‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ n gyii mʋ!’

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: “Mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ yɩn mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Fʋ waa kusun, fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ danbɩrasa yɛ kesintin gyipu. Fʋ gyii kesintin lɩɩ atɔ pii anɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n taa atɔ damantɛ waa fʋ abaa tɔ kɛ fʋ kɩɩ sʋ. Ba kɛ a bʋga gyii kensi‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ n gyii mʋ!’

english_verse: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n sʋ kufuu nɩɩ n taa afulee mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa waa mɛ abaa tɔ mʋ, naa kuu ɩmʋ pula. Kɩɩ, fu afulee ndee.’

english_verse: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Fʋ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ libi yɛ ɔwʋlɛgyɔpʋ. Fu nyi n maa tɩŋɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n man duu, nɩɩ n kʋʋla n waa ɩbɔɔta tɔ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n man kyaalɛ.

english_verse: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Fan kɔɔlɛ afulee kagbɩn kʋlʋn mʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ, kɛ fɛn taa sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ngbɩn kudu mʋ.

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ, bee biti ba sa mʋ tii sʋ, kɛ ɔ nyɛ naa bansʋ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ, nɩɩ halɩɩ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ gbaa mʋ, bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
translation_verse: Fan taa kɩyaafɔlɛ gyaga mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ kyaarɛ falɛ, naa tʋʋ kitentenbiri tumm mʋ tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ sawʋ kɛ o duŋi mʋ anobi.’ ” Abʋlʋpʋ yɛ nbwɛ

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, “Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n biti n ba yɛ mɛ bʋnyaa, maa sʋsʋ nbɔɔ kpini mʋ, n biti n kyɩna mɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ bʋnyaa tɔ.

english_verse: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
translation_verse: N biti ɩ bɩya ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini ba gyaŋɛ mi ansi tɔ, nɩɩ n biti n barɩgɛ asa mʋ tɔ ntun nnyɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya ɔkpapʋ ɩɩ barɩgɛ nbʋlʋpʋ ɛɛ lɩɩ nbwɛ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
translation_verse: N biti n taa nbʋlʋpʋ yɩlɛ mɛ gyisa sʋ, kɛ n taa nbwɛ mɔɔ yɩlɛ mɛ bɩna sʋ.

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Owura mʋ n biti n tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ mɛ gyisa sʋ mʋ yɛ, ‘Fanɛ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ waa kusee mʋ, fan ba kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩla sa fanɛ lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ.

english_verse: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋn ɩ maa sʋ mɛ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ sa mɛ agyitɔ nɩɩ n gyii, bʋlɩfɔ maa sʋ mɛ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ sa mɛ nkyu nɩɩ n nuu, n maa gyɛ ɔfɔ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ kɔɔlɛ mɛ fanɛ ayɛ.

english_verse: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
translation_verse: N maa man sʋ atɔ bunsa mʋ, fanɛ ɩ sa mɛ nɩɩ n bun, n maa lɔ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ kɩɩ mɛ sʋ, n maa bʋ obu tiisa tɔ mʋ, fanɛ ɩ bɔɔ mɛ sʋ.’

english_verse: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Owura mʋ, n biti n lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan waa sa mɛ asa, halɩɩ bamʋ tɔ opii gbaa mʋ, mɛ nɩɩ fan waa sa.’

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ mɛ bɩna sʋ mʋ yɛ, ‘Fan taa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, fanɛ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ nnɔ waa mʋ. Fan natɛ loo awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ nɩɩ ii mee dun, nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ dɛɛla yɩla Sɩtaanɛ maa mʋ nbɔɔ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋn ɩ maa sʋ mɛ mʋ, fan man sa mɛ agyitɔ nɩɩ n gyii, bʋlɩfɔ maa sʋ mɛ mʋ, fan man sa mɛ nkyu nɩɩ n nuu.

english_verse: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
translation_verse: N maa gyɛ ɔfɔ mʋ, fan man kɔɔlɛ mɛ fanɛ ayɛ, atɔ bunsa ɩ maa tiri mɛ mʋ, fan man sa mɛ nɩɩ n bun, n maa lɔ, abɛɛ n maa bʋ obu tii tɔ mʋ, fan man ba bɔɔ mɛ sʋ.’

english_verse: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: “Ngbaa bamʋ bee biti ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ an wu akʋn ɩ maa sʋ fʋ, abɛɛ bʋlɩfɔ maa sʋ fʋ, abɛɛ fʋ maa gyɛ ɔfɔ, abɛɛ atɔ bunsa ɩ maa tiri fʋ, abɛɛ fʋ maa lɔ, abɛɛ fʋ maa bʋ obu tii tɔ, nɩɩ an man kyɛ fʋ tɔ?’

english_verse: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Owura mʋ n biti n lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin nɩn fɛɛ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan kina kɩwaa sa mɛ asa, halɩɩ bamʋ tɔ opii gbaa mʋ, mɛ nɩɩ fan kina kɩwaa sa.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ mʋ, bee biti ba naa loo kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ kakpaa tɔ, amaa ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ana bɩrɛ bee biti ba kyɩna nkpa kakpaa tɔ.”

english_verse: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen nyi mʋ, ɩ san nkɛ nnyɔ kɛ kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kensi kigyii saŋa mʋ ɔ fʋʋ. Saŋa maa nɩɩ bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sa, kɛ ba da mɛ mata oyu laasa sʋ.”

english_verse: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o bwii ba mʋ abɩɩlapʋ asɛ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan san fan dɛ fɛn fʋʋtɛ nɩn? Fan kɩɩ, saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ ba taa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa alibi awaapʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
translation_verse: naa ŋmina kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kanyaasɩn ɔkpa sʋ kɩtaa Yesu kɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
translation_verse: Amaa ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “A man waa kanɩn kensi‐gyii saŋa nɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, sʋgʋrɛ‐sʋgʋrɛ ii biti ɔ ba kadɛ mʋ tɔ.” Ɔkyɩɩ kʋ kyɛɛgɛ tulale wʋrɩgɛ Yesu sʋ (Maki

english_verse: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”

english_verse: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, ɩ basa bamʋ nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ii nyita tulale maa kanɩn?

english_verse: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba maa bɩɩ ɔlʋn lʋwɛ mʋ, ba kʋsʋ kyʋn olifu kɩbɩɩ sʋ. Yesu tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ Pita ii biti o kina mʋ (Maki

english_verse: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa taa kyʋn mʋ, Gyudasi piili ee biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔla sʋ lɛɛ Yesu sa. Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kesu kensi‐gyii mʋ (Maki

english_verse: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”

english_verse: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
translation_verse: Bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa kigyii kakɛ gyankpaasa mʋ, Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti fɛɛ a naa lɔŋɔ yɩla, kɛ a naa gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kesu kensi‐gyii mʋ?”

english_verse: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
translation_verse: Ba maa gyii mʋ Yesu taa bodobodo, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ɔ gyayɩ kɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan kɔɔlɛ kɛ fan gyii, kɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
translation_verse: Kɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ nkalan mʋ nɩɩ ki ŋmina Ɩbwaarɛ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ sa asa gaalaagaa alibi kɩtaa kɛɛ sʋ.

english_verse: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩn yɛ, n man bɩla yɛ n nuu falɛ nta nɩmʋ naa fʋʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ n maa fanɛ an biti a nuu nta pʋpwɛ mɛ‐Sɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Kagyanbwɛ nɩmʋ fanɛ kpini fen biti fan sɩlɛ yɛgɛ mɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘N biti n mɔɔ nbʋlʋpʋ ɔkpapʋ mʋ, kɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ ba yaasɛ tɔ.’

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin yɛ, kagyanbwɛ nɩmʋ pɔyɩ kɛ kyaasɛ ɔ bʋn mʋ, fii biti fu kina mɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa.”

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Gesemani, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan kyɩna nfɩɩ kɛ n kpee falɛ naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.”

english_verse: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ ɩɩ kɔ mɛ lewu‐lewu, halɩɩ ii biti ɩ mɔɔ mɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kyɩna nfɩɩ tii mɛ sʋ kɛ fan man dɛ.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Halɩɩ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ sɩlɛ yɛgɛ fʋ gbaa, mɛɛ mɛ n mɛɛ yɛ n sɩlɛ yɛgɛ fʋ.”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Halɩɩ bee biti ba mɔɔ n maa fʋ gbaa n mɛɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ kanɩn kpini‐kpini.” Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ abanban mʋ gbaa ba tɔwɛ kanɩn. Yesu kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ Gesemani (Maki

english_verse: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
translation_verse: Yesu bɩla bwii kpee ɩlɩɩ nyɔsapʋ naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, “Mɛ‐Sɛ, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ waa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ lɛɛ falɛ awʋrʋfɔ kɔɔpʋ nɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n gyii awʋrʋfɔ maa mʋ, yɛgɛ fu kebiti kɩ waa kanɩn.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ o nin kpee ansi tɔ gbɛrɛɛ naa kyʋn bun ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, “Mɛ‐Sɛ, nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa mʋ, lɛɛ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ ba mɛ sʋ falɛ lɩɩ mɛ sʋ. Amaa yɛgɛ ɩ waa fu kebiti, man gyɛ mi kebiti.”

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
translation_verse: Fan kʋsʋ kɛ a kpee. Fan kɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa mʋ tiri tɔ.” Ba kɩtaa Yesu (Maki

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa Pita maa Sebedi mu‐bii ana anyɔ buu mʋ n‐yɩɩ kpee. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ nyita mʋ.

english_verse: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o bwii ba abɩɩlapʋ asa mʋ asɛ ba tu ba maa dɛ nsɩɩrɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ Pita yɛ, “Amɔɔ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ kyɩna yɛ mɛ kerefi kʋlʋn yɛgɛ fan man dɛ?

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Fan kyɩna fan man dɛ, yɛgɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, kɛ fan man tɩyɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ tɔ. Fanɛ n‐yaayu ii biti ɩ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn, amaa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ‐wʋlɛ pɔɔ.”

english_verse: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ o kpee naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kɩsasapʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn kʋlʋn maŋa.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kyɛmɩnɛ, waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ ba kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ.” Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba kyʋn naa kɩtaa Yesu tɔ kinkin.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
translation_verse: Maa san ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyudasi mʋ ba tʋtɔ. Asa damantɛ akʋ ba buu mʋ, yɛgɛ ba sʋ isakunde yɛ agulungu. Aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ asa mʋ abɩlɩsa i sun bamʋ.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss.”
translation_verse: Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ Yesu sa mʋ, wʋla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ n dan naa baari mʋ tɔ mʋ, mʋ nɩmʋ, fan kɩtaa mʋ.”

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
translation_verse: Ba maa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ opula tɔ Gyudasi kpee Yesu asɛ nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, n fwaala fʋ.” Nɩɩ o baari Yesu tɔ.

translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ, asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Yesu asɛ mʋ ɔkʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ sakunde lɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ ŋɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋsʋ tɩn.

english_verse: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Bwiiyaa fʋ sakunde mʋ waa kɩmʋ kikufe tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩtaa sakunde ɛɛ kɔ mʋ, sakunde lewu nɩɩ ee biti o wu.

english_verse: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩtaa mʋ kaalɛ kyatɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Nbusaa asɩn! A man bɩla an biti adansɛ kʋ. Fan nu nbusaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu man tɔwɛ sɛhn. Nɩɩ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ bwii tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “N bɔ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kakpaa mʋ ntan yɛ tɔwɛ anɛ. Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii mʋ?”

english_verse: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Maa lɩɩ kpee kewusunɔ mʋ, nɩɩ kibite kʋ mɔɔ bɩla wu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ daa o tii Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ sʋ nɩn.”

english_verse: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Yesu mʋ ba taa mʋ kpee Kefasi, oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ asa mʋ abɩlɩsa ba gyaŋɛ mʋ.

english_verse: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
translation_verse: Amaa Pita buu bamʋ kɩyaa‐kɩyaa, halɩɩ ba naa fʋʋ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ. Nɩɩ Pita sii kyɩna kɩkpaara mʋ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ asɛ, kɛ o wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ naa lʋwɛ.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
translation_verse: Aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa amu‐amu ngyaŋɛ mʋ kpini ba tɔwɛ kɔnɔ waa Yesu bee biti antɩŋɛsa ba tɩn gyaga mʋ sʋ, kɛ ba nyɛ ɔkpa mɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
translation_verse: Antɩŋɛsa adansɛ agyipu gaalaagaa ba ba tɔwɛ, amaa ba man nyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa gyaga mʋ sʋ. Kamaa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ anyɩn anyɔ akʋ ba lɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
translation_verse: “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘N taalɛ boyi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ nɩmʋ, kɛ n taa nkɛ nsa bwii pɔyɩ kɩmʋ.’ ”

english_verse: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ mʋ. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩn yɛ, lɩɩ ndaga ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, fen biti fan wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n maa tɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Owura lala mʋ gyisa sʋ, nɩɩ fen biti fan bɩla wu maa lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ sʋsʋ n ba.”

english_verse: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ waa mu ansi tɔ nɩɩ ba dayɩ mʋ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba kpa mʋ ɩtan.

english_verse: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Krisito, nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ, bɩɩ tɔwɛ anɛ, ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da fʋ falɛ?” Pita kyɛɛ yɛ ɔ man nyi Yesu (Maki

english_verse: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita bɔ ntan kyɛɛ yɛ, “N man nyi ɔnyɩn maŋa gbaa kpini‐kpini.”

english_verse: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Pita maa tɛ kɩkpaara mʋ, nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ kibite kʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ngbaa fʋ, fu tii Yesu, Kagalile‐nyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
translation_verse: Amaa Pita kyɛɛ bamʋ kpini ansi tɔ yɛ, “Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ falɛ, n man nyi ɩmʋ sʋ gbaa kpini‐kpini.”

english_verse: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Ɩ maa waa gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ ba tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, “Kesintin tɔ mʋ, fʋ gyɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ katɔwɛ tɔ ɩɩ kaapʋ kanɩn.”

english_verse: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ nnɔ gyaga mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɔ ntan tɔwɛ yɛ, “N man nyi ɔnyɩn maa kpini‐kpini.” Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ kyaasɛ bʋn.

english_verse: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita nyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu daa tɔwɛ mʋ mʋ, fɛɛ “Pɔyɩ kɛ kyaasɛ ɔ bʋn mʋ, fii biti fu kina mɛ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa.” Nɩɩ Pita lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ naa su gaa.

english_verse: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ o nyi ɩ maa gyɛ kayɩɩ basa sʋ, nɩɩ ba taa Yesu waa mʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
translation_verse: Ba maa naa bee kpee mʋ, ba gyaŋa Simon Kesirene‐nyɩn, nɩɩ ba nyan mʋ yɛ ɔ sʋla Yesu oyu laasa mʋ.

english_verse: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “N waa alibi fɛɛ n maa lɛɛ ɔmʋnɩ ɔ man waa alibi akʋ mʋ sa fanɛ.” Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ man gyɛ anɛ asɩn. Fʋ nkʋn asɩn nɩn.”

english_verse: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba tɔwɛ kyula abaa nɩɩ ba taa afulee maŋa naa sɔɔ nlobi apɔyɩpʋ kʋ kasɛ, kɛ ba baa bee pula afɔ‐kyilim tʋtɔ.

english_verse: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ tʋtɔ yɛ, Nkalan kasɛ ba lɩɩ halɩɩ ndaga.

english_verse: So the words of the prophet Jeremiah came true, “They took the thirty silver coins, the price of a person among the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyeremiya tɔwɛ mʋ ba tɔ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba taa gbiti nderebi adɩsa mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ afulee mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ ba taa sɔɔ mʋ mʋ, kɔnɔ.

english_verse: They paid it for a potter's field, as the Lord had commanded me.” Pilate Questions Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taa ɩmʋ sɔɔ nlobi apɔyɩpʋ kʋ kasɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ sa kɔnɔ mʋ.” Paleti taasɛ Yesu asɩn (Maki

english_verse: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa yɛgɛ Yesu yɩlɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Paleti ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyiwu abi owura lala mʋ?” Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɛɛhn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ kadɛ mʋ abɩlɩsa bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɔ man lɛɛ kɔnɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu man lɛɛ kɔnɔ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ tɔ kʋkʋlʋn gbaa. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin Paleti gaa. Ba bun Yesu lewu kʋpwɛ (Maki

english_verse: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kyʋn mʋ, bɛɛ sʋ asebi bɛɛ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ sʋ yɛgɛ bee fi‐fi bamʋ amu,

english_verse: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
translation_verse: Paleti maa wu fɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ waa asɩn mʋ sɛhn mʋ, yɛgɛ ɩ sʋ sʋgʋrɛ‐sʋgʋrɛ ɩɩ ba mʋ, ɔ taa nkyu fwɩɩ mʋ abaa sakyɔ mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ abaa ɩ man tii kanyɩn nɩmʋ lewu sʋ, ɩ gyɛ fanɛ asɩn nɩn!”

english_verse: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
translation_verse: Asa mʋ kpini ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Yɛgɛ mu lewu kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ɩ baa ɩ gyan anɛ yɛ anɛ‐bii ana sʋ!”

english_verse: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
translation_verse: nɩɩ ba taa iwii bɔɔ fɛɛ kuwura‐wʋrʋ gyaga mu kumu sʋ, nɩɩ ba taa owura kikpebi waa mʋ gyisa kɩbaa tɔ, nɩɩ ba muŋa mu ansi tɔ nɩɩ ba ŋmasɛ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Gyiwu abi Owura! An muŋa fʋ”

english_verse: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
translation_verse: Ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii mʋ. Fan yɛgɛ a kɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.”

english_verse: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Ba maa ŋmasɛ mʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba bugi kaalɛ pɩpɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba bwii taa mʋ atɔ bunsa mʋ bun mʋ. Nɩɩ ba taa mʋ bee kpee ba naa da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ. Ba da Yesu mata oyu laasa sʋ (Maki

english_verse: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba sa Yesu nta mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kɩfɛ bʋlɩlɩsa kʋ kyagɩlɛ tɔ, maa daa kɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ o kina kinuu.

english_verse: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
translation_verse: Ba maa da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba tʋʋ nbii barɩgɛ mʋ atɔ bunsa tɔ.

english_verse: Then they sat down to guard him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kyʋn kyɩna bee kun mʋ.

english_verse: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
translation_verse: Ba kyʋrɔɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bun mʋ kʋpwɛ mʋ gyaga mu amu sʋ yɛ, ƆNƖMƲ Ɩ GYƐ YESU, GYIWU ABI OWURA MƲ.

english_verse: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
translation_verse: yɛgɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ yɛ fii biti fʋ boyi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ, kɛ fʋ pɔyɩ kɩmʋ nkɛ nsa mʋ, mɔlɩgɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ! Nɩɩ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii‐nyɩnsa mʋ, kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ!”

english_verse: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
translation_verse: Asansa mʋ ba yɛ, “Nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan yɛgɛ mʋ, kɛ a kɩɩ fɛɛ Eligya ee biti ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ mʋ abɛɛ?”

english_verse: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kyɔwɛ amu nsana naa fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛ kerefi asa tɔ mʋ, kitentenbiri bun ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini.

english_verse: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kerefi asa maŋa tɔ mʋ, Yesu kuusi kenken yɛ, “Eli, Eli lamaa sabatani?” Ɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Mɛ‐Sɛ, mɛ‐Sɛ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fu kina mɛ?”

english_verse: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ sɩlɛ naa taa ɔkyɛɛ dufudufu taa naa nyɔ nta nyaŋɛsa kʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ŋmina oyu sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa naa tii Yesu kɔnɔ yɛ o nuu.

english_verse: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa kɩtaa kunsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ barɩgɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, kyatɛ tɔ anyɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba yii kasɛ. Nɩɩ kasɛ gyingyen, nɩɩ afʋlɛ ɩ baaŋɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
translation_verse: Nkyan i bugi‐bugi tɔ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu mʋ, gaalaagaa ba kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

english_verse: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, ba tii Yesu sʋ kpee kadɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba wu bamʋ.

english_verse: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
translation_verse: Anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa ɔbɩlɩsa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mʋ asɛ bee kun Yesu mʋ, ba maa wu kasɛ maa gyingyen, yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa maŋa kpini mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kesintin tɔ, ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii nɩn!”

english_verse: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
translation_verse: Akyɩɩ gaalaagaa ba bʋ tʋtɔ ba yɩlɛ kata‐kata bɛɛ kɩɩ. Bamʋ ɩ kyɩna ba gyɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu lɩɩ Galile nɩɩ ba kyɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
translation_verse: O kpee Paleti asɛ naa kʋlɛ Yesu kubuni, nɩɩ Paleti sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa sa mʋ.

english_verse: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
translation_verse: Gyosefu taa kibuni mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kɩkalala pʋpwɛ maalɛ mʋ,

english_verse: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
translation_verse: nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ pula mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ kakyan pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ŋɛ kɩfʋlɛ waa ɔbɔ yɩla mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ o gbiliti kubuu lala kʋ tii kakyan mʋ kɔnɔ nɩɩ ɔ natɛ kyʋn.

english_verse: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
translation_verse: Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen maa Mɛɛrɩ banban mʋ, be sii bɛ tɛ, yɛgɛ bamʋ ansi ɩ kaapʋ kakyan mʋ. Kakyan mʋ akunpu

english_verse: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ maa gyɛ Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Farasii abi mʋ ba kpee Paleti asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
translation_verse: “Ɔbɩlɩsa! An nyiŋi fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔpɩnapʋ mʋ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, n biti n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ.’

english_verse: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ wʋla sa kɔnɔ kɛ asa ba naa kyɩna kun mʋ kakyan mʋ naa fʋʋ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ bee biti ba be yuuri mu kibuni mʋ, kɛ ba tɔwɛ asa yɛ o kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba kanɩn mʋ, mʋ npɩna mʋ ii biti ɩ ŋara ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla pɩna falɛ.”

english_verse: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Paleti lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan taa anaakɔpʋ, kɛ ba naa yɩlɛ kun kakyan mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen nyi fɛɛ fɛn taalɛ waa mʋ.”

english_verse: So they sealed it tight and placed soldiers there to guard it.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kpee kakyan mʋ asɛ naa waa kʋtɔ kʋ tarɩ kubuu mʋ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ yɩla yɛ ba kun kɩmʋ.

english_verse: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ kudu ɔkʋ mʋ ba kpee kɩbɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu wʋla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba kpee mʋ.

english_verse: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
translation_verse: Aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ abɩlɩsa ba maa ba gyaŋɛ mʋ, ba ŋmina yɩla kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba waa. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba sa anaakɔpʋ akunpu mu afulee kʋkyɔ,

english_verse: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
translation_verse: kɛ fan kaapʋ bamʋ kɛ ba buu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n sa fanɛ kɔnɔ yɛ fan waa mʋ. Fan nyiŋi fɛɛ n bʋ fanɛ asɛ saŋa kʋmaa naa fʋʋ dulinyaa laalaalʋwɛ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan man waa kufuu! Fan kpee naa tɔwɛ mɛ bɩrɩsa ana yɛ ba kpee Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba wu mɛ.” Akunpu mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen gaa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba gbiliti kubuu mʋ nɩɩ ki tii kakyan mʋ kɔnɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn kyɩna kɩmʋ sʋ.

english_verse: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ, nɩɩ mʋ atɔ bunsa mɔɔ ii fuuli parɛparɛ fɛɛ ateebi.

english_verse: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bee kun kakyan mʋ, ba maa wu kabɔɔ mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba kyɩkyaa tɩyɛ fɛɛ asa wusa mʋ.

english_verse: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ akyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Fan man waa kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ n nyi fan maa kɩɩ fen biti Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ba da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mʋ nɩn.

english_verse: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
translation_verse: Fan bwii bilen naa tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, ‘O kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa fanɛ ee kpee Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ fen biti fan wu mʋ.’ Fan nu, n tɔwɛ fanɛ nɩmʋ.”

english_verse: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
translation_verse: Opula tɔ nɩɩ Yesu gyaŋa bamʋ ɔkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “N fwaala fanɛ.” Nɩɩ ba nin tiri mʋ tɔ, kɩtaa mʋ ayaa tɔ muŋa sun mʋ.

english_verse: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
translation_verse: Akyɩɩ mʋ ba maa san ba bʋ ɔkpa tɔ bee kpee mʋ, kakyan mʋ akunpu mʋ akʋ ba kpee Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ naa buu sa aseepu abɩlɩsa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa.

english_verse: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
translation_verse: lan tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ɩ ba kagyanbwɛ ba yuuri Yesu kibuni mʋ taa kyʋn, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an dɛ nsɩɩrɛ mʋ.’

english_verse: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ afulee mʋ, nɩɩ ba waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba waa mʋ. Nɩɩ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ yaasɛ loo Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ ba lɩɩ halɩɩ ndaga. Yesu taa kusun lala mʋ waa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ abaa tɔ (Maki

english_verse: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu nin tiri bamʋ, tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ taa sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ sʋ ɔlʋn kpini sa mɛ.

english_verse: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kpee dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa, naa waa asa kpini mɛ abɩɩlapʋ. Fan gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, lɩɩ Ɔsɛ yɛ Obii yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ,

english_verse: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
translation_verse: Ba maa wu Yesu mʋ, ba muŋa sun mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ yɛ man gyɛ mʋ nɩn.

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ asewu mʋ yɩla, nɩɩ ba buu Yesu.

english_verse: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
translation_verse: Yesu maa bɩla kpee mu ansi tɔ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, ɔ bɩla wu Gyemisi maa mu‐supu Gyɔn, Sebedi mu‐bii ana, ba bʋ bamʋ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ bɛɛ lɔŋɔ bamʋ asewu.

english_verse: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ, Yesu asɩn nɩmʋ a tɛɛ naa nu Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kpini gyaŋɛ. Yesu Kyɛ Asa Damantɛ (Matiwu

english_verse: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
translation_verse: Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ i piili fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya wʋla kyʋrɔɔ yɩla mʋ. Ɔ kyʋrɔɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla tɔwɛ Mu‐Bii Yesu Krisito mʋ yɛ, “Kɩɩ, n biti n sun mɛ kabɔɔ kɛ ɔ gyankpaa fʋ, mʋ ii biti ɔ ba lɔŋɔ fʋ ɔkpa mʋ.”

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Nyɔɔma ifuu nɩɩ ba taa lʋʋ Gyɔn kaalɛ bunsa, nɩɩ atɔbwaaya ɔwʋlɛ mɔɔ nɩɩ ɔ kyaŋɛ waa mʋ kɩrɩsʋ. Mʋ agyitɔ gyisa mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ ntuntunbi yɛ ŋmasan.

english_verse: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
translation_verse: Gyɔn ɩɩ tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ, “Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ kamaa ɛɛ ba mʋ, kyɔ mɛ gaa, nɩɩ n man dagaa gbaa fɛɛ n muŋa lɛɛ mʋ asɩbɩta.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
translation_verse: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba natɛ ba Kapanum kadɛ tɔ. Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ maa fʋʋ mʋ, Yesu kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa naa kaapʋ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ ba maa nu mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan, lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ sʋ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ kɩ kwɩɩ yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ, Yesu lɩɩ Nasareti kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Galile kasɛ sʋ mʋ ba Gyɔn asɛ. Nɩɩ Gyɔn gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
translation_verse: Yesu maa dan lɩɩ ɔbʋn mʋ tɔ yɩlɛ mʋ, o wu sʋsʋ maa bugi tɔ, nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ kuwura‐lɔpɔ ba gyɔŋɔ Yesu sʋ.

english_verse: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ, bootipu kʋ ba muŋa Yesu ayaa tɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fʋ kɩpɩrɛ tɔ mʋ, fɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.”

english_verse: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki tiri tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa kɛ fan kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ gyii.”

english_verse: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Yesu maa dan wu bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba bwiiyaa bamʋ‐sɛ Sebedi maa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛgɛ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ, nɩɩ ba natɛ buu Yesu. Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ Nbwɩɩ Libi (Luka

english_verse: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu loo ɔkyɩɩ mʋ asɛ obu‐to, kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, kʋsaa mʋ kyɩna. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya, nɩɩ ɔ waa agyitɔ kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ.

english_verse: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
translation_verse: Ba maa naa wu mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ naa ee biti fʋ.”

english_verse: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
translation_verse: Bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, kuusi kenken taasɛ Yesu yɛ,

english_verse: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: “Yesu, Kanasareti‐nyɩn, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti anɛ asɛ nfɩɩ? Fʋ ba kɛ fʋ ba nyita anɛ nɩn, abɛɛ nɛnɛ? N nyi ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ. Fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Nyɩmɩsa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu pʋntɛ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ sʋ yɛ, “Laatɔ, kɛ fʋ kʋsʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ ki gyingyen ɔnyɩn mʋ, nɩɩ o kuusi kenken kʋsʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
translation_verse: Kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ waa asa mʋ kɩyan, nɩɩ bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Mɩnɛ asɩn ndee? Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ bɩla ɩ gyɛ kɩkaapʋ pʋpwɛ kʋ nɩn? Kɩɩ, ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ pʋntɛ nbwɩɩ libi sʋ nɩɩ bee nu mʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan yɛgɛ kɛ a kpee ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tiri nfɩɩ mʋ, kɛ n nyɛ naa tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn tʋtɔ. Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ asɩn ɩ waa Yesu kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa mata mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩ gyɛ mɛ kɩpɩrɛ tɔ, ɩmʋ sʋ fʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ lɔŋɔ sʋ.”

english_verse: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ kpee Simon maa Andiruwu kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ Gyemisi maa Gyɔn gbaa ba tii sʋ.

english_verse: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Simon mʋ‐saa‐kyɩɩsa ɩɩ lɔ kibirima ɔ dɛ kalan sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu maa loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba buu sa mʋ yɛ ɔkyɩɩ maŋa ɩɩ lɔ.

english_verse: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
translation_verse: Kalaatɩpwɛɛ maŋa kyɔwɛ maa dan ɛɛ tɩyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ asa ba taa alɔpʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ bamʋ sʋ bɩya Yesu.

english_verse: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
translation_verse: Kadɛ mʋ asa kpini ba ba gyaŋɛ kɩkpaara maŋa kebunɔ.

english_verse: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
translation_verse: Yesu kyɛ asa gaalaagaa alobi, nɩɩ ɔ gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ asa gaalaagaa sʋ. Nbwɩɩ libi mʋ maa nyi Yesu sʋ mʋ, ɔ man sa bamʋ ɔkpa yɛ ba tɔwɛ kʋtɔ kʋ. Yesu Tɔwɛ Asɩn Galile (Luka

english_verse: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
translation_verse: Kyaasɛ gyankpaasa maa dan bʋn mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu kʋsʋ lɩɩ kɩkpaara kpee opula mʋ nɩɩ ki du surum, kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ.

english_verse: Simon and the others started looking for him.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ, Simon maa mʋ bɩrɩsa ana ba naa bee biti Yesu.

english_verse: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ natɛ Galile ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kpini gyaŋɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, yɛgɛ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi ɛɛ lɩɩ asa sʋ ɛɛ sa bamʋ. Yesu Kyɛ Ɔnyɩn kʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ, nɩɩ kibootoo kɩ yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
translation_verse: Pɔyɩ kɛ Yesu ɔ yɛgɛ ɔ natɛ mʋ, ɔ da mu kumu tɔ yɛ,

english_verse: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
