translation_verse: “Man kan waa kɛ fʋ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ sa ɔkʋ. Amaa taa fʋ n‐yɩɩ naa kaapʋ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu, kɛ ɔ taa fʋ atɔ waa saraga sa fʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi kaapʋ yɩla mʋ, kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ o wu fɛɛ fʋ kʋlɔ mʋ kɩ lʋwɛ.”

english_verse: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ dan lɩɩ kpee katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɛɛ da kibonbon ɛɛ sa asa yɛ Yesu ɩ kyɛ mʋ kibootoo. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Yesu man bɩla ɛɛ nyɛ ee loo kadɛ kʋ tɔ tentengilen. O kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ i du diin, yɛgɛ asa bɛɛ lɩɩ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Abiyata kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ, Owura Dawidi loo Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa obu mʋ tɔ naa taa bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɛɛ saraga sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ wɛ, nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ kamaa tɔ abi, yɛgɛ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba wɛ ɩmʋ, amɔɔ aseepu mʋ nkʋn.”

english_verse: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa kʋkyɔ ba ba bɔla tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ɔkpa man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ, halɩɩ kebunɔ gbaa, yɛgɛ Yesu ɩ tɔwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn.

english_verse: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa ana akʋ ba sʋla mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ‐nyɩn boobi kʋ bɛɛ bɩya Yesu.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
translation_verse: Amaa sakyɔ sʋ mʋ, ba man nyɛ ɔkpa, nɩɩ ba taa ɔnyɩn mʋ dii obu mʋ sʋsʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ɩsɛ nyalɛ munla mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba foti ɔbɔ opula mʋ nɩɩ Yesu bʋ mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ, nɩɩ ba kpɩlɩgaa ɔlɔpʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ dɛ mʋ kalan sʋ ba yɩla kasɛ.

english_verse: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
translation_verse: “Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nbusaa asɩn nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ falɛ? Anɩmʋ ɩɩ taalɛ taa alibi ɛɛ kɛɛ tii Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
translation_verse: N biti n kaapʋ fanɛ yɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n sʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n taalɛ taa alibi kɛɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ.” Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: “Mɛ ɩ tɔwɛ fʋ fɛɛ, kʋsʋ taa fʋ kalan kɛ fu kpewu.”

english_verse: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
translation_verse: Yesu bwii kpee Galile ɔbʋn‐kaa, nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ.

english_verse: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, “Nyɩmɩsa kɩdanbɩrasa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ, man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa nɩɩ ba waa sa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ sa.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ, Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba tɛ bee gyii Lewi kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ damantɛ ba buu mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ gaalaagaa ba be tii mʋ sʋ bee gyii agyitɔ.

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Farasii abi mʋ, ba maa wu fɛɛ Yesu maa alibi awaapʋ yɛ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ ba maa gyii mʋ, ba taasɛ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ sʋ nɩɩ maa falɛ asa ɩ tɛ bee gyii?”

english_verse: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Gyɔn Okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ yɛ Farasii abi bee ŋmina kɔnɔ. Nɩɩ asa akʋ ba kpee Yesu asɛ naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn Okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ yɛ Farasii abi abɩɩlapʋ bee ŋmina kɔnɔ, nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ bɩrɛ be mee ŋmina?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ asa bɛɛ taalɛ ŋmina kɔnɔ yɛgɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli san ɔ bʋ bamʋ asɛ? Ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ waa kanɩn. Fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli san bʋ bamʋ asɛ falɛ.

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa kʋ ɩɩ ba nɩɩ bee biti ba taa ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ. Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba ŋmina kɔnɔ.

english_verse: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: “Ba mɛɛ taa kikyibi pʋpwɛ bɛɛ mata kaalɛ dɩdaa ɔbɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kii biti kɩ kyatɛ kaalɛ mʋ tii sʋ.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
translation_verse: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taa nta pʋpwɛ waa kʋtɔbwaaya ɔwʋlɛ taakpe dɩdaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa kanɩn, nɩɩ nta mʋ ɩɩ bɩlɛ mʋ, ii biti ɩ kyatɛ taakpe dɩdaa mʋ nyita, kɛ nta mʋ ɩ kyɛɛgɛ fɔyɩ. Amaa bɛɛ taa nta pʋpwɛ waa taakpe pʋpwɛ tɔ, kɛ ɩmʋ anyɔ mʋ ɩ man nyita.” Kakɛ Fʋʋtɛsa Asɩn (Matiwu

english_verse: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
translation_verse: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kʋ, nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba naa alʋn kadɔɔ kʋ tɔ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba piili bɛɛ gyayɩ alʋn mʋ ɩkʋ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ ba yɛ, “Kɩɩ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɛɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan man pɩɩta kalɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Owura Dawidi maa mʋ kamaa tɔ abi ba waa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ akʋn a mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ nɩn?

english_verse: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n kyɔ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa mʋ gbaa.”

english_verse: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
translation_verse: Sakyɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ ba lɔŋɔ ɔkʋlɩ yɩla mʋ, kɛ ba man nyɛ nin tiri mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Asa akʋ mɔɔ ba bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ bee biti kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ asɩn akʋ taa pwɩɩ Yesu. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba tɛ bee gyoo fɛɛ Yesu ii biti ɔ kyɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ, abɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ kyɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ nfɩɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ bamʋ kyaabɔɔ agbʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ kɔ mʋ gaa lɩɩ bamʋ kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, “Tɛɛŋɛ fʋ kɩbaa.” Nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛŋɛ mʋ kɩbaa, nɩɩ ki kyiŋi.

english_verse: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
translation_verse: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ kpee Galile ɔbʋn ɔkaa, nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba lɩɩ Galile yɛ Gyudeya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ ba buu mʋ.

english_verse: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
translation_verse: Asa kudu anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ atɩɩ ndee. Simon .

english_verse: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kpee kɩbɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti, nɩɩ ba ba mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
translation_verse: Bamʋ akʋ ɩ gyɛ Andiruwu yɛ Filipu yɛ Batolome yɛ Matiwu yɛ Tomasi yɛ Alifeyo mu‐bii Gyemisi yɛ Simon Seloti,

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem mʋ akʋ ba yɛ, “Yesu sʋ Beelisebu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ nbwɩɩ libi kumu mʋ ɔlʋn. Mʋ ɩɩ sa mʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi falɛ.”

english_verse: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa mʋ, asa ba kyɩna kyaabɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kɩɩ fu‐nyi yɛ fu‐supu ana ba bʋ nbwama bee biti fʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu tɩɩ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kɩŋasan tɔ yɛ, “Ɔkpa mɔɔ sʋ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ɩ taalɛ gya Sɩtaanɛ?

english_verse: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii kpee kewu tɔ. Nɩɩ sakyɔ bɩla ba mʋ asɛ, halɩɩ maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba man nyɛ saŋa kɛ ba gyii.

english_verse: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ asa ba kan ba barɩgɛ tɔ bɛɛ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa i boyi.

english_verse: And a family that fights won't last long either.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩkpaara sʋ asa ba kan bɛɛ barɩgɛ tɔ bɛɛ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kɩkpaara maŋa i boyi.

english_verse: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ kuwura‐gyii kɩ kan kɩ barɩgɛ tɔ kɩɩ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kɩɩ tɩyɛ kakpaa.

english_verse: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ loo ɔlʋnpʋ kɩkpaara sʋ naa swii mʋ atɔ natɛ. Amɔɔ ɩ dagaa maa gyankpaa ŋmina mʋ pɔyɩ, kɛ ɔ nyɛ ɔkpa loo mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ yuuri mʋ atɔ.

english_verse: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
translation_verse: N gyii fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ taa nyɩmɩsa alibi kʋmaa yɛ mʋ kɩtɔwɛ yii mʋ kʋmaa ɛɛ kɛɛ.

english_verse: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
translation_verse: Amaa nyɩmɩsa kɩtɔwɛ yii Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa kɛɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ waa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ kakpaa nɩn.”

english_verse: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋ bɛɛ yɛ ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi. Yesu mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana ba ba yɩla nbwama, nɩɩ ba sun mʋ asɛ yɛ ba tɩɩ mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana?”

english_verse: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ gyaŋɛ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ! Banɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Mi‐nyi yɛ mi‐supu ana.

english_verse: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐supu, yɛ mɛ‐daa, yɛ mɛ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ yɛ mi‐nyi.”

english_verse: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla piili ɛɛ kaapʋ Galile ɔbʋn kaa, nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba ba kyaabɔɔ mʋ bɛɛ nu mʋ asɛ. Sakyɔ sʋ mʋ, o loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan nkyu sʋ ɔbʋn kaa, yɛgɛ asa ba yɩla kidenbi sʋ.

english_verse: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ŋmatɛ ayu mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ ɔkpa kaa, nɩɩ nbwiibi ba ba tasɛ gyii.

english_verse: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa aŋasan tɔ kaapʋ bamʋ atɔ damantɛ. Mʋ kɩkaapʋ tɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: “Fan nu, ɔdɔɔpʋ kʋ ɩ kyɩna kpee ɔ naa ŋmatɛ ayu.

english_verse: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ kyɔwɛ maa da mʋ, ɩ bwɩɩ, nɩɩ i wu lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ man sʋ ilin.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ, nɩɩ iwii mʋ ɩ dan nyan ɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man talɛ sɔyɩ abi.

english_verse: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ fɛɛ, “Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nɩɩ an biti a taa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ waa sɩnkaala? Mɩnɛ kɩŋasan nɩɩ an biti a taa kaapʋ tɔ?

english_verse: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
translation_verse: Ɩ maa ba san Yesu nkʋn mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa mʋ akʋ ba ba taasɛ mʋ kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
translation_verse: Amaa ɩ maa mee loo bamʋ nkɔlɔ kasɛ tɔ sʋ mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ bamʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ abɛɛ kanyanla dan ba bamʋ sʋ lɩɩ abwaarɛsɩn sʋ mʋ, bɛɛ yɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kesun.

english_verse: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ, ‘bee biti ba kɩɩ kɛ ba kɩɩ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu, bee biti ba nu, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nu kasɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn sʋ, naafɔɔ ba tee bwii ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, kɛ ɔ taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ.’ ” Yesu lɛɛ ɔdɔɔpʋ kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ (Matiwu

english_verse: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
translation_verse: Ɔdɔɔpʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ abwaarɛsɩn ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

english_verse: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, “Be mee tuwa fɩtɩla kɛ ba taa waa kolobi tɔ, abɛɛ ba taa waa npaa kasɛ. Bee tuwa fɩtɩla ba sʋ bɛɛ gyaga kɩmʋ ɔgyagakpa nɩn.

english_verse: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ fɛɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii du nɩn fɛɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ŋmatɛ ayu mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ.

english_verse: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
translation_verse: Kasɛ mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ ayu mʋ ɩɩ dan nɩɩ ɩɩ kɔyɩ. Oyu mʋ ɩ gyankpaa kɔyɩ kɛ ɩ nyɛ ɔtɔ kɛ ɩ kʋʋgɛ abi.

english_verse: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɩ dan mʋ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ ɩ taa deebi naa tɩŋɛ ɩmʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ saŋa fʋʋ.” Sinapi kibi kɩŋasan (Matiwu

english_verse: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
translation_verse: Ki du nɩn fɛɛ sinapi oyu kibi atɔ duusa tɔ kpini kibi pii, nɩɩ bee duu kɩmʋ.

english_verse: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kan duu kɩmʋ mʋ, kɩ kɔyɩ, nɩɩ kɩɩ dan kɩ gyɔɔ apʋnfa kpini, nɩɩ kɩɩ waa n‐yaabi lala halɩɩ nbwiibi ba ba waa nsʋswaa kɩmʋ onyin tɔ.”

english_verse: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
translation_verse: Asɩn akʋ ɩ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kɩŋasan. Amaa saŋa kʋmaa nɩɩ ɩ ba san maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ nkʋn mʋ, ɛɛ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ. Yesu yuula apaa (Matiwu

english_verse: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
translation_verse: Kakɛ maŋa kalaatɩpwɛɛ asɛ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ fɛɛ, “Fan yɛgɛ a faa kpee benbe.”

english_verse: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
translation_verse: Aŋasan tɔ falɛ nɩɩ Yesu taa tɔwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn. Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ nu kasɛ.

english_verse: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɛ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ sakyɔ mʋ ɔbʋn kaa, ɩkʋlɩ ɩkʋ gbaa i tii bamʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
translation_verse: Ba maa kpee mʋ, nɩɩ afuu i tigi kɔnɔ kʋlʋn, nɩɩ apaa ɩ waa nɩɩ ɩ da nkyu ɩ waa ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ naa san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ ɔ bɔla.

english_verse: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu taa mu kumu gyaga keputu sʋ dɛ nsɩɩrɛ ɔkʋlɩ kɔnɔ. Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kyiŋi mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, a maa biti a wu mʋ, ɩ man tiri fʋ?”

english_verse: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kʋsʋ yɩlɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ afuu mʋ fɛɛ, “Waa diin.” Nɩɩ katɩn kʋmaa waa diin.

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ kufuu? Fan man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nɩn?”

english_verse: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ gaa nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Mɩnɛ Nyɩmɩsa ndee? Halɩɩ afuu yɛ apaa gbaa i nu mʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ mu kumu tɔ yɛ, “Naafɔɔ n daa nyɛ mata mʋ kaalɛ gbaa nɩn, n biti n nyɛ alanfɩya.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ fɛɛ, “Mi‐bii, fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya, kpewu kɔkɔlɔ yuuli sʋ, fʋ awʋrʋfɔ ɩ lʋwɛ.”

english_verse: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
translation_verse: Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nyita mu afulee mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kpini akyɛpʋ asɛ kpɩnɛ, fɛɛ naafɔɔ kʋlɔ mʋ kɩ bɔɔsɛ mʋ, amaa kʋlɔ mʋ kɩ san kii tii sʋ.

english_verse: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu loo taasɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fɛn waa kanɩn? Mɩnɛ kusu nɩɩ fen su kanɩn? Kebii mʋ man wu, ɔ dɛ nsɩɩrɛ nɩn.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
translation_verse: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba faa kpee Galile ɔbʋn benbe, Gerasene kabaafʋn.

english_verse: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn maŋa tɛ nkyan mʋ tɔ nɩn. Ɔkʋ man bɩla taalɛ da mʋ ɩgbɛɛ.

english_verse: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa kʋmaa nɩɩ ba da mʋ ayaa yɛ abaa ɩgbɛɛ mʋ, ɛɛ tɩŋɛ ɩmʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ kɛ ɔ taalɛ gyii mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
translation_verse: Ɔ naa ɔ naa nkyan yɛ abɩɩ tɔ kyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taa afʋlɛ ɛɛ kyaŋɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛgɛ ee kuusi.

english_verse: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
translation_verse: Maa wu Yesu kata mʋ, ɔ sɩlɛ naa gyaŋa mʋ, nɩɩ o muŋa mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o kuusi kenken tɔwɛ yɛ, “Yesu, fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Owura lala Mu‐Bii. Maa fʋ an sʋ mɩnɛ? Bɔ Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ tɔɔrɔɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Akuuri damantɛ akʋ ba bʋ tiri tʋtɔ kɩbɩɩ kʋ sʋ bee gyii.

english_verse: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, “Yɛgɛ a naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu sa bamʋ ɔkpa, nɩɩ ba lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ akuuri mʋ kpini bamʋ kɔnɔ ɩ waa fɛɛ ngbɩn nyɔ mʋ, ba sɩlɛ kpɩlɩgɛ kɩbɩɩ mʋ ogee tɔ naa loo nkyu tɔ muuri.

english_verse: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ akuuri mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba sɩlɛ kpee kadɛ tɔ yɛ ndɔɔ ana tɔ naa tɔwɛ asa. Nɩɩ asa ba ba kɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa.

english_verse: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
translation_verse: Ba maa ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, ba wu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ, bun ɩkaalɛ danbɩrasa ɔ tɛ, nɩɩ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ ɩ dɛ. Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

english_verse: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba yɛ Yesu ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kina, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Bwii kpee kewu naa tɔwɛ fʋ asa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ waa sa fʋ, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o wu fʋ kʋwɛɛ.”

english_verse: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɩɩ tʋtɔ naa natɛ kabaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Ndɛ Kudu mʋ sʋ kpini, ɛɛ tɔwɛ asa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa sa mʋ. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu mʋ kpini. Gyarosi mu‐bii kyɩɩsa yɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mata Yesu kaalɛ mʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba ŋmasɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ gya bamʋ kpini lɩɩ kewu, nɩɩ maa kebii mʋ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi yɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ asa mʋ ba loo obu mʋ nɩɩ kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ dɛ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ faa kpee ɔbʋn mʋ benbe, nɩɩ asa kʋkyɔ ba ba sarɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyarosi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ba tʋtɔ. Maa wu Yesu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩyɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ.

english_verse: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kʋlɛ mʋ gaa yɛ, “N kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, mi kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi ɛɛ lɔ ee biti o wu, ba taa fʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ kɛ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.”

english_verse: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ maa Yesu ba yii sʋ bee kpee, yɛgɛ sakyɔ ba mili Yesu sʋ ba buu mʋ.

english_verse: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
translation_verse: Ɔkyɩɩ kʋ nɩɩ nkalan ɩɩ da mʋ, nsu kudu anyɔ, bʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
translation_verse: O nu Yesu sʋ nɩɩ o loo sakyɔ mʋ tɔ ba Yesu kamaa.

english_verse: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ mata Yesu kaalɛ, nɩɩ nkalan mʋ ɩ tɩn opula tɔ. Nɩɩ o wu mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ fɛɛ mʋ awʋrʋfɔ kpini ɩ kyʋn.

english_verse: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
translation_verse: Opula tɔ nɩɩ Yesu bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔlʋn kʋ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ o biliŋi kɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ ɩ mata mɛ kaalɛ?”

english_verse: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba ba sa mʋ kɔnɔ fɛɛ, “Fu wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ mili fʋ sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ taasɛ yɛ, ‘Anɩmʋ ɩ mata mɛ kaalɛ?’ ”

english_verse: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi ɔ dan san ɛɛ kɩɩ ee biti ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mata mʋ kaalɛ mʋ.

english_verse: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kufuu kyɩkyaa natɛ ba Yesu ansi tɔ, ba muŋa mʋ ayaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kesintin kpini tɔwɛ mʋ.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Yesu maa san ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ nbɔɔ ba lɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa Gyarosi kɩkpaara sʋ ba tɔwɛ Gyarosi yɛ, “Fu kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ wu, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ san fɩɩ tɔɔrɔɔ ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ?”

english_verse: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mu ansi yɛgɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Gyarosi yɛ, “Man waa kufuu, dan kɔɔlɛ gyii kanɩn.”

english_verse: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ man bɩla yɛgɛ ɔkʋ buu mʋ tii Pita yɛ Gyemisi yɛ mu‐supu Gyɔn sʋ.

english_verse: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
translation_verse: Ba maa fʋʋ Gyarosi kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, ba wu asa ba maa waa ɩlawʋ bɛɛ sawʋ kenken.

english_verse: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
translation_verse: Opula tɔ nɩɩ kebii mʋ kʋsʋ, nɩɩ ɔ natɛ. Kebii mʋ gyii nsu kudu anyɔ. Nɩɩ asɩn mʋ i kyinkyin bamʋ gaa.

english_verse: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu sa bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ ba man kan tɔwɛ ɔkʋ kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ ba sa kebii mʋ agyitɔ kɛ o gyii.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba faa kpee benbe ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩɩ yɛ Kanesareti, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ɔkʋlɩ dan.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ katɩn kʋmaa, amɔɔ mʋ kadɛ tɔ yɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi nkʋn asɛ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ.”

english_verse: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
translation_verse: Herodi maa nu Yesu asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Ɔ gyɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu nɩn. Mɛ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba tɩn mu kumu, amaa o bwii ba nkpa tɔ.”

english_verse: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
translation_verse: Yesu asunpu mʋ ba bwii ba Yesu asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba waa yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ba kaapʋ.

english_verse: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
translation_verse: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ, o kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɛɛ kaapʋ. Nɩɩ mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ki kyinkyin asa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu mʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Fɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kɩkaapʋ nɩmʋ lɩɩ? Fɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kanyaasɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ halɩɩ ɛɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa anɩmʋ?

english_verse: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
translation_verse: Man gyɛ kapinta mʋ ndee? Man gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ mu‐bii mʋ nɩn? Man gyɛ mu‐supu ana ɩ gyɛ Gyemisi yɛ Gyosefu yɛ Gyuda yɛ Simon? Man gyɛ anaa mʋ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ ana ɩ bʋ nfɩɩ?” Nɩɩ ba kina kɩkɔɔlɛ mʋ.

english_verse: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, “Fan maa kpee falɛ, fan man taa sɛhn, amɔɔ fanɛ akpebi nkʋn. Fan man taa agyitɔ abɛɛ adiikun abɛɛ afulee waa fanɛ igyifa tɔ.

english_verse: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan kan kpee kadɛ kʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man kɔɔlɛ fanɛ abɛɛ nu fanɛ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ fan kan fɛn lɩɩ kanɩn kadɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ, fan kpɩkpaa fanɛ ayaa tɔ ɩsɛ wʋrɩgɛ tʋtɔ, kɛ ɩmʋ ii biti i gyii bamʋ.”

english_verse: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn okyugyeepu nyɛ kilibi Herodiya asɛ, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ mɔɔ mʋ, amaa ɔ man taalɛ.

english_verse: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
translation_verse: Herodi sɩlɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu, lɩɩ fɛɛ o nyi maa gyɛ nyɩmɩsa danbɩrasa yɛ nyɩmɩsa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ o kun mʋ. Herodi ii biti Gyɔn abwaarɛsɩn kunu, amaa saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu ɩmʋ mʋ ɩɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa.

english_verse: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ naa taasɛ mu‐nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ Owura Herodi asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ fii biti Gyɔn okyugyeepu mu kumu.”

english_verse: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ waa bilen bwii kpee Owura Herodi asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “N biti Gyɔn okyugyeepu kumu tangɩran sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.”

english_verse: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Owura Herodi kɔkɔlɔ ki nyita mʋ gaa, amaa ɔ man taalɛ kina lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛɛ bɔ ntan mʋ afɔ ansi tɔ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: At once he ordered a guard to cut off John's head there in prison.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o sun ɔnaakɔpʋ kʋlʋn yɛ ɔ naa bɩya Gyɔn kumu. Nɩɩ o kpee obu tiisa mʋ tɔ naa tɩn mʋ Gyɔn kumu.

english_verse: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa gyaga tangɩran sʋ taa ba sa kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ. Nɩɩ kebii mʋ taa naa sa mu‐nyi.

english_verse: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
translation_verse: Gyɔn okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba ba taa mu kibuni sansa naa pula. Yesu sa asa ngbɩn nnuu agyitɔ nɩɩ ba gyii ŋmɛ (Matiwu

english_verse: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ asa kʋkyɔ bɛɛ ba Yesu asɛ bee bwii sʋ mʋ, ba man nyɛ saŋa ba gyii. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, “Fan yɛgɛ a lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ kpee opula mʋ nɩɩ asa ba man bʋ, kɛ fan nyɛ saŋa fʋʋtɛ gbɛrɛɛ.”

english_verse: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ bamʋ nkʋn bee kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ mʋ.

english_verse: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
translation_verse: Ɩ maa sʋ ii kpee kalaatɩpwɛɛ asɛ mʋ, Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kyɔwɛ tɛɛ ɛɛ lʋwɛ. Nɩɩ nfɩɩ mɔɔ waa opula mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ sɛhn man bʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan sa bamʋ kʋtɔ kʋ kɛ ba gyii.” Nɩɩ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Fii biti fɛɛ a taa gbiti nderebi anyɔ naa sɔɔ bodobodo sa bamʋ?”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Bodobodo afɩnɛ nɩɩ fan su? Fan naa kɩɩ.” Ba maa naa kɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ba ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Bodobodo inuu yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ nɩɩ an sʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ ba yɛgɛ asa mʋ ba kyɩna kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ afitiri bunboli sʋ.

english_verse: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu Yesu mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ kpini gaa. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ opula tɔ yɛ, “Fan nyɛ nkɔlɔ, fan man waa kufuu, mɛ nɩn.”

english_verse: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taa bodobodo inuu yɛ ɩkɩn ɩnyɔ mʋ, nɩɩ o diyaa mu ansi kɩɩ sʋsʋ, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa sa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba barɩgɛ sa asa mʋ kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla barɩgɛ ɩkɩn mʋ tɔ sa asa mʋ kpini.

english_verse: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ gyii naa fʋʋ mʋ ɔkaa.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba tasɛ bodobodo yɛ ɩkɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ san mʋ waa alantan kudu anyɔ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ bɔla.

english_verse: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Yesu yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba taa ɔkʋlɩ gyankpaa mʋ, bee kpee Betisayida mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ benbe. Nɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ lɛɛ asa mʋ ɔkpa.

english_verse: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Maa kala bamʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee kɩbɩɩ sʋ, ɛɛ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
translation_verse: Ɩ maa ba fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛɛ asɛ mʋ, abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba fʋʋ nkyu mʋ nsana, yɛgɛ Yesu nkʋn mɔɔ bʋ kidenbi sʋ.

english_verse: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu wu fɛɛ kɩpalɛ mʋ ki nu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ apaa mʋ ɩ gyaŋa bamʋ. Kɩbwaayɩkɛ sʋ mʋ, Yesu natɛ nkyu sʋ naa fʋʋ bamʋ asɛ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kyʋn bamʋ sʋ.

english_verse: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
translation_verse: Amaa ba wu maa naa nkyu mʋ sʋ, be nyi fɛɛ obuni nɩn, nɩɩ ba waa ɩlawʋ.

english_verse: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu loo bamʋ asɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ afuu mʋ a yɔɔ opula tɔ. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin abɩɩlapʋ mʋ gaa,

english_verse: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
translation_verse: lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man taalɛ nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu sa anyɩn ngbɩn nnuu agyitɔ mʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ gaa. Yesu kyɛ alɔpʋ Kanesareti (Matiwu

english_verse: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
translation_verse: Ba maa dan bɛɛ lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ tɔ mʋ, asa ba bɩɩ Yesu.

english_verse: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa ba sɩlɛ lɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini bɛɛ ba Yesu asɛ, nɩɩ ba taa alɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba dɛ ɩkalan sʋ bɩya mʋ.

english_verse: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
translation_verse: Katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu kpee ndɛ‐bii abɛɛ ndɛ lala tɔ yɛ ndɔɔ ana tɔ mʋ, asa bɛɛ kpiya alɔpʋ agya tɔ yɛgɛ bɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ba mata mʋ kaalɛ. Bamʋ nɩɩ ba mata mʋ kaalɛ kpini mʋ, ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
translation_verse: Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ akʋ, mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, ba sarɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan sʋ kanyaasɩn ɩkpa libi mʋ nɩɩ fɛn sʋ fɛn taa Ɩbwaarɛ nbara yɩlɛ ɩkaa sʋ, kɛ fan nyɛ buu nyɩmɩsa kɩkaapʋ sʋ.

english_verse: They noticed that some of his disciples ate without first washing their hands.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba wu fɛɛ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ ba tɛ bee gyii agyitɔ yɛgɛ ba man fwɩɩ bamʋ abaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ adɩdaasɩn ɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Farasii abi mʋ yɛ Gyiwu abi kpini ba buu bamʋ naana ana kɩkaapʋ dɩdaa mʋ nɩn, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba gyii agyitɔ, amɔɔ ba fwɩɩ bamʋ abaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ adɩdaasɩn mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ.

english_verse: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kan lɩɩ kɩgya tɔ ba mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba gyii, amɔɔ ba fwɩɩ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ pɔyɩ. Nɩɩ ba bɩla ba sʋ ikisii lɩɩ ɩkɔɔpʋ yɛ npulii yɛ ɩtasa atɔ kɩfwɩɩ sʋ.

english_verse: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ bɩrɛ be mee buu anɛ naana ana kɩkaapʋ dɩdaa mʋ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ bamʋ bɩrɛ ba sʋ abaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ bee gyii agyitɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ yɩla lɩɩ fanɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin. Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Fan sʋ fanɛ nnɔ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ fen sun mɛ, yɛgɛ ɩ man lɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

english_verse: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
translation_verse: Kɩyan nɩɩ fen sun mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa nɩɩ fɛn sʋ fɛn kaapʋ fɛɛ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ.’ ”

english_verse: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan kina Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ, nɩɩ fan buu nyɩmɩsa lɛɛ.”

english_verse: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
translation_verse: Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ, fɛn yɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ abɛɛ mu‐nyi yɛ, ‘Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ fii biti fʋ nyɛ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Koriban.’ .

english_verse: You won't let those people help their parents.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ mʋ, ɔ sʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ o kina kɩkyɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi tɔ.

english_verse: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
translation_verse: Kanɩn kɩkaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ fɛn kaapʋ asa falɛ ɩɩ yɛgɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ ii biliŋi kɩyan. Atɔ damantɛ fɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ fɛn waa.” Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ waa nyɩmɩsa ineesi (Matiwu

english_verse: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa tɔ mʋ, kɩmʋ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi.

english_verse: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɩɩ asa ba sarɛ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ o nu mɛ asɛ, kɛ ɔ bɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
translation_verse: Yesu maa lɩɩ asa mʋ asɛ kpee kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ mʋ kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ fɛɛ, “Ɩmʋ sʋ, fanɛ gbaa fen mee nu asɩn kasɛ bilen? Fan man nyi fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋ man bʋ tɔ kɩɩ lɩɩ kewu nɩɩ kii loo nyɩmɩsa tɔ nɩɩ kɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi.

english_verse: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kii loo nyɩmɩsa tɔ mʋ, ki mee kpee mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, amɔɔ mʋ ɔtɔ tɔ, kɛ kɩ bɩla bɔla lɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ.”

english_verse: unfaithfulness in marriage, greed, meanness, deceit, indecency, envy, insults, pride, and foolishness.
translation_verse: yɛ kensi payɩ libi yɛ kumu tɔ ɔlʋn, yɛ kapɩna asa, yɛ kɩgyagaa, yɛ kayɩɩ basa yɛ nyɩmɩsa kinyita yɛ alala‐sɩn yɛ kimelensi.

english_verse: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩɩ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩn, nɩɩ ɩɩ waa mʋ ineesi.” Ɔkyɩɩ kʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii (Matiwu

english_verse: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ sa kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩn, amaa nbii ba kan bee gyii agyitɔ nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ mʋ, igyoonɔ bɛɛ tasɛ bee gyii.”

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ Galile kpee Taayi katɩnɛ. Nɩɩ o loo kɩkpaara kʋ sʋ, ɔ man daa ee biti fɛɛ ɔkʋ o wu mʋ, amaa ɔ man taalɛ baala mʋ n‐yɩɩ.

english_verse: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
translation_verse: Ɔkyɩɩ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba tɛ mu‐bii sʋ mʋ nu Yesu sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ ba mʋ asɛ bilen ba muŋa mʋ ayaa tɔ.

english_verse: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
translation_verse: Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ man gyɛ Gyiwu obii, ɔ lɩɩ Fonesiya mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ ɔ gya nbwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ mu kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ sʋ sa mʋ.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ dagaa nbii mʋ ba maa gyii ŋmɛ pɔyɩ. Ɩ man bʋrɔn fɛɛ ba taa nbii agyitɔ sa igyoonɔ.”

english_verse: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ bwii kpewu naa wu kebii mʋ maa dɛ npaa sʋ yɛgɛ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba tɛɛ wʋla lɩɩ mʋ tɔ. Yesu kyɛ kpawu kʋ

english_verse: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ Taayi katɩnɛ, nɩɩ ɔ bɔla Sidon kpee Galile ɔbʋn‐kaa naa fʋʋ Dekapoli ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa akʋ ba taa kpawu kʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ dan ba Yesu asɛ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taa kpawu mʋ lɩɩ asa tɔ kpee ɩkaa sʋ, taa mʋ abaa‐bi waa mʋ asʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ nɩɩ ɔ mata mʋ kanandʋlʋ.

english_verse: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu diyaa mu ansi kɩɩ sʋsʋ, nɩɩ o muusi. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, “Ɩfata.” Kɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Bugi tɔ.”

english_verse: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
translation_verse: Opula tɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ asʋ a tigi, nɩɩ ee nu katɔwɛ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ danbɩrasa.

english_verse: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ ba man kan tɔwɛ sa ɔkʋ. Amaa kɩgya damantɛ nɩɩ ɔ gya bamʋ mʋ, saamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔŋɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ bee tii sʋ.

english_verse: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin asa mʋ gaa nɩɩ ba yɛ, “Ɔ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ danbɩrasa. Ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ akpawu bee nu, nɩɩ anaamuu bɛɛ tɔwɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ danbɩrasa kɛ fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, lɩɩ Farasii abi yɛ Herodi asa bamʋ kɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii tin bodobodo mʋ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu maa bugi mʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ pensen kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Pita tɩɩ mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yii mʋ.

english_verse: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
translation_verse: “Asa nɩmʋ asɩn ɩ sʋ mɛ kʋwɛɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ndaga nkɛ nsa ndee ba maa bʋ mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ ba man sʋ agyitɔ akʋ ba gyii.

english_verse: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n kan tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba bwii kpewu mʋ, bee biti ba naa kirin‐kirin ɔkpa tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ akʋ ba lɩɩ kata‐kata.”

english_verse: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba kyʋn kyɩna kasɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa bodobodo asunɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ‐kpɛɛ ɩmʋ tɔ taa sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ ba barɩgɛ tɔ sa asa mʋ ba gyii, nɩɩ ba barɩgɛ tɔ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla taa ɩkɩn gbɛrɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ, fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ bɩla sa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ ba barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ sa asa mʋ.

english_verse: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ, nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ faa kyʋn Dalimanuta ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Farasii abi bee biti adangana (Matiwu

english_verse: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
translation_verse: Farasii abi akʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ ba gyii mʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ, kɛ ba nyɛ wu mʋ kilibi. Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ Yesu tɔ kɩɩ yɛ ɔ waa sʋsʋ adangana akʋ kaapʋ bamʋ.

english_verse: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
translation_verse: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba tan sʋ fɛɛ ba maa man taa bodobodo kʋkyɔ, amaa bodobodo kukuli kʋlʋn ɩ bʋ bamʋ asɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed?
translation_verse: Yesu maa bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ fanɛ kɩman sʋ bodobodo kʋkyɔ mʋ asɩn? Ɩmʋ sʋ, fan man pɩɩta bɩɩ mɛ awaasa kasɛ? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ ɩ kɩtaa atɔ bilen kanɩn?

english_verse: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩtaa gyaatanapʋ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, taa mʋ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ nbwama. Nɩɩ Yesu tʋʋ nkyɔlɛ waa mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fɩɩ wu atɔ?”

english_verse: The man looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees walking around.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ gyaatanapʋ mʋ diyaa mu ansi sʋsʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “N wu asa ba maa naa, nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ iyu ɩ maa naa mʋ.”

english_verse: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla taa mʋ abaa gyaga ɔnyɩn mu ansi sʋ, nɩɩ mu ansi mʋ i bugi, nɩɩ mu ansi mʋ ɩ nyɛ alanfɩya, ɩɩ kɩɩ danbɩrasa.

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ gyaatanapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Kpewu! Amaa man bɩla bwii kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ.” Pita lɛɛ kaapʋ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu gyɛ (Matiwu

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fanɛ mɔɔ, fan yɛ anɩmʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ?” Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu gya bamʋ yɛ ba man kan tɔwɛ ɔkʋ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ. Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ awʋrʋfɔ kuwu yɛ mʋ kelewu asɩn (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “The nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa maa wu awʋrʋfɔ gaa, kɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba kina mɛ. Bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ, kɛ nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, n biti n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba.”

english_verse: But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, “Satan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu, biliŋi kɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Pita yɛ, “Lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, fʋ Sɩtaanɛ nɩmʋ. Fʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ man lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, ɩ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa asɛ nɩn.”

english_verse: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ yɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ o buu mɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ o kina mʋ n‐yɩɩ, kɛ ɔ sʋla mʋ oyu laasa kɛ o buu mɛ.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me and for the good news, you will save it.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ o kun mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa mʋ, ee biti ɔ pan nkpa mʋ. Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa lɩɩ mɛ yɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ mʋ nkpa.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fʋ kan nyɛ dulinyaa tɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa, nɩɩ fʋ pan fʋ nkpa mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ?

english_verse: What could you give to get back your soul?
translation_verse: Abɛɛ mɩnɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ taalɛ ɔ taa kyarɛ mʋ nkpa?

english_verse: Don't be ashamed of me and my message among these unfaithful and sinful people! If you are, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan waa ipeeli lɩɩ mɛ yɛ mɛ kɩkaapʋ sʋ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ abi mʋ nɩɩ alibi yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩman‐sɩlɛ kɩ bʋ bamʋ tɔ falɛ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ kan ba yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa yɛ mʋ sʋsʋ nbɔɔ mʋ, ngbaa mɛ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ sʋ mɛ ipeeli sa kanɩn ɔsa maŋa.”

english_verse: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ Eligya tee bwii ba, nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ asa bee biti mʋ, ba tɛɛ taa waa mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ.” Yesu kyɛ kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi (Matiwu

english_verse: After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, “Why couldn't we force out that demon?”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ, nɩɩ Yesu loo kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nkʋn ba naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ anɛɛnɛ a man taalɛ kʋsaa nbwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ kebii mʋ sʋ?”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked a child to stand near him. He put his arm around the child and said,
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taa kebii kʋ ba yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa kebii mʋ abaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: The shadow of a cloud passed over and covered them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, and I love him. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kabwaarɛ wʋlɛ ba bun bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ tɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐Bii‐nyɩnsa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ kebiti sa. Fan nu mʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: At once the disciples looked around, but they saw only Jesus.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ, abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa kɩɩ kyaabɔɔ mʋ, ba man bɩla wu ɔkʋ bamʋ asɛ, amɔɔ Yesu nkʋn.

english_verse: So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words “raised from death.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba nu sa mʋ, amaa bamʋ wʋlɛ‐wʋlɛ ɩɩ taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Kikyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ nɩmʋ kasɛ kaapʋ nɛnɛ?”

english_verse: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say that Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ɩ dagaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya maa gyankpaa ba pɔyɩ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Elijah certainly will come to get everything ready. But don't the Scriptures also say that the Son of Man must suffer terribly and be rejected?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ Eligya ii biti ɔ gyankpaa ba, kɛ ɔ ba lɔŋɔ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩnɩmʋ. Amaa mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n wu awʋrʋfɔ gaa kɛ asa ba kina mɛ?

english_verse: When Jesus and his three disciples came back down, they saw a large crowd around the other disciples. The teachers of the Law of Moses were arguing with them.
translation_verse: Ba maa kpɩlɩgɛ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ bɛɛ ba abɩɩlapʋ asansa mʋ asɛ mʋ, ba wu sakyɔ damantɛ kʋ maa yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ bamʋ, yɛgɛ ba maa nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ bee gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ.

english_verse: They brought the boy, and as soon as the demon saw Jesus, it made the boy shake all over. He fell down and began rolling on the ground and foaming at the mouth.
translation_verse: Ba maa taa kebii mʋ bɩya mʋ, nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ maa wu Yesu mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɩ taa kebii mʋ tʋʋ kasɛ fɛɛ kigbingbin. Ɔ dɛ ee gbiliti yɛgɛ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ kii fuu afʋfʋʋ.

english_verse: “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me.” For or against Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: “Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan kɔɔlɛ kebii kɩnɩmʋ dubi mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, mɛ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan kɔɔlɛ mɛ mɔɔ mʋ, man gyɛ mɛ nkʋn nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ, amaa ɔ kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋnɩ o sun mɛ mʋ gbaa.” Bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ Yesu kamaa mʋ asɩn (Luka

english_verse: The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ ba maa wu Yesu maa ba mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ, nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ kpee mʋ asɛ naa fwaala mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What are you arguing about?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩmɔɔrɛ nɩɩ fanaa bamʋ fen gyii mʋ?”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “O! Fanɛ ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa mʋ nɩɩ fan man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii. Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fen biti yɛ n nyɛ kenyiita sa fanɛ? Fan taa kebii mʋ bɩya mɛ nfɩɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked the boy's father, “How long has he been like this?” The man answered, “Ever since he was a child.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ kebii mʋ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ, “Kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ falɛ mʋ, ɩ tɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ do?” Nɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Lɩɩ koo mu kebii tɔ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ kanɩn.

english_verse: At once the boy's father shouted, “I do have faith! Please help me to have even more.”
translation_verse: Kebii mʋ mʋ‐sɛ taa kensi payɩ kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, “N kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, amaa kyɛ mɛ tɔ kɛ n nyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tii sʋ.”

english_verse: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu sakyɔ mʋ maa nyɛ akyɔ ii tii sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ pʋntɛ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ sʋ tɔwɛ ɩmʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ naamuu yɛ akpawu nbwɩɩ libi, n yɛ fan kʋsʋ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ, kɛ fan man bɩla bwii ba mʋ sʋ kakaakʋ.”

english_verse: The spirit screamed and made the boy shake all over. Then it went out of him. The boy looked dead, and almost everyone said he was.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba nyaŋɛ tɔ muusi tɔ taa kebii mʋ tʋʋ ɔlʋn fɛɛ kigbingbin, nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ mʋ sʋ. Kebii mʋ dɛ fɛɛ obuni, nɩɩ asa mʋ ba yɛ o wu.

english_verse: But Jesus took hold of his hand and helped him stand up.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu kɩtaa kebii mʋ kɩbaa tɔ kʋsaa mʋ yɩlɛ mʋ ayaa sʋ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Only prayer can force out this kind of demon.” Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Sɛhn man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ taalɛ gya ɩnɩmʋ ana kudubi, amɔɔ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ nkʋn.” Yesu bɩla bwii tɔwɛ mʋ kelewu asɩn (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus left with his disciples and started through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know about it,
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ bɔla Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu mee biti fɛɛ ba bɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ.

english_verse: because he was teaching the disciples that the Son of Man would be handed over to people who would kill him. But three days later he would rise to life.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sa asa, kɛ ba mɔɔ mɛ, amaa nkɛ nsa kamaa tɔ mʋ, n biti n kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.”

english_verse: The disciples did not understand what Jesus meant, and they were afraid to ask. Who Is the Greatest? (Matthew
translation_verse: Maa tɔwɛ bamʋ kanɩn mʋ, ba man nu ɩmʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ɩ sʋ bamʋ kufuu fɛɛ ba taasɛ mʋ ɩmʋ kasɛ. Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔlala? (Matiwu

english_verse: If your foot causes you to sin, chop it off. You would be better off to go into life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asʋnswɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee wu, nɩɩ fʋlɔn mʋ gbaa mee dun kakaakʋ.

english_verse: The worms there never die, and the fire never stops burning.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ, asʋnswɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ be mee wu, nɩɩ fʋlɔn mʋ gbaa mee dun kakaakʋ.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to his home in Capernaum. After they were inside the house, Jesus asked them, “What were you arguing about along the way?”
translation_verse: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba kpee Kapanum kadɛ lala tɔ. Ba maa bʋ kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan maa naa ɔkpa tɔ fɛn ba mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩmɔɔrɛ nɩɩ fen gyii mʋ?”

english_verse: They had been arguing about which one of them was the greatest, and so they did not answer.
translation_verse: Ba laatɔ nɩn ba man lɛɛ kɔnɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba naa ɔkpa tɔ bee kpee mʋ, bee gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ yɛ ɔmɔmɔ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ tɔ ɔlala.

english_verse: After Jesus sat down and told the twelve disciples to gather around him, he said, “If you want the place of honor, you must become a slave and serve others!”
translation_verse: Yesu maa kyʋn kyɩna mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan ee biti fɛɛ o biliŋi ɔlala mʋ, ɔ waa mʋ n‐yɩɩ kalʋwɛ yɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ sa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana.”

english_verse: Salt is good. But if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made salty again? Have salt among you and live at peace with each other.
translation_verse: “Nfɔlɛ ɩ bʋrɔn, amaa nɩɩ nfɔlɛ kan fɔyɩ ɩmʋ ɔkɔn bɩrɛ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fɛn bɩla taalɛ waa mʋ kɛ ɩ nyɛ ɩmʋ ɔkɔn? Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ fanɛ awaasa ɩ baa ɩ bʋkɔn fɛɛ nfɔlɛ, kɛ fan kyɩna yɛ abaa kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ.”

english_verse: John said, “Teacher, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But he wasn't one of us, and we told him to stop.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, an wu ɔnyɩn kʋ maa sʋ fʋ kɩtɩɩ ɛɛ kʋsaa nbwɩɩ libi ɛɛ lɩɩ asa sʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ man tii anɛ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ an gya mʋ yɛ ɔ man bɩla waa kanɩn.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan man gya kanɩn ɔsa maŋa. Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ɔ mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ tɔwɛ asɩn kʋ nyita mɛ kɩtɩɩ.

english_verse: Anyone who isn't against us is for us.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o mee nyita anɛ kusun mʋ, o tii anɛ sʋ nɩmʋ.

english_verse: And anyone who gives you a cup of water in my name, just because you belong to me, will surely be rewarded. Temptations To Sin (Matthew
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ ɔkʋ kan sa fanɛ nkyu nuusa lɩɩ fan maa gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ ɩmʋ kʋkɔka. Atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ bɩya alibi (Matiwu

english_verse: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan yɛgɛ nɩɩ nbii mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii falɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan waa alibi mʋ, kanɩn odunwura maŋa, nɩɩ ba kan taa kɩfʋlɛ‐buu lala ŋmina kyaga mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ taa mʋ tʋʋ ɔpʋ tɔ gbaa mʋ, ɩ bɔ.

english_verse: If your eye causes you to sin, get rid of it. You would be better off to go into God's kingdom with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ fu kinsi kɩ kan kɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa alibi mʋ, kyɔlɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ. Ɩ bɔ sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa kinsi kʋlʋn kpee Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ fʋ maa taa ansi anyɔ naa loo awʋrʋfɔ tɔ kakpaa.

english_verse: After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.
translation_verse: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, o kpee Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ faa Gyɔdan ɔbʋn mʋ kpee benbe. Nɩɩ sakyɔ damantɛ kʋ kɩ ba kyaabɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɛɛ waa mʋ.

english_verse: A woman who divorces her husband and marries again is also unfaithful.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn sʋ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ kan kina mu‐kuli naa kili ɔnyɩn pʋpwɛ mʋ, o nyita kekili.” Yesu waa nbii kusee (Matiwu

english_verse: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. So they came up to him and asked if it was right for a man to divorce his wife.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ Farasii abi akʋ ba ba sʋʋ Yesu ɔdʋʋ sɩntaasɛ tɔ yɛ, “Ɩ dɛ ɔkpa sa ɔnyɩn fɛ o kina mʋ‐ka?”

english_verse: Jesus asked them, “What does the Law of Moses say about this?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nbara nɩɩ Mosesi kaapʋ fanɛ?”

english_verse: They answered, “Moses allows a man to write out divorce papers and send his wife away.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mosesi sa ɔkpa fɛɛ ɔnyɩn ɔ kyʋrɔɔ kekina ɔwʋlɛ sa mʋ‐ka kɛ o kina mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Moses gave you this law because you are so heartless.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ maa bʋlʋn sʋ nɩɩ Mosesi kyʋrɔɔ kanɩn nbara mʋ sa fanɛ.

english_verse: But in the beginning God made a man and a woman.
translation_verse: Naafɔɔ lɩɩ koo npiili asɛ, atɔ kʋtʋʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ waa bamʋ ɔnyɩn yɛ ɔkyɩɩ.

english_verse: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi, kɛ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ mata mʋ‐ka,

english_verse: He becomes like one person with his wife. Then they are no longer two people, but one.
translation_verse: kɛ bamʋ asa anyɔ ba biliŋi nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn, yɛgɛ ba man bɩla ba gyɛ asa anyɔ, amaa ba gyɛ ɔkʋlʋn nɩn.

english_verse: And no one should separate a couple that God has joined together.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa kʋkʋlʋn mʋ, ɔkʋ man kan barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples were back in the house, they asked him about what he had said.
translation_verse: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba maa loo kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɔŋɔ taasɛ mʋ asɩn lɩɩ kekili mʋ asɩn sʋ.

english_verse: He told them, “A man who divorces his wife and marries someone else is unfaithful to his wife.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan kina mʋ‐ka nɩɩ ɔ naa kili ɔkyɩɩ pʋpwɛ mʋ, o nyita kekili, nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ‐ka mu alibi.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus so he could bless them by placing his hands on them. But his disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa akʋ ba taa bamʋ nbii wuribi bɛɛ ba Yesu asɛ yɛ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ bɛɛ gya bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩya nbii mʋ.

english_verse: I promise you that you cannot get into God's kingdom, unless you accept it the way a child does.”
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ fɛɛ kebii mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ nyɛ loo kɩmʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: When Jesus saw this, he became angry and said, “Let the children come to me! Don't try to stop them. People who are like these little children belong to the kingdom of God.
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu ɩmʋ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ agbʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan yɛgɛ nbii wuribi mʋ ba ba mɛ asɛ, kɛ fan man tii bamʋ ɔkpa, lɩɩ fɛɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyɛ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ nbii wuribi mʋ nɩn.

english_verse: Jesus looked closely at the man. He liked him and said, “There's one thing you still need to do. Go sell everything you own. Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come with me.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ diin, mʋ asɩn ɩ pɩrɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ san fʋ kʋtɔ kʋlʋn, kpee naa fɛ fʋ kapatɩyɛ kpini, kɛ fʋ taa afulee mʋ sa atiripu. Ɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ fʋ nyɛ ɩmʋ kʋkɔka, kɛ fʋ ba buu mɛ.”

english_verse: When the man heard Jesus say this, he went away gloomy and sad because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn mʋ maa nu kanan mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyaŋɛ mu ansi tɔ taa kɔkɔlɔ nyita kpewu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ atɔ wuya gaa nɩn.

english_verse: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “It's hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ tɔ kyaabɔɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩ bʋlʋn gaa sa atɔ wuya ana fɛɛ ba loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Asɩn mʋ i kyinkyin abɩɩlapʋ mʋ. Amaa Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mi‐bii ana, yɛ fan wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ kiloo bʋlʋn.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Ɩ bʋpɔɔ sa nyɔɔma fɛɛ ɔ taalɛ loo baasa ɔbɔ tɔ bʋlɛ, ɩ kyɔ atɔ wuya o loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus' disciples were even more amazed. They asked each other, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ dɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ gaa, bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Anɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ surum, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Nyɩmɩsa asɛ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ waa, man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ bɩrɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩɩ taalɛ waa.”

english_verse: The disciples were confused as Jesus led them toward Jerusalem, and his other followers were afraid. Once again, Jesus took the twelve disciples aside and told them what was going to happen to him. He said:
translation_verse: Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba naa bee kpee Gyerusalem nɩɩ Yesu ɩ gyankpaa bamʋ bee kpee. Asɩn mʋ i kyinkyin abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ kufuu kɩ sʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba buu bamʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla lɛɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti ki tu mʋ.

english_verse: We are now on our way to Jerusalem where the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses. They will sentence him to death and hand him over to foreigners,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “An kpee Gyerusalem, nɩɩ bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sa Gyiwu aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ. Bee biti ba bun mɛ lewu kʋpwɛ, kɛ ba taa mɛ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi abaa tɔ.

english_verse: and they answered, “When you come into your glory, please let one of us sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ kan ba kyɩna fʋ kuwura‐gya, fʋ bʋnyaa kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ mʋ, fʋ yɛgɛ anɛ ɔkʋ ɔ kyɩna fu gyisa sʋ, kɛ ɔkʋ mɔɔ ɔ kyɩna fʋ‐bɩna sʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “You don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn kʋlɛ falɛ. Fɛn taalɛ gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n gyii falɛ, nɩɩ kekyugyee mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba gyee mɛ mʋ, fɛn taalɛ gyee kanɩn kekyugyee maŋa?”

english_verse: “Yes, we are!” James and John answered. Then Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from the cup from which I must drink. And you will be baptized just as I must!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kyula yɛ, “An taalɛ.” Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fen biti fan gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n gyii falɛ, kɛ fan gyee mɛ kekyugyee mʋ nɩɩ n gyee mʋ.

english_verse: But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for God to decide.”
translation_verse: Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna mɛ gyisa sʋ abɛɛ mɛ bɩna sʋ bɩrɛ, man gyɛ mɛ ii biti n sa. Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɔŋɔ yɩla mʋ.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with James and John.
translation_verse: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ kudu mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, ba maa nu asɩn mʋ, ba nyɛ agbʋ waa Gyemisi maa Gyɔn.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know that those foreigners who call themselves kings like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over the people they rule.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɩɩ bamʋ kpini sarɛ mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan nyi mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi bamʋ agyankpaapʋ mʋ, bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ ɔlʋn asa sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ alʋlʋnpʋ gbaa bɛɛ kaapʋ ɔlʋn asa sʋ.

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be everyone's slave.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa waa fɛɛ kɩnyɛ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Blind Bartimaeus (Matthew
translation_verse: Halɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa gbaa n man ba yɛ asa ba sun mɛ nɩn. N ba n ba sun asa nɩn, kɛ n taa mɛ nkpa kɛɛ, kɛ ɩ ka asa gaalaagaa alibi kʋkɔ.” Yesu Kyɛ Gyaatanapʋ Batimiyo (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to Jericho. And as they were leaving, they were followed by a large crowd. A blind beggar by the name of Bartimaeus son of Timaeus was sitting beside the road.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba kpee Gyeriko kadɛ tɔ. Maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ kʋbʋ damantɛ ba maa lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba wu gyaatanapʋ kʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Batimayu, maa tɛ ɔkpa kaa ɛɛ kʋlɛ.

english_verse: When he heard that it was Jesus from Nazareth, he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Gyaatanapʋ mʋ maa nu fɛɛ Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ ɩ kyʋn mʋ, nɩɩ o kuusi tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Yesu, Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ.”

english_verse: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Maa kuusi kenken sʋ mʋ, asa ba pʋntɛ mʋ sʋ yɛ o bun mʋ kɔnɔ sʋ, yɛgɛ ɛɛ lɔŋɔ ee kuusi kenken ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him over!” They called out to the blind man and said, “Don't be afraid! Come on! He is calling for you.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu yɩlɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan tɩɩ mʋ sa mɛ.” Nɩɩ ba tɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Yɛgɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ yuuli fʋ, Yesu yɛ fʋ ba.”

english_verse: The man threw off his coat as he jumped up and ran to Jesus.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ fugi yii mʋ ayaa sʋ ŋmanyan mʋ kɩtaa tʋʋ ɩkaa sʋ nɩɩ o kpee Yesu asɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man answered, “Master, I want to see!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti yɛ n waa sa fʋ?” Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, n biti n baa n kɩɩ nɩn.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “You may go. Your eyes are healed because of your faith.” At once the man could see, and he went down the road with Jesus.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Natɛ! Fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.” Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ mu ansi mʋ a bugi, nɩɩ o buu Yesu kpee ɔkpa tɔ.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu maa fʋʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, ɔ naa loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kyaabɔɔ. Amaa kpalɩ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ maa tɛɛ lʋwɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ taa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ kpee Betani. Yesu tɔwɛ nnɔ waa figi oyu (Matiwu

english_verse: That evening, Jesus and the disciples went outside the city. A Lesson from the Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Kalaatɩpwɛɛ asɛ nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba lɩɩ kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ. Figi oyu mʋ asɩn (Matiwu

english_verse: They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini? Anɩmʋ ɩ sa fʋ kanɩn ɔlʋn maŋa?”

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‘The Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.’ ”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taasɛ fanɛ kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan saŋɛ mʋ mʋ, fan tɔwɛ odunwura maŋa yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti mʋ, amaa ee biti o bwiiyaa mʋ ba nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ.’ ”

english_verse: The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it,
translation_verse: Kesintin, nɩɩ ba naa wu kekuruma‐bi mʋ maa kyaa kigbenbi sʋ kɩkpaara kʋkʋ kebunɔ. Ba maa saŋɛ mʋ mʋ,

english_verse: some of the people standing there asked, “Why are you untying the donkey?”
translation_verse: nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɛn saŋɛ kekuruma‐bi mʋ?”

english_verse: They told them what Jesus had said, and the people let them take it.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu dan tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ asa mʋ ba yɛgɛ ba taa natɛ.

english_verse: The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Ba taa kekuruma‐bi mʋ bɩya Yesu, taa bamʋ ataa gyaga kuruma mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Yesu dii kyɩna sʋ.

english_verse: In front of Jesus and behind him, people went along shouting, “Hooray! God bless the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu kamaa mʋ kpini bee kuusi yɛ, “Hosana! Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ɛɛ ba mʋ!

english_verse: God bless the coming kingdom of our ancestor David. Hooray for God in heaven above!”
translation_verse: Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa anɛ‐Sɛ Owura Dawidi kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba mʋ! Hosana ɩ gyɛ sa anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ!”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses heard what Jesus said, and they started looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the crowds were completely amazed at his teaching.
translation_verse: Aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba nu kanɩn asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba piili bee biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa mɔɔ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ kɩkaapʋ ki kyinkyin asa damantɛ mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ mʋ.

english_verse: So Jesus said to the tree, “Never again will anyone eat fruit from this tree!” The disciples heard him say this. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ figi oyu mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o gyii fʋ abii mʋ kakaakʋ!” Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ oyu mʋ. Yesu kpee Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ (Matiwu

english_verse: Then he taught the people and said, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship for all nations.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ lan kaapʋ bamʋ yɛ, “Ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘Bee biti ba tɩɩ mɛ kakpaara yɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛkpa sa dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini.’ Amaa fan taa kɩmʋ biliŋaa bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ ɔbaalakpa.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples: Have faith in God!
