translation_verse: Yesu maa gyii nsu kudu anyɔ mʋ, o buu Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu kpee naa gyii kanɩn Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ anaana‐tɔ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: After Passover his parents left, but they did not know that Jesus had stayed on in the city.
translation_verse: Ba maa gyii bamʋ nkɛ lʋwɛ nɩɩ ba bwii bee kpewu mʋ, Yesu sii kamaa Gyerusalem tɔ yɛgɛ mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ ana mʋ ba man nyi.

english_verse: They thought he was traveling with some other people, and they went a whole day before they started looking for him.
translation_verse: Ba nyi fɛɛ ɔ tii kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba maa bamʋ ba naa mʋ sʋ nɩn, nɩɩ ba natɛ kakɛ ibiri pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba piili bee biti mʋ bamʋ kʋʋpʋ ana yɛ bamʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: When they could not find him with their relatives and friends, they went back to Jerusalem and started looking for him there.
translation_verse: Ba maa man wu mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba bwii kpee Gyerusalem ba naa biti mʋ.

english_verse: Three days later they found Jesus sitting in the temple, listening to the teachers and asking them questions.
translation_verse: Kakɛ sasapʋ akɛ mʋ, ba naa wu mʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, maa tɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ asɛ, yɛgɛ ee nu bamʋ asɛ yɛgɛ ɛɛ taasɛ bamʋ asɩn mʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: Everyone who heard him was surprised at how much he knew and at the answers he gave.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kasɛ mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ.

english_verse: When his parents found him, they were amazed. His mother said, “Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been very worried, and we have been searching for you!”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ ba naa wu mʋ mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ, nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Mi‐bii, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ waa anɛ kanɩn? N maa fʋ‐sɛ ansi ɩ payɩ an biti fʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Why did you have to look for me? Didn't you know that I would be in my Father's house?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fan naa fen biti mɛ? Fan man nyi fɛɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n baa n bʋ mɛ‐sɛ ayɛ?”

english_verse: But they did not understand what he meant.
translation_verse: Amaa ba man nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth with his parents and obeyed them. His mother kept on thinking about all that had happened.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o buu Mɛɛrɩ maa Gyosefu kpee Nasareti, yɛgɛ saŋa kʋmaa ee nu bamʋ asɛ. Amaa mu‐nyi taa asɩn nɩmʋ kpini waa mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ ɛɛ fɛ ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

english_verse: Jesus became wise, and he grew strong. God was pleased with him and so were the people.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu dan n‐yɩɩ tɔ yɛ kanyaasɩn tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ asa ansi.

english_verse: When tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked John, “Teacher, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ akʋ gbaa ba ba yɛ o gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa?”

english_verse: Do something to show that you really have given up your sins. Don't start saying you belong to Abraham's family. God can turn these stones into children for Abraham.
translation_verse: Fan waa adanbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fan kyɛɛgɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ fanɛ alibi tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Kɛ fan yɛgɛ kɩtɔwɛ yɛ, ‘An lɩɩ Abraham kɩnaana tɔ nɩn, ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ bɩɩtɛ anɛ kʋsʋ.’ N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ abuu nɩmʋ a biliŋi Abraham anaana‐bii.

english_verse: But to Herod the ruler, he said, “It was wrong for you to take Herodias, your brother's wife.” John also said Herod had done many other bad things.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ Gyɔn pwɩɩ owura Herodi, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ taa mu‐supu Filipu mʋ‐ka Herodiya kili, yɛ asɩn libi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa tii sʋ.

english_verse: Annas and Caiaphas were the Jewish high priests. At that time God spoke to Zechariah's son John, who was living in the desert.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maa nɩɩ Anasi maa Kayifasi ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn sa Sakariya mu‐bii Gyɔn, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ ofuli sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Isaiah the prophet wrote about John when he said, “In the desert someone is shouting, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord! Make a straight path for him.
translation_verse: Gyɔn nɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya wʋla kyʋrɔɔ yɩla mʋ ɔwʋlɛ tɔ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋ bʋ ofuli sʋ ee kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Fan lɔŋɔ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ɔkpa, kɛ fan kyiigi mʋ ɩkpa‐bi mʋ.

english_verse: Fill up every valley and level every mountain and hill. Straighten the crooked paths and smooth out the rough roads.
translation_verse: Kɛ fan tii‐tii igee mʋ, kɛ fan tɩtɛ abɩɩ yɛ abɩɩ‐bi mʋ ba kasɛ waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Kɛ fan kyiigi ɩkpa nyɔnsa mʋ, kɛ ɩkpa gogyigogyi mʋ i tii waa tɔlɩtɔlɩ.

english_verse: Then everyone will see the saving power of God.’ ”
translation_verse: Kɛ daadimaadi kpini ba wu Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ yɛ ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ asa mʋ.’ ”

english_verse: Crowds of people came out to be baptized, but John said to them, “You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ sakyɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ yɛ o gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ yɛ, “Fan du fɛɛ anansɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kʋbwɛ. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ anɩmʋ ɩ da fanɛ amu tɔ yɛ fan sɩlɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ mʋ tɔ?

english_verse: Some soldiers asked him, “And what about us? What do we have to do?” John told them, “Don't force people to pay money to make you leave them alone. Be satisfied with your pay.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ akʋ ba ba taasɛ Gyɔn yɛ, “Nɛnɛ nɩɩ anɛ gbaa anɛ an biti a waa?” Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan man nyan ɔkʋ kɔɔlɛ mu afulee, kɛ fan man pwɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ asɩn a dɛ ɔkpa, amaa ɩmʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ ka fanɛ mʋ ɩ kʋkwɛ fanɛ.”

english_verse: John said, “I am just baptizing with water. But someone more powerful is going to come, and I am not good enough even to untie his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Gyɔn tɔwɛ asa mʋ kpini yɛ, “Nkyu nɩɩ n sʋ n gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɔ mɛ gaa mʋ ɩɩ ba. Nɩɩ n man dagaa gbaa fɛɛ n muŋa saŋɛ mʋ asɩbɩta ɩfɛ. Nɩɩ mɛɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ taa Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ yɛ fʋlɔn gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: In many different ways John preached the good news to the people.
translation_verse: Ɩkpa damantɛ sʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn taa kɩtaa asa mʋ nkɔlɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: Finally, Herod put John in jail, and this was the worst thing he had done. The Baptism of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa alibi mʋ nɩɩ Herodi bɩla waa tii sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ kamaa tɔ mʋ ɔ kɩtaa Gyɔn tii obu. Gyɔn Gyee Yesu Ɩbwaarɛ Kekyugyee (Matiwu

english_verse: While everyone else was being baptized, Jesus himself was baptized. Then as he prayed, the sky opened up,
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn i gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, o gyee Yesu gbaa. Nɩɩ Yesu maa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ sʋsʋ bugi tɔ.

english_verse: and the Holy Spirit came down upon him in the form of a dove. A voice from heaven said, “You are my own dear Son, and I am pleased with you.” The Ancestors of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ kpɩlɩgɛ fɛɛ kuwura‐lɔpɔ ba gyɔŋɔ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐bii bitisa mʋ, nɩɩ fʋ ɩ bɔla mi ansi.” Kɩnaana Mʋ Nɩɩ Yesu Lɩɩ kɩmʋ Tɔ (Matiwu

english_verse: Enosh, and Seth. The family of Jesus went all the way back to Adam and then to God.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Keyinan mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Enosi, nɩɩ Enosi mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Seti, nɩɩ Seti mʋ‐sɛ ɩ gyɛ Adam. Nɩɩ Adam gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii.

english_verse: The Scriptures say: ‘God will tell his angels to take care of you. They will catch you in their arms, and you will not even hurt your feet on the stones.’ ”
translation_verse: Bee biti ba sɔgɩta fʋ, kɛ fʋ man tɩyɛ kasɛ kɛ fʋ ayaa a man da abuu.”

english_verse: After the sun had set, people with all kinds of diseases were brought to Jesus. He put his hands on each one of them and healed them.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ kpee ɔ naa tɩyɛ mʋ, asa mʋ ba taa alɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ alɔ yiri‐yiri mʋ ba Yesu asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ kpini.

english_verse: The devil said to Jesus, “If you are God's Son, tell this stone to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii kʋlʋn mʋ de, yɛgɛ kubuu nɩmʋ ki biliŋi bodobodo.”

english_verse: Then the devil led Jesus up to a high place and quickly showed him all the nations on earth.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kpee kɩbɩɩ bʋnswɩɩsa kʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa awuragyi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ dulinyaa tɔ mʋ kpini kaapʋ mʋ opula tɔ.

english_verse: Just worship me, and you can have it all.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ kan muŋa sun mɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ kpini ii biti i biliŋi fʋ lɛɛ.”

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus to Jerusalem and had him stand on top of the temple. The devil said, “If you are God's Son, jump off.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ ɔ nɩɩ bɩla kpiya mʋ Gyerusalem naa yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ ketin tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ kan fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii kʋlʋn mʋ, yɛgɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ ba tɩyɛ kasɛ.

english_verse: He taught in the Jewish synagogues, and everyone praised him. The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ Abwaarɛsɩn Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ yɩn mʋ. Ba Kina Yesu Mʋ Gbaa‐gbaa Mʋ Kadɛ Tɔ (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and as usual he went to the synagogue on the Sabbath. When he stood up to read from the Scriptures,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o kpee Nasareti katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, o kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɛɛ waa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɛ ɔ kalɛ Abwaarɛsɩn.

english_verse: “The Lord's Spirit has come to me, because he has chosen me to tell the good news to the poor. The Lord has sent me to announce freedom for prisoners, to give sight to the blind, to free everyone who suffers,
translation_verse: “Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ bʋ mɛ asɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ mɛ nɩn fɛɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa atiripu. O sun mɛ yɛ n ba tɔwɛ kamɔlɩgɛ asɩn sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ ibu mʋ. O sun mɛ yɛ n ba bugi agyaatanapʋ ansi, kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ asa bee sisii bamʋ mʋ, bee biti ba nyɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ.

english_verse: and to say, ‘This is the year the Lord has chosen.’ ”
translation_verse: O sun mɛ yɛ n ba da kibonbon fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kamɔlɩgɛ saŋa mʋ fʋʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus closed the book, then handed it back to the man in charge and sat down. Everyone in the synagogue looked straight at Jesus.
translation_verse: Yesu maa kalɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ bɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ bwiiyaa sa ɔmʋnɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ kpini ansi i yii mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: All the people started talking about Jesus and were amazed at the wonderful things he said. They kept on asking, “Isn't he Joseph's son?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ i kyinkyin bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini, nɩɩ ba yɩn mʋ. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, man gyɛ Gyosefu mu‐bii mʋ nɩn?

english_verse: Jesus answered: You will certainly want to tell me this saying, “Doctor, first make yourself well.” You will tell me to do the same things here in my own hometown that you heard I did in Capernaum.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “N nyi fɛɛ fen biti fan bɔ mɛ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ ‘Ɔkyɛpʋ, kyɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.’ Nɩɩ fen biti fan bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an nu fɛɛ fʋ waa Kapanum kadɛ tɔ mʋ, waa ɩmʋ fʋ kadɛ tɔ nfɩɩ.’

english_verse: But you can be sure that no prophets are liked by the people of their own hometown.
translation_verse: “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋmaa nɩmʋ man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ mʋ ayɛ.

english_verse: Once during the time of Elijah there was no rain for three and a half years, and people everywhere were starving. There were many widows in Israel,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n biti fan wu kesintin fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya saŋa mʋ, akulapu‐kyɩɩ ba kyɩna ba kyɔ Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ. Saŋa maŋa tɔ bʋlayɩ yɩlɛ nsu nsa yɛ ɩbwaayɩ isiye nɩɩ akʋn a ba ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kpini sʋ.

english_verse: but Elijah was sent only to a widow in the town of Zarephath near the city of Sidon.
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ man sun Eligya bamʋ ɔkʋlʋn gbaa asɛ, amɔɔ okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Sarefati kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Sidon kabaafʋn sʋ mʋ nkʋn asɛ.

english_verse: During the time of the prophet Elisha, many men in Israel had leprosy. But no one was healed, except Naaman who lived in Syria.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Elisa saŋa mʋ, abootipu ba kyɔ Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, amaa Elisa man kyɛ bamʋ ɔkʋ, amɔɔ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Naaman mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Kesiriya‐nyɩn mʋ nkʋn nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ.”

english_verse: But Jesus slipped through the crowd and got away. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Mark
translation_verse: Amaa ɔ dan natɛ bʋlɛ asa damantɛ mʋ nsana kyʋn. Yesu Gya nbwɩɩ libi Lɩɩ Ɔnyɩn Kʋ Tɔ (Maki

english_verse: His teaching amazed them because he spoke with power.
translation_verse: Mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ki kyinkyin bamʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ sʋ ɔlʋn.

english_verse: There in the synagogue was a man with an evil spirit. He yelled out,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi, nɩɩ kayaayu bʋ mʋ sʋ, maa wu Yesu mʋ, nɩɩ o kuusi kenken yɛ,

english_verse: Jesus ordered the evil spirit to be quiet and come out. The demon threw the man to the ground in front of everyone and left without harming him.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu pʋntɛ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ sʋ yɛ, “Laatɔ, kɛ fʋ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ.” Nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ taa kanyɩn mʋ tʋʋ kasɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ kyʋn yɛgɛ ɔ man taalɛ waa mʋ sɛhn.

english_verse: They all were amazed and kept saying to each other, “What kind of teaching is this? He has power to order evil spirits out of people!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ waa asa mʋ kpini kɩyan nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, “Mɩnɛ kɩkaapʋ ndee? Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ taa kɩnyɩn yɛ ɔlʋn ɛɛ pʋntɛ n‐yaayu libi sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɩɩ asa tɔ!”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn mʋ ɩ yɛlɩgɛ kyaabɔɔ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini. Yesu Kyɛ Alɔpʋ damantɛ (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus left the synagogue and went to Simon's home. When Jesus got there, he was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick with a high fever.
translation_verse: Yesu maa lɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa obu mʋ tɔ mʋ, o kpee Simon ayɛ. Yɛgɛ Simon mʋ‐saa kyɩɩsa dɛ ɛɛ lɔ kibirima bʋlʋnsa, nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ ɔ ba kɩɩ mʋ.

english_verse: So Jesus went over to her and ordered the fever to go away. Right then she was able to get up and serve them a meal.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ naa yɩlɛ mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kibirima mʋ yɛ kɩ lɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kibirima mʋ kɩ yɛgɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ.

english_verse: Demons went out of many people and shouted, “You are the Son of God!” But Jesus ordered the demons not to speak because they knew he was the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ asa gaalaagaa tɔ, yɛgɛ bee kuusi kenken bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ.” Amaa nɩɩ ɔ pʋntɛ bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man yɛgɛ bamʋ ba tɔwɛ sɛhn, lɩɩ fɛɛ be nyi maa gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: The next morning Jesus went out to a place where he could be alone, and crowds came looking for him. When they found him, they tried to stop him from leaving.
translation_verse: Kayɩɩ maa kɛ mʋ Yesu kpee opula mʋ nɩɩ i du surum, nɩɩ asa mʋ ba lɩɩ ba naa bee biti mʋ. Ba maa ba wu mʋ mʋ, ba man bɩla bee biti fɛɛ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “People in other towns must hear the good news about God's kingdom. This is why I was sent.”
translation_verse: Amaa ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n maa tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa ndɛ kʋ asa gbaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mɛ.”

english_verse: But Jesus would often go to some place where he could be alone and pray. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Walk (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa kʋmaa Yesu ɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ nkʋn ee kpee ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, opula mʋ nɩɩ o du surum. Yesu Kyɛ N‐yɩɩ Wusa Wuya (Matiwu

english_verse: When Jesus saw how much faith they had, he said to the man, “My friend, your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ “Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ n taa fu alibi kɛɛ fu.”

english_verse: Jesus got into the boat that belonged to Simon and asked him to row it out a little way from the shore. Then Jesus sat down in the boat to teach the crowd.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ kʋkʋ, mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Simon lɛɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ o nin waa tɔ gbɛrɛɛ. Ɔkʋlɩ maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Yesu kyʋn kyɩna yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sarɛ tʋtɔ mʋ abwaarɛsɩn.

english_verse: “Master,” Simon answered, “we have worked hard all night long and have not caught a thing. But if you tell me to, I will let the nets down.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Simon lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mɛ ɔbɩlɩsa, an tʋʋ nɩn halɩɩ ba kalayɩ, a man kɩtaa sɛhn, amaa nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ, n biti n tʋʋ.”

english_verse: They did this and caught so many fish that their nets began ripping apart.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba maa tʋʋ asawu mʋ, ba kɩtaa ɩkɩn kɩbansa halɩɩ bamʋ asawu mʋ ɩ san gbaa kyatɛ.

english_verse: Then they signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. The men came, and together they filled the two boats so full that they both began to sink.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba da abaa tɩɩ bamʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔkʋlɩ nyɔsapʋ mʋ tɔ yɛ ba ba kyɛ bamʋ tɔ. Ɩkʋlɩ ɩnyɔ mʋ kpini yɛ ɩmaa ɩmʋ ɩkɩn mʋ ɩ san gbaa muuri.

english_verse: His partners James and John, the sons of Zebedee, were surprised too. Jesus told Simon, “Don't be afraid! From now on you will bring in people instead of fish.”
translation_verse: Ɩkɩn mʋ kɩkɩta mʋ gbaa ki kyinkyin Gyemisi maa Gyɔn, Sebedi mu‐bii ana mʋ nɩɩ ba maa Simon bee sun kusun mʋ. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Simon yɛ, “Man waa kufuu, lɩɩ ndaga ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, fii biti fʋ ba fɩɩ kɩtaa asa fɩɩ bɩya mɛ nɩn.”

english_verse: The men pulled their boats up on the shore. Then they left everything and went with Jesus. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba bɩɩtɛ ɩkʋlɩ mʋ ba dan kidenbi sʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu. Yesu Kyɛ Kibootoo Wuya Kʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: News about Jesus kept spreading. Large crowds came to listen to him teach and to be healed of their diseases.
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn mʋ ɩ lɔŋɔ yalagɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa damantɛ ba be nu mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ mʋ kpini.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they went up on the roof, where they removed some tiles and let the mat down in the middle of the room.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba man nyɛ ɔkpa ba taa mʋ loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba dii kpee obu mʋ sʋsʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ɩsɛɛ nyalɛ munla mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba foti ɔbɔ kpɩlɩgaa mʋ yɛgɛ ɔ dɛ mʋ kalan sʋ sakyɔ mʋ nsana yɩla Yesu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: The Pharisees and the experts began arguing, “Jesus must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ ba piili bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ “Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔsa nɩmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn kanɩn? Anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ taa alibi kɛɛ tii Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ?”

english_verse: Is it easier for me to tell this man that his sins are forgiven or to tell him to get up and walk?
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kɩmɔɔ ɩ bʋsɩn kɩtɔwɛ. N taa fʋ alibi kɛɛ fʋ, abɛɛ kʋsʋ natɛ?

english_verse: But now you will see that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” Jesus then said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and walk home.”
translation_verse: N biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n sʋ ɔlʋn dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ n taalɛ taa alibi kɛɛ.” Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ i wu mʋ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, Kʋsʋ! Taa fʋ kalan kpewu.”

english_verse: At once the man stood up in front of everyone. He picked up his mat and went home, giving thanks to God.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kalan nɩɩ ɔ dɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ ee kpewu.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed and praised God. What they saw surprised them, and they said, “We have seen a great miracle today!” Jesus Chooses Levi (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa bamʋ kufuu nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ndaga an wu asɩn kyinkyinsa.” Yesu Tɩɩ Lewi Yɛ Ɔ Ba Buu Mʋ Matiwu

english_verse: Later, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Yesu lɩɩ nɩɩ ɔ naa wu lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Lewi mu kusun obu to. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ buu mɛ.”

english_verse: Levi left everything and went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ kʋsʋ yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩɩ o buu Yesu.

english_verse: In his home Levi gave a big dinner for Jesus. Many tax collectors and other guests were also there.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Lewi dɩŋa agyitɔ kʋkyɔ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ sa Yesu, nɩɩ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ gaalaagaa yɛ asa akʋ ba bʋga tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ba maa Yesu bee gyii.

english_verse: The Pharisees and some of their teachers of the Law of Moses grumbled to Jesus' disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with these tax collectors and other sinners?”
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ gbaa bamʋ gyɛ Farasii abi mʋ bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ bɛɛ sa Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fanaa lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ fen bʋga fen gyii fen nuu kanɩn?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔkyɛpʋ man tiri sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ alanfɩya, amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ mʋ nkʋn.

english_verse: I didn't come to invite good people to turn to God. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Matthew
translation_verse: N man ba yɛ n ba tɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩn. Amaa n ba nɩn yɛ n ba tɩɩ alibi awaapʋ kɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.” Asɩn Taasɛsa Lɩɩ, Kɔnɔ Ŋmina Sʋ Matiwu

english_verse: Some people said to Jesus, “John's followers often pray and go without eating, and so do the followers of the Pharisees. But your disciples never go without eating or drinking.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa akʋ ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Gyɔn okyugyeepu abɩɩlapʋ bee ŋmina kɔnɔ yɛgɛ bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ saŋa kʋmaa, kanɩn nɩɩ Farasii abi abɩɩlapʋ mʋ gbaa bɛɛ waa nɩn. Amaa fɛɛ fʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ bɩrɛ ba dan bee gyii bee nuu nɩn saŋa kʋmaa.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ afɔ ba ŋmina kɔnɔ yɛgɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli bʋ bamʋ asɛ?

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.”
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, kɛ ba taa ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ, kanɩn nkɛ maŋa tɔ bɩrɛ bee biti ba ŋmina kɔnɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus then told them these sayings: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and make the hole even bigger.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kɩnɩmʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋ mɛɛ tɩn kikyibi pʋpwɛ naa baata mata kaalɛ dɩdaa ɔbɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan waa kanɩn mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kikyibi pʋpwɛ mʋ ɩ kyatɛ kaalɛ dɩdaa mʋ i tii sʋ nɩn, nɩɩ kikyibi dɩdaa mʋ yɛ kɩpʋpwɛ mʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ baa i duli abaa.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The new wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined.
translation_verse: Kanɩn nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taa ɔwʋlɛ dɩdaa ee sun kʋkɔrɛ pʋpwɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa kanɩn bɩrɛ, ɔwʋlɛ dɩdaa mʋ ii biti ɔ kyatɛ, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ yɛgɛ kʋkɔrɛ pʋpwɛ mʋ kɩ waa ɔdan.

english_verse: New wine must be put only into new wineskins.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɔwʋlɛ pʋpwɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bee sun kʋkɔrɛ pʋpwɛ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he told the man to stand up where everyone could see him. And the man stood up.
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu wʋla o n‐yɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ kɩbaa ki wu mʋ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ asa mʋ kpini ansi tɔ.” Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees said, “Why are you picking grain on the Sabbath? You're not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Farasii abi mʋ akʋ ba taasɛ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan waa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ ikisii?”

english_verse: Long ago your own people did these same things to the prophets. So when this happens to you, be happy and jump for joy! You will have a great reward in heaven.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ba kan waa fanɛ kanɩn mʋ fan yɛgɛ ɩ waa fanɛ ɔkɔn, kɛ fan taa kensi‐gyii kyaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ kɛkɛɛ lala kɩ dɛ sʋsʋ kii gyoo fanɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ kanɩn nɩɩ bamʋ naana ana gbaa ba taa waa Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Ask God to bless anyone who curses you, and pray for everyone who is cruel to you.
translation_verse: Kɛ fan waa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ nnɔ mʋ kusee, kɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyanla fanɛ.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “On the Sabbath should we do good deeds or evil deeds? Should we save someone's life or destroy it?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “N taasɛ fanɛ, mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nɩɩ anɛ nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ a waa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ? A waa adanbɩrasa abɛɛ alibi? A mɔlɩgɛ asa nkpa abɛɛ a nyita ɩmʋ?”

english_verse: One was Simon, and Jesus named him Peter. Another was Andrew, Peter's brother. There were also James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
translation_verse: Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Simon , yɛ mu‐supu Andiruwu, yɛ Gyemisi maa Gyɔn, yɛ Filipu maa Batolome,

english_verse: On another Sabbath Jesus was teaching in a synagogue, and a man with a paralyzed right hand was there.
translation_verse: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kʋ biyo Yesu bɩla kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɛɛ kaapʋ. Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ mʋ gyisa kɩbaa ki wu.

english_verse: The teachers and the Pharisees were furious and started saying to one another, “What can we do about Jesus?” Jesus Chooses His Twelve Apostles (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa agbʋ a kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ bee ŋmina kanan mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa Yesu. Yesu Asunpu Kudu‐Anyɔ Mʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: The next morning he called his disciples together and chose twelve of them to be his apostles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kayɩɩ kɩ maa kɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kpini ba mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ tɔ kudu anyɔ, nɩɩ ɔ yɩla bamʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛ asunpu.

english_verse: Everyone was trying to touch Jesus, because power was going out from him and healing them all. Blessings and Troubles (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ kpini ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ ba mata mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔlʋn kʋ bʋ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ ɩ kyɛ bamʋ kpini alɔ. Amu Ɔkɔn Wuya Ana Yɛ Amu Libi Wuya Ana

english_verse: Matthew, Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus. The rest of the apostles were Simon, known as the Eager One,
translation_verse: yɛ Matiwu maa Tomasi, yɛ Alifeyo mu‐bii Gyemisi, yɛ Simon mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Seloti,

english_verse: You well-fed people are in for trouble. You will go hungry! You people who are laughing now are in for trouble. You are going to cry and weep!
translation_verse: Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan gyii ŋmɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ falɛ mɔɔ, fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋn ii biti ɩɩ tɩyɛ fanɛ sʋ. Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn ŋmasɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ falɛ mɔɔ mʋ, fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fen biti fan su kɛ fan bɔɔta tɔ.

english_verse: These people had come to listen to Jesus and to be healed of their diseases. All who were troubled by evil spirits were also healed.
translation_verse: Ba ba nu mʋ asɛ kɛ ba nyɛ bamʋ alɔ kɩ kyɛ. Bamʋ nɩɩ n‐yaayu libi bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ ba nyɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ.

english_verse: God will bless you hungry people. You will have plenty to eat! God will bless you people who are now crying. You will laugh!
translation_verse: Amu ɔkɔn wuya ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ akʋn a mɔɔ fanɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ fan ŋmɛ. Amu ɔkɔn wuya ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fen su nbɩɩnbɩɩ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ fan ŋmasɛ.

english_verse: God will bless you when others hate you and won't have anything to do with you. God will bless you when people insult you and say cruel things about you, all because you are a follower of the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Amu ɔkɔn wuya ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa bee kisi fanɛ, bee kina fanɛ, bɛɛ saalɛ fanɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩn antɩŋɛsa libi bɛɛ gyaga fanɛ sʋ, lɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: You are in for trouble when everyone says good things about you. That is what your own people said about those prophets who told lies. Love for Enemies (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, fanɛ laakʋ. Kanɩn nɩɩ bamʋ naana ana ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩn atɩŋɛsa bɛɛ gyaga Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ mʋ.” Fan Biti Fanɛ Dʋn Ana (Matiwu

english_verse: If you give to others, you will be given a full amount in return. It will be packed down, shaken together, and spilling over into your lap. The way you treat others is the way you will be treated.
translation_verse: Fan sa, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ɔ sa fanɛ. Ee biti ɔ kaala, bɔla kʋtɔ kaalasa mʋ, kɛ ɔ nyɛɛ sʋ, kɛ o gyingyen kɩmʋ, kɛ ɩ bɔla wʋrɩgɛ fanɛ abaa tɔ. Kʋtɔ kaalasa mʋ nɩɩ fan taa kaala mʋ, kɩmʋ maa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa kaala sa fanɛ.”

english_verse: is like someone who dug down deep and built a house on solid rock. When a flood came and the river rushed against the house, it was built so well that it didn't even shake.
translation_verse: Kanɩn ɔsa maŋa du nɩn fɛɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ pɔyɩ kɩkpaara, nɩɩ o kuu loo kasɛ nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ ɔpɔrɛ gyankpaasa mʋ gyaga kɩfʋlɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ nkyu n bɔla nɩɩ ɩ da kɩkpaara mʋ, amaa kɩ man gyingyen lɩɩ fɛɛ maa pɔyɩ kɩmʋ danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: If someone slaps you on one cheek, don't stop that person from slapping you on the other cheek. If someone wants to take your coat, don't try to keep back your shirt.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan da fʋ ɔtan mʋ, biliŋi kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ sa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan kɔɔlɛ fʋ balɩmaasʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ taa fʋ kaalɛ tii sʋ.

english_verse: Give to everyone who asks and don't ask people to return what they have taken from you.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba kʋlɛ fʋ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, sa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taa fʋ kʋtɔ kʋ mʋ, man bɩla naa taasɛ kʋtɔ maŋa sʋ lɩɩ odunwura mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: Treat others just as you want to be treated.
translation_verse: Kɛ fan waa fanɛ tɔɔmaa ana fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ ba waa sa fanɛ mʋ.

english_verse: If you love only someone who loves you, will God praise you for that? Even sinners love people who love them.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ nɩɩ fan kan fen biti bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti fanɛ nkʋn mʋ, mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ bɩla yɩn fanɛ? Halɩɩ alibi awaapʋ gbaa bee biti bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti bamʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Have pity on others, just as your Father has pity on you. Judging Others (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan wu asa kʋwɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ii wu asa kʋwɛɛ mʋ. Fan Man Bun Abaa Kʋpwɛ (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus said: Don't judge others, and God won't judge you. Don't be hard on others, and God won't be hard on you. Forgive others, and God will forgive you.
translation_verse: “Fan man talɛ fanɛ tɔɔmaa ana, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ɔ man talɛ fanɛ. Fan man bun asa kʋpwɛ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ɔ man bun fanɛ kʋpwɛ. Fan taa kɛɛ abaa, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa ɔ taa kɛɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: Jesus also used some sayings as he spoke to the people. He said: Can one blind person lead another blind person? Won't they both fall into a ditch?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ, “Gyaatanapʋ ɩ taalɛ gyankpaa mʋ tɔɔmaa gyaatanapʋ ɔkpa? Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba kanɩn mʋ, bamʋ asa anyɔ kpini bee biti ba naa lɩɩ tɩyɛ kɩkyabɔ tɔ.

english_verse: Are students better than their teacher? But when they are fully trained, they will be like their teacher.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔbɩɩlapʋ mɛɛ taalɛ ŋara mʋ ɔkaapʋpʋ, amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla bɩɩ bɩrɛ ɛɛ taalɛ waa fɛɛ mʋ ɔkaapʋpʋ.

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fu wu keyuubi kɩ maa bʋ fʋ tɔɔmaa kensi tɔ, nɩɩ fu mee wu kagyapoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ lɛɛ tɔ?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye? You show-offs! First, get the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye. A Tree and Its Fruit (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ fʋ tɔɔmaa yɛ, ‘Mɛ tɔɔmaa, yɛgɛ n lɛɛ keyuubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fu kensi tɔ falɛ! Yɛgɛ fʋ mɛɛ kɩɩ kagyapoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fu kensi tɔ?’ Fʋ kebunbun ansi wuya, gyankpaa lɛɛ kagyapoti mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ kensi tɔ, pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ taalɛ kɩɩ danbɩrasa lɛɛ keyuubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fʋ tɔɔmaa lɛɛ tɔ. Oyu Kɩmaa Nɩmʋ Kɩmaa Kɩmʋ Abi (Matiwu

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ oyu danbɩrasa kʋ man bʋ tɔ kɩ sɔyɩ abi libi, abɛɛ oyu libi mɛɛ sɔyɩ abi danbɩrasa.

english_verse: You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces. You cannot pick figs or grapes from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Oyu kʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩmʋ abi sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ bɩɩ kɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ tɩyɛ akutu ɛɛ lɩɩ kɩkpalɛ oyu sʋ, abɛɛ kididobi lɩɩ kimango oyu sʋ.

english_verse: Good people do good things because of the good in their hearts, but bad people do bad things because of the evil in their hearts. Your words show what is in your heart. Two Builders (Matthew
translation_verse: Nyɩmɩsa danbɩrasa ɩɩ waa atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla yɩla mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa libi mɔɔ ɩɩ waa atɔ libi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla yɩla mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa kɔnɔ ɩɩ tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ nɩn. Kɩŋasan Lɩɩ Asa Anyɔ Akpaara Kɩpɔyɩ Sʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: Why do you keep on saying that I am your Lord, when you refuse to do what I say?
translation_verse: “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fan tɩɩ mɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ,’ yɛgɛ fan mɛɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan waa?

english_verse: But anyone who hears what I say and doesn't obey me is like someone whose house wasn't built on solid rock. As soon as the river rushed against that house, it was smashed to pieces!
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu mɛ asɩn, nɩɩ ɔ man buu ɩmʋ mʋ, o du nɩn fɛɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kuu ɔbɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ɔ pɔ́yɩ́ kɩkpaara mʋ gyaga kusuupu sʋ. Nɩɩ nkyu maa bɔla da kɩkpaara mʋ, nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ yaasɛ tɔ kpini‐kpini.”

english_verse: He loves our nation and even built us a synagogue.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa anɛ Gyiwu abi, halɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa sa anɛ.”

english_verse: The leaders went to Jesus and begged him to do something. They said, “This man deserves your help!
translation_verse: Ba maa ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, ba dan baasɛ kʋlɛ mʋ gaa tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ maa kyɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Simon answered, “I suppose it would be the one who had owed more and didn't have to pay it back.” “You are right,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Simon lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ mu afulee akyɔ mʋ.” Nɩɩ Yesu yɛ, “Fʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ kesintin.”

english_verse: And I am certainly not worthy to come to you. Just say the word, and my servant will get well.
translation_verse: Kanɩn gbaa nɩɩ n wu fɛɛ n man dagaa fɛɛ n ba fʋ asɛ. Fɛɛ fʋ dan sa kɔnɔ mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ ii biti ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: The boy sat up and began to speak. Jesus then gave him back to his mother.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ olewupu mʋ kʋsʋ kyɩna, nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ, nɩɩ Yesu taa mʋ sa mu‐nyi.

english_verse: Everyone was frightened and praised God. They said, “A great prophet is here with us! God has come to his people.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ kpini nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ lala kʋ lɩɩ anɛ sʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ ba ɔ ba kyɛ mʋ asa tɔ.”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over Judea and everywhere else in that part of the country. John the Baptist (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ yalagɛ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, yɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ ɩ maa kɩmʋ mʋ. Gyɔn Okyugyeepu Sun Asa Yesu Asɛ (Matiwu

english_verse: John's followers told John everything that was being said about Jesus. So he sent two of them to ask the Lord, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: nɩɩ o sun bamʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu asɛ yɛ ba naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ ba mʋ abɛɛ a gyoo kɩɩ ɔkʋ ɔkpa?”

english_verse: When these messengers came to Jesus, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to ask, ‘Are you the one we should be looking for? Or are we supposed to wait for someone else?’ ”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Gyɔn okyugyeepu i sun anɛ fʋ asɛ yɛ abaa taasɛ fʋ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ ba mʋ abɛɛ a gyoo kɩɩ ɔkʋ ɔkpa?”

english_verse: At that time Jesus was healing many people who were sick or in pain or were troubled by evil spirits, and he was giving sight to a lot of blind people.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maa tɔ mʋ Yesu kyɛ asa gaalaagaa alobi, nɩɩ ɔ gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ asa tɔ, nɩɩ o bugi agyaatanapʋ gaalaagaa ansi.

english_verse: Jesus said to the messengers sent by John, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard. Blind people are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People who have leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can now hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ lɛɛ Gyɔn nbɔɔ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan bwii naa tɔwɛ Gyɔn kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan wu yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan nu. Agyaatanapʋ ansi ii bugi, nɩɩ aboobi bɛɛ kʋsʋ natɛ, nɩɩ abootipu bɛɛ nyɛ alanfɩya, nɩɩ akpawu asu ii tigi, nɩɩ alewupu bee kyiŋi bɛɛ ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ atiripu bee nu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Fan bɩla tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ san ɔ sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mɛ tɔ.”

english_verse: After John's messengers had gone, Jesus began speaking to the crowds about John: What kind of person did you go out to the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn nbɔɔ ba lɩɩ mʋ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ Gyɔn asɩn ɛɛ sa kʋbʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan maa lɩɩ kpee Gyɔn asɛ ofuli mʋ sʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fan waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ fen kpee fɛn naa kɩɩ? Kʋkyɔ mʋ nɩɩ afuu a da ii fiilaa mʋ?

english_verse: What kind of man did you really go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who wear expensive clothes and live in luxury are in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ fan kpee naa wu? Abɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ o bun ɔgyalɩn atɔ? Ayee! Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bun ɔgyalɩn atɔ nɩɩ ba sʋ kakyɩna danbɩrasa mʋ, ba bʋ awurakpa nɩn.

english_verse: In the Scriptures, God calls John his messenger and says, “I am sending my messenger ahead of you to get things ready for you.”
translation_verse: Gyɔn nɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘N biti n sun mɛ kabɔɔ kɛ ɔ gyankpaa fʋ, mʋ ii biti ɔ lɔŋɔ fʋ ɔkpa gyoo fʋ.’

english_verse: No one ever born on this earth is greater than John. But whoever is least important in God's kingdom is greater than John.
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ mʋ, man kyɔ Gyɔn, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o du pii sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii tɔ mʋ kyɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: Everyone had been listening to John. Even the tax collectors had obeyed God and had done what was right by letting John baptize them.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ kpini yɛ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba nu Gyɔn asɩn tɔwɛsa mʋ, ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ i kyiigi, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyɔn gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: But the Pharisees and the experts in the Law of Moses refused to obey God and be baptized by John.
translation_verse: Amaa Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ anyipu ba kina nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba man yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyɔn gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: Jesus went on to say: What are you people like? What kind of people are you?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa ndaga kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ asa kaala. Anɩmʋ ana nɩɩ ba duli?

english_verse: You are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other, “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not cry!”
translation_verse: Ba du nɩn fɛɛ nbii mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kɩgya tɔ bɛɛ tɩɩ abaa bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘An fʋlɛ kensi‐gyii nbɩlɛ sa fanɛ, amaa fan man kyaa, nɩɩ an sawʋ keli alengyi sa fanɛ, amaa fan man su’.

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “John has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: “Lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu maa ba mʋ, ee ŋmina kɔnɔ gaa, nɩɩ ɔ man nuu nta, nɩɩ fan yɛ ‘Ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi.’

english_verse: But because the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, you say, “Jesus eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n maa ba mʋ, n gyii nɩɩ n nuu, nɩɩ fan yɛ, ‘N gyɛ ogyipu lala yɛ ɔtabʋʋpʋ, nɩɩ n bɩla n gyɛ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ bamʋ kyɛmɩnɛ.’

english_verse: Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what its followers do. Simon the Pharisee
translation_verse: Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ bee buu Ɩbwaarɛ kanyaasɩn, lɩɩ bamʋ‐daa sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ kanyaasɩn mʋ bʋrɔn.” Ɔkyɩɩ Alibi Wuya Kʋ Taa Tulale Sʋswa Yesu (Matiwu

english_verse: A Pharisee invited Jesus to have dinner with him. So Jesus went to the Pharisee's home and got ready to eat.
translation_verse: Saŋa kʋ tɔ nɩɩ Kafarasii‐nyɩn kʋ tɩɩ Yesu yɛ ɔ ba kɛ ba naa bʋga gyii. Nɩɩ Yesu kpee mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna o gyii.

english_verse: When a sinful woman in that town found out that Jesus was there, she bought an expensive bottle of perfume.
translation_verse: Kanɩn kadɛ maŋa tɔ mʋ ɔkyɩɩ gyaga kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ. Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ maa nu fɛɛ Yesu bʋ Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ ee gyii mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩya tulale liifensa pʋrʋntʋwa bɔla.

english_verse: Then she came and stood behind Jesus. She cried and started washing his feet with her tears and drying them with her hair. The woman kissed his feet and poured the perfume on them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba yɩlɛ Yesu kamaa tiri mʋ ayaa asɛ ee su ansikyu ɛɛ pɔɔla mʋ ayaa. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mu inyini ɛɛ kpayɩ Yesu ayaa sʋ, nɩɩ o munkura ɩmʋ bɛɛrɛɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ tulale mʋ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ ayaa sʋ.

english_verse: The Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this and said to himself, “If this man really were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him! He would know that she is a sinner.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Yesu mʋ maa wu ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ yɛ, “Fɛɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ dɩn dan ɔ gyɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kesintin nɩn, ɔ dɩn bɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ mata mʋ falɛ, ɔ dɩn bɩɩ kɩbaabʋtɔ gyaga mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the Pharisee, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Teacher, what is it?” Simon replied.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ Simon yɛ, “N sʋ kʋtɔ kʋ kɛ n tɔwɛ fʋ.” Nɩɩ Simon yɛ, “N nu, Ɔkaapʋpʋ, tɔwɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “Two people were in debt to a moneylender. One of them owed him
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔɔ mʋ kɩŋasan yɛ, “Anyɩn anyɔ akʋ ɩ naa paala afulee lɩɩ afulee wuya kʋ asɛ. Ɔkʋ paala gbiti nderebi anu, ɔnyɔsapʋ mɔɔ adunuu.

english_verse: But Jesus told the woman, “Because of your faith, you are now saved. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ mɔlɩgɛ fʋ. Natɛ yɛ kɔkɔlɔ yuuli.”

english_verse: Since neither of them could pay him back, the moneylender said that they didn't have to pay him anything. Which one of them will like him more?”
translation_verse: Bamʋ ɔkʋ man taalɛ nyɛ ɔ naa ka mʋ kʋkɔ. Nɩɩ afulee wuya mʋ taa kɛɛ bamʋ asa anyɔ. Asa anyɔ maŋa tɔ mʋ, anɩmʋ ii biti o biti afulee wuya mʋ asɩn gaa?”

english_verse: He turned toward the woman and said to Simon, “Have you noticed this woman? When I came into your home, you didn't give me any water so I could wash my feet. But she has washed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi kɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Simon yɛ, “Yɛ fu wu ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ? N maa ba fʋ kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, fʋ man sa mɛ nkyu kɛ n fwɩɩ mɛ ayaa sʋ, amaa ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ taa mʋ ansikyu fwɩɩ mɛ ayaa, nɩɩ ɔ taa mu inyini kpayɩ ɩmʋ.

english_verse: You didn't greet me with a kiss, but from the time I came in, she has not stopped kissing my feet.
translation_verse: Fʋ man baari mɛ waa mɛ nsa‐nsa, amaa lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n ba nfɩɩ mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ man pɩɩ yɛgɛ mɛ ayaa sʋ kimunkura.

english_verse: You didn't even pour olive oil on my head, but she has poured expensive perfume on my feet.
translation_verse: Fʋ man taa olifu nfɔ wʋrɩgɛ mi kumu tɔ, amaa ɔkyɩɩ nɩmʋ taa tulale wʋrɩgɛ mɛ ayaa sʋ.

english_verse: So I tell you that all her sins are forgiven, and that is why she has shown great love. But anyone who has been forgiven for only a little will show only a little love.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, kebiti lala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ falɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ba taa mu alibi damantɛ mʋ kɛɛ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ mu alibi a man kyɔ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kɩtaakɛɛ mʋ, ɛɛ kaapʋ kebiti mʋ gbaa gbɛrɛɛ nɩn.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “N taa fu alibi kɛɛ fʋ.”

english_verse: Some other guests started saying to one another, “Who is this who dares to forgive sins?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san ba tɛ ogyikpa mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, “Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔnɩmʋ, halɩɩ ɔ sʋ alibi ɛɛ kɛɛ?”

english_verse: and so were some women who had been healed of evil spirits and all sorts of diseases. One of the women was Mary Magdalene, who once had seven demons in her.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ akyɩɩ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gya n‐yaayu libi lɩɩ bamʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ alɔ mʋ, gbaa ba buu mʋ. Bamʋ ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu gya nbwɩɩ libi asunɔ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ,

english_verse: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it. But they don't have deep roots, and they believe only for a little while. As soon as life gets hard, they give up.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ afʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ bɛɛ taa kensi‐gyii kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ. Amaa asɩn mʋ man loo bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ. Bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ kɩ kan ba tu bamʋ de, bɛɛ lɩɩ tɩyɛ nɩn.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on rocky ground and started growing. But the plants did not have enough water and soon dried up.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ tɩyɛ afʋlɛ sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩsɛ ɩ man kyɔ sa ɩmʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ maa kɔyɩ mʋ, ɩmʋ ilin ɩ man taalɛ kpee kata. Ɩmʋ sʋ kyɔwɛ maa daa ɩmʋ mʋ nɩɩ i fugya.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩkʋ mɔɔ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ nɩɩ ɩmaa ɩmʋ ɩ bʋga dan nɩɩ iwii mʋ ɩ dan nyan ɩmʋ.

english_verse: So he answered: I have explained the secrets about God's kingdom to you. But for others I use stories, so they will look, but not see, and they will hear, but not understand. Jesus Explains the Story about a Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fanɛ bɩrɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mu kuwura‐gyii mʋ asiri asɩn kaapʋ fanɛ, amaa asansa bɩrɛ aŋasan tɔ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ sa bamʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ, ‘bee biti ba ba bɛɛ kɩɩ, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu, kɛ ba ba bee nu, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nu kasɛ.’ ” Yesu Lɛɛ Kɩŋasan mʋ Kasɛ Lɩɩ Ɔdɔɔpʋ Mʋ Sʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: There is nothing hidden that will not be found. There is no secret that will not be well known.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ baala gbaa kii biti kɩ lɩɩ ifuli, nɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ asiri atɔ kpini ii biti ɩ lɩɩ ifuli.

english_verse: and while they were sailing across, he went to sleep. Suddenly a storm struck the lake, and the boat started sinking. They were in danger.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ palɛ bee kpee mʋ Yesu dɛ nsɩɩrɛ. Nɩɩ afuu lala akʋ a tigi lɩɩ bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ apaa a da a loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ o muuri, nɩɩ bamʋ nkɔlɔ ɩ ŋmaa bamʋ.

english_verse: This is what the story means: The seed is God's message,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kɩŋasan mʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, abi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ŋmatɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ.

english_verse: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they are so eager for riches and pleasures that they never produce anything.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ tɩyɛ iwii tɔ mɔɔ mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn mʋ, amaa lɩɩ kakyɩna tɔ nfɛɛrɛ kɩfɛ, kufulee biti yɛ kensi‐gyii bansa i kun bamʋ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ taalɛ dan kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the disciples, “Don't you have any faith?” But they were frightened and amazed. They said to each other, “Who is this? He can give orders to the wind and the waves, and they obey him!” A Man with Demons in Him (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii bʋ fɩnɛ?” Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ i kyinkyin bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Nyɩmɩsa mɔɔ ndee nɩɩ ɛɛ sa afuu yɛ apaa kɔnɔ nɩɩ i nu mʋ asɛ.” Yesu Kyɛ Ɔnyɩn Kʋ mʋ nɩɩ Ɔ Sʋ Nbwɩɩ Libi (Matiwu

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and puts it under a bowl or under a bed. A lamp is always put on a lampstand, so people who come into a house will see the light.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ kɩŋasan kʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋ mee tuwa fɩtɩla kɛ ɔ taa waa kolobi tɔ abɛɛ ɔ taa yii npaa kasɛ, amaa ɛɛ taa ɛɛ gyaga kɩmʋ ɔgyagakpa nɩn, kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ ba mʋ ba wu fɩtɩla mʋ.

english_verse: But Jesus answered, “Someone touched me, because I felt power going out from me.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔsa kʋ mata mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n wu nɩɩ n bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔlʋn kʋ maa lɩɩ mɛ tɔ.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he told Jairus, “Don't worry! Have faith, and your daughter will get well.”
translation_verse: Yesu maa nu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Gyarosi yɛ, “Man waa kufuu, fɛɛ fʋ dan nyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ee biti ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.”

english_verse: They begged Jesus not to send them to the deep pit, where they would be punished.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, ɔ man taa bamʋ naa waa ɔbɔ kyinkyingilin mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The people went out to see what had happened, and when they came to Jesus, they also found the man. The demons had gone out of him, and he was sitting there at the feet of Jesus. He had clothes on and was in his right mind. But the people were terrified.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba ba kɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa. Ba maa ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, ba wu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba kʋsʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, o bun kaalɛ ɔ tɛ Yesu ayaa kasɛ yɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ i du kyɛɛkyɛɛ, nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers went to see him, but because of the crowd they could not get near him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana ba ba yɛ ba be wu mʋ, amaa lɩɩ sakyɔ sʋ mʋ, ba man nyɛ nin tiri mʋ.

english_verse: As Jesus was getting out of the boat, he was met by a man from this town. The man had demons in him. He had gone naked for a long time and no longer lived in a house, but in the graveyard.
translation_verse: Yesu maa dan ɛɛ lɩɩ ɛɛ yɩlɛ ɔbʋn kaa mʋ, nɩɩ Kagerasene‐nyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba bʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ ba gyaŋa mʋ. Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ natɛ kubolonkpan kyɛɛrɛɛ yɛgɛ ɔ mɛɛ kyɩna kɩkpaara sʋ, amɔɔ nkyan tɔ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ.

english_verse: Just then the man in charge of the synagogue came and knelt down in front of Jesus. His name was Jairus, and he begged Jesus to come to his home
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyarosi, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ ba tɩyɛ Yesu ayaa kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ ba mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.

english_verse: One day, Jesus and his disciples got into a boat, and he said, “Let's cross the lake.” They started out,
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Fan yɛgɛ a faa kpee benbe.” Nɩɩ ba loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ faa kyʋn.

english_verse: So they went to Jesus and woke him up, “Master, Master! We are about to drown!” Jesus got up and ordered the wind and waves to stop. They obeyed, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba naa kyiŋi mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ! An biti a sii nkyu tɔ!” Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ, pʋntɛ afuu mʋ yɛ apaa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ waa diin.

english_verse: The man saw Jesus and screamed. He knelt down in front of him and shouted, “Jesus, Son of God Most High, what do you want with me? I beg you not to torture me!”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ wu Yesu mʋ, ɔ pʋntɛ tɩyɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ, nɩɩ o kuusi kenken tɔwɛ yɛ, “Yesu, sʋsʋ Owura Lala mu‐bii! Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti mɛ asɛ? N kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, man nyanla mɛ!”

english_verse: When the men taking care of the pigs saw this, they ran to spread the news in the town and on the farms.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ akuuri mʋ sʋ ba maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, ba sɩlɛ kpee kadɛ tɔ yɛ ndɔɔ ana tɔ naa tɔwɛ asa.

english_verse: He said this because Jesus had already told the evil spirit to go out of him. The man had often been attacked by the demon. And even though he had been bound with chains and leg irons and kept under guard, he smashed whatever bound him. Then the demon would force him out into lonely places.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu wʋla sa nbwɩɩ libi mʋ kɔnɔ fɛɛ ba lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ. Saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba mʋ sʋ mʋ, bɛɛ taa agbeeleebi ŋmina mʋ abaa yɛ mʋ ayaa, yɛgɛ bee kun mʋ. Amaa ɛɛ tɩŋɛ agbeeleebi mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ŋmina mʋ mʋ, nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ bɛɛ sʋla mʋ kpee ofuli sʋ.

english_verse: Jesus asked the man, “What is your name?” He answered, “My name is Lots.” He said this because there were “lots” of demons in him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Nɛnɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ fʋ?” Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ bɛɛ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ, “Kʋbʋ!” Lɩɩ fɛɛ nbwɩɩ libi kʋkyɔ ba tɛ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: A large herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside. So the demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs, and Jesus let them go.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ akuuri damantɛ akʋ ba bʋ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ bee gyii. Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Yesu fɛɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ba naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ Yesu sa bamʋ ɔkpa.

english_verse: Then the demons left the man and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
translation_verse: Nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba maa lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba naa loo akuuri mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ kpɩlɩgɛ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ naa loo ɔbʋn lala mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba muuri.

english_verse: Everyone from around Gerasa begged Jesus to leave, because they were so frightened. When Jesus got into the boat to start back,
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ Gerasene abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kɩbaafʋn maŋa sʋ kpini, ba kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ. Nɩɩ o loo ɔkʋlɩ tɔ bwii.

english_verse: the man who had been healed begged to go with him. But Jesus sent him off and said,
translation_verse: Pɔyɩ kɛ ba bwii mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba kʋsʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ o buu mʋ. Amaa Yesu kina tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: “Go back home and tell everyone how much God has done for you.” The man then went all over town, telling everything that Jesus had done for him. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: “Bwii kpee kewu naa tɔwɛ kʋtɔ lala mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa sa fʋ.” Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ naa tɔwɛ kʋtɔ lala mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa sa mʋ. Yesu kyiŋi Gyarosi Mu‐bii Nɩɩ Ɔ Kyɛ Ɔkyɩɩ Mʋ Nɩɩ Nkalan Ɩ Da Mʋ Mʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent everything she had on doctors, but none of them could make her well.
translation_verse: Ɔkyɩɩ kʋ bʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ nkalan ɩ da mʋ nsu kudu anyɔ, amaa ɔkʋ man taalɛ kyɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Everyone had been waiting for Jesus, and when he came back, a crowd was there to welcome him.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu bwii faa kpee ɔbʋn benbe mʋ, sakyɔ mʋ ba waa mʋ nsa‐nsa kɔɔlɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba wʋla ba tɛ ba gyoo mʋ.

english_verse: because his twelve-year-old child was dying. She was his only daughter. While Jesus was on his way, people were crowding all around him.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mu kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ o gyii nsu kudu anyɔ ii biti o wu. Yesu maa yii sʋ ee kpee mʋ, sakyɔ mʋ mili mʋ sʋ ba buu mʋ.

english_verse: As soon as she came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes, her bleeding stopped.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ loo sakyɔ mʋ tɔ ba Yesu kamaa ba mata mʋ kaalɛ kɔnɔ‐bi, nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ mʋ nkalan mʋ ɩ tɩn.

english_verse: “Who touched me?” Jesus asked. While everyone was denying it, Peter said, “Master, people are crowding all around and pushing you from every side.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ ɩ mata mɛ?” Bamʋ kpini ba maa kina yɛ ɩ man gyɛ bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, sakyɔ bee mili fʋ sʋ, bɛɛ mata fʋ.”

english_verse: The woman knew that she could not hide, so she came trembling and knelt down in front of Jesus. She told everyone why she had touched him and that she had been healed at once.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ maa wu fɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ taa baala mʋ, ɔ kyɩkyaa ba tɩyɛ Yesu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɩlɛ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ mata Yesu, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kɩkyɛ opula maŋa tɔ.

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Mi‐bii kyɩɩsa, fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya, kpewu kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ.”

english_verse: While Jesus was speaking, someone came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu san ɛɛ tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ kabɔɔ kɩ lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa Gyarosi kɩkpaara sʋ, ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Man bɩla tɔɔrɔɔ ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ, fu kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ wu.”

english_verse: Jesus went into the house, but he did not let anyone else go with him, except Peter, John, James, and the girl's father and mother.
translation_verse: Yesu fʋʋ Gyarosi kɩkpaara sʋ mʋ, ɔ man yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ buu mʋ loo obu to, amɔɔ Pita maa Gyɔn yɛ Gyemisi yɛ kebii mʋ, mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi nkʋn.

english_verse: Everyone was crying and weeping for the girl. But Jesus said, “The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Saŋa maa kpini yɛgɛ asa bɛɛ sawʋ bɛɛ waa ɩlawʋ, bee su kebii mʋ keli. Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan yɛgɛ kusu, ɔ man wu, ɔ dɛ nɩn.”

english_verse: The people laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba ŋmasɛ Yesu, lɩɩ fɛɛ be nyi kebii mʋ maa wu.

english_verse: Jesus took hold of the girl's hand and said, “Child, get up!”
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu kɩtaa kebii mʋ kɩbaa tɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mi‐bii, kʋsʋ!”

english_verse: She came back to life and got right up. Jesus told them to give her something to eat.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ, mʋ kala bwii ba mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ opula maŋa tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ba sa kebii mʋ agyitɔ kɛ o gyii.

english_verse: Her parents were surprised, but Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ waa kebii mʋ akʋʋgɛpʋ kɩyan, amaa nɩɩ ɔ da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ ba man waa kɛ ba tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa falɛ sa ɔkʋ.

english_verse: The apostles came back and told Jesus everything they had done. He then took them with him to the village of Bethsaida, where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asunpu mʋ ba maa bwii ba mʋ, ba ba tɔwɛ Yesu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba naa waa. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ taa bamʋ nkʋn ba lɩɩ kpee kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Betisayida mʋ.

english_verse: They did this, and all the people sat down.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba waa kanɩn, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ asa mʋ kpini ba kyɩna‐kyɩna kasɛ.

english_verse: Herod the ruler heard about all that was happening, and he was worried. Some people were saying John the Baptist had come back to life.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ Owura Herodi maa nu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kpini mʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ Gyɔn okyugyeepu mʋ i kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.

english_verse: He told them, “Don't take anything with you! Don't take a walking stick or a traveling bag or food or money or even a change of clothes.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan maa kpee falɛ, fan man taa sɛhn tii sʋ. Fan man taa kikpebi abɛɛ adiikun abɛɛ agyitɔ abɛɛ afulee abɛɛ kaalɛ nyɔsapʋ kʋ tii sʋ.

english_verse: Late in the afternoon the twelve apostles came to Jesus and said, “Send the crowd to the villages and farms around here. They need to find a place to stay and something to eat. There is nothing in this place. It's like a desert!”
translation_verse: Kyɔwɛ maa biti ɔ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Yɛgɛ asa mʋ ba kpee ndɛ‐bi‐ndɛ‐bi yɛ ndɔɔ ana mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tiri nfɩɩ mʋ, kɛ ba naa nyɛ ilookpa yɛ agyitɔ gyii. Lɩɩ fɛɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ an bʋ falɛ gyɛ kiyii nsana.”

english_verse: Jesus strictly warned his disciples not to tell anyone about this. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ ba man kan waa tɔwɛ ɔkʋ.

english_verse: “Pay close attention to what I am telling you! The Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies.”
translation_verse: “Fan kan fanɛ asʋ kɛ fan nu yɛ ɔdan. Bee biti ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa asa abaa tɔ.”

english_verse: But a lot of people found out about this and followed him. Jesus welcomed them. He spoke about God's kingdom and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Sakyɔ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ba buu mʋ kpee tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ tɔwɛ kuwura‐gyii asɩn sa bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti kɩkyɛ mʋ.

english_verse: “Don't stop him!” Jesus said. “Anyone who isn't against you is for you.” A Samaritan Village Refuses To Receive Jesus
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan man gya mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ tɔwɛ ee yii fanɛ mʋ, o tii fanɛ sʋ.” Samariya Abi Ba Man Kɔɔlɛ Yesu Gyii

english_verse: About eight days later Jesus took Peter, John, and James with him and went up on a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Yesu maa tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ lʋwɛ nkɛ nsunɔ kamaa mʋ, ɔ taa Pita maa Gyɔn yɛ Gyemisi nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba kpee kɩbɩɩ kʋkʋ sʋ ba naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will save it.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa mʋ, ii biti ɩ lɩɩ mʋ abaa tɔ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ nkpa sa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti o bwii nyɛ mʋ nkpa.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself or waste your life?
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kan nyɛ dulinyaa tɔ atɔ kpini nɩɩ ɔ pan mʋ nkpa mʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ?

english_verse: If you are ashamed of me and my message, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in his glory and in the glory of his Father and the holy angels.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ mʋ ipeeli lɩɩ mɛ yɛ mɛ asɩn mʋ sʋ mʋ, kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ sʋ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ ipeeli sa kanɩn ɔsa maŋa, lɩɩ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ maa Mɛ‐Sɛ yɛ mʋ nbɔɔ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ an biti a ba bʋnyaa tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: They appeared in heavenly glory and talked about all that Jesus' death in Jerusalem would mean.
translation_verse: Ba taa bʋnyaa sʋ ba Yesu asɛ, nɩɩ maa bamʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɩla fɛɛ ee biti o kpee Gyerusalem naa wu mʋ.

english_verse: ) While everyone was still amazed at what Jesus was doing, he said to his disciples,
translation_verse: Pita maa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ ba dɛ nsɩɩrɛ, amaa ba maa kyiŋi mʋ, ba wu Yesu bʋnyaa mʋ yɛ asa anyɔ mʋ ba maa yɩlɛ mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, a shadow from a cloud passed over, and they were frightened as the cloud covered them.
translation_verse: Ɔ dan san ɛɛ tɔwɛ nɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ a ba bun bamʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ kufuu kanan mʋ nɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ a bun bamʋ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: From the cloud a voice spoke, “This is my chosen Son. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ tɔwɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ yɛ, “Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐bii mʋ nɩɩ n wʋla lɛɛ. Fen nu mʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: After the voice had spoken, Peter, John, and James saw only Jesus. For some time they kept quiet and did not say anything about what they had seen. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɔbʋlɛ mʋ maa tɔwɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba wu fɛɛ ɩ san Yesu nkʋn nɩn. Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ asɩn mʋ a sii yɛ bamʋ nkʋn, ba man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba wu saŋa maŋa. Yesu kyɛ kebii kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɔ kigbingbin (Matiwu

english_verse: A demon often attacks him and makes him scream. It shakes him until he foams at the mouth, and it won't leave him until it has completely worn the boy out.
translation_verse: Kayaayu libi kʋ ɩɩ taalɛ ɛɛ ba mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ opula tɔ ee kuusi yɛgɛ ɩɩ sʋ mʋ ɩɩ tʋʋ kasɛ, ɩɩ bɩɩtɛ mʋ fɛɛ kabwɩɩ mʋ, yɛgɛ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ ɩ lɩɩ afʋfʋʋ ɩ taa mʋ i kpii nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ pɔyɩ kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You people are stubborn and don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you?” Then Jesus said to the man, “Bring your son to me.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “O! Fanɛ ndaga asa, fan man kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, nɩɩ fanɛ ɩkpa ɩ man du kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Nkɛ nfɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n kyɩna fanɛ asɛ nyɛ kenyiita sa fanɛ?” Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, “Taa fu‐bii mʋ ba nfɩɩ.”

english_verse: While the boy was being brought, the demon attacked him and made him shake all over. Jesus ordered the demon to stop. Then he healed the boy and gave him back to his father.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kebii mʋ maa ba Yesu asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ kayaayu libi mʋ ba taa mʋ tʋʋ kasɛ nɩɩ i kpii mʋ fɛɛ kigbingbin mʋ. Amaa Yesu pʋntɛ kayaayu libi mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ kebii mʋ bwiiyaa mʋ sa mʋ‐sɛ.

english_verse: But the disciples did not know what he meant. The meaning was hidden from them. They could not understand it, and they were afraid to ask. Who Is the Greatest? (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba man nu ɩmʋ kasɛ. Ɩ gyɛ asɩn baalasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man lɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ, amaa nɩɩ ba sʋ kufuu fɛɛ ba taasɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ kasɛ. Anɩmʋ Ɩ Gyɛ Ɔlala Ɩbwaarɛ Kuwura‐gyii Mʋ Tɔ (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus' disciples were arguing about which one of them was the greatest.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩmɔɔrɛ ɩ tɩyɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nsana fɛɛ bamʋ tɔ mʋ, anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔlala.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he had a child stand there beside him.
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu maa bɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ mʋ, ɔ taa kebii pii ba yɩlɛ mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: Then he said to his disciples, “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me. Whichever one of you is the most humble is the greatest.” For or against Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ kebii nɩmʋ dubi mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, fʋ kɔɔlɛ mɛ nɩmʋ. Nɩɩ fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ mɛ mʋ, fʋ kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋnɩ o sun mɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋnɩ ɔ gyɛ pii fanɛ kpini tɔ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔlala.” Ɔkʋmaa Mʋ Nɩɩ Ɔ Mɛɛ Tɔwɛ Yii Fanɛ Mʋ, Tii Fanɛ Sʋ (Maki

english_verse: John said, “Master, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But we told him to stop, because he isn't one of us.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ sa Yesu yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ! An wu ɔnyɩn kʋ maa sʋ fʋ kɩtɩɩ ɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi. Ɩmʋ sʋ an gya mʋ lɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ man tii anɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: Not long before it was time for Jesus to be taken up to heaven, he made up his mind to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yesu kibwii kpee sʋsʋ saŋa maa tiri tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ, nɩɩ o yii sʋ kpee Gyerusalem.

english_verse: He sent some messengers on ahead to a Samaritan village to get things ready for him.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ nbɔɔ sun yɛ ba gyankpaa mʋ kpee Samariya kadɛ‐bi tɔ naa kʋʋla gyoo mʋ.

english_verse: But he was on his way to Jerusalem, so the people there refused to welcome him.
translation_verse: Amaa tʋtɔ asa mʋ ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba dan be nyi maa kpee Gyerusalem nɩn.

english_verse: When the disciples James and John saw what was happening, they asked, “Lord, do you want us to call down fire from heaven to destroy these people?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ, Gyemisi maa Gyɔn ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fii biti fɛɛ a tɩɩ fʋlɔn lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tuwa bamʋ abɛɛ?”

english_verse: But Jesus turned and corrected them for what they had said.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi kɩɩ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Fanɛ katɔwɛ maŋa man bʋrɔn.”

english_verse: Then they all went on to another village. Three People Who Wanted To Be Followers (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ taa kpee kadɛ‐bi pʋpwɛ kʋ sʋ. Awʋrʋfɔ Mʋ Nɩɩ Ɩ Bʋ Yesu Kubuu Tɔ (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus said, “Foxes have dens, and birds have nests, but the Son of Man doesn't have a place to call his own.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ yɛ, “Agboogboo bɛɛ dɛ ɩbɔ tɔ, nɩɩ nbwiibi ba sʋ nsʋswaa, amaa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa bɩrɛ n man sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n dɛ fʋʋtɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told someone else to come with him. But the man said, “Lord, let me wait until I bury my father.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ mɔɔ yɛ, “Buu mɛ.” Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ tɔwɛ Yesu nɩn yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, yɛgɛ n bwii kpee naa pula nsɛ pɔyɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Let the dead take care of the dead, while you go and tell about God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ alewupu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ba kpee naa pula bamʋ alewupu. Kɛ fɛɛ fʋ, fu kpee naa tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn.”

english_verse: Then someone said to Jesus, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first let me go back and take care of things at home.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ mɔɔ bɩla yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n biti n buu fʋ, amaa yɛgɛ kɛ n bwii kpee naa kala mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ asa kɛ n ba.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Anyone who starts plowing and keeps looking back isn't worth a thing to God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii biliŋi fɩɩ kɩɩ kamaa mʋ, nɩɩ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ man dɛ katɩn kʋlʋn mʋ, fʋ man dagaa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii.”

english_verse: He said to them: A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers. Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan yɛ, “Ndɔɔ tɔ atɔ sɔyɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa a tɩŋɛ mʋ ɩ kyɔ gaa, amaa atɩŋɛpʋ mʋ ba man kyɔ. Fan kʋlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kusun mʋ wuya, kɛ ɔ bɩya atɩŋɛpʋ ba tii su kɛ ba ba waa mu kusun mʋ.

english_verse: Now go, but remember, I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves.
translation_verse: Fan kpee, n sun fanɛ nɩn fɛɛ nbʋlʋpʋ‐bi ba maa kpee akuntun tɔ.

english_verse: Don't take along a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals. And don't waste time greeting people on the road.
translation_verse: Fan man waa fan taa afulee abɛɛ adiikun abɛɛ asɩbɩta, kɛ fan man fwaala ɔkʋ ɔkpa tɔ.

english_verse: As soon as you enter a home, say, “God bless this home with peace.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan kan loo kɩkpaara kʋmaa sʋ mʋ, fan daa kɩtɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Keyuula kɩ ba kɩkpaara nɩmʋ sʋ.’

english_verse: If the people living there are peace-loving, your prayer for peace will bless them. But if they are not peace-loving, your prayer will return to you.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ keyuula nyɩmɩsa kan ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, fanɛ keyuula ii biti ɔ ba mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, ii biti i bwii ba fanɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Stay with the same family, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers are worth what they earn. Don't move around from house to house.
translation_verse: Fan kyɩna kɩkpaara maŋa sʋ, kɛ fen gyii kɛ fen nuu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sa fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ osunpu kʋmaa kʋkɔka dagaa mʋ. Fan man ba fen loo fen loo akpaara‐akpaara sʋ.

english_verse: If the people of a town welcome you, eat whatever they offer.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ kadɛ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fan loo kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, fan gyii agyitɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sa fanɛ.

english_verse: Heal their sick and say, “God's kingdom will soon be here!”
translation_verse: Fan kyɛ alɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ, kɛ fan tɔwɛ ɔkʋmaa yɛ, ‘Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii ki tiri fanɛ tɔ.’

english_verse: But if the people of a town refuse to welcome you, go out into the street and say,
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ fan kan loo kadɛ kʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ fanɛ mʋ, fan lɩɩ kpee kadɛ mʋ agbenbi sʋ kɛ fan tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: I tell you that on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than the people of that town! The Unbelieving Towns (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, katalɛ lala kakɛ mʋ, kadɛ maa kii biti kɩ nyɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ kɩ kyɔ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba Sodom kadɛ tɔ mʋ.” Ndɛ mʋ Nɩɩ Ba Man Kɔɔlɛ Yesu Gyii (Matiwu

english_verse: But don't be happy because evil spirits obey you. Be happy that your names are written in heaven! Jesus Thanks His Father (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa fan man yɛgɛ ɩ waa fanɛ ɔkɔn fɛɛ n‐yaayu libi ba bɩya bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa fanɛ. Amaa fan yɛgɛ ɩ waa fanɛ ɔkɔn fɛɛ ba maa kyʋrɔɔ fanɛ atɩɩ yɩla Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ.” Yesu Ansi A Gyii Nɩɩ Ɔ kʋlɛ Kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ Matiwu

english_verse: Many prophets and kings were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear.” The Good Samaritan
translation_verse: N bɩla n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ gaalaagaa yɛ awura, ba daa bee biti ba wu atɔ mu nɩɩ fen wu falɛ amaa ba man wu. Nɩɩ ba daa bee biti ba nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fen nu falɛ, amaa ba man nu ɩmʋ.” Kɩŋasan Lɩɩ Kasamariya‐nyɩn Danbɩrasa Sʋ

english_verse: On the day of judgment the people of Tyre and Sidon will get off easier than you will.
translation_verse: Amaa katalɛ lala kakɛ mʋ, Taayi maa Sidon abi kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bɔɔ fanɛ lɛɛ.

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? Well, you will go down to hell!
translation_verse: Fanɛ Kapanum abi, bee biti ba kukuri fanɛ kpee sʋsʋ nɩn abɛɛ? Ayee, bee biti ba bɩya fanɛ kasɛ kamandɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told them: I saw Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “N wu Sɩtaanɛ maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tɩyɛ kasɛ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you.
translation_verse: N sa fanɛ ɔlʋn fɛɛ fen biti fan kyigya anansɛ yɛ ɩkyankpatɛ sʋ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔlʋn fanɛ dʋn Sɩtaanɛ sʋ, nɩɩ sɛhn mɛɛ yɛ ɔ waa fanɛ.

english_verse: An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ onyipu kʋ kʋsʋ ba Yesu asɛ o be nu mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa kɛ n nyɛ nkpa kakpaa?”

english_verse: The man replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.’ They also say, ‘Love your neighbors as much as you love yourself.’ ”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Taa fʋ kɔkɔlɔ yɛ fʋ kala yɛ fʋ ɔlʋn yɛ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ kpini biti fʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ fu biti fʋ tɔɔmaa fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.”

english_verse: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa Yesu kɔkɔlɔ ki fuuli mʋ lɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ‐Sɛ, sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n fwaala fʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ taa atɔ nɩmʋ baala anyaasɩnpʋ yɛ ɩwʋlɛ anyipu sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ kaapʋ nbii wuribi. Nsɛ, fʋ kɩpɩrɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ waa ɩnɩmʋ.

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: “Mɛ‐Sɛ taa atɔ nɩmʋ kpini waa mɛ kɩbaa tɔ. Ɔkʋ mee nyi ɔmʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Obii mʋ n gyɛ, amɔɔ Ɔsɛ mʋ nkʋn, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee nyi Ɔsɛ mʋ, amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ n biti n lɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ mʋ nkʋn i nyi Ɔsɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu biliŋi tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ nkʋn yɛ, “Kumu ɔkɔn ɩ gyɛ sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan wu falɛ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Nɛnɛ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ? Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fʋ kalɛ ɩmʋ nu kasɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ. Waa ɩmʋ kɛ fʋ nyɛ nkpa.”

english_verse: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”
translation_verse: Amaa ɔnyɩn mʋ ii biti ɔ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi sʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ tɔɔmaa?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kɔnɔ kɩlɛɛ tɔ mʋ, ɔ bɔɔ mʋ kɩŋasan yɛ, “Kakaakʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ lɩɩ Gyerusalem ee kpee Gyeriko. Nɩɩ ɔ naa tɩyɛ kumu ɔlʋn tɔ ayu abaa tɔ, nɩɩ ba dayɩ mʋ dɩlɩgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ o wu nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ mʋ.

english_verse: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
translation_verse: Ɩ ba fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu kʋ naa ɔkpa maŋa sʋ ɛɛ ba. Maa wu ɔnyɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩlɩgɛ taa ɔkpa mʋ kɩbaafʋn kyʋn yɛgɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
translation_verse: Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa nɩɩ Lewi‐nyɩn kʋ gbaa bɔla ba wu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩlɩgɛ mʋ kyʋn.

english_verse: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
translation_verse: Amaa Kasamariya‐nyɩn kʋ gbaa bɔla ɔkpa maŋa ba lɩɩ mʋ sʋ. Maa ba wu ɔnyɩn mʋ, ɩ kɩtaa mʋ kʋwɛɛ.

english_verse: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kasamariya‐nyɩn mʋ natɛ kpee mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa wayɩn nta kpayɩ mʋ ɩlɔ ana mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ waa kɩfɛ mʋ ɩlɔ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ o ŋmina ɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ gyaga mʋ kuruma sʋ kpee kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ afɔ bee loo kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩkaakɛ, Kasamariya‐nyɩn mʋ maa biti ɔ kʋsʋ kpee mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ afulee sa afɔ kɩkpaara ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Kɩɩ ɔlɔpʋ nɩmʋ sʋ sa mɛ, nɩɩ fu kan nyita afulee akʋ tii ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ n kan bwii ba mʋ n biti n ba ka fʋ ɩmʋ kpini.’ ”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ yɛ, “To, fʋ asɛ mʋ, asa asá nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔmɔɔ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ tɔɔmaa ɩ gyɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ amu tɔ ɔlʋn ayu ba dayɩ mʋ mʋ?”

english_verse: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ onyipu mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔmʋ nɩɩ o wu ɔnyɩn mʋ kʋwɛɛ mʋ.” Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ gbaa fʋ naa waa kanɩn.” Yesu Kpe Maata Maa Mɛɛrɩ Asɛ

english_verse: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ bee kpee mʋ, ba naa fʋʋ kadɛ‐bi kʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Maata waa mʋ nsa‐nsa taa mʋ kpee mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.
