english_verse: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
translation_verse: Maata sʋ osupu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Mɛɛrɩ. Nɩɩ Mɛɛrɩ naa kyɩna Yesu asɛ ee nu mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
translation_verse: Amaa kanɩn asɩn mʋ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ Maata lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ nkʋn ɩɩ dɩŋa afɔ mʋ agyitɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ ba Yesu asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ, ɩ man tiri fʋ fɛɛ mi‐supu maa yɛgɛ mɛ nkʋn n waa kusun mʋ kpini? Tɔwɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ ba kyɛ mɛ tɔ.”

english_verse: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Maata, Maata! Asɩn gaalaagaa ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fʋ, nɩɩ i tiri fʋ.”

english_verse: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
translation_verse: Amaa kʋtɔ kʋlʋn i tiri. Mɛɛrɩ lɛɛ kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba taalɛ kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ lɩɩ mʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, Fan kyaga sʋ fɛn kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, ee biti ɔ sa fanɛ, fan kyaga sʋ fen biti, fen biti fan nyɛ, fan kyaga sʋ fɛn da ɔpʋnʋ, ee biti o tigi sa fanɛ.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
translation_verse: Fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn gyɛ alibi awaapʋ gbaa mʋ, fen nyi atɔ danbɩrasa kɩtaa sa fanɛ‐bii ana. Ɩ waa nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ mʋ mʋ?” Yesu maa Beelisebu (Matiwu

english_verse: And suppose your friend answers, “Don't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.”
translation_verse: “A taa fɛɛ, mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ mʋ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ obu to yɛ, ‘Man tɔɔrɔɔ mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n tɩyɛ tii biri sʋ, nɩɩ maa mi‐bii ana an tɩyɛ dɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ n mɛɛ yɛ n bɩla taalɛ kʋsʋ sa fʋ sɛhn.’

english_verse: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔsɛ mɔɔ nɩɩ mu‐bii ɩɩ kʋlɛ mʋ ɔkɩn, nɩɩ ɛɛ taa kɩnansɛ sa mʋ?

english_verse: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan kan fen biti fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, fan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, yɛgɛ asa ba bʋnyaa fʋ kɩtɩɩ, yɛgɛ fu kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩ ba kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ.

english_verse: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”
translation_verse: Taa anɛ alibi kɛɛ anɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ an sʋ an kɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa anɛ alibi mʋ. Kɛ fʋ man yɛgɛ a loo kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ.’ ”

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ, bɛɛ sa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ, ɛɛ nyɛ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ da ɔpʋnʋ sʋ mʋ, bee tigi sa mʋ.

english_verse: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyona kyɩna ɔ gyɛ sɩnkaala sa Ninewe abi mʋ, kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa gbaa n biti n ba waa Ɩbwaarɛ sɩnkaala sa ndaga asa kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
translation_verse: Yesu maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, sakyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkyɩɩ kʋ diyaa mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa onyi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ fʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sa fʋ mʋ kɩnyapʋ‐kyu mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ Yesu maa gya kabwɩɩ libi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛgɛ nyɩmɩsa ɔ tɔwɛ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa ɔ gyɛ naamuu mʋ, piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ, lan tɔwɛ yɛ, nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ mʋ ɔlʋn maŋa lɩɩ mʋ, ɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa kɛ ba kɩɩ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu maa wʋla bɩɩ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ barɩgɛ tɔ kɩɩ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ, kɩ mɛɛ yɛ kɩ taalɛ yɩlɛ. Nɩɩ kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ barɩgɛ tɔ kɩɩ kɔ yɛ abaa mʋ gbaa, kɩ mɛɛ yɛ kɩ taalɛ yɩlɛ.

english_verse: If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ gbaa barɩgɛ tɔ kɩɩ kɔ yɛ kɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ kɩmʋ kuwura‐gyii ɩ taalɛ yɩlɛ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, Beelisebu ɔlʋn nɩɩ n sʋ n gya nbwɩɩ libi sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ kanɩn.

english_verse: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Beelisebu ɔlʋn nɩɩ n sʋ n gya nbwɩɩ libi mʋ, anɩmʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ fanɛ abuupu bɩrɛ ba sʋ bɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi bɛɛ lɩɩ asa tɔ? Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ abuupu ba talɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fanɛ lɛɛ man dɛ ɔkpa.

english_verse: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa bʋlʋnsa wʋla kʋʋla mʋ adʋntɔ ee kun mʋ kɩkpaara bɩrɛ, mʋ kapatɩyɛ kɩ dɛ sa mʋ bwahn.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ Farasii abi fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fen biti ɩkyɩnakpa kparɛ‐kparɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ fen biti bɛɛrɛɛ afwaala agya tɔ ipula.

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ onyipu kʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ falɛ mʋ, fɩɩ saalɛ anɛ gbaa anɛ tii sʋ.”

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi kan lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa tɔ mʋ, ee kpee ɛɛ naa ɛɛ kyaabɔɔ ifuli sʋ ee biti ɔfʋʋtɛkpa. Nɩɩ ɔ man kan nyɛ mʋ, ɛɛ tɔwɛ sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, ‘N biti n bwii kpee mɛ kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ.’

english_verse: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
translation_verse: Maa ba mʋ o be wu fɛɛ ba fɔgɛ kɩkpaara mʋ, lɔŋɔ kɩmʋ atɔ kʋmaa yɩla kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
translation_verse: Kɛ ɔ bɩla naa taa nbwɩɩ libi asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba libi ba kyɔ mʋ tii mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, kɛ ba kpee naa kyɩna tʋtɔ. Kɛ ɩ yɛgɛ kanɩn nyɩmɩsa maŋa kakyɩna tɔ ki nyita ɩ kyɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa o du mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu abwaarɛsɩn nɩɩ ba buu ɩmʋ mʋ.” Bee Biti Yesu Ɔ Waa asɩn Kyinkyinsa (Matiwu

english_verse: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
translation_verse: Amaa ɩ waa Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ kanankʋ, fɛɛ Yesu maa man fwɩɩ mʋ abaa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anaana tɔ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ee gyii.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ Farasii abi fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan barɩgɛ fanɛ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ ntun kudu nɩɩ fan taa ketun kʋlʋn fɛn sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Amaa fan kina fen mee gyii asɩn ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, nɩɩ fen mee biti Ɩbwaarɛ. Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fan daa kɩwaa ɩnɩmʋ, kɛ fan taa ketun kʋlʋn mʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ tii sʋ.

english_verse: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ ba maa nyɛ akyɔ bee tii sʋ mʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ndaga asa libi kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ bɛɛ taasɛ bee biti asɩn kyinkyinsa, amaa mɛɛ yɛ n waa sɛhn sa bamʋ, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ Ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyona lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ katalɛ akɛ mʋ, Owura kyɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna lɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ mʋ, ii biti ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɛ o bun ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa kʋpwɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɩɩ kata‐kata dulinyaa ɔkaa o be nu Solomon mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ owura mʋ kanyaasɩn. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ n kyɔ Owura Solomon mʋ.

english_verse: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ katalɛ akɛ mʋ Ninewe abi mʋ nɩɩ ba daa kyɩna dɩdaa mʋ, bee biti ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ talɛ fanɛ ndaga asa nɩmʋ tɔ, kɛ ba bun fanɛ kʋpwɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyona tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn sa bamʋ mʋ, ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ n kyɔ Gyona mʋ. Fɩtɩla Kusun Sunsa (Matiwu

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
translation_verse: “Ɔkʋ mee tuwa fɩtɩla ɔ sʋ ɛɛ yɩla katɩn mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ baala, abɛɛ ɔ taa waa kolobi tɔ. Amaa ɛɛ taa gyaga kɩmʋ ɔgyagakpa nɩn, kɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba ba tʋtɔ mʋ, ɩ baa ɩ kɩɩ bamʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
translation_verse: Fu ansi ɩ gyɛ fɩtɩla sa fʋ n‐yɩɩ. Nɩɩ fu ansi ɩ kan ɩ bʋrɔn mʋ, fʋ n‐yɩɩ kpini gbaa gyɛ fɩtɩla nɩn. Amaa nɩɩ fu ansi ɩ man kan ɩ bʋrɔn mʋ, fʋ n‐yɩɩ kpini gbaa bʋ kitentenbiri tɔ.

english_verse: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan pɔyɩ nkyan sa Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ, nɩɩ fanɛ naana ana ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, Kafarasii‐nyɩn kʋ tɩɩ Yesu yɛ, ɔ ba kɛ maa mʋ ba bʋga gyii. Ɩmʋ sʋ o kpee nɩɩ maa mʋ ba naa bʋga bee gyii.

english_verse: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ Farasii abi falɛ nɩɩ fen du, fɛn fwɩɩ nwɛ‐bi yɛ ɩlapɛ nmaa, amaa kumu tɔ ɔlʋn yɛ kensi payɩ libi ɩ bɔla fanɛ tɔ.

english_verse: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
translation_verse: Amaa fan taa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ fanɛ nwɛ‐bi yɛ ɩlapɛ mʋ tɔ sa atiripu, kɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ sa fanɛ.

english_verse: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fen du nɩn fɛɛ nkyan mʋ nɩɩ ba man daasɛ ɩmʋ fɛɛ nkyan, nɩɩ asa ba naa ɩmʋ sʋ yɛgɛ ba man nyi fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ nkyan.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ anyipu, fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan taa ɩsʋla budusa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋsɩn kasʋla mʋ sʋla asa. Nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan mɛɛ taa kɩbaa‐bi kʋlʋn gbaa kyɛ bamʋ tɔ.

english_verse: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ kesintin fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ naana ana ba waa mʋ, fan kɔɔlɛ kyula. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ mɔɔ fan pɔyɩ bamʋ nkyan.

english_verse: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ lɩɩ mʋ kanyaasɩn tɔ yɛ, ‘N biti n sun mɛ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ mi asunpu ba kpee bamʋ asɛ, kɛ ba mɔɔ mɛ asa mʋ akʋ kɛ ba nyanla asansa.’

english_verse: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba kyɩna kasɛ sʋ lɩɩ koo dulinyaa npiili mʋ, ee biti ɔ taa kanɩn kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ bɩɩtɛ ndaga asa libi kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ lɩɩ Abeli sʋ naa fʋʋ Sakariya mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ kaswɩɩ yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ nsana mʋ, n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa amɔɔpʋ mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ ɔ bɩɩtɛ fanɛ ndaga kakyɩna tɔ asa libi banɩmʋ.

english_verse: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ anyipu, fanɛ laakʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan taa ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ asa bee biti ba bɔla sʋ bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ baala. Nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan man naa ɔkpa maa sʋ, nɩɩ fen tii bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba natɛ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ ɔkpa.”

english_verse: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
translation_verse: Yesu maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ ba piili bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ kenken, yɛgɛ bɛɛ taasɛ mʋ asɩn lɩɩ atɔ gaalaagaa sʋ,

english_verse: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, asa mʋ ba maa nyɛ akyɔ bee tii sʋ, halɩɩ bee kyigya abaa ayaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ lɩɩ Farasii abi kebunbun ansi wuya ana sʋ. Bamʋ akaapʋsa i du nɩn fɛɛ bodobodo kɩfɛ tinsa.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
translation_verse: Fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii pii wuya ana, nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ falɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ i bun afitiri tɔ ntʋtʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ ndaga kɛ ɔkɛ ba taa tʋʋ fʋlɔn tɔ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ sa fanɛ atɔ bunsa?

english_verse: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa fan kɩɩ biti Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ, kɛ ɔ sa fanɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini tii sʋ.”

english_verse: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana, fan man sɩlɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ mɔɔ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ba mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ waa kɩmʋ sɛhn.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
translation_verse: Halɩɩ fanɛ kumu tɔ inyini kpini gbaa Ɩbwaarɛ nyi ɩmʋ kɔnɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man waa kufuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan gyɛ akparɩ fan kyɔ alangba ngbɩn‐ngbɩn.

english_verse: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
translation_verse: Fan fɛ fanɛ npatɩyɛ kpini, kɛ fan taa afulee mʋ sa atiripu. Fan nyɛ afulee mʋ nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ lʋwɛ mʋ yɩla sʋsʋ, ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ oyu mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taalɛ nin tiri tɔ, nɩɩ tabɩrɩta mɛɛ taalɛ nyita ɩmʋ mʋ.

english_verse: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ kaapʋ fanɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ saŋa maŋa.” Kɩŋasan Lɩɩ Afulee Wuya Omelensipu Kʋ Sʋ

english_verse: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
translation_verse: “Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Omelensipu nɩmʋ, kagyanbwɛ nɩmʋ fʋ nkpa ii biti ɩ lɩɩ fʋ abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kʋʋla sa fʋ n‐yɩɩ yɩla mʋ ii biti i biliŋi anɩmʋ lɛɛ?’ ”

english_verse: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
translation_verse: “N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ sakyɔ tɔ yɛ ɔ gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ mʋ, mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa gbaa n biti n tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ansi tɔ yɛ ɔ gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ.

english_verse: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mɛ sakyɔ tɔ mʋ, ngbaa mɛ n biti n kina mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ansi tɔ.

english_verse: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ba kan bɩya fanɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa abɩlɩsa, abɛɛ awura abɛɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ alʋnpʋ ansi tɔ mʋ, fan man kan fɛ nfɛɛrɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan lɛɛ fanɛ asɩn mʋ kɔnɔ, abɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan tɔwɛ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn, anɩmʋ i yii mɛ asɩn ogyipu abɛɛ kapatɩyɛ ɔbarɩgɛpʋ?”

english_verse: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ danbɩrasa kɛ fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ lɩɩ kensi payɩ libi sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa nkpa man gyɛ mʋ atɔ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ.”

english_verse: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn mʋ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ, ‘Nɛnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa? N man sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n taa mɛ agyitɔ anɩmʋ kpini waa.’

english_verse: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Falɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa. N biti n nyita mɛ agyitɔ ɩwaakpa mʋ, kɛ n lɔŋɔ waa ɩmʋ alala‐alala. Kɛ n taa mɛ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ yɛ mɛ atɔ kpini waa tʋtɔ.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ, “Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, fan man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ kakyɩna kanɩmʋ tɔ atɔ tirisa fɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan gyii, abɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan nyɛ bun fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ.

english_verse: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
translation_verse: “Fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ iyu ntʋtʋlɛ i du, i mee sun nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ lʋʋ ataa ɩ sa ɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, Owura lala Solomon maa mʋ atɔ danbɩrasa kpini gbaa mʋ, ɔ man taalɛ nyɛ atɔ bunsa danbɩrasa fɛɛ ntʋtʋlɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɛɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ɩ fɛ ɩnɩmʋ ana kpini nfɛɛrɛ. Nɩɩ fanɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nyi fɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ ana kpini i tiri fanɛ.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
translation_verse: Amaa a taa fɛɛ nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kan tɔwɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ, ‘Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti ɔ kyɛɛrɛɛ!’ Nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ dayɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ abite, nɩɩ ee gyii nɩɩ ee nuu bʋʋ bɩrɛ,

english_verse: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
translation_verse: Fen nyi fɛɛ keyuula nɩɩ n bɩya kasɛ sʋ? Ayee, n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ nɩɩ n bɩya.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
translation_verse: Ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ba tu bamʋ yɛgɛ ba tɛ ba gyoo mʋ, bamʋ ansi ii biti i gyii. N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ ee biti ɔ kʋʋla mʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ ɔ taa agyitɔ naa yii bamʋ kɛ ba gyii.

english_verse: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
translation_verse: Ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ba tu bamʋ kagyanbwɛ kiyiridi nsana abɛɛ kɩbwayɩkɛ yɛgɛ ba kʋʋla ba gyoo mʋ mʋ, bamʋ ansi ii biti i gyii.

english_verse: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
translation_verse: Amaa fan baa fen nyi fɛɛ, nɩɩ kɩkpaara wuya daa o nyi saŋa mʋ nɩɩ oyu ii biti o be yuuri nɩn, naafɔɔ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ba be boyi loo mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.

english_verse: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, anɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ bɔɔ kɩŋasan kanɩmʋ fɩɩ sa abɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ?”

english_verse: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ bɩrɛ ɩ gyɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kesintin yɛ kanyaasɩn? Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋnɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ taa mʋ ayaafɔlɛ waa mʋ abaa tɔ, yɛ ɔ sa bamʋ agyitɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Kensi‐gyii ɩ gyɛ sa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ba tu mʋ yɛgɛ ɛɛ waa kanɩn.

english_verse: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ taa mʋ kapatɩyɛ kpini‐kpini waa mʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
translation_verse: kanɩn kɩyaafɔlɛ maŋa mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti o be purigi mʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ ba, yɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nyi sʋ. Mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ mʋ biti ɔ tɩŋɛ mʋ kʋkyʋkyʋʋ, kɛ ɔ bɩɩtɛ mʋ kʋsʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ bɩɩtɛ asʋ tɔ ɔlʋn wuya ana kʋsʋ mʋ.

english_verse: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
translation_verse: “Kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ o nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ii biti, nɩɩ ɔ man kʋʋla mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɔ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ, bee biti ba tɩŋɛ mʋ kʋkyʋkyʋʋ gaa.

english_verse: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa ɔmʋnɩ ɔ man nyi nɩɩ ɔ waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ, bee biti ba tɩŋɛ mʋ pii. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sa mʋ gaa mʋ, bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ gaa lɩɩ mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa gaa waa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ mʋ, bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ kʋkyɔ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.” Lɩɩ Yesu Sʋ Mʋ Asa Bɛɛ Barɩgɛ Tɔ (Matiwu

english_verse: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
translation_verse: “N ba nɩn yɛ n ba yɛgɛ fʋlɔn ɔ dɛɛ kasɛ sʋ, naafɔɔ n daa n biti gbaa fɛɛ fʋlɔn mʋ tɛɛ ɩ dɛɛ.

english_verse: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
translation_verse: Awʋrʋfɔ lala ii biti i munla mɛ, nɩɩ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mɛ gaa fɛɛ ɩ ba kɛ ɩ ba kyʋn.

english_verse: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Nbarɩgɛ tɔ ii biti ɩ ba ɔsɛ yɛ obii‐nyɩnsa nsana, obii‐nyɩnsa yɛ ɔsɛ nsana, onyi yɛ obii‐kyɩɩsa nsana, obii‐kyɩɩsa yɛ onyi nsana, ɔsaa‐kyɩɩsa yɛ ɔsaa nsana, ɔsaa yɛ ɔsaa‐kyɩɩsa nsana.” Atɔ mʋ Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ Waa Mʋ Kasɛ kunu (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
translation_verse: Yesu tɔwɛ sakyɔ mʋ yɛ, “Saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen wu kabwaarɛ wʋlɛ maa gyaga kyɔwɛ kɩtɩyɛ mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Bʋlayɩ ii biti ɔ daa,’ nɩɩ ɛɛ daa.

english_verse: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ afuu a daa a lɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ mʋ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Kududuu kii biti kɩ waa,’ nɩɩ ɩ waa.

english_verse: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
translation_verse: Fanɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana fɛn taalɛ fɛn kɩɩ kasɛ yɛ sʋsʋ fɛn kaapʋ ɩmʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ waa nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ kasɛ?

english_verse: Why don't you understand the right thing to do?
translation_verse: “Mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ talɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn kɛ fan waa?

english_verse: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan pwɩɩ fʋ, nɩɩ faa mʋ fen kpee asɩn ogyikpa mʋ, kpan fʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ faa mʋ fan tɔwɛ lɔŋɔ abaa ɔkpa tɔ pɔyɩ kɛ fan fʋʋ tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ, ee biti ɔ taa fʋ naa sa asɩn ogyipu kɛ o bun fʋ kʋpwɛ, kɛ ɔ taa fʋ naa sa obu otiipu, kɛ mɔɔ mʋ ɔ taa fʋ naa tii obu.

english_verse: You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fʋ fɛɛ fii biti fu sii obu maŋa tɔ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ ka kʋkɔ mʋ kpini‐kpini.”

english_verse: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ asa akʋ ba ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ Galile abi akʋ ba taa atɔbwaaya ba bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga bɛɛ sa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Paleti yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nkalan ɩ bʋga yɛ atɔbwaaya nkalan maa tɔ.

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
translation_verse: Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan nyi fɛɛ kanɩn Galile abi maŋa awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩɩ fɛɛ ba gyɛ alibi awaapʋ ba kyɔ Galile abi kpini?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
translation_verse: Ayee! N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ nɩɩ fan man kan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ alibi tɔ bɩrɛ, fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fen biti fan wu fɛɛ bamʋ.

english_verse: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, “Nɛnɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki du? Mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n taa kɩmʋ kaala?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
translation_verse: Ayee! N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ nɩɩ fan man kan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ alibi tɔ bɩrɛ, fanɛ gbaa fanɛ kpini fen biti fan wu fɛɛ bamʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn kʋ ɩ kyɩna duu figi oyu mʋ kʋdɔɔ tɔ, nɩɩ o kpee ɔ naa biti kɩmʋ abi amaa ɔ man nyɛ.

english_verse: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ yɛgɛ kɩmʋ kɛ kɩ yɩlɛ kesu kʋlʋn tii sʋ. N biti n kuu kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ kɛ n waa kɩmʋ atɔbwaaya ibini.’

english_verse: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩ kan sɔyɩ abi kesu kakʋ mʋ, ɩ bʋrɔn, nɩɩ kɩ kan kɩ man sɔyɩ bɩrɛ kɛ fʋ ŋɛ kɩmʋ tʋʋ.” Yesu Kyɛ Boobi Kyɩɩsa Kakɛ Fʋʋtɛsa Akɛ

english_verse: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
translation_verse: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kʋ Yesu ɩ kaapʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa kʋ.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu mʋ mʋ, ɔ tɩɩ mʋ ba ansi tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkyɩɩ, fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.”

english_verse: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fanɛ kebunbun ansi wuya ana! Fanɛ anɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ saaŋɛ mʋ naatɛ‐nyɩnsa abɛɛ mu kuruma kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ sʋ mʋ ee kpee ɛɛ sa mʋ nkyu?

english_verse: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
translation_verse: Maa tɔwɛ falɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ipeeli ɩ kɩtaa mʋ akisipu kpini. Amaa asa mʋ bɩrɛ ansi i gyii lɩɩ adangana damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ sʋ. Oyu kibi pii kɩŋasan (Matiwu

english_verse: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Farasii abi akʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Lɩɩ nfɩɩ kpee katɩn kʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Owura Herodi ii biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔɔ fʋ.”

english_verse: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kanan kʋmaa sʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n maa kyaga mɛ ɔkpa sʋ n kpee ndaga yɛ ɔkɛ yɛ kakɛ sasapʋ. Ɩ waa nɛnɛ, Gyerusalem ɩ gyɛ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

english_verse: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kamaa mʋ akʋ, bee biti ba ba gyankpaa, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa mʋ mɔɔ akʋ bee biti ba sii kamaa.” Yesu Su Sa Gyerusalem Abi (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan kpee naa tɔwɛ kanɩn gboogboo mʋ yɛ, n biti n gya nbwɩɩ libi kɛ n kyɛ asa ndaga yɛ ɔkɛ kɛ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ n lʋwɛ mɛ kusun.”

english_verse: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
translation_verse: Ki du nɩn fɛɛ gowa kibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔdɔɔpʋ taa du mʋ kʋdɔɔ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ dan biliŋi oyu, nɩɩ nbwiibi bɛɛ ba bɛɛ kyɩna kɩmʋ n‐yaabi sʋ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩlaa bwii taasɛ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n taa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kaala?

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu maa kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, ɔ bɔla ndɛ yɛ ndɛ‐bii mʋ sʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ abwaarɛsɩn.

english_verse: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
translation_verse: “Fan kpan fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kenken kɛ fan bɔla kebunɔ‐bii mʋ tɔ loo to. N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba taalɛ.

english_verse: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩkpaara wuya kan kʋsʋ tii kebunɔ mʋ, fen biti fan ba yɩlɛ kewusunɔ fen daa ɔpʋnʋ yɛgɛ fɛn kʋlɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tigi kebunɔ sa anɛ.’ “Amaa ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘N man nyi fanɛ, n man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan lɩɩ.’

english_verse: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
translation_verse: “Fen biti fan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Anaa fʋ ɩ bʋga gyii, nɩɩ an bʋga nuu, nɩɩ fʋ kaapʋ anɛ ndɛ tɔ.’

english_verse: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
translation_verse: Amaa ee biti ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ‘N man nyi fanɛ abɛɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan lɩɩ. Fanɛ alibi awaapʋ kpini fan lɩɩ mɛ asɛ!’

english_verse: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan wu Abraham maa Isaki yɛ Gyekobu yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ kpini, ba maa bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ fan man nyɛ ɔkpa loo to mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ fen biti fan su kɛ fan wɛ fanɛ anyi tɔ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “O Gyerusalem abi, Gyerusalem abi, fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ, nɩɩ fan tʋʋ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun fanɛ asɛ mʋ abuu. N kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa fɛɛ n kʋʋla fanɛ waa mɛ ɔtɔ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kakyaasɛ‐nyi maa bʋga mu‐bii ana mʋ atɛɛ tɔ mʋ. Amaa fan mɛɛ sa mɛ ɔkpa!”

english_verse: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Kɩɩ, fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kii biti ki sii kɩ dɛ kɩkpan sa fanɛ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ, fanɛ man bɩla yɛ fan wu mɛ kakɛ akʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋnɩ ɔ ba Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ.”

english_verse: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ mu ansi tɔ tʋtɔ, ɛɛ lɔɔ kyankpan.

english_verse: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ anyipu mʋ ba maa Farasii abi mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkpa dɛ fɛɛ a kyɛ ɔkʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ abɛɛ a man kyɛ?”

english_verse: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
translation_verse: Amaa ba man tɔwɛ sɛhn nɩɩ Yesu kɩtaa ɔnyɩn mʋ, kyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ natɛ.

english_verse: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
translation_verse: Amaa ba man taalɛ lɛɛ ɩmʋ kɔnɔ. Bɩya Fʋ N‐yɩɩ Kasɛ

english_verse: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu fɛɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩɩ bamʋ ogyikpa mʋ bɛɛ ŋmaarɛ ɩkyɩnakpa kparɛ‐kparɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ,

english_verse: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɩɩ fʋ mʋ kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ mʋ, man naa kyɩna ɔkyɩnakpa kparɛ, akʋtɔ fɛɛ ɔ tɩɩ ɔsa kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ kyɔ fʋ.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩya mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kʋsaa mʋ sʋsʋ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ mʋ, mɔɔ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ kan tɩɩ asa yɛ ba be gyii kakyʋwapɛ abɛɛ kalaatɩpwɛ agyitɔ mʋ, man kan waa fʋ tɩɩ fʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana abɛɛ fʋ‐daa abɛɛ fʋ kʋʋpʋ ana abɛɛ afulee wuya ana mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ tiri fʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ fʋ kan waa kanɩn mʋ, akʋtɔ fɛɛ bee biti ba tɩɩ fʋ gbaa fʋ ba bamʋ lɛɛ tɔ kɛ ɩ ka fʋ kʋkɔ.

english_verse: They cannot pay you back. But God will bless you and reward you when his people rise from death. The Great Banquet (Matthew
translation_verse: kɛ fʋ nyɛ kusee, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ ka fʋ kʋkɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka fʋ kʋkɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ akyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ bee biti ba kʋsʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ.” Kɩŋasan Lɩɩ Asa Mʋ Nɩɩ Ba Kina Kensigyi Agyitɔ Mʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
translation_verse: “Ɩ man kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n waa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sa kɔnɔ mʋ, amaa ɩkyɩnakpa san ɩ bʋ tɔ.’

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba tɛ bee gyii mʋ ɔkʋ maa nu asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Kusee ɩ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o gyii Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn kʋ ɩ dɩŋa kensi‐gyii agyitɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ asa damantɛ yɛ ba be gyii.

english_verse: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ maa fʋʋ fɛɛ ba be gyii mʋ, nɩɩ o sun mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ yɛ ɔ naa tɔwɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ bamʋ mʋ yɛ, ba waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa lʋwɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba ba.

english_verse: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba piili bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn bɛɛ lɛɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ. Ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ yɛ, ‘N dan sɔɔ kasɛ nɩn, nɩɩ ɩ daaga fɛɛ n naa kɩɩ kɩmʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ n kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn n mɛɛ yɛ n taalɛ ba.’

english_verse: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɔɔ yɛ, ‘N dan sɔɔ ɩnaatɛ‐nyɩnsa kudu nɩn, nɩɩ n dan kpee n naa taa bamʋ dɔɔ kɩɩ, ɩmʋ sʋ n mɛɛ yɛ n taalɛ ba.’

english_verse: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ taa asɩn mʋ ba tɔwɛ mʋ‐nyɩrɩpɛ. Nɩɩ kɩkpaara wuya mʋ nyɛ agbʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Bilen, kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ agbenbi sʋ yɛ ɩkpa‐bi naa bɩya atiripu yɛ aboobi yɛ agyaatanapʋ yɛ amʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ wuya ana.’

english_verse: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ bwii tɔwɛ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ yɛ, ‘Kpe ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ ii kpee ndɛ sʋ, yɛ ndɔɔ ana tɔ, kɛ fʋ yɛgɛ ba ba kɛ mɛ kɩkpaara kɩ bɔla.

english_verse: Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!” Being a Disciple (Matthew
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ, asa mʋ nɩɩ n tɩɩ bamʋ Sososi mʋ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ daa mɛ kensi‐gyii agyitɔ mʋ kɩɩ.’ ” Krisito Obuupu Kasʋla Mʋ Gbaa‐gbaa Oyu laasa (Matiwu

english_verse: Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
translation_verse: Yesu maa kʋbʋ damantɛ kʋkʋ ba maa naa mʋ, nɩɩ o biliŋi tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɛɛ ba mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ o mee biti mɛ ɔ kyɔ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ mu‐nyi, mʋ‐ka yɛ mu‐bii ana, mʋ‐daa ana yɛ mu‐supu ana, apɩɩkyɩɩ yɛ apɩɩnyɩn, halɩɩ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa dee, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ waa mɛ obuupu.

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ sʋla mu oyu laasa, kɛ o buu mɛ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ waa mɛ obuupu.

english_verse: Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it?
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ pɔyɩ kɩkpaara lala mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa? Pɔyɩ kɛ o piili mʋ ɔ mɛɛ gyankpaa kɩɩ mu afulee mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa pɔyɩ, kɛ o wu fɛɛ mu afulee mʋ ii biti ɩ fʋʋ kɩpɔyɩ mʋ abɛɛ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ fʋʋ?

english_verse: Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ akʋtɔ ee piili ɔpɔrɛ gyankpaasa, nɩɩ ɔ man taalɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o wu kɩmʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ ŋmasɛ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: They will say, “You started building, but could not finish the job.”
translation_verse: ‘Odunwura nɩmʋ piili kɩkpaara kɩpɔyɩ amaa ɔ man taalɛ pɔyɩ lʋwɛ.’

english_verse: If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ kɔ yɛ mʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ lɛɛ asa sun ɔbanban mʋ asɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ san ɔ bʋ kata‐kata, kɛ ɔ taasɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba waa kɛ keyuula kɩ ba bamʋ nsana.

english_verse: So then, you cannot be my disciple unless you give away everything you own. Salt and Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ waa mɛ obuupu.

english_verse: Salt is good, but if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made to taste salty again?
translation_verse: “Nfɔlɛ ɩ bʋrɔn, amaa nɩɩ nfɔlɛ ɔkɔn kan lʋwɛ mʋ, mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba taalɛ taa waa kɛ ɩ bɩla ɩ waa ɔkɔn?

english_verse: It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!
translation_verse: Ɩ man sʋ kyaarɛ sa kʋtɔ kʋ kɩlɔŋɔ, amɔɔ bɛɛ taa kyɛɛgɛ fɔyɩ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ, o nu.”

english_verse: I will go to my father and say to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you.
translation_verse: N biti n kʋsʋ bwii kpee nsɛ asɛ kɛ n naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, Nsɛ, n waa Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ fʋ alibi.

english_verse: The younger son said to his father, “Give me my share of the property.” So the father divided his property between his two sons.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kebii asɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ, ‘Mɛ‐Sɛ, barɩgɛ fʋ kapatɩyɛ mʋ kpini tɔ kɛ fʋ taa mɛ lɛɛ sa mɛ.’ Ɩmʋ sʋ, mʋ‐sɛ barɩgɛ kapatɩyɛ mʋ tɔ ntun nyɔ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: He had spent everything, when a bad famine spread through that whole land. Soon he had nothing to eat.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ o gyii mu afulee mʋ kpini lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ akʋn damantɛ a tɩyɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini, nɩɩ kitiri fufuli kɩ kɩtaa mʋ.

english_verse: Tax collectors and sinners were all crowding around to listen to Jesus.
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ nɩɩ lenpoo akɔɔlɛpʋ yɛ alibi awaapʋ ba ba sarɛ bee nu Yesu asɛ.

english_verse: So the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses started grumbling, “This man is friendly with sinners. He even eats with them.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Farasii abi yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩɩ kɔɔlɛ alibi awaapʋ halɩɩ maa bamʋ bee bʋga bee gyii.”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them this story:
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan kɩnɩmʋ yɛ,

english_verse: And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kan wu mʋ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ mu ansi i gyii, kɛ ɔ taa mʋ gyaga mʋ abaakpan sʋ,

english_verse: and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.”
translation_verse: kɛ ɔ taa mʋ ba kewu. Kɛ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana yɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi sarɛ, kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ‘Fan ba tii mɛ sʋ kɛ i gyii ansi, lɩɩ fɛɛ n wu mɛ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ fɔyɩ mʋ.’

english_verse: Jesus told the people another story: What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it?
translation_verse: “Abɛɛ a taa fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ gbiti nderebi kudu nɩɩ ɩmʋ kʋkʋlʋn fɔyɩ. Nɩɩ o mee tuwa fɩtɩla kɛ ɔ fɔgɛ kɩkpaara kɩɩ biti kɩmʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o wu kɩmʋ?

english_verse: Jesus said, “In the same way God's angels are happy when even one person turns to him.” Two Sons
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ kensi‐gyii ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asɛ fɛɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ kʋlʋn maa kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ.” Kebii fɔyɩsa mʋ kɩŋasan

english_verse: Jesus told them yet another story: Once a man had two sons.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn kʋ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ nbii‐nyɩnsa‐bii anyɔ.

english_verse: Not long after that, the younger son packed up everything he owned and left for a foreign country, where he wasted all his money in wild living.
translation_verse: “Nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ kamaa tɔ mʋ, kebii asɛ fɛ mʋ kapatɩyɛ mʋ kpini, kʋʋla kʋsʋ loo to kpee ɔsʋwʋlɛ kʋ sʋ kata‐kata. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa gyii mu afulee mʋ kpini kʋsʋbɔ tɔ lʋwɛ.

english_verse: He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ o kpee naa waa kusun sa kasɛ maŋa sʋ ɔdɛ‐bi kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ fɛɛ ɔ kɩɩ mu akuuri sʋ.

english_verse: He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
translation_verse: Ee biti agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ akuuri bee gyii mʋ kigyii, amaa ɔ man nyɛ.

english_verse: Finally, he came to his senses and said, “My father's workers have plenty to eat, and here I am, starving to death!
translation_verse: “Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ ba mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ sa mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, ‘Mɛ‐Sɛ adɔɔpʋ mʋ kpini agyitɔ a gbigi bamʋ, yɛgɛ n bʋ nfɩɩ n biti n wu akʋn lewu.

english_verse: I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.’ ”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n man bɩla dagaa gbaa fɛɛ fʋ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ fu‐bii, taa mɛ waa fɛɛ fʋ adɔɔpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.’

english_verse: The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ ɔ kʋsʋ yii sʋ bwii ɛɛ ba mʋ‐sɛ asɛ. “Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dan san ɔ bʋ kata yɛ kewu mʋ, mʋ‐sɛ wu mʋ, nɩɩ o wu mʋ kʋwɛɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ sɩlɛ naa gyaŋa mu‐bii mʋ, nɩɩ o baari mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The son said, “Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ kebii mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ‐sɛ yɛ, ‘Nsɛ n waa Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ fu alibi. Nɩɩ n man bɩla dagaa gbaa fɛɛ fʋ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ fu‐bii.’

english_verse: But his father said to the servants, “Hurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
translation_verse: “Amaa nɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ tɔwɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ, ‘Fan waa bilen! Fan taa kɩtaa kparɛ ba bun mʋ, fan lɛɛ kepini ba waa mʋ kɩbaa‐bi tɔ, kɛ fan waa mʋ asɩbɩta.

english_verse: Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate.
translation_verse: Kɛ fan naa kɩtaa naatɛ bonbi‐nyɩnsa mʋ ba mɔɔ kɛ a gyii, kɛ a kyaa gyii anɛ ansi.

english_verse: This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.” And they began to celebrate.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mi kebii‐nyɩnsa‐bii nɩmʋ daa wu nɩn, nɩɩ o bwii kyiŋi, ɔ fɔyɩ nɩn, nɩɩ n bwii benti mʋ.’ Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba piili bee gyii bamʋ ansi.

english_verse: The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing.
translation_verse: “Saŋa maa tɔ mʋ yɛgɛ obii bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ bʋ ndɔɔ tɔ. Maa lɩɩ ndɔɔ ɛɛ ba nɩɩ o be tiri kewu tɔ mʋ, o nu ɩlʋn yɛ kakyaa ɩ maa kpira mʋ‐sɛ kɩkpaara sʋ.

english_verse: So he called one of the servants over and asked, “What's going on here?”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ waa.

english_verse: The servant answered, “Your brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Fu‐supu i bwii ba, nɩɩ fʋ‐sɛ mɔɔ mʋ naatɛ bonbi‐nyɩnsa mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fu‐supu bwii ba kewu yɛ kumu nkpa yɛ alanfɩya.’

english_verse: The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house. His father came out and begged him to go in.
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔdaa mʋ nyɛ agbʋ yɛ ɔ man bɩla yɛ o loo kɩkpaara sʋ. Kanɩn sʋ mʋ‐sɛ lɩɩ kpee naa tɔwɛ waa mʋ yɛ o loo ba.

english_verse: But he said to his father, “For years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔ lɛɛ mʋ‐sɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Kɩɩ! Nsu ɩnɩmʋ kpini n maa sun fʋ fɛɛ kɩnyɛ. Nɩɩ n man kina fʋ kɔnɔ kunu. Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ nɩɩ fʋ pɩɩta sa mɛ? Fʋ man sa mɛ halɩɩ kabwɩɩ gbaa kɛ maa mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana a mɔɔ gyii anɛ ansi.

english_verse: This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.”
translation_verse: Amaa fu kebii‐nyɩnsa nɩmʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa nyita fʋ kapatɩyɛ lɩɩ akyɩɩ gyaga sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa bwii ba kewu mʋ, fʋ mɔɔ naatɛ bonbi‐nyɩnsa sa mʋ.’

english_verse: His father replied, “My son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔsɛ mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Mi‐bii, fʋ bʋ mɛ asɛ nfɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa, nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ mʋ gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ nɩn.

english_verse: But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ yɛgɛ ɩ waa anɛ ɔkɔn kɛ a kyaa gyii anɛ ansi, lɩɩ fɛɛ fu‐supu nɩmʋ daa wu nɩn, nɩɩ o bwii kyiŋi, ɔ fɔyɩ nɩn, nɩɩ n bwii benti mʋ.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money.
translation_verse: Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ, “Atɔ wuya kʋ ɩ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ mʋ kapatɩyɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ atɔ wuya mʋ nu fɛɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ii nyita mʋ kapatɩyɛ.

english_verse: So the rich man called him in and said, “What is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ n nu n lɩɩ fʋ sʋ falɛ? Taa mɛ kapatɩyɛ mʋ nɩɩ n taa waa fʋ kɩbaa tɔ mʋ kpini bɩya mɛ, kɛ fʋ karagɛ ɩmʋ kɔnɔ sa mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ man bɩla yɛ fʋ kɩɩ mɛ kapatɩyɛ sʋ.’

english_verse: The manager said to himself, “What shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ, ‘Nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɛnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa? Mɛ‐nyɩrɩpɛ ɩɩ kɔɔlɛ mi kusun ɛɛ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ n man bʋlʋn kɛ n waa kasɛ kukuu kusun, nɩɩ ɩ sʋ mɛ ipeeli yɛ n naa kʋlɛ.

english_verse: I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.”
translation_verse: N nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ n biti n waa nɩɩ kusun mʋ kɩ kan lɩɩ mɛ kɩbaa tɔ mʋ, asa bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ bamʋ akpaara sʋ.’

english_verse: “A hundred barrels of olive oil,” the man answered. So the manager said, “Take your bill and sit down and quickly write ‘
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘N sʋ kʋkɔ olifu nfɔ galɔn alɩfa aburuwa.’ “Nɩɩ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Kɔɔlɛ fʋ ɔwʋlɛ, kɛ fʋ kyɩna bilen kɛ fʋ kyʋrɔɔ ana.’

english_verse: The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, “How much do you owe?” “A thousand sacks of wheat,” the man replied. The manager said, “Take your bill and write ‘
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ ɔnyɔsapʋ mʋ yɛ, ‘Afɩnɛ kʋkɔ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ?’ Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Akyembee ɩbɔɔta kagbɩn kʋlʋn.’ “Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Kɔɔlɛ fʋ ɔwʋlɛ kɛ fʋ kyʋrɔɔ aduburuwa.’

english_verse: The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
translation_verse: “Atɔ wuya mʋ ba yɩn ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ mu ansi ɩ tɩn fɛɛ maa sʋ kanyaasɩn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn nɩɩ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ asa ba sʋ kensi tɩn kɩkyɔ bɛɛ waa asa ɩ kyɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba sʋ bɛɛ waa asa mʋ.”

english_verse: My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan taa fanɛ dulinyaa tɔ atɔ kyɛ asa tɔ biti akyɛmɩnɛ sa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ atɔ mʋ saŋa ɩ kyʋn mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kɔɔlɛ fanɛ ba nkpa kakpaa tɔ.

english_verse: So Abraham said, “If they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ba kan ba man nu Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ, halɩɩ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ gbaa kpee bamʋ asɛ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.’ ”

english_verse: The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him.
translation_verse: Farasii abi mʋ nɩɩ bee biti afulee gaa ba maa nu ɩnɩmʋ kpini mʋ, ba gbɛɛlɛɛ Yesu.

english_verse: But Jesus told them: You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ banɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ fɛn waa fɛɛ fanɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi asa ansi tɔ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ i du. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ kparɛ asa ansi tɔ mʋ, kɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ kisisa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Until the time of John the Baptist, people had to obey the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets. But since God's kingdom has been preached, everyone is trying hard to get in.
translation_verse: “Ba tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ kɩkaapʋ naa fʋʋ Gyɔn saŋa. Lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ ee loo to.

english_verse: Heaven and earth will disappear before the smallest letter of the Law does.
translation_verse: Ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋpɔɔ sa sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ ɩ maa nyaa, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ pii ɩ maa lɩɩ tɔ.

english_verse: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
translation_verse: “Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina mʋ‐ka nɩɩ ɔ naa kili ɔkyɩɩ kʋ mʋ, o nyita kekili, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ o kili ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ mu‐kuli kina mʋ mʋ gbaa ɔ kɔɔlɛ ɔkʋ mʋ‐ka.” Atɔ Wuya Kʋ Maa Lasarusi

english_verse: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Afulee wuya kʋ ɩ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ. Kakɛ kʋmaa ee bun atɔ bunsa danbɩrasa fɛɛ agoo maa kente, yɛgɛ ee gyii mʋ n‐yɩɩ kakɛ kʋmaa.

english_verse: He was happy just to eat the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. His body was covered with sores, and dogs kept coming up to lick them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ daa nyɛ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ atɔ wuya mʋ teeburi sʋ ɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ mʋ gbaa gyii. Halɩɩ igyoonɔ gbaa bɛɛ ba bee nyita mʋ ɩlɔ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
translation_verse: “Saŋa kʋ tɔ mʋ odibapu mʋ be wu, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ba taa mʋ kpee Ɩbwaarɛ nyɩmɩsa Abraham asɛ sʋsʋ. Atɔ wuya mʋ gbaa be wu nɩɩ ba pula mʋ.

english_verse: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,
translation_verse: Ɔ naa loo Sɩtaanɛ fʋlɔn tɔ yɛgɛ ɔ bʋ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ. O diyaa mu ansi mʋ, o wu Lasarusi maa tɛ Abraham asɛ kata‐kata.

english_verse: he said to Abraham, “Have pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ, ɔ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ɔsɛ Abraham, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ kɛ fʋ sun Lasarusi kɛ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbaa‐bi nyɔ nkyu tɔ, kɛ ɔ taa ba yuula mɛ kanandʋlʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n gyii awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn nɩmʋ tɔ.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “My friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain.
translation_verse: “Amaa Abraham lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ. ‘Mi‐bii, nyiŋi fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kyɩna fʋ tɛ nkpa tɔ kasɛ sʋ mʋ fʋ nyɛ atɔ danbɩrasa nɩɩ Lasarusi mɔɔ nyɛ atɔ libi, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ mʋ ee gyii ɔkɔn yɛgɛ fɛɛ fii gyii awʋrʋfɔ.

english_verse: But the rich man said, “Abraham, then please send Lazarus to my father's home.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ atɔ wuya mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Kanɩn bɩrɛ mɛ‐sɛ n kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, sun Lasarusi o bwii kpee mɛ‐sɛ kɩkpaara sʋ.

english_verse: Let him warn my five brothers, so they won't come to this horrible place.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ, n sʋ mi‐supu ana anuu. Yɛgɛ ɔ naa da bamʋ amu tɔ kɛ ba man ba katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ n gyii awʋrʋfɔ falɛ.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “Your brothers can read what Moses and the prophets wrote. They should pay attention to that.”
translation_verse: “Abraham lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ ɩ bʋ bamʋ asɛ, bɛ taalɛ nu lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ.’

english_verse: Then the rich man said, “No, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ayee, mɛ‐sɛ Abraham, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan lɩɩ lewu tɔ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ, bee biti ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa.’

english_verse: Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?”
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ kadɛndɛmandɛ nɩmʋ nkʋn ɩ taalɛ bwii ba fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii pii fɛɛ gowa abi gbaa mʋ, fɛn taalɛ tɔwɛ oyu lala nɩmʋ yɛ, ‘Faa fu ilin kpini fii kyiigi naa yii ɔpʋ tɔ,’ kɛ i nu fanɛ asɛ.

english_verse: If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, “Welcome! Come on in and have something to eat”?
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ ɔkʋ sʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ dɔɔ abɛɛ ɛɛ kɩɩ nbʋlʋpʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kan lɩɩ ndɔɔ tɔ ba mʋ, fɩɩ dan taalɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Waa bilen kɛ fʋ ba kyɩna gyii?’

english_verse: There is no use saying, ‘Look! Here it is’ or ‘Look! There it is.’ God's kingdom is here with you.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taalɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Kɩɩ kɩmʋ ndee’ abɛɛ ‘kɩ bʋ falɛ’. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura mʋ kɩ bʋ fanɛ tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ, “Saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, nɩɩ fen biti fɛɛ fan wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ mɛ nkɛ mʋ kʋkʋ, amaa fan mɛɛ yɛ fen wu.

english_verse: Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ i tiri fɛɛ ɔ fwaala kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ waa sa mʋ abɛɛ?

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ yɛ, “Ɩ waa nɛnɛ, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ waa alibi mʋ ii biti ɩ ba. Amaa ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ bɔla mʋ sʋ kɛ ɩ ba mʋ, mʋ laakʋ.

english_verse: So be careful what you do. Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan baa fan da fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ. Nɩɩ fu‐supu kan waa fʋ alibi mʋ taasɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kan nu mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ mʋ, kɛ fʋ taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, “I am sorry,” you should still forgive that person.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa fʋ alibi ɩlɩɩ isunɔ kakɛ kʋlʋn, nɩɩ ɔ kan nu mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ ba tɔwɛ fʋ ɩlɩɩ isunɔ yɛ, ‘N man waa danbɩrasa mʋ,’ taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ.” Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii Asɩn

english_verse: The apostles said to the Lord, “Make our faith stronger!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asunpu mʋ ba tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, “Tii anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ kɛ kɩ nyɛ akyɔ.”

english_verse: No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, “Prepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.”
translation_verse: Ayee! Fii biti fʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Dɩŋa mɛ kalaatɩpwɛ agyitɔ ba yii mɛ kɛ n gyii kɛ n nuu. N kan lʋwɛ mʋ, kɛ ngbaa fʋ, fʋ naa gyii kɛ fu nuu.’

english_verse: And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, “We are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.” Ten Men with Leprosy
translation_verse: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ, nɩɩ fan kan waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan waa mʋ, fan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘An gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ a man sʋ kyaarɛ, an dan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa a maa waa mʋ nɩn.’ ” Yesu Kyɛ Abootipu Kudu

english_verse: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu ii kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, ɔ bɔla Samariya yɛ Galile ɩsʋwʋlɛ ngyaŋɛ nɩn.

english_verse: As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance
translation_verse: Maa kpee kadɛ‐bi kʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ gyaŋa anyɩn kudu nɩɩ bɛɛ lɔ kibootoo. Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ ba yɩlɛ kata,

english_verse: and shouted, “Jesus, Master, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: nɩɩ ba kuusi kenken yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu, wu anɛ kʋwɛɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “Go show yourselves to the priests.” On their way they were healed.
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu bamʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ “Fan taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ naa kaapʋ aseepu.” Ba maa kpee mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God.
translation_verse: Bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, maa wu fɛɛ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya mʋ, o bwii ba ee kuusi kenken ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: He bowed down at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. The man was from the country of Samaria.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o be muŋa Yesu ayaa tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ fwaala mʋ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa sa mʋ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ kanɩn kanyɩn maŋa gyɛ Kasamariya‐nyɩn nɩn.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Weren't ten men healed? Where are the other nine?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ yɛ, “Man gyɛ asa kudu ɩ nyɛ alanfɩya. Fɩnɛ nɩɩ asansa akpanɔ mʋ ba bʋ?

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “You may get up and go. Your faith has made you well.” God's Kingdom (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ natɛ, fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.” Ɩbwaarɛ Kuwura‐gyii Mʋ Kɩɩ Ba (Matiwu

english_verse: When people say to you, “Look there,” or “Look here,” don't go looking for him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa bee biti ba tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ‘Mʋ ɩ bʋ falɛ mʋ’ abɛɛ ‘Mʋ ndee’. Amaa fan man waa fan buu bamʋ.

english_verse: When Lot lived, people were also eating and drinking. They were buying, selling, planting, and building.
translation_verse: “Kanɩn nɩɩ ɩ waa Loti saŋa mʋ, asa ba kyɩna bee gyii yɛgɛ bee nuu, bɛɛ fɛ yɛgɛ bɛɛ sɔɔ, bee duu yɛgɛ bɛɛ pɔyɩ.

english_verse: Then Jesus' disciples spoke up, “But where will this happen, Lord?” Jesus said, “Where there is a corpse, there will always be vultures.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu yɛ, “Fɩnɛ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa.” Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ belenbuni bʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ agyinbwii gbaa ba ba gyaŋɛ.”

english_verse: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today.
translation_verse: Amaa ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n gyankpaa wu awʋrʋfɔ gaa, kɛ kɩnaana nɩmʋ tɔ abi ba kina mɛ.

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: “Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa anɛ naana Nowa saŋa mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa kibwii ba saŋa mʋ gbaa.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Anɛ naana Nowa saŋa mʋ, asa ba kyɩna bee gyii yɛgɛ bee nuu, bee kili naa fʋʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ naana Nowa loo ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ nkyu ɩ ba muuri bamʋ kpini.

english_verse: But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone.
translation_verse: Amaa kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Loti lɩɩ Sodom kadɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ fʋlɔn yɛ kuduri lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba dɩyɛ bamʋ kpini.

english_verse: The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: “Kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n biti n lɛɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything.
translation_verse: Kanɩn kakɛ maa nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ɔ bʋ kewusunɔ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ atɔ a bʋ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla kpee kɩkpaara sʋ naa taa atɔ. Kanɩn ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ ndɔɔ tɔ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla bwii kpee kewu kɛ ɔ naa taa kʋtɔ kʋ.

english_verse: Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
translation_verse: Fan nyiŋi Loti mʋ‐ka.

english_verse: People who try to save their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives will save them.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkpa mʋ, ii biti ɩ lɩɩ mʋ abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ nkpa sa lɩɩ mɛ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kɩmʋ.

english_verse: On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Kanɩn kagyanbwɛ maŋa mʋ, asa anyɔ bee biti ba baa ba dɛ kalan kʋlʋn sʋ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa ɔkʋ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding wheat, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Anyɩn anyɔ bee biti ba baa bee sun kusun ndɔɔ tɔ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ ba taa ɔkʋ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ kɩŋasan mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ kaapʋ bamʋ fɛɛ, ba ba bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ saŋa kʋmaa, kɛ ba man yɛgɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ nnyɔ bamʋ.

english_verse: In a town there was once a judge who didn't fear God or care about people.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Asɩn ogyipu kʋ ɩ bʋ kadɛ kʋkʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ɔ man taa nyɩmɩsa tɩɩ sɛhn.

english_verse: In that same town there was a widow who kept going to the judge and saying, “Make sure that I get fair treatment in court.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ okulapu‐kyɩɩ kʋ gbaa bʋ kadɛ maŋa tɔ, ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ ba asɩn ogyipu mʋ asɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Kyɛ mɛ tɔ kɛ n gyii biri mɛ dʋn ana.’

english_verse: For a while the judge refused to do anything. Finally, he said to himself, “Even though I don't fear God or care about people,
translation_verse: Ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ gaa nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ waa sɛhn ɛɛ sa mʋ. Amaa kamaa tɔ mʋ, asɩn ogyipu mʋ tɔwɛ waa mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, ‘Ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ man taa nyɩmɩsa tɩɩ sɛhn.

english_verse: He will surely hurry and help them. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find on this earth anyone with faith? A Pharisee and a Tax Collector
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kɩɩ sʋ bilen‐bilen fɛɛ ɔ taa bamʋ kesintin sa bamʋ. Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa n kan ba mʋ, n biti n nyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii kasɛ sʋ?” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ Kafarasii‐nyɩn Yɛ Lenpoo Ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ sʋ

english_verse: Jesus told a story to some people who thought they were better than others and who looked down on everyone else:
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla bɔ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ sa asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi, yɛgɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩyan. Ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: The Pharisee stood over by himself and prayed, “God, I thank you that I am not greedy, dishonest, and unfaithful in marriage like other people. And I am really glad that I am not like that tax collector over there.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ yɩlɛ mʋ kʋkwɩɩ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɛɛ yɩn mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ, ‘Ɩbwaarɛ, n fwaala fʋ gaa fɛɛ maa n man du fɛɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, n mee yuuri, abɛɛ n mee puni asa abɛɛ n man gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ n nyita kekili abɛɛ n man du fɛɛ lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ nfɩɩ falɛ.

english_verse: I go without eating for two days a week, and I give you one tenth of all I earn.”
translation_verse: N ŋmina kɔnɔ nkɛ nnyɔ, nkɛ nsunɔ kʋmaa tɔ, nɩɩ n barɩgɛ mɛ atɔ tɔ ntun kudu, nɩɩ n taa ketun kʋlʋn n sa fʋ.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “When the two men went home, it was the tax collector and not the Pharisee who was pleasing to God. If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: Ba bwii kpewu. Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, lenpoo ɔkɔɔlɛpʋ mʋ i gyii asʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, man gyɛ Kafarasii‐nyɩn mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ bɩya mʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ kʋsaa mʋ sʋsʋ.” Yesu waa Nbii Mʋ Kusee (Matiwu

english_verse: Some people brought their little children for Jesus to bless. But when his disciples saw them doing this, they told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa akʋ gbaa ba sʋ bamʋ nbii wuribi bɛɛ ba Yesu asɛ, kɛ ɔ taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ. Mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, ba gya bamʋ.

english_verse: will be given much more in this life. And in the future world they will have eternal life.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: ee biti ɔ nyɛ falɛ atɔ gaa, nbɩɩnbɩɩ kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ yɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba kamaa tɔ mʋ?” Yesu Tɔwɛ Mu Kelewu Yɛ Mu Kyiŋi Asɩn

english_verse: Jesus took the twelve apostles aside and said: We are now on our way to Jerusalem. Everything that the prophets wrote about the Son of Man will happen there.
translation_verse: Saŋa kʋ Yesu taa abɩɩlapʋ kudu anyɔ mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “An kpee Gyerusalem kɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla lɩɩ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa sʋ mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

english_verse: An important man asked Jesus, “Good Teacher, what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ɔgyankpaapʋ kʋ taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ danbɩrasa, mɩnɛ nɩɩ n biti n waa kɛ n nyɛ nkpa kakpaa?”

english_verse: He told Jesus, “I have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N buu nbara ɩnɩmʋ kpini lɩɩ mi kibii tɔ.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he said, “There is one thing you still need to do. Go and sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Yesu maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ san fʋ kʋtɔ kʋlʋn. Naa fɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ kɛ fʋ taa afulee mʋ sa atiripu, ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ fu biliŋi atɔ wuya sʋsʋ. Kɛ fʋ ba buu mɛ.”

english_verse: When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Amaa maa nu falɛ mʋ, ɩ kɔ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ atɔ wuya gaa.

english_verse: Jesus saw how sad the man was. So he said, “It's terribly hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu kɩɩ mʋ kyiim, tɔwɛ yɛ, “I biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋlʋn sa atɔ wuya ana fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ ba buu Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩkaapʋ.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Kesintin ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋpɔɔ sa nyɔɔma fɛɛ ɔ taalɛ loo baasa ɔbɔ tɔ bʋlɛ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ atɔ wuya maa yɛgɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ o buu Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ kɩkaapʋ.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard this, they asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu mʋ ba taasɛ yɛ, “Kanɩn bɩrɛ anɩmʋ ɩɩ taalɛ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa mɛɛ taalɛ waa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ bɩrɛ ɛɛ taalɛ waa.”

english_verse: Peter said, “Remember, we left everything to be your followers!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kɩɩ! An yɛgɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩɩ an baa an buu fʋ falɛ!”

english_verse: They will beat him and kill him, but three days later he will rise to life.
translation_verse: Kɛ ɩmʋ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ kɛ n bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ.”

english_verse: When Jesus was coming close to Jericho, a blind man sat begging beside the road.
translation_verse: Yesu maa tiri Gyeriko tɔ mʋ, yɛgɛ gyaatanapʋ kʋ tɛ ɔkpa asɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ atɔ.

english_verse: The man heard the crowd walking by and asked what was happening.
translation_verse: Maa nu asa damantɛ ba maa kyʋn mʋ, ɔ taasɛ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩɩ waa?”

english_verse: Some people told him that Jesus from Nazareth was passing by.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ ɩ kyʋn kanɩn maŋa.”

english_verse: So the blind man shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o kuusi tɩɩ yɛ, “Yesu, Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ!”

english_verse: The people who were going along with Jesus told the man to be quiet. But he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Yesu ansi tɔ mʋ, ba gya mʋ yɛ o bun mʋ kɔnɔ sʋ, amaa ɔ dan san ɔ maa sʋ ee kuusi kenken yɛ, “Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii, wu mɛ kʋwɛɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa mʋ bɩya mʋ. Maa tiri mʋ tɔ mʋ, Yesu taasɛ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: “What do you want me to do for you?” “Lord, I want to see!” he answered.
translation_verse: “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti fɛɛ n waa sa fʋ?” Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n biti fɛɛ mi ansi ɩ bɩla bugi.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Look and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fu ansi i bugi! Fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.”

english_verse: At once the man could see, and he went with Jesus and started thanking God. When the crowds saw what happened, they praised God.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ mu ansi ɩ bɩla bugi nɩɩ o buu Yesu, yɛgɛ ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ asa mʋ kpini ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, ngbaa bamʋ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ sɩlɛ kpee ansi tɔ naa dii oyu kʋ kɛ ɔ nyɛ wu Yesu, lɩɩ fɛɛ tʋtɔ sʋ nɩɩ Yesu ii biti ɔ bɔla.

english_verse: When Jesus got there, he looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down! I want to stay with you today.”
translation_verse: Yesu maa fʋʋ opula maŋa mʋ ɔ kɩɩ sʋsʋ wu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Sakiyosi, kpɩlɩgɛ ba bilen. N biti n sʋgɛ fʋ ayɛ ndaga.”

english_verse: Zacchaeus hurried down and gladly welcomed Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kpɩlɩgɛ bilen taa kinsi gyii waa mʋ nsa‐nsa.

english_verse: Everyone who saw this started grumbling, “This man Zacchaeus is a sinner! And Jesus is going home to eat with him.”
translation_verse: Asa ba wu ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ yɛ, “O kpee naa sʋgɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: Later that day Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “I will give half of my property to the poor. And I will now pay back four times as much to everyone I have ever cheated.”
translation_verse: Amaa Sakiyosi kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu yɛ, “Kɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n biti n barɩgɛ mɛ kapatɩyɛ tɔ kɩnyɔ, kɛ n taa kɩbaafʋn sa atiripu. Nɩɩ n kan kɔɔlɛ afulee lɩɩ ɔkʋ asɛ ɩ kyɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n kɔɔlɛ mʋ, n biti n ka mʋ kanɩn kʋkɔ maŋa kɩlɩɩ kɩnaa.”

english_verse: Jesus said to Zacchaeus, “Today you and your family have been saved, because you are a true son of Abraham.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kamɔlɩgɛ kɩ ba fʋ ayɛ ndaga, lɩɩ fɛɛ ngbaa fʋ, fʋ gyɛ Abraham kanaana‐bii nɩn.

english_verse: The Son of Man came to look for and to save people who are lost.” A Story about Ten Servants (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ n ba nɩn yɛ n ba biti kɛ n mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba fɔyɩ.” Ayaafɔlɛ Danbɩrasa Anyɔ Yɛ Kɩyaafɔlɛ Ɔwʋlɛgyɔpʋ Mʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: But they said, “Sir, he already has ten times as much!”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, ɔ wʋla nyɛ afulee mʋ nɩɩ i budu.’

english_verse: But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, “We don't want this man to be our king.”
translation_verse: “Amaa ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ asa ba kisi mʋ, nɩɩ ba sun gya mʋ sʋ naa tɔwɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ee kpee mʋ owura lala mʋ yɛ, ‘A mɛɛ biti ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ o be gyii anɛ sʋ kuwura.’

english_verse: He told them, “Go into the next village, where you will find a young donkey that has never been ridden. Untie the donkey and bring it here.
translation_verse: “Fan kpee kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ fanɛ ansi tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ fan maa dan fen loo kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, fen biti fan wu kekuruma‐bon kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ man pɩɩta dii mʋ maa kyaa oyu. Fan Saaŋɛ mʋ bɩya mɛ nfɩɩ.

english_verse: The first servant came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me I have earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: “Ɔgyankpaasa mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fu afulee mʋ, ɩ kʋʋgɛ kanɩn ɩlɩɩ kudu tii sʋ’.

english_verse: The second one came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me, I have earned five times as much.”
translation_verse: “Ɔnyɔsapʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ fu afulee mʋ i bwii kʋʋgɛ ɩlɩɩ kunuu tii sʋ.’

english_verse: Another servant came and said, “Sir, here is your money. I kept it safe in a handkerchief.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋ gbaa ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fu afulee ndee. N taa kɩmʋ ŋmina kikyibi tɔ taa yɩla katɩnkʋ.

english_verse: Why didn't you put my money in the bank? On my return, I could have had the money together with interest.”
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ man taa mɛ afulee maŋa naa yɩla afulee ɔyɩlɛkpa kɛ ɩ kʋʋgɛ agyaga sa mɛ? Naafɔɔ n maa ba falɛ kɛ n ba kɔɔlɛ ɩmaa ɩmʋ agyaga.’

english_verse: Then he said to some other servants standing there, “Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ mʋ yɛ, ‘Fan kɔɔlɛ afulee mʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ, kɛ fan taa sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ɩlɩɩ kudu tii sʋ mʋ.’

english_verse: Now bring me the enemies who didn't want me to be their king. Kill them while I watch!” Jesus Enters Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ taa agbʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ mɛ dʋn ana, nɩɩ be mee biti n maa gyii bamʋ sʋ kuwura mʋ, fan taa bamʋ ba mɔɔ mi ansi tɔ nfɩɩ.’ ” Yesu kyɩna kuruma sʋ loo Gyerusalem fɛɛ Owura (Matiwu

english_verse: When Jesus had finished saying all this, he went on toward Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ gyankpaa kʋbʋ mʋ ee kpee Gyerusalem.

english_verse: As he was getting near Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples on ahead.
translation_verse: Maa tiri Betifage maa Betani ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Olifu kɩbɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ mʋ, o sun mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”
