translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taasɛ fanɛ yɛ, ‘Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fen saaŋɛ mʋ mʋ?’ fan tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti mʋ.’ ”

english_verse: They went off and found everything just as Jesus had said.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ Yesu sun mʋ, ba naa wu mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ.

english_verse: While they were untying the donkey, its owners asked, “Why are you doing that?”
translation_verse: Ba maa saaŋɛ kuruma mʋ, mʋ wuya ana ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fen saaŋɛ kuruma mʋ?”

english_verse: They answered, “The Lord needs it.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti mʋ.”

english_verse: Then they led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back and helped Jesus get on.
translation_verse: Ba taa mʋ ba sa Yesu, nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ataa gyaga mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ba kyɛ Yesu tɔ nɩɩ o dii kyɩna mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: And as he rode along, the people spread clothes on the road in front of him.
translation_verse: Maa kpee mʋ, asa ba taa bamʋ ataa tʋʋ ɔkpa tɔ.

english_verse: When Jesus started down the Mount of Olives, his large crowd of disciples were happy and praised God because of all the miracles they had seen.
translation_verse: Yesu maa tiri Gyerusalem opula mʋ nɩɩ ɔkpa ɩɩ kpɩlɩgɛ tɔ ii kpee Olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ, abɩɩlapʋ yɛ kʋbʋ damantɛ ba piili bee kuusi bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ kinsigyi tɔ lɩɩ adangana mʋ nɩɩ ba wu Yesu maa wʋla waa mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: They shouted, “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory to God.”
translation_verse: Bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ waa Owura mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba mʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ kusee. Keyuula kɩ bʋ sʋsʋ. Nɩɩ bʋnyaa ɩ dagaa Ɩbwaarɛ.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, make your disciples stop shouting!”
translation_verse: Farasii abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ mʋ ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, tɔwɛ fu abuupu yɛ ba bun bamʋ nnɔ sʋ.”

english_verse: When Jesus came closer and could see Jerusalem, he cried
translation_verse: Ba maa tiri Gyerusalem tɔ nɩɩ o wu kadɛ mʋ, o su sa kɩmʋ asa.

english_verse: and said: It is too bad that today your people don't know what will bring them peace! Now it is hidden from them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fan daa fan nyi ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ bɩya fanɛ keyuula ndaga kakɛ nɩmʋ mʋ, naafɔɔ ɩ bʋrɔn. Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ fen mee yɛ fen wu ɔkpa mʋ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, the time will come when your enemies will build walls around you to attack you. Armies will surround you and close in on you from every side.
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ ii biti ɩ ba, kɛ fanɛ akisipu ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ fanɛ sʋ, kyaabɔɔ fanɛ kɛ ba nyan fanɛ lɩɩ fanɛ abaafʋn kpini.

english_verse: They will level you to the ground and kill your people. Not one stone in your buildings will be left on top of another. This will happen because you did not see that God had come to save you. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Bee biti ba da fanɛ kasɛ, fanɛ yɛ nbii mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ fanɛ asɛ kpini. Bɛ mɛɛ yɛ ba yɛgɛ fanɛ ɔpɔrɛ kubuu kʋlʋn kɩ gyaga kɩmʋ bɩrɩsa sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, fan man taalɛ pɛɛsɛ bɩɩ.” Yesu gya asa lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ (Matiwu

english_verse: When Jesus entered the temple, he started chasing out the people who were selling things.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ opula tɔ, nɩɩ o piili ɛɛ gya bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ atɔ mʋ.

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be a place of worship.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ba tɔwɛ yɩla Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘Mɛ kɩkpaara kii biti kɩ baa kɩ gyɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kɩkpaara nɩn, amaa fan taa kɩmʋ biliŋi bamʋ nɩɩ bee yuuri ɔlʋn sʋ mʋ ɔbaalakpa.’ ”

english_verse: Each day, Jesus kept on teaching in the temple. So the chief priests, the teachers of the Law of Moses, and some other important people tried to have him killed.
translation_verse: Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ asa mʋ tɔ agyankpaapʋ bee biti fɛɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: But they could not find a way to do it, because everyone else was eager to listen to him.
translation_verse: Amaa ba man taalɛ nyɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba bɔla sʋ kɛ ba waa kanɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ kpini ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ mata mʋ bee nu mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: The spies said to him, “Teacher, we know you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ apɛɛtɩpʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, an nyi fɛɛ fʋ ɩ tɔwɛ nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn, nɩɩ fʋ mɛɛ mɛɛgɛ tɔ, amaa nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ.

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus
translation_verse: Gyiwu abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Sadusi mʋ nɩɩ ba yɛ kelewu kyiŋi man bʋ tɔ mʋ, akʋ ba ba Yesu asɛ yɛ asɩn taasɛsa. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: asked him, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Tɔwɛ anɛ, anɩmʋ ɔlʋn sʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fɩɩ waa atɔ nɩmʋ? Anɩmʋ ɩ sa fʋ ɔlʋn maŋa?”

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn gyee asa mʋ, kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn abɛɛ kɩ lɩɩ asa asɛ?”

english_verse: They talked this over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ ba taa kɩmʋ sʋ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ ba yɛ, “Nɩɩ an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ’, ee biti ɔ taasɛ yɛ, ‘Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fan man kɔɔlɛ gyii?’

english_verse: And we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize. The crowd will stone us to death, because they think John was a prophet.”
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ an kan tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɩ lɩɩ asa asɛ mʋ’, asa mʋ kpini bee biti ba twɩɩ anɛ abuu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Gyɔn gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.”

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ, ba lɛɛ Yesu kɔnɔ yɛ, “An man nyi ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn kekyugyee mʋ lɩɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” Renters of a Vineyard (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Kanɩn bɩrɛ, ngbaa mɛ n mɛɛ yɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini.” Kɩŋasan lɩɩ ahayɩɩpʋ kʋ sʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes, and they like to be greeted in the market. They want the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: “Fan kɩɩ fan n‐yɩɩ sʋ danbɩrasa lɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ sʋ. Ba gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ɩkaalɛ lala kubun bɛɛ kyaabɔɔ tɔ, nɩɩ bee biti fɛɛ ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ fwaala bamʋ agya tɔ, bee biti ba kyɩna ngya kparɛ sʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa yɛ ɔgyaŋɛ gyiikpa.

english_verse: Jesus told the people this story: A man once planted a vineyard and rented it out. Then he left the country for a long time.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn kʋ dɔɔ wayɩn kʋdɔɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa hayɩɩ sa adɔɔpʋ kʋ, nɩɩ o kpee ɔkpa naa kyɛɛrɛɛ.

english_verse: When it was time to harvest the crop, he sent a servant to ask the renters for his share of the grapes. But they beat up the servant and sent him away without anything.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ oyu mʋ abi kɩtɩyɛ fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ kʋdɔɔ wuya mʋ sun mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ, kpee ahayɩɩpʋ mʋ asɛ yɛ ba sa mʋ wayɩn oyu abi mʋ ɩkʋ lɩɩ mʋ kʋdɔɔ mʋ tɔ. Amaa ahayɩɩpʋ mʋ ba dayɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ bwiiyaa mʋ abaa kpan.

english_verse: When the renters saw the owner's son, they said to one another, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: “Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kʋdɔɔ mʋ ahayɩɩpʋ ba wu obii mʋ, ba taa nfɛɛrɛ mʋ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kapatɩyɛ ogyipu mʋ, fan yɛgɛ a mɔɔ mʋ, kɛ atɔ mʋ a biliŋi anɛ lɛɛ.’

english_verse: I'll tell you what. He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.” When the people heard this, they said, “This must never happen!”
translation_verse: Kʋdɔɔ wuya mʋ ii biti ɔ ba mɔɔ ahayɩɩpʋ mʋ, kɛ ɔ taa oyu kʋdɔɔ mʋ sa akʋ.” Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba maa nu falɛ mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ “Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa kanɩn kpee kata.”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses knew that Jesus was talking about them when he was telling this story. They wanted to arrest him right then, but they were afraid of the people. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ yɛ aseepu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba bɩɩ fɛɛ kɩŋasan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔɔ mʋ i yii bamʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ bee biti ɔkpa ba kɩtaa mʋ opula maŋa tɔ. Amaa ba sɩlɛ asa mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus' enemies kept watching him closely, because they wanted to hand him over to the Roman governor. So they sent some men who pretended to be good. But they were really spies trying to catch Jesus saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Yɛgɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba taa bamʋ ansi yii Yesu sʋ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ apɛɛtɩpʋ akʋ yɛ ba waa mu ansi sʋ kɩdanbɩrasa. Bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ bee biti ba nyɛ Yesu kɩtaa lɩɩ mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa sʋ, kɛ ba taa mʋ sa ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ alʋlʋnpʋ.

english_verse: “Show me a coin.” Then he asked, “Whose picture and name are on it?” “The Emperor's,” they answered.
translation_verse: “Fan taa nderebi mʋ ba kɛ n kɩɩ.” Maa wu kɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ foto yɛ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyan kɩmʋ sʋ?”

english_verse: Jesus' enemies could not catch him saying anything wrong there in front of the people. They were amazed at his answer and kept quiet. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ sʋ mʋ, i kyinkyin apɛɛtɩpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba laatɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba man taalɛ ba nyɛ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn kʋ ba taa kɩtaa mʋ sakyɔ mʋ tɔ. Kikyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ yɛ kekili asɩn (Matiwu

english_verse: and said: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, anɛ naana Mosesi kyʋrɔɔ sa anɛ yɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan wu san okulapu‐kyɩɩ, nɩɩ ba man kʋʋgɛ mʋ, i tiri fɛɛ mu‐supu ɔ taa mʋ kili, kʋʋgɛ nbii sa mʋ‐daa mʋ.

english_verse: There were once seven brothers. The first one married, but died without having any children.
translation_verse: To! Anyibii asunɔ akʋ ba kyɩna ba bʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔgyankpaapʋ mʋ kili ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kʋʋgɛ nɩɩ o wu.

english_verse: The second one married his brother's widow, and he also died without having any children.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɔsapʋ mʋ kili okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ gbaa mʋ wu.

english_verse: The same thing happened to the third one. Finally, all seven brothers married this woman and died without having any children.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔsasapʋ mʋ gbaa kili mʋ, nɩɩ o wu. Nɩɩ ɩ waa kanan halɩɩ asa asunɔ mʋ kpini ba kili mʋ, yɛgɛ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ maa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba man kʋʋgɛ.

english_verse: At last the woman died.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ okulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ gbaa ba wu.

english_verse: Jesus answered: The people in this world get married.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Dulinya nɩmʋ tɔ bɩrɛ asa bee kili.

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, you have given a good answer!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ akʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, fʋ tɔwɛ kɩmʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Why do people say that the Messiah will be the son of King David?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ gyɛ Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii?

english_verse: In the book of Psalms, David himself says, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Dawidi gbaa‐gbaa tɔwɛ mʋ Ɩlʋn ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, “Kyɩna mɛ gyisa sʋ nfɩɩ,

english_verse: until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa fʋ dʋn ana ba waa fʋ ayaa ɔgyagakpa.” ’

english_verse: David spoke of the Messiah as his Lord, so how can the Messiah be his son?” Jesus and the Teachers of the Law of Moses (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Dawidi gbaa‐gbaa ɩɩ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ’ bɩrɛ, nɛnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ mʋ kanaana‐bii?” Nbara Akaapʋpʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: While everyone was listening to Jesus, he said to his disciples:
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba san bee nu mʋ, Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: But they cheat widows out of their homes and then pray long prayers just to show off. These teachers will be punished most of all.
translation_verse: Bee pɩna akulapu‐kyɩɩ bɛɛ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ akpaara, nɩɩ bɛɛ yɩlɛ ipula bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ bʋnswɩɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ nyɛ pɩna asa fɛɛ ba gyɛ asa danbɩrasa. Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ bɩɩtɛ kanɩn asa maŋa asʋ gaa.”

english_verse: Before all this happens, you will be arrested and punished. You will be tried in your synagogues and put in jail. Because of me you will be placed on trial before kings and governors.
translation_verse: “Amaa pɔyɩ kɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini ɩ ba mʋ, bee biti ba kɩtaa fanɛ kɛ ba nyanla fanɛ. Bee biti ba taa fanɛ kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa naa gyii fanɛ asɩn, kɛ ba naa tii fanɛ ibu. Bee biti ba taa fanɛ kpee awura yɛ alʋnpʋ ansi tɔ, lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Some people were talking about the beautiful stones used to build the temple and about the gifts that had been placed in it. Jesus said,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ akʋ bee biri kiligyima bɛɛ lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn, yɛ abuu danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa pɔyɩ, yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ asa ba kɛɛ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ obu mʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Amaa Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: “Do you see these stones? The time is coming when not one of them will be left in place. They will all be knocked down.” Warning about Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan wu falɛ kpini, saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba yɛgɛ fɛn mɛɛ bɩla yɛ fen wu kubuu kʋlʋn gbaa kɩ maa gyan kʋkʋ sʋ, bee biti ba nɩn kubuu kʋmaa nɩmʋ tʋʋ kasɛ.”

english_verse: Some people asked, “Teacher, when will all this happen? How can we know when these things are about to take place?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, saŋa mɔmɔ nɩɩ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ a biti a waa? Nɩɩ mɩnɛ sɩnkaala ii biti ɩ waa kaapʋ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ kanɩn atɔ nɩmʋ ii biti ɩ waa?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Don't be fooled by those who will come and claim to be me. They will say, “I am Christ!” and “Now is the time!” But don't follow them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan baa fan da sʋ, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man pɩna fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa gaalaagaa bee biti ba ba mɛ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ’. Nɩɩ bee biti ba bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Laalaalʋwɛ saŋa mʋ tee tiri tɔ’. Amaa fan man buu bamʋ.

english_verse: When you hear about wars and riots, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but this isn't the end.
translation_verse: Fan kan nu kɩnaa yɛ ɩkɔ sʋ mʋ, fan man waa kufuu. Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ ɩ maa gyankpaa ba kanɩn pɔyɩ. Amaa ɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ tee tiri tɔ nɩn.”

english_verse: Nations will go to war against one another, and kingdoms will attack each other.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɩla kyaga sʋ yɛ, “Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ii biti ɔ kɔ yɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ, kɛ awuragyi yɛ awuragyi ba kɔ yɛ abaa.

english_verse: There will be great earthquakes, and in many places people will starve to death and suffer terrible diseases. All sorts of frightening things will be seen in the sky.
translation_verse: Kɛ kasɛ ki gyingyen ipula kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ, kɛ akʋn yɛ alɔ kyaŋɛsa a tɩyɛ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ, kɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ a sʋ kufuu yɛ ɩsɩnkaala lala‐lala a lɩɩ sʋsʋ.

english_verse: You will be betrayed by your own parents, brothers, family, and friends. Some of you will even be killed.
translation_verse: Fanɛ akʋʋgɛpʋ abɛɛ adaa yɛ asupu abɛɛ fanɛ kʋʋpʋ ana abɛɛ akyɛmɩnɛ bee biti ba lɛɛ fanɛ sa, kɛ ba mɔɔ fanɛ tɔ akʋ.

english_verse: Because of me, you will be hated by everyone.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti o kisi fanɛ, lɩɩ mɛ sʋ.

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at this time, run to the mountains. If you are in the city, leave it. And if you are out in the country, don't go back into the city.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya kasɛ sʋ mʋ, ba lɩɩ sɩlɛ kpee abɩɩ tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ mɔɔ ba lɩɩ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba man loo Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by soldiers, you will know that it will soon be destroyed.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ fan kan wu fɛɛ anaakɔpʋ ba kyaabɔɔ Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ, fen biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ kɩmʋ kinyita ki tiri tɔ.

english_verse: This time of punishment is what is written about in the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Kpalɩ fɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ nkɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kpini ɩ ba tɔ.

english_verse: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children! Everywhere in the land people will suffer horribly and be punished.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɩtɔ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ nbii‐nyɛpʋsa mʋ laakʋ! Lɩɩ fɛɛ awʋrʋfɔ lala a biti a ba tɩyɛ kasɛ maŋa sʋ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ lala mʋ ɩ ba Gyiwu abi sʋ.

english_verse: Then Jesus told them a story: When you see a fig tree or any other tree
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bɔɔ bamʋ kɩŋasan nɩmʋ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ kɩdɩnpɔ oyu yɛ iyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ san mʋ kpini.

english_verse: So, when you see these things happening, you know that God's kingdom will soon be here.
translation_verse: Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ, nɩɩ fan kan wu atɔ nɩmʋ a maa waa mʋ, fan bɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ ki tiri tɔ.

english_verse: Then the Son of Man will be seen, coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ bee biti ba wu mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa maa sʋ ɔlʋn yɛ bʋnyaa lala n lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ n ba.

english_verse: Strange things will happen to the sun, moon, and stars. The nations on earth will be afraid of the roaring sea and tides, and they won't know what to do.

english_verse: People will be so frightened that they will faint because of what is happening to the world. Every power in the sky will be shaken.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba maa kɩɩ ba gyoo kanɩn atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ waa falɛ mʋ, kufuu ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ asa ba kirin‐kirin, kpalɩ fɛɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ kpini ii biti i gyingyen lɩɩ ɩmʋ ipula.

english_verse: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all of this takes place.
translation_verse: “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin fɛɛ, ndaga kakyɩna nɩmʋ tɔ asa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu lʋwɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ atɔ nɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa.

english_verse: like a trap. This day will surprise everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Kpalɩ fɛɛ ii biti i purigi asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kpini.

english_verse: putting out leaves, you know that summer will soon come.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fan wu ɩ maa pɔrɔgɛ mʋ, fen nyi fɛɛ kɩkyɛ ki tiri tɔ.

english_verse: The sky and the earth won't last forever, but my words will. A Warning
translation_verse: Sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ kpini ii biti ɩ kyʋn, amaa mɛ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ bɩrɛ, ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyʋn kpaa.

english_verse: Don't spend all of your time thinking about eating or drinking or worrying about life. If you do, the final day will suddenly catch you

english_verse: Watch out and keep praying that you can escape all that is going to happen and that the Son of Man will be pleased with you.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa kʋmaa fan baa fan da sʋ, kɛ fɛn kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kɛ ɩ lɛɛ fanɛ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba falɛ, kɛ fan taalɛ yɩlɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mu ansi tɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus taught in the temple each day, and he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
translation_verse: Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ Yesu ɩɩ kaapʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kalaatɩpwɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ mɔɔ ee kpee ɛɛ dɛ Olifu kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Everyone got up early and came to the temple to hear him teach.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nyenyenfuu kʋmaa nɩmʋ mɔɔ asa mʋ bee kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ bee nu mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: They were very pleased and offered to pay Judas some money.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ ɔkɔn, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ bee biti ba sa mu afulee.

english_verse: I tell you I will not eat another Passover meal until it is finally eaten in God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, n man bɩla yɛ n gyii falɛ agyitɔ anɩmʋ, naa fʋʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ agyitɔ nɩmʋ kigyii kasɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ ɩ waa Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were looking for a way to get rid of Jesus, because they were afraid of what the people might do.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ nbara akaapʋpʋ bee biti ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kɩtaa Yesu mɔɔ kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man wu, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba sɩlɛ sakyɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus told them, “As you go into the city, you will meet a man carrying a jar of water. Follow him into the house
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fan maa dan fen loo kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ mʋ, fen biti fan gyaŋa ɔnyɩn kʋ maa sʋ kolobi yɛ nkyu. Fan buu mʋ loo kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ ee loo mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Jesus got up from praying and went over to his disciples. They were asleep and worn out from being so sad.
translation_verse: Maa kʋsʋ mʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ tɔ mʋ, o bwii naa tu mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba maa dɛ nsɩɩrɛ lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkpɩnɛ yɛ kɔkɔlɔ nyita sʋ.

english_verse: Judas went to talk with the chief priests and the officers of the temple police about how he could help them arrest Jesus.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyudasi kpee aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa kɩkpaara akunpu bɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba naa taa nfɛɛrɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ Yesu sa.

english_verse: He agreed and started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus when the crowds were not around. Jesus Eats with His Disciples (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o kyula nɩɩ ee biti ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa lɛɛ Yesu sa bamʋ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ ba man nyɛ wu. Yesu asunpu ba lɔŋɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ agyitɔ (Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he told the apostles, “Take this wine and share it with each other.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu taa kɔɔpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan kɔɔlɛ kɩnɩmʋ barɩgɛ tɔ sa abaa.

english_verse: The one who will betray me is here at the table with me!
translation_verse: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ mɛ sa mʋ, maa mʋ abaa ɩ bʋ agyitɔ nɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: You will eat and drink with me in my kingdom, and you will each sit on a throne to judge the twelve tribes of Israel. Jesus' Disciples Will Be Tested (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n kan be gyii kuwura mʋ, n biti n yɛgɛ maa fanɛ an gyii, kɛ a nuu teeburi kʋlʋn sʋ, kɛ fan kyɩna nwuragya sʋ kɛ fan talɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ.” Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ Pita ii biti o be kina mʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: The day had come for the Festival of Thin Bread, and it was time to kill the Passover lambs.
translation_verse: Kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bee gyii bamʋ Bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ ba man taa kɩfɛ tinsa waa tɔ, yɛ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kɩmɔɔ fʋʋ.

english_verse: So Jesus said to Peter and John, “Go and prepare the Passover meal for us to eat.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu sun Pita maa Gyɔn nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan naa lɔŋɔ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an biti a taa gyii Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ agyitɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: But they asked, “Where do you want us to prepare it?”

translation_verse: Amaa ba lɔŋɔ kuusi tɔ kenken fɛɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ mʋ ba da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ kekuusi mʋ i gyii sʋ.

english_verse: He freed the man who was in jail for rioting and murder, because he was the one the crowd wanted to be set free. Then Pilate handed Jesus over for them to do what they wanted with him. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ lɛɛ sa bamʋ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sa bamʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Barabasi mʋ nɩɩ ba tii mʋ obu lɩɩ mʋ kɩtɔwɛ kʋsaa asa nkɔlɔ nɩɩ ba kɔ yɛ kɩbanɛ, yɛ mʋ ɔsa kɩmɔɔ sʋ mʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ɔ taa Yesu sa bamʋ ba naa waa bamʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti. Ba da Yesu oyu laasa sʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: If this can happen when the wood is green, what do you think will happen when it is dry?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ waa oyu bunboli mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba waa iyu wʋlɛsa mʋ?”

english_verse: Around noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until the middle of the afternoon.
translation_verse: Ɩ kyɩna ɩ gyɛ kyɔwɛ amu nsana tɔ nɩn, nɩɩ kitentenbiri kɩ tɩyɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kpini sʋ naa fʋʋ kakyʋwapɛ kerefi asá saŋa,

english_verse: Two criminals were led out to be put to death with Jesus.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taa asun libi wuya ana anyɔ tii Yesu sʋ ba naa mɔɔ.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Father, forgive these people! They don't know what they're doing.” While the crowd stood there watching Jesus, the soldiers gambled for his clothes. The leaders insulted him by saying, “He saved others. Now he should save himself, if he really is God's chosen Messiah!”
translation_verse: Asa mʋ ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ ba kɩɩ mʋ halɩɩ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa mʋ bɛɛ waa mʋ yɛrɛyɛrɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔ mɔlɩgɛ akʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kan ɔ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ, ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ.”

english_verse: The soldiers made fun of Jesus and brought him some wine.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ gbaa ba ba ŋmasɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba sa mʋ nta nyaŋɛsa.

english_verse: They said, “If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ Gyiwu abi owura kesintin mʋ, mɔlɩgɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.”

english_verse: But the other criminal told the first one off, “Don't you fear God? Aren't you getting the same punishment as this man?
translation_verse: Amaa ɔbanban mʋ pʋntɛ mʋ sʋ yɛ, “Fʋ man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anɛ kpini an bʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ kʋlʋn tɔ falɛ?

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise that today you will be with me in paradise.” The Death of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin tɔ yɛ, ndaga kakɛ nɩmʋ fu biti fʋ kyɩna maa mɛ‐Sɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ Paradise tɔ.” Yesu kelewu (Matiwu

english_verse: The sun stopped shining, and the curtain in the temple split down the middle.
translation_verse: lɩɩ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ yɛgɛ kɩda. Nɩɩ kɩtaa kunsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ barɩgɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, kɩ kyatɛ tɔ kɩnyɔ.

english_verse: Then they went to prepare some sweet-smelling spices for his burial. But on the Sabbath they rested, as the Law of Moses commands.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kpewu naa kʋʋla atɔ liifensa yɛ ituulale mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ba sʋswa Yesu kibuni mʋ. Amaa ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ ba fʋʋtɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kakɛ maŋa.

english_verse: A crowd had gathered to see the terrible sight. Then after they had seen it, they felt brokenhearted and went home.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba ba gyaŋɛ tʋtɔ bɛɛ kɩɩ mʋ, ba maa wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa mʋ, bamʋ kpini ba taa nkɔlɔ nyita sawʋ kpewu.

english_verse: All of Jesus' close friends and the women who had come with him from Galilee stood at a distance and watched. Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi Yesu mʋ, yɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ lɩɩ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ ba mʋ, ba yɩlɛ kata gbɛrɛɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa maŋa kpini. Yesu kipula (Matiwu

english_verse: Joseph went to Pilate and asked for Jesus' body.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o kpee Paleti asɛ naa kʋlɛ Yesu kibuni mʋ.

english_verse: He took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in fine cloth. Then he put it in a tomb that had been cut out of solid rock and had never been used.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ saatɛ Yesu kibuni mʋ lɩɩ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kɩtaa fufuli maalɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ naa pula kakyan mʋ nɩɩ ba baaŋɛ kɩfʋlɛ tɔ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta pula ɔkʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: It was Friday, and the Sabbath was about to begin.
translation_verse: Ɩ kyɩna ɩ gyɛ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kʋʋla bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ba gyoo kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ.

english_verse: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and watched how Jesus' body was placed in the tomb.
translation_verse: Akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu lɩɩ Galile mʋ, ba buu Gyosefu nɩɩ ba naa wu kakyan mʋ, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa Yesu kibuni mʋ pula mʋ.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and some other women were the ones who had gone to the tomb. When they returned, they told the eleven apostles and the others what had happened.
translation_verse: Akyɩɩ maŋa ɩ gyɛ Mɛɛrɩ Magidalen, Gyowana, Gyemisi mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ yɛ akyɩɩ akʋ, ɩ tɔwɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sa abɩɩlapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: When they found the stone rolled away from the entrance,
translation_verse: Ba naa wu fɛɛ kubuu mʋ nɩɩ ba taa tii kakyan mʋ, kɩ maa gbiliti lɩɩ kɩmʋ kɔnɔ sʋ.

english_verse: they went in. But they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus,
translation_verse: Amaa ba maa loo kakyan mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba man wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kubuni mʋ.

english_verse: The women were afraid and bowed to the ground. But the men said, “Why are you looking in the place of the dead for someone who is alive?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa akyɩɩ mʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba nyɔn bamʋ amu yii tɔ. Nɩɩ anyɩn anyɔ mʋ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan baa fen biti nyɩmɩsa kɩɩsa lɩɩ alewupu tɔ.”

english_verse: ‘The Son of Man will be handed over to sinners who will nail him to a cross. But three days later he will rise to life.’ ”
translation_verse: “Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba lɛɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ waa asa libi abaa tɔ, kɛ ba da mɛ oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ, kɛ nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, n kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ.”

english_verse: That same day two of Jesus' disciples were going to the village of Emmaus, which was about eleven kilometers from Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ ba kyɩna bee kpee Emawusi kadɛ tɔ. Lɩɩ Gyerusalem kpee tʋtɔ gyɛ ɩmaalɩ asunɔ.

english_verse: Jesus asked them, “What were you talking about as you walked along?” The two of them stood there looking sad and gloomy.
translation_verse: Ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ kiligyima nɩɩ fan naa fen biri kanɩn?” Nɩɩ ba sii yɩlɛ nyaŋɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ.

english_verse: We had hoped that he would be the one to set Israel free! But it has already been three days since all this happened.
translation_verse: Amaa naafɔɔ an kyɩna an sʋ tamaa fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba ɔ ba kɔɔlɛ anɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛgɛ. Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɩlaa ki tii sʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ndaga ɩ gyɛ kakɛ sasapʋ nɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ a maa waa.

english_verse: Some women in our group surprised us. They had gone to the tomb early in the morning,
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɩla i tii sʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, anɛ akyɩɩ mʋ akʋ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ i kyinkyin anɛ. Ba kpee kakyan mʋ asɛ naa loo to nyenyenfuu nɩmʋ,

english_verse: Some men from our group went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said. But they didn't see Jesus either.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anɛ tɔ akʋ ba kpee kakyan mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba naa wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ akyɩɩ mʋ ba ba tɔwɛ mʋ, amaa ba man wu mʋ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the two disciples, “Why can't you understand? How can you be so slow to believe all that the prophets said?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ kimelensi ndee? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ i du swɩɩ‐swɩɩ kanɩn, nɩɩ fan mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ba wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ gyii bilen?

english_verse: Didn't you know that the Messiah would have to suffer before he was given his glory?”
translation_verse: Yɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ o gyii falɛ awʋrʋfɔ pɔyɩ kɛ o loo mʋ bʋnyaa tɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus then explained everything written about himself in the Scriptures, beginning with the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu maa piili lɩɩ Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kpini ɩ tɔwɛ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ kaapʋ bamʋ.

english_verse: They begged him, “Stay with us! It's already late, and the sun is going down.” So Jesus went into the house to stay with them.
translation_verse: Amaa ba kʋlɛ mʋ gaa tɔwɛ yɛ, “Dɛ anɛ asɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa kyʋn, kyɔwɛ lʋwɛ.” Ɩmʋ sʋ o loo naa kyɩna bamʋ asɛ.

english_verse: After Jesus sat down to eat, he took some bread. He blessed it and broke it. Then he gave it to them.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba kyɩna kɛ ba gyii mʋ, ɔ taa bodobodo nɩɩ ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ taa sa bamʋ.

english_verse: At once they knew who he was, but he disappeared.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i bugi nɩɩ ba bɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyaa bamʋ ansi sʋ.

english_verse: They said to each other, “When he talked with us along the road and explained the Scriptures to us, didn't it warm our hearts?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anaa mʋ an naa ɔkpa tɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa anɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ anɛ mʋ, anɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ man dii anɛ?”

english_verse: So they got up and returned to Jerusalem. The two disciples found the eleven apostles and the others gathered together.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ bwii kpee Gyerusalem bilen. Tʋtɔ maŋa nɩɩ ba naa wu kudu ɔkʋ mʋ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ bamʋ asɛ mʋ ba maa gyaŋɛ,

english_verse: And they learned from the group that the Lord was really alive and had appeared to Peter.
translation_verse: nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩn yɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ kyiŋi nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ Simon.”

english_verse: Then the disciples from Emmaus told what happened on the road and how they knew he was the Lord when he broke the bread. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa anyɔ mʋ gbaa ba tɔwɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa ɔkpa tɔ yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩɩ Yesu saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kpɛɛ bodobodo mʋ tɔ mʋ. Yesu lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: While Jesus' disciples were talking about what had happened, Jesus appeared and greeted them.
translation_verse: Ba maa san ba yɩlɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ Yesu gbaa‐gbaa ba yɩlɛ bamʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Keyuula kɩ ba fanɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: They were frightened and terrified because they thought they were seeing a ghost.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nkɔlɔ n ŋmaa bamʋ nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba wu obuni nɩn.

english_verse: Look at my hands and my feet and see who I am! Touch me and find out for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones as you see I have.”
translation_verse: Fan kɩɩ mɛ abaa yɛ mɛ ayaa. Man gyɛ mɛ nɩn abɛɛ? Fan mata mɛ kɛ fan kɩɩ, obuni man sʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ yɛ awuya, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ falɛ?”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
translation_verse: Maa tɔwɛ falɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ taa mʋ abaa yɛ mʋ ayaa kaapʋ bamʋ.

english_verse: The disciples were so glad and amazed that they could not believe it. Jesus then asked them, “Do you have something to eat?”
translation_verse: Ba maa man pɩɩta kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii lɩɩ kensi‐gyii yɛ adangana nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ “Fan sʋ agyitɔ kʋ nfɩɩ fan gyii?”

english_verse: They gave him a piece of broiled fish.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taa ɔkɩn ŋasɩsa kɩdɩyɛ sa mʋ,

english_verse: He took it and ate it as they watched.
translation_verse: nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ kɩmʋ wɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “While I was still with you, I told you that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Books of the Prophets, and in the Psalms had to happen.”
translation_verse: Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kyɩna n bʋ fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n tɔwɛ fanɛ asɩn nɩmʋ yɛ, asɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla lɩɩ mɛ sʋ, Mosesi nbara tɔ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ kɩkaapʋ yɛ Ɩlʋn mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɩ maa ba tɔ.”

english_verse: Then he helped them understand the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o bugi bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ kɛ ba nyɛ nu Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: He told them: The Scriptures say that the Messiah must suffer, then three days later he will rise from death.
translation_verse: Ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Asɩn mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ ii biti o gyii awʋrʋfɔ kɛ o wu, kɛ o kyiŋi lɩɩ alewupu tɔ kakɛ sasapʋ.

english_verse: They also say that all people of every nation must be told in my name to turn to God, in order to be forgiven. So beginning in Jerusalem,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa asa kpini mʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ, kɛ asa ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ kɛ ɔ taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba piili asɩn nɩmʋ kɩtɔwɛ lɩɩ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: you must tell everything that has happened.
translation_verse: Fanɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin agyipu lɩɩ atɔ nɩmʋ kpini sʋ.

english_verse: I will send you the one my Father has promised, but you must stay in the city until you are given power from heaven. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Mark
translation_verse: N biti n bɩya fanɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ mɛ‐Sɛ kyɩna da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa fanɛ mʋ. Amaa fan kyɩna kadɛ lala nɩmʋ tɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ ɔlʋn mʋ ii biti ɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba fanɛ sʋ.” Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn kukuri Yesu kpee sʋsʋ (Maki

english_verse: Jesus led his disciples out to Bethany, where he raised his hands and blessed them.
translation_verse: Yesu maa taa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ lɩɩ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ bee kpee naa tiri Betani kadɛ tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ abaa sʋsʋ nɩɩ, ɔ waa bamʋ kusee.

english_verse: As he was doing this, he left and was taken up to heaven.
translation_verse: Maa dan san ɛɛ waa bamʋ kusee maŋa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ kpee sʋsʋ.

english_verse: After his disciples had worshiped him, they returned to Jerusalem and were very happy.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba muŋa sun mʋ nɩɩ ba taa kensi‐gyii lala bwii kpee Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: They spent their time in the temple, praising God.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kyaga sʋ kyɩna kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: From the very beginning the Word was with God.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ koo npiili asɛ mʋ, Kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ maa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ bʋ tɔ.

english_verse: received its life from him, and his life gave light to everyone.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ Kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ tɔ nɩɩ nkpa ɩ bʋ, nɩɩ nkpa maŋa ɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ tɔ ɩɩ sa asa kpini.

english_verse: The light keeps shining in the dark, and darkness has never put it out.
translation_verse: Kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ ɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ kitentenbiri tɔ, nɩɩ kitentenbiri mʋ man taalɛ dunla kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ kakaakʋ.

english_verse: who came to tell about the light and to lead all people to have faith.
translation_verse: Gyɔn ba nɩn ɔ ba tɔwɛ kesintin asɩn lɩɩ kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ sʋ sa asa, kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ nyɛ nu kɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii.

english_verse: The true light that shines on everyone was coming into the world.
translation_verse: Kesintin kaŋmaŋɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ sa asa kpini mʋ, kɩɩ ba dulinyaa tɔ.

english_verse: The Word was in the world, but no one knew him, though God had made the world with his Word.
translation_verse: Dulinya nɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ wʋla ɔ bʋ. Nɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ, nkɛɛ dulinyaa asa ba man bɩɩ mʋ.

english_verse: He came into his own world, but his own nation did not welcome him.
translation_verse: Ɔ ba mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, amaa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa asa ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ.

english_verse: They were not God's children by nature or because of any human desires. God himself was the one who made them his children.
translation_verse: Ba man biliŋi mu‐bii ana bɔla nyɩmɩsa kakʋʋgɛ sʋ, abɛɛ nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ biti sʋ, abɛɛ ɔnyɩn kebiti sʋ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba be biliŋi mu‐bii ana.

english_verse: The Word became a human being and lived here with us. We saw his true glory, the glory of the only Son of the Father. From him the complete gifts of undeserved grace and truth have come down to us.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kɔnɔ tɔ Asɩn mʋ ba biliŋi nyɩmɩsa, nɩɩ ɔ ba kyɩna anɛ asɛ kasɛ sʋ. An wu mʋ bʋnyaa kparɛ, mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii kʋlʋn kpen mʋ. Nɩɩ kabwaala yɛ kesintin kɩ bɔla mʋ.

english_verse: Because of all that the Son is, we have been given one blessing after another.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ mʋ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bɔla mʋ tɔ gaa sʋ mʋ, anɛ kpini an nyɛ kusee kʋkyɔ‐kʋkyɔ.

english_verse: No one has ever seen God. The only Son, who is truly God and is closest to the Father, has shown us what God is like. John the Baptist Tells about Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man pɩɩta wu Ɩbwaarɛ kakaakʋ, amɔɔ mu‐bii kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mʋ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ, ɩ lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kedubi kaapʋ. Gyɔn Okyugyeepu Tɔwɛ Asɩn Lɩɩ Yesu Sʋ (Matiwu

english_verse: The religious authorities in Jerusalem sent priests and temple helpers to ask John who he was. He told them plainly, “I am not the Messiah.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyɔn man kina kɩtɔwɛ, amaa ɔ tɔwɛ bugi tɔ yɛ, “Man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Then when they asked him if he were Elijah, he said, “No, I am not!” And when they asked if he were the Prophet, he also said “No!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kanɩn bɩrɛ, anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fʋ? Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Eligya mʋ?” Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Ayee! Man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Eligya.” Nɩɩ ba bɩla taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ an kɩɩ mʋ ɔkpa mʋ?” Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ayee.”

english_verse: Finally, they said, “Who are you then? We have to give an answer to the ones who sent us. Tell us who you are!”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ ba bɩla taasɛ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ bɩrɛ ɩ gyɛ fʋ? Lɛɛ kɔnɔ sa anɛ, kɛ a nyɛ asɩn kʋ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sun anɛ mʋ. Ɔsa mɔɔ bɩrɛ gbaa ɩ gyɛ fʋ?”

english_verse: John answered in the words of the prophet Isaiah, “I am only someone shouting in the desert, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord!’ ”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyɔn lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii kuusi ofuli sʋ yɛ, ‘Fan kyiigi ɔkpa mʋ sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ.’ Falɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya kyʋrɔɔ yɩla Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ tɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees had also been sent to John.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Farasii abi akʋ ba sun nbɔɔ Gyɔn asɛ,

english_verse: They asked him, “Why are you baptizing people, if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?”
translation_verse: ba naa taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ maa man gyɛ Krisito mʋ, abɛɛ Eligya, abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fii gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee?”

english_verse: John told them, “I use water to baptize people. But here with you is someone you don't know.
translation_verse: Gyɔn lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N sʋ nkyu nɩn n gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee. Amaa ɔkʋ yɩlɛ fanɛ tɔ nɩɩ fan man nyi mʋ.

english_verse: Even though I came first, I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mɛ kamaa tɔ ɛɛ ba mʋ. Amaa n man dagaa gbaa n saŋɛ mʋ asɩbɩta ɩfɛ.”

english_verse: John said this as he was baptizing east of the Jordan River in Bethany. The Lamb of God
translation_verse: Asɩn nɩmʋ kpini ɩ waa Betani kadɛ tɔ nɩn. Betani bʋ Gyɔdan ɔbʋn benbe kyɔwɛ ɔlɩɩkpa, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn ii gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ. Yesu, Ɩbwaarɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ

english_verse: The next day, John saw Jesus coming toward him and said: Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ Gyɔn maa wu Yesu maa naa ɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ, ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabʋlʋpʋ, mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɛɛ saraga kɛ asa kpini ba nyɛ alibi kɩtaakɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: He is the one I told you about when I said, “Someone else will come, who is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.”
translation_verse: Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ n wʋla n tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ mɛ kamaa ɛɛ ba, nɩɩ ɔ kyɔ mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ wʋla ɔ bʋ tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mɛ.’

english_verse: I didn't know who he was. But I came to baptize you with water, so that everyone in Israel would see him.
translation_verse: Mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ bɩrɛ n man n daa n nyi mʋ, amaa kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mɛ yɛ n ba gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, kɛ n ba nyɛ lɛɛ mʋ ifuli kaapʋ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ.”

english_verse: I was there and saw the Spirit come down on him like a dove from heaven. And the Spirit stayed on him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyɔn gyii ɩmʋ kesintin tɔwɛ yɛ, “N wu Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ maa kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ kuwura‐lɔpɔ nɩɩ kɩ ba kyɩna sii mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Before this I didn't know who he was. But the one who sent me to baptize with water had told me, “You will see the Spirit come down and stay on someone. Then you will know that he is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Naafɔɔ ngbaa mɛ n man n wʋla n nyi mʋ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ yɛ n ba gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, ɩ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔmʋ nɩɩ fʋ kan wu mɛ Kayaayu kɩ maa kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kyɩna sii mʋ sʋ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa mɛ Kayaayu mʋ gyee asa.’

english_verse: I saw this happen, and I tell you that he is the Son of God. The First Disciples of Jesus
translation_verse: N wu ɩnɩmʋ kpini nɩɩ n gyii ɩmʋ kesintin yɛ ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ.” Yesu Abɩɩlapʋ Gyankpaasa

english_verse: The next day, John was there again, and two of his followers were with him.
translation_verse: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, Gyɔn maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ ba bɩla ba yɩlɛ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: When he saw Jesus walking by, he said, “Here is the Lamb of God!”
translation_verse: Maa wu Yesu maa naa ɛɛ kyʋn mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ndee!”

english_verse: John's two followers heard him, and they went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Gyɔn abɩɩlapʋ anyɔ mʋ ba maa nu maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba buu Yesu.

english_verse: When Jesus turned and saw them, he asked, “What do you want?” They answered, “Rabbi, where do you live?” The Hebrew word “Rabbi” means “Teacher.”
translation_verse: Yesu maa biliŋi kɩɩ mʋ, o wu ba maa buu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fen biti?” Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Rabi, fɩnɛ nɩɩ fʋ tɛ?” .

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Come and see!” It was already about four o'clock in the afternoon when they went with him and saw where he lived. So they stayed on for the rest of the day.
translation_verse: Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan ba kɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɛ.” Ɩmʋ sʋ ba buu mʋ naa wu katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ. Saŋa maŋa tɔ gyɛ kalaatɩpwɛ sʋ kerefi ana tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyɩna mʋ asɛ kakɛ maŋa.

english_verse: One of the two men who had heard John and had gone with Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Asa anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ, nɩɩ ba buu Yesu mʋ, bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ Simon Pita mu‐supu Andiruwu.

english_verse: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother and tell him, “We have found the Messiah!” The Hebrew word “Messiah” means the same as the Greek word “Christ.”
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Andiruwu gyankpaa waa ɩ gyɛ yɛ, o biti mʋ‐daa Simon nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “An wu Mesaya mʋ.” Mesaya mʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ yɛ ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ asa.

english_verse: Andrew brought his brother to Jesus. And when Jesus saw him, he said, “Simon son of John, you will be called Cephas.” This name can be translated as “Peter.” Jesus Chooses Philip and Nathanael
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Andiruwu taa mʋ‐daa Simon kpee Yesu asɛ. Yesu kɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “N nyi fɛɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ Simon, Gyɔn mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa mʋ. Amaa bee biti ba tɩɩ fʋ yɛ Kefasi” Yesu Tɩɩ Filipu Maa Nataneli

english_verse: Philip then found Nathanael and said, “We have found the one that Moses and the Prophets wrote about. He is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Filipu wu Nataneli nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “An wu ɔmʋ nɩɩ Mosesi wʋla kyʋrɔɔ mʋ asɩn waa Nbara ɩwʋlɛ tɔ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ gbaa ba kyʋrɔɔ mʋ asɩn yɩla mʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ Yesu, Gyosefu nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Nasareti kadɛ tɔ mʋ mu‐bii.”

english_verse: Nathanael asked, “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” Philip answered, “Come and see.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Nataneli taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Amɔɔ, kʋtɔ danbɩrasa kʋ ɩ taalɛ lɩɩ Nasareti kadɛ tɔ?” Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Filipu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ba kɩɩ.”

english_verse: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said, “Here is a true descendant of our ancestor Israel. And he isn't deceitful.”
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu Nataneli maa naa ɛɛ ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kesintin tɔ, Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔnyɩn gbaa‐gbaa ndee, nɩɩ antɩŋɛsa kʋ man bʋ mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, “Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Nataneli taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Nɛnɛ nɩɩ fʋ waa fii nyi mɛ?” Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N wu fʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ figi onyin tɔ, pɔyɩ nɩɩ Filipu tɩɩ fʋ mʋ.”

english_verse: Nathanael said, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God and the King of Israel!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Nataneli tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, fʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ! Nɩɩ fʋ ɩ bɩla fʋ gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi Owura mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Did you believe me just because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see something even greater.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “N maa tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, n wu fʋ fʋ maa bʋ figi onyin tɔ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii? Fii biti fʋ ba wu atɔ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ ɩnɩmʋ gbaa.”

english_verse: I tell you for certain you will see heaven open and God's angels going up and coming down on the Son of Man.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu bwii kyaga sʋ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, fanɛ kpini fen biti fan wu sʋsʋ maa bugi tɔ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ba ba bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa sʋ.”

english_verse: Three days later Mary, the mother of Jesus, was at a wedding feast in the village of Cana in Galilee.
translation_verse: Nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, ba kpɩɩ ɔkyɩɩfɔ Kena kadɛ tɔ Galile Ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Yesu mu‐nyi bʋ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited and were there.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba da kɩbaa tɔ tɩɩ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ gbaa kɩkyɩɩfɔ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: When the wine was all gone, Mary said to Jesus, “They don't have any more wine.”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ nta ɩ pwagɛ bamʋ mʋ, Yesu mu‐nyi tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Bamʋ nta ɩ lʋwɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Mother, my time hasn't yet come! You must not tell me what to do.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔkyɩɩ, man tɔwɛ mɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n waa. Mɛ saŋa man pɩɩ fʋʋ.”

english_verse: Mary then said to the servants, “Do whatever Jesus tells you to do.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mu‐nyi tɔwɛ nta ayɛpʋ mʋ yɛ, “Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ fan waa mʋ, fan waa.”

english_verse: At the feast there were six stone water jars that were used by the people for washing themselves in the way that their religion said they must. Each jar held about
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nkyu afʋlɛ alan asiye ɩ yɩlɛ tʋtɔ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ waa Gyiwu abi anaana‐tɔ ineesi kɩfwɩɩ. Nɩɩ kʋfʋlɛ kɩlan kʋmaa kɩ taalɛ gyii ɩgalɔn adʋnyɔ abɛɛ adɩsa.

english_verse: Jesus told the servants to fill them to the top with water. Then after the jars had been filled,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ nta ayɛpʋ mʋ yɛ, “Fan saa nkyu bɔla alan mʋ kpini.” Ɩmʋ sʋ ba saa nkyu bɔla alan mʋ kpini bɩlɩbɩlɩ.

english_verse: he said, “Now take some water and give it to the man in charge of the feast.” The servants did as Jesus told them,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ ba mʋ yɛ, “Fan nyɛ ɩmʋ ɩkʋ naa sa nta ayɛpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ.” Nɩɩ ba waa kanɩn.

english_verse: and the man in charge drank some of the water that had now turned into wine. He did not know where the wine had come from, but the servants did. He called the bridegroom over
translation_verse: Nta ayɛpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ maa daa nuu nkyu mʋ nɩɩ i biliŋi nta mʋ kɩɩ mʋ, o wu fɛɛ ɩ bʋrɔn. Ɔ man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ, amaa ayɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba saa nkyu mʋ bɩrɛ be nyi. Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ.

english_verse: and said, “The best wine is always served first. Then after the guests have had plenty, the other wine is served. But you have kept the best until last!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩ daa kɩlɛɛ nta danbɩrasa pɔyɩ. Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nuu bʋʋ mʋ, kɛ ɔ lan bɩya nta mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man bʋrɔn mʋ. Amaa fʋ taa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn yɩla ba fʋʋ saŋa nɩmʋ.”

english_verse: Not long before the Jewish festival of Passover, Jesus went to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ kɩ san gbɛrɛɛ kɩ fʋʋ mʋ, Yesu kpee Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: So he took some rope and made a whip. Then he chased everyone out of the temple, together with their sheep and cattle. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and scattered their coins.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔfɛ waa kʋkyʋkyʋʋ gyan bamaa bamʋ ɩnaatɛ yɛ nbʋlʋpʋ lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa opula. Nɩɩ ɔ yasɛ bamʋ afulee abi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ bala bamʋ iteeburu bun.

english_verse: The disciples then remembered that the Scriptures say, “My love for your house burns in me like a fire.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba nyiŋi Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kebiti mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ sa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ kii gyii mi kumu.”

english_verse: “Destroy this temple,” Jesus answered, “and in three days I will build it again!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ kɩŋasan tɔ yɛ “Fan boyi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ nɩmʋ, kɛ n taa nkɛ nsa kʋsaa kɩmʋ yii.”

english_verse: There he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves in the temple. He also saw moneychangers sitting at their tables.
translation_verse: Maa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa opula mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ o wu anyɩn akʋ ba maa fɛ ɩnaatɛ, yɛ nbʋlʋpʋ yɛ awura‐lɔpɔ yɛgɛ akʋ mɔɔ ba tɛ iteeburu asɛ bɛɛ kyɛrɛ afulee.

english_verse: Jesus said to the people who had been selling doves, “Get those doves out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ fɛ awura‐lɔpɔ mʋ yɛ, “Fan lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ nfɩɩ. Fan man taa Mɛ‐Sɛ ayɛ biliŋi kɩgya ogyikpa.”

english_verse: The Jewish leaders asked Jesus, “What miracle will you work to show us why you have done this?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩɩ fɩɩ taalɛ waa kaapʋ anɛ, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fʋ sʋ ɔkpa fʋ waa ɩnɩmʋ?”

english_verse: And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus. Jesus Knows What People Are Like
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ, mʋ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba nyiŋi yɛ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ bamʋ ɩnɩmʋ. Nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ yɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ mʋ gyii.

english_verse: In Jerusalem during Passover many people put their faith in Jesus, because they saw him work miracles.
translation_verse: Asɩn kyinkyinsa kʋkyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bee gyii bamʋ Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ, asa gaalaagaa ba wu nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

english_verse: But Jesus knew what was in their hearts, and he would not let them have power over him.
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu bɩrɛ, ɔ man taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ bamʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ o nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ asa kpini ba du.

english_verse: There was a man named Nicodemus who was a Pharisee and a Jewish leader.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn kʋ lɩɩ Farasii kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Nikodemusi, ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ nɩn.

english_verse: One night he went to Jesus and said, “Rabbi, we know that God has sent you to teach us. You could not work these miracles, unless God were with you.”
translation_verse: Mʋ ɩ ba Yesu asɛ kagyanbwɛ kʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, an nyi yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ i sun fʋ yɛ fʋ ba kaapʋ anɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa falɛ, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ tii mʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɩɩ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ wu kɛ ɔ bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ, amɔɔ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba bwii kʋʋgɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin yɛ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ ɔ taalɛ loo Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ, amɔɔ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee maa Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kigyee, kɛ ɔ nyɛ kakʋʋgɛ pʋpwɛ.

english_verse: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
translation_verse: Nyɩmɩsa kʋʋgɛ nyɩmɩsa fɛɛ mu keduli nɩn, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩ kʋʋgɛ nyɩmɩsa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ keduli nɩn.

english_verse: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
translation_verse: Man yɛgɛ i kyinkyin fʋ fɛɛ, n maa tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ ɩ dagaa ba bwii kʋʋgɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

english_verse: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
translation_verse: Afuu ɩɩ da ii kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ii biti. Fii nu ɩmʋ sʋ, amaa fʋ man nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ yɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ii kpee. Kanɩn nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ tɔ mʋ du.”

english_verse: “How can this be?” Nicodemus asked.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Nikodemusi taasɛ yɛ, “Nɛnɛ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ waa?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N nyi fʋ maa gyɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔkaapʋpʋ kparɛ, nɩɩ fʋ man nu ɩnɩmʋ kasɛ?

english_verse: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fʋ kesintin yɛ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ an nyi mʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ an wu mʋ, nɩɩ an lɛɛ an tɔwɛ. Amaa fan mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ anɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa fen gyii.

english_verse: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
translation_verse: N maa tɔwɛ fanɛ kasɛ sʋ asɩn nɩɩ fan maa kɔɔlɛ gyii falɛ mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ fan taalɛ kɔɔlɛ sʋsʋ asɩn gyii?

english_verse: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋ man pɩɩta lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ kpee sʋsʋ, amɔɔ mɛ, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ nɩɩ n lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
translation_verse: kɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ ɔ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

english_verse: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
translation_verse: “Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ kebiti sa dulinyaa tɔ asa gaa sʋ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ mɛ, Mu‐Bii kʋlʋn kpen mʋ sa, fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, mɛɛ yɛ ɔ fɔyɩ, amaa ee biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

english_verse: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man sun mɛ, Mu‐Bii mʋ ba dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ, yɛ n ba bun kɩmʋ tɔ asa kʋpwɛ, amaa o sun mɛ nɩn yɛ n ba waa bamʋ ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ.

english_verse: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ o bun mʋ kʋpwɛ, amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla bun mʋ kʋpwɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ Mu‐Bii kʋlʋn kpen mʋ gyii.

english_verse: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
translation_verse: Kanan mʋ nɩɩ kʋpwɛ kubun mʋ kɩ ba mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ ɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn ba dulinyaa tɔ, amaa asa bee biti kitentenbiri mʋ ba kyɔ ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ asunsunsa ɩ gyɛ alibi nɩn.

english_verse: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa alibi mʋ ii kisi ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ ba ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ tɔ, kɛ mu alibi ɩ ba lɩɩ ifuli.

english_verse: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ ɔkpa mʋ bɩrɛ, ɛɛ ba ɩŋmaŋɛsa mʋ tɔ, kɛ asa ba nyɛ wu fɛɛ mʋ atɔ waasa mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti.” Yesu maa Gyɔn Okyugyeepu

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ Yesu maa mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem kpee Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Ɔ kyɩna tʋtɔ nkɛ nyɔ kʋ maa bamʋ, nɩɩ o gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩmɔɔrɛ ɩ tɩyɛ Gyɔn abuupu yɛ Gyiwu nyɩmɩsa kʋ nsana lɩɩ anaana tɔ ineesi kɩfwɩɩ sʋ.

english_verse: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyɔn abɩɩlapʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ an wu mʋ Gyɔdan ɔbʋn benbe mʋ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ fu gyii mʋ asɩn kesintin mʋ gbaa ii gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, yɛgɛ asa kpini bee kpee mʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyɔn lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man kan taa kʋtɔ kʋ sa ɔkʋ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ nyɛ kɩmʋ.

english_verse: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
translation_verse: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fen biti fan gyii mɛ kesintin fɛɛ, n wʋla tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, ‘Man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.’ Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ sun mɛ nɩn yɛ n gyankpaa ba lɔŋɔ ɔkpa yɩla mʋ.”

english_verse: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɔ kɩŋasan yɛ, “Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli. Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mu‐kuli mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ bɩrɛ ɛɛ yɩlɛ ee gyoo nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔ kan nu mʋ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ, mu ansi i gyii. Kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ mi ansi i gyii gaa.

english_verse: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
translation_verse: Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ kɩ ba ki dii ki tii sʋ, kɛ mɛ Gyɔn kɩtɩɩ mɔɔ kɩ ba kasɛ.

english_verse: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
translation_verse: “Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba mʋ kyɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ. Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ mʋ gyɛ kasɛ nɩmʋ sʋ nyɩmɩsa nɩn, nɩɩ kasɛ nɩmʋ sʋ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ kyɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

english_verse: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ o wu yɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ o nu mʋ, ɩmʋ kesintin nɩɩ ee gyii mʋ, nkɛɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ kesintin kigyii mʋ ee gyii.

english_verse: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ gyii mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ kesintin wuya.

english_verse: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
translation_verse: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sa mʋ Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa.

english_verse: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ii biti Mu‐Bii kʋlʋn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kʋtɔ kʋmaa waa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ.

english_verse: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Mu‐Bii mʋ gyii mʋ sʋ nkpa kakpaa. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man nu sa Mu‐Bii mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ mʋ ɩ dɛ mʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: But Jesus' disciples were really the ones doing the baptizing, and not Jesus himself. Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
translation_verse: Kesintin tɔ bɩrɛ, Yesu gbaa‐gbaa man gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, amɔɔ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ nkʋn ii gyee asa.

english_verse: Jesus left Judea and started for Galilee again.
translation_verse: Yesu maa bɩɩ kanɩn mʋ, ɔ kʋsʋ lɩɩ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ bwii kpee Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: This time he had to go through Samaria,
translation_verse: Maa kpee mʋ ɩ dagaa maa bɔla lɩɩ Samariya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ lɛɛ Yesu kɔnɔ yɛ, “Fʋ gyɛ Gyiwu obii nɩn, nɩɩ mɛɛ mɛ n gyɛ Samariya obii nɩn. Mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ kʋlɛ mɛ nkyu kɛ fu nuu?” Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ kanɩn lɩɩ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi yɛ Samariya abi ba mɛɛ bʋga lapɛ kʋlʋn.

english_verse: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ taasɛ yɛ, “Ɔbɩlɩsa, fʋ man sʋ kuuka, nɩɩ kitiribo mʋ mɔɔ bʋ nyɛ. Fɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ nyɛ falɛ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa nkpa mʋ sa mɛ?

english_verse: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
translation_verse: Anɛ naana Gyekobu ɩ sa anɛ kitiribo maŋa. Mʋ gbaa‐gbaa maa mu‐bii ana yɛ mʋ atɔbwaaya kpini kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba nuu. Fɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fʋ kyɔ anɛ naana Gyekobu nɩn abɛɛ?”

english_verse: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nuu nkyu mʋ nɩɩ n biti n sa mʋ mʋ, bʋlɩfɔ man bɩla yɛ ɔ kɩtaa mʋ. Kanɩn nkyu maŋa ii biti i biliŋi fɛɛ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kyʋn mʋ tɔ, kɛ kɩɩ sa mʋ nkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa.”

english_verse: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔbɩlɩsa, n kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, sa mɛ kanɩn nkyu maŋa kɛ bʋlɩfɔ ɔ man bɩla kɩtaa mɛ, kɛ n man bɩla n baa n sa nkyu nfɩɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Bwii kpee naa tɩɩ fu‐kuli kɛ fan ba.”

english_verse: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
translation_verse: Fʋ wʋla kili anyɩn anu nɩɩ fʋ yɛgɛ, nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ faa mʋ fan tɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ falɛ gbaa, man gyɛ fu‐kuli. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ falɛ gyɛ kesintin nɩn.”

english_verse: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔbɩlɩsa n wu fɛɛ fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn.

english_verse: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
translation_verse: Anɛ naana ana Samariya abi bɩrɛ kɩbɩɩ nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba daa bɛɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ, amaa fanɛ Gyiwu abi ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ Gyerusalem ɩ gyɛ opula mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a sun Ɩbwaarɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkyɩɩ, kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii fɛɛ saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, nɩɩ man gyɛ kɩbɩɩ kɩnɩmʋ sʋ abɛɛ Gyerusalem nɩɩ asa bee biti ba sun Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

english_verse: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
translation_verse: Fanɛ Samariya abi ɩ man nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ fen sun danbɩrasa, amaa anɛ Gyiwu abi bɩrɛ an nyi Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ an sun. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ kɩ bɔla Gyiwu abi sʋ nɩn ba.

english_verse: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
translation_verse: Saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba, nɩɩ kɩ tɛɛ fʋʋ lʋwɛ, nɩɩ kesintin asunpu bee biti ba sun Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ yɛ kesintin tɔ. Kanɩn asunpu banɩmʋ falɛ nɩɩ Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ii biti.

english_verse: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ kayaayu nɩn, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bee sun mʋ mʋ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba sun mʋ Kayaayu yɛ kesintin tɔ.”

english_verse: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔwɛ Yesu yɛ, “N nyi fɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Krisito mʋ, ee biti ba. Nɩɩ ɔ kan ba mʋ, ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tɔ kaapʋ anɛ.”

english_verse: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fʋ asɛ falɛ, mɛ ɩ gyɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba bwii lɩɩ kadɛ tɔ ba. Nɩɩ ba maa wu Yesu maa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ ba maa tɛɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, i kyinkyin bamʋ. Amaa bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ man taalɛ taasɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ fii biti?” Abɛɛ ba taasɛ Yesu yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ asɛ?”

english_verse: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ, ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛgɛ mʋ kɔlɔ‐bi yɩla sɩlɛ kpee kadɛ tɔ naa tɔwɛ asa yɛ,

english_verse: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
translation_verse: “Fan ba kɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa tɔwɛ mɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ n waa mʋ kpini. Abɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Krisito, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ?”

english_verse: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ asa mʋ ba lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ bee kpee ba naa kɩɩ Yesu.

english_verse: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
translation_verse: Saŋa maa tɔ nɩɩ mʋ abɩɩlapʋ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔkaapʋpʋ, waa aniya kɛ fu gyii agyitɔ.”

english_verse: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “N sʋ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ n gyii, yɛgɛ fan man nyi ɩmʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ abɩɩlapʋ mʋ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Abɛɛ ɔsa kʋ wʋla bɩya mʋ agyitɔ nɩn nɩɩ o gyii?”

english_verse: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɛ agyitɔ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ n maa waa Mɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ mʋ kebiti, kɛ n waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa mɛ mʋ lʋwɛ.

english_verse: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
translation_verse: Man gyɛ fanɛ kɩŋasan ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɩ san ɩbwaayɩ ɩna kɛ a tɩŋɛ ayu mʋ?’ Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fan bugi fanɛ ansi kɩɩ adɔɔ mʋ, fen biti fan wu fɛɛ ɩ payɩ fʋʋ kɩtɩŋɛ.

english_verse: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
translation_verse: Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩŋɛ mʋ ɩɩ nyɛ mʋ kʋkɔka danbɩrasa, nɩɩ ayu mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kʋʋla ɛɛ yɩlɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa ba nkpa kakpaa tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee duu yɛ ɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩŋɛ mʋ kpini bee biti ba bʋga gyii bamʋ ansi.

english_verse: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔkʋ ii duu nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩɩ kpa,’ ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

english_verse: I am sending you to harvest crops in fields where others have done all the hard work.
translation_verse: N sun fanɛ ndɔɔ tɔ yɛ fan naa tɩŋɛ ayu mʋ nɩɩ fan man duu. Akʋ ɩ waa ɩmʋ kusun nɩɩ fan ba nyɛ ɩmʋ kʋkɔka.” Samariya abi gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii

english_verse: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
translation_verse: Samariya abi gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n wʋla waa” mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Samariya abi mʋ ba ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nkɛ nnyɔ.

english_verse: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
translation_verse: Asa gaalaagaa ba ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii tii sʋ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba tɔwɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, an kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii, yɛgɛ man gyɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ anɛ mʋ sʋ, amaa kpalɩ fɛɛ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ an nu yɛ anɛ asʋ, nɩɩ an bɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ asa ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ.” Yesu kyɛ ɔbɩlɩsa kʋ mu‐bii

english_verse: Jesus had said, “Prophets are honored everywhere, except in their own country.” Then two days later he left
translation_verse: Yesu gbaa‐gbaa ɩ wʋla kyɩna tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ kʋmaa man sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ ayɛ.”

english_verse: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
translation_verse: Yesu maa naa fʋʋ Galile mʋ, Galile abi ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ abaa anyɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ngbaa bamʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kpee Gyerusalem naa gyii Kesu kɔnɔ Kanyaŋɛ nbunɔ mʋ, ba wu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa tʋtɔ mʋ.

english_verse: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
translation_verse: Yesu maa bʋ Galile ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, o bwii kpee Kena kadɛ tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna taa nkyu biliŋi nta mʋ. Maa bʋ tʋtɔ kanɩn mʋ, kɩbanɛ ɔbɩlɩsa kʋ mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa dɛ Kapanum kadɛ tɔ ɛɛ lɔ.

english_verse: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn mʋ maa nu fɛɛ Yesu lɩɩ Gyudeya ba Galile mʋ, ɔ ba mʋ asɛ ba kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ ba Kapanum ba kyɛ mu‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dɛ ee biti o wu mʋ sa mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii, amɔɔ fen wu asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ adangana.”

english_verse: The man replied, “Lord, please come before my son dies!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩbanɛ bɩlɩsa mʋ kʋlɛ Yesu yɛ, “Ɔbɩlɩsa, yɛgɛ a kpee mi kebii mʋ asɛ pɔyɩ kɛ o wu.”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, “Natɛ, fu kebii mʋ mɛɛ yɛ o wu.” Ɔnyɩn mʋ kɔɔlɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ gyii, nɩɩ ɔ kɩtaa ɔkpa kyʋn.

english_verse: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
translation_verse: Maa kpewu mʋ, mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba ba gyaŋa mʋ ɔkpa tɔ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, mu‐bii mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kebii mʋ nyɛ alanfɩya. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ndee kyʋwapɛ kerefi kʋlʋn saŋa mʋ, nɩɩ kibirima mʋ yɛgɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ bɩɩ yɛ kanɩn saŋa maa kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fu‐kebii mʋ mɛɛ yɛ o wu” mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii.

english_verse: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Yesu maa lɩɩ Gyudeya ba Galile mʋ, ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa nyɔsapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa.

english_verse: Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Yesu kpee Gyerusalem naa gyii Gyiwu abi Kesu kɔnɔ ngyaŋɛ kʋ.

english_verse: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
translation_verse: Kɩpatɛ kʋ kɩ bʋ Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri kebunɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ nbʋlʋpʋ kebunɔ mʋ. Bɛɛ tɩɩ kapatɩyɛ maa Hibriwu kadɛ tɔ yɛ Betesada. Nɩɩ agyan anu ɩ dɛ kyaabɔɔ kɩmʋ.

english_verse: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ ɛɛ lɔ nsu adɩsa yɛ aburuwa.

english_verse: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
translation_verse: nɩɩ mʋ asɩn mʋ a man bʋ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fan man kɔɔlɛ mɛ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ o sun mʋ gyii.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
translation_verse: Yesu maa wu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ dɛ tʋtɔ lɔ kyɛɛrɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fii biti fɛɛ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya?”

english_verse: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔlɔpʋ mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔbɩlɩsa, nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ kan lupu nkyu mʋ tɔ mʋ, n man sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mɛ loo kapatɩyɛ mʋ tɔ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ n loo to mʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩ daa gyankpaa mɛ loo to.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ taa fʋ kalan kɛ fʋ natɛ.”

english_verse: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya, nɩɩ o kukuri mʋ kalan natɛ. Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa gyɛ Gyiwu abi kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ nɩn.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba tɔwɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya mʋ yɛ, “Ndaga gyɛ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ. Anɛ nbara mʋ ɩ mɛɛ sa ɔkpa yɛ fʋ sʋla fʋ kalan.”

english_verse: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
translation_verse: Amaa ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ mɛ mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, ‘Taa fʋ kalan kɛ fʋ natɛ.’ ”

english_verse: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn ɔnyɩn maŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ fʋ taa fʋ kalan kɛ fʋ natɛ?”

english_verse: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ man nyi kanɩn ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ mʋ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa ba kyɔ nɩɩ Yesu loo asa damantɛ mʋ tɔ kyʋn.

english_verse: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
translation_verse: Kamaa tɔ mʋ Yesu wu mʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kɩɩ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ fʋ nyɛ alanfɩya, ɩmʋ sʋ yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn, ɩmʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba fʋ sʋ mʋ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ kyɔ ɩnɩmʋ.”

english_verse: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩn mʋ lɩɩ tʋtɔ naa tɔwɛ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ mʋ mʋ. Yesu ɩ gyɛ Nkpa kumu

english_verse: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ sʋ mʋ, Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba piili bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɛ‐Sɛ ii sun kusun saŋa kʋmaa, ɩmʋ sʋ ngbaa mɛ n sun kusun.”

english_verse: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
