translation_verse: Nkyu nɩɩ Gyɔn sʋ ee gyee fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, amaa nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba falɛ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ gyee fanɛ.” Yesu sʋsʋ kikpe

english_verse: While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, “Lord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asunpu mʋ ba maa Yesu ba bwii sarɛ mʋ, ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩɩ fii biti fu bwii taa kuwura‐gyii mʋ sa anɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi abɛɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You don't need to know the time of those events that only the Father controls.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Saŋa abɛɛ kakɛ yɛ mʋ awaasa mʋ kpini mʋ, mɛ‐Sɛ ɩ yɩla ɩmʋ lɩɩ mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ sʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ man tiri fɛɛ fan bɩɩ saŋa mʋ.

english_verse: But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.”
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kan ba fanɛ sʋ mʋ, fen biti fan nyɛ ɔlʋn kɛ fan naa tɔwɛ mɛ asɩn mʋ Gyerusalem tɔ yɛ Gyudeya kpini yɛ Samariya tɔ, taa naa fʋʋ dulinyaa ɔkaa.”

english_verse: After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him,
translation_verse: Maa tɔwɛ ɩ nɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kpee sʋsʋ bamʋ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ a bun mʋ sʋ lɩɩ bamʋ ansi sʋ.

english_verse: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ mʋ ii kpee yɛgɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ sʋsʋ haa mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ anyɩn anyɔ akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bun atɔ fufuli ba ba yɩlɛ bamʋ asɛ,

english_verse: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
translation_verse: nɩɩ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Galile abi, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fan yɩlɛ nfɩɩ fɛn kɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ kanɩn? Yesu mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ kpee sʋsʋ mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ee biti o bwii ba fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan kɩɩ mʋ maa kpee sʋsʋ falɛ.” Asunpu yɛ Akyɩɩ akʋ ba gyaŋɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ

english_verse: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
translation_verse: Ba maa loo Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba dii naa loo abansʋrʋ obu, mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla ba bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ. Asunpu mʋ ɩ gyɛ Pita maa Gyɔn, yɛ Gyemisi yɛ Andiruwu yɛ Filipu yɛ Tomasi yɛ Batolome yɛ Matiwu yɛ Alifeyo mu‐bii Gyemisi maa Simon Seloti mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔ bee kun bamʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ bamʋ ɔkʋ, yɛ Gyemisi mu‐bii Gyudasi.

english_verse: The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers.
translation_verse: Bamʋ kpini ba taalɛ ba bʋga saŋa kʋmaa yɛ akyɩɩ akʋ yɛ Yesu mu‐nyi yɛ mu‐supu ana. Yesu mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ yɛ Yesu mu‐supu ana.

english_verse: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
translation_verse: “Afulee mʋ nɩɩ Gyudasi nyɛ lɩɩ mu alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa sʋ mʋ, ɔ sɔɔ kasɛ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩyɛ ketulaamu nɩɩ o wu, ɔ patɩyɛ nɩɩ mʋ ipuni kpini ɩ patɩyɛ lɩɩ.

english_verse: When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Gyerusalem abi kpini ba nu asɩn nɩmʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba yɩla ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ bamʋ katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ Akeledama, kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ‘Nkalan kasɛ.’ ”

english_verse: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ owura Dawidi wʋla kyʋrɔɔ Gyudasi asɩn waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, “Mʋ kɩkpaara ki biliŋi kɩtakpan, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man kyɩna kɩmʋ tɔ.” Nɩɩ Dawidi bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ba yɛgɛ kɛ ɔkʋ o yii mu kusun kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ ayaa tɔ.”

english_verse: So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
translation_verse: lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn okyugyeepu gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa Yesu kpee sʋsʋ. Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ kanɩn asa maŋa tɔ ɔkʋ maa tii anɛ sʋ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kelewu kyiŋi kesintin ogyipu.

english_verse: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ asa anyɔ yɛ ba yɩlɛ. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Gyosefu mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Basabasi, nɩɩ ba bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Gyusitu, nɩɩ ɔbanban mʋ mɔɔ gyɛ Matiyasi.

english_verse: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fu nyi ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fii biti fʋ kaapʋ anɛ asa anyɔ anɩmʋ tɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ.

english_verse: to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.”
translation_verse: Kɛ ɔ ba waa fu asunpu mʋ kusun mʋ nɩɩ Gyudasi yɛgɛ yɩla mʋ, kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ mʋ.”

english_verse: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ nbii biti ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba tii bamʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Matiyasi gyii, nɩɩ ba taa mʋ tii bamʋ asunpu kudu‐ɔkʋ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi Pentekosi kakɛ gyisa maa fʋʋ mʋ, Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ kpini ba ba sarɛ opula kʋlʋn.

english_verse: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
translation_verse: Opula tɔ nɩɩ ɩlawʋ ɩkʋ ɩ lɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ a maa kpira fɛɛ afuu bʋlʋnsa akʋ a maa da ba loo kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ kpini.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɔla bamʋ kpini tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ yii kasɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ katɔwɛ pʋpwɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ kɩ sa bamʋ yɛ ba tɔwɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ Gyiwu abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ be sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, ba lɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ ɩsʋwʋlɛ kpini sʋ ba baa ba bʋ Gyerusalem tʋtɔ.

english_verse: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
translation_verse: Ba maa kanan ɩlawʋ maŋa mʋ, nɩɩ kʋbʋ damantɛ kʋkʋ kɩ sarɛ. Ɩ waa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩyan, kpalɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ kpini ba maa tɔwɛ asa mʋ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kadɛ tɔwɛsa.

english_verse: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
translation_verse: Ɩ waa bamʋ kanankʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, “Asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ falɛ mʋ, ba man gyɛ Galile abi nɩn abɛɛ?

english_verse: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
translation_verse: Ɩ waa nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ anɛ kpini an nu ba maa tɔwɛ anɛ ntɔwɛ nusa?

english_verse: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
translation_verse: Anɛ kpini an lɩɩ‐lɩɩ ndɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ fɛɛ, Patiya yɛ Medeya yɛ Elam yɛ Mesopotamiya yɛ Gyudeya yɛ Kapadosiya yɛ Pontisi yɛ Asiya yɛ

english_verse: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
translation_verse: Anɛ akʋ an lɩɩ Kereti nɩɩ anɛ akʋ mɔɔ an lɩɩ Arabiki. Anɛ kpini an nu asa anɩmʋ ntɔwɛ tɔ ba maa tɔwɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa.”

english_verse: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
translation_verse: Ɩ maa waa bamʋ kɩyan nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, nɩɩ bɛ taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ kasɛ ɩɩ kaapʋ?”

english_verse: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Amaa yɛgɛ akʋ mɔɔ be keesi akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Asa maŋa ba bʋʋ nɩn.” Pita lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kasɛ sa sakyɔ mʋ

english_verse: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Pita maa asunpu kudu‐ɔkʋ bambam mʋ, ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ Pita diyaa mʋ ɔbʋlɛ sʋsʋ kenken ɔ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa kʋbʋ mʋ yɛ, “Mɛ tɔmaa ana Gyiwu abi yɛ ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ Gyerusalem mʋ kpini, fan kan fanɛ asʋ kɛ n lɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ kasɛ sa fanɛ.

english_verse: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
translation_verse: Asa anɩmʋ ba man bʋʋ nɩn, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan fɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ falɛ, ɩ dan san ɩ gyɛ kerefi akpanɔ nyanyan nɩmʋ.

english_verse: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
translation_verse: Amaa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ ba falɛ ɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Gyoweli wʋla tɔwɛ ɩmʋ asɩn yɛ,

english_verse: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
translation_verse: ‘Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ nkɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, n biti n yɛgɛ mɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɔ ba asa kpini sʋ. N biti n bɔla fanɛ nbii‐nyɩnsa yɛ akyɩɩsa sʋ kɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn. Fanɛ ayaafɔlɛ bee biti be wu ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ, kɛ fanɛ anyɩn bɩlɩsa bɛ kɔɔ idee.

english_verse: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
translation_verse: Kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ n biti n yɛgɛ mɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɔ sa fanɛ ayaafɔlɛ yɛ abite kɛ ba tɔwɛ mɛ asɩn.

english_verse: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
translation_verse: N biti n waa asɩn kyinkyinsa sʋsʋ, kɛ n waa ɩsɩnkaala kasɛ sʋ. Nkalan yɛ fʋlɔn yɛ idiisii ii biti ɩ waa kudu‐kudu.

english_verse: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
translation_verse: Kyɔwɛ ii biti o biliŋi kitentenbiri, kɛ ɔbwaayɩ ɔ payɩ fɛɛ nkalan, pɔyɩ kɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kakɛ bʋnyaa lala mʋ kɩ ba.

english_verse: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ mʋ, ee biti ɔ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ.’

english_verse: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
translation_verse: “Mɛ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi fan bɩla nu asɩn nɩmʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Kanasareti‐nyɩn yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ ɩsɩnkaala yɛ adangana nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ tɔ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ i sun mʋ. Nɩɩ fanɛ kpini fen nyi ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa falɛ.

english_verse: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o nyi kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ kyula, nɩɩ o ŋmina yɩla fɛɛ bee biti ba lɛɛ Yesu sa fanɛ, nɩɩ ɩ ba kanɩn. Fan yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa libi ba da Yesu anyenbi mata oyu laasa sʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ, lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ lewu awʋrʋfɔ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ lewu man taalɛ tii mʋ yɩla.

english_verse: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
translation_verse: Dawidi wʋla tɔwɛ Yesu n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn yɛ, ‘Saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ, n wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mi ansi sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ o tii mɛ sʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ sɛhn mɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ ki fuuli mɛ, nɩɩ kensi‐gyii kɩ bɔla mɛ atɔwɛsa tɔ, nɩɩ halɩɩ n wu gbaa mʋ, n sʋ tamaa.

english_verse: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ yɛgɛ mɛ kala o sii kamandɛ, nɩɩ fʋ mɛɛ yɛ fʋ yɛgɛ fʋ Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ ɔ gyɔɔ nyita.

english_verse: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
translation_verse: Fʋ kaapʋ mɛ nkpa ɔkpa, nɩɩ fʋ bʋ mɛ asɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ n nyɛ kensi‐gyii gaa.’

english_verse: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
translation_verse: “Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fɛn yɛgɛ n lɛɛ anɛ naana Dawidi nɩmʋ asɩn kaapʋ fanɛ. Dawidi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ gbaa wu nɩn, nɩɩ ba pula mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ kakyan kɛ san kɩ dɛ anɛ asɛ.

english_verse: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔ dan ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ nɩn, nɩɩ o nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔ ntan da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ kɩnaana tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ ba kyɩna mʋ Dawidi kuwura‐gya sʋ.

english_verse: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
translation_verse: Dawidi wʋla wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa, kanɩn sʋ ɔ tɔwɛ Krisito kiwu kyiŋi mʋ asɩn yɛ, ‘Ɔ man yɛgɛ nɩɩ o sii abuni tɔ, nɩɩ mu kibuni man gyɔɔ kakyan tɔ.’

english_verse: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
translation_verse: Yesu nɩmʋ asɩn nɩɩ Dawidi tɔwɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Anɛ kpini ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ kesintin agyipu.

english_verse: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ diyaa mʋ yɩla mʋ gyisa sʋ, sa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ mʋ. Ɩmʋ nɩɩ fen nu, nɩɩ fen wu falɛ ɩ gyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ.

english_verse: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
translation_verse: Dawidi gbaa‐gbaa man kpee sʋsʋ, amaa ɔ tɔwɛ fɛɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ tɔwɛ sa mɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ, “Kyɩna mɛ gyisa sʋ,

english_verse: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
translation_verse: amɔɔ n taa fu akisipu kpini waa fʋ ayaa kasɛ.” ’

english_verse: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: “Fanɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini, fan nu danbɩrasa fɛɛ, Yesu nɩmʋ, mʋ nɩɩ fan da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ waa mʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ yɛ Krisito nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ.” Asa ngbɩn nsa ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii

english_verse: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba nu ɩnɩmʋ kanɩn mʋ, i loo bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ Pita maa asunpu bambam mʋ yɛ, “Anɛ‐daa ana, mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa?”

english_verse: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, kɛ ba gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ alibi kɩtaakɛɛ, kɛ fan nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ mʋ Kayaayu mʋ.

english_verse: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa fanaa fanɛ‐bii ana mʋ, yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kata‐kata mʋ kpini, bamʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ tɩɩ ba mʋ asɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita gyii bamʋ kesintin bwii tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ gaalaagaa waa bamʋ aniya tɔ yɛ, “Fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ ndaga asa libi nɩmʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini bɛ bɩɩla bɛɛ lɩɩ asunpu mʋ asɛ, ba sʋ kakyɛmɩnɛ ɔkɔn yɛ abaa, nɩɩ bee gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ agyitɔ, nɩɩ bɛɛ bʋga bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
translation_verse: Asunpu mʋ ba waa ɩsɩnkaala yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa gaalaagaa, nɩɩ asɩn mʋ ɩ waa ɔkʋmaa anɩmʋ kɩyan.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
translation_verse: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ kpini bɛɛ bʋga katɩn kʋlʋn, nɩɩ ba sʋ kɔnɔ kʋlʋn, yɛgɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ gyɛ bamʋ kpini lɛɛ.

english_verse: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ fɛ bamʋ npatɩyɛ yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ kpini, kɛ ba barɩgɛ afulee mʋ tɔ sa abaa kanan kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ i tiri ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

english_verse: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
translation_verse: Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ bɛɛ naa sarɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kasɔrɔga tɔ fɛɛ kɩbʋlɛ kʋlʋn, bɛɛ bʋga nɩn bee gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ agyitɔ bamʋ akpaara sʋ, nɩɩ bɛɛ bʋga bee gyii bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ agyitɔ kensi‐gyii yɛ nkɔlɔ fuuli,

english_verse: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
translation_verse: yɛgɛ bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ awaasa kpini a gyii asa ansi. Nɩɩ kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ, ee tii bamʋ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ ɩ waa fɛɛ kakyʋwapɛ kerefi asa nɩɩ Pita maa Gyɔn bee kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa tɔ ba naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

english_verse: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
translation_verse: Kewusunɔ Danbɩrasa, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba naa tu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ yɛgɛ ɔ gyɛ boobi maa tɛ. Kakɛ kʋmaa bɛɛ sʋla mʋ bee kpee naa yɩla tʋtɔ yɛgɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ afulee.

english_verse: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
translation_verse: Maa wu Pita maa Gyɔn ba maa biti ba loo tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kʋlɛ bamʋ yɛ ba sa mu afulee.

english_verse: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita maa Gyɔn ba kɩɩ mʋ kyimm, nɩɩ Pita yɛ, “Diyaa ansi kɩɩ anɛ danbɩrasa.”

english_verse: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ kɩɩ bamʋ kyimm, nɩɩ ɔ waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ nyɛ kʋtɔ kʋ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

english_verse: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
translation_verse: Amaa Pita tɔwɛ mʋ nɩn yɛ, “Man sʋ afulee kpini‐kpini mɛ asɛ, amaa n biti n sa fʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ. Lɩɩ Yesu Krisito Kanasareti‐nyɩn kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ n yɛ fʋ, kʋsʋ natɛ.”

english_verse: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Pita kɩtaa mʋ gyisa kɩbaa tɔ kyɛ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ ayaa yɛ mu amulii ɩ waa ɔlʋn,

english_verse: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
translation_verse: nɩɩ o fugi yii mʋ ayaa sʋ, o piili ɔ naa. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba loo obu mʋ kasɔrɔga tɔ, ɔ naa ee fugi yɛgɛ ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Everyone saw him walking around and praising God.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asa mʋ kpini ba wu mʋ maa naa ɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ,

english_verse: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
translation_verse: nɩɩ ba bɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ boobi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kewusunɔ Danbɩrasa ɛɛ kʋlɛ afulee mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan, nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ kanankʋ maa kʋsʋ ɔ naa mʋ. Pita abwaarɛsɩn katɔwɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ

english_verse: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ ba Pita maa Gyɔn asɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ waa asa mʋ kanankʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ kpee bamʋ asɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ yɛ Solomon lenbu mʋ.

english_verse: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
translation_verse: Pita maa wu asa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩsɩrayɩ abi, mɩnɛ ɩ yɛgɛ sʋ nɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ a waa fanɛ kanankʋ? Nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fan kɩɩ anɛ gbaa fɛɛ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ ɔlʋn yɛ anɛ kabwaarɛ‐sun ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ kʋsʋ ɔ naa falɛ?

english_verse: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
translation_verse: Ɩ gyɛ anɛ naana Abraham maa Isaki maa Gyekobu bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ anɛ naana ana kpini Ɩbwaarɛ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ Yesu bʋnyaa kparɛ. Mʋ maŋa nɩɩ fan taa mʋ sa agyankpaapʋ, nɩɩ fan kina mʋ Paleti ansi tɔ, yɛgɛ Paleti waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ.

english_verse: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
translation_verse: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋrɔn nɩɩ o du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ, mʋ, nɩɩ fan kina, nɩɩ fan kpalɩ ɩmʋ sʋ tɔwɛ Paleti yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ asa ɔmɔɔpʋ mʋ sa fanɛ.

english_verse: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
translation_verse: Fan taa Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ sa nkpa mʋ mɔɔ, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ ba nkpa tɔ. Anɛ ɩ gyɛ asɩn maŋa kesintin agyipu.

english_verse: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn boobi mʋ nɩɩ fen nyi nɩɩ fen wu mʋ falɛ nyɛ alanfɩya. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ maa kɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ gyii sʋ mʋ, ɔ kpaarɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen wu mʋ falɛ.

english_verse: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
translation_verse: “Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, nbɩɩnbɩɩ n bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ yɛ fanɛ agyankpaapʋ ba waa Yesu falɛ, ɩ lɩɩ fanɛ kɩman‐nyi sʋ.

english_verse: But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise.
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla bɔla mʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ kpini sʋ tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ fɛɛ, Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ ii biti o wu awʋrʋfɔ gaa, nɩɩ ɩ ba kanɩn mɔɔ kesintin.

english_verse: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa fanɛ kakyɩna tɔ kɛ fɛn buu Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ɔ taa fanɛ alibi kɛɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Fɛn kan waa kanɩn mʋ, kayɩɩ yuuli kakyɩna ii biti ɩ lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ asɛ, kɛ o sun Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ sa fanɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ ba fanɛ asɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
translation_verse: Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Yesu maa sii sʋsʋ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ki biliŋi kɩpʋpwɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla bɔla mʋ atɔwɛpʋ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ sʋ tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ koo dɩdaa mʋ.

english_verse: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Mosesi tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔtɔwɛpʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o sun mɛ falɛ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ nyɩmɩsa. Nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fɛn kan fanɛ asʋ, kɛ fen nu kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o mee nu kanɩn ɔtɔwɛpʋ maŋa asɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ mʋ asa tɔ kɛ o nyita mʋ.’

english_verse: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ maa bɔla mʋ atɔwɛpʋ sʋ ɛɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ mʋ kpini ɩ dɛ sa fanɛ. Kɛ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ fanaa Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ fanɛ naana ana ba waa mʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Abraham yɛ, ‘Lɩɩ fʋ kɩnaana sʋ mʋ, n biti n waa kasɛ sʋ asa kpini kusee.’

english_verse: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ Yesu nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa sun mʋ fanɛ asɛ, kɛ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ kɛ ɔ waa fanɛ kusee.”

english_verse: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
translation_verse: Pita maa Gyɔn ba san ba maa sʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ asa mʋ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu akʋ, yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ akunpu mʋ ɔbɩlɩsa yɛ, Sadusi abi ba ba fʋʋ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba nyɛ agbʋ waa Pita maa Gyɔn fɛɛ ba maa kaapʋ bɛ tɔwɛ asa mʋ yɛ Yesu bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ kelewu kyiŋi kɩ bʋ tɔ sa asa kpini.

english_verse: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kɩtaa Pita maa Gyɔn naa tii bamʋ obu naa fʋʋ kayɩkɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ maa tɛɛ lʋwɛ sʋ mʋ ba tii bamʋ obu naa fʋʋ kayɩkɛ.

english_verse: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
translation_verse: Amaa asa gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu Yesu Krisito asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, ba kɔɔlɛ gyii nɩɩ kʋbʋ mʋ kɩ nyɛ akyɔ bɛɛ waa fɛɛ anyɩn ngbɩn‐nnuu.

english_verse: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ Gyiwu abi agyankpaapʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa yɛ Mosesi nbara akaapʋpʋ ba naa sarɛ Gyerusalem.

english_verse: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ tʋtɔ ɩ gyɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ Anasi yɛ Kefasi yɛ Gyɔn yɛ Alekisanda yɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ, mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana.

english_verse: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
translation_verse: Ba taa Pita maa Gyɔn ba ba yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɔlʋn nɩɩ fan nyɛ abɛɛ anɩmʋ kɩtɩɩ nɩɩ fan sʋ fan waa ɩnɩmʋ falɛ?”

english_verse: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ, Pita mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ bɔla mʋ tɔ mʋ lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Agyankpaapʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa,

english_verse: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
translation_verse: fan taasɛ anɛ ndaga asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ an waa boobi nɩmʋ, nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fanɛ yɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini fan bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ nfɩɩ falɛ, Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Nasareti, ɔmʋ nɩɩ fan da mʋ mata oyu laasa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bwii kyiŋi mʋ ba nkpa tɔ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Kubuu mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ, apɔyɩpʋ fan kina yɛ kɩ man bʋrɔn mʋ, kanɩn kubuu maŋa ɩ ba biliŋi kubuu kparɛ ɩmʋ kpini tɔ.’

english_verse: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ mɔlɩgɛ asa, amɔɔ Yesu nkʋn, lɩɩ fɛɛ kɩtɩɩ pʋpwɛ kʋ man bɩla ɔ bʋ dulinyaa tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa sa yɛ kɩ mɔlɩgɛ anɛ.”

english_verse: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɩ waa abɩlɩsa mʋ kpini kanankʋ fɛɛ Pita maa Gyɔn ba maa yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ, nɩɩ ba bɩɩ fɛɛ ba man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi ɩwʋlɛ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba wu fɛɛ ba wʋla ba gyɛ Yesu abuupu nɩn.

english_verse: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
translation_verse: Kpalɩ fɛɛ ba maa wu boobi mʋ nɩɩ ba kyɛ mʋ, maa yɩlɛ Pita maa Gyɔn asɛ mʋ, ba man taalɛ nyɛ asɩn kʋ ba tɔwɛ.

english_verse: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba dan sii tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩn yɛ ba lɩɩ nsarɛ obu mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba sii ba taa Pita maa Gyɔn sʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

english_verse: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taasɛ abaa yɛ, “Nɛnɛ nɩɩ an biti a waa asa anɩmʋ? Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Gyerusalem nyi yɛ bamʋ ɩ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ a mɛɛ taalɛ gyii ɩ mʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ.

english_verse: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
translation_verse: Amaa pɔyɩ kɛ a tii asɩn nɩmʋ kɛ ɩ man naa lɩɩ asa asɛ kata‐kata mʋ, a da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ, ɩ nɩmʋ i gyii bamʋ ɔkaa, ba man bɩla taa Yesu kɩtɩɩ asɩn tɔwɛ sa asa.”

english_verse: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba bwii tɩɩ Pita maa Gyɔn ba lenbu mʋ tɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ kakaakʋ ba man bɩla lɩɩ tɔwɛ abɛɛ kaapʋ lɩɩ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ.

english_verse: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
translation_verse: Amaa Pita maa Gyɔn ba lɛɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ nɩn yɛ, “Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan kɩɩ damu maŋa tɔ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ. An nu fanɛ asɛ abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ?

english_verse: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ a mɛɛ taalɛ yɛgɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ gbaa‐gbaa anɛ an wu nɩɩ an nu mʋ kɩtɔwɛ.”

english_verse: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba waa asɩn kyinkyinsa kyɛ mʋ mʋ, gyii ɔ kyɔ nsu adɩna. Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ

english_verse: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba dan yɛgɛ Pita maa Gyɔn mʋ, ba bwii kpee bamʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ abɩlɩsa yɛ aseepu‐bɩlɩsa mʋ ba tɔwɛ.

english_verse: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba maa dan nu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ tʋʋ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ yɛ ɔpʋ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ ɩmʋ tɔ kpini.

english_verse: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
translation_verse: Fʋ bɔla Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ sʋ nɩɩ fʋ yɛgɛ anɛ naana Dawidi, fʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ ndɛndɛmandɛ bɛɛ nyɛ agbʋ? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ asa anɩmʋ bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ libi kanɩn?

english_verse: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
translation_verse: Kasɛ sʋ awura bɛɛ kʋʋla bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ, nɩɩ akɩɩsʋpʋ mɔɔ bɛɛ sarɛ bɛɛ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ sʋ.’

english_verse: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Kesintin, asɩn nɩmʋ a ba tɔ fɛɛ Herodi maa Pontiyu Paleti yɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ ba sarɛ kadɛ nɩmʋ tɔ, bee ŋmina agyina kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Yesu sʋ fʋ Kɩyaafɔlɛ Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
translation_verse: Kesintin ba sarɛ kanɩn nɩn ba ba waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ lɩɩ fʋ ɔlʋn sʋ, yɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti mʋ nɩɩ fʋ wʋla paa yɛ ɩ waa mʋ.

english_verse: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩɩ aŋaala mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ ŋaala anɛ falɛ kɛ fʋ sa anɛ, fʋ ayaafɔlɛ kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ kɛ a taalɛ tɔwɛ fʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

english_verse: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
translation_verse: Tɛɛŋɛ fʋ abaa kɛ alɔpʋ ba nyɛ alanfɩya, kɛ fʋ waa adangana yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa bɔla fʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ sʋ waa kaapʋ asa.”

english_verse: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, opula mʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ mʋ gyingyen. Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ loo bɔla bamʋ kpini nɩɩ ba piili bɛ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ yɛ kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ. Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu kakyɩna tɔ kɔnɔ kʋlʋn

english_verse: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
translation_verse: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu kʋbʋ mʋ kpini ba waa nfɛɛrɛ kʋlʋn yɛ kɔkɔlɔ kʋlʋn. Bamʋ ɔkʋ mɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ. Kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ gyɛ bamʋ kpini lɛɛ.

english_verse: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
translation_verse: Asunpu mʋ ba tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito kelewu kyiŋi mʋ asɩn ba kaapʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn lala mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa bamʋ kpini kusee.

english_verse: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
translation_verse: Ɔkʋ man bʋ bamʋ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋ ki tiri mʋ kpaa. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kasɛ abɛɛ akpaara mʋ, ba fɛ ɩmʋ yɛgɛ ba sʋ afulee mʋ ba ba,

english_verse: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
translation_verse: ba yɩla asunpu mʋ kagya kasɛ, nɩɩ ba barɩgɛ ɩmʋ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ i tiri ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ.

english_verse: Joseph was one of the followers who had sold a piece of property and brought the money to the apostles. He was a Levite from Cyprus, and the apostles called him Barnabas, which means “one who encourages others.”
translation_verse: Mʋ gbaa mʋ fɛ mʋ kasɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa afulee mʋ ba waa asunpu mʋ kagya kasɛ.

english_verse: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ananiyasi maa mʋ‐ka Safira ba fɛ bamʋ kasɛ kʋkʋ.

english_verse: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ananiyasi maa mʋ‐ka ba waa kɔnɔ nɩɩ ɔ barɩgɛ afulee mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ɩkʋ yɩla, nɩɩ ɔ taa asansa mʋ kpiya asunpu mʋ kagya kasɛ.

english_verse: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ananiyasi, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ i gyii fu kumu nɩɩ fʋ tɩn antɩŋɛsa sa Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kanɩn? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fʋ fɛ fʋ kasɛ nyɛ afulee nɩɩ fʋ barɩgɛ tɔ taa ɩkʋ yɩla?

english_verse: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
translation_verse: Pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ fɛ kasɛ maŋa mʋ, kɩ gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ nɩn, nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fʋ fɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, afulee mʋ gbaa a gyɛ fʋ lɛɛ nɩn. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ fʋ waa kanɩn asɩn maŋa? Man gyɛ asa nɩɩ fʋ tɩn antɩŋɛsa sa, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ fʋ tɩn antɩŋɛsa sa.”

english_verse: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
translation_verse: Ananiyasi maa dan nu kanɩn asɩn maŋa mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɩ tɩn mʋ tʋʋ nɩɩ o wu. Nɩɩ ɩ waa ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu kanɩn mʋ kufuu gaa.

english_verse: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ayaafɔlɛ ba loo bɔlɔɔ mʋ, kukuri lɩɩ naa pula.

english_verse: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
translation_verse: Ɩ maa waa gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, mʋ‐ka Safira gbaa loo ba ɔ man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa.

english_verse: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ, mʋ yɛ, “Tɔwɛ mɛ kesintin, kasɛ afulee mʋ nɩɩ faa fu‐kuli fan fɛ mʋ kpini ndee?” Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Oo, ɩmʋ afulee kpini nɩn.”

english_verse: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ faa fu‐kuli fan waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn yɛ fan tʋʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Kayaayu mʋ tɔ kɩɩ. Kɩɩ! Ayaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba naa pula fu‐kuli mʋ ba yɩlɛ kebunɔ tʋtɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ, kanɩn nɩɩ bee biti ba kukuri ngbaa fʋ lɩɩ naa pula nɩn.”

english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Opula tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ tɩn mʋ tʋʋ Pita ayaa tɔ nɩɩ o wu. Nɩɩ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ ba be loo, nɩɩ ba wu yɛ o wu ɔ dɛ, nɩɩ ba bɔlɔɔ mʋ kukuri lɩɩ naa pula mata mu‐kuli.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Krisito abi kʋbʋ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kufuu gaa. Krisito asunpu mʋ ba kyɛ asa gaalaagaa

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Asunpu mʋ ba waa ɩsɩnkaala yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa asa mu ansi tɔ. Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ kpini bɛɛ taalɛ bɛɛ gyaŋɛ Ɩbwaarɛ osunkpa ayɛ mʋ opula mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Solomon kasɔrɔga tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ bɛ sa bamʋ gaa, amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ ɔkʋ man taalɛ nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ naa tii bamʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Amaa kamaa tɔ mʋ, anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ gyii, nɩɩ ba naa tii bamʋ kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ, asa ba sʋla alɔpʋ ba yɩla agbenbi ɔkaa yɛgɛ ba dɛ bamʋ mpaa yɛ ɩkalan sʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kɛ Pita kan ɛɛ kyʋn mʋ, mʋ kayaayu kɩ natɛ bamʋ sʋ.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kyaabɔɔ Gyerusalem mʋ asa ba taa bamʋ alɔpʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi bɛɛ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ kpini ba, nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya. Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ ba nyanla Krisito asupu mʋ

english_verse: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ, yɛ mʋ kamaa abi mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Sadusi kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba nyɛ kayɩɩ basa lɩɩ asunpu mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: Ba kɩtaa asunpu mʋ naa tii katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ asa obu mʋ.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Amaa kanɩn kagyanbwɛ maŋa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ba tigi obu mʋ kebunɔ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ.

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan kpee naa yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ kɛ fan tɔwɛ asa mʋ, nkpa pʋpwɛ nɩmʋ asɩn.”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: Kayɩ kɩ maa kɛ mʋ Krisito asunpu mʋ ba kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ katɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba yii kasɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ asa mʋ nkpa mʋ asɩn. Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ maa mʋ kamaa abi, ba maa ba mʋ, ɔ tɩɩ Sanhediri mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ kpini ba ba gyaŋɛ, nɩɩ o sun yɛ ba naa lɛɛ asunpu mʋ ba.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Apurusi mʋ nɩɩ ba sun mʋ, ba maa fʋʋ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba man wu asunpu mʋ obu maŋa tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba bwii naa tɔwɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bee gyoo bamʋ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: “An wu obu mʋ maa biri sʋ danbɩrasa yɛgɛ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ ba yɩlɛ nbunɔ, amaa a maa tigi nbunɔ mʋ a man wu ɔkʋ obu mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ akɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ i kyinkyin bamʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩ ba kanɩn.

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkʋ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ! Anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan tii bamʋ obu mʋ bɛɛ yɩlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ kasɔrɔga tɔ bɛɛ kaapʋ asa.”

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ akɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba kpee naa bɩya asunpu mʋ. Ba maa sɩlɛ kufuu fɛɛ asa mʋ, bee biti ba tʋʋ bamʋ abuu su mʋ, ba man taa bamʋ yɛ ɔlʋn sʋ. Ɔbɩlɩsa Gamaliye kɩtɔwɛ waa

english_verse: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ ba yɩlɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: “Yɛ an da fanɛ amu tɔ yɛ, fan man bɩla taa Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ kaapʋ? Amaa fan taa fanɛ kɩkaapʋ kyaabɔɔ Gyerusalem kpini. Fen biti fan taa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ kelewu ba anɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita maa asunpu banban mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a maa nu Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔ nɩn, man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa lɛɛ.

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: Anɛ naana ana bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ i kyiŋi Yesu mʋ nɩɩ fan da mʋ oyu laasa mɔɔ mʋ, lɩɩ lewu tɔ.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ diyaa mʋ naa kyɩna mʋ gyisa sʋ fɛɛ Ɔgyankpaapʋ yɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o bugi ɔkpa sa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kɛ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ mʋ, yɛ anɛ asunpu mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ kesintin agyipu ɩnɩmʋ kpini sʋ.”

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbara abɩlɩsa kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ɩ nyɛ bamʋ agbʋ gaa nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba mɔɔ asunpu mʋ.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Amaa bamʋ tɔ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gamaliye, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Farasii obii yɛ Mosesi nbara ɔkaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ asa kpini bɛɛ sa mʋ bɛɛrɛɛ mʋ, kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ nsana nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, ba yɛgɛ asunpu mʋ ba lɩɩ kewu gbɛrɛɛ.

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩsɩrayɩ abi, fan lɔŋɔ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan taa lɩɩ anyɩn anɩmʋ sʋ danbɩrasa.

english_verse: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
translation_verse: Mʋ kamaa mʋ Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Galile mʋ, asa kɩkalɛ saŋa mʋ ngbaa mʋ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kʋbʋ lala kʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ. Amaa ba mɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ mu abuupu gbaa ba yaasɛ tɔ.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ndaga ɩ ba falɛ, n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fɛn yɛgɛ asa anɩmʋ ba natɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ bamʋ kusun kɩnɩmʋ kɩ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa lɛɛ bɩrɛ kii biti ki nyita.

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ bɩrɛ fɛn mɛɛ taalɛ tii asa anɩmʋ, nɩɩ ɩ waa kanɩn bɩrɛ fen biti fen wu fɛɛ fɛn kɔ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.”

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: Ba nu sa Gamaliye katɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɩɩ asunpu mʋ tɩŋɛ bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba da bamʋ amu tɔ yɛ, ba man bɩla tɔwɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ asɩn. Nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ yɛ ba natɛ.

english_verse: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
translation_verse: Asunpu mʋ ba lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ kpini abɩlɩsa mu ansi tɔ, taa kensi‐gyii fɛɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kalɛ bamʋ tii bamʋ nɩɩ ba wu awʋrʋfɔ lɩɩ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ba naa kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ nsɔrɔga tɔ yɛ asa akpaara sʋ. Ba man yɛgɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kɩtɔwɛ yɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu abuupu mʋ ba nyɛ akyɔ mʋ, kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii kɩ ba Gyiwu ndɛ‐bii mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Giriki mʋ yɛ Gyiwu ndɛ‐bii mʋ nsana. Ba fabɩlɛ yɛ Gyiwu ndɛ‐bii mʋ ba mɛɛ sa bamʋ akulapu‐kyɩɩ atɔ barɩgɛsa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa.

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, asunpu kudu‐anyɔ mʋ ba sarɛ Yesu abuupu mʋ kpini. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Ɩ man dagaa fɛɛ a yɛgɛ abwaarɛsɩn kɩtɔwɛ yɩla kɛ a ba kɩɩ atɔ kɩbarɩgɛ sʋ.

english_verse: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ anɛ‐daa ana yɛ anɛ‐supu ana, fan lɛɛ fanɛ tɔ anyɩn asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛ sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ kanyaasɩn nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ bɔla bamʋ tɔ mʋ, kɛ a taa kusun nɩmʋ waa bamʋ abaa tɔ,

english_verse: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
translation_verse: kɛ a nyɛ saŋa sa kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ abwaarɛsɩn kɩtɔwɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
translation_verse: Kanɩn nfɛɛrɛ mʋ ɩ pɩrɛ bamʋ kpini. Nɩɩ Yesu abuupu mʋ ba lɛɛ Sitefen mʋ nɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ kyɔ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ Filipi yɛ Pokoro yɛ Nikano yɛ Timon yɛ Pamena maa Nikolasi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Antiyoki, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ biliŋi Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ɔ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ.

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ kaapʋ asunpu mʋ, nɩɩ asunpu mʋ ba taa bamʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ ɩ yɛlɩgɛ. Yesu abuupu mʋ ba nyɛ akyɔ tii sʋ Gyerusalem ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu mʋ gaalaagaa gbaa ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii. Gyiwu abɩlɩsa ba kɩtaa Sitefen

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Sitefen mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bwaala mʋ mʋ, waa asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ asɩnkaala asa mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
translation_verse: Amaa Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Kirini yɛ Alekisanda kabwaarɛ kʋlɛkpa mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ yɛ ba kɔɔlɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Silisiya, Asiya ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ kpini ba gyii Sitefen ɩmɔɔrɛ.

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ mʋ kanyaasɩn yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ɔkpa katɔwɛ tɔ sʋ mʋ, ba man taalɛ yɩlɛ mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: Ba lɩɩ asiiri tɔ naa wu asa akʋ yɛ ba ba tɩn atɩntɩnsa gyaga Sitefen sʋ, kɛ ba ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “An nu Sitefen maa tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn ɛɛ gyaga Mosesi maa Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ amɔɔrɛ‐gyipu ba tɔwɛ fun waa abɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ Mosesi nbara akaapʋpʋ yɛ asa mʋ kpini nkɔlɔ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Sitefen ba Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Ba bɩya antɩŋɛsa wuya ana nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Saŋa kʋmaa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn ee yii kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛ Mosesi nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ mʋ.

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ an bɩla nu maa tɔwɛ yɛ Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ biti o boyi Kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ, kɛ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ anɛ anaana atɔ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ naana Mosesi taa sa anɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ asɩn mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba kɩɩ Sitefen kyimm, nɩɩ ba wu mu ansi tɔ ɩ maa du fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ansi tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ taasɛ Sitefen yɛ, “Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ lɩɩ fʋ sʋ falɛ kpini, ɩ gyɛ kesintin?”

english_verse: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
translation_verse: Sitefen lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mɛ‐Sɛ ana yɛ mɛ‐daa ana, fen nu mɛ asɛ! Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa wuya mʋ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ anɛ naana Abraham, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Mesopotamiya kasɛ sʋ, pɔyɩ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ ba Haran mʋ.

english_verse: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Kʋsʋ lɩɩ fʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ yɛ fʋ asa asɛ, kɛ fu kpee ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n kaapʋ fʋ falɛ sʋ.’

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ Abraham lɩɩ Kalidiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna Haran. Mʋ‐sɛ lewu kamaa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ naa kyɩna ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ndaga fan tɛ sʋ falɛ.

english_verse: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ man pɩɩta taa kasɛ mʋ ntɩn kʋ sa mʋ, halɩɩ kɩyaa tantan tɔ kasɛ gbaa man pɩɩta biliŋi mʋ lɛɛ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ ii biti o be biliŋi maa mʋ anaana‐bii lɛɛ nɩn. Yɛgɛ saŋa maa Abraham man pɩɩta kʋʋgɛ.

english_verse: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
translation_verse: Asɩn nɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ Abraham yɛ, ‘Fʋ anaana‐bii bee biti ba naa biliŋi afɔ ɔkʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, kɛ ba taa bamʋ biliŋi anyɛ kɛ ba nyanla bamʋ nsu alɩfa ana.

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: N biti n bɩɩtɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba biliŋaa bamʋ anyɛ mʋ asʋ, ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, fʋ anaana‐bii mʋ bee biti ba lɩɩ kanɩn ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kɛ ba be sun mɛ nfɩɩ’.

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ maa Abraham ba nyɛ katɔwɛ kyula fɛɛ, mʋ yɛ mʋ kamaa tɔ kpini ba tɩŋɛ iduu. Ɩmʋ sʋ Abraham maa kʋʋgɛ Isaki mʋ ɔ tɩn mʋ oduu kakɛ buruwasapu, nɩɩ Isaki kʋʋgɛ Gyekobu nɩɩ ɔ tɩn mʋ oduu, nɩɩ Gyekobu kʋʋgɛ anɛ naana ana kudu‐anyɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩŋɛ bamʋ kpini iduu.

english_verse: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
translation_verse: “Gyosefu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kudu anyɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ, mʋ daa ana ba nyɛ mʋ sʋ kayɩɩ basa, nɩɩ ba fɛ mʋ fɛɛ kɩnyɛ sa Egyipiti abi.

english_verse: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ Gyosefu lɩɩ mʋ awʋrʋfɔ kpini tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ kanyaasɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kebiti lɩɩ Egyipiti Owura Faro asɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ o yii mʋ kuwura fɛɛ ɔ kɩɩ mʋ owura‐kpa yɛ Egyipiti ɔsʋwʋlɛ kpini sʋ.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ akʋn damantɛ ɩ ba tɩyɛ Egyipiti yɛ Kaanan ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩ bɩya awʋrʋfɔ gaa. Nɩɩ anɛ naana ana ba man nyɛ agyitɔ.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Bamʋ‐sɛ Gyekobu maa nu fɛɛ agyitɔ ɩ bʋ Egyipiti mʋ, o sun mu‐bii ana mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ anɛ naana ana kpee tʋtɔ kɩgyankpaasa ba naa sɔɔ agyitɔ.

english_verse: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
translation_verse: Ba maa bwii kpee Egyipiti kɩnyɔsapʋ ba naa sɔɔ agyitɔ mʋ, Gyosefu lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ‐daa ana, nɩɩ Faro gbaa nu Gyosefu mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana sʋ.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyekobu kʋsʋ kpee Egyipiti, tʋtɔ nɩɩ maa mu‐bii ana ba naa wu.

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba bwiiyaa bamʋ ba Sekem ba pula kakyan mʋ nɩɩ Abraham sɔɔ lɩɩ Hamo mu‐bii ana asɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: “Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔkɔlɔ kɩda sa Abraham maa tiri tɔ fɛɛ ɩ ba tɔ mʋ, anɛ asa mʋ ba nyɛ akyɔ Egyipiti ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maa nɩɩ owura pʋpwɛ kʋ ba kyɩna Egyipiti kagya sʋ, amaa ɔ man nyi Gyosefu asɩn.

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ owura pʋpwɛ nɩmʋ nyanla anɛ asa nɩɩ ɔ waa bamʋ awʋrʋfɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ nyan bamʋ yɛ ba lɛɛ bamʋ nbii pʋpwɛ kewu kɛ ɔ mɔɔ bamʋ.

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: “Saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ Mosesi nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kebii danbɩrasa Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩɩ mʋ kɩbaabʋtɔ kwɩɩ. Nɩɩ ba baala mʋ, kɩɩ mʋ sʋ ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa mʋ‐sɛ kɩkpaara sʋ.

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, ba lɛɛ mʋ kewu nɩɩ Owura Faro mu‐bii kyɩɩsa taa mʋ naa bɩla nɩɩ o biliŋi mu‐bii.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kaapʋ Mosesi, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ Egyipiti abi kɩbɩɩla yɛ bamʋ kanyaasɩn, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kinyi yɛ katɔwɛ yɛ awaasa tɔ.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ maa nɩɩ o wu Egyipiti ɔnyɩn kʋ maa nyanla Ɩsɩrayɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ Mosesi naa kɔɔlɛ mʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ mɔɔ Egyipiti ɔnyɩn mʋ ka kʋkɔ.

english_verse: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
translation_verse: Mosesi nyi fɛɛ mʋ ayɛ abi bee biti ba bɩɩ fɛɛ, mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ biti ɔ bɔla sʋ mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ, amaa ba man bɩɩ.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
translation_verse: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, Mosesi ba tu Ɩsɩrayɩ abi asa anyɔ ba maa kɔ. Maa biti ɔ barɩgɛ bamʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, ‘Fan gyɛ asa kʋlʋn nɩn, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɛn nyanla abaa kanɩn?’

english_verse: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ nyanla mʋ bɩrɩsa mʋ nin Mosesi naa yɩlɛ ɩkaa sʋ nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ yɛ, ‘Anɩmʋ ɩ yɩla fʋ yɛ fʋ kɩɩ anɛ sʋ kɛ fu gyii anɛ asɩn?

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: Fii biti fʋ mɔɔ mɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ndee fʋ mɔɔ Egyipiti ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩn?’

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Mosesi maa nu kanɩn mʋ ɔ sɩlɛ lɩɩ Egyipiti kpee Midiyan ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, naa kyɩna fɛɛ ɔfɔ. Tʋtɔ maŋa nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ nbii nnyɔ.

english_verse: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: “Mosesi maa kyɩna tʋtɔ nsu adɩna kamaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ, kɩpʋnfa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛɛ ofuli sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ maa Sinayi kɩbɩɩ mʋ.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Maa wu falɛ atɔ mʋ, ɩ waa mʋ kanankʋ. Maa nin tiri kɛ ɔ kɩɩ danbɩrasa mʋ, o nu Ɩbwaarɛ maa tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
translation_verse: ‘Mɛ ɩ gyɛ fʋ naana ana Ɩbwaarɛ, Abraham yɛ Isaki yɛ Gyekobu Ɩbwaarɛ.’ Mosesi maa yɩnŋɛ nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa mʋ gaa sʋ mʋ, ɔ man bɩla taalɛ kɩɩ kʋtɔ maŋa.

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Lɛɛ fʋ asɩbɩta, lɩɩ fɛɛ ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ yɩlɛ mʋ gyɛ kasɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ nɩn.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Kesintin, n wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyanla mɛ asa Egyipiti tɔ. Nɩɩ n nu bamʋ kusu, nɩɩ n kpɩlɩgɛ ba n ba mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ. Kanɩn sʋ n sun fʋ kɛ fʋ bwii kpee Egyipiti tɔ.’

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: “Mosesi kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ asa ba kina mʋ, tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Anɩmʋ ɩ yɩla fʋ anɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ asɩn ogyipu?’ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩla bɔla mʋ kabɔɔ sʋ fʋlɔn nnɔ‐bi tɔ yɛ, ɔ naa waa bamʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ.

english_verse: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
translation_verse: Ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ Egyipiti nɩɩ ɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ adangana Egyipiti tɔ yɛ ɔpʋ pɩpɛɛ mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ ba gyii nsu adɩna ofuli mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: “Mosesi nɩmʋ ɩ tɔwɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ, ‘Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ pʋpwɛ lɩɩ fanɛ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mɛ mʋ’.

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Mosesi nɩmʋ tii Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ mʋ sʋ lɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ofuli sʋ mʋ. Mosesi maa anɛ naana ana yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ba bʋ tʋtɔ. Sɩnayɩ kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ nkpa asɩn mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ asɛ yɛ ɔ taa bɩya anɛ.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: “Ba waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ba man nu Mosesi asɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, naafɔɔ ba daa nyɛ nɩn bee biti ba bwii kpee Egyipiti.

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maa tɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba pɔyɩ kanaatɛ‐bi taa biliŋi bamʋ ɔdakpa. Nɩɩ ba lɔŋɔ ɔdakpa maŋa yɛgɛ bee gyii bamʋ ansi lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ abaa pɔyɩ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ palɛ mʋ kamaa sa bamʋ yɛ ba kyaga sʋ be sun kyɔwɛ yɛ ɔbwaayɩ yɛ akyikpebi, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba wʋla kyʋrɔɔ waa Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ɩwʋlɛ tɔ yɛ, ‘Ɩsɩrayɩ abi! Man gyɛ mɛ nɩɩ fɛn daa fɛn lɔŋɔ atɔbwaaya fɛn sa nsu adɩna ofuli sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ gyan ɔdakpa Moleki yɛ ɔdakpa Refan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fanɛ kikyikpebi ɔdakpa, mʋ nɩɩ fan taa fanɛ abaa pɔyɩ nɩɩ fen sun mʋ sʋ nɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n yɛgɛ fanɛ akiisipu ba taa fanɛ kpee kata‐kata kyʋn Babilon sʋ’.

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: “Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anɛ naana ana ba naa ba naa ofuli mʋ sʋ mʋ, ba sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kubuu lala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋ bamʋ asɛ. Ba waa kɩmʋ nɩn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ kaapʋ Mosesi yɛ ɔ waa kɩmʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, anɛ naana ana ba nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kubuu mʋ, lɩɩ bamʋ‐sɛ ana asɛ, nɩɩ Gyosuwa maa bamʋ ba sʋla Ɩbwaarɛ kubuu lala maŋa, naa fʋʋ ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gya asɛ lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ asa, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ anɛ asa ba kyɩna tʋtɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ kubuu mʋ sii tʋtɔ naa fʋʋ halɩɩ Dawidi saŋa mʋ.

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Dawidi mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi gyii mʋ sʋ mʋ kʋlɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ɔ pɔyɩ osunkpa ayɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ anɛ naana Gyekobu Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Amaa mu‐bii Solomon ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba pɔyɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: “Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ Owura lala mɛɛ kyɩna akpaara mʋ nɩɩ asa ba taa bamʋ abaa pɔyɩ mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: ‘Sʋsʋ ɩ gyɛ mɛ kuwura gya, nɩɩ kasɛ mɔɔ ɩ gyɛ mɛ ayaa ɔgyagakpa. Mɩnɛ obu nɩɩ fen biti fan pɔyɩ sa mɛ? Abɛɛ fɩnɛ ɩ taalɛ waa mɛ ɔkyɩnakpa?

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mɔɔ ɩ kyɩna nɩɩ fanɛ naana ana ba man nyanla mʋ? Ba mɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ dɩdaa ba wʋla tɔwɛ ɔkpa kyiigisa wuya mʋ kɩba asɩn. Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan waa mʋ kadɛ‐boyi nɩɩ fan mɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: Fanɛ ɩ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ba taa bɩya fanɛ mʋ, amaa fan kina ɩmʋ ki buu.” Ba tʋʋ Sitefen abuu

english_verse: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
translation_verse: Ɩsɩrayɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyaŋɛ mʋ ba maa nu Sitefen asɛ mʋ, ba nyɛ agbʋ nɩɩ ba duŋi bamʋ anobi.

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Amaa Sitefen mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ bɔla mʋ tɔ mʋ, diyaa mu ansi kɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩɩ o wu Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa, nɩɩ o wu Yesu maa yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyisa sʋ.

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sitefen yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ! N wu sʋsʋ maa bugi nɩɩ n wu Yesu, Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ maa yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyisa sʋ.”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn kamaa tɔ mʋ ba tii bamʋ asʋ nɩɩ ba kuusi kenken. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba naa mili mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba bɩɩtɛ mʋ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ naa twɩɩ mʋ abuu. Nyamaanyɩlɛpʋ mʋ ba taa bamʋ ɩkaalɛ sa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Sɔɔlɩ yɛ ɔ kɩɩ sʋ.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ twɩɩ mʋ abuu mʋ, Sitefen kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu, kɔɔlɛ mɛ kala.”

english_verse: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sitefen muŋa mʋ ayaa muli sʋ tɔwɛ kenken yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu, man taa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ba waa falɛ ka bamʋ kʋkɔ.” Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ nɩɩ o wu.

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Amaa Sɔɔlɩ bɩrɛ piili ee nyita Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ naa akpaara‐akpaara sʋ ɛɛ bɩɩtɛ anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu mʋ ee kpee ee tii ibu. Filipu abwaarɛsɩn katɔwɛ Samariya

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yaasɛ tɔ mʋ ba naa bɛɛ tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn danbɩrasa katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kpee mʋ.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Filipu gbaa kpee Samariya kadɛ lala kʋ tɔ naa tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kʋbʋ damantɛ mʋ nu Filipu asɛ nɩɩ ba wu asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ ɩsɩnkaala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ, ba kan bamʋ asʋ ba nu mʋ katɔwɛ.

english_verse: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi ba kuusi kenken lɩɩ asa gaalaagaa tɔ, nɩɩ amʋgʋlɛ‐mʋgʋlɛ yɛ aboobi gaalaagaa ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ kensi‐gyii lala kɩ ba kadɛ lala maŋa tɔ. Sɩtaanɛ adangana ɔwaapʋ Simon

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simon, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ adangana ɔwaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ Samariya kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ mʋ awaasa mʋ ɩ waa Samariya abi kɩyan. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ ɔ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa kparɛ.

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ mʋ asa kparɛ yɛ asansa mʋ kpini bɛɛ nu mʋ asɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ‘Ɔlʋn lala mʋ’.”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Ɔ waa Sɩtaanɛ adangana ɩnɩmʋ kyɛɛrɛɛ, nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ asa bee nu mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Filipu taa Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii sʋ mʋ, asa mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ gyii. Nɩɩ ba gyee anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Simon mʋ gbaa wu asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ ɩsɩnkaala mʋ nɩɩ Filipu waa mʋ, ɩ waa mʋ kanankʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii, nɩɩ ba gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee, nɩɩ o buu Filipu kpee katɩn kʋmaa.

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito asunpu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem ba nu yɛ Samariya abi ba kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ gyii mʋ, ba sun Pita maa Gyɔn bamʋ asɛ.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ba maa fʋʋ bamʋ asɛ mʋ, ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa bamʋ yɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ sa bamʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, ba man pɩɩta nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Pita maa Gyɔn ba taa bamʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ nɩɩ ba nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Simon mʋ maa wu fɛɛ Pita maa Gyɔn ba taa bamʋ abaa gyaga akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ, Simon biti fɛɛ ɔ sa Pita maa Gyɔn afulee. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ,

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: “Fan sa mɛ kanɩn ɔlʋn maŋa, nɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n kan taa mɛ abaa gyaga ɔkʋmaa sʋ mʋ, kɛ ɔ nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ maŋa.”

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: Amaa Pita tɔwɛ Simon yɛ, “Faa fʋ afulee a biliŋi kɩyan. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ fɩɩ taalɛ taa afulee sɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ.

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: Fʋ man tii sʋ abɛɛ fʋ man sʋ ɔ lɛɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kakɛɛ kusun nɩmʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ man dɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ n wu fɛɛ kayɩɩ basa kɩ bʋ fʋ tɔ gaa, nɩɩ alibi ɩ bɔla fʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Simon lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kʋlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sa mɛ kɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ tɔwɛ falɛ ɩ man ba mɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: Pita maa Gyɔn ba gyii Yesu asɩn mʋ kesintin, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ gaa sa bamʋ. Kamaa tɔ mʋ, ba bwii kpee Gyerusalem. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bee bwii bee kpee mʋ, ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ Samariya ndɛ‐bii gaalaagaa tɔ. Filipu maa Etiyopiya‐nyɩn kʋ

english_verse: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kabɔɔ tɔwɛ Filipu yɛ, “Kʋsʋ kpee akpɩlɩgɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɛ ofuli sʋ mʋ. Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa kɩ dɛ lɩɩ Gyerusalem naa fʋʋ Gasa kadɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Filipu kʋsʋ yii ɔkpa sʋ ee kpee. Maa kpee mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa gyaŋa Etiyopiya ɔwʋlɛ nyipu kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Etiyopiya owura kyɩɩsa afulee sʋ. Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ kpee Gyerusalem naa sun Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɛ mʋ ɩgbaŋɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩɩtɛ asa ɔkyɩnakpa sʋ ee bwii ee kpewu yɛgɛ ɛɛ kalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya ɔwʋlɛ.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔwɛ Filipu yɛ, “Kpe kanɩn ɩgbaŋɛ nɩmʋ asɛ naa yɩlɛ tiri mʋ.”

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Filipu sɩlɛ kpee Etiyopiya‐nyɩn mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ o nu ɔnyɩn mʋ maa kalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya ɔwʋlɛ. Nɩɩ Filipu taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩmʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ kalɛ falɛ fii nu kasɛ?”

english_verse: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Nɛnɛ nɩɩ n waa nu kasɛ amɔɔ n nyɛ ɔkʋ kɛ ɔ lɛɛ kasɛ sa mɛ.” Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ tɩɩ Filipu yɛ ɔ ba kyɩna tiri mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ ɩɩ kalɛ ɛɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlɛsa mʋ tɔ yɛ, “O du fɛɛ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ ba naa mɔɔ mʋ, abɛɛ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ be kyiigi mʋ ifuu, yɛgɛ ɔ man bugi mʋ kɔnɔ halɩɩ ee su.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba gyila mʋ ipeeli, nɩɩ ba man gyii kesintin sa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man taalɛ tɔwɛ kaapʋ mʋ kɩnaana, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba tɩn mʋ nkpa sʋ kasɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ taasɛ Filipu yɛ, “Ɔbɩlɩsa, n kʋlɛ fʋ lɛɛ kasɛ sa mɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ, ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn nɩn abɛɛ ɔkʋ lɛɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Filipu piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kalɛ lɩɩ Isaya ɔwʋlɛ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Yesu n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa mʋ.
