english_verse: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Filipu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ nyɛ nɩɩ fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii fʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ bɩrɛ, fii biti fʋ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.” Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Yesu Krisito ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii kʋlʋn mʋ.”

english_verse: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Etiyopiya‐nyɩn mʋ yɛgɛ mʋ gbaŋɛ mʋ yɩla. Nɩɩ maa Filipu kpini ba kpɩlɩgɛ loo nkyu mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Filipu gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyu gyee.

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ nkyu mʋ tɔ mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Kayaayu taa Filipu lɩɩ ɔnyɩn mu ansi sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man bɩla wu mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taa kensi‐gyii kyaga mʋ ɔkpa sʋ ee kpewu.

english_verse: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ taa Filipu naa lɩɩ Asotusi kadɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ natɛ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini, ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa halɩɩ ɔ naa fʋʋ Kasariya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Amaa Sɔɔlɩ nyɛ ɔlʋn tii sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ, ɛɛ kaapʋ tɔ yɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋnɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ yɛ ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ asa. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Damasiko kadɛ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ kʋsʋ kpee Damasiko kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ bee biti ba tɔwɛ fʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ waa.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n biti n kaapʋ mʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o gyii awʋrʋfɔ lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ sʋ.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, o biti fɛɛ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ naa tii abɩɩlapʋ mʋ sʋ, amaa ba waa kufuu yɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii yɛ o tii Yesu abuupu mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ gbaa wu ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ananiyasi maa ba kɛ ɔ ba taa mʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ kɛ mu ansi i bugi.

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ opula tɔ atɔ kʋ fɛɛ ɔkɩn agyafɔyɩ ɩ lɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ ansi tɔ tɩyɛ, nɩɩ mu ansi i bugi. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ nɩɩ ba naa gyee mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, Sɔɔlɩ dan san ɔ yɩlɛ sʋ, ɔ sʋ kanyanla yɛ kɩmɔɔ aŋala lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu abuupu mʋ sʋ. Kanɩn sʋ o kpee Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ asɛ,

english_verse: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: nɩɩ ɔ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ sa mʋ ɩwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sa ɔkpa fɛɛ o kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Damasiko kadɛ mʋ tɔ, kɛ ɔ naa kɩtaa anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bee buu Yesu kɩkaapʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan nyɛ kanɩn abuupu maŋa kɛ ɔ kɩtaa bamʋ bwii ba Gyerusalem ba tii obu.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ tɩn mʋ tʋʋ kasɛ nɩɩ o nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa taasɛ yɛ, “Sɔɔlɩ, Sɔɔlɩ, mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fii kisi mɛ kanɩn?”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ taasɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, fʋ anɩmʋ nɩn?” Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ gyɛ mɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ fii kisi mʋ nɩn.

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba maa Sɔɔlɩ ba naa mʋ, ba nu katɔwɛ mʋ, amaa ba man wu ɔkʋ. Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ be sii ba yɩlɛ ba man bɩla bugi bamʋ nnɔ.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ maa yɩlɛ nɩɩ o bugi mu ansi mʋ, ɔ man bɩla ɔ kɩɩ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba sii kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ nɩn taa mʋ kpee Damasiko.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ biliŋi gyaatanapʋ nkɛ nsa, o mee gyii yɛgɛ o mee nu.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu ɔbɩɩlapʋ kʋ bʋ Damasiko kadɛ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ananiyasi. Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu lɛɛ ansi sʋ atɔ kaapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Ananiyasi.” Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ n kyula.”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ Ananiyasi yɛ, “Kʋsʋ kpee Gyudasi kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kigbenbi kyiigisa mʋ sʋ, naa taasɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Tasusi kadɛ tɔ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Sɔɔlɩ mʋ. Ɔ bʋ tʋtɔ ɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.”

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Amaa Ananiyasi lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, n nu asɩn damantɛ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyanla fʋ asa kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ.

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: An nu yɛ Sɔɔlɩ nɩmʋ nyɛ ɔlʋn lɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ ba Damasiko nfɩɩ, ɔ ba kɩtaa asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ fʋ Yesu kɩtɩɩ mʋ.”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Amaa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu tɔwɛ Ananiyasi yɛ, “Kʋsʋ kpee! Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ ɩ lɛɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ fɛɛ mɛ kɩbaa tɔ nyɩmɩsa, kɛ ɔ taa mɛ asɩn mʋ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be mee sun Ɩbwaarɛ, yɛ ba maa bamʋ awura, yɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ananiyasi kpee kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa taa mʋ abaa gyaga Sɔɔlɩ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ bɩrɩsa Sɔɔlɩ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ ba nfɩɩ mʋ, i sun mɛ fʋ asɛ kɛ fu ansi i bwii bugi, kɛ fʋ nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: Maa gyii agyitɔ kamaa tɔ mʋ, ɔ nyɛ ɔlʋn. Sɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn Damasiko Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ gyii nkɛ nkʋ Damasiko Yesu abuupu mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa naa tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn yɛ, “Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii.”

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu Sɔɔlɩ katɔwɛ mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan nɩɩ ba taasɛ yɛ, “Man gyɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ kyɩna ɛɛ nyanla asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ? Nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ o bwii ba, kɛ ɔ ba kɩtaa bamʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa o tii obu?”

english_verse: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ ɩ maa gyii nkɛ nkʋ mʋ Gyiwu abi ba ŋmina yɛ ba mɔɔ Sɔɔlɩ.

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: Amaa Sɔɔlɩ bɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ mʋ kɩmɔɔ mʋ sʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ ba sii ba tɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ kadɛ mʋ nwusunɔ mʋ sʋ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ kʋmaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ mʋ mɔɔ.

english_verse: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Amaa Yesu abuupu ba taa mʋ waa kɩlantan tɔ kakyanbwɛ, baala mʋ taa mʋ bʋlɛ kɩbandɩyɛ ɔbɔ tɔ kpɩlɩgaa mʋ yii. Sɔɔlɩ kpee Gyerusalem

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Banabasi taa mʋ kpee asunpu mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ Damasiko ɔkpa tɔ, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɔwɛ Sɔɔlɩ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ man waa kufuu, nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ tɔwɛ lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ Damasiko kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Sɔɔlɩ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ Gyerusalem yɛgɛ ɔ sʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛgɛ ɔ man sʋ kufuu.

english_verse: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ maa Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Gyiwu katɔwɛ mʋ, ba gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ, amaa yɛgɛ ba biti mʋ kɩmɔɔ ɔkpa.

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ tɔɔmaa ana ba bɩɩ kanɩn mʋ, ba taa mʋ kpee Kasariya kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ naa kyaga sʋ kpee mʋ kadɛ tɔ Tasusi.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Krisito abi kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Gyudeya yɛ Galile yɛ Samariya kpini ba nyɛ kayɩɩ yuuli. Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ waa bamʋ aniya tɔ, nɩɩ ba nyɛ akyɔ bɛɛ kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ, yɛgɛ ba sɩlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ. Pita waa asɩnkaala Lida yɛ Gyopa

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita kʋsʋ ɔ naa ɔ naa ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ naa bɔɔ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Lida mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa wu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ i wu, nɩɩ ɔ dɛ nsu nburuwa nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Aneyasi.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Aneyasi, kʋsʋ taa fʋ kalan, Yesu Krisito kyɛ fʋ.” Opula tɔ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Lida yɛ Saron mʋ kpini ba maa wu kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ biliŋi ba Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɛ.

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyopa kadɛ tɔ ɔkyɩɩ kɔɔlɛ‐gyipu kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Tabita. Tabita ɩmʋ Giriki katɔwɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Dokasi. Dokasi maŋa ɩ waa adanbɩrasa yɛgɛ ɛɛ kyɛ atiripu tɔ saŋa kʋmaa.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maa mʋ kɩlɔ kɩ daa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ o wu. Nɩɩ ba gyee mʋ kubuni naa dɩɩla abansʋrʋ obu to.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Lida kadɛ kɩ man bʋ kata yɛ Gyopa kadɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu abuupu mʋ ba maa nu fɛɛ Pita bʋ Lida mʋ, nɩɩ ba sun anyɩn anyɔ Pita asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Waa bilen kɛ fʋ ba anɛ asɛ.”

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pita maa bamʋ nɩɩ ba sun mʋ asɛ mʋ, ba bwii kpee Gyopa. Nɩɩ ba taa Pita kpee abansʋrʋ obu mʋ tɔ, ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ obuni mʋ dɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ akulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ obu mʋ tɔ mʋ kpini, ba kyaabɔɔ Pita bɛ sawʋ yɛgɛ ba sʋ atɔ bunsa mʋ nɩɩ Dokasi baata sa bamʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ bɛɛ kaapʋ Pita.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ obu mʋ tɔ kpini yɛ, ba lɩɩ kewu. Nɩɩ Pita muŋa mʋ ayaa muuli sʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, nɩɩ o biliŋi mu ansi kaapʋ obuni mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Tabita, kʋsʋ.” Nɩɩ o bugi mʋ ansi wu Pita, nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ kyɩna.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ kʋsaa mʋ yɩlɛ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu yɛ akulapu‐kyɩɩ mʋ taa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ o kyiŋi mʋ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ yaasɛ loo Gyopa kadɛ tɔ kpini. Nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu mʋ gyii.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita kyɩna Gyopa kadɛ tɔ, ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Simon nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ gbansaba mʋ asɛ nkɛ nnyɔ kʋ.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Ɔbɩlɩsa kʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Koneliyo. Nɩɩ ɔ tɛ Kasariya kadɛ tɔ. Ɔ kyɩna ɔ gyɛ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa sa Roma anaakɔpʋ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Itali abi kʋbʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: Maa mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Ɔ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɛɛ atiripu atɔ nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ sʋ saŋa sa kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Kanyɩn nɩmʋ nyɛ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ kakɛ kʋ kakyʋwapɛ ɩ waa fɛɛ kerefi asa saŋa tɔ. O wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ maa ba mʋ asɛ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ, “Koneliyo!”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kɩɩ mʋ yɛ kufuu nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mɩnɛ nɩn?” Nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ ansi i gyii fʋ kakɛɛ yɛ fʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ee biti o kyula fʋ.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, sun asa kʋ kpee Gyopa ba naa tɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simon Pita mʋ ba.

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: Ɔ bʋ Simon mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ gbansaba mʋ ayɛ nɩn, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kɩ maa ɔpʋ mʋ.”

english_verse: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ɩ kyʋn mʋ, nɩɩ Koneliyo tɩɩ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ anyɔ yɛ mʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa ɛɛ sa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o wu nɩɩ o sun bamʋ Gyopa. Pita wu ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ Koneliyo nbɔɔ ba maa tiri Gyopa mʋ Pita mɔɔ dii kpee abansʋrʋ sʋsʋ ɔ naa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kakyʋwapɛ maŋa.

english_verse: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
translation_verse: Akʋn a kɩtaa mʋ, nɩɩ ee biti kʋtɔ kʋ o gyii. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa agyitɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ.

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: O wu sʋsʋ maa bugi nɩɩ o wu kʋtɔ kʋ, maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ kɩtaa lala kɩɩ kpɩlɩgɛ kɩɩ ba kasɛ. Kɩ maa kɩmʋ anobi ana.

english_verse: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Kɩtaa mʋ sʋ mʋ, o wu atɔbwaaya kʋmaa yɛ nbwiibi yɛ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kpɩɩ ɩtɔ sʋ kpini.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Pita! Kʋsʋ mɔɔ kɛ fu gyii.”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ayee, n man pɩɩta gyii falɛ ineesi atɔ abɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ anɛ Gyiwu abi ikisii.”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ bɩla tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Man bɩla tɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ kɩmʋ mʋ, yɛ ineesi kʋtɔ.”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa kanɩn ɩlɩɩ ɩsa, nɩɩ ɩ taa kɩtaa mʋ yɛ atɔ mʋ bwii kyʋn sʋsʋ.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pita san ɛɛ fɛ ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ Koneliyo sun mʋ ba bɩɩ Simon kɩkpaara mʋ, nɩɩ ba ba yɩlɛ kɩmʋ kewusunɔ.

english_verse: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tɩɩ ɔkʋ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ nfɩɩ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simon Pita?”

english_verse: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pita san ɛɛ fɛ mʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Anyɩn asa akʋ bee biti fʋ.

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita kpɩlɩgɛ ba kasɛ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ anyɩn mʋ yɛ, “Mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ fen biti mʋ. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa?”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ “Anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Koneliyo mʋ i sun anɛ. Ɔ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ ɔ sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi kpini bɛ sa mʋ bɛɛrɛɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ tɩɩ fʋ ba mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, kɛ o nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ sa mʋ.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita taa anyɩn mʋ loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ, kɩtaa bamʋ kɩfɔ. Pita maa Koneliyo Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Pita maa Yesu abɩɩlapʋ akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyopa mʋ ba buu bamʋ.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ nɩɩ ba fʋʋ Kasariya. Yɛgɛ Koneliyo maa mʋ kʋʋpʋ ana yɛ mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana ba sarɛ bee gyoo mʋ.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Pita maa biti o loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ Koneliyo gyaŋa mʋ, nɩɩ o muŋa o sun mʋ.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Amaa Pita kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ “Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ! Ngbaa mɛ n dan n gyɛ nyɩmɩsa nɩn fɛɛ fʋ falɛ!”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ maa Pita bɛɛ waa kiligyima bee loo kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ naa tu asa ba maa sarɛ ba tɛ.

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan dan fen nyi fɛɛ naafɔɔ ɩ man dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ Gyiwu nyɩmɩsa maa bʋga ɔmʋ nɩɩ o mee sun Ɩbwaarɛ abɛɛ ɔ bɔɔ mʋ sʋ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kaapʋ mɛ fɛɛ n man tɔwɛ ɔkʋ yɛ ɔ man du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

english_verse: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan maa sun tɩɩ mɛ mʋ n man kina, kanɩn sʋ fan tɔwɛ mɛ kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ fan tɩɩ mɛ.”

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Koneliyo lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ndaga nkɛ nna nɩmʋ n maa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kerefi asa kakyʋwapɛ saŋa mʋ. Opula tɔ nɩɩ n wu ɔnyɩn kʋ maa bun kaalɛ fufuli ba yɩlɛ mi ansi tɔ.”

english_verse: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Koneliyo Ɩbwaarɛ nu fʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, nɩɩ o wu fʋ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ kɛɛ atiripu mʋ.

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ sun asa kpee Gyopa kɛ ba naa tɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Simon Pita mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔfɔ ɔ bʋ Simon mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ gbansaba nɩɩ mʋ kɩkpaara bʋ mata ɔpʋ mʋ.

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n sun tɩɩ fʋ bilen, nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn fɛɛ fʋ maa ba. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ an gyaŋɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ an nu fʋ asɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ taa sa fʋ yɛ fʋ ba tɔwɛ anɛ mʋ.” Pita abwaarɛsɩn katɔwɛ

english_verse: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ n bɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ kɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ansi tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋ.

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ kɔɔlɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ ndɛndɛmandɛ ɔ sɩlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa mʋ.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan nu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa bɩya Gyiwu abi nɩɩ ɔ bɔla Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ atɔ kpini Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ sʋ taa kayɩɩ yuuli asɩn danbɩrasa sa bamʋ.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan nyi asɩn kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa Gyiwu ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ nɩɩ i piili lɩɩ Galile mʋ. Ɩ waa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee asɩn mʋ kamaa.

english_verse: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: Fan nu Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ asɩn, kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ yɛ ɔlʋn bɔla mʋ tɔ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tii mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kpee mʋ ɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa yɛgɛ ɛɛ kyɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ bʋ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: “Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa Gyerusalem yɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ kpini‐kpini mʋ, anɛ asunpu nɩmʋ an gyɛ ɩmʋ kesintin agyipu. Ba da mʋ oyu laasa sʋ mɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: Amaa nkɛ nsa kamaa tɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ.

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: Man gyɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ amɔɔ anɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ yɛ an baa an gyɛ mʋ kesintin agyipu mʋ maa wu kyiŋi kamaa tɔ mʋ ana mʋ a bʋga gyii nɩɩ an nu.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu sa anɛ kɔnɔ yɛ a taa tɔwɛ Abwaarɛsɩn mʋ sa Gyiwu kasɛ sʋ asa kpini. Kɛ a lɛɛ kesintin mʋ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ asa kɩɩsa yɛ alewupu kpini ɔtalɛpʋ.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Pita maa san ɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ba loo bamʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn mʋ kpini tɔ.

english_verse: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
translation_verse: I kyinkyin Gyiwu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Pita lɩɩ Gyopa ba mʋ gaa, fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi.

english_verse: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba nu ba maa tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ pʋpwɛ yɛgɛ bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ Pita taasɛ yɛ,

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: “Ba maa nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ an nyɛ falɛ mʋ, ɔkʋ ɩ taalɛ tii ɔkpa fɛɛ ba man gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee?”

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ Pita yɛ ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nkɛ nyɔ nkʋ.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pita bwii kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tɔ sa iduu kɩtɩn mʋ

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fu loo bamʋ nɩɩ ba man tɩn iduu mʋ akpaara sʋ nɩɩ faa bamʋ fan bʋga gyii.”

english_verse: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita lɛɛ kasɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋkʋlʋn sa bamʋ tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: “Kakaakʋ n bʋ Gyopa kadɛ tɔ n kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ. Nɩɩ n nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ wu sʋsʋ maa bugi. Nɩɩ n wu kʋtɔ kʋ maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ kɩtaa lʋʋsa kɩlala, nɩɩ ba sʋ kɩmʋ anobi ana mʋ tɔ ba kasɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ.

english_verse: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: N maa kɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, n wu atɔbwaaya yɛ nbwiibi yɛ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ bɛ kpɩɩ ɩtɔ sʋ.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, ‘Pita kʋsʋ mɔɔ kɛ fu gyii.’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ n lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ayee, falɛ ineesi atɔ abɛɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ anɛ Gyiwu abi ii kisi ɩ man pɩɩta loo mɛ kɔnɔ tɔ.’

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ maŋa bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ man bɩla tɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ kɩmʋ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛ ineesi kʋtɔ.

english_verse: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa kanɩn ɩlɩɩ ɩsa, nɩɩ ɩ taa kɩtaa mʋ yɛ atɔ mʋ bwii kyʋn sʋsʋ.

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: “Saŋa maŋa tɔ anyɩn asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ lɩɩ Kasariya ba ba mɛ asɛ mʋ, ba ba yɩlɛ kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ n bʋ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ man kan gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ, faa bamʋ fan kpee. Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu asiye anɩmʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyopa mʋ i sun mɛ kpee naa loo, ɔnyɩn mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Ɔ tɔwɛ anɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ maa ba yɩlɛ mu obu to, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Sun asa akʋ kpee Gyopa ba naa tɩɩ Simon mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Pita mʋ.

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: Ee biti ɔ tɔwɛ fanɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ faa fʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini fan nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.’

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: “N maa piili n tɔwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ba bamʋ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o loo anɛ tɔ npiili asɛ mʋ.

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Gyɔn taa nkyu nɩn gyee asa Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee. Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ gyee fanɛ.’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ kakɛɛ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu gyii mʋ, mɛ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ kɛ n taalɛ tii Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa?”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Krisito abuupu mʋ maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ba man bɩla taalɛ tɔwɛ sɛhn, nɩɩ ba yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa ɔkpa fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa kɛ ba nyɛ nkpa. Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Antiyoki, Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba twɩɩ abuu mɔɔ Sitefen mʋ, kanyanla kɩ ba Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ akʋ ba yaasɛ tɔ loo Fonesiya yɛ Sipuru yɛ Antiyoki kasɛ sʋ yɛgɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ bɛɛ sa Gyiwu abi nkʋn.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Sipuru yɛ Siren ba Antiyoki mʋ, ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ sa Giriki abi gbaa.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩbaa kɩmaa gyan bamʋ sʋ sʋ mʋ, asa damantɛ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii nɩɩ ba be buu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ ba maa nu kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba sun Banabasi kpee Antiyoki.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Maa naa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ o wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bwaala bamʋ mʋ, mu ansi i gyii nɩɩ ɔ waa bamʋ aniya tɔ yɛ ba nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ kʋlʋn Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Banabasi kyɩna ɔ gyɛ nyɩmɩsa danbɩrasa nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩkyɔ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ bɔla mʋ tɔ lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ asa damantɛ ba ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ gyii.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Banabasi kpee Tasusi kadɛ tɔ ɔ naa biti Sɔɔlɩ.

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: Maa naa wu mʋ mʋ, o bwiiyaa mʋ ba Antiyoki. Nɩɩ maa mʋ kpini ba sii Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ kesu laalaamuu yɛgɛ bɛ kaapʋ asa damantɛ. Antiyoki tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba piili kasɛ bɛɛ tɩɩ Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ yɛ Krisito abi.

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ akʋ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem kpee Antiyoki.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: Bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Agabu mʋ, kʋsʋ yɩlɛ lɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ɔlʋn tɔ sʋ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ, akʋn lala a biti a tɛ Roma Ɩsʋwʋlɛ kpini sʋ.

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ nkyɛtɔ mʋ sa Banabasi maa Sɔɔlɩ yɛ ba taa naa sa Krisito abɩɩlapʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Owura Herodi yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba naa kɩtaa Krisito abɩɩlapʋ akʋ, nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ nyanla bamʋ.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: Ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba tɩn Gyɔn mʋ‐daa Gyemisi kumu.

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pita san o tii obu, amaa Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ba payɩ kinsi bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ bɛ sa mʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ba lɛɛ Pita lɩɩ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kagyanbwɛ pɔyɩ kayɩ kɩ kɛ, kɛ Owura Herodi lɛɛ mʋ o be gyii mʋ asɩn mʋ, ba da mʋ ɩgbɛɛ yɛgɛ ɔ dɛ anaakɔpʋ anyɔ nsana yɛgɛ anaakɔpʋ akʋ ba bɩla ba yɩlɛ kebunɔ.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ba obu tiisa mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɩ ŋmaŋɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ da Pita kyiŋi, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bilen.” Nɩɩ agbeeleebi mʋ nɩɩ ba taa da mʋ ɩgbɛɛ mʋ ɩ saŋɛ ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Bun fʋ atɔ kɛ fʋ waa asɩbɩta, kɛ fu bun fʋ balɩmaasʋ kɛ fʋ buu mɛ.”

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Ba kyʋn nbunɔ akɩɩsʋpʋ gyankpaasa kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ kebunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa abɩlɩtɔ waa mʋ, nɩɩ lɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ taa kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ. Ba maa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kebunɔ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa tigi nɩɩ ba lɩɩ. Ba maa natɛ ɔkpa mʋ sʋ kpee ansi tɔ gbɛrɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ kɩ nyaa mu ansi sʋ.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɩ ŋmaŋɛ Pita sʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩn fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sun mʋ kabɔɔ yɛ ɔ ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ lɩɩ Owura Herodi abaa tɔ yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba da ŋmina yɛ ba waa mɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: Maa fɛ ɩnɩmʋ kpini nfɛɛrɛ mʋ o kpee Maki Gyɔn mu‐nyi Mɛɛrɩ kɩkpaara sʋ, opula mʋ nɩɩ asa kʋkyɔ, ba gyaŋɛ bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita da kewusunɔ ɔpʋnʋ, nɩɩ kibite mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Roda mʋ ba suŋa kɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ daa.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: Kebite mʋ maa bɩɩ yɛ ɩ gyɛ Pita ɔbʋlɛ mʋ, ɩ maa waa mʋ ɔkɔn gaa sʋ o man tigi ɔpʋnʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ sɩlɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ Pita ɩ yɩlɛ kewusunɔ.

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: Roda maa tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fɩɩ lɔ ɩbʋn nɩn?” Maa dan san ɔ yɩlɛ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛ, “Falɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Pita sʋ nɩn.”

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Amaa Pita dan san ɔ yɩlɛ kewusunɔ ɛɛ daa ɔpʋnʋ mʋ. Ba maa tigi ɔpʋnʋ mʋ, ba wu Pita, nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ kɩyan.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pita nyɛɛ bamʋ kɩbaa yɛ ba bun bamʋ nnɔ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ lɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Fan naa tɔwɛ sa Gyɔn maa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa.” Pita maa tɔwɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ kpee katɩnkʋ.

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ nyenyenfuu mʋ, nfɛɛrɛ ɩ kɩtaa anaakɔpʋ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa Pita.

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Owura Herodi sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba naa biti Pita ba. Amaa ba biti mʋ kpɩnɛ nɩɩ ba man wu mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ Owura mʋ taasɛ bamʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn kpɩnɛ, nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba mɔɔ bamʋ kpini. Nɩɩ Owura Herodi lɩɩ Gyudeya kpee Kasariya naa kyɩna tʋtɔ gyii nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ. Herodi kelewu

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: Owura Herodi nyɛ agbʋ lɩɩ Tayi yɛ Sidon abi sʋ. Nɩɩ ba waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn sun kpee Herodi asɛ. Ba bɔla Owura Herodi kɩyaafɔlɛ bɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Bilasitu mʋ sʋ, sun ɩmata Herodi asɛ lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyɛ bamʋ agyitɔ bɛɛ lɩɩ.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩla maa fʋʋ mʋ, Owura Herodi bun mʋ awura tɔ naa kyɩna mʋ kuwura‐gya sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ asɩn mʋ tɔwɛ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba kuusi nɩɩ ba gyaabii mʋ yɛ, “Ɩnɩmʋ bɩrɛ ɩ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa ɔbʋlɛ nɩn, amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kʋ ɔbʋlɛ nɩn.

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Owura Herodi maa man taa kanɩn bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ. Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kɩlɔ kɩ da mʋ, nɩɩ ɔtɔ tɔ asʋnswɛɛ ba wɛ mʋ mɔɔ.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Banabasi maa Sɔɔlɩ ba lʋwɛ bamʋ kusun mʋ, ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem bwii kpee Antiyoki, nɩɩ ba taa Gyɔn mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Maki mʋ tii bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ.

english_verse: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Antiyoki mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ akaapʋpʋ akʋ ba bʋ tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Banabasi, yɛ Simeyon mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Nigya, yɛ Lusiyu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Siren, yɛ Sɔɔlɩ maa Maniya mʋ nɩɩ ba bɩla maa Herodi bʋga mʋ.

english_verse: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
translation_verse: Ba maa kyaga sʋ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ bee ŋmina kɔnɔ mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan lɛɛ Banabasi maa Sɔɔlɩ kɛ ba naa waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ n tɩɩ bamʋ yɛ ba ba waa mʋ.”

english_verse: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba bwii ŋmina kɔnɔ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ taa bamʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ sa bamʋ ɔkpa. Banabasi maa Sɔɔlɩ ba kpee Sipuru

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ maŋa ɩ kaapʋ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Selusiya, nɩɩ ba bɔla nkyu sʋ kpee Sipuru kifintin sʋ.

english_verse: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
translation_verse: Ba maa fʋʋ Salami mʋ ba tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ Gyɔn Maki gbaa kyɛ bamʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa ba natɛ kyaabɔɔ kifintin mʋ sʋ kpini naa fʋʋ Pafosi. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba tu Kagyiwu‐nyɩn kʋ mu nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Baa‐Yesu. Ɔ sʋ nbwɩɩ libi ɛɛ waa adangana nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

english_verse: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
translation_verse: Kifintin mʋ sʋ owura mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Segusi Polusi kyɩna ɔ gyɛ Baa Yesu mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ nɩn. Owura mʋ sʋ kanyaasɩn gaa. O sun tɩɩ Banabasi maa Pɔɔlɩ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ee biti o nu abwaarɛsɩn lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

english_verse: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Amaa nbwɩɩ libi wuya Baa‐Yesu, nɩɩ mʋ Giriki katɔwɛ tɔ ɩ gyɛ Elimasi mʋ, mee biti fɛɛ owura mʋ o nu kɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii.

english_verse: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sɔɔlɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛ bɩla bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Pɔɔlɩ, nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɔla mʋ tɔ mʋ, kɩɩ Elimasi kyiim nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ,

english_verse: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
translation_verse: “Fʋ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ obii nɩn nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ ɔdʋn sa kesintin kpini. Fʋ naa alibi ɔkpa sʋ fɩɩ pɩna asa, nɩɩ fii biti fu biliŋi Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɩn mʋ yɛ ɩ man waa kesintin.

english_verse: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
translation_verse: Kɩɩ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ kɩbaa fʋ sʋ. Fii biti fu biliŋi gyaatanapʋ kɛ fʋ man bɩla wu kyɔwɛ naa fʋʋ saŋa kʋ.” Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɩ waa mu ansi sʋ kitentenbiri. Nɩɩ o sii ɛɛ kyaabɔɔ ee biti ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɩtaa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ kaapʋ mʋ ɔkpa.

english_verse: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
translation_verse: Owura mʋ maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, yɛ abwaarɛsɩn kɩkaapʋ mʋ, ɩ waa mʋ kɩyan nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɩn mʋ gyii. Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi abwaarɛsɩn katɔwɛ Antiyoki kadɛ tɔ

english_verse: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa mʋ kʋbʋ ba lɩɩ Pafosi bɔla nkyu sʋ ba Pega kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Panfiliya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba maa Gyɔn Maki ba barɩgɛ abaa nɩɩ Gyɔn bwii kpee Gyerusalem.

english_verse: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
translation_verse: Amaa Banabasi maa Pɔɔlɩ ba lɩɩ Pega kpee Antiyoki mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Pisidiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ. Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ, ba kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa tɔ naa kyɩna.

english_verse: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
translation_verse: Asa mʋ ɔkʋ maa kalɛ lɩɩ Mosesi nbara ɩwʋlɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ɩwʋlɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, tʋtɔ alʋlʋnpʋ ba taa tʋʋ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ansi tɔ yɛ, “Anɛ bɩrɩsa ana, nɩɩ fan sʋ asɩn tɔwɛ waa asa mʋ, fan tɔwɛ anɛ.”

english_verse: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ kɩbaa nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Gyiwu abi yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ fen sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, fan nu mɛ asɛ.

english_verse: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
translation_verse: Anɛ Gyiwu abi anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, lɛɛ anɛ naana ana nɩɩ ɔ sa bamʋ kanyɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Egyipiti ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ ɔlʋn lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa tɔ.

english_verse: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kɩɩ sʋ danbɩrasa kɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ba tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ, ɩ man ba fanɛ sʋ. Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ,

english_verse: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
translation_verse: Ɩsʋwʋlɛ isunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Kena kasɛ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gya bamʋ lɩɩ tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa kasɛ mʋ sa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nɩɩ ki biliŋi bamʋ kapatɩyɛ.

english_verse: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ maa kʋsaa Sɔɔlɩ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa Dawidi yii Ɩsɩrayɩ kuwura. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, ‘N wu Dawidi. Gyeese mu‐bii mʋ nɩɩ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ ki biti, nɩɩ mʋ ii biti ɔ waa mɛ kɔkɔlɔ biti.’

english_verse: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
translation_verse: “Dawidi kanaan tɔ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ Yesu mʋ lɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ mʋ.

english_verse: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
translation_verse: Pɔyɩ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ Yesu ɔ ba mʋ, Gyɔn wʋla gyankpaa ba tɔwɛ Gyiwu abi yɛ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, kɛ ba nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyɔn ii biti ɔ lʋwɛ mu kusun mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ, ‘Fen nyi fɛɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ? N man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ, mɛɛ mʋ ɔ bʋ mɛ kamaa ɛɛ ba. N man dagaa fɛɛ n saŋɛ mu asɩbɩta gbaa.’

english_verse: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
translation_verse: “Mɛ tɔmaa ana Abraham anaana abi yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ fen sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, anɛ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa kamɔlɩgɛ asɩn mʋ.

english_verse: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyerusalem abi yɛ bamʋ abɩlɩsa kpini ba man bɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ mʋ. Yɛgɛ bee nu mʋ asɩn mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ɔwʋlɛ tɔ mʋ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kʋmaa, amaa ba man nu kasɛ. Ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ ba wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

english_verse: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
translation_verse: Ba maa waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ba wʋla kyʋrɔɔ yɩla lɩɩ mʋ sʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba kpɩlɩgaa mʋ lɩɩ oyu laasa mʋ sʋ naa waa kakyan tɔ.

english_verse: But God raised him from death!
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ ba nkpa tɔ.

english_verse: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nkɛ gaalaagaa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ bamʋ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba daa bɛɛ natɛ lɩɩ Galile bee kpee Gyerusalem mʋ. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ mu kuwu kyiŋi kesintin agyipu bɛɛ sa Gyiwu abi.

english_verse: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ an tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa anɛ naana ana mʋ sa fanɛ.

english_verse: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
translation_verse: Kanɩn asɩn maŋa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩ ba tɔ sa anɛ, mʋ nɩɩ an gyɛ bamʋ‐bii ana mʋ, kpalɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi Yesu lɩɩ lewu tɔ sʋ. Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Dawidi ɔlʋn nyɔsapʋ mʋ tɔ yɛ, ‘Ndaga n lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ mi‐bii.’

english_verse: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
translation_verse: Kikyiŋi mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi Yesu lɩɩ kelewu tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ gyɔɔ kakaakʋ kɩɩ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ ɩmʋ asɩn gbaa yɛ, ‘N biti n waa fanɛ kusee sa fanɛ atɔ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n da mɛ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ Dawidi mʋ’.

english_verse: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Dawidi tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, ‘Fʋ mɛɛ yɛgɛ kɛ fʋ Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ mʋ ɔ gyɔɔ nyita’.

english_verse: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
translation_verse: “Asɩn nɩmʋ man gyɛ Dawidi asɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ natɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti sʋ, mʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna, nɩɩ o wu, nɩɩ ba pula mʋ naa tii mʋ naana ana sʋ nɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ gyɔɔ.

english_verse: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
translation_verse: Amaa Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ mʋ bɩrɛ man gyɔɔ.

english_verse: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
translation_verse: “Kanan sʋ mɛ bɩrɩsa ana Gyiwu abi, lɩɩ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ nɩɩ an tɔwɛ alibi kɩtaa kɛɛ asɩn an sa fanɛ falɛ.

english_verse: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
translation_verse: ‘Fanɛ ahin‐sɩn wuya ana, ii biti ɩ waa fanɛ kɩyan fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n biti n mɔɔ fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ saŋa nɩmʋ tɔ n biti n waa kʋtɔ kʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan ba tɔwɛ gbaa, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii’.”

english_verse: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba maa lɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ asa mʋ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba bɩla bwii ba kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ, kɛ ba ba tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn nɩmʋ tii sʋ.

english_verse: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ asa mʋ ba lɩɩ ɔgyaŋɛkpa nɩɩ ba yaasɛ tɔ mʋ, Gyiwu abi mʋ gaalaagaa, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ ba Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa tɔ mʋ, ba buu Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi. Nɩɩ ba waa asa mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba kyɩna Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa pʋpwɛ mʋ maa fʋʋ mʋ, kadɛ mʋ tɔ asa kɩbaafʋn mʋ nɩɩ ba kpee mʋ ɩ kyɔ gaa nɩɩ ba naa gyaŋɛ ba nu abwaarɛsɩn.

english_verse: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
translation_verse: Amaa Gyiwu abɩlɩsa ba maa wu asa damantɛ ba maa nu Banabasi maa Pɔɔlɩ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, ba nyɛ bamʋ kayɩɩ basa yɛgɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn bee nyita asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a maa gyankpaa tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn sa pɔyɩ. Amaa fan maa man kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ gyii nɩɩ fan kɩɩ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ fan man dagaa sa nkpa kakpaa mʋ, an biti a taa asɩn mʋ naa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi.

english_verse: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ sa kɔnɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘N taa fanɛ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ fɩtɩla. Kɛ fan taa kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ naa sa asa dulinyaa katɩn kʋmaa’.

english_verse: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, ɩ waa bamʋ ɔkɔn gaa, nɩɩ ba taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ bamʋ fɛɛ ba ba nyɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.”

english_verse: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ ɩ yɛlɩgɛ kanɩn ɔsʋwʋlɛ maŋa sʋ kpini.

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba fun waa akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ, yɛ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Antiyoki kadɛ mʋ tɔ agyankpaapʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi sʋ bɛɛ nyanla bamʋ, nɩɩ ba gya bamʋ lɩɩ kanɩn ofuli maŋa sʋ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba kpɩkpaa bamʋ ayaa ɩsɛ, nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ ahee. Nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Ikoniyum kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Antiyoki tʋtɔ mʋ kpini ba nyɛ kensi‐gyii gaa. Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɔla bamʋ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba wʋla bɛɛ waa mʋ, ba kpee Ikoniyum Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.

english_verse: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Amaa Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ gyii mʋ, ba fun waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ, nyita bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ nɩɩ ba nyɛ nfɛɛrɛ libi lɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi sʋ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba gyii nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ tʋtɔ. Ba yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ kabwaala asɩn. Nɩɩ ngbaa mʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ kamaa nɩɩ ba waa asɩn kyinkyinsa yɛ ɩsɩnkaala.

english_verse: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ asa mʋ ba barɩgɛ tɔ ntun nnyɔ, akʋ ba buu Gyiwu abi nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba buu Yesu asunpu mʋ.

english_verse: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
translation_verse: Saŋa maŋa mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ akʋ yɛ Gyiwu abi, taa tii bamʋ agyankpaapʋ sʋ mʋ ba waa kɔnɔ kʋlʋn fɛɛ ba tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ kɛ ba tʋʋ bamʋ abuu mɔɔ.

english_verse: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn kʋ tɛ Lisitira tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana, nɩɩ ɔ man natɛ kakaakʋ.

english_verse: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
translation_verse: Ɔ tɛ tʋtɔ ee nu Pɔɔlɩ maa tɔwɛ. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kɩɩ mʋ kyimm wu fɛɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, nɩɩ ɔ taalɛ nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ pʋntɛ yɛ, “Kʋsʋ yɩlɛ fʋ ayaa sʋ.” Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ fugi yɩlɛ mʋ ayaa sʋ nɩɩ o piili ɔ naa.

english_verse: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
translation_verse: Sakyɔ mʋ maa wu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ waa mʋ, ba kuusi Likoniya katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ, “Anɛ ɩdakpa mʋ ba biliŋi asa ba anɛ asɛ.”

english_verse: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba sa Banabasi kɩtɩɩ yɛ Sewu, nɩɩ ba sa Pɔɔlɩ gbaa kɩtɩɩ yɛ Hemesi lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn mʋ ɔtɔwɛpʋ.

english_verse: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔdakpa Sewu oseepu obu yɩlɛ kadɛ mʋ ɩkaa sʋ. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ oseepu taa ɩnaa‐nyɩn yɛ ntʋtʋlɛ ba kadɛ mʋ kewusunɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ maa sakyɔ mʋ bee biti ba waa kɔlɔŋɔ sa Krisito asunpu mʋ.

english_verse: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
translation_verse: Amaa ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, ba kɩtaa bamʋ atɔ bunsa kyatɛ sɩlɛ loo sakyɔ mʋ tɔ, bee kuusi yɛ,

english_verse: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
translation_verse: Dɩdaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mu ansi yɛgɛ nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini ba natɛ waa bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ nkɔlɔ biti.

english_verse: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ waa adanbɩrasa kaapʋ asa fɛɛ maa bʋ tɔ, fɛɛ maa sa bʋlayɩ nɩɩ atɔ duusa a waa saŋa kyɛɛkyɛɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɛɛ sa fanɛ agyitɔ kɩkyɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ yɛgɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii.”

english_verse: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
translation_verse: Halɩɩ ba maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kamaa gbaa mʋ, ɩ waa ɔlʋn pɔyɩ nɩɩ ba taalɛ gya sakyɔ mʋ yɛ ba man waa kɔlɔŋɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Antiyoki yɛ Ikoniyum mʋ ba ba tɔwɛ kyɛɛgɛ sakyɔ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ. Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ Pɔɔlɩ abuu waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ o wu nɩn, nɩɩ ba kpɩɩ mʋ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba ba kyaabɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ mʋ, ɔ kʋsʋ nɩɩ o bwii kpee kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Debe. Banabasi maa Pɔɔlɩ kibwii kpee Antiyoki, Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ Debe kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ asa kɩkyɔ ba be buu Yesu. Nɩɩ ba bwii kpee Lisitira yɛ Ikoniyum ndɛ yɛ Antiyoki mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Pisidiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Ba tɔwɛ waa Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ, waa bamʋ ɔlʋn tɔ fɛɛ ba yɩlɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, pɔyɩ kɛ ba nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ loo mʋ, ɩ dagaa ba maa gyii awʋrʋfɔ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
translation_verse: Krisito abi kʋbʋ kʋmaa tɔ mʋ, ba lɛɛ abɩlɩsa sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba ŋmina kɔnɔ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ taa bamʋ waa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii mʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, ba bɔla lɩɩ Pisidiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ taa ba fʋʋ Panfiliya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ Pega kadɛ tɔ kyaga sʋ kpee Ataliya kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
translation_verse: Ba lɩɩ Ataliya bɔla nkyu sʋ kpee Antiyoki mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Siriya. Antiyoki tʋtɔ nɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ba wʋla kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ taa bamʋ waa Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbaa tɔ, fɛɛ ɔ bwaala bamʋ kɛ ba taalɛ waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ ba waa lʋwɛ falɛ.

english_verse: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba fʋʋ Antiyoki mʋ, ba tɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ba sarɛ katɩn kʋlʋn. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla bamʋ sʋ waa, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ o bugi ɔkpa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ gyii mʋ.

english_verse: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba kyɩna Yesu abɩɩlapʋ mʋ asɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ.

english_verse: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
translation_verse: Amaa asa akʋ ba lɩɩ Gyudeya ba Antiyoki, nɩɩ ba ba bɛɛ kaapʋ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ yɛ, nɩɩ ba kan man tin bamʋ iduu fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara ɩ kaapʋ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.

english_verse: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bɩɩtɛ yɛ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii kɩ ba Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi yɛ asa mʋ nsana. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi yɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ akʋ fɛɛ ba kpee Gyerusalem naa wu Krisito asunpu yɛ abɩlɩsa lɩɩ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ba lɛɛ bamʋ ɔkpa. Ba maa kpee mʋ ba bɔla lɩɩ Fonesiya yɛ Samariya. Yɛgɛ bɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ Krisito gyii mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ waa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ɔkɔn.

english_verse: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba fʋʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, Krisito abi kʋbʋ yɛ Krisito asunpu yɛ kʋbʋ mʋ bamʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba waa bamʋ nsa‐nsa. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba lɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla bamʋ sʋ waa mʋ.

english_verse: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
translation_verse: Amaa Farasii kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ akʋ ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ tɔ yɛ, “Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Krisito gyii mʋ, ba maa tɩŋɛ iduu fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ tɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ Krisito asunpu mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba gyaŋɛ kɛ ba taa ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ.

english_verse: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
translation_verse: Ba maa kyɩna asɩn mʋ sʋ gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ mʋ, Pita kʋsʋ yɩlɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɛ daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan nyi fɛɛ nsu nkʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ maa lɛɛ mɛ lɩɩ fanɛ tɔ yɛ n tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba nu kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.

english_verse: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ o nyi nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ, waa nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔ kɔɔlɛ bamʋ, fɛɛ ɔ maa sa bamʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ gbaa anɛ mʋ.

english_verse: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man sʋ nbarɩgɛ tɔ yɛ anaa bamʋ nsana tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ, lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ ɔ lɔŋɔ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

english_verse: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fen biti fan tʋʋ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔ kɩɩ, nɩɩ fɛn taa ɩsʋla budusa fɛn sʋla Yesu abuupu mʋ nɩɩ anɛ yɛ anɛ naana ana gbaa an man taalɛ sʋla?

english_verse: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
translation_verse: An kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ, lɩɩ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kabwaala mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mɔlɩgɛ anɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ gbaa bamʋ ba nyɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini ba laatɔ bee nu Banabasi yɛ Pɔɔlɩ ba maa tɔwɛ ɩsɩnkaala yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla bamʋ sʋ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
translation_verse: Ba maa tɔwɛ lʋwɛ mʋ, Gyemisi kʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan nu mɛ asɛ.

english_verse: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
translation_verse: Simon Pita tɔwɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gyankpaa waa, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ ee biti bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ tɔ akʋ biliŋi mʋ lɛɛ.

english_verse: This agrees with what the prophets wrote,
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn nɩmʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ waa kʋkʋlʋn.

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
translation_verse: Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, ‘Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa n biti n bwii kɛ n pɔyɩ Dawidi kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ mʋ. N biti n lɔŋɔ‐lɔŋɔ ɩmʋ atɔ nyitasa mʋ kɛ n bwii kʋsaa kɩmʋ yɩlɛ.

english_verse: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
translation_verse: Kɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, nɩɩ mɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ kɩ gyan bamʋ sʋ mʋ, kɛ ba kɩɩ biti mɛ.

english_verse: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
translation_verse: “Kanɩn sʋ mɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n tɔwɛ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, an man nyan bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ ba kyɛɛgɛ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ.

english_verse: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Amaa ɩ dagaa a maa kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ba lɛɛ ɩdakpa agyitɔ kigyii yɛ kagyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn‐biti. Kɛ ba man gyii atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ba man tɩn bamʋ amu nɩɩ ba wu abɛɛ nkalan.

english_verse: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Nɩɩ koo dɩdaa mʋ, ba wʋla bɛɛ kaapʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ ndɛ kʋmaa tɔ, nɩɩ bɛɛ kalɛ ɩmʋ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kʋmaa.” Ɔwʋlɛ naa sa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ

english_verse: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Krisito asunpu mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ kpini ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ, ba lɛɛ bamʋ tɔ anyɩn akʋ, kɛ ba sun ba maa Pɔɔlɩ yɛ Banabasi kɛ ba kpee Antiyoki. Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ Gyudasi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ Basabasi yɛ Silasi. Bamʋ anyɔ ba gyɛ Krisito abi agyankpaapʋ mʋ akʋ.

english_verse: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
translation_verse: “An nu fɛɛ anɛ tɔ asa akʋ ba ba fanɛ asɛ, ba sʋ asɩn akʋ ba nyan fanɛ, yɛgɛ bee nyita fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ. Yɛgɛ an man sa bamʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba waa kanɩn, nɩɩ an man nyi bamʋ sʋ.

english_verse: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ anɛ kpini a tɔwɛ kyula fɛɛ a lɛɛ asa akʋ kɛ ba maa anɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana Banabasi maa Pɔɔlɩ, an sun bamʋ ba fanɛ asɛ.

english_verse: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba taga apʋ lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ.

english_verse: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ an bɩla sun Gyudasi maa Silasi fanɛ asɛ an tii sʋ. Kɛ ba naa taa bamʋ kɔnɔ tɔ tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an kyʋrɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ sa ɩdakpa yɛ atɔbwaaya nkalan yɛ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ba man tɩn bamʋ amu nɩɩ ba wu mʋ, kɛ fan bɩla lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kagyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn‐biti tɔ. Ii biti ɩ waa ɔdan sa fanɛ fɛɛ fan maa lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ tɔ. “An kala fanɛ.”

english_verse: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ ɔkpa. Nɩɩ nbɔɔ mʋ ba maa naa fʋʋ Antiyoki mʋ, ba tɩɩ Krisito abi mʋ sarɛ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
translation_verse: Ba maa kalɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ, katɔwɛ waa nkyɛtɔ asɩn mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi i gyii.

english_verse: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Gyudasi maa Silasi mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ ba tɔwɛ waa Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ.

english_verse: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba maa gyii nkɛ nkʋ bamʋ asɛ mʋ, ba lɛɛ bamʋ ɔkpa kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ. Nɩɩ ba bwii naa fʋʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba sii Antiyoki. Nɩɩ ba maa asa gaalaagaa bɛɛ tɔwɛ bɛɛ kaapʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɩn mʋ. Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi ba barɩgɛ abaa

english_verse: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ Banabasi yɛ, “Yɛgɛ a bwii kpee naa bɔɔ Krisito abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ndɛ‐ndɛ sʋ mʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ an wʋla tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ asɩn mʋ. Kɛ a kpee naa wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba du.”

english_verse: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Banabasi ii biti fɛɛ ɔ taa Gyɔn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Maki mʋ o tii bamʋ sʋ.

english_verse: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ man kyula fɛɛ ba taa Maki buu bamʋ n‐yɩɩ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ tɔ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Panfiliya bɛɛ waa kusun mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bɩɩtɛ tɩyɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Banabasi nsana, nɩɩ ba barɩgɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ Banabasi taa Maki buu mʋ n‐yɩɩ bɔla nkyu sʋ kpee Sipuru.

english_verse: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ bɩrɛ taa Silasi tii mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ Krisito abi mʋ ba kʋlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ fɛɛ ɔ bwaala bamʋ, nɩɩ ba kyʋn.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba bɔla lɩɩ Siriya yɛ Silisiya nɩɩ ba waa Krisito abi kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ba ba Debe, nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Lisitira. Tʋtɔ maa nɩɩ ba wu Yesu obuupu kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Timoti. Nɩɩ mu‐nyi gyɛ Gyiwu obii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Krisito gyii, amaa mʋ‐sɛ bɩrɛ gyɛ Giriki obii nɩn.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Lisitira yɛ Ikoniyum mʋ, ba tɔwɛ Timoti n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ ii biti fɛɛ ɔ taa Timoti buu mʋ n‐yɩɩ kpee ɔkpa. Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ tɩn Timoti oduu, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ kpini be nyi fɛɛ Timoti mʋ‐sɛ maa gyɛ Giriki obii nɩn.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: Ba maa naa ndɛ sʋ mʋ, bɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ kɔnɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito asunpu mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem ba sa bamʋ fɛɛ ɔsa kʋmaa o buu ɩmʋ.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Krisito abi ba nyɛ ɔlʋn kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ, yɛgɛ kakɛ kʋmaa bɛɛ nyɛ akyɔ bee tii sʋ. Pɔɔlɩ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ maa tɩɩ mʋ lɩɩ Masedoniya kʋ sʋ

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa mʋ bɩrɩsa ana ba bɔla Firigiya maa Galati ifuli sʋ kyʋn, lɩɩ fɛɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ man sa bamʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba fʋʋ Misiya ngyaŋɛ mʋ, ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ ba faa loo Bitiniya, amaa Yesu Kayaayu mʋ man sa bamʋ ɔkpa.

english_verse: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɩɩ Misiya kpee Trowa kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ. O wu Masedoniya ɔnyɩn kʋ maa yɩlɛ ɛɛ kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, “Faa ba Masedoniya, ba kyɛ anɛ tɔ.”

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ wu ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ, opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ an kʋʋla anɛ n‐yɩɩ an biti a kpee Masedoniya. Lɩɩ fɛɛ an kɔɔlɛ kyula fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ tɩɩ anɛ yɛ a naa tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa bamʋ. Lidiya ɩ kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ gyii Filipi kadɛ tɔ

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
