translation_verse: An lɩɩ Trowa kyɩna nkyu sʋ yii sʋ kpee Samotiri. Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, nɩɩ an fʋʋ Neyapoli.

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ an lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyaga sʋ natɛ kpee Filipi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Masedoniya kabaafʋn kʋ kadɛ lala, nɩɩ tʋtɔ bʋ Roma abi kasɛ. An gyii tʋtɔ nkɛ nkʋ.

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ mʋ an lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ kpee ɔlɩɩkaa. An waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ an biti an wu kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ opula. A maa wu kʋkʋ mʋ, an kyɩna piili an tɔwɛ an sa akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba ba gyaŋɛ tʋtɔ mʋ.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: Akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bee nu asɩn mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ yɛ Lidiya, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ataa kɩrɩman‐kyu ɔfɛpʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Tatira kadɛ lala tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ bugi mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ gyii.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an gyee maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mʋ, ɔ tɩɩ anɛ ba mʋ ayɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fan kɩɩ mɛ fɛɛ n kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ gyii bɩrɛ, fan ba kyɩna mɛ kɩkpaara sʋ.” Nɩɩ ɔ dan mata sʋ halɩɩ an kyula naa kyɩna mʋ asɛ. Ba tii Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi obu

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ an maa kpee kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ opula mʋ, an gyaŋa kɩnyɛ kibite mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kabwɩɩ libi. Nɩɩ ɛɛ kpalɩ ee wu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba, yɛgɛ ɛɛ nyɛ afulee gaa ɛɛ sa mʋ wuya ana.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi nɩmʋ buu anaa Pɔɔlɩ, yɛgɛ ee kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Asa anɩmʋ ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan bɔla sʋ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.”

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: Ɔ maa sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kakɛ kʋmaa. Amaa kamaa tɔ mʋ kibite mʋ asɩn mʋ i fii Pɔɔlɩ, nɩɩ o biliŋi tɔwɛ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ yɛ, “N tɔwɛ fʋ yɛ, lɩɩ Yesu Krisito kɩtɩɩ tɔ sʋ mʋ, lɩɩ mʋ tɔ.” Nɩɩ opula tɔ nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ lɩɩ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kebii kyɩɩsa‐bi mʋ wuya ana, ba wu fɛɛ ba man bɩla ba sʋ tamaa mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ afulee lɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ ba kɩtaa Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi nɩɩ ba bɩɩtɛ bamʋ kpee asɩn ogyikpa alʋnpʋ mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
translation_verse: yɛgɛ bɛɛ kaapʋ anɛ atɔ pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dagaa anɛ Roma abi fɛɛ a buu abɛɛ a waa.”

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ nyaŋɛ tɔ ba tii Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi waa tɔ, nɩɩ asɩn agyipu mʋ ba sa ɔkpa fɛɛ ba bugi bamʋ ɩkaalɛ mʋ kɛ ba tɩŋɛ bamʋ.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩŋɛ bamʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, ba taa bamʋ naa tii obu. Nɩɩ ba sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee kun obu mʋ sʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ɔ kɩɩ bamʋ sʋ danbɩrasa.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Ba maa sa mʋ kɔnɔ lʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ taa bamʋ naa tii nsana tɔ obu mʋ tɔ, taa agbeeleebi ŋmina bamʋ ayaa kyaga kigbilin. Obu okunpu mʋ kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ gyii

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: Amaa kiyiridi nsana mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi bɛɛ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛgɛ bɛɛ bɩɩ ɩlʋn bɛɛ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ, yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii obu mʋ gbaa bee nu bamʋ asɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen nɩɩ obu mʋ gyingyen naa lɩɩ halɩɩ kɩmʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ saŋa maŋa tɔ obu mʋ ɩpʋnʋ i tigi nɩɩ agbeeleebi mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ŋmina bamʋ ɔkʋmaa mʋ ɩ saŋɛ.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ obu tiipu okunpu kyiŋi wu fɛɛ ɩpʋnʋ mʋ i tigi mʋ ɔ bɩɩtɛ deebi lɛɛ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔɔ mʋ n‐yɩɩ, o nyi fɛɛ obu atiipu mʋ ba lɩɩ sɩlɛ nɩn.

english_verse: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ pʋntɛ mʋ sʋ yɛ, “Man waa fʋ n‐yɩɩ sɛhn, anɛ kpini a bʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ okunpu mʋ biti fɩtɩla, sɩlɛ taa kufuu yɛ kɩkyɩkya ba tɩyɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ayaa tɔ.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ bamʋ ba kewu taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Anyɩn, mɩnɛ ɩ dagaa n waa kɛ n nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ?”

english_verse: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu gyii kɛ faa fʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi fan nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ.”

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ asɩn sa mʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ kpini.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: Kanɩn kagyanbwɛ saŋa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ okunpu mʋ taa Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi lɩɩ naa nyɛɛ bamʋ ɩlɔ. Nɩɩ kanɩn opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ba gyee maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ obu atiipu okunpu mʋ taa bamʋ kpee mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ naa dɩŋa agyitɔ sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini ansi i gyii lɩɩ ba maa kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii mʋ sʋ. Ba yɛgɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi lɩɩ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Amaa ɩ maa ba ŋmaŋɛ tɔ mʋ, asɩn agyipu mʋ ba sun Apurusi yɛ ba naa tɔwɛ okunpu mʋ yɛ, “O tigi anyɩn mʋ kɛ ba natɛ.”

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ okunpu mʋ tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, “Asɩn agyipu mʋ ba sa kɔnɔ yɛ, n tigi faa Silasi. Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛn taalɛ natɛ kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ.”

english_verse: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ Apurusi mʋ yɛ, “Ba kɩtaa anɛ ba man gyii anɛ asɩn nɩɩ ba dayɩ anɛ sakyɔ tɔ, yɛgɛ an gyɛ Roma abi nɩɩ ba kɩtaa anɛ tii. Nɩɩ bee biti ba lɛɛ anɛ lɩɩ nfɩɩ asiri tɔ? Ayee! Kanɩn sʋ yɛgɛ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ ba ba lɛɛ anɛ.”

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Apurusi mʋ ba kpee naa tɔwɛ asɩn agyipu mʋ. Nɩɩ ba maa nu fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ba gyɛ Roma abi mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ.

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ asɩn agyipu mʋ ba ba kɩtaa bamʋ ayaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ, kʋlɛ bamʋ yɛ ba lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ba maa lɩɩ obu tiisa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba kpee Lidiya kɩkpaara sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba naa tu Krisito abi mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ waa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyʋn.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi ba bɔla lɩɩ Amfipolisi yɛ Apoloniya ndɛ tɔ mʋ, ba naa fʋʋ Tesalonika kadɛ tɔ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa bʋ mʋ.

english_verse: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɛɛ waa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ kpee ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn sa bamʋ nkɛ fʋʋtɛsa akɛ kanɩn ɩlɩɩ ɩsa.

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ tɔ fɛɛ, “Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Wʋla lɛɛ mʋ o gyii awʋrʋfɔ, kɛ o wu kɛ ɔ bɩla kyiŋi.” Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Yesu mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ asɩn falɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
translation_verse: Gyiwu abi mʋ akʋ yɛ Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, yɛ akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ kpini ba kɔɔlɛ Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ kyula yɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩɩ ba buu bamʋ.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Amaa Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ ba nyɛ kayɩɩ basa. Nɩɩ ba kpee kɩgya tɔ naa biti asa gyaga. Nɩɩ ba be tii bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ ba piili bɛɛ waa ɩlawʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kpala kpee Yasin ayɛ ba naa biti Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi, kɛ ba kɩtaa bamʋ ba sakyɔ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Amaa ba maa man wu bamʋ mʋ, ba kɩtaa Yasin maa Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu akʋ bɩya bamʋ kadɛ mʋ abɩlɩsa ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kuusi yɛ, “Ba nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba biliŋaa dulinyaa bee bun mʋ, ba ba nfɩɩ.

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yasin kɔɔlɛ bamʋ waa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ. Bamʋ kpini ba fɔyɩ Owura lala Kasaro kɔnɔsa nbara, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ Owura pʋpwɛ kʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Yesu.”

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: Sakyɔ mʋ yɛ kadɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba maa nu falɛ asɩn mʋ, ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ bamʋ gaa.

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ Yasin maa mʋ tɔmaa ana ba ka afulee mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ bamʋ. Ba kpiya Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi Bereya kadɛ tɔ

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa kagyanbwɛ, nɩɩ Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu ba sun Pɔɔlɩ maa Silasi kpee Bereya kadɛ tɔ. Ba maa fʋʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ba kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Bereya mʋ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ dɛ kasɛ ɩ kyɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Tesalonika. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba taa bamʋ nkɔlɔ kpini kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ yɛgɛ bɛɛ bɩla bɛɛ kɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ, kakɛ kʋmaa bɛɛ kɩɩ yɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ bʋ tɔ kanɩn.

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ Gyiwu abi damantɛ yɛ Giriki akyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ anyɩn gaalaagaa ba kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ gyii.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Tesalonika ba nu yɛ Pɔɔlɩ bʋ Bereya ɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ, ba ba tʋtɔ, bee fun bɛɛ waa asa mʋ.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba waa bilen taa Pɔɔlɩ kpee kebunɔ, amaa Silasi maa Timoti ba sii Bereya kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba sun Pɔɔlɩ ɔkpa mʋ, ba sun mʋ ɔkpa kpee Antensi. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kala bamʋ fɛɛ Silasi maa Timoti ba waa bilen ba tu mʋ Antensi. Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn Antensi yɛ Aropago

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ gyoo Silasi maa Timoti Antensi mʋ, maa wu fɛɛ ɩdakpa ɩ bɔla kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ mʋ gaa.

english_verse: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ, maa Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ bee gyii abwaarɛsɩn mʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ. Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ ee kpee kɩgya tɔ kakɛ kʋmaa yɛgɛ maa asa bee gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ.

english_verse: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Epikuriyan ɩwʋlɛ anyipu kʋbʋ yɛ Sitoki ɩwʋlɛ anyipu kʋbʋ bee piili bee gyii Pɔɔlɩ ɩmɔɔrɛ. Mʋ katɔwɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ ɩ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ Yesu maa mu kiwu kyiŋi asɩn. Nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba taasɛ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa gyaga nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ kaapʋ?” Nɩɩ akʋ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “I du fɛɛ ɛɛ kaapʋ akʋ ɩdakpa asɩn nɩn.”

english_verse: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Pɔɔlɩ taa mʋ kpee Aropago ɔgyaŋɛkpa abɩlɩsa ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ taalɛ tɔwɛ anɛ kɩkaapʋ pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bɩya mʋ?

english_verse: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
translation_verse: Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fʋ bɩya anɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ anɛ asʋ tɔ asɩn pʋpwɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kaapʋ anɛ ɩmʋ kasɛ.”

english_verse: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
translation_verse: Antensi abi yɛ afɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ gaalaagaa ba mɛɛ waa sɛhn tii asɩn pʋpwɛ mʋ kunu sʋ yɛ ɩmʋ katɔwɛ sʋ.

english_verse: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Aropago ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ, tɔwɛ yɛ, “Antensi anyɩn, n wu fɛɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ mʋ, kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fen sun mʋ, fɛn sʋ kensi payɩ fen sun mʋ.

english_verse: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n maa naa kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, n wu atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen muŋa fen sun. Nɩɩ n wu saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛ kyʋrɔɔ kɩmʋ sʋ yɛ, ƖBWAARƖ MƲ NƖƖ AN MAN NYI MƲ MƲ LƐƐ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ fen sun, nɩɩ fen mee nyi mʋ mʋ, mʋ asɩn nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
translation_verse: “Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ dulinyaa yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ. Ɔ mɛɛ kyɩna kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa abaa pɔyɩ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
translation_verse: Ɔ tʋʋ nyɩmɩsa kʋlʋn nɩɩ ɔ bɔla mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba kyɩna dulinyaa ɩsʋwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ kpini. Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla ŋmina yɩla opula mʋ nɩɩ asa ba kyɩna yɛ ɩmʋ saŋa.

english_verse: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ waa ɩnɩmʋ nɩn, lɩɩ fɛɛ ee biti fɛɛ asa ba kɩɩ biti mʋ kɛ ba wu mʋ. Amaa yɛgɛ ɔ man bʋ kata yɛ anɛ tɔ ɔkʋ.

english_verse: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ an sʋ nkpa, nɩɩ an taalɛ an naa yɛgɛ an bʋ tɔ falɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fanɛ ɔlʋn‐bɩɩpʋ kʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Anɛ gbaa anɛ an gyɛ mu‐bii ana nɩn’.

english_verse: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kesintin fɛɛ anɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii ana bɩrɛ mʋ, an man kan fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ keduli du fɛɛ suwa abɛɛ gbiti abɛɛ kubuu nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa taalɛ taa mʋ kanyaasɩn yɛ adawʋ pɔyɩ mʋ fɛɛ ɔdakpa.

english_verse: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
translation_verse: Dɩdaa mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ man taa asa kimennyi waa sɛhn, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɔ sa asa kpini kɔnɔsa katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa.

english_verse: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɛɛ kakɛ yɩla mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ bɔla ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ talɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kesintin ɔkpa sʋ. O kyiŋi kanɩn nyɩmɩsa maŋa lɩɩ lewu tɔ, waa kanɩn kaapʋ sa asa kpini yɛ ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ waa.”

english_verse: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
translation_verse: Ba maa nu Pɔɔlɩ asɛ lɩɩ kelewu kyiŋi asɩn mʋ, akʋ ba waa mʋ ahin‐sɩn, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “An biti fɛɛ fu bwii tɔwɛ anɛ kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ.”

english_verse: When Paul left the council meeting,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ.

english_verse: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anyɩn akʋ ba buu mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii. Bamʋ akʋ atɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Diyonisi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Aropago kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ, yɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Damarisi, yɛ asa mʋ akʋ.

english_verse: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ Antensi kpee Korinto kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa tu Gyiwu obii kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Akwila, nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Pontu kadɛ tɔ, maa mʋ‐ka Prisila. Ba lɩɩ Itali ba Korinto ɩ man pɩɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Owura lala Kiladiyo maa gya Gyiwu abi kpini lɩɩ Roma kadɛ tɔ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kpee ɔ naa kɩɩ bamʋ.

english_verse: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ maa bamʋ kpini ba gyɛ tantɩ awaapʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nɩɩ maa bamʋ ba bʋga waa kusun.

english_verse: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
translation_verse: Kakɛ fʋʋtɛsa kʋmaa nɩmʋ, Pɔɔlɩ ii kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, ɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ ɛɛ sa Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.

english_verse: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Amaa Gyiwu abi ba maa kina asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ba saalɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ fwɩɩ mʋ abaa yɛ mʋ ayaa nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Fanɛ lewu i bwii fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ amu sʋ. Mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ ɩ man gyɛ mɛ asɩn. Lɩɩ ndaga ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, n biti n kpee bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Titusi Gyusitu mʋ asɛ. Ɔ man gyɛ Gyiwu obii, amaa ee sun Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ mʋ kɩkpaara kɩ man bʋ kata yɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ.

english_verse: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
translation_verse: Kirisipu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu gyii. Nɩɩ Korinto abi gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ ba nu abwaarɛsɩn mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii, nɩɩ ba gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee.

english_verse: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ kagyanbwɛ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ, nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Man waa kufuu, man laatɔ, kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ.

english_verse: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
translation_verse: N tii fʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ mata fʋ abɛɛ ɔ waa fʋ kʋtɔ kʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kadɛ kɩnɩmʋ tɔ asa gaalaagaa ba gyɛ mɛ lɛɛ.”

english_verse: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ kyɩna tʋtɔ kesu kʋlʋn yɛ kɩdɩyɛ, ɛɛ kaapʋ abwaarɛsɩn ɛɛ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Galiyo gyɛ Akiya kadɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, Gyiwu abi akʋ ba bʋga kʋsʋ naa tii Pɔɔlɩ, taa mʋ kpee asɩn ogyikpa.

english_verse: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba pwɩɩ mʋ fɛɛ, “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ asa Kabwaarɛ‐sun kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩmaa anɛ nbara mʋ ɩ man gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn.”

english_verse: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ piili yɛ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ, kadɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Galiyo tɔwɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ pwɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ daa ɩ gyɛ alibi akʋ abɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man dɛ ɔkpa nɩn, naafɔɔ n daa nu fanɛ asɛ.

english_verse: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
translation_verse: Amaa ɩ maa gyɛ ntɔwɛ kɩtaasɛ yɛ atɩɩ yɛ fanɛ Gyiwu abi nbara falɛ bɩrɛ, fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan naa gyii asɩn mʋ. Man gyɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ ɔtalɛpʋ.”

english_verse: Then he sent them out of the court.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ gya bamʋ lɩɩ asɩn ogyikpa mʋ.

english_verse: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kɩtaa Sotene mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, dayɩ mu asɩn ogyikpa ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Galiyo mu ansi tɔ. Amaa ɔ man tɔwɛ sɛhn. Pɔɔlɩ naa ɔ naa lɩɩ Korinto naa fʋʋ Antiyoki

english_verse: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kyɩna akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Korinto mʋ asɛ nkɛ nkʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ kpee Siriya. Maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ ee kpee mʋ, Prisila maa Akwila ba buu mʋ kpee. Pɔyɩ kɛ ba faa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ sɩyɛ mu inyini Kenkera kadɛ tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɔ ntan mʋ.

english_verse: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
translation_verse: Ba maa fʋʋ Efeso mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ yɛgɛ Prisila maa Akwila, nɩɩ o kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, kɛ maa Gyiwu abi ba naa gyii Yesu asɩn mʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ.

english_verse: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
translation_verse: Ba maa kʋlɛ mʋ yɛ ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nkɛ nnyɔ kʋ mʋ, nɩɩ o kina.

english_verse: He told them goodbye and said, “If God lets me, I will come back.”
translation_verse: Amaa maa kpee mʋ, ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kyula, n biti n bwii ba.” Nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Efeso bɔla nkyu sʋ kyʋn.

english_verse: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ naa dan Kasariya mʋ, ɔ naa fwaala Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kyaga sʋ kpee Antiyoki.

english_verse: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa gyii nkɛ nkʋ Antiyoki mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ ɔ naa ɛɛ bɔɔ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ o kpee Galati yɛ Firigiya yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ waa bamʋ ɔlʋn tɔ. Apolosi kpee Efeso yɛ Korinto naa tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn

english_verse: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Saŋa maa mʋ, Gyiwu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Apolosi nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mʋ Alekisanda kadɛ tɔ mʋ ba Efeso. Ɔ gyɛ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kinyi katɔwɛ tɔ, nɩɩ o nyi Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ danbɩrasa.

english_verse: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
translation_verse: Ba kaapʋ mʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɔkpa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛgɛ ɛɛ kaapʋ lɩɩ Yesu sʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ. Amaa Gyɔn kekyu gyee nkʋn nɩɩ o nyi.

english_verse: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
translation_verse: Prisila maa Akwila ba maa nu Apolosi maa tɔwɛ Yesu asɩn Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa yɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ mʋ, ba tɩɩ mʋ kpee bamʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, nɩɩ ba naa lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa mʋ kaapʋ mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ tii sʋ.

english_verse: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Apolosi waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti o kpee Akiya mʋ, akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Efeso mʋ ba waa mʋ ɔlʋn tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ naa sa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Akiya mʋ yɛ ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ. Mu kikpe tʋtɔ mʋ, ɩ kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Apolosi yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ gyii biri Gyiwu abi mʋ sakyɔ tɔ. Ɔ taa Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ kaapʋ bamʋ fɛɛ Yesu ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Apolosi bʋ Korinto mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ taa kasɛ sʋ halɩɩ ɔ ba fʋʋ Efeso. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ naa wu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu akʋ.

english_verse: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan maa kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, fan nyɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ?” Nɩɩ ba yɛ, “Ayee, an man pɩɩta nu gbaa yɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kʋ bʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ bɩla taasɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Kanɩn bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee mɔmɔ nɩɩ fan nyɛ?” Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Gyɔn kekyugyee mʋ nɩɩ an nyɛ.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Gyɔn kekyugyee kɩ gyɛ nfɛɛrɛ kɩkyɛɛgɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ nɩn. Kanɩn sʋ Gyɔn tɔwɛ asa yɛ, ba kɔɔlɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mʋ kamaa tɔ ɛɛ ba mʋ gyii, mʋ ɩ gyɛ Yesu.”

english_verse: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Ba maa nu kanɩn mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ gyee bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kekyugyee lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ.

english_verse: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa taa mʋ abaa gyaga bamʋ sʋ mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kɩ ba bɔla bamʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ ntɔwɛ pʋpwɛ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla bamʋ sʋ tɔwɛ asɩn.

english_verse: There were about twelve men in this group.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ ba gyɛ anyɩn kudu‐anyɔ nɩn.

english_verse: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
translation_verse: Ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa mʋ tɔ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ i kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɔgyaŋɛkpa, ɔ yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn gyii.

english_verse: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
translation_verse: Amaa akʋ ba waa nkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ kina, ba man kɔɔlɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ gyii. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn libi yii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɔkpa mʋ sakyɔ maŋa tɔ. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ ɔgyaŋɛkpa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ buu mʋ n‐yɩɩ. Kakɛ kʋmaa nɩmʋ maa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ bee kpee Tiranu ɔkaapʋkpa nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ.

english_verse: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
translation_verse: Ɔ kaapʋ kanɩn nsu nnyɔ nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini ba nu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ.

english_verse: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla Pɔɔlɩ sʋ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa gaa.

english_verse: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa ba taa atɔ fɛɛ bɔrɩdɩba‐bi yɛ ntaa‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ wʋla mata Pɔɔlɩ n‐yɩɩ mʋ, ba taa naa mata alɔpʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya, nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mɔɔ nɩn, kɛ ba lɩɩ.

english_verse: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
translation_verse: Gyiwu abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba naa bɛɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi mʋ, ba waa kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ taa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ fɛɛ ba taalɛ gya nbwɩɩ libi lɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ nbwɩɩ libi yɛ, “Lɩɩ Yesu kɩtɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ sʋ mʋ, n sa fanɛ kɔnɔ yɛ fan lɩɩ.”

english_verse: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
translation_verse: Ɩ gyɛ Gyiwu oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Sikefa mu‐bii anaa anyɩnsa asunɔ ɩ waa kanɩn mʋ.

english_verse: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
translation_verse: Kakaakʋ ba maa kpee ba naa waa kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N nyi Yesu, nɩɩ n bɩla n nyi Pɔɔlɩ, amaa anɩmʋ ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ?”

english_verse: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ kabwɩɩ libi mʋ bʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, tɩyɛ bamʋ sʋ, taalɛ bamʋ kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ dayɩ bamʋ larɩgɛ, nɩɩ ba taa kubolonkpan sɩlɛ lɩɩ kɩkpaara mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Efeso mʋ, ba maa nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, ɩ kɩtaa bamʋ kufuu. Nɩɩ ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ taa sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ mʋ.

english_verse: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ, ba lɛɛ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ alibi mʋ nɩɩ ba waa tɔwɛ sakyɔ tɔ.

english_verse: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ nɩɩ ɩ nyɛ ɔlʋn. Ɔlanba lala tɩyɛ Efeso sʋ

english_verse: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini kamaa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ bɔla lɩɩ Masedoniya yɛ Akiya kɛ o kpee Gyerusalem. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nɩɩ n kan kpee tʋtɔ kamaa mʋ, ɩ dagaa maa bɔɔ Roma gbaa sʋ.”

english_verse: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o sun mʋ akyɛtɔpʋ Timoti maa Erasitu kpee Masedoniya. Nɩɩ mɛɛ mʋ sii Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ gbɛrɛɛ.

english_verse: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɔkpa mʋ sʋ mʋ, tɔɔrɔɔ damantɛ tɩyɛ Efeso kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
translation_verse: Gbiti ɔŋalagɛpʋ kʋ bʋ Efeso tʋtɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Demetirisu. Ɔ sʋ gbiti ɛɛ bɩlɛ ibu‐pipi fɛɛ ɔdakpa kyɩɩsa Atemisi obu. Nɩɩ mʋ yɛ abuupu mʋ kpini ba sʋ kusun mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyɛ kɩmʋ afulee.

english_verse: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Demetirisu tɩɩ abuupu mʋ kpini yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ɩ maa bamʋ kusun kɩ gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn mʋ gyaŋɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɛ tɔɔmaa ana fen nyi fɛɛ an nyɛ anɛ kigyii an lɩɩ kusun nɩmʋ sʋ.

english_verse: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
translation_verse: Yɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fan wu nɩɩ fan nu, fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ nɩmʋ naa ee nyita asa damantɛ nfɛɛrɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ Efeso nfɩɩ nkʋn, amaa Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ naa ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Anyɩrɩpɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa abaa pɔyɩ mʋ, man gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ gbaa kpini‐kpini.’

english_verse: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
translation_verse: Man gyɛ anɛ kusun wʋlɛ nkʋn ii biti i nyita. Amaa anɛ ɔdakpa kyɩɩsa obu yɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ Atemisi mʋ, ee biti o wu. Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ yɛ dulinyaa tɔ kpini, mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ bɛɛ sa ɔdakpa lala nɩmʋ mʋ, ii biti i biliŋi kɩyan.”

english_verse: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, ɩ nyɛ bamʋ agbʋ. Nɩɩ ba piili bee kuusi yɛ, “Anɛ Efeso abi, anɛ ɔdakpa Atemisi mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ɔdakpa lala!”

english_verse: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
translation_verse: Opula tɔ nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ kyaŋɛ ɔlanba. Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba naa kɩtaa Gayusi maa Arisitako mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Masedoniya abi, nɩɩ bee buu Pɔɔlɩ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba bʋga bamʋ kpee asa ɔgyaŋɛkpa.

english_verse: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ daa ee biti fɛɛ o kpee sakyɔ mʋ tɔ, amaa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ ba man sa mʋ ɔkpa.

english_verse: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana gbaa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Asiya abɩlɩsa akʋ ba sa kɔnɔ kʋlɛ mʋ fɛɛ ɔ man waa kɛ o kpee sakyɔ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ be mee nu abaa kasɛ. Akʋ bee kuusi yɛ falɛ, nɩɩ banɩmʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ bamʋ lɛɛ ndee. Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba bʋ tʋtɔ yɛgɛ ba man nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii kpee sʋ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba nin Alekisanda kyʋŋɛ kʋbʋ mu ansi tɔ ɔ yɩlɛ bamʋ ayaa tɔ tɔwɛ sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ nyɛɛ bamʋ kɩbaa yɛ ba laatɔ kɛ ɔ lɛɛ asa mʋ kɔnɔ sa bamʋ.

english_verse: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Amaa asa mʋ ba maa bɩɩ fɛɛ Alekisanda gyɛ Gyiwu obii mʋ, ba kuusi kyɛɛrɛɛ fɛɛ kerefi anyɔ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Anɛ Efeso abi, anɛ ɔdakpa Atemisi mʋ, ɩ gyɛ ɔdakpa lala!”

english_verse: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ ɔwʋlɛ kyanpʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ yɛgɛ ɔlanba mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Efeso abi, ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ nyi fɛɛ anɛ ɔdakpa lala Atemisi, yɛ ɩmʋ kubuu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tɩyɛ mʋ, ɩmʋ kumu ɩ gyɛ Efeso kadɛ nɩmʋ.

english_verse: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ man sʋ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii, fan laatɔ kɛ fan man kpala.

english_verse: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ fan bɩya nfɩɩ falɛ, ba man yuuri ɔdakpa kʋtɔ kʋkʋ, nɩɩ ba man tɔwɛ da anɛ ɔdakpa kyɩɩsa mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Demetirisu maa mʋ tɔɔmaa ana be nyi fɛɛ ba sʋ asɩn akʋ ba pwɩɩ bamʋ bɩrɛ, an sʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ an gyii asɩn kɩbanɛ abɩlɩsa ansi tɔ. Kanɩn sʋ bɛɛ taalɛ ba pwɩɩ bamʋ asɩn ogyikpa tʋtɔ.

english_verse: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan san fan sʋ asɩn akʋ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fan naa gyii fanɛ asɩn ogyikpa fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ an man bɩɩ mʋ, an biti an nyɛ ndaga ɔlanba nɩmʋ kɩpwɩɩ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ nɩɩ ba naa taasɛ anɛ ɩmʋ asɩn mʋ, an man sʋ asɩn akʋ nɩɩ an taalɛ taa kaapʋ tɔ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔlanba mʋ waa.”

english_verse: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
translation_verse: Ɔlanba mʋ kamaa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ sun tɩɩ Krisito akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ waa bamʋ, kala bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Masedoniya.

english_verse: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
translation_verse: Ɔ natɛ tʋtɔ kyaabɔɔ, ɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ waa asa, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Akiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kyɩna tʋtɔ ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa. Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ee biti o kpee Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi ba ŋmina asɩn lɩɩ mʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ bwii kamaa naa bɔla lɩɩ Masedoniya.

english_verse: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ kpee ɩ gyɛ Piru mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Bereya mu‐bii‐nyɩnsa Sofata, maa Arisitako maa Sekundu mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Tesalonika, yɛ Gayusi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Debe, yɛ Timoti, taa tii Tikiku maa Tirofimu mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
translation_verse: Asa anɩmʋ ɩ gyankpaa naa gyoo anɛ Trowa kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
translation_verse: Amaa Kesu kɔnɔ kɔlɔŋɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kɩfɛ tinsa kamaa tɔ mʋ, an bɔla nkyu sʋ lɩɩ Filipi. Lɩɩ nkɛ nnuu kamaa mʋ, an naa tu bamʋ Trowa. Nɩɩ an gyii nkɛ nsunɔ tʋtɔ. Pɔɔlɩ kyiŋi Yutiku ba nkpa tɔ

english_verse: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
translation_verse: Nkɛ nsunɔ tɔ kakɛ gyankpaasa mʋ, an ba gyaŋɛ kɛ an gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ agyitɔ mʋ. Pɔɔlɩ maa biti fɛɛ lɩɩkaakɛ ɔ lɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ asɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ naa fʋʋ halɩɩ kiyiridi nsana.

english_verse: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
translation_verse: Ɩfɩtɩla ɩ kyɔ abansʋrʋ obu mʋ nɩɩ an gyaŋɛ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩyaafɔlɛ kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Yutiku. Ɔ tɛ nfansɩrɛ sʋ ee nu asɩn mʋ yɛgɛ ɛɛ gyɔɔsɛ. Kpalɩ fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ maa tɔwɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ taa dɛ nsɩɩrɛ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ abansʋrʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyan sʋ kɩsasapʋ mʋ sʋ ba tɩyɛ kasɛ, nɩɩ o wu.

english_verse: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kpɩlɩgɛ ba mʋ asɛ, ba bun mʋ sʋ, taa mʋ abaa kyaabɔɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan man yɛgɛ ɩ tɔɔrɔɔ fanɛ, ɔ san ɛɛ fʋʋtɛ.”

english_verse: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ bwii dii kpee abansʋrʋ mʋ sʋ, naa kpɛɛ bodobodo wɛ. Nɩɩ o bwii kyaga mʋ katɔwɛ sʋ naa fʋʋ kayɩkɛ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kyʋn.

english_verse: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba taa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ san ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ kpee kewu. Nɩɩ asa mʋ nkɔlɔ i fuuli bamʋ. Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ Trowa kpee Miletu

english_verse: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
translation_verse: An gyankpaa naa taa ɔkʋlɩ lala fɛɛ pantuu bɔla nkyu sʋ kpee Asosi. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ an kpee a naa taa Pɔɔlɩ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ taa ayaa tɔ naa fʋʋ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
translation_verse: An naa mʋ a naa gyaŋɛ Asosi mʋ, an taa mʋ, nɩɩ an ba Mitilene.

english_verse: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, an lɩɩ tʋtɔ bɔla nkyu sʋ ba Kiyo. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ kayɩ maa bɩla kɛ mʋ, an faa nkyu kpee Samosi, nɩɩ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ, an naa fʋʋ Miletu.

english_verse: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɩlɛ Efeso, kɛ ɔ man nyɛ ee nyita saŋa Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ee biti bilen, fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔ taalɛ mʋ, o loo Gyerusalem Pentekosi kensi‐gyii kakɛ. Pɔɔlɩ kala Efeso Krisito abi kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa

english_verse: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an fʋʋ Miletu mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ sun tɩɩ Efeso Krisito abi kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ.

english_verse: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
translation_verse: Ba maa ba mʋ asɛ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fen nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ n kyɩna yɛ fanɛ, lɩɩ kakɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ n ba fanɛ asɛ Asiya ofuli sʋ nfɩɩ.

english_verse: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n bɩya mɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ taa ansi‐kyu, yɛ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ n gyii lɩɩ ɩkpa libi mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba biti mɛ mʋ sʋ kpini sun Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kusun.

english_verse: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan nyi fɛɛ n man taa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ mʋ baala fanɛ sʋ. Amaa n tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn nɩɩ n kaapʋ fanɛ alandɛ sʋ yɛ akpaara sʋ.

english_verse: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: N lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kesintin tɔwɛ Gyiwu abi yɛ Giriki abi fɛɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ, kɛ ba bwii ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu gyii.

english_verse: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
translation_verse: “Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, ɩ fʋʋ fɛɛ n kpee Gyerusalem fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔwɛ waa mɛ mʋ, amaa n mee nyi kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ki biti kɩ waa mɛ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n nyi ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, kadɛ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n kpee mʋ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩ da mi kumu tɔ fɛɛ awʋrʋfɔ yɛ obu kitii ɩ dɛ i gyoo mɛ.

english_verse: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Amaa man taa mɛ nkpa tɩɩ sɛhn, nɩɩ n mɛɛ kɩɩ mɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ n gyɛ kʋtɔ kʋ. Amɔɔ n kyaga mɛ kɩnatɛ sʋ, kɛ n waa kusun mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu taa sa mɛ mʋ lʋwɛ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ n lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala Asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ kesintin kaapʋ.

english_verse: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ n nyi fɛɛ fanɛ tɔ bamʋ nɩɩ n natɛ tɔwɛ sʋsʋ kuwura‐gyii asɩn sa mʋ, fanɛ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o wu mi ansi tɔ.

english_verse: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ndaga, n lɛɛ n tɔwɛ fanɛ kesintin yɛ nɩɩ fanɛ ɔkʋ kan ɔ mɛɛ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ, ɩ man lɩɩ mɛ asɛ.

english_verse: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ n man taa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn kʋ baala fanɛ sʋ, amaa n lɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔwɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɩɩ kɩɩ mʋ nbʋlʋpʋ sʋ mʋ, fan kɩɩ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ taa waa fanɛ abaa tɔ, yɛ fan kɩɩ sʋ mʋ danbɩrasa. Fan bɩla Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa nkalan sɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nyi fɛɛ nɩɩ n kan kpee kamaa tɔ mʋ, akuntun bee biti ba be loo fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba yɛgɛ nbʋlʋpʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kɩɩ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ danbɩrasa, kɛ fan nyiŋi fɛɛ nsu nsa mʋ nɩɩ n maa fanɛ an kyɩna mʋ, n sʋ ansikyu n da fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kumu tɔ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: “Nbɩɩnbɩɩ n sʋ fanɛ n waa Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbaa tɔ yɛ mʋ kabwaala asɩn tɔ. Mʋ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fan dan, kɛ ɔ sa fanɛ mu kusee yɛ mʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɔŋɔ yɩla kpini.

english_verse: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mi ansi ɩ man payɩ ɔkʋ suwa abɛɛ mu gbiti abɛɛ mʋ kaalɛ sʋ.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fan nyi fɛɛ mɛ abaa nɩmʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n sun n nyɛ maa mɛ tɔɔmaa ana atɔ mʋ nɩɩ i tiri anɛ.

english_verse: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ kʋmaa tɔ mʋ, n kaapʋ fanɛ fɛɛ ɩ dagaa fan waa kusun ɔlʋn sʋ kanɩn kyɛ atiripu tɔ. Kɛ fan nyiŋi Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ yɛ, ‘Kakɛɛ sʋ kusee kɩ kyɔ kɩkɔɔlɛ.’ ”

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn lʋwɛ mʋ, maa bamʋ kpini ba muŋa amulii sʋ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

english_verse: Everyone cried and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba taa ansikyu biri mʋ tɔ fwaala mʋ.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ku duŋi bamʋ ɩbʋlɛ tɔ gaa mʋ, ɩ gyɛ maa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ba man bɩla yɛ ba wu abaa. Nɩɩ ba sun mʋ ɔkpa kpee ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ ku du fɛɛ pantuu mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an barɩgɛ tɔ mʋ, an yii sʋ bɔla ɔpʋ sʋ kpee Kosi kadɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, an kpee Rodo kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ an lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Patara kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ an nyɛ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kii kpee Fonesiya ofuli sʋ, nɩɩ an loo kɩmʋ tɔ naa kyʋn.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: Anɛ ansi ɩ maa kɩɩ Kiporo kifintin mʋ, nɩɩ an bwii anɛ bɩna sʋ naa fʋʋ Siriya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ an yɩlɛ Trowa kadɛ tɔ, tʋtɔ maa nɩɩ bee biti bɛ lɛɛ ɩsʋla ɔkʋlɩ maa tɔ.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Amaa anɛ saŋa maa fʋʋ mʋ, nɩɩ an bwii kyaga anɛ ɔkpa sʋ. Nɩɩ ba maa bamʋ‐ka ana yɛ bamʋ‐bii ana kpini ba sun anɛ ɔkpa lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ ba kebunɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ an muŋa kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ.

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an kala abaa lʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ an loo ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba bwii kyʋn kewu.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: An kyaga anɛ ɔkpa sʋ lɩɩ Tayi naa fʋʋ Polimesi. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ an fwaala akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ, nɩɩ an kyɩna bamʋ asɛ kakɛ kʋlʋn.

english_verse: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
translation_verse: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, an kpee Kasariya naa loo Filipu mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ abwaarɛsɩn ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ ayɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ gyɛ asa asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba kyɩna lɛɛ bamʋ Gyerusalem yɛ ba barɩgɛ agyitɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an san an bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ Ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Agabu lɩɩ Gyudeya ba.

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Maa ba anɛ asɛ mʋ, ɔ taa Pɔɔlɩ kɩrɩsʋ mʋ ŋmina mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ abaa yɛ mʋ ayaa. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Falɛ maa nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, bee biti ba ŋmina kɩrɩsʋ nɩmʋ wuya, kɛ ba taa mʋ waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ abaa tɔ.’ ”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Amaa an maa nu falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ mʋ, anaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Kasariya kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, an kʋlɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ ɔ man kpee Gyerusalem.

english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fen su, nɩɩ fɛn yaagɛ mɛ kɔkɔlɔ kanɩn? Man gyɛ kiŋmina mʋ nkʋn, amaa n kʋʋla mɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ n wu Gyerusalem lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ mʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: A maa bɩɩ fɛɛ an pʋpɔlɛ mʋ kpɩnɛ mʋ, nɩɩ an tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ kebiti nɩn kɩ waa.”

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ an kʋsʋ kʋʋla kpee Gyerusalem.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Kasariya mʋ akʋ ba sun anɛ kpee Gyerusalem. Nɩɩ ba taa anɛ kpee Kasiporo‐nyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Manson mʋ ayɛ nɩɩ a naa kyɩna tʋtɔ. Ɔ gyɛ akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu agyankpaapʋ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ. Pɔɔlɩ kpee Gyemisi asɛ

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: “Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an fʋʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, anɛ tɔɔmaa ana ba taa kensi‐gyii kʋlɛ anɛ.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, anaa Pɔɔlɩ an kpee a naa wu Gyemisi, nɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ gbaa ba bʋ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ fwaala bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ kalɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɔla mu kusun sʋ waa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ kaapʋ bamʋ kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋkʋlʋn.”

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Ba maa nu falɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, “Anɛ bɩrɩsa, yɛ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ, fu wu kanan mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ngbɩn‐ngbɩn ba kɔɔlɛ gyii ba, nɩɩ ba bɩla ba sʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ tɔ kenken.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Gyiwu abi akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyerusalem nfɩɩ mʋ, ba nu yɛ fɩɩ kaapʋ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ndɛndɛmandɛ sʋ mʋ, yɛ ba man buu Mosesi nbara kɛ ba man tɩŋɛ bamʋ‐bii ana iduu, kɛ ba yɛgɛ Gyiwu abi anaana tɔ mʋ kpini.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ nɩɩ an biti an waa? Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ waa nɛnɛ bee biti ba nu fɛɛ fʋ ba.

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ anɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ an sa fʋ ndee. Asa ana akʋ ba bʋ anɛ asɛ nfɩɩ nɩɩ ba bɔ ntan sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Taa bamʋ kɛ faa bamʋ fan kpee, naa lɛɛ fanɛ ineesi, kɛ fʋ ka bamʋ amu su akɔ sa bamʋ, kɛ ba sɩyɛ bamʋ amu. Kɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ bɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ lɩɩ fʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩ man gyɛ kesintin. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ, fu buu Mosesi nbara mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Amaa akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ bɩrɛ, an tɔwɛ kyula nɩɩ an kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ naa sa bamʋ yɛ, ba man gyii agyitɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba dɩla sa ɔdakpa abɛɛ nkalan kʋmaa, kɛ ba man gyii atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ba man tɩn bamʋ amu nɩɩ ba wu kibelinboni, kɛ ba lɛɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ kakyɩɩ abɛɛ kanyɩn‐biti tɔ.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ taa asa ana mʋ tii mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ nɩɩ ba naa fwɩɩ bamʋ amu tɔ. Nɩɩ o kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ naa yɩla nkɛ mʋ nɩɩ amu tɔ kɩfwɩɩ mʋ ii biti ɩ lʋwɛ, yɛ saraga mʋ nɩɩ bee biti bɛ lɛɛ sa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ. Ba kɩtaa Pɔɔlɩ kabwaarɛ‐sun obu lala tɔ

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: Nkɛ nsunɔ mʋ ɩ maa san gbɛrɛɛ kɛ ɩ lʋwɛ mʋ, Gyiwu abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Asiya kɩbaafʋn kʋ sʋ mʋ, ba maa wu Pɔɔlɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba fun waa sakyɔ mʋ lɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ sʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa mʋ.

english_verse: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bee kuusi bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩsɩrayɩ abi fan kyɛ tɔ. Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ naa katɩn kʋmaa, ɛɛ kaapʋ asa kpini, yɛgɛ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ee yii anɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ anɛ nbara mʋ yɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɩla i tii sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɔ taa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi ba anɛ obu kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ lala nɩmʋ tɔ ba waa kɩmʋ ineesi.”

english_verse: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
translation_verse: Ba maa wʋla wu Efeso ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Tirofimu maa Pɔɔlɩ ba maa naa kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ taa mʋ kpee kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ kpini asa ba maa nu Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ, ba sɩlɛ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ abaafʋn kʋmaa ba. Ba kɩtaa mʋ bɩɩtɛ lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, opula tɔ nɩɩ ba tii nbunɔ mʋ.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Ba maa dayɩ Pɔɔlɩ bee biti mʋ kɩmɔɔ mʋ, asɩn mʋ a naa tu Roma abi anaakɔpʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa fɛɛ ɩlawʋ ɩ kʋsʋ Gyerusalem kadɛ mʋ kpini tɔ.

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: Ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ taa mʋ kamaa tɔ abi yɛ anaakɔpʋ mʋ akʋ kpee sakyɔ mʋ tɔ. Amaa asa mʋ ba maa wu ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ anaakɔpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ Pɔɔlɩ kɩdayɛ.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ kpee naa kɩtaa Pɔɔlɩ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba taa agbeeleebi anyɔ ŋmina mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ asa mʋ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ nɩn, nɩɩ mɩnɛ nɩɩ ɔ waa?”

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ akʋ ba yɛ falɛ, nɩɩ banɩmʋ mɔɔ ba yɛ bamʋ lɛɛ ndee. Bamʋ nnɔ ɩ maa kwɩɩ‐kwɩɩ sʋ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ man nu asɩn mʋ kasɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ kpee anaakɔpʋ opula.

english_verse: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
translation_verse: Ba maa taa Pɔɔlɩ naa fʋʋ anaakɔpʋ opula mʋ, nɩɩ bee biti ba dii loo tʋtɔ mʋ, lɩɩ asa mʋ ɔlanba sʋ mʋ, anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba kukuri Pɔɔlɩ kɛ asa mʋ ba man nyɛ dayɩ mʋ.

english_verse: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Nɩɩ sakyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba buu bamʋ mʋ, bee kuusi yɛ, “Fan mɔɔ mʋ.” Pɔɔlɩ kɔnɔ lɛɛ sa asa mʋ

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ba sʋ Pɔɔlɩ bee loo tʋtɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, “Naafɔɔ n biti fɛɛ n tɔwɛ fʋ asɛ.” Nɩɩ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fii nu Giriki katɔwɛ?”

english_verse: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ayee, n gyɛ Gyiwu obii nɩn, nɩɩ n lɩɩ Taasu kadɛ kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Silisiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ asa be nyi kɩmʋ mʋ. N kʋlɛ fʋ nɩn, yɛgɛ n tɔwɛ asa mʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: Ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ maa sa mʋ ɔkpa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ obu adenbi diisa sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ nyɛɛ bamʋ kɩbaa yɛ ba laatɔ. Ba maa laatɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ Gyiwu abi katɔwɛ.

english_verse: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nsɛ ana yɛ mɛ‐daa ana, fan nu mɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n biti n lɛɛ kɔnɔ fanɛ asɛ falɛ!”

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba maa nu fɛɛ Gyiwu abi katɔwɛ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ bamʋ asɛ mʋ, bamʋ kpini ba laatɔ diin. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “N gyɛ Gyiwu obii nɩn, nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ mɛ Taasu, Silisiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Amaa ba bɩla mɛ Gyerusalem kadɛ lala nɩmʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ Gamaliye ɩ kaapʋ mɛ anɛ naana ana nbara mʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ. Nɩɩ n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ n sun Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ tɔ falɛ.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nyanla bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu Yesu ɔkpa mʋ naa fʋʋ bamʋ lewu tɔ. Nɩɩ n kɩtaa anyɩn yɛ akyɩɩ gbaa naa tii obu.

english_verse: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ yɛ Gyiwu kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ gbaa bɛɛ taalɛ gyii falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kesintin. Nɩɩ n bɩla n nyɛ ɩwʋlɛ n lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ fɛɛ n taa naa sa bamʋ bɩrɩsa ana Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Damasiko kadɛ tɔ. Kɛ ɩ sa mɛ ɔkpa n naa kɩtaa asa anɩmʋ, ŋmina bamʋ taa bamʋ ba Gyerusalem kɛ ba bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ. Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ gyii

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: “N maa naa n kpee nɩɩ n naa tiri Damasiko kakyʋwapɛ asɛ mʋ, opula tɔ nɩɩ ɩ ŋalɩgɛ mi ansi sʋ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ mʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kyaabɔɔ mɛ.

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n lɩɩ tɩyɛ kasɛ nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa taasɛ mɛ yɛ, ‘Sɔɔlɩ! Sɔɔlɩ! Mɩnɛ waa nɩɩ fɩɩ nyanla mɛ kanɩn?’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ n taasɛ yɛ, ‘Fʋ anɩmʋ nɩn, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ?’ Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, ‘Mɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Ganasareti‐nyɩn nɩɩ fɩɩ nyanla mʋ nɩn.’

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mɛ asɛ mʋ, ba wu ɩŋalɩgɛsa mʋ, amaa ba man bɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ tɔwɛ mɛ asɛ mʋ.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ n taasɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ n waa?’ “Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, ‘Kʋsʋ, kɛ fu kpee Damasiko. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ ba tɔwɛ fʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ fʋ waa.’

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ ŋalɩgɛ mi ansi sʋ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ n biliŋi gyaatanapʋ. Nɩɩ mɛ bɩrɩsa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mɛ asɛ mʋ ba kɩtaa mɛ kɩbaa tɔ kpee Damasiko.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Ananiyasi. Nɩɩ ee sun Ɩbwaarɛ ee buu Gyiwu abi nbara mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sʋ, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ tʋtɔ mʋ, ba sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ ba sa mʋ.

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ ba mɛ asɛ ba yɩlɛ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, ‘Mɛ bɩrɩsa Sɔɔlɩ, fu ansi i bugi.’ Nɩɩ opula tɔ nɩɩ mi ansi a bugi nɩɩ n wu mʋ.

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: “Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, ‘Anɛ naana ana Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ lɛɛ fʋ yɩla fɛɛ fʋ bɩɩ mu kebiti, kɛ fu wu mʋ kɩyaafɔlɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ Yesu mʋ, kɛ fu nu mʋ asɩn mʋ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ.

english_verse: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
translation_verse: “Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n bwii kpee Gyerusalem nɩɩ n kpee n kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, n nyɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ.

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, ‘Waa bilen lɩɩ Gyerusalem, lɩɩ fɛɛ fu kesintin asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fii biti fu gyii lɩɩ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ mʋ, ba mɛɛ yɛ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii.’

english_verse: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa be nyi kanan mʋ nɩɩ n loo bamʋ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛkpa ibu ana tɔ, n dayɩ yɛgɛ n kɩtaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ fu gyii mʋ n kpee n tii obu.

english_verse: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ mɔɔ fʋ kesintin ogyipu Sitefen mʋ, n yɩlɛ tʋtɔ kyula mu kelewu mʋ, nɩɩ mɛ ɩ kɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ mʋ ɩkaalɛ sʋ.

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “natɛ, n biti n sun fʋ lɩɩ nfɩɩ kpee kata‐kata bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ asɛ”.’ ”

english_verse: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba nu Pɔɔlɩ katɔwɛ naa fʋʋ mʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ falɛ sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba diyaa bamʋ ɩbʋlɛ kuusi yɛ, “Fan lɛɛ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ, ɔ man dagaa maa bɩla kyɩna nkpa tɔ.”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bee kuusi bɛɛ lɛɛ bamʋ ɩkaalɛ bee fugiti, yɛgɛ bɛɛ waa apurutiti mʋ,

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ loo bamʋ kɩkpaara sʋ. Kɛ ba tɩŋɛ mʋ taasɛ mʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa, nɩɩ bee kuusi bɛɛ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ sʋ kanɩn.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba ŋmina Pɔɔlɩ kɛ ba bun mʋ tɩŋɛ mʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa sʋ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩ dɛ ɔkpa fɛɛ ba tɩŋɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ Roma obii ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ fɛɛ odibii, nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta gyii mʋ asɩn kɛ ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ?”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ naa taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, “Tɔwɛ mɛ, Roma abi ba kɔɔlɛ fʋ fɛɛ odibii?” Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɛɛhn, n gyɛ.”

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, “N nyita afulee gaa pɔyɩ nɩɩ Roma abi ba kɔɔlɛ mɛ fɛɛ odibii.” Amaa Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mɛɛ mɛ bɩrɛ ba kʋʋgɛ mɛ nɩn fɛɛ Roma odibii.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba daa bee biti fɛɛ ba taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ, ba bwii kamaa bilen. Nɩɩ ɩ waa anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ gbaa kufuu fɛɛ maa yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba ŋmina Pɔɔlɩ yɛgɛ ɔ gyɛ Roma obii. Pɔɔlɩ yɩlɛ Gyiwu abi kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mu ansi tɔ

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: Anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ maa biti o wu kʋtɔ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ dan waa sʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba saŋɛ Pɔɔlɩ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ Gyiwu abi aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ bamʋ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba sarɛ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ bɩya Pɔɔlɩ ba yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ.

english_verse: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kɩɩ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ kyimm, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ tɔɔmaa ana, n nyi mɛ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ fɛɛ lɩɩ mi kusun mʋ nɩɩ n sun sa Ɩbwaarɛ ba fʋʋ ndaga, ɩ bʋrɔn Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.”

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: Opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ Ananiyasi mʋ, sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tiri Pɔɔlɩ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, ba da mʋ kɔnɔ sʋ.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Kɩda nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ da fʋ gbaa fʋ, fʋ kebunbun ansi wuya. Fʋ kyɩna tʋtɔ bɔla nbara sʋ talɛ mɛ, amaa yɛgɛ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ, fʋ fɔyɩ nbara, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba da mɛ kɔnɔ sʋ.”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tiri Pɔɔlɩ mʋ, ba taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ nɩɩ fʋ saalɛ falɛ?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Mɛ tɔmaa ana, n man nyi fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ nɩn. Kpalɩ fɛɛ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla yɛ, ‘Man tɔwɛ asɩn libi gyaga ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ fʋ asa sʋ.’ ”

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa ba bɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ akʋ ba gyɛ Sadusi nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba gyɛ Farasii abi mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ tɔmaa ana, ngbaa mɛ n gyɛ Farasii nɩn, mɛ‐sɛ gbaa gyɛ Farasii obii nɩn. Kelewu kyiŋi tamaa mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ sʋ, nɩɩ bee gyii mɛ asɩn falɛ.”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: Maa tɔwɛ kanɩn asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɩmɔɔrɛ ɩ tɩyɛ Sadusi yɛ Farasii abi mʋ nsana. Nɩɩ kʋbʋ mʋ kɩ barɩgɛ tɔ anyɔ.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔlanba mʋ waa ɔlʋn. Nɩɩ nbara akaapʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ Farasii abi mʋ, ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bee gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ ɔlʋn sʋ yɛ, “An man wu alibi akʋ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ sʋ. Akʋtɔ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu abɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ ɩ tɔwɛ mʋ asɛ.”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔlanba mʋ nyɛ akyɔ gaa mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, fɛɛ bɛ kan bɩɩtɛ Pɔɔlɩ kyatɛ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ sa mʋ anaakɔpʋ kɔnɔ fɛɛ, ba kpɩlɩgɛ loo sakyɔ mʋ tɔ naa gbebi Pɔɔlɩ kɔɔlɛ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ, kɛ ba taa mʋ kpee anaakɔpʋ opula.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Lɩɩkaakɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu ba yɩlɛ mʋ asɛ, tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ! Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ lɛɛ mɛ kesintin mʋ kaapʋ Gyerusalem mʋ, kanɩn maa nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ bɩla lɛɛ mɛ asɩn mʋ kaapʋ Roma kadɛ gbaa.” Ba ŋmina fɛɛ ba mɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kpee bamʋ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “An bɔ ntan yɛ an man dan mɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ, an mɛɛ yɛ i gyii.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nbɩɩnbɩɩ fʋ yɛ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ, fen sun naa tɔwɛ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ, o bwiiyaa Pɔɔlɩ ba fanɛ ansi tɔ. Kɛ fan waa fɛɛ fen biti fan lɔŋɔ nu mʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ danbɩrasa. Ɩmʋ sʋ an kʋʋla anɛ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ, nɩɩ ba kan ba sʋ mʋ bɛɛ ba mʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ ɔ fʋʋ nfɩɩ mʋ an biti a mɔɔ mʋ ɔkpa tɔ.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ kawʋpa‐bii maa nu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba ŋmina mʋ, nɩɩ o kpee naa loo anaakɔpʋ mʋ ayɛ, naa tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɩɩ anaakɔpʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ ɔkʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Taa kɩyaafɔlɛ nɩmʋ kpee fanɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa asɛ. Ɔ sʋ asɩn kparɛ kʋ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ taa mʋ kpee ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Pɔɔlɩ mʋ nɩɩ an taa ba tii obu mʋ, ɩ tɩɩ mɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ n taa kɩyaafɔlɛ nɩmʋ ba fʋ asɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sʋ asɩn akʋ ɔ tɔwɛ fʋ.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Anaakɔpʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, kɩtaa kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ lɩɩ ɩkaa sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ mʋ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ fʋ sʋ fʋ tɔwɛ mɛ?”

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kawʋpa‐bii mʋ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa mʋ ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ, bee biti ba kʋlɛ fʋ fɛɛ fʋ taa Pɔɔlɩ ba kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ ɔkɛ. Kɛ ba pɩna fʋ yɛ bee biti ba lɔŋɔ nu mʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ danbɩrasa.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa da kɩyaafɔlɛ mu kumu yɛ, “Man kan tɔwɛ ɔkʋ fɛɛ fʋ tɛɛ ba tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ sa mɛ.” Nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mʋ ɔkpa. Ba taa Pɔɔlɩ kpee Kasariya

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
translation_verse: Fan sa Pɔɔlɩ gbaa ɩgbaŋɛ akʋ, kɛ fan kɩɩ mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Felisi asɛ.”

english_verse: The commander wrote a letter that said:
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ naa sa Felisi. Ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
