translation_verse: “Gyiwu abi akʋ ɩ kɩtaa ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ yɛ bee biti ba mɔɔ mʋ. N maa bɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ gyɛ Roma odibii mʋ, nɩɩ maa mɛ ayaafɔlɛ an kɔɔlɛ mʋ yɛgɛ.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n maa biti n bɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa nɩɩ bɛɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, n taa mʋ ba bamʋ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: N bɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛ pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ bamʋ Gyiwu abi nbara asɩn nɩn. Amaa n man wu alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ abɛɛ ba tii mʋ obu.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: N maa nu fɛɛ Gyiwu abi akʋ ba ŋmina fɛɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ mʋ, n waa bilen taa mʋ bɩya fʋ. Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ mʋ apwɩɩpʋ mʋ yɛ ba ba pwɩɩ mʋ fu ansi tɔ.”

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba waa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba sa bamʋ kɔnɔ mʋ. Kagyanbwɛ maŋa nɩɩ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ kpee naa loo Antipatirisi.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba naa ayaa tɔ mʋ, ba bwii kpee bamʋ anaakɔpʋ opula. Nɩɩ ɩgbɛŋɛ adiipu mʋ ba taa mʋ kpee Kasariya.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba maa fʋʋ Kasariya mʋ, ba taa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ sa Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ waa mʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Maa kalɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ sʋ. Maa bɩɩ fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ lɩɩ Silisiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ,

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, “Nɩɩ fʋ apwɩɩpʋ ba kan ba mʋ, n biti n nu fʋ asɛ.” Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ fɛɛ ba naa kɩɩ mʋ sʋ Owura lala Herodi lenbu tɔ.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa fʋʋ Kasariya nkɛ nnuu kamaa tɔ mʋ, oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ Ananiyasi yɛ abɩlɩsa akʋ yɛ lɔya mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Tetulu ba ba. Nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ asɩn pwɩɩsa ba pwɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Felisi ansi tɔ.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ ba loo tʋtɔ mʋ, nɩɩ Tetulu taa asɩn tʋʋ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Felisi ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Ɔsʋwʋlɛ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ kparɛ, fʋ ɔsʋwʋlɛ kɩkɩta danbɩrasa sʋ mʋ, an nyɛ kayɩɩ yuuli gaa, nɩɩ lɩɩ fʋ kanyaasɩn sʋ mʋ, fʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ atɔ damantɛ a lɔŋɔ sa anɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ nɩmʋ sʋ.

english_verse: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
translation_verse: Katɩn kʋmaa yɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, an taa kensi‐gyii kɔɔlɛ fʋ awaasa mʋ, nɩɩ an fwaala fʋ gaa.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Amaa kanan mʋ nɩɩ n man nyita fʋ saŋa sʋ mʋ, n kʋlɛ fʋ fɛɛ fu nu anɛ asɛ gbɛrɛɛ lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ asɩn sʋ.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: An wu ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ maa gyɛ alibi wuya, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii ɛɛ kɔla Gyiwu abi dulinyaa tɔ kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ gyɛ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Nasarene mʋ ɔgyankpaapʋ.

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ mʋ apwɩɩpʋ ba ba fu ansi tɔ.] Nɩɩ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ tʋʋ mʋ tɔ kɩɩ mʋ, fii biti fu wu fɛɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an taa an pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin.”

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ba yɩlɛ kyula yɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, kpini ɩ bʋ tɔ kanɩn. Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ Felisi ansi tɔ

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ maa sa Pɔɔlɩ ɔkpa yɛ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “N nyi fɛɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ asɩn ogyipu danbɩrasa lɩɩ Gyiwu ɔsʋwʋlɛ nɩmʋ sʋ nsu damantɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ waa mɛ ɔkɔn fɛɛ fu ansi tɔ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ mɛ asɩn.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Fɩɩ taalɛ taasɛ kɛ fu wu fɛɛ ɩ waa fɛɛ nkɛ kudu anyɔ nɩmʋ nɩɩ n kpee Gyerusalem n naa sun Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mɛ apwɩɩpʋ banɩmʋ ba man wu n maa gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, abɛɛ n maa fun n waa asa kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, kadɛ mʋ tɔ katɩn kʋ.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba mɛɛ taalɛ kaapʋ tɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩn gyaga mɛ sʋ falɛ, fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Amaa n kyula fɛɛ Yesu Ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ba kɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ gyɛ alibi ɔkpa mʋ, kɩmʋ kɩkaapʋ nɩɩ n sʋ n sun anɛ naana ana Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Mosesi nbara ɩwʋlɛ tɔ, yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Atɔwɛpʋ ɩwʋlɛ tɔ mʋ n kɔɔlɛ ɩmʋ kpini gyii.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n kpan mɛ n‐yɩɩ, n waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ nyɩmɩsa asɛ.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: “N maa lɩɩ Gyerusalem nsu damantɛ kamaa mʋ, n bwii kpee tʋtɔ, taa afulee ba yɛ sa mɛ asa, kɛ n taa kɛkɛɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: N maa dan lʋwɛ mɛ ineesi kɩfwɩɩ mʋ, nɩɩ n waa ɩnɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ mɛ apwɩɩpʋ mʋ ba wu mɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ. Amaa ba man wu ɔkʋ mɛ asɛ, nɩɩ n man waa ɩlawʋ ɩkʋ.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Amaa Gyiwu abi akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Asiya kɩbaafʋn ɩ kyɩna ba bʋ tʋtɔ. Nɩɩ naafɔɔ n waa alibi akʋ mʋ, bamʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba ba pwɩɩ mɛ fu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Abɛɛ yɛgɛ asa anɩmʋ ba tɔwɛ alibi mʋ nɩɩ ba wu lɩɩ mɛ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kpee naa yɩlɛ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ abɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ mʋ.

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n yɩlɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ mʋ, kʋtɔ kʋlʋn nkʋn nɩɩ n kuusi tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ‘Kelewu kyiŋi asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ nɩɩ ndaga ba pwɩɩ mɛ fu ansi tɔ falɛ.’ ”

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Felisi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla o nyi Yesu Ɔkpa asɩn mʋ sʋ mʋ, o nin asɩn mʋ naa yɩla kakɛ pʋpwɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Anaakɔpʋ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Lisiya kan ba mʋ, n biti n kɩɩ fʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ.”

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ sa anaakɔpʋ mʋ ɔbɩlɩsa kɔnɔ yɛ, ɔ taa Pɔɔlɩ naa kɩɩ mʋ sʋ danbɩrasa. Kɛ ɔ man yɛgɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩ nyan mʋ, kɛ ɔ sa ɔkpa kɛ mʋ asa ba ba bɔɔ mʋ sʋ kɛ ba kyɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Nkɛ kalɩsa nkʋ kamaa mʋ, Felisi maa mʋ‐ka Durusila mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu‐kyɩɩ mʋ ba ba. Nɩɩ o sun tɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ Yesu Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ asɩn sa mʋ.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa máá sʋ ɛɛ tɔwɛ nbara kyiigisa asɩn yɛ n‐yɩɩ kɩkɩta yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ katalɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba mʋ, ɩ waa Felisi kufuu. Nɩɩ ɔ yɛ, “Ɩ kʋkwɛ kanɩn, natɛ. Nɩɩ n kan nyɛ saŋa mʋ n biti n tɩɩ fʋ.”

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ yɛgɛ ɛɛ waa tamaa fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ ii biti ɔ sa mu afulee kɛ ɔ yɛgɛ mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ, ee sun ɛɛ tɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ nɩɩ bee biri aligyima.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Nsu nyɔ kamaa mʋ ba lɛɛ Felisi nɩɩ ba taa Pokiyu Fesitu ba yii mʋ ayaa tɔ. Amaa Felisi maa biti fɛɛ ɔ waa kʋtɔ kʋ bɔla Gyiwu abi ansi mʋ, ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ san o tii obu.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Fesitu maa ba Kasariya fɛɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Gyerusalem.

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: Nɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa ba taa Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ naa tʋʋ mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ Fesitu gaa fɛɛ ɔ waa bamʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti sa bamʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ ɔ yɛgɛ Pɔɔlɩ ɔ lɩɩ Kasariya ba Gyerusalem. Kpalɩ fɛɛ ba waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ ba naa bun ɔkpa tɔ, kɛ ba nyɛ mɔɔ Pɔɔlɩ.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Fesitu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Pɔɔlɩ tii obu Kasariya, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n biti n kpee tʋtɔ.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Fan lɛɛ fanɛ abɩlɩsa akʋ kɛ maa bamʋ a kpee Kasariya. Nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ ɔnyɩn mʋ asɩn libi akʋ mʋ, kɛ fan naa mʋ fan tɔwɛ.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Fesitu maa kyɩna bamʋ asɛ fɛɛ nkɛ nburuwa abɛɛ nkɛ kudu kamaa tɔ mʋ, ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kpee Kasariya. Kayɩ kɩ maa kɛ mʋ, ɔ naa kyɩna asɩn ogyikpa kagya sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa Pɔɔlɩ ba mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ ba tʋtɔ mʋ, Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ Gyerusalem ba tʋtɔ mʋ, ba yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩn atɩntɩnsa bɛɛ gyaga mʋ sʋ, amaa yɛgɛ ba mɛɛ taalɛ kaapʋ ɩmʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “N man waa alibi akʋ lɩɩ Gyiwu abi nbara sʋ, abɛɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, abɛɛ Roma Owura lala Kayisa.”

english_verse: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
translation_verse: Amaa Fesitu maa biti fɛɛ ɔ waa Gyiwu abi kɔkɔlɔ biti sʋ mʋ, ɔ taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, “Fii biti fɛɛ n kpiya fʋ Gyerusalem, kɛ n naa gyii fʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ pwɩɩ fʋ falɛ sʋ mi ansi tɔ abɛɛ?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Nbɩɩnbɩɩ n yɩlɛ opula mʋ nɩɩ Roma Owura lala Kayisa ee gyii asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa be gyii mɛ asɩn. Amaa Gyiwu abi bɩrɛ n man waa bamʋ alibi akʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ, fu nyi falɛ.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nɩɩ mɛ asɩn akʋ a bun mɛ kʋpwɛ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n wu bɩrɛ, man kina kɩmʋ. Amaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba tɩn gyaga mɛ sʋ falɛ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kesintin mʋ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ mɛ sa bamʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n taa mɛ asɩn mʋ naa tʋʋ Kayisa ansi tɔ.”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Fesitu maa mʋ atɔwɛ kyatɔpʋ ba maa tɔwɛ lʋwɛ kamaa mʋ, ba tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, “Fʋ maa tɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ taa fʋ asɩn kpee Kayisa ansi tɔ mʋ, Kayisa ansi tɔ nɩɩ fii kpee.” Fesitu buu Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ sa Owura Agiripa

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: Ɩ maa gyii nkɛ nkʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, Owura Agiripa yɛ mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ Benisi ba kpee Kasariya ba naa fwaala Ɔsʋwʋlɛ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Fesitu.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: Ba maa kyɩna tʋtɔ nkɛ nkʋ mʋ, Fesitu tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn sa owura Agiripa yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn kʋ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ Felisi yɛgɛ nɩɩ o tii obu.

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kpee Gyerusalem mʋ, aseepu bɩlɩsa yɛ Gyiwu abi abɩlɩsa ba bɩya Pɔɔlɩ asɩn pwɩɩsa mi ansi tɔ, nɩɩ bee biti fɛɛ n bun mʋ kʋpwɛ.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, ɩ man gyɛ anɛ Roma abi adɩdaa‐sɩn fɛɛ a bun ɔkʋ kʋpwɛ, amɔɔ maa bamʋ nɩɩ ba pwɩɩ mʋ mʋ, ba gyaŋɛ kɛ ba gyii asɩn mʋ pɔyɩ kɛ ba bun mʋ kʋpwɛ

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ abɩlɩsa ba buu mɛ ba nfɩɩ mʋ, n man taa asɩn mʋ kpɩɩ kasɛ. Amaa ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, n kpee naa kyɩna asɩn mʋ ogyikpa, nɩɩ n sa kɔnɔ yɛ ba taa ɔnyɩn mʋ ba tʋtɔ.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ apwɩɩpʋ ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ mʋ, ba man taalɛ kaapʋ tɔ asɩn libi mʋ nɩɩ n da fɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ ɩkʋ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
translation_verse: Amaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ ɩ gyɛ maa bamʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii, yɛ ɔnyɩn kʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Yesu nɩɩ o wu mʋ, nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ ɩ tɔwɛ fɛɛ ɔ san ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ.

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asɩn nɩmʋ kɩmɛɛgɛ tɔ ɩ kɩtaa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n taasɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Fii biti fɛɛ fu kpee Gyerusalem kɛ ba naa gyii fʋ asɩn nɩmʋ tʋtɔ abɛɛ?

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ maa kʋlɛ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ baa o tii obu mʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Roma Owura lala Kayisa ii biti o gyii mʋ asɩn mʋ, kanɩn sʋ n kɩtaa mʋ n yɩla naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa mʋ naa sa owura lala Kayisa.’ ”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Owura Agiripa tɔwɛ Fesitu yɛ, “Mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n biti fɛɛ n nu lɩɩ ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ asɛ.” Nɩɩ Fesitu lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔkɛ fii biti fu nu lɩɩ mʋ asɛ.” Pɔɔlɩ ba yɩlɛ Agiripa maa Benisi ansi tɔ

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ Owura Agiripa maa mʋ‐pɩɩkyɩɩ Benisi ba suu‐tɔ, nɩɩ asa ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa bamʋ, nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ abɩlɩsa lala yɛ kadɛ mʋ tɔ abɩlɩsa ba naa gyaŋɛ asɩn ogyikpa mʋ. Nɩɩ Fesitu sa kɔnɔ nɩɩ ba naa bɩya Pɔɔlɩ.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Fesitu kʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Owura Agiripa yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan gyaŋɛ anɛ asɛ nfɩɩ kpini fan nu asɩn nɩmʋ. Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi kpini ba kʋlɛ mɛ lɩɩ Gyerusalem yɛ Kasariya nfɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ man dagaa maa bɩla kyɩna nkpa tɔ.

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: N wu fɛɛ ɔ man waa alibi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dagaa lewu. Amaa lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ maa taa mʋ asɩn naa sa Owura lala Kayisa sʋ mʋ, n waa mɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ n kpiya mʋ Roma.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Amaa n man nyɛ asɩn kitii mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ naa sa mɛ Owura lala Kayisa. Ɩmʋ sʋ n taa mʋ ba fanɛ ansi tɔ, fɔnfɔn fʋ owura Agiripa, fɛɛ an kan kɩɩ mʋ asɩn mʋ tɔ mʋ, n nyɛ asɩn kʋ lɩɩ mʋ sʋ kɛ n kyʋrɔɔ.

english_verse: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ man sʋ kasɛ fɛɛ n lɛɛ obu tiipu kyasɛ Owura Kayisa, yɛgɛ n man kaapʋ tɔ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ ba pwɩɩ mʋ.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Owura Agiripa tɔwɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, “Nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɔkpa dɛ sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ tɔwɛ fʋ asɩn.” Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kʋsaa mʋ kɩbaa piili ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: “Owura Agiripa, ɩ gyɛ kumu ɔkɔn sa mɛ fɛɛ ndaga n maa yɩlɛ fu ansi tɔ n biti n lɛɛ kɔnɔ lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba tɩn gyaga mɛ sʋ falɛ kpini.

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: Fɔnfɔn fɛɛ fʋ bɩrɛ fu nyi Gyiwu abi anaana tɔ kpini yɛ bamʋ ɩmɔɔrɛ‐libi. Ɩmʋ sʋ n kʋlɛ fʋ fɛɛ fʋ nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ kɛ fu nu mɛ asɛ.

english_verse: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bee biti ba gyii kesintin mʋ, ba wʋla be nyi mɛ koo dɩdaa fɛɛ, n kyɩna n gyɛ Farasii kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ anɛ kabwaarɛ‐sun, nɩɩ kɩ sʋ anɛ nbara kyɛɛkyɛɛ mʋ ɔkʋ.

english_verse: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
translation_verse: Ndaga n yɩlɛ nfɩɩ nɩɩ bɛɛ talɛ mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ n sʋ tamaa lɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa anɛ naana ana mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: Falɛ kɔkɔlɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nbunɔ kudu anyɔ ba sʋ tamaa yɛ bee biti ba nyɛ sʋ, nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ. Owura danbɩrasa, falɛ tamaa nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ bɛɛ pwɩɩ mɛ.

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ɩ bʋlʋn sa fanɛ Gyiwu abi nɩɩ fan mɛɛ kɔɔlɛ fen gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ i kyiŋi alewupu?

english_verse: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
translation_verse: “Mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ n daa kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ n waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n taalɛ waa kun Yesu Kanasareti‐nyɩn mʋ kɩtɩɩ.

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kanɩn gbaa nɩɩ n waa Gyerusalem kadɛ tɔ. N nyɛ ɔlʋn lɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ n naa n kɩtaa Ɩbwaarɛ asa tii obu. Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ, mɛ kɔnɔ ki tii sʋ.

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Saŋa kʋmaa n kpee Gyiwu abi kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩgyaŋɛkpa, nɩɩ n yɛgɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyanla bamʋ. Nɩɩ n nyan bamʋ ba tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn gyaga Yesu sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n kisi bamʋ sʋ mʋ, n kpee asa ndɛ sʋ n naa nyanla bamʋ. Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyaŋɛ Yesu nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: “Kanɩn asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n kyɩna naa kɔɔlɛ ɔwʋlɛ yɛ ɔlʋn lɩɩ aseepu bɩlɩsa mʋ asɛ, nɩɩ n kpee Damasiko.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Mɛ Owura danbɩrasa, n maa kpee nɩɩ ɩ naa fʋʋ kakyʋwapɛ asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ n wu fɩtɩla kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ kɩ ŋalɩgɛ kɩ kyɔ kyɔwɛ ba kyaabɔɔ maa bamʋ nɩɩ anaa mʋ.

english_verse: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anɛ kpini an tɩyɛ kasɛ, nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ Hibriwu katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ, ‘Sɔɔlɩ, Sɔɔlɩ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɩɩ nyanla mɛ kanɩn? Ɩ bʋlʋn sa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ maa kɔɔ yɛ mi kebiti’.

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ n taasɛ yɛ, ‘Fʋ anɩmʋ nɩn, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ?’ Nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Mɛ Yesu mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ nyanla mʋ nɩn.

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: Amaa kʋsʋ yɩlɛ fʋ ayaa sʋ. N lɛɛ mɛ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ, kɛ n taa fʋ waa mɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ yɛ mi kesintin ogyipu. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fu wu lɩɩ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ, yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n lɛɛ kaapʋ fʋ mʋ, kɛ fʋ taa tɔwɛ asa.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: N biti n sun fu kpee fʋ gbaa‐gbaa fʋ asa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ asɛ, kɛ n mɔlɩgɛ fʋ lɩɩ bamʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: Kɛ fʋ naa bugi bamʋ ansi, kɛ fʋ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ kitentenbiri tɔ ba fɩtɩla tɔ. Kɛ fʋ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ ɔlʋn tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Kɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, ba nyɛ alibi kɩtaakɛɛ, kɛ ba nyɛ ɔkyɩnakpa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ yɩla sa mʋ asa mʋ.’ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ mu kusun waasa sa Agiripa

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ n gyankpaa tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ sa Damasiko abi, yɛ Gyerusalem abi. N bɩla tɔwɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Gyudeya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi. N naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ, kɛ ba ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, kɛ ba waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ libi kɩwaa.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba kɩtaa mɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ bee biti ba mɔɔ mɛ.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ kyɛ mɛ tɔ ba fʋʋ ndaga kakɛ nɩmʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n yɩlɛ nfɩɩ n lɛɛ Yesu kesintin asɩn n kaapʋ asa kpini. Nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ Mosesi tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ ii biti ɩ ba mʋ, ɩmʋ asɩn maŋa nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ.

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Asɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ maa gyii awʋrʋfɔ, kɛ ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ kelewu kyiŋi tɔ ɔgyankpaapʋ, kɛ ɔ tɔwɛ fɩtɩla mʋ asɩn sa Gyiwu abi yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi kpini.”

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa lɛɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ kasɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ Fesitu tɩn mʋ sʋ pʋntɛ yɛ, “Pɔɔlɩ, fɩɩ lɔɔ ɩbʋn nɩn? Fʋ ɔwʋlɛ kinyi kɩbansa ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋ nfɛɛrɛ i nyita.”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔsʋwʋlɛ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Fesitu, n mɛɛ lɔɔ ɩbʋn nɩn, n tɔwɛ kesintin asɩn mʋ nɩɩ kanyaasɩn bʋ ɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: Owura Agiripa, n kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ fʋ gbaa fu nyi asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ, n tɔwɛ falɛ. Nɩɩ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ falɛ ɩmʋ ɩkʋ ɩ man baala fʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man waa ɩkʋ asiiri tɔ.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: Owura Agiripa, fʋ kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ asɩn gyii? N nyi fɛɛ fʋ maa kɔɔlɛ gyii.”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ owura Agiripa taasɛ Pɔɔlɩ yɛ, “Fu nyi fɛɛ fɩɩ taalɛ taa saŋa dɛɛ nɩmʋ tɔ kyɛɛgɛ mɛ nfɛɛrɛ, kɛ n ba biliŋi Krisito obii abɛɛ?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɩ gyɛ saŋa dɛɛ nɩmʋ tɔ nɩn o, ii biti ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩn o, ɩmʋ nɩɩ n kʋlɛ n lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɔ yɛgɛ fʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sarɛ bee nu mɛ asɛ nfɩɩ mʋ kpini ba biliŋi fɛɛ mɛ, amaa ba man taa agbeeleebi ŋmina bamʋ fɛɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
translation_verse: Saŋa maa tɔ nɩɩ Owura Agiripa maa mʋ pɩɩkyɩɩ Benisi yɛ Ɔsʋwʋlɛ Ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ Fesitu yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ tʋtɔ mʋ kpini ba kʋsʋ lɩɩ.

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: Ba maa lɩɩ obu mʋ tɔ mʋ, bee biri asɩn mʋ kiligyima, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ abaa yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ man waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba mɔɔ mʋ, abɛɛ ba tii mʋ obu.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Agiripa tɔwɛ Fesitu yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ man dɩn tɔwɛ, yɛ ee biti ɔ taa mʋ asɩn naa sa Owura lala Kayisa nɩn, naafɔɔ an taalɛ yɛgɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ a kpee Roma kadɛ tɔ mʋ, ba taa Pɔɔlɩ maa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ obu mʋ akʋ, waa anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ɔbɩlɩsa Yuriyo abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Owura lala Kayisa anaakɔpʋ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ an taa ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ Adaramitiyo kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ kii biti ki kpee Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ kabaafʋn kʋkʋ sʋ kebunɔ. Nɩɩ Kamasedoniya‐nyɩn Arisitako mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Tesalonika mʋ gbaa tii anɛ sʋ.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ, a naa dan Sidon kadɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ kɩlɩfa Yuriyo waa Pɔɔlɩ adanbɩrasa, sa mʋ ɔkpa fɛɛ ɔ naa wu mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana, kɛ ɔ nyɛ nkyɛtɔ lɩɩ bamʋ asɛ.

english_verse: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ an lɩɩ tʋtɔ bɔla ɔpʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ afuu mʋ ɩmaa daa ɩ gyaŋa anɛ sʋ mʋ, an bɔla kpee Sipuru kifintin mʋ asɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ afuu mʋ a man bʋlʋn.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: A maa bɔla ɔpʋ sʋ naa kara Kilikiya yɛ Panfiliya, nɩɩ a naa dan Mira mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Lisiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ wu ɔkʋlɩ lala kʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ Alekisandiya kadɛ tɔ kii kpee Itali. Nɩɩ ɔ taa anɛ naa waa kɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ an natɛ nkyu sʋ bwahn‐bwahn gyii nkɛ nkʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa ɔlʋn sa anɛ, pɔyɩ nɩɩ an naa fʋʋ Kinido. Lɩɩ fɛɛ afuu mʋ ɩ maa man sa anɛ ɔkpa sʋ mʋ, an bɔla naa tiri Kereti kefintin mʋ, nɩɩ an kara Salimone kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: A maa naa an kara an kyʋn nbunɔ‐nbunɔ sʋ mʋ, an kpɩnɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ an ba fʋʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ Ɩkʋlɩ lala Ɔdankpa Danbɩrasa, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri Laseya kadɛ.

english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ an maa kyɛɛrɛɛ ɔkpa tɔ gaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ kɔnɔ‐ŋmina saŋa maa kyʋn mʋ, nkyu sʋ kɩbɔla waa kufuu. Ɩmʋ sʋ Pɔɔlɩ da bamʋ amu tɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ,

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: “Anyɩn, n wu fɛɛ anɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ an kpee falɛ, ii biti ɩ bɩya kumu libi, yɛ gyerebi lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ yɛ kɩmʋ ɩsʋla. Nɩɩ anɛ gbaa anɛ an man lɩɩ.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Amaa anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ ɔ man nu Pɔɔlɩ asɩn mʋ, ɔkʋlɩ wuya mʋ yɛ kɩmʋ ɔkpa ɔkaapʋpʋ asɛ nɩɩ ee nu.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩkʋlɩ ɔdankpa maŋa man bʋrɔn sa awɔɔ saŋa. Nɩɩ bamʋ gaalaagaa ba tɔwɛ kyula yɛ ba kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ ba fʋʋ Fonike. Tʋtɔ ɩkʋlɩ ɔdankpa mʋ ɩ bʋrɔn sa awɔɔ saŋa. Fonike gyɛ ɔdankpa nɩɩ kɩ dɛ Kereti kefintin, mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ kɩtɩyɛ yɛ adii kɩtɩyɛ nsana tɔ. Afuu lala a tigi waa bamʋ ɔpʋ sʋ

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ afuu pii ɩ daa ɩ lɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ mʋ, asa mʋ be nyi fɛɛ ba nyɛ afuu mʋ nɩɩ bee biti. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ kɩbɩlɩtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kɩtaa ɔkʋlɩ lala kɩ yɩla, nɩɩ ba kyaga bamʋ ɔkpa sʋ ba naa Kereti ɩkaa sʋ ɩkaa sʋ.

english_verse: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ anɛ ɔkʋlɩ ɩ gyaŋa afuu mʋ, ɔkʋlɩ mʋ man bɩla ɛɛ taalɛ ee kpee ansi tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ an yɛgɛ anɛ n‐yɩɩ sa afuu mʋ, nɩɩ i sii ɩ sʋ anɛ ɩ naa.

english_verse: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ sʋla anɛ naa lɩɩ kifintin pii mʋ nɩɩ bɛ tɩɩ yɛ Kuda, nɩɩ kii kun afuu mʋ gbɛrɛɛ. Nɩɩ ɩ waa ɔlʋn sa anɛ pɔyɩ nɩɩ an taalɛ kɩtaa ɔkʋlɩ pii mʋ nɩɩ kɩlala mʋ kɩ bɩɩtɛ mʋ.

english_verse: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
translation_verse: Anyɩn mʋ ba maa nyɛ ɔkʋlɩ‐pii mʋ, ba taa kɩmʋ waa kɩlala mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba taa ɩfɛ ŋmina kyaabɔɔ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ kinkin. Nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ kufuu fɛɛ afuu mʋ ii biti ɩ sʋla bamʋ naa wɔtɛ Libiya kɩkyafa. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba bɔɔ abaala mʋ, nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ afuu mʋ i sii ɩ sʋ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ ɩ naa.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: Afuu mʋ ɩ maa nyanla anɛ kanɩn mʋ, kayɩ kɩ maa kɛ mʋ, ba piili bɛɛ lɛɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ ɩsʋla bɛɛ tʋʋ nkyu mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kakɛ sasapʋ mʋ, ba taa bamʋ abaa bɛɛ lɛɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa tɔ atɔ bɛɛ tʋʋ nkyu mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: An gyii nkɛ gaalaagaa yɛgɛ a mee wu kyɔwɛ abɛɛ akyikpebi, yɛgɛ afuu mʋ mɔɔ ɩ san ɩ daa kanɩn. Ɩmʋ sʋ anɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ man bɩla ɔ sʋ tamaa fɛɛ ɔ sʋ nkpa.

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: A maa kyɩna akʋn nkɛ gaalaagaa nɩmʋ yɛgɛ a mee gyii sɛhn mʋ, nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ nsana tɔwɛ yɛ, “Anyɩn, fan daa nu mɛ katɔwɛ yɛ an man lɩɩ Kereti mʋ, naafɔɔ falɛ awʋrʋfɔ yɛ gyerebi nɩmʋ man dɩn ba anɛ sʋ.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ fɛɛ fan nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ, fanɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ yɛ o sii tɔ, amɔɔ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ nkʋn ii biti o nyita.

english_verse: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ndee kagyanbwɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ, nɩɩ n sun mɛ mʋ, mʋ kabɔɔ ba yɩlɛ mɛ asɛ.

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Pɔɔlɩ, man waa kufuu. Ɩ dan ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ fʋ naa yɩlɛ Owura lala Kayisa ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ fʋ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ taa bamʋ nɩɩ be tii fʋ sʋ falɛ kpini nkpa sa bamʋ’.

english_verse: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ anyɩn, fan nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ. N kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii fɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ ee biti ɔ waa.

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ba tɩŋɛ ɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ abɩlɩtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kɩtaa ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ tɔ ɩ yɩlɛ mʋ waa ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ opula maŋa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ba saŋɛ ɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ iyu mʋ nɩɩ bɛ sʋ bɛ kaapʋ ɔkpa. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ba ŋmina kɩtaa lala mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ abaala ɔkʋlɩ mʋ kɔnɔ asɛ sa afuu, kɛ afuu mʋ a sʋla bamʋ i kpee kebunɔ.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ tɔ kusun awaapʋ mʋ ba maa paa fɛɛ ba sɩlɛ lɩɩ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba lɛɛ ɔkʋlɩ‐pii mʋ gyaga ɔpʋ sʋ, bɛɛ pɩna fɛɛ bee biti ba naa lɛɛ abɩlɩtɔ mʋ akʋ ɔkʋlɩ mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ waa anɛ kufuu fɛɛ akʋtɔ an biti a naa wɔtɛ afʋlɛ‐buu fɛɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ abɩlɩtɔ ana mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kɩtaa ɔkʋlɩ ɩɩ yɩlɛ mʋ, tʋʋ nkyu mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ fɛɛ kayɩ kɩ kɛ bamʋ.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ maa anaakɔpʋ mʋ yɛ, fɛɛ nɩɩ anyɩn anɩmʋ ba man kyɩna ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ tɔ bɩrɛ, fan mɛɛ yɛ fan nyɛ nkpa.

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba kɩtaa ɩfɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔkʋlɩ‐pii mʋ tɔ mʋ tɩŋɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba yɛgɛ kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ kyʋn.”

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n kʋlɛ fanɛ nɩn fan kʋsʋ gyii agyitɔ, kɛ fan nyɛ ɔlʋn. Fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋlʋn kinyinibi kʋlʋn gbaa mɛɛ yɛ kɩ fɔyɩ.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, ɔ taa bodobodo nɩɩ ɔ taa afwaala sa Ɩbwaarɛ bamʋ ansi tɔ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ gya bodobodo mʋ tɔ piili ɛɛ wɛ.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ kpini nkɔlɔ n tɩyɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ gyii agyitɔ.

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba gyii ŋmɛ mʋ, ba lɛɛ ɩbɔɔta tɔ agyitɔ mʋ tʋʋ ɔpʋ tɔ, kɛ ɔkʋlɩ mʋ ɔ kʋsaa oduu. Ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ kiboyi

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Kayɩ maa kɛ mʋ, ba man taalɛ bɩɩ kebunɔ mʋ. Amaa ba wu kidenbi kʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kɩkyafa. Nɩɩ ba waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ nɩɩ bɛɛ taalɛ mʋ, ba taa ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ naa dan tʋtɔ.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ kasɛ tɔ naa wɔtɛ kɩkyafa. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ ansi tɔ mʋ kɩ man bɩla kɩ taalɛ ki biliŋi. Nɩɩ apaa a daa kɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ gyɛ‐gyɛ tɔ.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anaakɔpʋ mʋ ba waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba mɔɔ obu atiipu mʋ, kɛ ba man nyɛ tɩyɛ nkyu tɔ sɩlɛ.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Amaa anaakɔpʋ ɔbɩlɩsa mʋ, biti fɛɛ ɔ mɔlɩgɛ Pɔɔlɩ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ man sa bamʋ ɔkpa kɛ ba waa bamʋ nkɔlɔ biti. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi nkyu kɩtɩyɛ mʋ yɛ, ba gyankpaa fugi tɩyɛ tɔ kɛ ba tɩyɛ lɩɩ kpee ofuli sʋ.

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bun‐bun itaabo dɩyɛ‐dɩyɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ bun ɔkʋlɩ iyu boyisa mʋ sʋ. Kanɩn nɩɩ ɩ sʋla ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ naa dan yɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
translation_verse: A maa fʋʋ kifintin mʋ sʋ yɛ alanfɩya nɩɩ, an bɩɩ fɛɛ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ Malita.

english_verse: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
translation_verse: Kifintin mʋ sʋ ndɛ abi ba waa anɛ nsa‐nsa kɔɔlɛ anɛ abaa anyɔ. Bʋlayɩ maa da nɩɩ awɔɔ a bʋ tɔ sʋ mʋ, ba dɛɛla fʋlɔn sa anɛ yɛ a wʋlɛ.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ naa kʋʋla ngya ɔ ba waa fʋlɔn mʋ tɔ. Maa taa ngya mʋ ɛɛ waa fʋlɔn mʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ fʋlɔn mʋ awulawula ɩ maa wiili kɩnansɛ mʋ, nɩɩ o dun Pɔɔlɩ kɩbaa yɛgɛ ɔ kyɔlɛ sʋ.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ kifintin mʋ sʋ ndɛ abi ba wu kɩnansɛ mʋ maa kyɔlɛ mʋ kɩbaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ abaa asɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩn nɩmʋ biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ ɔmɔɔpʋ nɩn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ maa nyɛ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ gbaa mʋ, ɔmʋnɩ ɛɛ ka alibi akɔ mʋ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ yɛgɛ ɔ nyɛ nkpa.”

english_verse: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔɔlɩ maa ŋmanyan kɩnansɛ mʋ tʋʋ fʋlɔn mʋ tɔ mʋ, sɛhn man waa mʋ.

english_verse: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ ba fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ Pɔɔlɩ ii biti o puŋa abɛɛ ɔ tɩyɛ opula maŋa tɔ kɛ o wu. Amaa ba maa gyoo naa fʋʋ saŋa kʋ nɩɩ ba wu fɛɛ sɛhn man waa Pɔɔlɩ mʋ, ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ɔ gyɛ ɔdakpa kʋ nɩn.

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: Kanɩn kifintin maŋa sʋ ɔkɩɩsʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Pubilo sʋ kasɛ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri katɩn mʋ nɩɩ an yɩlɛ mʋ. Ɔnyɩn mʋ waa anɛ nsa‐nsa, nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ anɛ mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ, kɩtaa anɛ kɩfɔ nkɛ nsa.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: Saŋa maa Pubilo mʋ‐sɛ dɛ ɛɛ lɔ kibirima yɛgɛ ɛɛ kyaalɛ. Nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ naa loo taa mʋ abaa gyaga mʋ sʋ, kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ maa waa kanɩn mʋ, nɩɩ kifintin mʋ sʋ alɔpʋ mʋ kpini ba ba Pɔɔlɩ asɛ nɩɩ ba nyɛ alanfɩya.

english_verse: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ ba taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa anɛ ɔkpa kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ saŋa maa fʋʋ fɛɛ a lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, ba kɛɛ anɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ i tiri sa anɛ. Pɔɔlɩ naa fʋʋ Roma

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: A maa kyɩna tʋtɔ ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa kamaa mʋ, a naa loo ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ Alekisanda nɩɩ kɩ ba sii tʋtɔ awɔɔ saŋa mʋ. Ba kyan ɩdakpa nbwaara‐bii mʋ gyaga kɩmʋ sʋ.

english_verse: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
translation_verse: A maa lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, an naa yɩlɛ Sirakusi kadɛ tɔ, nɩɩ an gyii tʋtɔ nkɛ nsa.

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ, an naa fʋʋ Regiyo kadɛ tɔ. Lɩɩkaakɛ mʋ afuu a da lɩɩ akpɩlɩgɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ kakɛ nyɔsapʋ mʋ, an naa fʋʋ Putoli kadɛ tɔ.

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ maŋa nɩɩ an wu akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu akʋ, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ a ba kyɩna bamʋ asɛ nkɛ nsunɔ. Falɛ nɩɩ an natɛ naa fʋʋ Roma.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: Akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Roma ba maa nu fɛɛ an ba mʋ, ba kʋsʋ ba gyaŋa anɛ. Akʋ ba natɛ kanɩn ba gyaŋa anɛ Apiya Kɩgya opula, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba ba gyaŋa anɛ ɔfʋʋtɛkpa asa opula mʋ tɔ. Pɔɔlɩ maa wu bamʋ kanɩn mʋ, ɔ fwaala Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩ tɩyɛ mʋ.

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: An maa fʋʋ Roma mʋ, ba sa Pɔɔlɩ ɔkpa fɛɛ maa ɔnaakɔpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ mʋ sʋ mʋ ba naa kyɩna ntɩn mʋ nɩɩ mʋ Pɔɔlɩ ii biti. Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn Roma kadɛ tɔ

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Nkɛ nsa kamaa mʋ, Pɔɔlɩ tɩɩ Gyiwu abɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tʋtɔ mʋ ba sarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Mɛ tɔɔmaa ana Gyiwu abi, amaa n maa man waa anɛ asa alibi akʋ gbaa, nɩɩ man nyita anɛ naana ana nbara mʋ, ba kɩtaa mɛ naa tii Gyerusalem, nɩɩ ba taa mɛ waa Roma abi abaa tɔ.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Amaa Roma abi ba maa gyii mɛ asɩn mʋ, ba waa nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba yɛgɛ mɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man wu alibi mʋ nɩɩ n waa nɩɩ ɩ dagaa ba mɔɔ mɛ.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Gyiwu abi ba kina fɛɛ ba man yɛgɛ mɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ nyan mɛ, nɩɩ n taa asɩn mʋ ba Owura lala Kayisa asɛ. Amaa ɩ man gyɛ fɛɛ n biti n ba tɩɩ mɛ asa ntalɛ nɩn.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: Falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n tɩɩ fanɛ, kɛ n lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kasɛ sa fanɛ. Lɩɩ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kpini ba sʋ tamaa mʋ sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ndaga ba ŋmina mɛ agbeeleebi.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba lɛɛ kɔnɔ yɛ, “An man nyɛ ɔwʋlɛ lɩɩ Gyudeya lɩɩ fʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ anɛ bɩrɩsa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ ɔkʋ man ba waa adiboyi‐sɩn, nɩɩ ba man tɔwɛ asɩn libi kʋ lɩɩ fʋ sʋ.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Amaa anɛ Gyiwu abi an biti an wu fʋ nfɛɛrɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ an nyi fɛɛ kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ falɛ, katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ asa bɛɛ tɔwɛ bee yii kɩmʋ.”

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Gyiwu abi mʋ ba yɩla kakɛ kʋ, kɛ ba maa Pɔɔlɩ ba gyaŋɛ. Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa maa fʋʋ mʋ, bamʋ asa damantɛ ba kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɛ mʋ. Lɩɩ nyenyenfuu naa fʋʋ kalaatɩpwɛ mʋ, ɔ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔwɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn. Ɔ bɔla Mosesi nbara yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ ɩwʋlɛ mʋ sʋ kaapʋ bamʋ lɩɩ Yesu sʋ yɛ, ba kɔɔlɛ mʋ gyii.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ akʋ ba kɔɔlɛ asɩn mʋ gyii, amaa akʋ mɔɔ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ asɩn mʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, asa mʋ ba gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba yaasɛ tɔ. Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Pɔɔlɩ tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, “Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ Isaya sʋ tɔwɛ kesintin sa fanɛ naana ana yɛ,

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: ‘Kpe asa anɩmʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fen biti fan nu asɩn, amaa fan mɛɛ yɛ fan nu kasɛ. Fen biti fan kɩɩ kɛ fan kɩɩ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fen wu.”

english_verse: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa anɩmʋ nkɔlɔ i budu, nɩɩ bamʋ asʋ a bʋlʋn, nɩɩ ba kɩtaa bamʋ ansi bun sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn sʋ mʋ, naafɔɔ bamʋ ansi ii biti i wu, kɛ bamʋ asʋ a nu, kɛ ba bɩya bamʋ nkɔlɔ kasɛ, kɛ ba biliŋi ba mɛ asɛ kɛ n kyɛ bamʋ.’ ”

english_verse: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ maa tɔwɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, Gyiwu abi mʋ ba kʋsʋ lɩɩ ba gyii abaa ɩmɔɔrɛ.

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Pɔɔlɩ kyɩna kɩkpaara mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ nɩɩ ɛɛ ka kɩmʋ sʋ kʋkɔ nsu nnyɔ laalaamuu. Nɩɩ ɛɛ kɔɔlɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba ɛɛ bɔɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: Ɔ yɩlɛ kɩnyɩn tɔ ɛɛ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ asɩn, nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ asɩn lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ, yɛgɛ sɛhn man tii mʋ ɔkpa.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
translation_verse: Mɛ, Pɔɔlɩ ɩ kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ n sa fanɛ. N gyɛ Yesu Krisito kɩyaafɔlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ mɛ yɛ n ba waa mu osunpu, nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ mɛ yɩla yɛ n ba tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ.

english_verse: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ, bɔla mʋ atɔwɛpʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ asɩn danbɩrasa nɩmʋ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
translation_verse: Anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ɔlʋn mʋ kyiŋi mʋ lɩɩ lewu tɔ, lɛɛ mʋ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii mʋ.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ mʋ yɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ sa anɛ kabwaala, nɩɩ ɔ waa anɛ Krisito kusun asunpu, kɛ a tɔwɛ kaapʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi, kɛ ba nu asɩn mʋ kasɛ kɛ ba kɔɔlɛ Krisito mʋ gyii.

english_verse: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan tii bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ yɛ ba be biliŋi Yesu Krisito lɛɛ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ n sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan bʋ Roma, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ sʋ kebiti sa, nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ fanɛ yɛ fan ba waa mʋ lɛɛ mʋ. N fwaala fanɛ, nɩɩ n kʋlɛ kabwaala yɛ kayɩɩ‐yuuli mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ anɛ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito asɛ mʋ kɩ ba fanɛ sʋ. Pɔɔlɩ ii biti Roma kikpe naa tɔwɛ Abwaarɛsɩn

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyankpaa mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, fanɛ kpini sʋ mʋ, n bɔla Yesu Krisito sʋ n sʋ afwaala n sa mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ asa bɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nɩmʋ asɩn dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ n taa mɛ kɔkɔlɔ kpini n sun mʋ, nɩɩ n tɔwɛ Mu‐Bii asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ, ɩ gyɛ mi kesintin ogyipu fɛɛ saŋa kʋmaa n nyiŋi fanɛ sʋ, mɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ tɔ.

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ saŋa kʋmaa nɩmʋ fɛɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kyula mʋ, o bugi ɔkpa sa mɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ kɛ n ba bɔɔ fanɛ sʋ.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mi ansi ɩ dɛ fanɛ sʋ fɛɛ n ba wu fanɛ, kɛ n taa Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu tɔ kakɛɛ ba sa fanɛ, kɛ ɩ waa fanɛ ɔlʋn fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ a kpan anɛ n‐yɩɩ kɛ a taa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ an bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ falɛ kyɛ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
translation_verse: Mɛ bɩrɩsa ana akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu, n biti fan bɩɩ fɛɛ n ŋmina ɩlɩɩ fɩnɛ kpini fɛɛ n ba fanɛ asɛ. Amaa ba lɩɩ halɩɩ ndaga kʋtɔ kʋ kii tii mɛ ɔkpa. N biti kanan mʋ nɩɩ n waa kɛ n nyɛ akʋ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ tʋtɔ tɔ ba ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ n nyɛ akʋ lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ mi ansi ɩ payɩ fɛɛ n ba tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sa fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan bʋ Roma mʋ gbaa.

english_verse: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ɩ man sʋ mɛ ipeeli lɩɩ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sa ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ kamɔlɩgɛ. Gyiwu abi mʋ ɩ gyankpaa kɔɔlɛ gyii, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa.

english_verse: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ anɛ ɩkpa i kyiigi mu ansi tɔ. Ɩkpa kyiigisa nɩmʋ npiili yɛ ɩmʋ laalaalʋwɛ kpini ɩ yɩlɛ sa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ yɩla yɛ, “Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ nɩɩ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ii biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa.” Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ lɩɩ alibi sʋ

english_verse: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ agbʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ kaapʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa ɩ man kyiigi yɛ alibi awaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ alibi tii kesintin mʋ ɔkpa.

english_verse: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taalɛ bɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa ɩ lɛɛ ɩmʋ ifuli kaapʋ bamʋ.

english_verse: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ, ɔ bɔla mʋ atɔ tʋʋsa mʋ sʋ lɛɛ mu keduli mʋ nɩɩ a mɛɛ taalɛ taa ansi wu mʋ kaapʋ nyɩmɩsa. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ mʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa, yɛ mu keduli mʋ nɩɩ kɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, man sʋ kɔnɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

english_verse: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
translation_verse: Ba nyi Ɩbwaarɛ maa bʋ tɔ, amaa ba man sʋ bʋnyaa abɛɛ afwaala bɛɛ sa mʋ. Amaa nfɛɛrɛ danbɩrasa kʋ ɩ man bɩla ɩ bʋ bamʋ tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ i biliŋi kitentenbiri.

english_verse: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
translation_verse: Ba yɛ bamʋ ɩ gyɛ anyaasɩnpʋ, amaa ba gyɛ amelensipu nɩn.

english_verse: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba biliŋaa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ bʋnyaa ba sʋ bɛɛ sa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba pɔyɩ ɩmʋ fɛɛ anyɩmɩsa, nbwiibi yɛ atɔbwaaya yɛ atɔbwaaya kpɩɩsa.

english_verse: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mu ansi yɛgɛ bamʋ, nɩɩ ba taa kɩgyagaa bɛɛ waa bamʋ nkɔlɔ biti, nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kɩgyagaa libi maŋa bɛɛ ŋaabaa abaa.

english_verse: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba taa Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin mʋ kyɛrɛ antɩŋɛsa, nɩɩ bee sun atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ mʋ, lan yɛgɛ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ ɩmʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ a yɩn mʋ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ. Amen!

english_verse: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mu ansi yɛgɛ bamʋ sa ba maa bamʋ kɩgyagaa ipeeli, akyɩɩ ba yɛgɛ anyɩn asɛ kadɛ nɩɩ ba sii bɛɛ dɛ yɛ bamʋ bɩrɩsa ana akyɩɩ yɛgɛ ɩ man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkpa sʋ.

english_verse: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
translation_verse: Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ nɩɩ anyɩn gbaa ba biti ba yɛgɛ kadɛ yɛ akyɩɩ, nɩɩ kɩdɛ yɛ bamʋ bɩrɩsa ana anyɩn ɩ sʋ bamʋ ɔbʋlɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ waa apeeli‐sɩn bɛ dɛ yɛ abaa, nɩɩ ba nyɛ kɩgyagaa mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ lɩɩ bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kina Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ bɔrɔbɔrɔ sa bamʋ, kɛ ba waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man dagaa yɛ ba waa.

english_verse: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba kyaga sʋ bɛɛ waa asun libi, kensi payɩ libi, kikisi libi, kɩmɔɔ, kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii, npɩna, kɩkɩɩ ansi waa asɩn libi yɛ asa kukuli,

english_verse: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
translation_verse: kɩtɩn antɩŋɛsa gyaga asa sʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ akisipu, asa kɩsaalɛ, n‐yɩɩ kɩkaapʋ, alala‐sɩn, ɔkpa pʋpwɛ sʋ kɩtaa waa alibi, bɛɛ waa kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn bɛɛ sa bamʋ‐nyi ana yɛ bamʋ‐sɛ ana.

english_verse: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
translation_verse: Ba man sʋ nfɛɛrɛ, ba man sʋ kesintin, ba man sʋ kebiti sa asa, be mee su asa sʋ.

english_verse: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_verse: Yɛgɛ be nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa falɛ alibi mʋ, dagaa lewu gbaa mʋ, amaa man gyɛ falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ nkʋn nɩɩ bɛɛ waa, ba bɩla bɛɛ tɔwɛ bɛɛ yɩlɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanɩn mʋ kamaa.

english_verse: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fii bun fʋ bɩrɩsa kʋpwɛ mʋ, fʋ man sʋ kɔnɔ fʋ lɛɛ. Nɩɩ fʋ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ fu bun fʋ bɩrɩsa kʋpwɛ mʋ, fu bun fʋ n‐yɩɩ gbaa kʋpwɛ nɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ngbaa fʋ fɩɩ waa alibi kʋlʋn maŋa.

english_verse: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
translation_verse: Amaa an nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanɩn atɔ libi mʋ, ɩ dɛ kesintin ɔkpa sʋ.

english_verse: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
translation_verse: Abɛɛ fu bun ansi lɩɩ kɩdanbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ waa ɛɛ sa fʋ fɛɛ maa nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ ɛɛ sa fʋ yɛ maa sʋ kenyiita sa fʋ? Fu man nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ waa adanbɩrasa anɩmʋ kpini ɛɛ sa fʋ kɛ ɩ kyɛ fʋ tɔ kɛ fʋ yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa?

english_verse: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
translation_verse: Amaa lɩɩ fʋ kʋsʋ tɔ ɔlʋn yɛ alibi kɩ mɛɛ yɛgɛ kɩwaa sʋ mʋ, fʋ kʋʋla kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ sa fʋ n‐yɩɩ fu gyoo Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ lala mʋ kakɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kaapʋ alibi awaapʋ mʋ kesintin katalɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kenyiita bɛɛ waa kɩdanbɩrasa, bee biti Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ nkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ, bamʋ ii biti ba nyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

english_verse: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ bamʋ nkʋn lɛɛ, nɩɩ ba kina kesintin asɩn mʋ kunu nɩɩ bɛɛ waa asun libi mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ ii biti ɩ ba bamʋ sʋ kɛ ɔ man su bamʋ kʋwɛɛ.

english_verse: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ yɛ kayɩyɛɛ ii biti ɩ ba asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa alibi mʋ kpini sʋ. Ii biti ɩ gyankpaa ba Gyiwu abi sʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa sʋ.

english_verse: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Amaa bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ kayɩɩ yuuli ii biti ɩ ba sa asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa mʋ kpini, nɩɩ Gyiwu abi mʋ ii biti ba gyankpaa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ gbaa.

english_verse: God doesn't have any favorites!
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man sʋ nmɛɛgɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba man nyi Mosesi nbara, nɩɩ ba waa alibi mʋ, bee biti ba wu, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ yɛ ba maa kina Mosesi nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ be nyi Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ ba lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ waa alibi mʋ, kanɩn nbara maŋa nɩɩ bee biti ba taa bun bamʋ kʋpwɛ.

english_verse: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
translation_verse: Ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ awaasa ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, be nyi nbara mʋ. Nɩɩ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ gbaa i gyii bamʋ kesintin, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ ɩ kaapʋ yɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ bʋrɔn nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ falɛ ɩ man bʋrɔn.

english_verse: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ gyɛ Gyiwu nyɩmɩsa, nɩɩ fʋ taa fʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ Mosesi nbara mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ n‐yɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ.

english_verse: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fu nyi Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti, lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ bɩɩla Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ fu nyi atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn.

english_verse: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ agyaatanapʋ ɩkpa ɔkaapʋpʋ, nɩɩ fʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kitentenbiri tɔ mʋ fɩtɩla.

english_verse: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fʋ ɩ gyɛ amelensipu ɔtɔwɛwaapʋ, yɛ nbii wuribi ɔkaapʋpʋ. Nɩɩ fu nyi Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ kinyi yɛ kesintin mʋ bʋ.

english_verse: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ fɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ bɩrɩsa ana asansa, nɩɩ mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ fɩɩ mɛɛ kaapʋ fʋ n‐yɩɩ? Fʋ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn yɛ, keyu man bʋrɔn, yɛgɛ fɔɔ fʋ fii yuuri.

english_verse: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
translation_verse: Fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ kaapʋ fʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ fii nyi Mosesi nbara mʋ, man gyɛ fʋ i gyila Ɩbwaarɛ ipeeli fɛɛ fʋ maa nyita nbara mʋ?

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Lɩɩ fanɛ Gyiwu abi sʋ mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ asɩn bee nyita Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ.”

english_verse: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nɩɩ fɛn kan buu Mosesi nbara mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fu oduu katɩn mʋ sʋ tɔnɔ. Amaa nɩɩ fʋ tɩn fu oduu nɩɩ fu mee buu nbara mʋ bɩrɛ, i du nɩn fɛɛ fʋ maa man tɩn oduu mʋ.

english_verse: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ man tɩn mu oduu, yɛgɛ ɛɛ waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa kyɛɛkyɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ, ee biti o bun fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ gyɛ Gyiwu obii, nɩɩ fʋ tɩn fu oduu, nɩɩ fu nyita nbara mʋ kʋpwɛ.

english_verse: Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ man gyɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ atɔ awaasa ɩ waa ɔkʋ Gyiwu obii gbaa‐gbaa, nɩɩ oduu katɩn mʋ, man gyɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ tɔ atɔ waasa nɩn.

english_verse: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu obii kesintin mʋ, mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ ɩ kaapʋ, nɩɩ oduu katɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ kɔkɔlɔ awaasa mʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu mʋ ɩɩ waa, man gyɛ Mosesi nbara mʋ ɩɩ waa. Kanɩn ɔsa maŋa ɩɩ nyɛ kɩyɩn lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa asɛ.

english_verse: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan tɔwɛ yɛ ɔ gyɛ Gyiwu obii mʋ, mɩnɛ tɔnɔ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ lɩɩ tɔ? Abɛɛ mɩnɛ tɔnɔ ɩ bʋ oduu katɩn tɔ?

english_verse: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
translation_verse: Ɔkpa kʋmaa sʋ tɔnɔ bʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ tɔnɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyankpaa ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Gyiwu abi mʋ abaa tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn mʋ waa.

english_verse: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba fɛɛ akʋ ba man sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ, bamʋ kɩman kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ, ɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ man waa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ abɛɛ?

english_verse: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
translation_verse: Ayee! Ɩ mɛɛ ba kanɩn. Amaa nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa kʋmaa gyɛ antɩŋɛsa wuya mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ bɩrɛ san ɔ gyɛ mʋ kesintin wuya, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, “Ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn tɔwɛsa ɩ maa lɛɛ fu kesintin kaapʋ, nɩɩ ba kan pwɩɩ fʋ mʋ kɛ fu gyii asʋ.”

english_verse: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
translation_verse: A taa fɛɛ anɛ awaasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man bʋrɔn, nɩɩ ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ awaasa ɩ dɛ ɔkpa mʋ, ɩmɛɛ mʋ bɩrɛ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩɩtɛ anɛ kʋsʋ mʋ, an biti a tɔwɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man waa asɩn mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ? .

english_verse: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
translation_verse: Ayee! Nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kanɩn mʋ, nɛnɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa kɛ ɔ talɛ dulinyaa?

english_verse: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋ ii biti o gyii ɩmɔɔrɛ yɛ, fɛɛ nɩɩ mɛ antɩŋɛsa ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kesintin kɩ lɔŋɔ lɩɩ ifuli mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ bʋnyaa mʋ tii sʋ mʋ, mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ ee bun mɛ kʋpwɛ fɛɛ n gyɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ?

english_verse: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
translation_verse: Lɩɩ falɛ asɩn sʋ mʋ, akʋ bee biti ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan yɛgɛ a waa alibi, kɛ asa ba wu Ɩbwaarɛ adanbɩrasa.” Asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ bɛɛ saalɛ mɛ yɛ mɛ gbaa mɛ n tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o bun bamʋ kʋpwɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa. Ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ yɛ ɔ man gyɛ alibi ɔwaapʋ

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
translation_verse: Nɛnɛ nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ? Anɛ Gyiwu abi an gyɛ asa kparɛ an kyɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ abɛɛ? Ayee! N tɛ wʋla tɔwɛ fɛɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi mʋ kpini alibi i gyii bamʋ sʋ ɔlʋn.

english_verse: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ee nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ ɔ naa ee biti Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kpeli mu ansi lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba man sʋ kyaarɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa kɩdanbɩrasa halɩɩ ɔkʋlʋn gbaa.

english_verse: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
translation_verse: Bamʋ nnɔ tɔ asɩn tɔwɛsa ɩ mɔɔ fɛɛ lewu. Antɩŋɛsa nkʋn ɩ bɔla bamʋ nnandʋlʋ sʋ. Bamʋ nnɔ tɔ asɩn i du fɛɛ kɩnansɛ kʋbwɛ.

english_verse: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
translation_verse: Bɛɛ tɔwɛ asa nnɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ asɩn tɔwɛsa ɩ bʋlɩlɩ.

english_verse: These people quickly become violent.
translation_verse: Nyɩmɩsa kɩmɔɔ man bʋ bamʋ ɔsɩn.

english_verse: They don't know how to live in peace.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kayɩɩ yuuli kɛ ba asa tɔ.

english_verse: They don't even fear God.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kpini‐kpini.”

english_verse: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
translation_verse: To, an nyi fɛɛ asɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ, ɩɩ tɔwɛ ɩɩ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee buu nbara mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔkʋ mɛɛ nyɛ tɔwɛ sɛhn yɛ mɛɛ mʋ ɔ man waa alibi akʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ dulinyaa tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti o bun mʋ kʋpwɛ lɩɩ mu alibi sʋ.

english_verse: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ buu nbara mʋ lʋwɛ, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ o wu fɛɛ mʋ awaasa ɩ bʋrɔn mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ. Nbara mʋ ɩ dɛ sa nɩn kɛ a wu anɛ alibi. Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɩɩ bɩya ɩkpa kikyiigi

english_verse: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɔ bɔla sʋ kɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ mʋ kaapʋ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ nbara kibuu. Halɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ nbara mʋ kpini i gyii ɩmʋ kesintin Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ waa alibi nɩɩ ɔ pan Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa mʋ.

english_verse: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala kakɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ bɔla Yesu Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ asa yɛgɛ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ o kyula sa bamʋ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɔ nyɛ kenyiita gyoo ba fʋʋ Yesu Krisito kelewu mʋ nɩɩ o wu sa alibi mʋ. Ɔ bɩla lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ mʋ awaasa ɩ dɛ ɔkpa, nɩɩ ɛɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ Yesu gyii mʋ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ kʋtɔ sʋ nɩɩ an kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ? Sɛhn man bʋ tɔ. Mɩnɛ ɩ waa sʋ nɩɩ a mɛɛ taalɛ kaapʋ anɛ n‐yɩɩ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ man gyɛ nbara mʋ kibuu ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ i wu fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa i kyiigi, amaa ɩ lɩɩ anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ an nu kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ nyɩmɩsa kɩkɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ i wu fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ, amaa man gyɛ nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ.

english_verse: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ gyɛ Gyiwu abi nkʋn Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn? Ɔ man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn abɛɛ? Ɛɛhn! Ɔ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa Ɩbwaarɛ nɩn.

english_verse: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩnɩmʋ kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ nbara mʋ i biliŋi kɩyan abɛɛ? Ayee! Ɩ man dɛ kanɩn. Amaa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an buu nbara mʋ sʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

english_verse: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ an biti a tɔwɛ lɩɩ anɛ naana Abraham sʋ? Mɩnɛ ɩ waa Abraham asɩn mʋ tɔ?

english_verse: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ gyɛ Abraham awaasa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu mʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ, naafɔɔ ɔ sʋ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kaapʋ mʋ n‐yɩɩ, amaa man gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.”
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ nɩɩ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ? “Abraham kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii maŋa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.”

english_verse: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ Dawidi gbaa wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ kumu ɔkɔn wuya ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ mʋ awaasa sʋ. Nɩɩ Dawidi tɔwɛ yɛ,

english_verse: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
translation_verse: Kumu ɔkɔn wuya ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ man bɩla yɛ o nyiŋi mu alibi sʋ.”

english_verse: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
translation_verse: Kumu ɔkɔn mʋ nɩɩ Dawidi tɔwɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ, ɩ gyɛ sa Gyiwu abi nkʋn nɩn, abɛɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ Gyiwu abi gbaa? Nɩɩ an wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ, Abraham kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, nɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii maŋa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

english_verse: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
translation_verse: Saŋa mɔɔ tɔ nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ waa sa mʋ? Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩn oduu mʋ abɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩta tɩn oduu mʋ? Ɩ gyɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩta tɩn.

english_verse: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Abraham oduu katɩn mʋ gyɛ sɩnkaala nɩn, nɩɩ ɩ kaapʋ tɔ fɛɛ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man pɩɩta tɩn mu oduu mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi. Kanɩn sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ asa kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ gyii nɩɩ ba man pɩɩta tɩn bamʋ iduu mʋ kpini bamʋ‐sɛ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.

english_verse: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla ɔ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɩn iduu mʋ bamʋ‐sɛ, man gyɛ ba maa tɩn iduu sʋ, amaa lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ gbaa bamʋ, ba maa naa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɔkpa kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ anɛ naana Abraham natɛ sʋ pɔyɩ nɩɩ ɔ tɩn oduu mʋ.

english_verse: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa Abraham maa mʋ anaana‐bii yɛ bee biti ba baa ba gyɛ dulinyaa tɔ kapatɩyɛ agyipu. Falɛ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ kɩda mʋ man gyɛ nbara mʋ kibuu sʋ, amaa lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyula yɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
