translation_verse: Amaa Yesu gyii kiseepu kusun mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn gaa kɩ kyɔ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu banban mʋ lɛɛ. Nɩɩ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ Yesu waa yɩla anaa Ɩbwaarɛ nsana mʋ gbaa, kɩ bʋrɔn kɩ kyɔ kɩdɩdaa mʋ, nɩɩ kɩ bɩla kɩ sʋ kɔkɔlɔ kɩda danbɩrasa kɩ kyɔ kɩdɩdaa mʋ.

english_verse: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nɩɩ katɔwɛ kyula dɩdaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa maa asa nsana, nɩɩ kɩ daa ki du kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩn, naafɔɔ ɩ man tiri sa Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ ɔ bɩla waa kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
translation_verse: Amaa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ wu alibi lɩɩ asa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Saŋa kʋ ii biti ɔ ba kɛ mɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ n biti n waa katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ maa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ Gyiwu abi nsana.

english_verse: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ yɛ kɩ baa ki du fɛɛ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ n maa bamʋ naana ana a kyɩna waa mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n kɩtaa bamʋ abaa tɔ, gyankpaa bamʋ lɛɛ bamʋ lɩɩ Egyipiti mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba maa man gyii kesintin lɩɩ mɛ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ tɔ sʋ mʋ, n palɛ mɛ kamaa sa bamʋ.

english_verse: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Nkɛ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba falɛ tɔ mʋ, ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ n biti n waa maa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi nsana. N biti n taa mɛ nbara waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ, kɛ n kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ waa bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ. N biti n baa n gyɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ba baa ba gyɛ mɛ asa.

english_verse: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ ɔ kaapʋ mʋ tɔmaa abɛɛ mu‐nyi mu‐bii tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, ‘Bɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.’ Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti ɔ ba bɩɩ mɛ lɩɩ nbii sʋ naa fʋʋ abɩlɩsa sʋ.

english_verse: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
translation_verse: N biti n taa bamʋ alibi kɛɛ bamʋ, kɛ n man bɩla nyiŋi bamʋ alibi kakɛ akʋ. Mɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ n tɔwɛ kanɩn.”

english_verse: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ maa tɔwɛ lɩɩ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ katɔwɛ kyula gyankpaasa mʋ kɩ waa kɩdɩdaa. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ ba waa kɩdɩdaa, nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kyaarɛ mʋ, kii biti kɩ ba fɔyɩ.

english_verse: The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ katɔwɛ kyula gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa maa asa nsana mʋ, kɩ sʋ ɩmʋ kabwaarɛ‐sun nbara yɛ tantɩ kubuu opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kasɛ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taa kɩtaa lala sɩɩtasa barɩgɛ kɩmʋ tɔ ipula ɩnyɔ. Opula gyankpaasa mʋ tɔ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ nɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ fɩtɩla ɔgyagakpa yɛ teeburi yɛ bodobodo mʋ nɩɩ ba taa sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ bʋ.

english_verse: Behind the curtain was the most holy place.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩtaa mʋ kamaa ɩ gyɛ opula nyɔsapʋ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

english_verse: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ tʋtɔ nɩɩ suwa kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bee tuwa atɔ liifensa kɩmʋ sʋ, yɛ katɔwɛ kyula kɩdakaa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa suwa nyalɛ kɩmʋ mʋ bʋ. Kɩdakaa maŋa tɔ nɩɩ suwa bonburon mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ “Mana” agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ naana ana ofuli sʋ mʋ bʋ. Nɩɩ Aron kikpebi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ pɔrɔgɛ afatɛɛ bunboli, yɛ abuu panpasan anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi kyʋrɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara kudu mʋ gyaga sʋ mʋ bʋ.

english_verse: On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy. Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba waa atɔ anyɔ akʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ atɛɛ ba gyan kɩdakaa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋ tʋtɔ. Ba yaasɛ bamʋ atɛɛ bun kɩdakaa mʋ obunkpa nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ tʋtɔ yɛ Opula mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ alibi ɛɛ kɛɛ. Amaa an mɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ asɩn nɩmʋ kpini kasɛ sa fanɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn.

english_verse: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
translation_verse: Falɛ nɩɩ ba waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ yɩla, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu mʋ bee loo tantɩ opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa mʋ tɔ kakɛ kʋmaa bɛɛ waa bamʋ kiseepu kusun awaasa.

english_verse: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
translation_verse: Amaa, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ mʋ nkʋn ii loo Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ kesu kʋmaa tɔ mʋ, ee loo tʋtɔ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn nɩn. Nɩɩ atɔbwaaya nkalan nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ee loo tʋtɔ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taa kʋlɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kumu sʋ alibi yɛ alibi mʋ nɩɩ mʋ asa ba waa yɛgɛ ba man nyi yɛ ɩ gyɛ alibi mʋ.

english_verse: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
translation_verse: Bɔla ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɩ lɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ tantɩ kubuu lala gyankpaasa mʋ maa san ɔ yɩlɛ falɛ bɩrɛ, ɔkpa mʋ man pɩɩta bugi kpee Opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ kpini mʋ ɩ gyɛ ikisii lɩɩ agyitɔ kigyii yɛ atɔ nuusa sʋ yɛ atɔ kɩfwɩɩ yɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kigyee. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ansi sʋ awaasa nbara mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩya kabwaarɛ‐sun pʋpwɛ katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ tɔ. Krisito abi kpini ɩ gyɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kʋlʋn

english_verse: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
translation_verse: Amaa Krisito ba fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ sa atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ falɛ. Nɩɩ o loo tantɩ kubuu lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa, nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ kɩkparɛ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ nyɩmɩsa i yii kɩmʋ, nɩɩ kɩ man gyɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
translation_verse: Krisito maa loo tantɩ kubuu Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn kakpaa mʋ, ɔ man taa nbwɛ yɛ ɩnaatɛ nkalan loo naa waa kɔlɔŋɔ. Amaa ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ nkalan loo nɩn naa ka alibi kʋkɔ kɛ a nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ nkpa kakpaa.

english_verse: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
translation_verse: Dɩdaa lɛɛ mʋ bɩrɛ nbwɛ yɛ ɩnaatɛ nkalan, yɛ naatɛ kadanba mʋ nɩɩ ba tuwa mʋ nsɔ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ ŋmatɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba waa ineesi mʋ sʋ, kɛ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ ba nyɛ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ kɩfwɩɩ. Nɩɩ ɩnɩmʋ ɩ kan taalɛ fwɩɩ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ineesi mʋ,

english_verse: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
translation_verse: kanɩn bɩrɛ, Krisito nkalan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɔla Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ nkpa kakpaa sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa n‐yɩɩ waa kɔlɔŋɔ kparɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ, nɛnɛ sʋ nɩɩ mʋ nkalan maŋa ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ fwɩɩ anɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ awaasa libi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ anɛ ii kpee lewu tɔ mʋ? Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ a nyɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ kakpaa.

english_verse: In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nɩɩ ɔsa kʋ kan waa katɔwɛ yɩla lɩɩ mʋ kapatɩyɛ sʋ mʋ, i tiri fɛɛ ɔsa maŋa maa wu nɩn pɔyɩ kɛ i sun kusun.

english_verse: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ katɔwɛ yɩlasa mɛɛ taalɛ waa kusun yɛgɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa kɩmʋ mʋ, san ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ, amɔɔ o wu nɩn pɔyɩ kɛ ɩ waa kusun.

english_verse: Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ halɩɩ katɔwɛ kyula gyankpaasa mʋ gbaa nɩɩ nkalan ɩ man kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ mʋ, i mee sun kusun.

english_verse: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Mosesi lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ kpini tɔwɛ asa mʋ, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa ba waa mʋ, ɔ taa ɩnaatɛ yɛ nbwɛ nkalan bʋga yɛ nkyu. Nɩɩ ɔ taa kebula pɩpɛɛ taa ŋmina yɛ afatɛɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa nyɔ nkalan yɛ nkyu mʋ tɔ taa ŋmatɛ‐ŋmatɛ nbara ɔwʋlɛ mʋ yɛ asa mʋ kpini sʋ.

english_verse: He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nkalan nɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa fanɛ mʋ nsana, nɩɩ ɔ sa fanɛ kɔnɔ yɛ fan buu.”

english_verse: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fʋ kɩɩ mʋ, Mosesi Nbara saŋa mʋ, atɔbwaaya nkalan nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ fwɩɩ ineesi. Nɩɩ nkalan ɩ man kyɛɛgɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa alibi kɛɛ.

english_verse: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ tantɩ kubuu mʋ, yɛ atɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ yɩlɛ sa sʋsʋ gbaa‐gbaa lɛɛ mʋ, i tiri fɛɛ ba maa taa nkalan fwɩɩ ɩmʋ. Amaa sʋsʋ gbaa‐gbaa lɛɛ mʋ, ɩ dagaa ba maa taa nkalan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ atɔbwaaya lɛɛ mʋ fwɩɩ ɩmʋ.

english_verse: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Krisito man loo kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ asa ba taa bamʋ abaa waa mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ sʋsʋ lɛɛ kɩgbaa‐gbaa mʋ keduli. Amaa o loo sʋsʋ lɛɛ kɩgbaa‐gbaa mʋ tɔ naa yɩlɛ anɛ ayaa tɔ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ nbɩɩnbɩɩ.

english_verse: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Krisito man taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ ee loo sʋsʋ Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kesu kʋmaa, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ ɩɩ taa atɔbwaaya nkalan ee loo Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn kesu kʋmaa nɩmʋ mʋ.

english_verse: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
translation_verse: Kanɩn bɩrɛ, naafɔɔ Krisito ii biti ɔ ba ee gyii awʋrʋfɔ kesu kʋmaa lɩɩ koo saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ dulinyaa mʋ. Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ dulinyaa maa kpee kɩmʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, ɔ ba taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa n‐yɩɩ waa kɔlɔŋɔ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn lɛɛ alibi kakpaa.

english_verse: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
translation_verse: Ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ Krisito ba wu kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn kakpaa, kɛ ɔ lɛɛ asa gaalaagaa alibi. Nɩɩ ee biti o bwii ba kɩnyɔsapʋ, yɛgɛ ɩ man gyɛ alibi kɩlɛɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ ba. Amaa ee biti ɔ ba mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋla bee gyoo mʋ mʋ.

english_verse: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
translation_verse: Mosesi nbara mʋ man gyɛ kesintin atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kamaa ɩɩ ba mʋ gbaa‐gbaa, amaa ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ keduli nɩn. Lɩɩ falɛ sʋ mʋ atɔbwaaya saraga kɔlɔŋɔ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kesu‐kesu kanan mʋ nɩɩ nbara mʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ, mɛɛ taalɛ waa asa mʋ nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ naa lʋwɛ.

english_verse: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya saraga kɔlɔŋɔ ɩ dɩn taalɛ fwɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee sun Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ bamʋ alibi tɔ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn mʋ, naafɔɔ ba dɩn yɛgɛ saraga kɩlɛɛ, yɛgɛ ba man bɩla fɛ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ ba gyii alibi kʋpwɛ.

english_verse: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Krisito ɩɩ ba dulinyaa tɔ mʋ ɔ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, “Fu mee biti atɔbwaaya saraga kɩlɛɛ yɛ atɔ kɛkɛɛ, amaa fʋ sa mɛ nyɩmɩsa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ yɛ n ba taa lɛɛ saraga.

english_verse: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ, mɛ ndee! N ba nɩn kɛ n ba waa fu kebiti fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ mɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ daa kɩ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ, “Fu mee biti atɔbwaaya saraga kɩlɛɛ yɛ atɔ kɛkɛɛ, ɩmaa atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ bee tuwa fʋ kaswɩɩ sʋ bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga, yɛ alibi kɩtaa kɛɛ saraga kɩlɛɛ mʋ, ɩ mɛɛ bɔla fu ansi” .

english_verse: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Krisito bɩla bwii tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ mɛ ndee! N ba nɩn kɛ n ba waa fu kebiti.” Ɩmʋ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ atɔbwaaya saraga kɩlɛɛ mʋ tʋʋ, nɩɩ ɔ taa Krisito saraga kɩlɛɛ mʋ ba yii ayaa tɔ.

english_verse: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu kʋmaa nɩmʋ yɩlɛ ɛɛ waa mʋ kabwaarɛ‐sun asun kakɛ kʋmaa, nɩɩ saŋa kʋmaa ɛɛ lɛɛ ɩsaraga kʋlʋn maŋa, yɛgɛ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ lɛɛ asa alibi.

english_verse: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
translation_verse: Amaa Krisito bɩrɛ, taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɛɛ saraga kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn sa alibi kakpaa, nɩɩ ɔ naa kyɩna Ɩbwaarɛ kɩbaa gyisa sʋ.

english_verse: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
translation_verse: Taa lɩɩ kanɩn saŋa maŋa mʋ, o sii ɔ tɛ o gyoo fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ taa mʋ dʋn ana waa mʋ ayaa kasɛ.

english_verse: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ saraga kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ lɛɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ lɔŋɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ fwɩɩ bamʋ lɩɩ alibi tɔ mʋ yɩla sa Ɩbwaarɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ kakpaa.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ gbaa ii gyii asɩn nɩmʋ kesintin kaapʋ anɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gyankpaa tɔwɛ anɛ yɛ,

english_verse: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
translation_verse: “Nkɛ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba mʋ, katɔwɛ kyula nɩmʋ nɩɩ n biti n waa maa bamʋ nsana. Mɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ tɔwɛ kanɩn. N biti n taa mɛ nbara waa bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ kɛ n kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ.”

english_verse: by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
translation_verse: An taalɛ faa kɩtaa kunsa mʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Yesu ɩ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ bugi ɔkpa pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ sa nkpa mʋ, sa anɛ.

english_verse: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ a maa sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Oseepu kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkpaara asa sʋ mʋ,

english_verse: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
translation_verse: fan yɛgɛ a nin tiri Ɩbwaarɛ. Fan yɛgɛ a taa kɔkɔlɔ fuuli yɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ kamɔɔrɛ‐gyii, yɛ nfɛɛrɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ kaapʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ taa anɛ alibi kɛɛ anɛ, yɛ anɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa nkyu kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ fwɩɩ mʋ, ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

english_verse: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
translation_verse: Fan yɛgɛ a kɩtaa tamaa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ an yɩlɛ sʋ an tɔwɛ mʋ tɔ kenken, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ mʋ, ɩɩ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ ee biti ɔ waa mʋ.

english_verse: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
translation_verse: Fan yɛgɛ a fɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ a tɔwɛ an waa abaa, kɛ a kyɛ abaa tɔ, kɛ a waa adanbɩrasa sa abaa.

english_verse: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
translation_verse: Fan man yɛgɛ kɛ a yɛgɛ kɩgyaŋɛ bʋga, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ akʋ ba mɛɛ gyaŋɛ bɛɛ bʋga mʋ, amaa fan yɛgɛ a waa abaa aniya tɔ, fɛɛ fan maa kɩɩ wu fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Kakɛ lala mʋ kii biti kɩ ba mʋ ki tiri tɔ.

english_verse: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ a maa wʋla bɩɩ kesintin asɩn nɩmʋ, nɩɩ a kɩɩ ansi an san an waa alibi bɩrɛ, saraga kɩlɛɛ sa alibi kʋ man bɩla kɩ bʋ tɔ.

english_verse: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
translation_verse: Amaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ san ɩ gyɛ yɛ, kɔkɔlɔ ŋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ kii gyoo Ɩbwaarɛ kʋpwɛ kubun yɛ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba dɩyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ dʋn ana mʋ.

english_verse: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina Mosesi nbara mʋ, nɩɩ asa anyɔ abɛɛ asa asá be gyii kesintin yɛ ɔsa mʋ kina kanɩn nbara mʋ bɩrɛ, be mee wu mʋ kʋwɛɛ bɛɛ mɔɔ mʋ nɩn.

english_verse: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kanɩn bɩrɛ, bamʋ nɩɩ ba tʋʋ kɩyaa saalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐bii mɔɔ de? Kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ lala kɩɩ dɛ sa bamʋ, fɛɛ ba maa man taa Ɩbwaarɛ katɔwɛ kyula nkalan mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ fwɩɩ bamʋ lɩɩ alibi tɔ mʋ tɩɩ sɛhn, nɩɩ ba bɩla bwii saalɛ Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ bwaala asa mʋ?

english_verse: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ an nyi Ɩbwaarɛ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ ɩ tɛɛla alibi awaapʋ, Nɩɩ mɛ ii biti n bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ.” Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla bwii tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ n biti n gyii mɛ asa asɩn.”

english_verse: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ gyɛ kufuu fɛɛ ɔkʋ maa tɩyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Don't forget all the hard times you went through when you first received the light.
translation_verse: Fan nyiŋi fanɛ nkɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bugi fanɛ ansi, nɩɩ fan wu mʋ ɔkpa, nɩɩ fan yɩlɛ tɔ gyii awʋrʋfɔ, yɛgɛ fanɛ abaa ɩ man yɔɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
translation_verse: Saŋa kʋ tɔ mɔɔ mʋ, fan nyɛ asebi yɛ kanyanla asa nsana tɔ, ɩkʋ mɔɔ mʋ, fan kyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ kanɩn awʋrʋfɔ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
translation_verse: Fan su, nɩɩ fan yɩyɛɛ, nɩɩ fan sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɩtaa bamʋ tii obu mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ba maa kɔɔlɛ fanɛ npatɩyɛ gbaa mʋ, ɩ man kɔ fanɛ, amaa fanɛ ansi i gyii nɩn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ gbaa‐gbaa fanɛ fen nyi fɛɛ fan maa sʋ npatɩyɛ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ kakpaa sʋsʋ.

english_verse: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, bee biti ba nyɛ nkpa. Amaa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan bwii kamaa bɩrɛ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ bɔla mi ansi.”

english_verse: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ an man gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee bwii kamaa nɩɩ ba mɛɛ nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ, amaa an gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ an kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, nɩɩ an nyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ ɩ gyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii? Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɩ gyɛ yɛ an nyi fɛɛ an biti a nyɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an sʋ ɩmʋ tamaa, nɩɩ a mee wu ɩmʋ yɛ anɛ ansi yɛgɛ ɩ bʋ tɔ kesintin mɔɔ.

english_verse: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
translation_verse: Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi i gyii adɩdaapʋ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ an nu kasɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ kɔnɔsa nɩɩ ɔ taa tʋʋ dulinyaa. Ɩmʋ sʋ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ a mee wu yɛ ansi mʋ, ɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ an wu falɛ.

english_verse: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
translation_verse: Nowa kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mu kumu tɔ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man pɩɩta waa mʋ sʋ mʋ, o nu sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ salɛ ɔkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɔlɩgɛ maa mʋ kɩkpaara sʋ abi kpini. Lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, o bun dulinyaa kpini kʋpwɛ, nɩɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ɩkpa kyiigisa wuya ana mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ.

english_verse: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
translation_verse: Abraham kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ɔ kʋsʋ lɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ mʋ kpee kasɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba biliŋi mʋ kapatɩyɛ kakaakʋ mʋ, o nu sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ ee kpee, yɛgɛ ɔ man nyi katɩn mʋ ee kpee.

english_verse: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, maa fʋʋ kasɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa mʋ mʋ, o yii ɩtantɩ abu kyɩna tɔ fɛɛ ɔfɔ. Nɩɩ kanɩn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ mu‐bii Isaki, yɛ mʋ kanaana‐bii Gyekobu mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ, gbaa ba kyɩna.

english_verse: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
translation_verse: Abraham waa kanɩn lɩɩ fɛɛ mu ansi ɩɩ gyan kadɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa ɩ kyʋrɔɔ kɩmʋ, nɩɩ o yii kɩmʋ kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔ pɔyɩ kɩmʋ yɩla kakpaa mʋ.

english_verse: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
translation_verse: Sara kɩkɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii taa tii mu‐kuli Abraham lɛɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ kebii. Bamʋ kpini ba bɩlɛ, nɩɩ Sara gyɛ ogbentipu. Amaa ba kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ fɛɛ ee biti ɔ sa bamʋ kebii mʋ, gyɛ kesintin wuya.

english_verse: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Abraham mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bɩlɛ kyʋn sʋ, halɩɩ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ bɩla taalɛ kʋʋgɛ gbaa mʋ, kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa, nɩɩ mʋ kɩnaana kɩ nyɛ akyɔ fɛɛ sʋsʋ akyikpebi abɛɛ ɔpʋ kɔnɔ kɩkyafa, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ kalɛ bamʋ.

english_verse: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
translation_verse: Asa nɩmʋ kpini ba sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ nkpa tɔ halɩɩ naa fʋʋ bamʋ lewu. Nɩɩ ba man nyɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ yɛ ee biti ɔ sa bamʋ mʋ. Ba wu ɩmʋ kata‐kata, nɩɩ ɩ waa bamʋ ɔkɔn. Nɩɩ ba kɔɔlɛ kyula nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ba gyɛ afɔ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ kasɛ kanɩmʋ sʋ asa.

english_verse: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ kanɩn asɩn mʋ, ɩ kaapʋ nɩn fɛɛ bɛɛ kɩɩ bee biti bamʋ gbaa‐gbaa bamʋ ayɛ.

english_verse: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bɛɛ daa bɛɛ fɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ ɔsʋwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ kɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, naafɔɔ ba dɩn nyɛ ɔkpa bwii kpee tʋtɔ.

english_verse: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
translation_verse: Amaa bɛɛ kɩɩ bee biti kadɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn kɩ kyɔ kasɛ sʋ lɛɛ nɩmʋ. Kɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩmʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ sʋsʋ mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɩ man sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ipeeli sa bamʋ fɛɛ ba maa tɩɩ mʋ yɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ lɔŋɔ sʋsʋ kadɛ lala yɩla sa bamʋ.

english_verse: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla tɔwɛ Abraham yɛ, “Taa bɔla fu‐bii Isaki sʋ nɩɩ fii biti fʋ nyɛ anaana‐bii mʋ nɩɩ n da mɛ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa fʋ mʋ.”

english_verse: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
translation_verse: Abraham dan o nyi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ bɩla taalɛ kyiŋi Isaki lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Nɩɩ fʋ kɩɩ mʋ, i du nɩn fɛɛ o wu nɩn, nɩɩ Abraham bwii nyɛ mʋ, mʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: Isaac had faith, and he promised blessings to Jacob and Esau.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Isaki kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, ɔ waa Gyekobu maa Esawu kʋsee lɩɩ bamʋ kakaakʋ kakyɩna tɔ sʋ.

english_verse: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Gyekobu kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti o wu mʋ, ɔ waa Gyosefu mu‐bii ana anyɔ kusee. Ɔ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ kpasa mu kikpebi nɩɩ ɔ yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Gyosefu kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ mu lewu tiri tɔ mʋ, ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi yɛ saŋa kʋ ɩ ba bee biti ba lɩɩ Egyipiti tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ sa bamʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, bɛɛ kan bɛɛ lɩɩ mʋ, ba nyiŋi mʋ awiya sʋ kɛ ba taa.

english_verse: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Mosesi akʋʋgɛpʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋgɛ Mosesi mʋ, ba taa mʋ baala ɩbwaayɩ ɩsa, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba wu Mosesi maa pɔyɩ ɔdan, yɛgɛ ba man buu Owura kɔnɔsa mʋ gbaa mʋ ɩ man sʋ bamʋ kufuu.

english_verse: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Mosesi kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋsʋ dan mʋ o mee biti ba tɩɩ mʋ yɛ ɔ gyɛ Egyipiti Owura Faro mu‐bii kyɩɩsa mu‐bii.

english_verse: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ fɛɛ maa Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba bʋga gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ, ɩ kyɔ fɛɛ o gyii ɔkɔn kakyɩna libi tɔ saŋa gbɛrɛɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
translation_verse: Mosesi wu fɛɛ ɩ dagaa maa gyii ipeeli lɩɩ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ mʋ sʋ, ɩ kyɔ Egyipiti npatɩyɛ kparɛ kpini. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mu ansi ɩ gyan kʋkɔka mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ ka mʋ kakɛ kakʋ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ ɔ kʋsʋ lɩɩ Egyipiti tɔ, yɛgɛ ɔ man sʋ owura agbʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ kufuu. Ɔ kpan mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɩlɛ tɔ lɩɩ fɛɛ o wu Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mee wu mʋ mʋ.

english_verse: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, o yii Kanyaŋɛ Nbunɔ kigyii kakɛ mʋ. Kanɩn kakɛ maŋa tɔ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba taa atɔbwaaya nkalan ŋmatɛ‐ŋmatɛ bamʋ nbunɔ mʋ, nɩɩ ɩ yɛgɛ nbii gyankpaasa ɔmɔɔpʋ mʋ man nyɛ mata bamʋ.

english_verse: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ ba natɛ Ɔpʋ Pɩpɛɛ mʋ tɔ faa fɛɛ kasɛ wʋlɛsa sʋ. Amaa Egyipiti abi ba maa kpan bamʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ ba waa kanɩn mʋ, nkyu mʋ i gyii bamʋ.

english_verse: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ nɩɩ ba natɛ kyaabɔɔ Gyeriko kadɛ nkɛ nsunɔ nɩɩ kɩmʋ ɩpɔrɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyaabɔɔ kadɛ mʋ ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ.

english_verse: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ asɩn nɩɩ n bɩla yɛ n tɔwɛ tii sʋ? N mɛɛ yɛ n nyɛ saŋa tɔwɛ adɩdaapʋ asa fɛɛ Gideyon maa Baraki yɛ Samson yɛ Gyefeta yɛ Dawidi yɛ Samuweli yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ asɩn tii sʋ.

english_verse: and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, bamʋ akʋ ba dunla fʋlɔn yen‐yen, nɩɩ bamʋ akʋ ba lɩɩ isakunde lewu tɔ. Bamʋ akʋ kapɔɔsɛ ki biliŋi ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ba waa alʋlʋnpʋ ɩkɔ tɔ gyii bamʋ dun ana ndɛndɛmandɛ anaakɔpʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, akyɩɩ akʋ ba nyɛ bamʋ‐nyi mu‐bii ana mʋ nɩɩ ba wu, nɩɩ ba bwii kyiŋi ba nkpa tɔ. Akʋ ba nyɛ kanyanla nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tɔ kina, fɛɛ ba mɛɛ lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kamaa kɛ ba nyɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ lewu tɔ, amaa ba kyula fɛɛ bee biti ba wu, kɛ ba nyɛ kelewu kyiŋi yɛ nkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn.

english_verse: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, asa ba yooli akʋ nɩɩ ba tɩŋɛ akʋ, nɩɩ ba da akʋ mɔɔ ɩgbɛɛ taa bamʋ naa tii ibu to.

english_verse: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, asa ba twɩɩ akʋ abuu mɔɔ bamʋ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba baŋɩ bamʋ tɔ anyɔ‐anyɔ, nɩɩ akʋ mɔɔ ba taa isakunde mɔɔ bamʋ. Akʋ ba taa nbʋlʋpʋ yɛ nbwɛ ɩwʋlɛ ba bun, yɛgɛ ba naa ba naa kitiri tɔ yɛ awʋrʋfɔ yɛ kanyanla tɔ.

english_verse: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
translation_verse: Dulinya nɩmʋ tɔ asa libi ba man bɩla dagaa fɛɛ ba maa kanɩn asa maŋa ba kyɩna bʋga. Bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba sʋ ba naa bɛɛ yaalɛ ifuli sʋ yɛ abɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ bɛɛ baala afʋlɛ ɩbɔ tɔ yɛ kasɛ ɩbɔ lala tɔ.

english_verse: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
translation_verse: Asa banɩmʋ kpini ba nyɛ kɩyɩn lɩɩ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ, yɛgɛ bamʋ ɔkʋ man nyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ da mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ sa bamʋ mʋ.

english_verse: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ ŋmina atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ yɩla anɛ, nɩɩ ngbaa bamʋ bee biti ba nyɛ, amaa ba man nyɛ bamʋ kusun danbɩrasa mʋ kʋkɔka, amɔɔ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ an biti a lʋwɛ anɛ kusun pɔyɩ kɛ anaa bamʋ kpini a naa bʋga nyɛ kʋkɔka mʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ.

english_verse: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ asa damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ifuli kaapʋ, nɩɩ ba lɛɛ bamʋ ansi bɛɛ kɩɩ anɛ falɛ sʋ mʋ, fan yɛgɛ a lɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kii tii anɛ ɔkpa, yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ an tɩyɛ alibi tɔ bilen‐bilen mʋ fɔyɩ, kɛ a payɩ ansi sɩlɛ kɩsɩlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ anɛ ansi tɔ mʋ naa fʋʋ ɔkaa.

english_verse: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
translation_verse: Fan yɛgɛ an baa an kɩɩ Yesu. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ anɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ kɩ gyan mʋ sʋ lɩɩ npiili asɛ, nɩɩ mʋ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ a taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ naa fʋʋ ɔkaa. Nɩɩ o nyi ɔkɔn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ kii gyoo mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ man taa oyu laasa lewu yɛ ɩmʋ ipeeli mʋ tɩɩ sɛhn. Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɔ tɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ gyisa sʋ.

english_verse: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu yɩlɛ tɔ gyii awʋrʋfɔ alibi awaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ mʋ akisipu mʋ abaa tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩ man yɔɔ fanɛ kɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ ɩ man nyita fanɛ.

english_verse: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
translation_verse: Fɛn tan asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ waa fanɛ fɛɛ mu‐bii ana mʋ sʋ? Ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ yɛ, “Mi‐bii, nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kan ɛɛ bɩla fʋ mʋ, man taa ɩmʋ waa ɩwɔgya asɩn. Nɩɩ ɛɛ kan ɛɛ bɩɩtɛ fʋ kʋsʋ mʋ, man yɛgɛ fʋ abaa tɔ ɩ yɔɔ.

english_verse: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
translation_verse: Fan nyɛ nkɔlɔ yɩlɛ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ fɛɛ kɩbɩla, Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ waa fanɛ kanɩn fɛɛ mu‐bii ana. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kebii mɔɔ nɩɩ mʋ‐sɛ mɛɛ bɩla mʋ?

english_verse: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ fan man kan nyɛ kɩbɩla fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ mu‐bii kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩɩ nyɛ mʋ, kanɩn bɩrɛ fan man gyɛ mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mu‐bii ana, ɔkʋ mu‐bii ana ɩ gyɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ, an sʋ kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ asɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩla anɛ, yɛgɛ an sʋ bɛɛrɛɛ an sa bamʋ. Nɩɩ mɩnɛ sʋ nɩɩ a mɛɛ bɩya anɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ gaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ Anɛ‐Sɛ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ, kɛ a nyɛ nkpa?

english_verse: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anɛ kasɛ sʋ asɛ bɛɛ bɩla anɛ saŋa gbɛrɛɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ bʋrɔn sa bamʋ mʋ. Amaa anɛ kɩdanbɩrasa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ bɩla anɛ, kɛ a nyɛ‐nyɛ mʋ kɩbaabʋtɔ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ.

english_verse: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
translation_verse: Kɩbɩla kʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ kɩ bʋkɔn. Opula maŋa tɔ mʋ ɩɩ kɔɔ nɩn. Amaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba nu sa kɩbɩla mʋ, ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ kakyɩna tɔ ɩkpa i kyiigi, nɩɩ ɩɩ sa bamʋ kayɩɩ yuuli.

english_verse: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan man yɛgɛ fanɛ abaa a yɔɔ fanɛ, amaa fan kʋsʋ yɩlɛ kɩtaa tɔ kinkin.

english_verse: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
translation_verse: Fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ fɛn naa sʋ mʋ ɩ waa ntintin, kɛ ayaa yɛ abaa wusa wuya ana ba man fɔyɩ ɔkpa, amaa ba nyɛ ɔlʋn. Fan kɩɩ sʋ kɛ a man bwii kamaa

english_verse: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
translation_verse: Fan baa fan da sʋ kɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ man pan Ɩbwaarɛ bwaala, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man ba waa fɛɛ oyu bʋlɩlɩsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dan nɩɩ kɩɩ bɩya asɩn nɩɩ kii nyita asa gaalaagaa mʋ.

english_verse: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
translation_verse: Fan nyiŋi fɛɛ fan man ba Sinayi kɩbɩɩ mʋ asɛ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ adɩdaapʋ mʋ ba nin tiri kɩmʋ, ɩmaa kɩmʋ fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ dɛɛ yen‐yen, yɛgɛ ɩɩ waa kitentenbiri tumm, nɩɩ afuu lala ɩ maa da mʋ.

english_verse: or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba nu kabɩlɛ lala kʋ kusu yɛ ɔbɩlɛ kʋ ɩlawʋ maa tɔwɛ bamʋ asɩn, nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ ba kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ ɔ yɛgɛ katɔwɛ.

english_verse: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ asa mʋ ba man taalɛ yɩlɛ kɔnɔsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ mʋ. Kɔnɔsa mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Halɩɩ nɩɩ kʋtɔbwaaya gbaa kan mata kɩbɩɩ mʋ, ba tʋʋ abuu mɔɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
translation_verse: Amaa fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ fan nyɛ ɔkpa ba Siyon kɩbɩɩ mʋ asɛ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ sʋsʋ Gyerusalem, Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ kadɛ lala. Fan ba opula mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ngbɩn‐ngbɩn kensi‐gyii ngyaŋɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan ba Ɩbwaarɛ nbii gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ bamʋ atɩɩ sʋsʋ. Nɩɩ fan ba Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ asa kpini asɩn ogyipu mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ fan ba asa mʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ bamʋ kala ana ba ba waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan ba Yesu asɛ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ asa yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nsana sa katɔwɛ kyula pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa mʋ, nɩɩ mʋ ɩ taa mʋ nkalan ŋmatɛ, nɩɩ mʋ nkalan mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa ɩ kyɔ Abeli lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
translation_verse: Fan kɩɩ yɛ ɔdan kɛ fan man kina Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ asɩn mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kina ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn kasɛ sʋ nfɩɩ gbaa mʋ, ba man taalɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ tɔ. Kanɩn bɩrɛ, nɩɩ an kan kina ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ɛɛ sa anɛ asɛ kunu mɔɔ de, nɛnɛ nɩɩ an taalɛ sɩlɛ lɩɩ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ tɔ?

english_verse: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
translation_verse: Kanɩn saŋa maŋa tɔ Ɩbwaarɛ maa tɔwɛ mʋ, kasɛ ki gyingyen. Amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ, “Man gyɛ kasɛ nkʋn nɩɩ n biti n bɩla gyingyen, amaa sʋsʋ gbaa tii sʋ.”

english_verse: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
translation_verse: Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ “N biti n bɩla mʋ,” Ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ atɔ tʋʋsa mʋ ii biti i gyingyen kɛ ɩ lɩɩ kyʋn, kɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ taalɛ gyingyen mʋ i sii.

english_verse: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan yɛgɛ a taa afwaala lala sa Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ a maa nyɛ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ taalɛ gyingyen. Kɛ a taa kabwaarɛ sɩlɛ yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ sun Ɩbwaarɛ, ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bɔla mu ansi.

english_verse: Our God is like a destructive fire!
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyɛ fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ dɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa ɛɛ lʋwɛ.

english_verse: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
translation_verse: Fan kyaga sʋ fen biti abaa fɛɛ kinyi‐bii Krisito tɔ.

english_verse: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
translation_verse: Fan nyiŋi kɛ fan kɩtaa afɔ fanɛ akpaara sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ akʋ ba maa waa kanɩn mʋ, ba kɩtaa Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ kɩfɔ yɛgɛ ba man da sʋ.

english_verse: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
translation_verse: Fan nyiŋi Krisito abii mʋ nɩɩ ba tii bamʋ obu mʋ, fɛɛ fan maa tii bamʋ sʋ obu otiikpa mʋ. Kɛ fan nyiŋi bamʋ nɩɩ bee gyii awʋrʋfɔ mʋ, fɛɛ fanɛ ii gyii kanɩn awʋrʋfɔ maŋa.

english_verse: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
translation_verse: Fan man yɛgɛ kufulee biti bansa kɩ baa kɩ bʋ fanɛ kakyɩna tɔ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ mʋ ɩ kʋkwɛ fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kakaakʋ n mɛɛ yɛ n yɛgɛ fanɛ, nɩɩ kakaakʋ n mɛɛ yɛ n kina fan.”

english_verse: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ an taalɛ a tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ mɛ ɔkyɛtɔpʋ, n mɛɛ yɛ n baa n sʋ kufuu. Mɩnɛ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa ɩ taalɛ waa mɛ?”

english_verse: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
translation_verse: Fan nyiŋi fanɛ agyankpaapʋ dɩdaa mʋ nɩɩ ba da tɔwɛ fanɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ. Fan yɛgɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ ɩ baa ɩ gyan kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba kyɩna kakyɩna danbɩrasa mʋ, kɛ fan kasɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
translation_verse: Kanan mʋ nɩɩ Yesu Krisito du ndee mʋ, kanɩn nɩɩ o du ndaga, nɩɩ kanɩn nɩɩ ee biti ɔ baa o du kakpaa.

english_verse: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
translation_verse: Fan man yɛgɛ akaapʋ‐bi kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ ɩkʋ ɩ lɛɛ fanɛ lɩɩ kesintin ɔkpa mʋ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩ bʋrɔn sa anɛ fɛɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kayaayu mʋ kɩ sa anɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ ɔlʋn, nɩɩ ɩ man waa fɛɛ nbara mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ sa agyitɔ kigyii, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ akʋ ba gyii ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ tɔnɔ sa bamʋ mʋ.

english_verse: But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ dɩdaa mʋ, anɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi an sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga kɩmʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ aseepu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ tantɩ kubuu lala mʋ tɔ bɛɛ lɛɛ saraga mʋ, ba man sʋ ɔkpa be gyii ɩmʋ atɔ.

english_verse: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ oseepu bɩlɩsa asɛ ɩɩ taa atɔbwaaya nkalan ee loo Opula Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa Kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ tɔ ɛɛ lɛɛ alibi kɩtaakɛɛ saraga, amaa atɔbwaaya mʋ inun bɩrɛ bee tuwa ɩmʋ kanɩn kadɛ mʋ nbwama nɩn.

english_verse: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ Yesu gbaa gyii awʋrʋfɔ kadɛ nbwama, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa nkalan fwɩɩ asa lɩɩ bamʋ alibi tɔ.

english_verse: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ fan yɛgɛ a lɩɩ kpee mʋ asɛ kadɛ mʋ nbwama naa gyii ipeeli mʋ nɩɩ o gyii mʋ.

english_verse: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, a man sʋ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ a biti a kyɩna kɩmʋ tɔ kakpaa, amaa an kɩɩ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba mʋ ɔkpa nɩn.

english_verse: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
translation_verse: Amaa fan man tan adanbɩrasa kɩwaa sʋ, kɛ fan baa fɛn kyɛ abaa tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ falɛ ɩsaraga ɩnɩmʋ ɩ bɔla Ɩbwaarɛ ansi.

english_verse: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
translation_verse: Fan nu bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyankpaa fanɛ kabwaarɛ‐sun mʋ tɔ asɛ, kɛ fan bɩya fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa bamʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩɩ kɩɩ fanɛ kala ana sʋ kakɛ kʋmaa, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taasɛ bamʋ lɩɩ fanɛ ana sʋ. Nɩɩ fan kan nu bamʋ asɛ mʋ, ɩɩ yɛgɛ ba sun kusun mʋ yɛ kensi‐gyii, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn, bee biti ba taa kɔkɔlɔ nyita waa kusun mʋ, ɩmɛɛ mʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyɛ fanɛ tɔ.

english_verse: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
translation_verse: N kʋlɛ fanɛ gaa fɛɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ fɔnfɔn sa mɛ, kɛ n nyɛ ɔkpa bwii ba fanɛ asɛ bilen.

english_verse: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyɛ kayɩɩ‐yuuli wuya, mʋ ɩ bɔla katɔwɛ kyula mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ nkalan sʋ kyiŋi anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito lɩɩ lewu tɔ. Yesu ɩ gyɛ nbʋlʋpʋ Ɔkpapʋ kparɛ mʋ.

english_verse: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ n kʋlɛ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ sa fanɛ atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ii tiri fanɛ, kɛ fɛn taalɛ waa mu kebiti. Kɛ ɔ bɔla Yesu Krisito sʋ taa anɛ waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ bɔla mu ansi. Yesu Krisito nɩɩ bʋnyaa gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ kakpaa. Amen.

english_verse: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
translation_verse: Mɛ tɔmaa ana Krisito abuupu, n kʋlɛ fanɛ gaa fɛɛ fen nu mɛ asɩn tɔwɛ waasa anɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n kyʋrɔɔ n sa fanɛ falɛ man bʋnswɩɩ.

english_verse: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
translation_verse: N biti fɛɛ fɛn bɩɩ fɛɛ ba tigi anɛ bɩrɩsa Timoti mʋ nɩɩ ba daa kɩtaa tii obu mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan ba bilen mʋ, maa mʋ an biti a ba fanɛ asɛ.

english_verse: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
translation_verse: Fan fwaala fanɛ agyankpaapʋ mʋ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ kpini. Krisito abi mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Itali mʋ gbaa bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ.

english_verse: I pray that God will be kind to all of you!
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala kɩ baa kɩ bʋ fanɛ kpini asɛ. Amen.

english_verse: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kan tu fanɛ mʋ, fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii.

english_verse: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
translation_verse: Fan kɩtaa kanɩn kɔkɔlɔ ɔsɩɩ mʋ kpee ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ fan kan waa kanɩn mʋ, ɩɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fɛn dan naa fʋʋ ɔkaa, kɛ kʋtɔ danbɩrasa kʋ ɔ man pan fanɛ.

english_verse: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan ee biti kanyaasɩn de, ɔ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, ee biti ɔ sa mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ sa asa atɔ kɔkɔlɔ fuuli sʋ yɛgɛ ɔ man taasɛ kɔnɔ nkyu.

english_verse: But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers
translation_verse: Amaa obuupu atɔ wuya ɔ yɛgɛ mu ansi i gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa bɩya mʋ kasɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ee biti o wu kyʋn fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ iyu ntʋtʋlɛ danbɩrasa ɩ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩɩ ɩɩ kpɩkpaa mʋ.

english_verse: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
translation_verse: Kpalɩ fɛɛ kyɔwɛ ɩɩ lɩɩ ɩmʋ sʋ, kɛ ɩmʋ afatɛɛ mʋ ɩɩ bwɩɩ kɛ ntʋtʋlɛ danbɩrasa mʋ i nyita. Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn sʋ nɩɩ atɔ wuya gbaa ii biti i nyita yɛgɛ ɔ san ɛɛ fɛ ɩmʋ nfɛɛrɛ kʋlʋn maŋa.

english_verse: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii gyii Sɩtaanɛ sʋ mʋ, ɩ waa mʋ ɔkɔn. Kpalɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taalɛ gyii Sɩtaanɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ ee biti ɔ taa sa bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti mʋ mʋ.

english_verse: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa kan nyɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ mʋ, ɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ biti libi sʋ, nɩɩ ɩɩ pɩna mʋ ii kpee kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ tɔ.

english_verse: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɔkɔlɔ biti libi mʋ kan dan mʋ, kɩ nyɛ ɔtɔ kɛ kɩɩ kʋʋgɛ alibi, nɩɩ alibi mɔɔ ɩ kan dan lʋwɛ mʋ, a kʋʋgɛ lewu nɩn.

english_verse: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ danbɩrasa yɛ kakɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋrɔn kyɛɛkyɛɛ mʋ ɩɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn. Atɔ nɩmʋ kpini ɛɛ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ fɩtɩla kpini mʋ asɛ. Mɛɛ mʋ ɔ mɛɛ kyɛɛgɛ kakaakʋ, nɩɩ kitentenbiri man bʋ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth. Hearing and Obeying
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɩpɩrɛ sʋ nɩn, nɩɩ ɔ bɔla mʋ kesintin asɩn mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ nkpa pʋpwɛ, nɩɩ ɔ waa anɛ mʋ atɔ tʋʋsa tɔ kpini agyankpaapʋ. Fan nu kɛ fan waa

english_verse: My dear friends, you should be quick to listen and slow to speak or to get angry.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan baa fan nyi ɩnɩmʋ. Ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɔ nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ kɛ o nu asɩn, amaa ɔ man kpaala tɔwɛ asɩn, nɩɩ ɔ man nyɛ agbʋ bilen‐bilen.

english_verse: If you are angry, you cannot do any of the good things God wants done.
translation_verse: Kpalɩ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa agbʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti mʋ.

english_verse: You must stop doing anything immoral or evil. Instead be humble and accept the message planted in you to save you.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kɩtaa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ ineesi kpini tɔ, yɛ alibi kʋmaa sʋ kɛ fan bɩya fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ, kɔɔlɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba duu fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ mʋ, kɛ ɩ mɔlɩgɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: Obey God's message! Don't fool yourselves by just listening to it.
translation_verse: Fan man waa asɩn mʋ anupu nkʋn, amaa fan waa ɩmʋ awaasa, nɩɩ man gyɛ kanɩn mʋ fan pɩna fanɛ n‐yɩɩ nɩn.

english_verse: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ man waa ɩmʋ awaasa mʋ, o du nɩn fɛɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa digi kɩɩ mu ansi tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: and forget what they look like as soon as they leave.
translation_verse: Ɛ kɩɩ wu mʋ n‐yɩɩ kɛ ɔ kyʋn, opula maŋa tɔ mʋ ɔ tan kanan mʋ nɩɩ o du mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ gyaga ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ alibi kpini‐kpini, nɩɩ kɩɩ taalɛ lɛɛ asa lɩɩ alibi tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ man tan sʋ, amaa nɩɩ ee buu nɩɩ ɛɛ waa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ee nu mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa mʋ kusee.

english_verse: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
translation_verse: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɛ ɔ gyɛ ɔbwaarɛ‐sunpu danbɩrasa, yɛgɛ ɔ mɛɛ kɩtaa mʋ kanandʋlʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɩn tɔwɛsa sʋ mʋ, ɛɛ pɩna mʋ n‐yɩɩ nɩn, nɩɩ mʋ kabwaarɛ‐sun kɩ gyɛ kɩyan.

english_verse: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana. Fɛɛ fan maa gyɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito bʋnyaa wuya akɔɔlɛ‐gyipu sʋ mʋ, fan man kan kɩɩ daadimaadi ansi tɔ kɛ fan waa nmɛɛgɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ nɩɩ afulee wuya bun ɩkaalɛ danbɩrasa nɩɩ ɔ waa suwa kepini ba fanɛ nsarɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ otiripu gbaa bun ɩkaalɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ danbɩrasa ba tʋtɔ.

english_verse: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fan taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa afulee wuya tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Kyɩna kagya danbɩrasa nɩmʋ sʋ,” kɛ fan tɔwɛ otiripu mɔɔ yɛ, “Yɩlɛ tʋtɔ” abɛɛ “Kyɩna kasɛ tʋtɔ.”

english_verse: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ mʋ, man gyɛ nmɛɛgɛ tɔ nɩɩ fan bɩya fanɛ nsana tɔ falɛ? Nɩɩ fan kan waa kanɩn mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fan sʋ nfɛɛrɛ libi fɛn talɛ yɛ ɔkʋ dagaa ɔ kyɔ ɔkʋ.

english_verse: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana danbɩrasa, fan nu mɛ asɛ. Bamʋ nɩɩ dulinyaa nɩmʋ tɔ bɛɛ kɩɩ bamʋ yɛ ba gyɛ atiripu mʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ lɛɛ bamʋ kɛ ba be biliŋi kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii atɔ wuya ana. Lɩɩ fɛɛ bee biti ba bɛ nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ ee biti ɔ taa sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kebiti sa mʋ mʋ?

english_verse: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
translation_verse: Amaa fɛn kɩɩ atiripu mʋ kɩyan. Man gyɛ afulee wuya ana ɩɩ nyanla fanɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩɩtɛ fanɛ bee kpee asɩn igyikpa mʋ?

english_verse: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ nbara mʋ tɔ kɩlala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Biti fʋ tɔɔmaa fɛɛ fʋ n‐yɩɩ.” Nɩɩ fan kan buu nbara nɩmʋ mʋ, fan waa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dagaa nɩmʋ.

english_verse: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ fan kɩɩ asa tɔ waa nmɛɛgɛ tɔ bɩrɛ fan waa alibi nɩmʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ nbara sʋ mʋ i bun fanɛ kʋpwɛ fɛɛ fan gyɛ nbara mʋ anyitapu.

english_verse: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
translation_verse: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o buu nbara mʋ kpini, amaa nɩɩ ɛɛ fɔyɩ ɩmʋ tɔ kʋkʋlʋn gbaa mʋ, o gyii kʋpwɛ ɩmʋ kpini‐kpini tɔ.

english_verse: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Man lɩɩ fʋ‐ka abɛɛ fu‐kuli kamaa” mʋ, bɩla tɔwɛ yɛ, “Man mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa.” Nɩɩ fʋ kan fʋ man lɩɩ fʋ‐ka abɛɛ fu kuli kamaa, amaa nɩɩ fʋ mɔɔ nyɩmɩsa bɩrɛ, fu gyii kʋpwɛ nbara mʋ kpini‐kpini tɔ.

english_verse: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
translation_verse: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o mee wu asa kʋwɛɛ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa mɛɛ yɛ o wu mʋ kʋwɛɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ kʋwɛɛ kuwu kɩ nyɛ akyɔ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ katalɛ tɔ. Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii yɛ awaasa

english_verse: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana Krisito abuupu! Mɩnɛ kyaarɛ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ fɛɛ ɔkʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, ɔ sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, yɛgɛ ɔ man sʋ kɩmʋ awaasa mʋ, kanɩn kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii maŋa kɩɩ taalɛ mɔlɩgɛ mʋ?

english_verse: If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food,
translation_verse: Taa fɛɛ fu wu ɔpɩɩnyɩn abɛɛ ɔpɩɩkyɩɩ Krisito abuupu mʋ nɩɩ ba man sʋ atɔ bunsa yɛ agyitɔ gyisa.

english_verse: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
translation_verse: Kɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Natɛ kɔkɔlɔ yuuli sʋ kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ yɛgɛ fʋ ŋmɛ.” Amaa fʋ man sa mʋ atɔ bunsa yɛ agyitɔ kɛ o gyii. Man gyɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kɩyan nɩmʋ?

english_verse: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
translation_verse: Kanɩn nɩɩ i du sa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii. Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ awaasa bɩrɛ ki wu nɩmʋ.

english_verse: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋ ii biti ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fʋ sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii, nɩɩ mɛɛ mɛ n sʋ awaasa.” Amaa mɛɛ mɛ n tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kaapʋ mɛ fʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii yɛgɛ kɩ man sʋ awaasa.” Kɛ mɛɛ mɛ n taa mɛ awaasa lɛɛ mɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii kaapʋ fʋ.

english_verse: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
translation_verse: Fʋ kɔɔlɛ gyii fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kʋlʋn ɛɛ bʋ tɔ. Kesintin nɩn! Nbwɩɩ libi gbaa ba kɔɔlɛ gyii kanɩn, yɛgɛ ba sʋ kufuu bɛɛ kyɩkyaa.

english_verse: Does some stupid person want proof that faith without deeds is useless?
translation_verse: Fu, omelensipu nɩmʋ! Fii biti fɛɛ ba bɩla kaapʋ fʋ fɛɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ awaasa mʋ kɩ man sʋ kyaarɛ?

english_verse: Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did.
translation_verse: Fu wu fɛɛ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii yɛ mʋ awaasa kpini ɩ gyɛ kʋkʋlʋn nɩn. Mʋ awaasa mʋ ɛɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ kɩ naa fʋʋ ɔkaa.

english_verse: This is what the Scriptures mean by saying, “Abraham had faith in God, and God accepted him.” That's how Abraham became God's friend.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ ba tɔ kesintin yɛ, “Abraham kɔɔlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyii, kpalɩ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii maŋa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔɔlɛ mʋ fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi.” Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ Abraham yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ kyɛmɩnɛ.”

english_verse: You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ lɩɩ awaasa sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii wu fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa ɩkpa kpini i kyiigi, amaa man gyɛ mʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii nkʋn.

english_verse: For example, Rahab had been a prostitute. But she pleased God when she welcomed the spies and sent them home by another way.
translation_verse: Kanɩn nɩn nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ Rahabu awaasa ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu fɛɛ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ kɩtaa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi mʋ nɩɩ ba sun bamʋ mʋ kɩfɔ, nɩɩ ɔ kyɛ bamʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba nyɛ ɔkpa pʋpwɛ sʋ sɩlɛ lɩɩ Gyeriko kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: All of us do many wrong things. But if you can control your tongue, you are mature and able to control your whole body.
translation_verse: Anɛ kpini an taalɛ an fɔyɩ ɩkpa, nɩɩ ɔsa kʋ bʋ tɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ fɔyɩ ɔkpa lɩɩ mʋ atɔwɛsa tɔ mʋ, o du kyɛɛkyɛɛ nɩn, nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ kun mʋ n‐yɩɩ danbɩrasa.

english_verse: By putting a bit into the mouth of a horse, we can turn the horse in different directions.
translation_verse: An sʋ nbɩrɩga an waa ɩgbaŋɛ nnɔ tɔ nɩn, kanɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ waa kɛ ba nu anɛ asɛ kɛ a taalɛ biliŋaa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kpee katɩn mʋ nɩɩ an biti.

english_verse: It takes strong winds to move a large sailing ship, but the captain uses only a small rudder to make it go in any direction.
translation_verse: Fan yɛgɛ a bɩla taa ɩkʋlɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyɔ gaa, nɩɩ afuu bʋlʋnsa ii nin ɩmʋ waa sɩnkaala. Ɩmʋ abaŋɛ i du pii‐pii, nkɛɛ apalɩpʋ mʋ bɛɛ taalɛ biliŋaa ɩkʋlɩ mʋ kpee katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti.

english_verse: Our tongues are small too, and yet they brag about big things. It takes only a spark to start a forest fire!
translation_verse: Kanɩn nɩɩ kanandʋlʋ gbaa gyɛ kʋtɔ pii nyɩmɩsa n‐yɩɩ tɔ, amaa kɩ sʋ alala‐sɩn gaa. Bɩla kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩfʋlɔn‐gyi pii kɩɩ taalɛ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kɩpʋ lala kɩɩ dɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: The tongue is like a spark. It is an evil power that dirties the rest of the body and sets a person's entire life on fire with flames that come from hell itself.
translation_verse: Kanɩn nɩɩ kanandʋlʋ gbaa du fɛɛ fʋlɔn. I du fɛɛ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ alibi i gyii kɩmʋ kpini. Nɩɩ kɩ bʋ nyɩmɩsa n‐yɩɩ tɔ kɩɩ waa atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ man dagaa. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ alibi mʋ akyaŋaa nyɩmɩsa n‐yɩɩ kpini nɩɩ i nyita mʋ kakyɩna tɔ. Nɩɩ kɩɩ dɛɛla fʋlɔn lɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ sa kɩmʋ gbaa‐gbaa kɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ.

english_verse: All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles, and sea creatures can be tamed and have been tamed.
translation_verse: Nyɩmɩsa ɩɩ taalɛ pɔɔla atɔbwaaya libi kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ, nɩɩ bɛ pɔɔla atɔbwaaya fɛɛ nbwiibi yɛ atɔbwaaya kpɩɩsa yɛ atɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ nkyu tɔ kpini.

english_verse: But our tongues get out of control. They are restless and evil, and always spreading deadly poison.
translation_verse: Amaa nyɩmɩsa kʋ mɛɛ taalɛ pɔɔla kanandʋlʋ. Kɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ libi mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa mɛɛ taalɛ kun kɩmʋ, nɩɩ kɩ sʋ kʋbwɛ.

english_verse: My dear friends, with our tongues we speak both praises and curses. We praise our Lord and Father, and we curse people who were created to be like God, and this isn't right.
translation_verse: Kɔnɔ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ yɩn mʋ, kanɩn kɔnɔ kʋlʋn maŋa nɩɩ ba sʋ bɛɛ da nyɩmɩsa oyu. Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu anɛ, ɩ man dagaa ɩ maa ba kanɩn.

english_verse: Can clean water and dirty water both flow from the same spring?
translation_verse: Nkyu danbɩrasa yɛ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ i du mɛmɛɛ kpini ɩ taalɛ lɩɩ kitiribo kʋlʋn tɔ?

english_verse: Can a fig tree produce olives or a grapevine produce figs? Does fresh water come from a well full of salt water? Wisdom from Above
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, figi oyu ɩ taalɛ sɔyɩ olifu abi, abɛɛ kimango oyu kɩ taalɛ sɔyɩ akutu abɛɛ? Kanɩn ɔkpa kʋlʋn maŋa sʋ nɩɩ nkyu danbɩrasa yɛ nkyu mɛmɛɛ ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ lɩɩ kitiribo kʋlʋn tɔ. Kanyaasɩn yiri anyɔ

english_verse: Are any of you wise or sensible? Then show it by living right and by being humble and wise in everything you do.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ sʋ kanyaasɩn nɩɩ ee nu asɩn kasɛ mʋ, kanɩn ɔsa maŋa ɔ taa mʋ kakyɩna danbɩrasa yɛ mʋ awaasa danbɩrasa kɛ ɔ bɩya mʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ ɔ sʋ kanyaasɩn.

english_verse: But if your heart is full of bitter jealousy and selfishness, don't brag or lie to cover up the truth.
translation_verse: Amaa nɩɩ fan kan fan sʋ kayɩɩ basa yɛ kensi payɩ libi fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ, fan man kaapʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ ɩmʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ba kanɩn mʋ, fan tɩn antɩŋɛsa gyaga kesintin mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: This kind of wisdom doesn't come from above. It is earthly and selfish and comes from the devil himself.
translation_verse: Kanɩn kanyaasɩn maŋa kɩ man lɩɩ sʋsʋ nɩn, amaa kɩ lɩɩ nyɩmɩsa tɔ, nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ lɛɛ, nɩɩ kɩ man gyɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.

english_verse: Whenever people are jealous or selfish, they cause trouble and do all sorts of cruel things.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ kayɩɩ basa yɛ kensi payɩ libi bʋ mʋ, tʋtɔ nɩɩ sʋgʋrɛ‐sʋgʋrɛ yɛ alibi kɩwaa kpini bʋ.

english_verse: When peacemakers plant seeds of peace, they will harvest justice.
translation_verse: Kayɩɩ yuuli wuya ana mʋ nɩɩ bee duu kayɩɩ yuuli tɔ mʋ, bamʋ ɩɩ kpa adanbɩrasa bɛɛ lɩɩ ɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Why do you fight and argue with each other? Isn't it because you are full of selfish desires that fight to control your body?
translation_verse: Mɩnɛ ɩɩ bɩya fanɛ ɩkɔ yɛ nluu mʋ nɩɩ fɛn kɔɔ yɛ abaa falɛ? Man gyɛ fanɛ nkɔlɔ biti libi mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ tɔ ɩɩ kɔɔ yɛ fanɛ falɛ abɛɛ?

english_verse: You want something you don't have, and you will do anything to get it. You will even kill! But you still cannot get what you want, and you won't get it by fighting and arguing. You should pray for it.
translation_verse: Fen biti atɔ akʋ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ nyɛ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fɛn mɔɔ abaa. Fɛn taa ɔlʋn sʋ fen biti atɔ, amaa fɛn mɛɛ nyɛ ɩmʋ, ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ fɛn kɔɔ nluu. Fan maa mɛɛ nyɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn mɛɛ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Yet even when you do pray, your prayers are not answered, because you pray just for selfish reasons.
translation_verse: Fan maa taasɛ nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ nyɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fɛn taasɛ yɛ nfɛɛrɛ libi, kɛ fan nyɛ taa waa fanɛ nkɔlɔ biti.

english_verse: You people aren't faithful to God! Don't you know if you love the world, you are God's enemies? And if you decide to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God.
translation_verse: Fanɛ asa gyaga ba nɩmʋ, fan man nyi fɛɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ dulinyaa tɔ atɔ ii gyii mu kumu mʋ, ɔ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ okisipu nɩn. Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ dulinyaa tɔ atɔ ii gyii mu kumu mʋ, ee biliŋi Ɩbwaarɛ okisipu.

english_verse: Do you doubt the Scriptures that say, “God truly cares about the Spirit he has put in us”?
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa maa tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa anɛ tɔ mʋ, ɛɛ kɔɔ kɔkʋrʋkɔ” mʋ, fan nyi yɛ ɔ tɔwɛ ɩnɩmʋ kɩyan nɩn abɛɛ?

english_verse: In fact, God treats us with even greater kindness, just as the Scriptures say, “God opposes everyone who is proud, but he blesses all who are humble with undeserved grace.”
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sa anɛ mʋ bʋlʋn gaa. Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ ii tii bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kʋsaa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ ɔkpa, amaa nɩɩ ɛɛ taa kabwaala ɛɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ bɩya bamʋ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ.”

english_verse: Surrender to God! Resist the devil, and he will run from you.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan taa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ kɛ fan nu mʋ asɛ. Fan tii Sɩtaanɛ ɔkpa kɛ ɔ sɩlɛ lɩɩ fanɛ asɛ.

english_verse: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Clean up your lives, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you people who can't make up your mind.
translation_verse: Fan nin tiri Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ngbaa mʋ o nin tiri fanɛ. Fanɛ alibi awaapʋ, fan lɛɛ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ lɩɩ alibi tɔ. Nfɛɛrɛ nnyɔ‐nnyɔ wuya ana, fan lɛɛ nfɛɛrɛ libi lɩɩ fanɛ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

english_verse: Be sad and sorry and weep. Stop laughing and start crying. Be gloomy instead of glad.
translation_verse: Fan bɔta tɔ, kɛ fan su, kɛ fan saawʋ. Fan kyɛɛgɛ fanɛ ɩŋmasɛ biliŋi kusu, kɛ fan kyɛɛgɛ fanɛ kensi‐gyii biliŋi kʋwɛɛ.

english_verse: Be humble in the Lord's presence, and he will honor you. Saying Cruel Things about Others
translation_verse: Kɛ fan bɩya fanɛ n‐yɩɩ kasɛ sa Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ɔ kʋsaa fanɛ sʋsʋ.

english_verse: My friends, don't say cruel things about others! If you do, or if you condemn others, you are condemning God's Law. And if you condemn the Law, you put yourself above the Law and refuse to obey either it
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan man tɔwɛ alibi akʋ lɩɩ fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ, ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ alibi lɩɩ mʋ bɩrɩsa sʋ nɩɩ o bun mʋ kʋpwɛ mʋ, ɛɛ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbara mʋ ɩ man bʋrɔn, nɩɩ o bun nbara mʋ kʋpwɛ. Fɛɛ nɩɩ fʋ bun nbara mʋ kʋpwɛ mʋ, fʋ man bɩla fʋ gyɛ nbara mʋ obuupu. Fu biliŋi ɩmʋ kʋpwɛ obunpu nɩn.

english_verse: or God who gave it. God is our judge, and he can save or destroy us. What right do you have to condemn anyone? Warning against Bragging
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn ɩ gyɛ nbara mʋ ɔsapʋ, yɛ ɩmʋ ɔtalɛpʋ. Mʋ nkʋn ɛɛ taalɛ mɔlɩgɛ kɛ ɔ bɩla taalɛ nyita asa. Ɩmʋ sʋ fʋ ɩ gyɛ anɩmʋ nɩɩ fʋ talɛ fʋ bɩrɩsa ana? Man kaapʋ fʋ kayɩɩ lɩɩ ɔkɛ sʋ

english_verse: You should know better than to say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to the city. We will do business there for a year and make a lot of money!”
translation_verse: Nbɩɩnbɩɩ, fan nu mɛ asɛ, fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ndaga abɛɛ ɔkɛ an biti a kpee kadɛ lala kʋ tɔ naa kyɩna kesu kʋlʋn sun kusun kɛ a biliŋi afulee wuya ana.”

english_verse: What do you know about tomorrow? How can you be so sure about your life? It is nothing more than mist that appears for only a little while before it disappears.
translation_verse: Amaa fan man tɔwɛ kanɩn, yɛgɛ fan man nyi ɔkɛ asɩn yɛ fanɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fan du nɩn fɛɛ ɩfɛ‐kyu. Ɩɩ ba ɩ mɛɛ kyɛɛrɛɛ nɩɩ ɩɩ nyaa.

english_verse: You should say, “If the Lord lets us live, we will do these things.”
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fan tɔwɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, “Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kan kyula, nɩɩ an sʋ nkpa mʋ an biti n waa falɛ abɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ.”

english_verse: Yet you are stupid enough to brag, and it is wrong to be so proud.
translation_verse: Amaa fan dan fɛn kaapʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ, yɛgɛ fɛn gyɛɛrɛ. N‐yɩɩ kɩkaapʋ yɛ kɩkyɛɛrɛɛ maŋa kpini ɩ gyɛ alibi nɩn.

english_verse: You rich people should cry and weep! Terrible things are going to happen to you.
translation_verse: Fanɛ atɔ wuya ana, fan nu mɛ asɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ. Fan su kɛ fan saawʋ kenken lɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba fanɛ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Your treasures have already rotted, and moths have eaten your clothes.
translation_verse: Fanɛ atɔ mʋ ɩ gyɔɔ muuri, kɛ atɔ wɛsa ba wɛ fanɛ atɔ bunsa kpini.

english_verse: Your money has rusted, and the rust will be evidence against you, as it burns your body like fire. Yet you keep on storing up wealth in these last days.
translation_verse: Fanɛ isuwa yɛ igbiti kpini‐kpini ɩ waa nkandɩra, kɛ kanɩn nkandɩra maŋa ɩ lɛɛ fanɛ alibi kaapʋ, kɛ ɩ dɩyɛ fanɛ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ fɛɛ fʋlɔn mʋ. Fan kʋʋla atɔ gyaga abaa sʋ nkɛ lala lʋwɛ nɩmʋ.

english_verse: You refused to pay the people who worked in your fields, and now their unpaid wages are shouting out against you. The Lord All-Powerful has surely heard the cries of the workers who harvested your crops.
translation_verse: Fan kɩɩ, kusun awaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba sun fanɛ asun lɩɩ fanɛ ndɔɔ ana tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ fan kina bamʋ akɔ kɩka mʋ, bɛɛ fabɩlɛ bɛɛ sa fanɛ. Bamʋ nɩɩ ba kpa fanɛ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ sa fanɛ nɩɩ fan kina bamʋ akɔ kɩka mʋ, bamʋ kusu maŋa kpini i loo Ɩbwaarɛ Owura lala mʋ asʋ tɔ.

english_verse: While here on earth, you have thought only of filling your own stomachs and having a good time. But now you are like fat cattle on their way to be butchered.
translation_verse: Fan taa kɩnyɛ kyɩna danbɩrasa yɛ kensi‐gyii kyɩna dulinyaa tɔ. Nɩɩ fan kɩɩrɛ waa nfɔ gyoo fɛɛ kɩtɔbwaaya mʋ nɩɩ ɔ dan waa nfɔ o gyoo kakɛ mɔɔsa mʋ.

english_verse: You have condemned and murdered innocent people, who couldn't even fight back. Be Patient and Kind
translation_verse: Fan bun bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ ɩkpa ii kyiigi kʋpwɛ, mɔɔ bamʋ yɛgɛ ba man kʋsʋ yɩlɛ fanɛ sʋ. Fan nyɛ kenyiita yɩlɛ awʋrʋfɔ tɔ

english_verse: My friends, be patient until the Lord returns. Think of farmers who wait patiently for the autumn and spring rains to make their valuable crops grow.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan waa kenyiita yɩlɛ naa fʋʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩba saŋa mʋ. Fan kɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔdɔɔpʋ sʋ kenyiita o gyoo bʋlayɩ gyankpaasa yɛ kɩkyɛ bʋlayɩ ɔ ba da mʋ ndɔɔ tɔ. Yɛgɛ ɔ sʋ kenyiita o gyoo mʋ atɔ‐gyisa mʋ ɩɩ sɔyɩ danbɩrasa.

english_verse: Be patient like those farmers and don't give up. The Lord will soon be here!
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fanɛ gbaa fanɛ fan nyɛ kenyiita kɛ fan kɩtaa fanɛ nkɔlɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kibwii ba saŋa mʋ ki tee tiri tɔ.

english_verse: Don't grumble about each other or you will be judged, and the judge is right outside the door.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan man pwɩɩ abaa, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ man talɛ fanɛ. Ɔtalɛpʋ mʋ man bɩla ɔ bʋ kata, o tee tiri tɔ yɛ kɩba.

english_verse: My friends, follow the example of the prophets who spoke for the Lord. They were patient, even when they had to suffer.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fan nyiŋi Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ mʋ. Fan bɩɩla kanɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba wu awʋrʋfɔ nɩɩ ba nyɛ kenyiita.

english_verse: In fact, we praise the ones who endured the most. You remember how patient Job was and how the Lord finally helped him. The Lord did this because he is so merciful and kind.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba gyii awʋrʋfɔ nɩɩ ba nyɛ kɔkɔlɔ yɩlɛ tɔ mʋ, bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩɩ waa kusee. Nɩɩ fan nu ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyobu kenyiita mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ mʋ awʋrʋfɔ kigyii tɔ, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa adanbɩrasa sa mʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii su asa sʋ nɩɩ ee wu kʋwɛɛ.

english_verse: My friends, above all else, don't take an oath. You must not swear by heaven or by earth or by anything else. “Yes” or “No” is all you need to say. If you say anything more, you will be condemned.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, mɛ asɩn mʋ kpini ɩ gyɛ yɛ, fan man bɔ ntan tɩɩ sʋsʋ abɛɛ kasɛ abɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ. Fan yɛgɛ fanɛ kekyula kɩ baa kɩ gyɛ “Ɛɛhn,” kɛ fanɛ kekina kɩ waa “ayee.” Nɩɩ fan waa kanɩn mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ mɛɛ yɛ o bun fanɛ kʋpwɛ. Kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ

english_verse: If you are having trouble, you should pray. And if you are feeling good, you should sing praises.
translation_verse: Fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan ee wu awʋrʋfɔ mʋ, ɔ kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɛ ɔ kyɛ mʋ tɔ. Fanɛ ɔkʋ bʋ kensi‐gyii tɔ mʋ, ɔ bɩɩ ɩlʋn yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: If you are sick, ask the church leaders to come and pray for you. Ask them to put olive oil on you in the name of the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan ɛɛ lɔ mʋ, ɔ tɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ abɩlɩsa kɛ ba ba kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa mʋ, kɛ ba sʋswa mʋ nfɔ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ tɔ.

english_verse: If you have faith when you pray for sick people, they will get well. The Lord will heal them, and if they have sinned, he will forgive them.
translation_verse: Kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ falɛ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ ɔlɔpʋ mʋ ɔ nyɛ alanfɩya. Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ ii biti ɔ kyɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ kʋsaa mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan waa alibi akʋ gbaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa mu alibi kɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: If you have sinned, you should tell each other what you have done. Then you can pray for one another and be healed. The prayer of an innocent person is powerful, and it can help a lot.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan lɛɛ fanɛ alibi tɔwɛ abaa, kɛ fan kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sa abaa, kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ kyɛ fanɛ alɔ sa fanɛ. Ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi mʋ, mʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩ bʋgya nɩɩ i sun kusun.

english_verse: Elijah was just as human as we are, and for three and a half years his prayers kept the rain from falling.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Eligya gbaa gyɛ nyɩmɩsa nɩn fɛɛ anɛ falɛ. Ɔ kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ bʋlayɩ ɔ man da kasɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ bʋlayɩ yɩlɛ nsu ɩsa yɛ ɩbwaayɩ isiye.

english_verse: But when he did pray for rain, it fell from the skies and made the crops grow.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ bɩla bwii kʋlɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ yɛ bʋlayɩ ɔ da, nɩɩ bʋlayɩ tigi, nɩɩ atɔ duusa ɩ kɔyɩ, nɩɩ ɩ sɔyɩ abi kʋkyɔ.

english_verse: My friends, if any followers have wandered away from the truth, you should try to lead them back.
translation_verse: Mɛ‐daa ana yɛ mi‐supu ana, fɛɛ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ kan kina lɩɩ kesintin ɔkpa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ fanɛ tɔ ɔkʋ bwiiyaa mʋ ba mʋ,

english_verse: God the Father decided to choose you as his people, and his Spirit has made you holy. You have obeyed Jesus Christ and are sprinkled with his blood. I pray that God will be kind to you and will keep on giving you peace! A Real Reason for Hope
translation_verse: Fanɛ nɩɩ Ɔsɛ Ɩbwaarɛ wʋla o nyi kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ dɩdaa, nɩɩ ɔ bɔla mʋ Kayaayu sʋ lɛɛ fanɛ waa mʋ gbaa‐gbaa mʋ asa, kɛ fan ba buu Yesu kɛ ɔ taa mʋ nkalan kpayɩ fanɛ. N kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ ɔ bwaala fanɛ kʋkyɔ, kɛ ɔ sa fanɛ kayɩɩ‐yuuli. N yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ fɛɛ maa sa anɛ tamaa danbɩrasa

english_verse: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God is so good, and by raising Jesus from death, he has given us new life and a hope that lives on.
translation_verse: An yɩn anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito mʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ee su anɛ kʋwɛɛ gaa, ɩmʋ sʋ ɔ bɔla Yesu Krisito kelewu kyiŋi mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sa anɛ nkpa pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ tamaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa.

english_verse: God has something stored up for you in heaven, where it will never decay or be ruined or disappear.
translation_verse: Kanɩn sʋ an biti an nyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kapatɩyɛ mʋ nɩɩ ki mee nyita nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ gyɔɔ nɩɩ kɩ mɛɛ kpa sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɛ i gyoo fanɛ sʋsʋ mʋ.

english_verse: You have faith in God, whose power will protect you until the last day. Then he will save you, just as he has always planned to do.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ lɩɩ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔlʋn ii kun fanɛ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɛɛ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ kaapʋ kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Your faith will be like gold that has been tested in a fire. And these trials will prove that your faith is worth much more than gold that can be destroyed. They will show that you will be given praise and honor and glory when Jesus Christ returns.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fanɛ awʋrʋfɔ maŋa ɩ lɛɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mu kedubi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kyɔ suwa mʋ kaapʋ. Halɩɩ suwa maa gyɛ kʋtɔ nyitasa gbaa mʋ, ba sʋ fʋlɔn bɛɛ kaala kɩmʋ bɛɛ kɩɩ kɩmʋ kedubi. Ɩnɩmʋ sʋ mʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ba kaala fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kyɔ suwa mʋ kɩɩ. Nɩɩ fan kan taalɛ yɩlɛ tɔ bɩrɛ, fen biti fan nyɛ kɩyɩn yɛ bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Yesu Krisito ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: You have never seen Jesus, and you don't see him now. But still you love him and have faith in him, and no words can tell how glad and happy
translation_verse: Halɩɩ fan maa man wu Yesu gbaa mʋ, fen biti mʋ, yɛgɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ gbaa fen mee wu mʋ, amaa fɛn kɔɔlɛ mʋ fen gyii nɩɩ fan nyɛ kensi‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ kaala,

english_verse: you are to be saved. This is why you have faith.
translation_verse: lɩɩ fɛɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fɛn nyɛ tamaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kamɔlɩgɛ sa fanɛ kala ana.

english_verse: Some prophets told how God would treat you with undeserved grace, and they searched hard to find out more about the way you would be saved.
translation_verse: Falɛ kamɔlɩgɛ nɩmʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ ba kɩɩ tɔ taasɛ biti tɔ, nɩɩ ba wʋla tɔwɛ yɩla yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala mʋ gyɛ fanɛ lɛɛ.

english_verse: The Spirit of Christ was in them and was telling them how Christ would suffer and would then be given great honor. So they searched to find out exactly who Christ would be and when this would happen.
translation_verse: Krisito Kayaayu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, lɛɛ kaapʋ bamʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa Krisito awʋrʋfɔ kigyii yɛ bʋnyaa mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba kamaa tɔ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ba kɩɩ tɔ taasɛ biti tɔ saŋa yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba tɔ.
