translation_verse: N sʋ asɩn gaalaagaa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n tɔwɛ fanɛ, amaa n mɛɛ yɛ n kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ gyaga ɔwʋlɛ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ n sʋ tamaa fɛɛ n biti n ba fanɛ asɛ kɛ a tɔwɛ, kɛ maa fanɛ a kyɩna biri aligyima kɛ anɛ kpini ansi i gyii.

english_verse: Greetings from the children of your very special sister.
translation_verse: Fanɛ pɩɩkyɩɩ danbɩrasa mu‐bii ana bɛɛ fwaala fanɛ.

english_verse: From the church leader. To my dear friend Gaius. I love you because we follow the truth,
translation_verse: Mɛ ɔbɩlɩsa, n kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ n sa mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ danbɩrasa Gayusi mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ kebiti kesintin tɔ sa mʋ.

english_verse: dear friend, and I pray all goes well for you. I hope you are as strong in body, as I know you are in spirit.
translation_verse: Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ danbɩrasa, mɛ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ kinsigyi, yɛ alanfɩya, yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ i kpee sʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ sa fʋ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ kala sʋ alanfɩya mʋ.

english_verse: It makes me very happy when the Lord's followers come by and speak openly of how you obey the truth.
translation_verse: Fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anɛ‐nyi mu‐bii ana akʋ ba be gyii fu kesintin, tɔwɛ mɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fʋ taa kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii fʋ yɩlɛ kesintin mʋ tɔ mʋ, ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ mi ansi i gyii gaa.

english_verse: Nothing brings me greater happiness than to hear that my children are obeying the truth. Working Together
translation_verse: N man sʋ kinsigyi lala kʋ n kyɔ n maa nu yɛ mi‐nyi mu‐bii ana ba yɩlɛ kesintin mʋ tɔ. Gyɔn yɩn Gayusi

english_verse: Dear friend, you have always been faithful in helping other followers of the Lord, even the ones you didn't know before.
translation_verse: Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ danbɩrasa, kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fɩɩ waa fɩɩ sa fʋ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito abi afɔ mʋ, ɩɩ kaapʋ fɛɛ fʋ gyɛ kesintin wuya.

english_verse: They have told the church about your love. They say you were good enough to welcome them and to send them on their mission in a way God's servants deserve.
translation_verse: Ba tɔwɛ fu kebiti mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ sa asa, sa Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ nfɩɩ mʋ. Fʋ kɩɩ kɛ fʋ lɛɛ bamʋ ɔkpa kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa nɩɩ ii gyii Ɩbwaarɛ ansi.

english_verse: When they left to tell others about the Lord, they decided not to accept help from anyone who wasn't a follower.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kɩtɩɩ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba naa bee kpee yɛgɛ ba man kɔɔlɛ nkyɛtɔ kʋ lɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ Yesu Krisito gyii mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: We must support people like them, so we can take part in what they are doing to spread the truth.
translation_verse: Ɩ gyɛ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ sa anɛ yɛ a lɛɛ anɛ kesintin kaapʋ asa banɩmʋ, kɛ ana bamʋ a bʋga sun kesintin kusun mʋ. Diyotrefesi yɛ mʋ awaasa libi

english_verse: I wrote to the church. But Diotrephes likes to be the number-one leader, and he won't pay any attention to us.
translation_verse: N wʋla kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ naa sa Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ, amaa Diyotrefesi mʋ nɩɩ mu ansi ɩ payɩ kʋbʋ mʋ kɩbɩlɩsa kigyii sʋ mʋ, ɔ man taa anɛ waa sɛhn.

english_verse: So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ n kan ba fanɛ asɛ mʋ, n biti n tɩɩ fanɛ gyaŋɛ kɛ n tɔwɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ yɛ asɩn libi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩn ɛɛ gyaga anɛ sʋ. Ɛɛ bɩla ee kina mʋ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito abi kɩfɔ kɩkɔɔlɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ bamʋ kɩfɔ mʋ, ee tii bamʋ ɔkpa, nɩɩ ɛɛ gya bamʋ ɛɛ lɩɩ Krisito abi kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
translation_verse: Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ danbɩrasa, man bɩɩla alibi kɩwaa amɔɔ adanbɩrasa. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa mʋ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa alibi mʋ ɔ man pɩɩta bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true. Final Greetings
translation_verse: Demetirisu bɩrɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa lɩɩ mʋ kesintin kigyii sʋ. Anɛ gbaa anɛ a tɔwɛ mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa, nɩɩ fan nyi fɛɛ anɛ asɩn ɩ gyɛ kesintin. Laalaalʋwɛ asɩn

english_verse: I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink.
translation_verse: N sʋ asɩn kɩkyɔ n kyʋrɔɔ sa fʋ, amaa n mee biti n kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ waa ɔwʋlɛ tɔ.

english_verse: I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person.
translation_verse: N sʋ tamaa fɛɛ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ n biti n wu fʋ kɛ a tɔwɛ abaa asɛ.

english_verse: I pray that God will bless you with peace! Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ keyuula kɩ baa kɩ bʋ fʋ asɛ. Fʋ kyɛmɩnɛ ana mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ nfɩɩ kpini bɛɛ fwaala fʋ. Fwaala anɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana kpini kʋkʋlʋn kʋlʋn.

english_verse: From Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and the brother of James. To all who are chosen and loved by God the Father and are kept safe by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ Gyudi asɛ, n gyɛ Yesu Krisito kɩyaafɔlɛ, nɩɩ n bɩla n gyɛ Gyemisi mu‐supu. N kyʋrɔɔ n sa bamʋ nɩɩ Ɔsɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɩɩ bamʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kebiti sa bamʋ, nɩɩ Yesu Krisito ɩɩ kɩɩ bamʋ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people.
translation_verse: Mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana, naafɔɔ ɩ bʋ mɛ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ fɛɛ n kyʋrɔɔ fanɛ lɩɩ kamɔlɩgɛ mʋ nɩɩ anɛ kpini an nyɛ mʋ asɩn, amaa n bɩla wu fɛɛ ɩ dagaa n kyʋrɔɔ waa fanɛ ɔlʋn tɔ fɛɛ fan kɔ kun kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa waa mʋ asa abaa tɔ kɩlɩɩ kʋlʋn kakpaa mʋ keluu.

english_verse: Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ n tɔwɛ kanɩn mʋ ɩ gyɛ fɛɛ, asa kʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ ba baala loo fanɛ tɔ, bɛɛ kyɛɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabwaala asɩn mʋ kasɛ, kɛ ba nyɛ‐nyɛ ɔkpa waa kakyɩɩ yɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa. Kanɩn asa maŋa ba kina Yesu Krisito mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ anɛ Owura mʋ. Lɩɩ koo dɩdaa mʋ, nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ waa Asɩnkyan yɩlasa mʋ tɔ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba nyɛ.

english_verse: Don't forget what happened to those people the Lord rescued from Egypt. Some of them did not have faith, and he later destroyed them.
translation_verse: Fan tɛɛ wʋla fan nyi ɩnɩmʋ kpini, amaa n biti n nyiŋi fanɛ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ daa mɔlɩgɛ mʋ asa Ɩsɩrayɩ abi lɩɩ Egyipiti ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ. Amaa ɩmʋ kamaa tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ o muuri bamʋ tɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii mʋ.

english_verse: You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment.
translation_verse: Fan nyiŋi fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba man kɩtaa bamʋ kɩbɩlɩsa kigyii yɛ bamʋ ɩyɩlɛkpa, amaa ba kina bamʋ ɩkyɩnakpa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ŋmina bamʋ agbeeleebi kakpaa yɩla kitentenbiri naa fʋʋ katalɛ Kakɛ lala mʋ.

english_verse: We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
translation_verse: Kanɩn maŋa nɩɩ fan nyiŋi Sodom yɛ Gomora ndɛ, yɛ ndɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ kyaabɔɔ ɩmʋ mʋ. Ba daa kyɩna waa kakyɩɩ yɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa yɛ agyaga‐sɩn kɩbansa, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa fʋlɔn muuri bamʋ. Ndɛ maŋa tɔ asa gbaa be du nɩn fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ waa agyaga‐sɩn kayalaga. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ sɩnkaala sa alibi awaapʋ kpini fɛɛ bee biti ba loo awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ nɩɩ i mee dun kakpaa mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
translation_verse: Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ bɩlɩsa Maakɛlɩ gbaa man saalɛ Sɩtaanɛ. Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ maa mʋ bɛɛ bɩɩtɛ ɔdɩdaapʋ Mosesi kibuni sʋ mʋ, ɔ man tɔwɛ Sɩtaanɛ nnɔ, amaa ɔ tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ bɩɩtɛ fʋ kʋsʋ.”

english_verse: But these people insult powers they don't know anything about. They are like senseless animals that end up getting destroyed, because they live only by their feelings.
translation_verse: Amaa falɛ asa banɩmʋ bɩrɛ bɛɛ saalɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ be mee nu kɩmʋ kasɛ mʋ nɩn. Nɩɩ be du nɩn fɛɛ atɔbwaaya, lɩɩ fɛɛ ba man sʋ nfɛɛrɛ, kɛ ba fɛ lɩɩ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ kanɩn atɔ maŋa ii muuri bamʋ.

english_verse: Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
translation_verse: Kanɩn asa maŋa bamʋ laakʋ! Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkpa libi mʋ nɩɩ Ken kyɩna bɔla sʋ mʋ, nɩɩ ba buu falɛ. Be du nɩn fɛɛ Baalam mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kpalɩ afulee sʋ waa alibi mʋ. Nɩɩ ba kʋsʋ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ sʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Kora gbaa kʋsʋ yɩlɛ mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ bamʋ gbaa bamʋ bee biti ba muuri fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ Kora muuri mʋ.

english_verse: These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit.
translation_verse: Falɛ asa nɩmʋ ba kan naa loo fanɛ tɔ kɛ fan bʋga gyii mʋ, be du nɩn fɛɛ atɔ kpensa fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ba man sʋ ipeeli fɛɛ bamʋ nkʋn lɛɛ nɩɩ bee biti. Nɩɩ be du nɩn fɛɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ bɩya bʋlayɩ, nɩɩ afuu ɩ da ɩmʋ ɩ kyaa ɩ bɔɔ. Ba bɩla be du nɩn fɛɛ iyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sɔyɩ abi ɩmʋ kɩsɔyɩ saŋa kyɛɛkyɛɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba kyiigi ɩmaa ɩmʋ ilin nɩɩ i wu kpini‐kpini mʋ.

english_verse: Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said: Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels
translation_verse: Enoki mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyan Adam sʋ kakʋʋgɛ tɔ nkyaga sʋ sunɔsapʋ mʋ, tɔwɛ falɛ asa banɩmʋ asɩn koo dɩdaa yɩla yɛ, “Fan kɩɩ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɩɩ ba maa mʋ nbɔɔ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ ngbɩn‐ngbɩn mʋ,

english_verse: to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
translation_verse: kɛ o be gyii asa kpini asɩn. Ee biti o be bun bamʋ nɩɩ ba man sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ kʋpwɛ lɩɩ bamʋ alibi kɩwaa sʋ, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ asɩn libi yii Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ.

english_verse: These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want. More Warnings
translation_verse: Falɛ asa banɩmʋ ba tɛ nɩn bɛɛ kɔlɩtɛ, yɛgɛ bɛɛ pwɩɩ bamʋ asa. Ba buu bamʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kayalaga sʋ, nɩɩ bɛɛ kʋsaa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛgɛ ba sʋ kɔnɔ tɔ kɔn bɛɛ pɩna asa kɛ ɩ waa ɔdan sa bamʋ. Fan kɩtaa Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ

english_verse: My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: “Amaa mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana danbɩrasa, fan nyi atɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito asunpu ba daa tɔwɛ fanɛ.

english_verse: They told you that near the end of time, selfish and godless people would start making fun of God.
translation_verse: Ba daa tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, kakɛ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, asa bee biti ba taa Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn bɛɛ waa ahin‐sɩn, kɛ ba buu bamʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ alibi kayalaga.”

english_verse: And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
translation_verse: Falɛ asa banɩmʋ ɩɩ bɩya nbarɩgɛ tɔ fanɛ tɔ, bamʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ kayalaga i gyii bamʋ amu, nɩɩ ba man sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ.

english_verse: Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray.
translation_verse: Amaa mɛ kyɛmɩnɛ ana danbɩrasa, fanɛɛ fanɛ bɩrɛ, fan waa fanɛ n‐yɩɩ ɔlʋn tɔ lɩɩ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii danbɩrasa mʋ tɔ, yɛgɛ fɛn kʋlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, Kayaayu Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ ɔlʋn mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life.
translation_verse: Fan kyɩna Ɩbwaarɛ kebiti mʋ tɔ, yɛgɛ fan gyoo anɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito o be wu fanɛ kʋwɛɛ kɛ ɔ sa fanɛ nkpa kakpaa mʋ.

english_verse: Be helpful to all who may have doubts.
translation_verse: Fan wu bamʋ nɩɩ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii du sika‐sika mʋ kʋwɛɛ.

english_verse: Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds. Final Prayer
translation_verse: Fan mɔlɩgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ sʋ bamʋ ii kpee awʋrʋfɔ fʋlɔn yen‐yen tɔ mʋ, kɛ fan wu akʋ mɔɔ kʋwɛɛ yɛgɛ fan sʋ kufuu, kɛ fan kisi bamʋ alibi awaasa mʋ nɩɩ i du fɛɛ ataa kpensa mʋ. Kɩyɩn kabwaarɛ kʋlɛ

english_verse: Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
translation_verse: Mʋ nkʋn ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Ɔmɔlɩgɛpʋ. Nɩɩ mʋ ɩ dagaa bʋnyaa, yɛ kɩkparɛ, yɛ ɔlʋn, yɛ kɩbɩlɩsa bɔla anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu Krisito lɩɩ koo dɩdaa yɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ man sʋ ɔkaa. Amen.

english_verse: This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John.
translation_verse: Ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ asɩn ɩ gyɛ asɩnlɛɛ kaapʋsa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa sa Yesu Krisito fɛɛ, ɔ lɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba waa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ kyɛɛrɛɛ. Nɩɩ Yesu mɔɔ sun mʋ kabɔɔ kʋ nɩɩ ɔ ba lɛɛ asɩn mʋ kaapʋ mɛ Gyɔn, Krisito kɩyaafɔlɛ.

english_verse: And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mɛ Gyɔn, ɩ tɔwɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ maa Yesu Krisito tɔwɛ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ kaapʋ mɛ mʋ.

english_verse: From John to the seven churches in Asia. I pray that you will be blessed with kindness and peace from God, who is and was and is coming. May you receive kindness and peace from the seven spirits before the throne of God.
translation_verse: Mɛ Gyɔn ɩ kyʋrɔɔ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ n sʋ n sa Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ asunɔ, mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ Asiya ɔsʋwʋlɛ kɩbaafʋn kʋ mʋ sʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɔ bʋ tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ atɔ i piili, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ, yɛ mʋ n‐yaayu nsunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mu kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ mʋ, bamʋ kabwaala yɛ kayɩɩ yuuli kɩ gyɛ fanɛ lɛɛ.

english_verse: May kindness and peace be yours from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness. Jesus was the first to conquer death, and he is the ruler of all earthly kings. Christ loves us, and by his blood he set us free from our sins.
translation_verse: Yesu Krisito gbaa kabwaala yɛ kayɩɩ yuuli kɩ ba fanɛ asɛ. Mʋ ii gyii kesintin nɩɩ ɛɛ lɛɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ tɔ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyankpaa kyiŋi lɩɩ lewu tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kasɛ sʋ awura tɔ Owura. Nɩɩ mʋ ii biti anɛ asɩn gaa, nɩɩ o wu taa mʋ nkalan lɛɛ anɛ lɩɩ alibi ɔlʋn tɔ,

english_verse: He lets us rule as kings and serve God his Father as priests. To him be glory and power forever and ever! Amen.
translation_verse: nɩɩ ɔ waa anɛ mu kuwura‐gyii tɔ asa yɛ mu aseepu, kɛ a sun Mʋ‐Sɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ. Yesu Krisito nɩɩ bʋnyaa yɛ ɔlʋn gyɛ mʋ lɛɛ kakpaa. Amen!

english_verse: Look! He is coming with the clouds. Everyone will see him, even the ones who stuck a sword through him. All people on earth will weep because of him. Yes, it will happen! Amen.
translation_verse: Fan kɩɩ! Yesu bʋ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ ɛɛ ba. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ii biti o wu mʋ, halɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba yii mʋ kɩkpa mʋ gbaa, lɩɩ mʋ sʋ kasɛ sʋ asa kpini bee biti ba su. Kanɩn nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba. Amen!

english_verse: The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!” A Vision of the Risen Lord
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Alɩfa yɛ Omega. Mɛ ɩ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ n wʋla n bʋ tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ atɔ i piili, nɩɩ n biti n baa n bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ. Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔlʋlʋnpʋ.” Gyɔn Kɩlɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ o wu lɩɩ Yesu sʋ

english_verse: I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus.
translation_verse: Mɛ, Gyɔn mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ fanɛ bɩrɩsa, lɩɩ Yesu sʋ mʋ n gyii awʋrʋfɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fen gyii mʋ, lɩɩ an maa gyɛ mu kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ asa sʋ mʋ. Amaa an nyɛ kenyiita yɩlɛ tɔ. Lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn kɩtɔwɛ yɛ Yesu asɩn kɩlɛɛ tɔwɛ sʋ mʋ, ba kɩtaa mɛ naa tʋʋ Patimose kifintin mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kakɛ sunsa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ kɩ ba mɛ sʋ, nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ lala kʋ mɛ kamaa, kɩ maa su fɛɛ kabɩlɛ.

english_verse: The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fii wu falɛ waa ɔwʋlɛ tɔ, kɛ fʋ taa kyʋŋɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ asunɔ mʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Efeso yɛ Simirina yɛ Pegamum yɛ Tatira yɛ Sadisi yɛ Filadefiya maa Lawodisiya.”

english_verse: When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands.
translation_verse: N maa biliŋi n kɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ tɔwɛ mɛ asɛ mʋ, n wu ɩfɩtɩla ɩgyagakpa isunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa isuwa waa ɩmʋ.

english_verse: There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ nsana tɔ mʋ, n wu ɔkʋ fɛɛ Daadimaadi Obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ. O bun kaalɛ bʋnswɩɩsa ba yii kasɛ, nɩɩ ɔ taa suwa kɩrɩsʋ ŋmina mʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ.

english_verse: His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mu kumu inyini i fuuli parɛparɛ fɛɛ ateebi abɛɛ bwanbwan, nɩɩ mu ansi abi i du fɛɛ fʋlɔn anobi.

english_verse: His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ ayaa i du fɛɛ kɩbɩlɩtɔ pɩpɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa tʋʋ fʋlɔn tɔ, nɩɩ kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ, nɩɩ mʋ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ i du fɛɛ nkyu ɩ maa bɔla ɩ kyʋn akʋlɩ tɔ.

english_verse: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ sʋ akyikpebi asunɔ mʋ gyisa kɩbaa tɔ, nɩɩ sakunde gyimba anyɔ ɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ. Nɩɩ mu ansi tɔ mɔɔ ɩ ŋalɩgɛ fɛɛ kakyʋwapɛ kyɔwɛ.

english_verse: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said: Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n maa wu mʋ mʋ, n tɩyɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa wusa, nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ gyisa kɩbaa gyaga mɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Man waa kufuu. Mɛ ɩ gyɛ kagyankpaa yɛ laalaalʋwɛ.

english_verse: and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead.
translation_verse: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ n bʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ n kyɩna wu, amaa kɩɩ, n tɛ nkpa tɔ kakpaa. Nɩɩ lewu yɛ Kamandɛ ɩsafwɩɩ ɩ bʋ mɛ kɩbaa tɔ.

english_verse: Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things.
translation_verse: “Ɩmʋ sʋ, kyʋrɔɔ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fu wu ɩ maa waa nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba kamaa tɔ mʋ kpini yɩla.

english_verse: I will explain the mystery of the seven stars you saw at my right side and the seven gold lampstands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the lampstands are the seven churches.
translation_verse: Akyikpebi asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ fu wu mɛ gyisa kɩbaa tɔ mʋ, yɛ ɩfɩtɩla ɩgyagakpa asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa suwa waa ɩmʋ mʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Akyikpebi asunɔ mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ asunɔ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩfɩtɩla ɩgyagakpa asunɔ mʋ ɩ yɩlɛ sa Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ asunɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus: I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ asɩn nɩmʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Efeso kadɛ tɔ mʋ sʋ. Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ lɩɩ mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ akyikpebi asunɔ mɛ gyisa kɩbaa tɔ, nɩɩ n naa ɩfɩtɩla ɩgyagakpa asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa suwa waa mʋ nsana.

english_verse: I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars.
translation_verse: N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, N nyi fanɛ asɩn waasa yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan kpan fanɛ n‐yɩɩ waa kusun yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan nyɛ kenyiita yɩlɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ n nyi fan maa mee biti alibi awaapʋ fanɛ tɔ, nɩɩ fan kaala bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɛ ba gyɛ Krisito asunpu yɛgɛ ba man gyɛ mʋ kɩɩ, nɩɩ fan wu fɛɛ ba gyɛ antɩŋɛsa wuya ana.

english_verse: You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nyi fan maa nyɛ kenyiita yɩlɛ tɔ, nɩɩ fen gyii awʋrʋfɔ lɩɩ mɛ kɩtɩɩ sʋ, nɩɩ fanɛ abaa a man yɔɔ.

english_verse: But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to.
translation_verse: Amaa n sʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn akʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ nbɩɩnbɩɩ fan man bɩla fɛn sʋ kebiti fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan gyankpaa fɛn sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand.
translation_verse: Fan nyiŋi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ fan fʋʋ, nɩɩ fan bwii lɩɩ tɩyɛ mʋ. Fan kyɛɛgɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ, kɛ fan waa adanbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ fan kyɩna fɛn waa mʋ. Nɩɩ fan man kan kyɛɛgɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ mʋ, n biti n ba fanɛ asɛ ba lɛɛ fanɛ fɩtɩla ɔgyagakpa mʋ lɩɩ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ mʋ.

english_verse: But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
translation_verse: Amaa fanɛ kʋtɔ kʋlʋn mʋ nɩɩ fɛn waa nɩɩ ɩ bʋrɔn mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, fen kisi Nikolatan abi awaasa mʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n kisi mʋ.

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ asɩn nɩmʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Simirina mʋ sʋ. Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ lɩɩ mɛ mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Kagyankpaa yɛ Laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ n da wu nɩɩ n kyiŋi bɩla n tɛ nkpa tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
translation_verse: N nyi awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen gyii yɛ fanɛ kitiri, amaa kesintin tɔ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii sʋ mʋ fen gyɛ atɔ wuya ana. N nyi bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɛ ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi yɛgɛ ba man gyɛ, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩn antɩŋɛsa bɛɛ gyaga fanɛ sʋ mʋ. Ba gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ asa.

english_verse: Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
translation_verse: Fan man waa kufuu lɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ nɩɩ fen biti fan gyii mʋ sʋ. N tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ, fen biti fɛn nyɛ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ, mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ ii biti ɔ taa fanɛ asa mʋ tɔ akʋ naa tii obu. Fen biti fɛn nyɛ kanyanla nkɛ kudu. Halɩɩ lewu tɔ gbaa, fan yɩlɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ kenken, kɛ n sa fanɛ kʋkɔka mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ nkpa kakpaa.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. Whoever wins the victory will not be hurt by the second death. The Letter to Pergamum
translation_verse: “Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ, o nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ. Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔ lɩɩ mʋ, lewu nyɔsapʋ mʋ man bɩla yɛ o gyii mʋ.” Ɔkala mʋ nɩɩ ii kpee ɩɩ sa Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Pegamum kadɛ tɔ mʋ

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ asɩn nɩmʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Pegamum kadɛ tɔ mʋ sʋ. Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩɩ lɩɩ mɛ mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ sakunde gyimba anyɔ mɛ asɛ.

english_verse: I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
translation_verse: N nyi katɩn mʋ nɩɩ fan tɛ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ kɩ bʋ. Ɩmaa ɩmʋ kpini gbaa mʋ fan san kɔɔlɛ mɛ gyii. Halɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mɛ asɩn mʋ kesintin ogyipu Antipasi fanɛ kadɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ tɛ mʋ tɔ gbaa mʋ, fan man kina mɛ lɩɩ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ.

english_verse: I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral.
translation_verse: “Amaa n sʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn pwɩɩsa kʋ. Ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, asa mʋ nɩɩ bee buu Balaam asɩn kaapʋsa mʋ akʋ ba bʋ fanɛ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ. Balaam nɩmʋ ɩ kyɩna kaapʋ Balaki yɛ, kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa kɛ Ɩsɩrayɩ abi ba waa alibi mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, ɔ pɩna bamʋ kɛ ba gyii agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ sa ɩdakpa, kɛ ba waa kakyɩɩ yɛ kanyɩn biti kɩgyagaa.

english_verse: Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
translation_verse: Kanɩn ɔkpa maŋa sʋ, nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ bee buu Nikolatan abi asɩn kaapʋsa mʋ akʋ ba bʋ fanɛ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kyɛɛgɛ fanɛ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi tɔ ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ, nɩɩ ɩ man gyɛ kanɩn bɩrɛ, n biti n ba fanɛ asɛ bilen kɛ n ba taa mɛ kɔnɔ tɔ sakunde ba kɔ yɛ kanɩn asa maŋa.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone. The Letter to Thyatira
translation_verse: “Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ, o nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ. Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔ lɩɩ mʋ, n biti n sa mʋ agyitɔ baalasa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ mana, nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ mʋ. N biti n bɩla sa ɔsa maŋa kubuu fufuli mʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ kɩtɩɩ pʋpwɛ gyaga kɩmʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man nyi kanɩn kɩtɩɩ maŋa, amɔɔ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ kɩmʋ mʋ nkʋn.” Ɔkala mʋ nɩɩ ii kpee ɩɩ sa Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Tiyatira kadɛ tɔ mʋ

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ asɩn nɩmʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Tiyatira kadɛ tɔ mʋ sʋ. Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩɩ lɩɩ mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mu‐Bii mʋ nɩɩ mi ansi i du fɛɛ fʋlɔn anobi, nɩɩ mɛ ayaa sʋ ɩ du fɛɛ kɩbɩlɩtɔ pɩpɛɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ mʋ.

english_verse: I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before.
translation_verse: N nyi fanɛ asɩn waasa. N nyi fanɛ kebiti yɛ fanɛ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii yɛ fanɛ kusun, yɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fɛn nyɛ kenyiita yɩlɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ n nyi fan maa waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini fan kyɔ kanan mʋ nɩɩ fan daa kyɩna waa mʋ.

english_verse: But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols.
translation_verse: “Amaa n sʋ fanɛ n‐yɩɩ sʋ asɩn pwɩɩsa kʋ, fɛɛ fan maa sa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Gyesebeli ɔkpa, nɩɩ ɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ n‐yɩɩ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔtɔwɛpʋ. Nɩɩ ɛɛ taa mʋ kɩkaapʋ mʋ pɩna mɛ abuupu, nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanyɩn abɛɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa, nɩɩ ba bɩla bee gyii agyitɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa lɔŋɔ sa ɩdakpa mʋ.

english_verse: I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
translation_verse: N nyɛ saŋa sa mʋ fɛɛ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ mʋ kɩgyagaa mʋ tɔ, amaa o kina ɔ mɛɛ kyɛɛgɛ.

english_verse: I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n biti n yɛgɛ ɔ lɔɔ, kɛ n yɛgɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ bamʋ‐ka ana kamaa nyita kekili yɛ mʋ mʋ, ba wu awʋrʋfɔ lala, amɔɔ ba kyɛɛgɛ bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ lɩɩ alibi ɩkpa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
translation_verse: N biti n mɔɔ mu abuupu mʋ kpini, kɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ kpini ba bɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o nyi nyɩmɩsa kɔkɔlɔ tɔ yɛ mʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ asɩn. N biti n ka fanɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kʋkɔ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa sʋ.

english_verse: Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands.
translation_verse: Amaa n tɔwɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan san Tiyatira, nɩɩ fan man buu ɔkyɩɩ mʋ asɩn kaapʋsa libi, nɩɩ fan man bɩɩla mʋ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ mu abuupu bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Sɩtaanɛ asɩn budusa baalasa mʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ fanɛ yɛ n man bɩla yɛ n taa kɩkpɩnɛ kʋ tii fanɛ sʋ.

english_verse: But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
translation_verse: Amaa fan dan kɩtaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan wʋla fan sʋ tɔ mʋ kenken, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n ba.

english_verse: I will give power over the nations to everyone who wins the victory and keeps on obeying me until the end.
translation_verse: Ɔsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɔ ɛɛ lɩɩ, nɩɩ ɛɛ waa mi kebiti naa fʋʋ laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, n biti n sa mʋ ɔlʋn ndɛndɛmandɛ sʋ.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_verse: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ o nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu ɩɩ tɔwɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ.”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis: I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say. I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ asɩn nɩmʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Sadisi kadɛ tɔ mʋ sʋ. Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ lɩɩ mɛ, mʋ nɩɩ n sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ N‐yaayu nsunɔ yɛ akyikpebi asunɔ mʋ. N nyi fʋ asɩn waasa, fʋ nyɛ kɩtɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ maa tɛ nkpa tɔ, amaa fu wu.

english_verse: Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan kyiŋi, kɛ fan sa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ san nɩɩ ii biti i wu mʋ tɔ ɔlʋn. Lɩɩ fɛɛ n wu fanɛ asɩn waasa ɩ maa man du kyɛɛkyɛɛ mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ.

english_verse: Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ fan nyiŋi atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba kaapʋ fanɛ nɩɩ fan nu mʋ. Fan buu ɩmʋ, kɛ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Amaa nɩɩ fan man kan kyiŋi bɩrɛ, n biti n ba purigi fanɛ sʋ fɛɛ oyu, nɩɩ fɛn mɛɛ yɛ fan bɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n biti n ba fanɛ asɛ.

english_verse: A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy.
translation_verse: “Amaa ɩ san asa kalɩsa akʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Sadisi, nɩɩ ba man waa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ineesi. Bamʋ ii biti ba bun atɔ fufuli, kɛ n maa bamʋ a natɛ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩ dagaa kanɩn.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kan kɔ lɩɩ mʋ, mʋ ii biti o bun atɔ fufuli fɛɛ bamʋ. Nɩɩ n mɛɛ yɛ n kpayɩ mʋ kɩtɩɩ lɩɩ nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ kakaakʋ. Amaa n biti n lɛɛ tɔwɛ Nsɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa mʋ nbɔɔ mu ansi tɔ fɛɛ ba gyɛ mɛ asa.

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ asɩn nɩmʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Filadefiya kadɛ tɔ mʋ sʋ. Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ lɩɩ mɛ mʋ nɩɩ n gyɛ Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ yɛ kesintin wuya, nɩɩ mɛ kɩbaa tɔ nɩɩ Dawidi safɩɩ bʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n tigi mʋ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ tii, nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ n tii mɔɔ mʋ, ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ tigi.

english_verse: I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers.
translation_verse: N nyi fanɛ asɩn waasa. Fan nu, n tigi kebunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ tii mʋ yɩla sa fanɛ. N nyi fɛɛ fanɛ ɔlʋn man kyɔ, amaa fan buu mɛ asɩn mʋ, nɩɩ fan man kina mɛ.

english_verse: Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa waa bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ Sɩtaanɛ kʋbʋ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba gyɛ antɩŋɛsa wuya ana, nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ ba gyɛ Gyiwu abi yɛgɛ ba man gyɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, n biti n nyan kanɩn asa maŋa kɛ ba be muŋa fanɛ ayaa kasɛ, kɛ ba bɩɩ fɛɛ n biti fanɛ asɩn.

english_verse: You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through.
translation_verse: Kpalɩ fɛɛ fan maa man nu mɛ kɔnɔsa, nɩɩ fan nyɛ kenyiita yɩlɛ tɔ mʋ, ngbaa mɛ n biti n lɛɛ fanɛ lɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ kʋtʋʋ tɔ kɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ ba dulinyaa tɔ, kɛ ɩ ba tʋʋ dulinyaa asa kpini tɔ kɩɩ mʋ.

english_verse: I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
translation_verse: “N biti n ba bilen. Fan kɩtaa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ fan sʋ tɔ mʋ kenken, kɛ ɔkʋ ɔ man kɔɔlɛ kʋkɔka mʋ nɩɩ n biti n taa sa fanɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔ lɩɩ mʋ, ee biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ fɛɛ lenbu tɔ sabarɛ, Mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ osunkpa obu mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ lɩɩ tʋtɔ kakaakʋ kpaa. N biti n kyʋrɔɔ Mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩtɩɩ yɛ mʋ kadɛ lala mʋ kɩtɩɩ gyaga kanɩn ɔsa maŋa sʋ. Kanɩn kadɛ lala maŋa ɩ gyɛ Gyerusalem pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii biti kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ Mɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ ba kasɛ sʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ n biti n kyʋrɔɔ mɛ gbaa‐gbaa mɛ kɩtɩɩ pʋpwɛ gyaga kanɩn ɔsa maŋa sʋ.

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ asɩn nɩmʋ naa sa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ Krisito kʋbʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ Lawodisiya kadɛ tɔ mʋ sʋ. Asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ lɩɩ mɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Amen mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ n gyɛ kesintin wuya, nɩɩ n ba lɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ ifuli kaapʋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Nɩɩ mɛ ɩ gyɛ kumu sa kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tʋʋ mʋ.

english_verse: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
translation_verse: N nyi fanɛ asɩn waasa, fɛɛ fan man yuuli nɩɩ fan man bʋgya. Nɩɩ fan daa fan yuuli abɛɛ fan bʋgya mʋ, naafɔɔ ɩ bʋrɔn!

english_verse: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
translation_verse: Amaa lɩɩ fɛɛ fan maa du mɔlɔmɔlɔ, nɩɩ fan man yuuli abɛɛ fan man bʋgya sʋ mʋ, n biti n fura fanɛ lɩɩ mɛ kɔnɔ tɔ fɔyɩ.

english_verse: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
translation_verse: Fɛn tɔwɛ yɛ, an gyɛ atɔ wuya ana, nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa kɩ waa ɔdan ɩ sa anɛ, nɩɩ sɛhn man tiri anɛ. Amaa fan man nyi fɛɛ fan gyɛ awʋrʋfɔ agyipu, nɩɩ fanɛ asɩn a sʋ kʋwɛɛ, nɩɩ fan gyɛ atiripu, yɛ agyaatanapʋ nɩɩ fan naa kubolonkpan.

english_verse: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n tɔwɛ n waa fanɛ fɛɛ fan ba mɛ asɛ ba sɔɔ suwa kparɛ, kɛ fan ba waa atɔ wuya ana. Kɛ fan ba sɔɔ atɔ fufuli mɛ asɛ naa bun fanɛ ipeeli kubolonkpan mʋ sʋ. Kɛ fan bɩla sɔɔ kɩfɛ waa fanɛ ansi tɔ kɛ fan taalɛ nyɛ wu.

english_verse: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: “Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ n biti bamʋ asɩn mʋ, n gyii kadɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ n bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ fan payɩ fanɛ ansi kɛ fan yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa.

english_verse: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
translation_verse: Fan nu mɛ asɛ! N yɩlɛ kebunɔ n ŋɛ ɔpʋnʋ mʋ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o nu mɛ ɔbʋlɛ, nɩɩ o tigi ɔpʋnʋ mʋ, n biti n loo to ba, kɛ maa mʋ a bʋga gyii.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
translation_verse: “Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔ lɩɩ mʋ, n biti n sa mʋ ɔkpa ɔ ba kyɩna kuwura‐gya sʋ mata mɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ngbaa mɛ n kɔ lɩɩ nɩɩ n kyɩna mata Mɛ‐Sɛ mʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_verse: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ, o nu kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ Krisito kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ.”

english_verse: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.”
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n kɩɩ nɩɩ n wu sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ ɔpʋnʋ maa tigi kɩ dɛ. Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ daa tɔwɛ mɛ asɛ fɛɛ kabɩlɛ mʋ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Ba sʋsʋ nfɩɩ, kɛ n kaapʋ fʋ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɩ maa waa ɩnɩmʋ kamaa.”

english_verse: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
translation_verse: Opula tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ taa mɛ. Nɩɩ n ba kɩɩ mʋ, n wu kuwura‐gya mʋ kɩ maa bʋ sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ayɛ yɛgɛ ɔkʋ tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
translation_verse: Nwuragya adʋnyɔ yɛ ana ɩkʋ ɩ yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛgɛ abɩlɩsa adʋnyɔ yɛ ana ba tɛ ɩmʋ sʋ. Ba bun atɔ fufuli nɩɩ ba bun suwa awura‐wʋrʋ.

english_verse: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ bʋlayɩ ɩɩ wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ yɛgɛ ɛɛ pʋntɛ lɩɩ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ. Ɩfɩtɩla isunɔ ɩ bʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ ɩ dɩyɛ. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ N‐yaayu nsunɔ mʋ.

english_verse: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
translation_verse: Kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ mʋ, n bɩla wu kʋtɔ kʋ fɛɛ ɔpʋ, nɩɩ ki du fɛɛ kɩlasɛ, nɩɩ kɩ bɩla ki du kɩlɩn‐kɩlɩn. Nsana tɔ mʋ atɔ kɩsa ana ba kyaabɔɔ kuwura‐gya mʋ, yɛgɛ ba sʋ ansi bamʋ ansi tɔ yɛ bamʋ kamaa yɛ katɩn kʋmaa.

english_verse: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ kɩɩsa gyankpaasa mʋ ki du nɩn fɛɛ barɩnɛ, nɩɩ ɔnyɔsapʋ du fɛɛ naatɛ gbelu, nɩɩ ɔsasapʋ mu ansi tɔ i du fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa, nɩɩ ɔnasapʋ du fɛɛ kpaakpaatɛ mʋ nɩɩ ee fugi.

english_verse: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!”
translation_verse: Atɔbwaaya kɩɩsa ana maŋa ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ abanbantɛ asiye, nɩɩ ba sʋ ansi bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ katɩn kʋmaa nɩmʋ kyaabɔɔ halɩɩ bamʋ abanbantɛ tɔ. Ba mɛɛ yɛgɛ kɩtɔwɛ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ yɛ, “Kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ, ɩ gyɛ anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ Owura lala, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ wʋla ɔ bʋ tɔ pɔyɩ nɩɩ atɔ i piili, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ.”

english_verse: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ atɔbwaaya kɩɩsa mʋ bɛɛ bɩɩ ɩlʋn bɛɛ taa bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ afwaala bɛɛ sa ɔmʋnɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ kakpaa mʋ.

english_verse: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
translation_verse: “Anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ yɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, fʋ dagaa fɛɛ ba sa fʋ bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ, nɩɩ fʋ ɔlʋn dagaa fʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ ɩ tʋʋ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ, nɩɩ fʋ kebiti tɔ nɩɩ fʋ tʋʋ ɩnɩmʋ kpini, nɩɩ bamʋ kpini ba bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba sʋ nkpa falɛ.”

english_verse: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n wu ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa kʋ maa bʋ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ kɩbaa gyisa tɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ kɩmʋ ansi tɔ yɛ kamaa kpini, nɩɩ ba taa atɔ tarɩsa asunɔ tarɩ ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ kɔnɔ sʋ.

english_verse: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ɔlʋnpʋ maa taa ɔbʋlɛ lala ee kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ ɔ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa mʋ, kɛ o bugi ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ tɔ?”

english_verse: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔkʋ man bʋ sʋsʋ abɛɛ kasɛ sʋ abɛɛ kasɛ tɔ, nɩɩ ɛɛ taalɛ bugi ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ tɔ abɛɛ ɔ taalɛ kɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n su gaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ man bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ɔ dagaa fɛɛ o bugi ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ tɔ abɛɛ ɔ kɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Man bɩla su! Kɩɩ, Gyuda kebunɔ nyɩmɩsa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Bɩranɛ, nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ Dawidi kanaana kparɛ tɔ mʋ, ɔ kɔ gyii sʋ. Ɩmʋ sʋ ɛɛ taalɛ kyatɛ atɔ tarɩsa asunɔ mʋ, kɛ o bugi ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ.”

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n maa kɩɩ mʋ, n wu Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ɔbʋlɛ tɔ sʋ kɩlɔpanɛ fɛɛ ba maa taa mʋ waa saraga, maa yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ nsana. Ɔ sʋ agyakpaa asunɔ yɛ ansi asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ N‐yaayu nsunɔ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sun bamʋ kpee dulinyaa tɔ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ.

english_verse: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ba kɔɔlɛ ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ lɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ mʋ gyisa kɩbaa tɔ.

english_verse: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba bɩɩ ɔlʋn pʋpwɛ sa Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ yɛ, “Fʋ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ fʋ taa ɔwʋlɛ bɔɔsa mʋ, kɛ fʋ kyatɛ ɩmʋ ɩtarɛsa mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ba mɔɔ fʋ, nɩɩ fʋ taa fʋ nkalan mʋ sɔɔ asa sa Ɩbwaarɛ. Fʋ sɔɔ bamʋ lɩɩ nbunɔ kʋmaa tɔ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋmaa tɔ, yɛ asa kʋmaa tɔ yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ kʋmaa tɔ.

english_verse: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ fʋ taa bamʋ ba loo fu kuwura‐gyii mʋ tɔ, waa bamʋ aseepu kɛ be sun anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, nɩɩ bee biti ba gyii kuwura kasɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n bɩla kɩɩ, nɩɩ n nu Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ngbɩn‐ngbɩn kudu ɩbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ nyɩmɩsa mɛɛ taalɛ bɩɩ bamʋ kɔnɔ ba maa kyaabɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ.

english_verse: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taa ɔbʋlɛ lala bɩɩ ɔlʋn yɛ, “Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ, mʋ nɩɩ ɔlʋn yɛ atɔ kparɛ yɛ kanyaasɩn yɛ kuwura tɔ ɔlʋn yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ bʋnyaa yɛ ayɩn‐bi ɩ dagaa mʋ.”

english_verse: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nu atɔ tʋʋsa kpini mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ sʋ yɛ kasɛ tɔ yɛ ɔpʋ tɔ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɩmʋ kpini tɔ bɛɛ bɩɩ yɛ, “Ayɩn‐bi yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ bʋnyaa yɛ ɔlʋn, ɩ gyɛ sa ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ, yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kakpaa.”

english_verse: The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, “Amen.” Nɩɩ abɩlɩsa mʋ ba muŋa sun Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ.

english_verse: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
translation_verse: N wu Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ maa kyatɛ atɔ tarɩsa asunɔ mʋ tɔ kɩgyankpaasa mʋ. Nɩɩ n nu atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ɔbʋlɛ maa pʋntɛ fɛɛ bʋlayɩ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Ba!”

english_verse: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
translation_verse: N maa kɩɩ mʋ n wu gbaŋɛ fufuli! Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii mʋ mʋ sʋ kɩtaa, nɩɩ ba sa mʋ kuwura‐wʋrʋ, nɩɩ o dii fɛɛ ɔkɔ‐gyisapu mʋ nɩɩ ee kpee ɔ naa kɔ gyii sʋ.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa nyɔsapʋ mʋ, n nu kʋtɔ kɩɩsa nyɔsapʋ mʋ maa tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Ba!”

english_verse: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ gbaŋɛ kʋ bɩla lɩɩ ba, ɔ gyɛ ɔpɩpɛɛ nɩn. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii mʋ mʋ, ba sa mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ ɔ lɛɛ kayɩɩ yuuli lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ, kɛ asa ba mɔɔ abaa. Nɩɩ ba sa mʋ sakunde lala kʋ.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa sasapʋ mʋ, n nu kʋtɔ kɩɩsa sasapʋ mʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ba!” N maa kɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ n wu gbaŋɛ lenbiri maa yɩlɛ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii mʋ mʋ sʋ kɩbɩlɩtɔ kaalasa mʋ kɩbaa tɔ.

english_verse: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ lɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ asɛ yɛ, “Ayu mudu ii biti ɩ waa ɔgyalɩn, kɛ ɩmʋ kɩgya ii biti ɩ waa kakɛ kʋlʋn kusun kʋkɔka nɩɩ akyembee mɔɔ imuduu ɩsa kɩgya ii biti ɩ waa kakɛ kʋlʋn kusun kʋkɔka, amaa fan man kan nyita nfɔ yɛ nta!”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ maa kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa nasapʋ mʋ, n nu kʋtɔ kɩɩsa nasapʋ mʋ ɔbʋlɛ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ba!”

english_verse: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
translation_verse: N maa kɩɩ tʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ n wu gbaŋɛ mʋ nɩɩ mʋ ifuu ɩ du fɛɛ ndaara mʋ, maa yɩlɛ, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii mʋ mʋ, bɛɛ tɩɩ mʋ yɛ Lewu, yɛgɛ kamandɛ mɔɔ buu mʋ kamaa. Ba sa bamʋ ɔlʋn yɛ ba mɔɔ kasɛ sʋ asa nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nna mʋ tɔ kʋkʋlʋn. Ɩkɔ yɛ akʋn yɛ alɔ yɛ kasɛ sʋ ndʋnbwɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba taa mɔɔ bamʋ.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith.
translation_verse: Saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa nusapʋ mʋ, asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ katɔwɛ sʋ, yɛ kɩtɔwɛ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ lɩɩ Yesu Krisito sʋ mʋ, n wu bamʋ kala ana ba maa bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ ɔpɩɩ tɔ.

english_verse: They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kuusi kenken tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Owura lala, Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ yɛ kesintin wuya, saŋa mɔɔ nɩɩ fii biti fu gyoo naa fʋʋ, pɔyɩ kɛ fʋ talɛ, kɛ fʋ bɩɩtɛ kasɛ sʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ anɛ mʋ asʋ”

english_verse: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa akaala mʋ ɩbalɩmasʋ fufuli, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan nyɛ kenyiita saŋa gbɛrɛɛ tii sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ, fanɛ bɩrɩsa ana Krisito kusun awaapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ asa ba mɔɔ bamʋ fɛɛ fanɛ mʋ, kɔnɔ ɔ fʋʋ pɔyɩ.”

english_verse: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
translation_verse: N maa kɩɩ mʋ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa siyesapʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen ɔlʋn sʋ, nɩɩ kyɔwɛ biliŋi kitentenbiri tumm fɛɛ kɩtaa lenbiri. Nɩɩ ɔbwaayɩ payɩ kɔɔ fɛɛ nkalan.

english_verse: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ akyikpebi a lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɩyɛ kasɛ fɛɛ afuu bʋlʋnsa ɩ da nɩɩ ɩ yɩyaa figi abi ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ sʋsʋ nyaa fɛɛ kalan nɩɩ ba bɔɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ abɩɩ yɛ afintin kpini ɩ bɩɩtɛ lɩɩ ɩmʋ ipula.

english_verse: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ awura yɛ asa kparɛ, yɛ anaakɔpʋ abɩlɩsa yɛ afulee wuya ana, yɛ alʋnpʋ yɛ anyɛ kpini, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ bamʋ abaa sʋ kpini, ba sɩlɛ naa baala afʋlɛ ɩbɔ tɔ, yɛ afʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ abɩɩ sʋ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ abɩɩ yɛ afʋlɛ mʋ yɛ, “Fan tɩyɛ anɛ sʋ baala anɛ lɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ ansi sʋ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ agbʋ lala sʋ.”

english_verse: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ agbʋ kakɛ lala mʋ kɩ fʋʋ, nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ yɩlɛ?

english_verse: After this I saw four angels. Each one was standing on one of the earth's four corners. The angels held back the four winds, so that no wind would blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree. These angels had also been given the power to harm the earth and the sea. Then I saw another angel come up from where the sun rises in the east, and he was ready to put the mark of the living God on people. He shouted to the four angels,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n bɩla wu kabɔɔ kʋ maa lɩɩ kyɔwɛ ɔlɩɩkpa ɛɛ ba sʋsʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa mʋ kʋtɔ duligisa. Nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala kuusi tɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ ana mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ ɔlʋn yɛ ba nyita kasɛ yɛ ɔpʋ mʋ.

english_verse: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan man nyita kasɛ abɛɛ ɔpʋ abɛɛ iyu, amɔɔ an duligi anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asunpu bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ pɔyɩ.”

english_verse: Then I heard how many people had been marked on the forehead. There were
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nu asa mʋ nɩɩ ba duligi bamʋ mʋ kɔnɔ. Ba bʋ ngbɩn kɩlɩfa yɛ adɩna yɛ ana lɩɩ Ɩsɩrayɩ nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ kpini tɔ.

english_verse: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
translation_verse: bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Amen! Ayɩn‐bi yɛ bʋnyaa yɛ kanyaasɩn yɛ afwaala yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ yɛ kɩtɩɩ kɩnyɛ yɛ ɔlʋn ɩ gyɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ kakpaa‐kakpaa! Amen!”

english_verse: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ n wu sakyɔ damantɛ kʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ karagɛ bamʋ. Ba lɩɩ ndɛndɛmandɛ yɛ nbunɔ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ yɛ asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ ba yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mu ansi tɔ. Ba bun ɩbalɩmasʋ fufuli yɛgɛ ba sʋ abalaafa bamʋ abaa tɔ.

english_verse: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taa ɔbʋlɛ lala bee kuusi bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kamɔlɩgɛ gyɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya sʋ mʋ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ lɛɛ.”

english_verse: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ mʋ kpini ba yɩlɛ kyaabɔɔ kuwura‐gya mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa mʋ yɛ atɔ‐kɩɩsa ana mʋ. Nɩɩ ba muŋa taa bamʋ ansi bun kasɛ kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ba sun Ɩbwaarɛ,

english_verse: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ abɩlɩsa mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ taasɛ mɛ yɛ, “Nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba bun ɩbalɩmasʋ fufuli nɩmʋ mʋ, anɩmʋ ana nɩn? Nɩɩ fɩnɛ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ?”

english_verse: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n lɛɛ mʋ kɔnɔ yɛ, “Ɔbɩlɩsa, fu i nyi.” Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba lɩɩ awʋrʋfɔ lala mʋ kigyii tɔ, nɩɩ ba fwɩɩ bamʋ ɩkaalɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nkalan tɔ, nɩɩ i fuuli mʋ.

english_verse: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ bee sun mʋ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ osunkpa obu lala mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ mʋ, ee biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ bamʋ asɛ kɛ o kun bamʋ.

english_verse: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ akʋn man bɩla yɛ ɔ kɩtaa bamʋ, nɩɩ bʋlɩfɔ man bɩla yɛ ɔ kɩtaa bamʋ. Nɩɩ kyɔwɛ man bɩla yɛ ɔ da bamʋ, nɩɩ awulawula man bɩla yɛ ii wiili bamʋ.

english_verse: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ nsana mʋ ii biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ bamʋ ɔkpapʋ. Kɛ o kpiya bamʋ ɔbʋn kumu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ sʋ nkpa nkyu mʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ kpayɛ ansikyu kʋmaa lɩɩ bamʋ ansi tɔ.”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
translation_verse: Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ maa kyatɛ kʋtɔ tarɩsa sunɔsapʋ mʋ, nɩɩ sʋsʋ waa surum fɛɛ kerefi kʋlʋn kɩbaafʋn.

english_verse: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ba taa nbɩlɛ nsunɔ ba sa bamʋ.

english_verse: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ maa sʋ suwa kalapɛ‐bi nɩɩ ɔ ba yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kaswɩɩ asɛ. Nɩɩ ba taa atɔ liifensa kʋkyɔ sa mʋ kɛ ɔ taa bʋga yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ sʋ, taa ba suwa kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ mʋ, kɛ o tuwa sa Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
translation_verse: Kɛ atɔ liifensa mʋ nɩɩ ba tuwa mʋ idiisii, taa bʋga yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ kabwaarɛ‐kʋlɛ mʋ, ɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ abaa tɔ kpee sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ.

english_verse: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa suwa kalapɛ‐bi mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ nyɔ fʋlɔn lɩɩ suwa kaswɩɩ mʋ sʋ bɔla suwa kalapɛ‐bi mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ ɩmʋ wʋrɩgɛ kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ bʋlayɩ kpira nɩɩ o kpira, ɔlanba ɩ waa, nɩɩ bʋlayɩ wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ nɩɩ kasɛ gyingyen. Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ tɔ ana nbɩlɛ kʋfʋlɛ

english_verse: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ nbɩlɛ mʋ ba kʋʋla bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ba fʋlɛ nbɩlɛ mʋ.

english_verse: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ gyankpaasa mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ. Nɩɩ n wu bʋlayɩ abuu yɛ fʋlɔn ɩ maa kyagɩlɛ yɛ nkalan lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba wʋrɩgɛ kasɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ kasɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa ɩmʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn kɩ dɩyɛ, nɩɩ iyu gbaa nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn gbaa dɩyɛ, nɩɩ afitiri bunboli mʋ kpini gbaa ɩ dɩyɛ.

english_verse: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nyɔsapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ. Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ kʋtɔ kʋ fɛɛ kɩbɩɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɩyɛ yen‐yen waa ɔpʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔpʋ mʋ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa ɩmʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn biliŋi nkalan.

english_verse: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sasapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ. Nɩɩ kikyikpebi lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɩyɛ fɛɛ ɔgyaa ba tɩyɛ ɩbʋn amu‐amu yɛ atiribo mʋ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa ketun kʋlʋn tɔ.

english_verse: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
translation_verse: Kikyikpebi mʋ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ yɛ Ɔlɩlɩ. Nɩɩ nkyu mʋ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn ɩ bʋlɩlɩ. Nɩɩ asa gaalaagaa ba nuu nkyu mʋ, nɩɩ ba wu‐wu, lɩɩ fɛɛ nkyu mʋ ɩ bʋlɩlɩ.

english_verse: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nasapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ. Nɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ daa ɔ kɩɩ tɔ mʋ, kakɛ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa tɔ mʋ, ketun kʋlʋn ki biliŋi kitentenbiri. Nɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔbwaayɩ yɛ akyikpebi ɩ daa ɩ kɩɩ tɔ mʋ, kagyanbwɛ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa tɔ mʋ, ketun kʋlʋn biliŋi kitentenbiri.

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n maa kɩɩ mʋ, n nu kpaakpaatɛ maa fugi sʋsʋ afuu tɔ ee su kenken yɛ, “Asa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba sii kasɛ sʋ, naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ nsa mʋ nɩɩ ba san mʋ, ba ba fʋlɛ bamʋ nbɩlɛ mʋ, bamʋ laakʋ ɩlɩɩ ɩsa!”

english_verse: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nusapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ, nɩɩ n wu kikyikpebi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba tɩyɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ taa ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ kebunɔ saafɩɩ sa mʋ.

english_verse: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
translation_verse: Maa tigi ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ, idiisii ɩ lɩɩ kɩmʋ tɔ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ bodobodo obu lala tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ idiisii ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kyɔwɛ yɛ sʋsʋ waa kitentenbiri.

english_verse: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ntuntunbi ba lɩɩ idiisii mʋ tɔ ba kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ ɔlʋn mʋ nɩɩ ba taa dun fɛɛ kasɛ sʋ kyankpatɩ lɛɛ mʋ.

english_verse: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
translation_verse: Ba tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ ba man kan mata kasɛ sʋ afitiri abɛɛ atɔ duusa abɛɛ iyu, amaa ba nyanla bamʋ nɩɩ ba man sʋ Ɩbwaarɛ kakyʋlɛ bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ mʋ.

english_verse: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
translation_verse: Ba man sa ntuntunbi mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ba mɔɔ asa, amaa ba sa ba mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ba nyanla bamʋ ɩbwaayɩ inuu. Nɩɩ ntuntunbi mʋ kudun ɩɩ kɔ fɛɛ kyankpatɩ maa dun nyɩmɩsa mʋ.

english_verse: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
translation_verse: Kanɩn ɩbwaayɩ maŋa tɔ mʋ asa maŋa bee biti ba biti lewu, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ lewu mʋ. Bee biti fɛɛ ba wu gbaa, amaa ba mɛɛ yɛ ba wu.

english_verse: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
translation_verse: Ntuntunbi mʋ ba du nɩn fɛɛ ɩgbaŋɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba kʋʋla bamʋ bee kpee ɩkɔ. Ba bun atɔ kʋ fɛɛ suwa awura‐wʋrʋ bamʋ amu tɔ nɩɩ bamʋ ansi tɔ i du fɛɛ asa ansi.

english_verse: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
translation_verse: Bamʋ inyini i du nɩn fɛɛ akyɩɩ inyini, nɩɩ bamʋ anyi i du fɛɛ barɩnɛ anyi mʋ.

english_verse: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
translation_verse: Ba sʋ atɔ kʋ fɛɛ abɩlɩtɔ panpasan kun bamʋ kɔkɔlɔ sʋ, nɩɩ ba maa fugi mʋ bamʋ abanbantɛ tɔ mʋ ɩlawʋ i du fɛɛ abɩlɩtɔ itoroko damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɩgbaŋɛ bɛɛ bɩɩtɛ ba sɩlɛ bee kpee ɩkɔ tɔ.

english_verse: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
translation_verse: Ba sʋ ɩpɩɩ nɩɩ bee duŋi fɛɛ ɩkyankpatɛ, nɩɩ bamʋ ɩpɩɩ mʋ tɔ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɔlʋn bɛɛ nyanla asa ɩbwaayɩ inuu mʋ.

english_verse: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Gyerebi gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ kufuu mʋ kyʋn. Amaa gyerebi ntun nnyɔ sʋ ɩ bʋ ɔkpa ɩɩ ba. Kabɩlɛ siyesapʋ mʋ bɩya gyerebi nyɔsapʋ

english_verse: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ siyesapʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ, nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa lɩɩ suwa kaswɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ nbɔntɔnbʋlɔn ana tɔ.

english_verse: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ siyesapʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kabɩlɛ mʋ yɛ, “Saŋɛ nbɔɔ nna mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ŋmina bamʋ yɩla ɔbʋn lala Gyufereti asɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ saŋɛ nbɔɔ nna maŋa, mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ bamʋ yɩla sa saŋa nɩmʋ yɛ kakɛ nɩmʋ yɛ ɔbwaayɩ nɩmʋ yɛ kesu nɩmʋ, yɛ ba mɔɔ kasɛ sʋ asa nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn.

english_verse: By listening, I could tell there were more than
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nu anaakɔpʋ mʋ kɔnɔ. Ba gyɛ ngbɩn kudu‐kudu kanɩn kudu kanɩn anyɔ (mɩlɩyɔn alɩfa anyɔ abɛɛ

english_verse: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Mɛ ansi sʋ kɩlɛɛkaapʋ mʋ tɔ mʋ, n wu ɩgbaŋɛ yɛ bamʋ adiipu. Nɩɩ adiipu mʋ ba sʋ abɩlɩtɔ panpasan kunsa bamʋ nkɔlɔ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩɩ payɩ fɛɛ fʋlɔn, nɩɩ i du fɛɛ buluu, nɩɩ kɩ bɩla ki du fɛɛ kɩkyʋn‐nyufo. Ɩgbɛŋɛ mʋ amu i du nɩn fɛɛ abɩranɛ amu, nɩɩ fʋlɔn yɛ idiisii yɛ ferinwuta ɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ.

english_verse: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
translation_verse: Kanɩn gyerebi atɔ asá mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ fʋlɔn yɛ idiisii yɛ ferinwuta nɩmʋ ɩ mɔɔ asa nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn.

english_verse: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
translation_verse: Ɩgbaŋɛ mʋ ɔlʋn bʋ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ yɛ bamʋ ndufa tɔ. Bamʋ ndufa i du nɩn fɛɛ anansɛ, nɩɩ ɩ sʋ amu nɩɩ ba sʋ bee gyii gyerebi.

english_verse: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba san kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ gyerebi atɔ nɩmʋ gbaa ɩ man mɔɔ bamʋ mʋ, ba man yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa. Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi abɛɛ ɩdakpa mʋ nɩɩ ba taa suwa abɛɛ gbiti abɛɛ kɩtasan abɛɛ abuu abɛɛ iyu mʋ, ba man yɛgɛ ɩmʋ kusun. Bamʋ ɩdakpa maŋa ɩ man kɩɩ, nɩɩ i mee nu, nɩɩ ɩ mɛɛ natɛ.

english_verse: No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba man yɛgɛ asa kɩmɔɔ, yɛ afɛ kʋtʋʋ yɛ akpa‐sɩn, yɛ kɩgyagaa kakyɩɩ yɛ kanyɩn biti yɛ keyu tɔ.

english_verse: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n bɩla wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ɔlʋlʋnpʋ kʋ maa kpɩlɩgɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ɛɛ ba kasɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ a bun mʋ fɛɛ atɔ bunsa, nɩɩ okyimale kyaabɔɔ mu kumu. Nɩɩ mu ansi i du fɛɛ kyɔwɛ, nɩɩ mʋ ayaa i du fɛɛ sabarɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɛɛ fʋlɔn.

english_verse: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kawʋlɛ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ ki bugi kɩ bʋ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ. Ɔ taa mʋ gyisa kɩyaa yii ɔpʋ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ bɩna kɩyaa yii kasɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ pʋntɛ kenken fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɩranɛ muusi mʋ. Maa pʋntɛ kanɩn mʋ bʋlayɩ kikpira asunɔ mʋ kyula sʋ.

english_verse: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
translation_verse: Bʋlayɩ kikpira asunɔ maa tɔwɛ mʋ, nɩɩ n biti n kyʋrɔɔ. Amaa n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ yɛ, “Taa asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fu nu lɩɩ bʋlayɩ kikpira asunɔ mʋ tɔ mʋ waa asiiri tɔ, man kyʋrɔɔ asɩn mʋ yɩla!”

english_verse: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ n wu maa yɩlɛ ɔpʋ sʋ yɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ, kʋsaa mʋ gyisa kɩbaa sʋsʋ.

english_verse: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ kakpaa, nɩɩ ɔ tʋʋ sʋsʋ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ, yɛ kasɛ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ, yɛ ɔpʋ yɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ, bɔ ntan tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩbwaarɛ man bɩla yɛ o gyoo!”

english_verse: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
translation_verse: Amaa saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sunɔsapʋ mʋ ii biti ɔ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ mʋ, kanɩn saŋa maŋa nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn baalasa mʋ ii biti ɩ ba tɔ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla tɔwɛ sa mʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ.

english_verse: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ n nu lɩɩ sʋsʋ mʋ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kpe naa kɔɔlɛ kawʋlɛ‐bi bɔɔsa mʋ nɩɩ ki bugi mʋ, lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ ɔpʋ sʋ yɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kɩbaa gyisa tɔ.”

english_verse: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ n kpee Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ asɛ naa tɔwɛ mʋ yɛ, “Taa kawʋlɛ‐bi mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ mʋ sa mɛ.” Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kɔɔlɛ, kɛ fʋ wɛ kɩmʋ. Kii biti kɩ baa kɩ bʋkɔn fʋ kɔnɔ tɔ fɛɛ ŋmasan, amaa fʋ ɔtɔ tɔ bɩrɛ kii biti kɩ baa kɩ bʋlɩlɩ kɩtɛ‐kɩtɛ.”

english_verse: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n kɔɔlɛ kawʋlɛ‐bi mʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ kɩba tɔ, nɩɩ n wɛ kɩmʋ. Kɩ waa mɛ kɔnɔ tɔ ɔkɔn fɛɛ ŋmasan mʋ, amaa n maa wɛ kɩmʋ lʋwɛ mʋ, mɛ ɔtɔ tɔ waa mɛ ɔlɩlɩ.

english_verse: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Ɩ dagaa fʋ maa kyaga sʋ fɩɩ tɔwɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ii biti ɩ ba asa gaalaagaa sʋ yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ sʋ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ sʋ yɛ awura sʋ mʋ.”

english_verse: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
translation_verse: Amaa yɛgɛ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ kasɔrɔga opula mʋ yɩla, man kaala tʋtɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mɛ ɩ taa tʋtɔ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba man gyɛ mɛ asa. Nɩɩ bee biti ba ba kɔ kɔɔlɛ mɛ kadɛ mʋ ɩbwaayɩ adɩna yɛ anyɔ.

english_verse: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
translation_verse: Falɛ kesintin agyipu anyɔ anɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ olifu iyu ɩnyɔ yɛ ɩfɩtɩla ɩgyagakpa ɩnyɔ mʋ. Anyɩn anyɔ banɩmʋ ɩ yɩlɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kasɛ wuya ansi tɔ.

english_verse: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
translation_verse: Anyɩn anyɔ banɩmʋ ba sʋ ɔlʋn ba taalɛ kɩtaa bʋlayɩ sʋsʋ, kɛ ɔ man bɩla da saŋa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ abwaarɛsɩn mʋ. Nɩɩ ba sʋ ɔlʋn ba taalɛ yɛgɛ nkyu ii biliŋi nkalan, nɩɩ ba bɩla ba sʋ ɔlʋn ba tɩɩ gyerebi ba tʋʋ kasɛ sʋ saŋa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bee biti.

english_verse: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kan lʋwɛ bamʋ abwaarɛsɩn kɩtɔwɛ mʋ, kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ lɩɩ ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ tɔ mʋ, ee biti ɔ ba kɔ yɛ bamʋ, taalɛ bamʋ kɛ ɔ mɔɔ bamʋ.

english_verse: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
translation_verse: Kɛ bamʋ abuni ɩ baa ɩ dɛ kadɛ lala mʋ kigbenbi sʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba da bamʋ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Yesu oyu laasa sʋ mʋ. Kɩŋasan tɔ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kadɛ lala nɩmʋ yɛ Sodom abɛɛ Egyipiti.

english_verse: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
translation_verse: Asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ yɛ ɔsʋwʋlɛ sʋ asa kpini bee biti ba ba yɩlɛ kɩɩ bamʋ abuni mʋ nkɛ nsa yɛ kɩdɩyɛ, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ be kyula kɛ ɔkʋ o pula bamʋ.

english_verse: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
translation_verse: Atɔwɛpʋ anyɔ maŋa lewu ii biti ɔ waa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kpini ɔkɔn, kɛ ba gyii bamʋ ansi kɛ ba kɛɛ abaa atɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kanɩn Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ anyɔ maŋa ba yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa ba gyii awʋrʋfɔ kasɛ sʋ gaa.

english_verse: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
translation_verse: Amaa nkɛ nsa yɛ kɩdɩyɛ kamaa mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ nkpa ɔfʋʋtɛ ba loo bamʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba kyiŋi kʋsʋ yɩlɛ bamʋ ayaa sʋ. Tʋtɔ mʋ, kufuu kɩ kɩtaa asa mʋ nɩɩ ba wu bamʋ mʋ.

english_verse: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ anyɔ mʋ ba nu ɔbʋlɛ lala kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɔwɛ bamʋ yɛ, “Fan dii ba sʋsʋ nfɩɩ.” Nɩɩ ba bɔla abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ tɔ dii kpee sʋsʋ yɛgɛ bamʋ dʋn ana ba kɩɩ bamʋ.

english_verse: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
translation_verse: Kanɩn opula maŋa tɔ nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen, nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ nbarɩgɛ tɔ kudu mʋ tɔ ketun kʋlʋn boyi tɩyɛ. Nɩɩ kanɩn kasɛ kigyingyen maŋa mɔɔ asa ngbɩn nsunɔ. Nɩɩ kufuu kɩ kɩtaa bamʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ nkpa mʋ, nɩɩ ba taa bʋnyaa sa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ.

english_verse: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
translation_verse: Gyerebi nyɔsapʋ mʋ kyʋn nɩɩ gyerebi sasapʋ mɔɔ bʋ ɔkpa sʋ ɛɛ ba bilen. Kabɩlɛ sunɔsapʋ

english_verse: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sunɔsapʋ mʋ fʋlɛ mʋ kabɩlɛ, nɩɩ ɩbʋlɛ damantɛ kʋ ii tɔwɛ kenken lɩɩ sʋsʋ yɛ, “Kasɛ sʋ kuwura‐gyii ki biliŋi anɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla lɛɛ mʋ lɛɛ, nɩɩ ee biti o gyii kuwura kakpaa.”

english_verse: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
translation_verse: bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ, Ɔlʋlʋnpʋ, an sʋ afwaala an sa fʋ, fɛɛ fʋ maa taa fʋ ɔlʋn lala mʋ nɩɩ fu piili fii gyii kuwura.

english_verse: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
translation_verse: Kasɛ sʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ be mee sun fʋ mʋ ba nyɛ agbʋ, amaa nbɩɩnbɩɩ fʋ agbʋ mʋ ba. Saŋa fʋ fɛɛ fʋ talɛ alewupu, kɛ fʋ ka fʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ fʋ ayaafɔlɛ kʋkɔ, yɛ fʋ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sɩlɛ fʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ, asa kparɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba san kpini. Saŋa mʋ fʋʋ fɛɛ fu nyita bamʋ nɩɩ bee nyita kasɛ mʋ.”

english_verse: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ ki bugi, nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ maa asa nsana katɔwɛ kyula kɩdakaa mʋ maa bʋ obu lala mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ n wu bʋlayɩ maa wɛɛ ɩsɛɛ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔlanba yɛgɛ ɩɩ pʋntɛ, nɩɩ kasɛ ki gyingyen, nɩɩ bʋlayɩ abuu ɩ lɩɩ tɩyɛ gaa.

english_verse: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
translation_verse: Ɔ sʋ ɔtɔ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kʋʋgɛ. Nɩɩ keduŋidi mʋ maa kɔ mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ sawʋ kenken.

english_verse: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n bɩla bwii wu sɩnkaala kʋ maa lɛɛ ɩmʋ n‐yɩɩ kaapʋ sʋsʋ. N wu kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa kʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ amu asunɔ yɛ agyakpaa kudu nɩɩ o bun awura‐wʋrʋ asunɔ amu mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mʋ ɔpɩɩ bɩɩtɛ akyikpebi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ nbarɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa mʋ ketun kʋlʋn ba tʋʋ kasɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ ba yɩlɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kʋʋgɛ mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ kan kʋʋgɛ mʋ kɛ ɔ nyɛ taa kebii mʋ mɩyɛ bilen.

english_verse: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ kebii‐nyɩnsa‐bii mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa kɩbɩlɩtɔ kuwura‐kpebi gyii ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini sʋ kuwura. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ ba taa kebii mʋ kpee Ɩbwaarɛ maa mʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ.

english_verse: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ sɩlɛ kpee keperi sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ lɔŋɔ yɩla sa mʋ, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba kɩɩ mʋ sʋ nkɛ kagbɩn kʋlʋn yɛ alɩfa anyɔ yɛ adisiye (

english_verse: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩnaa kɩtɩyɛ sʋsʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ bɩlɩsa asɛ Maakɛlɩ maa mʋ nbɔɔ ba kɔ yɛ kɩnansɛ dansa mʋ, nɩɩ kanansɛ dansa mʋ maa mʋ nbɔɔ gbaa ba kɔ yɛ bamʋ.

english_verse: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
translation_verse: Amaa kɩnansɛ dansa maa mʋ nbɔɔ ba kɔ tɩyɛ, nɩɩ ba man bɩla nyɛ ɔkyɩnakpa sʋsʋ.

english_verse: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
