translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba ŋɛ kanɩn kɩnansɛ dansa maŋa kyʋŋɛ kasɛ sʋ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ dɩdaa kanansɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ okisipu, abɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ pɩna dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini mʋ. Ba ŋɛ maa mʋ nbɔɔ mʋ kyʋŋɛ kasɛ sʋ.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ lala kʋ sʋsʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, “Nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kamɔlɩgɛ kɩ ba. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kaapʋ mʋ ɔlʋn fɛɛ Owura. Nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩɩ Krisito mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Wʋla lɛɛ mʋ, kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ kɩ lɩɩ ifuli. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ, anɛ tɔɔmaa ana ɔpwɩɩpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ yɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ansi tɔ ɛɛ pwɩɩ bamʋ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ mʋ, ba ŋɛ mʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɔyɩ.

english_verse: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ anɛ tɔɔmaa ana ba taa Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nkalan, yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ kesintin kɩlɛɛ tɔwɛ mʋ gyii Sɩtaanɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba man taa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ tɩɩ sɛhn, amaa ba taa bamʋ nkpa yɩlɛ tɔ sa lewu.

english_verse: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ, sʋsʋ yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan tɛ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, fan yɛgɛ fanɛ ansi i gyii. Amaa kasɛ yɛ ɔpʋ bɩrɛ, laakʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Sɩtaanɛ kpɩlɩgɛ ba kasɛ sʋ fanɛ asɛ, nɩɩ agbʋ a bɔla mʋ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ o nyi mʋ saŋa maa man bɩla ɔ kyɔ.”

english_verse: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
translation_verse: Kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ maa wu fɛɛ ba ŋɛ mʋ kyʋŋɛ kasɛ sʋ mʋ, ɔ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ɔ gya ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋgɛ obii‐nyɩnsa mʋ.

english_verse: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa ɔkyɩɩ mʋ kpaakpaatɛ lala abanbantɛ anyɔ, kɛ ɔ taalɛ fugi kpee kepiri sʋ katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba lɔŋɔ yɩla sa mʋ. Kɛ ba kɩɩ mʋ sʋ naa fʋʋ nsu nsa yɛ kɩdɩyɛ, kɛ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ ɔ man nyɛ mʋ.

english_verse: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ kɩnansɛ mʋ gbʋʋ nkyu lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ fɛɛ ɔbʋn mʋ, kɛ kɩmʋ ɔgyalɛ o gyii ɔkyɩɩ mʋ.

english_verse: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
translation_verse: Amaa kasɛ kɩ kyɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɔ, fɛɛ kɩ maa bugi kɩmʋ kɔnɔ mɩyɛ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ kɩnansɛ mʋ gbʋʋ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩnansɛ mʋ nyɛ agbʋ lɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔ kyʋn ɔ naa kɔ yɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ mu‐bii ana asansa mʋ kpini. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba buu Ɩbwaarɛ nbara, nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ kenken bee gyii Yesu asɩn mʋ kesintin mʋ.

english_verse: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ naa sii ɔ yɩlɛ ɔpʋ kɔnɔ.

english_verse: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
translation_verse: N wu kadʋnbwɛ kʋ maa lɩɩ ɔpʋ tɔ ɛɛ ba. Ɔ sʋ agyakpaa kudu yɛ amu asunɔ yɛgɛ awura‐wʋrʋ kudu ɩɩ bun agyakpaa mʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ atɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ saalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɩ gyan mu kumu kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ.

english_verse: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
translation_verse: Kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ n wu mʋ du fɛɛ ɔpɩya, nɩɩ mʋ ayaa i du nɩn fɛɛ ɔkyʋkyʋrakyʋrɛ ayaa, nɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ ki du fɛɛ bɩranɛ kɔnɔ. Nɩɩ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ taa mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ mʋ kuwura‐gya yɛ kɩmʋ kɩbɩlɩsa mʋ kpini sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ.

english_verse: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
translation_verse: Mu amu mʋ tɔ kʋkʋ du nɩn fɛɛ kɩ maa nyɛ lewu ɔlɔ mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ taalɛ mɔɔ, amaa ki wu. Nɩɩ i kyinkyin dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini nɩɩ ba buu mʋ.

english_verse: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa ba sun kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔ sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ ɔlʋn nɩɩ ba bɩla ba sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ bɛɛ taasɛ yɛ, “Anɩmʋ ɩ fʋʋ kadʋnbwɛ nɩmʋ? Anɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ kɔ yɛ mʋ?”

english_verse: The beast was allowed to brag and claim to be God, and for
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ɔ tɔwɛ alala‐sɩn kɛ ɔ saalɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ sa mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ o gyii kɩbɩlɩsa ɩbwaayɩ adɩna yɛ anyɔ.

english_verse: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o bugi mʋ kɔnɔ tɔwɛ nbusaa asɩn ee yii Ɩbwaarɛ, ee nyita mʋ kɩtɩɩ yɛgɛ ɛɛ saalɛ mʋ ɔkyɩnakpa yɛ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ mʋ asɛ sʋsʋ mʋ kpini.

english_verse: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla sa mʋ ɔkpa yɛ ɔ kɔ mʋ asa, kɛ o gyii bamʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla sa mʋ ɔkpa yɛ o gyii kuwura nbunɔ kʋmaa yɛ asa kʋmaa yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋmaa yɛ ndɛndɛmandɛ kpini sʋ.

english_verse: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
translation_verse: Kasɛ sʋ asa kpini bee biti ba sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ. Amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ bamʋ atɩɩ waa nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ lɩɩ dulinyaa npiili asɛ mʋ nkʋn. Nkpa ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ gyɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ mʋ lɛɛ.

english_verse: If you have ears, then listen!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ asʋ mʋ o nu.

english_verse: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ dɛ sa fɛɛ bee biti ba tii ɔkʋ obu mʋ, bee biti ba kɩtaa mʋ naa tii. Nɩɩ ɩ kan ɩ dɛ fɛɛ sakunde nɩɩ bee biti ba taa mɔɔ ɔkʋ mʋ, sakunde lewu nɩɩ ee biti o wu. Asɩn nɩmʋ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba maa nyɛ kenyiita, kɛ ba nyɛ nkɔlɔ yɩlɛ bamʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii tɔ. Kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ mʋ

english_verse: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n bɩla wu kadʋnbwɛ kʋ maa paatɛ lɩɩ kasɛ tɔ. Ɔ sʋ agyakpaa anyɔ fɛɛ kabʋlʋpʋ, amaa ɛɛ tɔwɛ fɛɛ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ.

english_verse: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
translation_verse: Ɔ sʋ kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ kɩbɩlɩsa, nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ sun kusun. Nɩɩ ɔ nyan kasɛ yɛ kɩmʋ sʋ asa kpini, yɛ ba sun kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ mu lewu ɔlɔ mʋ wu mʋ.

english_verse: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kadʋnbwɛ nyɔsapʋ nɩmʋ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa lala, halɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ fʋlɔn lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ nɩɩ asa ba wu yɛ bamʋ ansi.

english_verse: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ ɔkpa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa mʋ sʋ mʋ, ɔ yɩlɛ kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ ayaa tɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa, ɔ sʋ ɛɛ pɩna dulinyaa tɔ asa kpini. Nɩɩ ɔ nyan dulinyaa tɔ asa yɛ ba pɔyɩ ɔdakpa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɩlɛ sa kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ nyɛ lewu ɔlɔ, nɩɩ o bwii kpaarɛ mʋ.

english_verse: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ sa kadʋnbwɛ nyɔsapʋ mʋ ɔkpa, fɛɛ ɔ fʋʋtɛ nkpa ɔfʋʋtɛ sa ɔdakpa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ yɩlɛ sa kadʋnbwɛ gyankpaasa mʋ. Kɛ kɩ taalɛ tɔwɛ, kɛ kɩ taalɛ mɔɔ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o kina yɛ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ o sun mʋ mʋ.

english_verse: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla nyan ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ, abʋlʋpʋ yɛ alʋlʋnpʋ, afulee wuya ana yɛ atiripu, bamʋ nɩɩ bee gyii bamʋ kɩbaa sʋ yɛ anyɛ yɛ ba nyɛ kakyʋlɛ bamʋ abaa gyisa sʋ abɛɛ bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ.

english_verse: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
translation_verse: Saamʋ, ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ man sʋ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kakyʋlɛ mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ taalɛ sɔɔ abɛɛ ɔ fɛ. Kakyʋlɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ, abɛɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ akaragɛ‐bi.

english_verse: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa ɔkʋmaa maa nyɛ kanyaasɩn. Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ kanyaasɩn mʋ, ɔ taalɛ bɩɩ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ akaragɛ‐bi mʋ kasɛ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ akaragɛ‐bi nɩmʋ yɩla sa nyɩmɩsa nɩn. Akaragɛ‐bi nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ alɩfa asiye yɛ adisiye yɛ asiye (

english_verse: who had been rescued from the earth.
translation_verse: ) mʋ nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ mɔlɩgɛ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ mʋ nkʋn.

english_verse: I looked and saw the Lamb standing on Mount Zion! With him were
translation_verse: N maa bɩla kɩɩ mʋ, n wu Kabʋlʋpʋ kʋ maa yɩlɛ Siyon kɩbɩɩ mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ asa ngbɩn kɩlɩfa yɛ adɩna yɛ ana (

english_verse: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nu ɩlawʋ kʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ fɛɛ ɔbʋn maa sʋ ɔgyalɛ abɛɛ bʋlayɩ maa pʋntɛ mʋ. Ɩlawʋ mʋ nɩɩ n nu mʋ, ii su nɩn fɛɛ adʋnlɛ alanpʋ kʋkyɔ ba maa lan bamʋ adʋnlɛ mʋ.

english_verse: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
translation_verse: Nɩɩ asa ngbɩn kɩlɩfa yɛ adɩna yɛ ana ba yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ yɛ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ yɛ abɩlɩsa adʋnyɔ yɛ ana mu ansi tɔ, nɩɩ ba bɩɩ ɔlʋn pʋpwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkʋ man taalɛ bɩɩla, amɔɔ asa ngbɩn kɩlɩfa yɛ adɩna yɛ ana (

english_verse: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
translation_verse: Kanɩn asa maŋa ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man taa kakyɩɩ‐biti waa bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ineesi, nɩɩ be du nɩn fɛɛ anyɩn mʋ nɩɩ ba man nyi akyɩɩ. Nɩɩ ba buu Kabʋlʋpʋ Yesu, mʋ katɩn kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee kpee. Nɩɩ ɔ ka bamʋ amu sʋ kʋkɔ lɩɩ asa tɔ, ba du nɩn fɛɛ abi gyankpaasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ Yesu mʋ lɛɛ.

english_verse: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
translation_verse: Atɩntɩnsa kʋ ɩ man pɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ nnɔ tɔ, nɩɩ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ ɩ man sʋ asɩn libi akʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asa mʋ

english_verse: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ maa fugi ɛɛ kyaabɔɔ sʋsʋ. Ɔ sʋ asɩn danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ tɔ kakpaa nɩɩ ɛɛ tɔwɛ ɛɛ sa ɔsʋwʋlɛ kʋmaa yɛ kebunɔ kʋmaa yɛ katɔwɛ kʋmaa yɛ asa kpini.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala kuusi tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan sɩlɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɛ fan taa bʋnyaa sa mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ katalɛ saŋa mʋ fʋʋ. Fan sun ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ, ɔpʋ yɛ ɩbʋn.”

english_verse: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ nyɔsapʋ kʋ buu ɔbanban mʋ lɩɩ sʋsʋ ɛɛ pʋntɛ kenken yɛ, “Babilon tɩyɛ, kadɛ kparɛ mʋ tɩyɛ. Mʋ ɩ gyɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ asa kpini ba waa kɩgyagaa fɛɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ i du fɛɛ maa sa bamʋ nta bʋlʋnsa nɩɩ ba nuu mʋ. Ɩnɩmʋ ii biti ɩ yɛgɛ kɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ɔ bɩɩtɛ bamʋ asʋ.”

english_verse: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ sasapʋ kʋ gbaa buu abanban mʋ, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɔbʋlɛ lala ɛɛ pʋntɛ yɛ, “Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ o sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ yɛ mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa, nɩɩ ɔ sʋ mʋ kakyʋlɛ mʋ kɩsɩkpɩɩ sʋ abɛɛ mʋ kɩbaa sʋ mʋ,

english_verse: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
translation_verse: kanɩn ɔsa maŋa gbaa mʋ ii biti o nuu Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ nta mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋlʋn nɩɩ ɔ kyɛɛgɛ waa mʋ agbʋ kawɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ. Bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa ɩnɩmʋ kpini mʋ bee biti ba nyɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ ferinwuta fʋlɔn tɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ yɛ mʋ Kabʋlʋpʋ mu ansi tɔ.

english_verse: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
translation_verse: Asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ yɛ mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ, abɛɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ mʋ kakyʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ mʋ, fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ dɩyɛ bamʋ mʋ, ii biti dɩyɛ kakpaa, nɩɩ ba mɛɛ yɛ ba nyɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ.”

english_verse: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Asɩn nɩmʋ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba nyɛ kenyiita. Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu mʋ nbara, nɩɩ ba nyɛ nkɔlɔ yɩlɛ Yesu Krisito kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa tɔwɛ lɩɩ sʋsʋ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ ɩnɩmʋ yɩla! Lɩɩ nbɩɩnbɩɩ nɩmʋ ɩ sʋ ii kpee mʋ, kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba san ba sʋ kɩkɔɔlɛ‐gyii Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu tɔ nɩɩ ba wu mʋ.” Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ gbaa tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kesintin nɩn, bee biti ba nyɛ ɔfʋʋtɛ lɩɩ bamʋ kɩkpɩnɛ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ awaasa kʋkɔka ii biti i buu bamʋ.” Adɔɔ‐gyitɔ kɩtɩŋɛ

english_verse: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
translation_verse: N maa kɩɩ mʋ, n wu kabwaarɛ wʋlɛ fufuli kʋ, nɩɩ n wu ɔkʋ maa tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa. O bun suwa kuwura‐wʋrʋ nɩɩ loogyi bʋyɛsa bʋ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ.

english_verse: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ gbaa lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala kuusi tɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ mʋ sʋ yɛ, “Taa fu loogyi sɔɔ adɔɔ‐gyitɔ mʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ kasɛ sʋ atɔ duusa mʋ a payɩ, nɩɩ ɩmʋ kɩtɩŋɛ saŋa fʋʋ.”

english_verse: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
translation_verse: Ɩmʋ sʋ ɔmʋnɩ ɔ tɛ abwaarɛ‐wʋlɛ sʋ mʋ, taa mʋ loogyi mʋ sɔɔ atɔ duusa mʋ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ kpini.

english_verse: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ bɩla lɩɩ sʋsʋ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, yɛgɛ ngbaa mʋ sʋ loogyi bʋyɛsa.

english_verse: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
translation_verse: Biyo, Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ fʋlɔn sʋ mʋ, lɩɩ saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala pʋntɛ tɔ tɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ loogyi bʋyɛsa mʋ yɛ, “Taa fu loogyi bʋyɛsa mʋ kʋʋla oyu abi akyaa mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ sʋ bɛɛ waa wayɩn nta mʋ lɩɩ kasɛ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩmʋ abi mʋ ɩɩ payɩ.”

english_verse: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa mu loogyi mʋ sɔɔ oyu abi mʋ kasɛ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla ɩmʋ naa waa katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyan oyu abi mʋ bɛɛ lɛɛ nkyu. Opula mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyan maŋa mʋ, ɩ yɩlɛ sa Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ lala mʋ.

english_verse: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n bɩla wu sɩnkaala yɛ asɩn kyinkyinsa kʋ sʋsʋ. Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ nsunɔ, nɩɩ ba sʋ laalaalʋwɛ igyerebi nsunɔ bɛɛ ba. Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ igyerebi laalaalʋwɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ mʋ ɩ fʋʋ ɔkaa.

english_verse: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu kʋtɔ kʋ fɛɛ ɔpʋ nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ki du fɛɛ digi nɩɩ kɩ bʋga yɛ fʋlɔn. Nɩɩ n wu bamʋ nɩɩ ba kɔ lɩɩ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ, yɛ mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ, yɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ akaragɛ‐bi mʋ. N wu ba maa yɩlɛ ɔpʋ mʋ kɔnɔ, yɛgɛ ba sʋ adʋnlɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ sa bamʋ mʋ.

english_verse: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba bɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kɩyaafɔlɛ Mosesi yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ɔlʋn yɛ, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, fʋ awaasa kparɛ mʋ ɩ kyɔ nɩɩ ii kyinkyin. Fʋ ɩ gyɛ kasɛ sʋ kpini owura, fʋ ɩkpa i kyiigi nɩɩ ɩ gyɛ kesintin.

english_verse: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
translation_verse: O Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, anɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ sɩlɛ fʋ, nɩɩ anɩmʋ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ taa bʋnyaa sa fʋ kɩtɩɩ? Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ nkʋn ii du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ kasɛ sʋ. Kasɛ sʋ asa kpini bee biti ba be muŋa fu ansi tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ fʋ ɩkpa kyiigisa mʋ ɩ lɩɩ ifuli.

english_verse: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n kɩɩ nɩɩ n wu ba maa tigi opula kyɩrɛkyɩrɛkpa kɩkyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tantɩ kubuu mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ sʋsʋ mʋ tɔ yɩla.

english_verse: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Tantɩ kubuu maŋa tɔ nɩɩ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ igyerebi asunɔ mʋ ba lɩɩ, yɛgɛ ba bun ɩkaalɛ fufuli parɛparɛ yɛgɛ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba taa suwa ɩkɩrɩsʋ ŋmina bamʋ nkɔlɔ tɔ.

english_verse: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa ana mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ sa Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ nsunɔ mʋ, suwa ɩlapɛ isunɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ nkpa tɔ kakpaa mʋ agbʋ a bɔla ɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa yɛ mʋ ɔlʋn ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ idiisii ɩ ba bɔla tantɩ kubuu mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ mɛɛ taalɛ loo tʋtɔ, amɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ nsunɔ mʋ ba lʋwɛ igyerebi isunʋ mʋ pɔyɩ.

english_verse: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ lala kʋ lɩɩ tantɩ kubuu mʋ tɔ, kɩ maa tɔwɛ kɩɩ sa Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ yɛ, “Fan kpee naa kyɛɛgɛ Ɩbwaarɛ agbʋ ɩlapɛ isunɔ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ kasɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ gyankpaasa mʋ naa kyɛɛgɛ agbʋ lapɛ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ɩlɔ libi mʋ nɩɩ ɩ gyɔɔ nɩɩ ɩɩ kɔ mʋ ɩ yaasɛ loo asa mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kakyʋlɛ nɩɩ bee sun mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ.

english_verse: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kabɔɔ nyɔsapʋ mʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ waa ɔpʋ tɔ, nɩɩ i biliŋi nkalan fɛɛ nyɩmɩsa wusa nkalan, nɩɩ atɔ kɩɩsa kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɔpʋ tɔ mʋ ba wu.

english_verse: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sasapʋ mʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ waa ɩbʋn yɛ ɩbʋn amu tɔ, nɩɩ nkyu mʋ i biliŋi nkalan.

english_verse: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ kɩɩ nkyu sʋ mʋ maa tɔwɛ yɛ, “O Ɔkyɩrɛkyɩrɛpʋ, Fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ fʋ wʋla fʋ bʋ tɔ, Fu gyii asɩn mʋ, ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ fu gyii asɩn mʋ kesintin.

english_verse: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ bamʋ ɩ mɔɔ fʋ asa yɛ fʋ atɔwɛpʋ, nɩɩ bamʋ nkalan ɩ kyɛɛgɛ wʋrɩgɛ, ɩmʋ sʋ fʋ sa bamʋ nkalan yɛ ba nuu kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa bamʋ.”

english_verse: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nu Ɩbwaarɛ saraga kaswɩɩ mʋ maa kyula sʋ yɛ, “Ɛɛhn, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, fʋ katalɛ kɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩɩ kɩ dɛ ɔkpa.”

english_verse: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nasapʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ kyɔwɛ sʋ, nɩɩ kyɔwɛ mʋ nyɛ ɔlʋn kɛ o fugya asa fɛɛ fʋlɔn.

english_verse: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kyɔwɛ mʋ awulawula i fugya bamʋ gaa, nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ yii Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ igyerebi ɩnɩmʋ sʋ ɔlʋn mʋ. Amaa ba kina ba man yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa, kɛ ba taa bʋnyaa sa mʋ.

english_verse: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ nusapʋ mʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kuwura‐gya sʋ, nɩɩ mu kuwura‐gyii tɔ kpini ki biliŋi kitentenbiri. Nɩɩ ɩmʋ kpini kʋkɔ ɩ yɛgɛ nɩɩ asa ba duŋi bamʋ anobi.

english_verse: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ lɩɩ bamʋ kayɩɩ‐kɔ yɛ ɩlɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba nyɛ sʋ mʋ, ba tɔwɛ yii sʋsʋ Ɩbwaarɛ. Amaa ba kina ba man yɛgɛ alibi kɩwaa kɛ ba ba Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ.

english_verse: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ siyesapʋ mʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ lapɛ mʋ wʋrɩgɛ ɔbʋn lala Gyufereti mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kɩmʋ nkyu ɩ nyaa, kɛ ɩ nyɛ lɔŋɔ ɔkpa sa awura mʋ nɩɩ bee biti ba lɩɩ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ ba mʋ.

english_verse: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu nbwɩɩ libi asa mʋ nɩɩ ba du fɛɛ apulo. Ba lɩɩ kɩnansɛ pɩpɛɛ dansa mʋ yɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ yɛ antɩŋɛsa atɔwɛpʋ mʋ nnɔ tɔ.

english_verse: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
translation_verse: Bamʋ ɩ gyɛ Sɩtaanɛ n‐yaayu mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa adangana. Nbwɩɩ libi asa nɩmʋ bee kpee dulinyaa tɔ awura kpini asɛ, naa kʋʋla bamʋ ba naa kɔ kɩnaa, Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Ɔlʋlʋnpʋ mʋ kakɛ lala mʋ akɛ.

english_verse: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ mʋ wʋla tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan nyiŋi! N biti n ba fɛɛ oyu. Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa ɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ daa mʋ n‐yɩɩ sʋ nɩɩ ɔ kʋʋla o gyoo mɛ, nɩɩ ɔ man dɩlɩgɛ mʋ atɔ bunsa, kɛ ɔ natɛ kibulunkpan kɛ o gyii ipeeli.”

english_verse: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbwɩɩ libi mʋ ba kʋʋla awura naa bʋga opula kʋlʋn, katɩn mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ Hibriwu katɔwɛ tɔ yɛ Amagadon mʋ.

english_verse: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ sunɔsapʋ kyɛɛgɛ mʋ agbʋ lapɛ mʋ waa afuu tɔ. Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ lala kɔ maa lɩɩ kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ mʋ tɔ, kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ yɛ, “Ɩ waa lʋwɛ!”

english_verse: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Babilon kadɛ lala mʋ kɩ barɩgɛ tɔ ntun nsa, nɩɩ ndɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kpini i nyita. Ɩbwaarɛ nyiŋi Babilon kadɛ lala mʋ alibi, nɩɩ ɔ yɛgɛ nɩɩ ba nuu mʋ agbʋ nta mʋ lɩɩ mʋ kawɛ tɔ.

english_verse: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kifintin kʋmaa nɩmʋ nyaa, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla wu abɩɩ kasɛ sʋ.

english_verse: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɩlapɛ isunɔ mʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ba tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Ba, kɛ n kaapʋ fʋ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ libi, mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ sa kadɛ lala, mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ tiri ɩbʋn lala mʋ, kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ kii biti kɩ baa kɩmʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
translation_verse: Kadɛ lala nɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ awura bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ anyɩn bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ ɔkyɩɩ‐kyagaa sʋ mʋ. Kasɛ sʋ asa kpini ba nuu mʋ kɩgyagaa nta mʋ bʋʋ.”

english_verse: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa mɛ kayaayu tɔ kpee ifuli sʋ. Tʋtɔ nɩɩ n wu ɔkyɩɩ maa tɛ kadʋnbwɛ pɩpɛɛ sʋ, nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ atɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ saalɛ Ɩbwaarɛ gyaga kadʋnbwɛ mʋ sʋ, yɛgɛ ɔ sʋ amu asunɔ yɛ agbakpaa kudu.

english_verse: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
translation_verse: Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ bun atɔ pɩpɛɛ yɛ kɩrɩman‐kyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔgyalɩn, yɛ suwa yɛ abuu mʋ nɩɩ ɩ sʋ ɔgyalɩn yɛ agyibi kparɛ. Nɩɩ ɔ sʋ suwa kɔɔpʋ mʋ kɩbaa tɔ, yɛgɛ atɔ kisisa yɛ ineesi atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɩgyagaa sʋ mʋ ɩ bɔla kɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ asiiri aŋasantɩɩ gyaga mʋ kɩsɩkpɩɩ sʋ yɛ, ASƖN KPARƐ BABILON KADƐ KPARƐ ABƲRƲKƆƆNƐ BAMƲ‐NYI, YƐ KASƐ SƲ INEESI KPINI ƖMƲ NYI.

english_verse: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
translation_verse: N wu fɛɛ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nuu Ɩbwaarɛ asa nkalan, bamʋ nɩɩ ba yɩlɛ tɔ bee gyii kesintin bɛɛ sa Yesu mʋ, nɩɩ ɩɩ bʋʋ mʋ fɛɛ nta mʋ. N maa wu kanɩn mʋ, i kyinkyin mɛ gaa.

english_verse: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taasɛ mɛ yɛ, “Mɩnɛ ɩ waa nɩɩ i kyinkyin fʋ? Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ yɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ sʋ amu asunɔ yɛ agbakpaa kudu, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ dii mʋ, n biti n lɛɛ ɩmʋ kɩŋasan tɔ kaapʋ fʋ.

english_verse: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
translation_verse: Kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ daa wu nɩɩ ɔ bʋ tɔ mʋ, nbɩɩnbɩɩ ɔ man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ee biti ɔ lɩɩ ɔbɔ bunkyinsa lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ man sʋ ɔkaa mʋ tɔ ba, kɛ ɔ ba gyaŋa mu lewu. Nɩɩ asa mʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ man kyʋrɔɔ bamʋ atɩɩ waa nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ, lɩɩ dulinyaa npiili asɛ mʋ, nɩɩ ba kan wu kadʋnbwɛ mʋ, ii biti i kyinkyin bamʋ fɛɛ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ maa kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ man bɩla ba ɔ bʋ tɔ, amaa nɩɩ ee biti ɔ bɩla bwii ba.

english_verse: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
translation_verse: “Asɩn nɩmʋ falɛ sʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asa ba maa nyɛ nfɛɛrɛ yɛ kanyaasɩn. Amu asunɔ mʋ ɩ gyɛ abɩɩ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩ mʋ tɛ ɩmʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ bɩla ɩ gyɛ awura asunɔ.

english_verse: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
translation_verse: Awura mʋ tɔ mʋ anuu ba tɩyɛ, nɩɩ ɔkʋlʋn san ee gyii kuwura, nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ san mʋ mɔɔ man pɩɩta ba. Nɩɩ ɔ kan ba mʋ, ee biti o gyii kuwura nkɛ gbɛrɛɛ kʋ.

english_verse: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
translation_verse: Kadʋnbwɛ mʋ nɩɩ naafɔɔ ɔ kyɩna ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ mʋ, mʋ ɩ gyɛ owura buruwasapu mʋ. Nɩɩ o tii awura gyankpaasa asunɔ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ ba gyaŋa mu lewu.

english_verse: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
translation_verse: “Agyakpaa kudu mʋ nɩɩ fu wu mʋ yɩlɛ sa awura kudu mʋ nɩɩ ba man pɩɩ nyɛ kuwura‐gyii mʋ. Amaa bee biti ba nyɛ kɩbɩlɩsa kɛ ba maa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ ba gyii kuwura kerefi kʋlʋn.

english_verse: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
translation_verse: Awura kudu mʋ ba sʋ nfɛɛrɛ kʋlʋn, nɩɩ bee biti ba taa bamʋ ɔlʋn kɩbɩlɩsa sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ.

english_verse: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
translation_verse: Bee biti ba kɔ yɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ, amaa Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ii biti ɔ taalɛ bamʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ ɩ gyɛ anyɩrɩpɛ tɔ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ, yɛ awura tɔ Owura. Nɩɩ mʋ asa ɩ gyɛ, bamʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɩɩ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee buu mʋ kesintin tɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Ɩbʋn mʋ nɩɩ fu wu nɩɩ kabʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ mʋ tɛ o tiri ɩmʋ mʋ, ɩ yɩlɛ sa asa damantɛ yɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ yɛ ntɔwɛ kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ.

english_verse: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
translation_verse: Kadʋnbwɛ yɛ agyakpaa kudu mʋ nɩɩ fu wu mʋ, bee biti ba kisi kabʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ mʋ. Nɩɩ bee biti ba kɔɔlɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ lɩɩ mʋ asɛ, kɛ ba yɛgɛ mʋ ɔ natɛ kibulunkpan.” Bee biti ba wɛ mʋ kayɩɩ‐wʋlɛ mʋ, kɛ ba taa ɩmʋ nɩɩ ɩ san mʋ tʋʋ fʋlɔn tɔ.

english_verse: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ taa kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ee biti fɛɛ ba waa mʋ waa bamʋ nfɛɛrɛ tɔ, kɛ ba nyɛ waa mu kebiti. Kanɩn sʋ nɩɩ awura kudu mʋ ba kyula taa bamʋ ɔlʋn mʋ sa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ, kɛ o gyii kuwura naa fʋʋ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɩla mʋ ɩ ba tɔ.

english_verse: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_verse: Ɔkyɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ fu wu mʋ, yɩlɛ sa kadɛ kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ kii gyii kuwura kasɛ sʋ awura kpini sʋ.

english_verse: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ mɔɔ maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ ɛɛ ba. Ɔ sʋ ɔlʋn lala, nɩɩ mʋ kɩŋalagɛ kɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ kasɛ sʋ kpini.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ taa ɔbʋlɛ lala kuusi yɛ, “Babilon kadɛ kparɛ mʋ lɩɩ tɩyɛ. Kadɛ kparɛ mʋ ki boyi biliŋi kɩtaakpan. Kɩ ba biliŋi nbwɩɩ libi yɛ n‐yaayu libi, yɛ nbwiibi libi yɛ nbwiibi kisisa ɔbaalakpa.

english_verse: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɩsʋwʋlɛ sʋ kpini asa ba nuu kadɛ mʋ kabʋrʋkɔɔnɛ nta bʋlʋnsa mʋ. Nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ awura kpini bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ bɛɛ waa kanyɩn yɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa kadɛ mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ ayawʋ‐gyipu kpini ba nyɛ afulee yɛ atɔ tii sʋ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ ɔfʋʋtɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ nyɛ fʋʋ mʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n bɩla nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ mɔɔ maa lɩɩ sʋsʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Mɛ asa, fan lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ, kɛ fan man nyɛ waa alibi mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ waa mʋ, kɛ ɩ man naa ba fɛɛ fan nyɛ mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ mʋ tɔ ɩkʋ.

english_verse: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mu alibi kpini ɩ kʋʋla ɩ gyaga abaa sʋ naa fʋʋ sʋsʋ, nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nyiŋi mu alibi kpini yɛ ɩmʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ sʋ.”

english_verse: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan waa kadɛ mʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa fanɛ mʋ, kɛ kɩmʋ alibi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ waa mʋ, fan ka kɩmʋ kʋkɔ ɩlɩɩ ɩnyɔ sʋ. Nta bʋlʋnsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ taa sa asa nɩɩ ba nuu mʋ, fan waa ɩmʋ ɔlʋn kanɩn ɩlɩɩ ɩnyɔ sʋ sa kɩmʋ kɛ ki nuu.

english_verse: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
translation_verse: Fan yɛgɛ ki gyii awʋrʋfɔ yɛ kanyanla, fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ kɩ kyɩna nyɛ bʋnyaa nɩɩ ki gyii ɔkɔn, nɩɩ kɩ kyɩna kʋsaa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tɔwɛ mʋ kɔkɔlɔ tɔ yɛ, ‘N gyɛ owura‐kyɩɩsa, n man gyɛ okulapu‐kyɩɩ nɩɩ n mɛɛ yɛ n bɔɔta tɔ kakaakʋ.’

english_verse: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ kanɩn sʋ, kakɛ kʋlʋn, gyerebi ii biti i gyii kɩmʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ lewu yɛ kɩbɔɔta tɔ yɛ akʋn lala. Nɩɩ fʋlɔn ii biti ɔ dɩyɛ mʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ talɛ kɩmʋ mʋ, ɩ gyɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ.

english_verse: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
translation_verse: “Kasɛ sʋ awura kpini mʋ nɩɩ bee dii bɛɛ kpɩlɩgɛ, bɛɛ waa kanyɩn yɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba gyii ɔkɔn mʋ, ba kan wu fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɩyɛ kɩmʋ idiisii mʋ, bee biti ba su kɛ ba bɔɔta tɔ sa kɩmʋ.

english_verse: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
translation_verse: Kɩmʋ awʋrʋfɔ mʋ ii biti i waa bamʋ kufuu, ɩmʋ sʋ bee biti ba yɩlɛ kata‐kata kɛ ba su mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘O! Kadɛ kparɛ Babilon, laakʋ. Ɔlʋn kadɛ lala Babilon, laakʋ. Fʋ nyɛ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ opula tɔ.’

english_verse: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
translation_verse: “Kasɛ sʋ ayawʋ‐gyipu bee biti ba su kɛ ba bɔɔta tɔ lɩɩ kɩmʋ sʋ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ ɔkʋ man bɩla ɛɛ sɔɔ bamʋ atɔ fɛsa mʋ.

english_verse: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋ man bɩla ɛɛ sɔɔ suwa yɛ gbiti atɔ waasa yɛ ɔgyalɩn abuu ŋalɩgɛsa yɛ ɔgyalɩn agyibi yɛ akalala kparɛ yɛ agoo yɛ ataa lʋʋsa yɛ ɔgyalɩn ataa pɩpɛɛ yɛ abondiri yɛ kʋntɩ anyi atɔ yɛ ɔgyalɩn iyu salɛsa yɛ mɛɛtɔ yɛ abɩlɩtɔ yɛ afʋlɛ fufuli danbɩrasa.

english_verse: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
translation_verse: “Ayawʋ‐gyipu mʋ bee biti ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Babilon, atɔ danbɩrasa mʋ nɩɩ fu ansi ɩ payɩ fɛɛ fii biti fʋ nyɛ mʋ, ɩ nyaa yɛgɛ fʋ. Fʋ kanyɛ yɛ fʋ kɩtɩɩ kparɛ mʋ kpini ɩ kyʋn. Nɩɩ fʋ man bɩla yɛ fʋ nyɛ ɩmʋ.’

english_verse: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
translation_verse: Ayawʋ‐gyipu mʋ nɩɩ ba fɛ ɩnɩmʋ, nɩɩ ba nyɛ tɔnɔ lɩɩ kadɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, lɩɩ kufuu sʋ bee biti ba yɩlɛ kata bɔɔta tɔ, kɛ ba su lɩɩ mʋ gyerebi mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
translation_verse: Kɛ ba tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘O! Kadɛ kparɛ Babilon laakʋ. Fʋ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ asa ba kyɩna bee bun atɔ danbɩrasa, fɛɛ akalala kparɛ yɛ ɔgyalɩn ataa pɩpɛɛ yɛ agoo. Naafɔɔ bamʋ n‐yɩɩ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ suwa atɔ yɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa ɔgyalɩn abuu waa yɛ ɔgyalɩn agyibi.

english_verse: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
translation_verse: Amaa opula tɔ mʋ, fʋ kanɩn npatɩyɛ damantɛ maŋa kpini i muuri.’ “Ɔpʋ sʋ ɩkʋlɩ lala akɩɩsʋpʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ kyɩna ɩkʋlɩ lala tɔ bee kpee ɩkpa, yɛ ɩmʋ apalɩpʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa ɩmʋ tɔ kusun mʋ kpini, bee biti ba yɩlɛ kata‐kata.

english_verse: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba kan wu fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dɩyɛ kɩmʋ idiisii mʋ, bee biti ba su kuusi yɛ, ‘Kadɛ lala kʋ daa ɔ bʋ tɔ nɩɩ ku du fɛɛ kɩnɩmʋ dubi?’

english_verse: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
translation_verse: Bee biti ba paata bamʋ amu yɛgɛ bɛɛ bɔɔta tɔ, nɩɩ bee su bɛɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘O! Kadɛ kparɛ Babilon, laakʋ. Lɩɩ fʋ sʋ nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɩkʋlɩ lala bɛɛ waa kusun ɔpʋ sʋ mʋ, ba biliŋi atɔ wuya ana. Amaa opula tɔ mʋ, atɔ mʋ nɩɩ fʋ sʋ mʋ kpini i muuri.

english_verse: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ lʋnpʋ kʋ kukuri kubuu lala kʋ fɛɛ kubuu kɔyɩsa tʋʋ waa ɔpʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Falɛ nɩɩ bee biti ba muuri Babilon kadɛ lala ɔlʋn sʋ waa ɔpʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ba man bɩla yɛ ba wu kɩmʋ kakaakʋ.

english_verse: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
translation_verse: Babilon kadɛ lala, ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o nu adʋwʋlɛ alanpʋ yɛ ɩlʋn bɩɩpʋ yɛ nleebi yɛ nbɩlɛ afʋlɛpʋ kpini sʋ kakaakʋ fʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o wu abaa tɔ kusun asunpu kʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o nu kubuu kɔyɩsa ɩlawʋ fʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o wu fɩtɩla fʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla yɛ o nu ɔkyɩɩfɔ abɛɛ mu‐kuli ɔbʋlɛ fʋ tɔ. Fʋ ayawʋ‐gyipu ɩ kyɩna ba gyɛ asa kparɛ kasɛ sʋ, nɩɩ fʋ taa Sɩtaanɛ adangana pɩna kasɛ sʋ asa kpini.

english_verse: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Kʋtɔ mʋ sʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ bɩɩtɛ Babilon kadɛ kʋsʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, kɩmʋ tɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ wu mʋ atɔwɛpʋ yɛ mʋ asa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba mɔɔ bamʋ kasɛ sʋ kpini, mʋ nkalan.”

english_verse: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
translation_verse: Ɩnɩmʋ kamaa mʋ, n nu ɩlawʋ lala kʋ fɛɛ sakyɔ kʋbʋ lala kʋ maa bʋ sʋsʋ nɩɩ bee kuusi yɛ, “Haleluya! Fan yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Kamɔlɩgɛ yɛ bʋnyaa yɛ ɔlʋn gyɛ anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ lɛɛ.

english_verse: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
translation_verse: Lɩɩ fɛɛ mʋ katalɛ ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩɩ kɩ dɛ ɔkpa. O bun bʋrʋkɔɔnɛ‐kyɩɩ lala mʋ nɩɩ ɔ taa mʋ kɩbʋrʋkɔɔnɛ nyita kasɛ sʋ mʋ kʋpwɛ. Ɩbwaarɛ bɩɩtɛ mʋ kʋsʋ lɩɩ kanan mʋ nɩɩ ɔ mɔɔ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ mʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba bwii kuusi yɛ, “Haleluya! Fan yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Kadɛ mʋ idiisii ii kpee sʋsʋ kakpaa.”

english_verse: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔbʋlɛ kʋ lɩɩ kuwura‐gya mʋ asɛ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan yɩn anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ. Fanɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ kpini yɛ fanɛ mʋ nɩɩ fan sɩlɛ mʋ mʋ, abɩlɩsa yɛ nbii kpini Fan yɩn anɛ Ɩbwaarɛ.”

english_verse: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nu ɩlawʋ kʋ fɛɛ sakyɔ maa kuusi mʋ, yɛ nkyu damantɛ maa kyʋn mʋ ɩlawʋ, nɩɩ i du fɛɛ bʋlayɩ kɩpʋntɛ ɩlawʋ ɩɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Haleluya! Fan yɩn Ɩbwaarɛ. Anɛ‐Nyɩrɩpɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɩbwaarɛ ii gyii kuwura.

english_verse: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
translation_verse: Fan yɛgɛ ɩ waa anɛ ɔkɔn kɛ an gyii anɛ ansi kɛ a taa bɛɛrɛɛ sa mʋ Lɩɩ fɛɛ saŋa fʋʋ fɛɛ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ɔ kpɩɩ mʋ ɔkyɩɩfɔ, nɩɩ ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ kʋʋla mʋ n‐yɩɩ o gyoo.

english_verse: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba sa mʋ kɩkyɩɩfɔ kɩtaa fufuli parɛparɛ ŋalɩgɛsa yɛ ɔ dɩla. .”

english_verse: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kusee ɩ gyɛ sa bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɩɩ bamʋ ba Ɩbwaarɛ kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kɩkyɩɩfɔ tɔ agyitɔ kigyii mʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ bwii tɔwɛ yɛ, ‘Ɩnɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kɔnɔ tɔ asɩn kesintin.’ ”

english_verse: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n muŋa mʋ ayaa tɔ kɛ n sun mʋ. Amaa nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Man waa kanɩn! Ngbaa mɛ n gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ osunpu nɩn fɛɛ fʋ, yɛ fʋ tɔɔmaa ana mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ Yesu asɩn mʋ tɔ kesintin mʋ. Muŋa sun Ɩbwaarɛ! Lɩɩ fɛɛ kesintin asɩn mʋ nɩɩ Yesu lɛɛ kaapʋ mʋ, ɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ ɩmʋ nɩɩ Kayaayu yɛgɛ nɩɩ mʋ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɔwɛ.” Gbaŋɛ fufuli mʋ odiipu

english_verse: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu sʋsʋ maa bugi tɔ nɩɩ n wu gbaŋɛ fufuli maa yɩlɛ. Gbaŋɛ mʋ odiipu kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Kesintin gyipu yɛ Kesintin wuya. Ee gyii asɩn ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ, nɩɩ ɛɛ kɔ ɩkɔ ɩmʋ ɔkpa sʋ.

english_verse: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ mu ansi i du fɛɛ fʋlɔn anobi, nɩɩ awura‐wʋrʋ i bun i bun mu kumu sʋ. Ɔ sʋ kɩtɩɩ kʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ gyaga mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ man nyi kɩmʋ kasɛ amɔɔ mʋ nkʋn.

english_verse: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ o bun balɩmaasʋ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa nyɔ nkalan tɔ, nɩɩ mʋ kɩtɩɩ ɩ gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn.

english_verse: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ sʋsʋ anaakɔpʋ ba buu mʋ, yɛgɛ bee dii ɩgbaŋɛ fufuli nɩɩ ba bun akalala parɛparɛ.

english_verse: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ sʋ sakunde bʋyɛsa kʋ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ, kɩmʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa kɔ gyii ndɛndɛmandɛ sʋ. Ee biti ɔ taa ɔlʋn fɛɛ kɩbɩlɩtɔ mʋ gyii kuwura bamʋ sʋ. Ee biti ɔ taa Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ kʋtɔ kʋmaa sʋ ɔlʋnpʋ mʋ agbʋ lala mʋ taa nyan bamʋ fɛɛ kanan mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ nyan oyu abi bɛɛ lɛɛ nkyu mʋ.

english_verse: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o dii gbaŋɛ fufuli mʋ sʋ kɩtɩɩ kʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ gyaga mʋ balɩmaasʋ yɛ mʋ ɔgyan sʋ yɛ, AWURA TƆ OWURA YƐ ANYƖRƖPƐ TƆ ƆNYƖRƖPƐ.

english_verse: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ kʋ maa yɩlɛ kyɔwɛ tɔ. Nɩɩ ɔ pʋntɛ kenken tɩɩ nbwiibi mʋ nɩɩ bee fugi afuu tɔ mʋ yɛ, “Fan ba! Fan ba bʋga kɛ fen gyii agyitɔ damantɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ waa sa fanɛ mʋ.

english_verse: You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Fan ba gyii awura yɛ anaakɔpʋ abɩlɩsa yɛ alʋlʋnpʋ yɛ ɩgbaŋɛ yɛ bamʋ adiipu yɛ asa kpini yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee gyii bamʋ abaa sʋ yɛ anyɛ yɛ asa kparɛ yɛ asansa n‐yɩɩ inun.”

english_verse: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu kadʋnbwɛ yɛ kasɛ sʋ awura yɛ bamʋ anaakɔpʋ ba maa naa bʋga ba kɔ yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee dii gbaŋɛ fufuli mʋ maa mʋ ɔnaakɔpʋ mʋ ɩkɔ.

english_verse: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
translation_verse: Amaa ba kɩtaa kadʋnbwɛ mʋ yɛ antɩŋɛsa ɔtɔwɛpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ yɩlɛ kadʋnbwɛ ayaa tɔ waa asɩn kyinkyinsa mʋ. Mʋ asɩn kyinkyinsa maŋa nɩɩ ɔ sʋ ɛɛ pɩna, bamʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ kadʋnbwɛ mʋ kakyʋlɛ, nɩɩ ba bɩla bee sun mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ. Ba taa bamʋ anyɔ maŋa yɛgɛ ba kɩɩ, tʋʋ waa ferinwuta fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ yɩlɛ kɩɩ dɩyɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ gbaŋɛ odiipu mʋ, taa sakunde bʋyɛsa mʋ nɩɩ kɩ lɩɩ mʋ kɔnɔ tɔ mʋ, mɔɔ bamʋ anaakɔpʋ mʋ. Nɩɩ nbwiibi mʋ kpini ba gyii bamʋ inun mʋ naa fʋʋ bamʋ ɔkaa.

english_verse: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu nwuragya, nɩɩ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɛ ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ taa kasɛ sʋ asɩn kigyii mʋ ɔlʋn waa bamʋ abaa tɔ. Nɩɩ n bɩla wu asa mʋ nɩɩ ba lɛɛ Yesu asɩn mʋ kaapʋ tɔ mʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩn nɩɩ ba tɩŋɛ bamʋ amu mʋ, bamʋ kala ana. Asa maŋa ba man sun kadʋnbwɛ mʋ abɛɛ mʋ apɔyɩtɔ ɔdakpa mʋ, nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ mʋ kakyʋlɛ mʋ bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ abɛɛ bamʋ abaa sʋ. Nɩɩ falɛ asa nɩmʋ ba bwii ba kyɩna nkpa tɔ nɩɩ ba maa Yesu ba gyii kuwura nsu kagbɩn.

english_verse: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
translation_verse: Kensi‐gyii yɛ kumu ɔkɔn lala wuya ana ɩ gyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba tii kelewu kyiŋi gyankpaasa mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ lewu nyɔsapʋ mʋ man bɩla ɔ sʋ ɔlʋn bamʋ sʋ. Amaa bee biti ba baa, ba gyɛ Ɩbwaarɛ yɛ Krisito aseepu, kɛ ba maa mʋ be gyii kuwura nsu kagbɩn. Sɩtaanɛ kimuuri

english_verse: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
translation_verse: Kɛ o kpee naa pɩna asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kasɛ sʋ mʋ kpini. Ɩsʋwʋlɛ nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ Gogi maa Magogi. Sɩtaanɛ ii biti ɔ taa bamʋ kpini ba bʋga tɔ, kɛ ba naa kɔ ɩkɔ. Nɩɩ ba kyɔ fɛɛ ɔpʋ kɔnɔ kɩkyafa.

english_verse: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
translation_verse: Ba yaasɛ kasɛ sʋ kpini bun kyaabɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ asa, yɛ kadɛ lala mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti mʋ. Amaa fʋlɔn lɩɩ sʋsʋ ba kasɛ sʋ ba dɩyɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kyaabɔɔ Ɩbwaarɛ asa mʋ.

english_verse: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Sɩtaanɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ pɩna bamʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa mʋ tʋʋ waa ferinwuta fʋlɔn mʋ tɔ. Katɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɔ wʋla taa kadʋnbwɛ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɔwɛ bɛɛ pɩna asa mʋ tʋʋ waa mʋ. Bee biti ba gyii awʋrʋfɔ kayɩɩ‐kɔ tɔ, kakyʋwapɛ yɛ kagyanbwɛ kakpaa. Katalɛ laalaalʋwɛ

english_verse: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu kuwura‐gya fufuli lala yɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kɩmʋ sʋ. Sʋsʋ yɛ kasɛ ɩ lɩɩ mu ansi sʋ. Nɩɩ ɔkʋ man bɩla wu ɩmʋ.

english_verse: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu alewupu mʋ nɩɩ ba gyɛ asa kparɛ yɛ asansa, ba maa yɩlɛ kuwura‐gya mu ansi tɔ. Nɩɩ ba bugi ɩwʋlɛ mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kyʋrɔɔ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ awaasa waa tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ ba bɩla bugi ɔwʋlɛ pʋpwɛ kʋ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ talɛ alewupu mʋ, taa bɔla lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba waa nɩɩ ɔ kyʋrɔɔ ɩmʋ waa ɩwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔpʋ lɛɛ alewupu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ kɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, nɩɩ lewu yɛ kamandɛ gbaa ɩ lɛɛ alewupu mʋ nɩɩ ba bʋ ɩmʋ tɔ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩbwaarɛ talɛ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ lɩɩ kʋtɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa sʋ.

english_verse: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba tʋʋ lewu yɛ kamandɛ waa fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ tɔ. Fʋlɔn yen‐yen nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ lewu nyɔsapʋ mʋ.

english_verse: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ba man kyʋrɔɔ mʋ kɩtɩɩ waa nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ mʋ, ba tʋʋ mʋ waa fʋlɔn yen‐yen mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n nu ɔbʋlɛ kʋ maa kuusi ɛɛ tɔwɛ lɩɩ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ yɛ, “Nbɩɩnbɩɩ bɩrɛ, Ɩbwaarɛ ɔkyɩnakpa bʋ asa asɛ, nɩɩ ee biti ɔ kyɩna bamʋ asɛ. Nɩɩ bee biti ba baa ba gyɛ mʋ asa. Ɩbwaarɛ gbaa‐gbaa ii biti ɔ baa ɔ bʋ bamʋ asɛ, nɩɩ mʋ ii biti ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ bamʋ Ɩbwaarɛ.

english_verse: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
translation_verse: Mʋ ii biti ɔ kpayɛ bamʋ ansikyu kpini lɩɩ bamʋ ansi sʋ. Nɩɩ lewu mɛɛ yɛ ɔ baa ɔ bʋ tɔ, abɛɛ kɩbɔɔta tɔ abɛɛ kusu abɛɛ kayɩɩ‐kɔ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ ɩ baa ɩ bʋ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ atɔ dɩdaa mʋ kpini ɩ kyʋn.”

english_verse: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɛ kuwura‐gya mʋ sʋ mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “N waa kʋtɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩpʋpwɛ!” Nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Kyʋrɔɔ ɩnɩmʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ kesintin, nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ ɩ taalɛ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ ɩmʋ sʋ.”

english_verse: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔ bɩla tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Ɩ lʋwɛ! Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Alɩfa yɛ Omega mʋ. Ɩmʋ kasɛ ɩ gyɛ yɛ, Npiili asɛ yɛ laalaalʋwɛ. Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bʋlɩfɔ sʋ mʋ mʋ, n biti n sa mʋ nkpa nkyu mʋ nɩɩ ɩɩ lɩɩ ɔbʋn kumu tɔ, nɩɩ ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ ka sɛhn.

english_verse: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɔ kɔ gyii lɩɩ mʋ, ee biti ɔ nyɛ kusee ɩnɩmʋ lɩɩ mɛ asɛ. Nɩɩ n biti n baa n gyɛ mʋ Ɩbwaarɛ, kɛ ɔ baa ɔ gyɛ mi‐bii.

english_verse: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
translation_verse: Amaa afuupu yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba man kɔɔlɛ gyii naa fʋʋ ɔkaa, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa ineesi yɛ asa amɔɔpʋ, yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa kanyɩn yɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa Sɩtaanɛ adangana yɛ ɩdakpa asunpu yɛ antɩŋɛsa wuya ana mʋ kpini, bamʋ opula ɩ gyɛ ferinwuta fʋlɔn mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ dɩyɛ yen‐yen mʋ tɔ. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ ɩ gyɛ lewu nyɔsapʋ.” Gyerusalem kadɛ pʋpwɛ mʋ

english_verse: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n wu Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ asunɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba sʋ ɩlapɛ isunɔ, nɩɩ laalaalʋwɛ igyerebi isunɔ ɩ bɔla ɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, bamʋ tɔ ɔkʋ ba mɛ asɛ ba tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Ba, kɛ n kaapʋ fʋ Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ. Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ gyɛ Yesu mʋ, mʋ‐ka ɩ gyɛ Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ taa mɛ kayaayu tɔ, dii kpee kɩbɩɩ lala kʋ kumu tɔ legelege. Nɩɩ ɔ kaapʋ mɛ kadɛ kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ nɩɩ kɩ gyɛ Gyerusalem mʋ, kɩ maa kpɩlɩgɛ kɩɩ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɛ sʋsʋ kɩɩ ba kasɛ.

english_verse: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
translation_verse: Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa mʋ yɛgɛ nɩɩ kadɛ lala mʋ kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ sʋ pɩlɛpɩlɛ. Nɩɩ kɩmʋ kɩŋalagɛ sʋ du fɛɛ kubuu kparɛ mʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ tɩɩ yɛ gyasipa, nɩɩ kɩ bɩla kɩ kɩɩ tɔ fɛɛ kɩlasɛ mʋ.

english_verse: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kɩ sʋ kɩbandɩyɛ lala, nɩɩ kɩ bʋ nswɩɩ, yɛgɛ kɩ sʋ nbunɔ kudu anyɔ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ nbɔɔ kudu anyɔ akʋ ba tɛ ba tɛ nbunɔ mʋ nnɔ asɛ. Nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ Ɩsɩrayɩ nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ atɩɩ kʋkʋlʋn‐kʋlʋn gyaga kebunɔ kʋmaa nɩmʋ sʋ kɩmaa kɩmʋ kɩtɩɩ.

english_verse: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ nbunɔ asa bʋ kyɔwɛ kɩlɩɩ, nɩɩ asa ɩ bʋ kyɔwɛ ɔtɩyɛkpa, nɩɩ asa ɩ bʋ adii sʋ, nɩɩ asa ɩ bʋ akpɩlɩgɛ sʋ.

english_verse: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kadɛ mʋ kɩbandɩyɛ gyan abuu kudu anyɔ mʋ nɩɩ ba taa yii kasɛ mʋ sʋ, nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ asunpu kudu anyɔ mʋ atɩɩ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ gyaga abuu kudu anyɔ mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ asɛ mʋ, sʋ suwa kʋtɔ kaalasa, mʋ nɩɩ ee biti ɔ taa kala kadɛ lala mʋ yɛ kɩmʋ nbunɔ yɛ kɩmʋ abandɩyɛ.

english_verse: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
translation_verse: Kadɛ lala mʋ npɛɛ tɔ yɛ kɩmʋ kasɛ nswɩɩ tɔ mʋ kpini gyɛ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ maa taa kʋtɔ kaalasa kaala kadɛ lala mʋ, ɩ waa mayɩlɩ kagbɩn kʋlʋn yɛ alɩfa anuu (

english_verse: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ gyasipa abuu kparɛ nɩɩ ba taa pɔyɩ kɩbandɩyɛ mʋ, nɩɩ suwa nɩɩ ba taa pɔyɩ kadɛ mʋ, yɛgɛ kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ tɔ fɛɛ kɩlasɛ.

english_verse: Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald,
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ba taa abuu ŋalɩgɛsa kparɛ kudu anyɔ yii kɩbandɩyɛ mʋ kasɛ. Kubuu gyankpaasa mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ gyasipa, kɩnyɔsapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ safire, kɩsasapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ agate, kɩnasapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ emeradi,

english_verse: the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
translation_verse: nɩɩ kunusapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ oniki, kisiyesapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ kaneliyan, kusunɔsapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ kirisoliti, nɩɩ kiburuwasapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ berili, nɩɩ kɩkpanɔsapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ topasi, nɩɩ kudusapu mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ kirisopasi, nɩɩ kudu ɔkʋsapʋ mʋ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ yasinti, nɩɩ kudu anyɔsapʋ mʋ mɔɔ bɛɛ tɩɩ kɩmʋ yɛ ametisi.

english_verse: Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔgyalɩn agyibi kparɛ kudu anyɔ nɩɩ ba taa waa nbunɔ kudu anyɔ mʋ. Kʋkʋmaa nɩmʋ maa kɩmʋ kigyibi. Nɩɩ suwa nɩɩ ba taa waa kigbenbi mʋ nɩɩ kɩ dɛ kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ, kɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ tɔ fɛɛ kɩlasɛ.

english_verse: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ n man wu kabwaarɛ‐sunkpa ayɛ kʋ kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔlʋnpʋ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ maa Kabʋlʋpʋ Yesu mʋ, bamʋ ɩ bʋ tʋtɔ nɩɩ asa ba sun bamʋ.

english_verse: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɩ man tiri fɛɛ kyɔwɛ abɛɛ ɔbwaayɩ ɔ lɩɩ kadɛ lala mʋ sʋ, lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɩbwaarɛ bʋnyaa mʋ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ kɩmʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ ɩ gyɛ kɩmʋ fɩtɩla.

english_verse: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ bee biti ba natɛ fɩtɩla mʋ tɔ, nɩɩ kasɛ sʋ awura bee biti ba taa bamʋ bʋnyaa ba kadɛ mʋ tɔ.

english_verse: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kadɛ lala mʋ nbunɔ ɩ mɛɛ yɛ i tii kakaakʋ kpaa, lɩɩ fɛɛ kagyanbwɛ kɩ man bɩla kɩ bʋ tʋtɔ.

english_verse: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ dulinyaa tɔ asa kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ mʋ, bee biti ba taa bamʋ bʋnyaa yɛ bɛɛrɛɛ ba kɩmʋ tɔ.

english_verse: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
translation_verse: Amaa ineesi atɔ akʋ abɛɛ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa ipeeli‐sɩn abɛɛ ɛɛ tɩn atɩŋɛsa, mɛɛ yɛ ɔ nyɛ loo tʋtɔ. Amɔɔ bamʋ nɩɩ ba kyʋrɔɔ bamʋ atɩɩ waa Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ nkpa ɔwʋlɛ mʋ tɔ nkʋn ii biti ba nyɛ loo tʋtɔ.

english_verse: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ kaapʋ mɛ ɔbʋn mʋ nɩɩ kɩmʋ nkyu ɩ sa nkpa, nɩɩ ɩɩ ŋalɩgɛ fɛɛ kɩlasɛ mʋ. Nɩɩ ɩ lɩɩ ɩ kyʋn Ɩbwaarɛ maa mʋ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ kasɛ.

english_verse: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
translation_verse: Ɔbʋn mʋ bɔla kigbenbi lala mʋ nɩɩ kɩ bʋ kadɛ mʋ nsana‐nsana mʋ sʋ. Nɩɩ nkpa oyu mʋ yɩlɛ ɔbʋn mʋ benbe yɛ benbe. Nɩɩ kɩɩ sɔyɩ abi kʋkwɩɩ‐kʋkwɩɩ kudu anyɔ kesu tɔ, nɩɩ ɔbwaayɩ kʋmaa nɩmʋ kɩ maa kɩmʋ abi sɔyɩsa. Nɩɩ oyu maŋa afatɛɛ ɩɩ kyɛ sa ndɛndɛmandɛ alobi kpini.

english_verse: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kʋtɔ kʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ tɔwɛ kɩmʋ nnɔ mʋ, man bɩla yɛ kɩ baa kɩ bʋ kadɛ lala maŋa tɔ. Amaa Ɩbwaarɛ maa mʋ Kabʋlʋpʋ mʋ kuwura‐gya mʋ ii biti ɩ baa ɩ bʋ tʋtɔ, kɛ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ ba sun mʋ.

english_verse: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
translation_verse: Bee biti ba wu mu ansi tɔ, kɛ mʋ kɩtɩɩ kɩ baa kɩ gyan bamʋ asɩkpɩɩ sʋ.

english_verse: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
translation_verse: Nɩɩ kagyanbwɛ man bɩla ɔ bʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ba man bɩla yɛ bee biti fɩtɩla abɛɛ kyɔwɛ ɩ baa ɩ kɩɩ tɔ. Lɩɩ fɛɛ Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ yɛgɛ ɩ baa ɩ kɩɩ tɔ sa bamʋ. Kɛ be gyii kuwura kakpaa.

english_verse: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
translation_verse: Tʋtɔ nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Asɩn mʋ nɩɩ fu nu falɛ, ɩ gyɛ kesintin nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa ɩ taalɛ taa mʋ n‐yɩɩ tʋʋ ɩmʋ sʋ. Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Ɩbwaarɛ mʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ taa mʋ Kayaayu ɛɛ sa atɔwɛpʋ mʋ, i sun mʋ kabɔɔ yɛ ɔ ba kaapʋ mʋ ayaafɔlɛ atɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɩ dagaa ɩ ba waa bilen.” Yesu kibwii‐ba mʋ

english_verse: Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Fan nu! N biti n ba bilen. Bamʋ nɩɩ bee buu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩnlɛɛ kaapʋsa mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ waa bamʋ kusee!”

english_verse: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
translation_verse: Mɛ, Gyɔn, ɩ gyɛ ɔsa mʋ nɩɩ n nu, nɩɩ n wu falɛ atɔ nɩmʋ kpini. Nɩɩ saŋa mʋ nɩɩ n nu nɩɩ n wu ɩnɩmʋ kpini lʋwɛ mʋ, n muŋa Ɩbwaarɛ kabɔɔ mʋ nɩɩ ɔ lɛɛ ɩnɩmʋ ɛɛ kaapʋ mɛ mʋ ayaa tɔ, fɛɛ n sun mʋ.

english_verse: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
translation_verse: Amaa ɔ tɔwɛ mɛ yɛ, “Man waa kanɩn! Maa fʋ, yɛ fʋ tɔmaa ana Ɩbwaarɛ atɔwɛpʋ mʋ yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ ba buu asɩn mʋ nɩɩ ɩ bʋ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, anɛ kpini an gyɛ mu kusun asunpu nɩn. Muŋa sun Ɩbwaarɛ nkʋn!”

english_verse: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
translation_verse: Fan yɛgɛ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa alibi mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ ɛɛ waa alibi. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa ineesi atɔ mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ ɛɛ waa ineesi atɔ. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ ɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ ɛɛ waa adanbɩrasa. Nɩɩ ɔmʋ nɩɩ o du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ mʋ, ɔ kyaga sʋ o du kyɩrɛkyɩrɛ.”

english_verse: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Yesu tɔwɛ yɛ, “Kɩɩ! N biti n ba bilen, n biti n taa mɛ kʋkɔka ba. Kɛ n ba ka ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ lɩɩ kusun mʋ nɩɩ ɔ waa mʋ sʋ.

english_verse: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
translation_verse: Mɛ ɩ gyɛ Alɩfa yɛ Omega, Ɔgyankpaapʋ yɛ Kamaa wuya, Npiili asɛ wuya yɛ Laalaalʋwɛ wuya.”

english_verse: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ ba fwɩɩ bamʋ atɔ bunsa, kɛ ba nyɛ nkpa oyu mʋ abi gyii, kɛ ba bɩla nyɛ ɔkpa bɔla kewusunɔ mʋ loo kadɛ lala mʋ tɔ mʋ, bamʋ ii gyii ɔkɔn.

english_verse: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
translation_verse: Bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa ipeeli‐sɩn yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bɛɛ waa Sɩtaanɛ adangana yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ waa kanyɩn yɛ kakyɩɩ biti kɩgyagaa, yɛ asa amɔɔpʋ yɛ ɩdakpa asunpu yɛ bamʋ nɩɩ bee biti antɩŋɛsa kɩtɩn mʋ, bamʋ ii biti ba sii kadɛ mʋ nbwama mʋ.

english_verse: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
translation_verse: “Mɛ, Yesu, i sun mɛ kabɔɔ yɛ ɔ ba lɛɛ falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ tɔwɛ fanɛ, mʋ nɩɩ fan gyɛ mɛ kʋbʋ‐kʋbʋ mʋ. N lɩɩ Owura Dawidi kanan tɔ, nɩɩ Owura Dawidi kanaana‐bii ɩ gyɛ mɛ. Nɩɩ n gyɛ Nyenyenfuu Kikyikpebi mʋ nɩɩ kɩɩ ŋmaŋɛ tɔ mʋ.”

english_verse: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
translation_verse: Nɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ Kayaayu mʋ maa Ɔkyɩɩfɔ mʋ ba yɛ, “Ba!” Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee nu falɛ asɩn nɩmʋ gbaa ɩ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ba!” Ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ bʋlɩfɔ ɩɩ mɔɔ mʋ mʋ, ɔ ba. Nɩɩ ɔkʋmaa mʋ nɩɩ ee biti mʋ, ɔ ba kɔɔlɛ nkpa nkyu mʋ, ɔ mɛɛ yɛ ɔ ka sɛhn.

english_verse: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
translation_verse: Mɛ Gyɔn, n da ɔkʋmaa nɩmʋ kumu tɔ yɛ, ɔmʋ nɩɩ o nu Ɩbwaarɛ asɩnlɛɛ kaapʋsa lɩɩ ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ, nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan taa asɩn akʋ tii ɩmʋ sʋ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ taa igyerebi mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ ɩmʋ asɩn ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, tii mʋ kʋsʋ bɩɩtɛ sʋ.

english_verse: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
translation_verse: Nɩɩ ɔkʋ kan lɛɛ asɩn akʋ lɩɩ Ɩbwaarɛ asɩnlɛɛ kaapʋsa ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ, Ɩbwaarɛ ii biti ɔ lɛɛ mʋ ogyikpa lɩɩ nkpa oyu mʋ asɛ yɛ Ɩbwaarɛ kadɛ mʋ nɩɩ ba tɔwɛ kɩmʋ asɩn ɔwʋlɛ nɩmʋ tɔ mʋ.

english_verse: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
translation_verse: Ɔmʋ nɩɩ ee gyii asɩn nɩmʋ kpini kesintin mʋ tɔwɛ yɛ, “Ɩ gyɛ kesintin! N biti n ba bilen.” Nɩɩ n tɔwɛ yɛ, ɩ waa kanɩn. Ba, Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu!

english_verse: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɩrɩpɛ Yesu kabwaala mʋ kɩ baa kɩ bʋ Ɩbwaarɛ asa kpini asɛ. Ɩ waa kanɩn.
